sessions.title: Claim Your Free Self-Paced Developer Training
sessions.abstract:
Maximize your GTC experience and unlock up to $90 in free developer training!


Visit the technical training area on the 2nd floor of the Signia Hotel to explore how NVIDIA can help you and your team level up your Al and accelerated computing skills with hands-on technical training.

Stop by the Paseo Room at the Signia Hotel to select your course and enroll. Connect with training experts, engage with instructors, and choose your free self-paced course.


Laptops are available to enhance your learning experience, with courses designed to prepare you for NVIDIA's Generative Al and data science certifications, including:

    Accelerated Computing & Data Science 

    • Accelerating End-to-End Data Science Workflows*
    • Accelerated Machine Learning for Time Series Forecasting*
    • Getting Started with Accelerated Computing in Modern CUDA C++
    • Optimizing CUDA Machine Learning Codes with Nsight Profiling Tools*

    Generative AI, LLM Development & Deep Learning 

    • Building LLM Applications With Prompt Engineering*
    • Generative AI with Diffusion Models*
    • Getting Started with Deep Learning*
    • Introduction to Transformer-Based Natural Language Processing*
    • Rapid Application Development With Large Language Models*

    Agentic AI & Production Deployment 

    • Building Agentic AI Applications with Large Language Models (LLMs)*
    • Building RAG Agents with LLMs*
    • Introduction to Deploying RAG Pipelines for Production at Scale*
    • The Art of Compressing LLMs: Pruning, Distillation, and Quantization Demystified

    *Part of a learning path to prepare for NVIDIA Certification


Hours of Operation:

Monday, March 16 | 2:00 p.m.-6:00 p.m.

Tuesday, March 17 | 8:00 a.m.-5:00 p.m.

Wednesday, March 18 | 8:00 a.m.-5:00 p.m.

Thursday, March 19 | 8:00 a.m.-3:00 p.m.


Location: Signia Hotel Paseo Ballroom (2nd Floor)
sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Your Learning Pathway: Get Certified for Career Success
sessions.abstract:
Join a discussion featuring industry practitioners, along with representatives from both the public and private sectors, as they share how professional certifications add value in today’s AI-driven job market. 

The panel will highlight practical examples of how certification bridges the gap between education to employment, showing why and how to get started, what support is available, and how to accelerate your professional journey.

speakers.name: Karine Vardazaryan
speakers.bio: Karine is a senior training advisor at NVIDIA's Deep Learning Institute, where she leads training sales and operations across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. With extensive experience in the training industry, she focuses on talent development and technology training, specializing in AI, accelerated data science, and accelerated computing. Skilled in learning management systems and curriculum integration, Karine's background in project management further complements her role, allowing her to oversee complex training initiatives from ideation through execution. Her passion for education and technology drives her to continuously innovate and enhance the learning experiences she delivers.

speakers.name: Radoslav Baťo Varga
speakers.bio: Radoslav is a strategic consultant with 30 years’ experience across media, public policy, and political communication. He has advised the Slovak prime minister and president on key domestic policies and managed the successful 2014 presidential campaign. Now advising the minister of education on the Agenda for Change, he leads efforts to bring AI into Slovakia’s education system for 2025–27.

speakers.name: David Senicic
speakers.bio: David Senicic is head of technology and has been with HTEC for last seven years. David has spent over a decade in robotics, building mobile robots and drones, using industrial machines, and even working on exoskeletons. He guides teams from proof of concept to product, and scales robotics development. Growing and scaling teams to solve tasks of modern robotics is what David does on day-to-day basis. He is pursuing a Ph.D. at the ETF School of Electrical Engineering in Belgrade, researching the sim-to-real gap and applying NVIDIA technologies in the lab.

speakers.name: Bora Ger
speakers.bio: Bora is the Global Lead for Human-AI Advantage at Capgemini Invent, where he spearheads global strategies that seamlessly integrate human talent with cutting-edge AI technologies. With deep expertise in enterprise-scale AI adoption and organizational transformation, he empowers Fortune 500 clients to navigate the complex shift toward AI-driven operating models. A recognized thought leader in the space, Bora's unique approach balances technological innovation with human-centric change management to deliver sustainable, high-impact business outcomes.

sessions.title: Your Path to Physical AI and OpenUSD Certification at GTC
sessions.abstract:
Ready to build the next generation of autonomous systems that perceive, reason, and act in the physical world? Join us for an in-depth look at NVIDIA's physical AI training and OpenUSD certification program at this year's GTC.

In this session, you'll discover how NVIDIA Omniverse libraries enable accelerated development across autonomous systems and identify a clear roadmap for earning professional OpenUSD certification for simulation workflows and physical AI data integration.

You'll learn:

  • What's covered in the NVIDIA-Certified Professional: OpenUSD Development exam, including domain areas, competencies, and exam structure.
  • How certification validates your expertise and expands your career opportunities in the growing ecosystem of robotics, factory automation, and autonomous vehicles.
  • Identify study and training resources to prepare for the exam.
  • Insights to more training offered at GTC to accelerate your skills for developing physical AI such as industrial digital twins and robotics simulation.

Whether you're a seasoned robotics engineer, automation architect, or a 3D developer looking to expand your skills in today's rapidly changing world, this session will empower you to build and validate your OpenUSD skills for the next era of physical AI.

speakers.name: Krista Glanville
speakers.bio: Krista Glanville is a technical marketing manager for physical AI, focusing on learning and training programs for NVIDIA Omniverse, OpenUSD, and NVIDIA Isaac robotics platforms. Prior to joining NVIDIA, she specialized in digital fabrication, design technology, and project management. She is a licensed architect, an award-winning product designer, and an alumna of Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute.

speakers.name: Matias Codesal
speakers.bio: Matias Codesal is a Technical Marketing Engineer at NVIDIA focused on demonstrating the value of OpenUSD for large-scale, collaborative workflows and accelerating the onboarding of OpenUSD developers. Matias brings over 15 years of experience developing 3D tools and pipelines for animated feature films and GIS applications

sessions.title: NVIDIA Certification Overview: What You Need to Know
sessions.abstract:
Considering an NVIDIA certification? Join this exclusive overview session to learn how NVIDIA’s certification program can help you validate your skills and advance your career in AI and accelerated computing.


This session provides an overview of all NVIDIA certification exams:

Associate Exams:

  • Generative AI - LLMs
  • Generative AI - Multimodal
  • AI Infrastructure and Operations
  • Accelerated Data Science (NEW)


Professional Exams: 

  • Agentic AI
  • Accelerated Data Science
  • OpenUSD Development
  • Generative AI and LLMs
  • AI Infrastructure
  • AI Operations
  • AI Networking

    • Once you’re ready, reserve your seat to take the exam onsite at GTC.

speakers.name: Aeshah Hadges
speakers.bio: Aeshah Hadges is the Certification Program Manager at NVIDIA's Deep Learning Institute, overseeing the creation and implementation of the developer certification program. Her extensive background includes curriculum development and management of training programs and initiatives aimed at empowering and educating professionals. With an MBA from the University of California, San Diego, she leverages her expertise in business operations and strategic planning to drive the company's educational initiatives forward.

speakers.name: Gary Fluitt
speakers.bio: Gary Fluitt works in NVIDIA’s Education and Enablement team focusing on technical certification development.

speakers.name: Marco Alves
speakers.bio: Marco Alves has worked in the networking industry’s education and certification services for over 20 years with experience in instructor led training, and content and certification development. He has a Master’s degree in Computer Science and Electronics and has worked for many Fortune 500 companies delivering content and professional services worldwide. At NVIDIA he helps facilitate AI skills recognition for individuals and organizations through NVIDIA’s technical certification program.

sessions.title: Get NVIDIA-Certified at GTC for Free: Associate Certification
sessions.abstract:
Register separately to book an exam slot and take your chosen exam onsite. 


Certiverse - Exam Registration Link


Maximize your GTC experience by earning an industry-recognized Associate NVIDIA certification at no additional cost. Conference attendees can register to take an Associate-level certification exam onsite -choosing from Generative AI LLMs, Gen AI Multimodal, Data Science, and AI Infrastructure and Operations.


Advance registration is required to book an exam slot. We recommend preparing in advance using official NVIDIA study guides and the on-demand certification overview session which covers exam format, topics, and test-day tips.


Please note:

  • Adding this session to your calendar is a reminder only and does not reserve an exam seat
  • You must click here to register separately to secure an exam slot
  • Exam seating is limited and available on a first-come, first-served basis
  • You will have 1 hour to take the exam
  • If your preferred time is full, walk-in slots may be available

Don’t miss this opportunity to validate your skills and stand out in the fast-growing fields of AI and accelerated computing—at no cost during GTC.

sessions.title: Leverage Acceleration for AI and HPC in Algorithmic Trading
sessions.abstract:
Join this interactive session with NVIDIA experts to discover how the convergence of artificial intelligence and high performance computing (HPC) is reshaping algorithmic trading. As trading strategies grow in complexity, the need for high-throughput processing and advanced modeling capabilities becomes critical. Speak with our experts about the latest methods to leverage acceleration for data-intensive tasks, from training sophisticated AI models to running massive-scale simulations.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Roman Yokunda Enzmann
speakers.bio: Roman studied economics in his undergraduate and graduate studies. He specialized in machine learning and dynamic optimization, graduating with his master’s thesis on the topic of explainable AI. He has a background in data science and risk modeling within the financial services industry. Roman concentrates on accelerated data science using RAPIDS, with a focus on fraud detection.

speakers.name: Manasa Murthy
speakers.bio: Manasa works on AI initiatives and applied research within the Digital Marketing Organization, focusing on responsible AI, accuracy in financial reasoning, and robust LLM orchestration. With 15-plus years of experience, she specializes in Gen AI, ML, and natural language processing solutions, having led multiple high-impact programs, including the development of financial investor relations agents, multi-modal conversational systems, and AI evaluation frameworks.

speakers.name: Martin Marciniszyn Mehringer
speakers.bio: Martin is a senior DevTech manager at NVIDIA. He holds a master’s degree in computer science from TU Munich and a Ph.D. in discrete mathematics at ETH Zurich. After graduating, Martin spent 15 years in the high-frequency trading industry, focusing on quantitative research and low-latency strategy development. At NVIDIA, he now focuses on optimizing deep neural network training and inference on NVIDIA GPUs for financial services applications.

speakers.name: Peihan Huo
speakers.bio: Peihan Huo is a solutions architect on the Financial Services Industry team at NVIDIA. He has expertise in mathematical optimization, reinforcement learning, and financial modeling. Currently, his work focuses on accelerating capital markets workflows by leveraging NVIDIA advanced computing technologies. Peihan holds a Ph.D. in applied mathematics from Cornell University.

speakers.name: Bogdan Vioreanu
speakers.bio: Bogdan is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, focused on AI solutions for the financial services industry. Previously, he was a quant at Goldman Sachs in the Risk and Asset Management divisions. His background is in quantitative modeling and scientific computing, with academic experience as a postdoctoral assistant professor at the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. He holds a Ph.D. in applied mathematics from Yale University.

speakers.name: Mark Bennett
speakers.bio: Mark Bennett focuses on acceleration for finance. He held prior engineering management positions at Argonne National Laboratory, Bell Laboratories, Northrop Grumman, XR Trading Securities, and Bank of America. Mark co-authored the book "Financial Analytics with R," from Cambridge University Press. He holds a Ph.D. in computer science from the University of California, Los Angeles, and has taught graduate business analytics for the University of Iowa and the University of Chicago.

speakers.name: Derek Beattie
speakers.bio: Derek is a solutions architect for the Financial Services Industry team at NVIDIA. He focuses on the reference architecture platforms for banking and capital markets customers. Derek has worked in similar roles at Dell, Emulex, and IBM. Derek is originally from Scotland, and has been in the United States for the past 15 years.

speakers.name: Siddharth Samsi
speakers.bio: Siddharth Samsi is a senior solutions architect in the Financial Services and Technology team at NVIDIA who focuses on GPU applications within the financial services industry. Prior to NVIDIA, he led research in distributed AI, high performance computing, and energy-aware computing for AI at the MIT Lincoln Laboratory Supercomputing Center. He earned his Ph.D. and master's degree in electrical and computer engineering from The Ohio State University.

speakers.name: Yuliana Zamora
speakers.bio: Yuliana Zamora is a senior developer relations manager driving the adoption of NVIDIA’s AI and accelerated computing across the Capital Markets ecosystem. She brings extensive experience from the high performance computing world, where she championed implementation strategies for critical workloads. She holds a Ph.D. and M.S. in computer science from the University of Chicago.

speakers.name: Dhruv Desai
speakers.bio: Dhruv is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, focusing on capital markets. His background spans quantitative finance and applied AI research. Before NVIDIA, he worked as a Data Scientist at BlackRock, where he led research on pricing / trading / liquidity algorithms. He holds a master's degree in electrical engineering from the University of Pennsylvania and has authored multiple peer-reviewed publications.

speakers.name: Pooja Aniker
speakers.bio: Pooja is a solutions architect specializing in high-performance networking for AI compute fabrics. She works with financial services customers to design low-latency, high-bandwidth AI factories. With 17 years of experience across architecture, management, and customer engagement, she helps organizations build scalable, reliable AI infrastructure and accelerate innovation through next-generation network and fabric technologies.

speakers.name: Lavinia Ghita
speakers.bio: Lavinia is a Solutions Architect Manager at NVIDIA, leading the EMEA technical team for the Financial Services Industry (FSI). Her work focuses on large‑scale AI systems, with emphasis on distillation methods, domain-adaption techniques and model compression methods for efficient AI pipelines. Her research also covers foundation‑model architectures for time‑series analysis and the development of general methods for modelling structure and latent dynamics in temporal processes. Prior to NVIDIA, Lavinia worked on research and applications in this space at a quantitative hedge fund and in big tech. She holds advanced degrees in Mathematics from the École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL).

speakers.name: Yongming Shi
speakers.bio: Yongming is the solution architect manager for NVIDIA China financial services industry, retail, smart spaces, and manufacturing.

sessions.title: Unleashing AI at Scale: Meet the NVIDIA Networking Experts
sessions.abstract:
Join us for an interactive session and engage directly with NVIDIA's networking experts. We'll cover our full-stack networking platform, including NVLINK, Spectrum-X Ethernet, InfiniBand, and BlueField DPUs, and networking software like Cumulus. Learn how these technologies accelerate every phase of AI, from training to inference, and how tools like NVIDIA AIR can help you validate your infrastructure. This session is designed to answer your specific questions and provide the strategic insights you need to unleash your AI at scale.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Taylor Allison
speakers.bio: Taylor Allison is responsible for networking for AI at NVIDIA. Taylor has extensive experience in product marketing, product management, and software engineering, with a focus on AI and HPC solutions. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Taylor held positions at Lenovo and IBM. Taylor has an M.S. in mathematics from the University of North Carolina.

speakers.name: Scot Schultz
speakers.bio: Scot Schultz is an HPC technology specialist with a focus on AI and machine learning systems. Scot has broad knowledge in distributed computing, operating systems, AI frameworks, high-speed interconnects, and microprocessor technologies. Throughout his career in high performance computing systems, he's held engineering and leadership roles, including strategic HPC technology ecosystem enablement.

speakers.name: David Iles
speakers.bio: David Iles is the senior director of Ethernet switching at NVIDIA. David has held leadership roles at 3COM, Cisco Systems, Nortel Networks, and IBM, where he promoted advanced network technologies including high-speed Ethernet, layer 4-7 switching, and software-defined networking. As a lifelong technologist, David invented new ways to benchmark the performance of layer 4-7 switches and has contributed several patents in data center networking.

speakers.name: Moshe Lavi
speakers.bio: Moshe Lavi is Senior Director of Product Management at NVIDIA, driving innovation in AI networking and cloud infrastructure. With deep expertise in high-performance fabrics and large-scale AI systems, Moshe focuses on enabling efficient training and inference across modern data centers.

speakers.name: Avi Alkobi"
speakers.bio: Avi Alkobi is a Sr. Director of Business Development and Technology Alliances. He holds a B.S. degree in Computer Science and M.B.A from the Bar-Ilan University in Israel.

speakers.name: Ranga Maddipudi
speakers.bio: Ranga Maddipudi is a Director of Product Management in the Networking group at NVIDIA in Santa Clara, where he leads product management for the NVIDIA Air Datacenter Simulation platform and SONiC. He brings over 20 years of industry experience, including prior roles at Cisco and VMware, with deep expertise in simulation, automation, telemetry, and observability.

sessions.title: From Foundation to Full-Throttle: Expert Advice on AI Model Customizations
sessions.abstract:
Specialized models are becoming necessary to win in the enterprise. Come speak to our field team about your challenges with deploying specialized models. They can share real stories from their experience with customization strategies, their successes and their failures.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Eric Pham-Hung
speakers.bio: Eric is a solutions architect at NVIDIA with a specialized focus on synthetic data generation for enterprises looking to evaluate agentic solutions, create private twin datasets, and generate training data for model fine-tuning.

speakers.name: Seth Henneman
speakers.bio: Seth has been a solutions architect for three years, focused on solving the technical challenges of NVIDIA's AI Foundry customers. His mission is to empower enterprises to build world-class Gen AI applications.

speakers.name: Ayush Dattagupta
speakers.bio: Ayush Dattagupta is a software engineer on the RAPIDS team at NVIDIA, where he primarily works on accelerating data processing workflows on the GPU for AI applications. Prior to NVIDIA, Ayush received his M.S. degree in Computer Science from UCLA.

speakers.name: Arham Mehta
speakers.bio: Arham Mehta is a product manager on NVIDIA’s deep learning software team, focused on NVIDIA NeMo. He previously worked at various startups in business strategy, machine learning, and software development roles. He holds a bachelor’s degree in computer engineering from the University of Mumbai and a master's in computer science from the University of Southern California.

speakers.name: Sukrit Rao
speakers.bio: Sukrit is a Gen AI solutions architect at NVIDIA with over five years of experience in helping organizations navigate the ML application life cycle—from developing proofs of concept to designing and deploying these solutions in production. In the Gen AI space, he has experience in fine-tuning LLMs and optimizing inference pipelines, having developed agentic and retrieval-augmented generation-based solutions for organizations in the telecom and banking industries.

speakers.name: Mehran Maghoumi
speakers.bio: Mehran Maghoumi is focused on generative AI and the NeMo framework ecosystem. Prior to that, he was a senior deep learning engineer at NVIDIA, working on parking space perception in surround camera systems for the NVIDIA DRIVE platform. He holds a Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Central Florida.

speakers.name: Shashank Verma
speakers.bio: Shashank Verma is a deep learning technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA who's responsible for developing and presenting developer-focused content on various deep learning frameworks, specializing in generative AI and inference optimization. He obtained his master's in electrical engineering from the University of Wisconsin-Madison, where he focused on computer vision, security aspects in data science, and HPC.

speakers.name: Robert Clark
speakers.bio: Robert has been at NVIDIA for five years. He's spent a lot of that time working with large-scale compute clusters and helping bring NVIDIA products to market, such as NeMo Framework for large language models. His claims to fame include breaking everything he touches and asking stupid questions.

sessions.title: Everything You Wanted to Know About AI Factories!
sessions.abstract:
Learn what goes into an AI Factory - Hardware, software, networking, storage ... nothing is off limits. Want to understand how NVIDIA RTX PRO servers can be used with NVIDIA Spectrum-X networking and Run:ai? Have questions about which AI factory is right for your use cases?  Want to know how to stand a AI factory quickly and make sure it is scalable into the future?  This is the place to be.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Paul Bryan
speakers.bio: Paul is a solutions architect focused on NVIDIA DGX systems, specializing in the use and administration of NVIDIA DGX BasePOD and DGX SuperPOD solutions, and the overall DGX platform.

speakers.name: Robert Magno
speakers.bio: Rob is a solution architects at NVIDIA with a background in virtualization, networking, Docker, and Kubernetes. He enjoys the challenge of architecting complex AI/ML environments that ensure data scientists have the necessary tools and underlying hardware resources to drive business results for enterprises.

speakers.name: Jeff Weiss
speakers.bio: Jeff Weiss is a senior director in North America, working on the Solution Architecture & Engineering team at NVIDIA. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Jeff had a seven-year stint at VMware as an end-user computing staff engineer, as well as time at Symantec and Sun Microsystems. His focus is AI infrastructure computing. Jeff is based in Los Angeles.

speakers.name: Yang Yang
speakers.bio: Yang specializes in DGX system and solutions. With 20 years of industry experience, he's held diverse roles in engineering, technical marketing, product development, and solution architects across varies technology domains. His expertise spans AI infrastructure both on-premises and in the cloud, GPU-ready cluster design and implementation, and more.

speakers.name: Scott Ellis
speakers.bio: A technology leader with over 25 years of experience, Scott specializes in architecting the high-performance infrastructure that powers modern artificial intelligence. He possesses deep expertise in designing massively parallel GPU clusters optimized for deep learning and generative AI workloads. Leading cross-functional engineering teams, he delivers scalable, first-to-market AI compute stacks. His work bridges the gap between hardware capabilities and algorithmic demands, transitioning complex AI systems from experimental concepts to production-grade reality.

speakers.name: John Fragalla
speakers.bio: John Fragalla has been with NVIDIA since March 2024. With over two decades of experience in the high performance computing industry, John has established himself as an expert in system architectures, storage solutions, and performance optimization.

speakers.name: Hans Mortensen
speakers.bio: Hans Mortensen is a senior solutions architect at NVIDIA, where he focuses on pioneering NVIDIA Grace solutions for the data center. With a lifetime of experience, Hans masterfully balances the compute, storage, and networking components of HPC solutions to seamlessly align with software requirements. Hans has extensive experience in migrating code to new CPU architectures including MIPS, Itanium, Xeon, and NVIDIA Grace.

sessions.title: Distributed Inference at Scale Using Dynamo
sessions.abstract:
NVIDIA Dynamo is a distributed inference serving framework built to run LLMs efficiently, reliably, and at massive scale. It helps AI providers reduce cost-per-token, meet strict service level objectives, and scale deployments using Kubernetes across large GPU clusters. Learn how Dynamo delivers high-performance distributed inference across frameworks such as SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, and vLLM. We’ll explore key capabilities — including disaggregated serving, KV-cache-aware routing, topology-aware scheduling, and intelligent memory and cache management — that make Dynamo a powerful foundation for production workloads. Whether you’re evaluating Dynamo, experimenting it, or deepening your understanding of its capabilities and real-world impact, join us to ask questions, see how the system works under the hood, and get practical guidance from the experts who build and deploy it.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Anish Maddipoti
speakers.bio: Anish Maddipoti is a product manager at NVIDIA. He currently works on building AI/ML frameworks, such as NVIDIA Dynamo and NVIDIA Grove. Previously, he was a founding team member of Brev.dev (acquired by NVIDIA) and co-founder of Agora Labs. He studied in the Plan II program at the University of Texas at Austin.

speakers.name: Vikram Sharma Mailthody
speakers.bio: Vikram is a founding member of the Systems and Storage research group at NVIDIA Research, with a special focus on addressing fundamental systems-level challenges in emerging data-center workloads and scalable GPU memory and storage systems designs

speakers.name: Akshatha Kamath
speakers.bio: Akshatha Kamath is a product manager at NVIDIA enabling efficient memory management for AI inference. She holds an M.B.A. and a master’s degree in data science for education from Stanford University. She brings experience developing and scaling ML/AI applications across industries, including AI-powered legal research at LawPavilion in Nigeria.

speakers.name: Neelay Shah
speakers.bio: Neelay Shah is the principal software architect for NVIDIA’s Triton Inference Server who helps developers deploy optimized inference solutions to solve real-world problems. He holds a master’s degree in computer science from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and has over 20 years of industry experience.

speakers.name: Amr Elmeleegy
speakers.bio: Amr Elmeleegy is a principal product marketing manager for accelerated computing in the data center, focused on the NVIDIA AI inference platform. Previously, he held business development and product marketing roles at AWS and SAP. He holds an M.B.A. from the University of California at Berkeley Haas School of Business and a bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering from Cairo University.

speakers.name: Tanmay Verma
speakers.bio: Tanmay Verma has been maintaining and developing Triton Inference Server from its early days in 2019, and through the course of time has driven various parts of the project. Additionally, he also serves as backend lead in Triton Inference Server Team, which entails working with various deep learning frameworks to integrate their solution with Triton. Tanmay holds master's degree in computer engineering from Texas A&M University.

speakers.name: Olga Andreeva
speakers.bio: Olga Andreeva is a software engineer at NVIDIA working on the Dynamo KV Block Manager (KVBM), a scalable runtime component for memory allocation, management, and remote sharing of key-value blocks across heterogeneous and distributed environments. Before Dynamo, she worked on core feature implementations for Triton Inference Server. Before NVIDIA, she conducted research in AI and machine learning while pursuing her Ph.D. at the University of Massachusetts Boston.

speakers.name: Kris Hung
speakers.bio: Kris Hung is a Senior System Software Engineer at NVIDIA. She received a Master of Science in Computer Science from the University of California, Irvine. At NVIDIA, her work focuses on AI inference systems and frameworks, previously on Triton Inference Server and now on NVIDIA Dynamo.

speakers.name: Ishan Dhanani
speakers.bio: My name is Ishan Dhanani, and I am a Deep Learning Algorithms engineer primarily focused on inference optimizations. I am currently working on our datacenter scale inference platform (Dynamo). I have a background in Applied Statistics and worked on 2 startups before I joined NVIDIA.

speakers.name: Rudy Pei
speakers.bio: Rudy is currently a deep learning engineer at NVIDIA focusing on optimizing inference pipelines for distributed LLM serving. Previously, he was a senior researcher at BrainChip, where he built a novel state-space model optimized for inference on the edge. He is a physicist by training, and his research spans the domains of statistical physics, unconventional computing methods, and artificial intelligence.

speakers.name: Hongkuan Zhou
speakers.bio: Hongkuan Zhou received M.S. and Ph.D. degree in Computer Engineering at University of Southern California. During his Ph.D., he researched Machine Learning on graphs, Graph Neural Networks, and parallel computing, with a focus on the applications and acceleration of Temporal Graph Neural Networks. He joined NVIDIA in 2024. After that, he  worked on Optimizing inference for LLMs. He joined NVIDIA in 2024. After that, he works on optimizing inference for large language models (LLMs), focusing on determining the best parallelization strategies, memory layout, and other key parameters to enhance performance. His work also involve designing a scheduler for an efficient datacenter-level LLM system, ensuring optimal resource utilization and scalability.

speakers.name: Thomas Montfort
speakers.bio: Thomas is a Senior Engineer on the Dynamo team focused on the Kubernetes stack and working on features such as supporting zero downtime Rolling Updates. Previously he was a Founding Engineer at brev.dev which was acquired by NVIDIA and is now brev.nvidia.com.

speakers.name: Biswa Ranjan Panda
speakers.bio: Biswa Ranjan Panda is a Senior Software Engineer at NVIDIA, where he builds data center–scale software and systems such as Dynamo and NeMo microservices to make large-scale AI serving more efficient. He brings experience developing low-latency distributed systems, ML systems and algorithms across industries, including core trading platforms at Robinhood and Bank of America. Biswa holds a Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology and Master’s degree in Computer Science from Arizona State University.

speakers.name: Qi Wang
speakers.bio: Qi Wang is a Senior Software Engineer focused on large-scale AI systems and distributed infrastructure. Previously, he worked at Meta and Google building large-scale data infrastructure and integrity systems. Outside of work, he enjoys playing piano and figure skating.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.title: How NVIDIA Can Help You, as a Researcher, Accelerate Scientific Discovery
sessions.abstract:
Join this engaging session led by NVIDIA experts who collaborate closely with the global academic research community, supporting leading researchers in leveraging NVIDIA software technologies to drive scientific advancements across diverse fields. We'll provide insights into how various NVIDIA teams, representing different functional areas, can assist in accelerating your scientific discoveries at multiple levels. The team will outline their approach to working with individual researchers and groups, share success stories, and introduce a range of developer support programs, including the Academic Grant Program, DLI courses and teaching kits, etc. We'll also focus on aligning discussions with specific research domains such as robotics, biology, Gen AI, data processing, and climate science—while showcasing tools and resources available to help researchers enhance research outcomes.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Chi-Cheng Fu
speakers.bio: Chi-Cheng Fu is a senior developer relations manager of strategic researcher engagement at NVIDIA, helping scientists advance breakthrough discoveries using accelerated computing and AI. He supports researchers in harnessing these technologies for scientific innovation. Chi-Cheng holds a Ph.D. in bioengineering from UC Berkeley.

speakers.name: Johan Barthelemy
speakers.bio: After earning a Ph.D. in applied mathematics at the University of Namur, Johan Barthélemy joined the University of Wollongong, where he was a senior lecturer and the head of the Digital Living Lab, researching and developing AI-of-things solutions for smart cities and environmental monitoring. Passionate about applied AI and how to accelerate it, he's now a senior developer relations manager at NVIDIA, helping researchers to build the next generation of AIs.

speakers.name: Tomasz Bednarz
speakers.bio: Tomasz's team at NVIDIA builds active collaborations with top researchers and premier research institutions that do compelling, computationally intense work to solve some of the world’s most challenging scientific problems.

speakers.name: Will Ramey
speakers.bio: Will is senior director at NVIDIA, where he leads global teams responsible for the company’s developer programs and the Deep Learning Institute. Since joining the company in 2003, he has served in various technical and leadership roles, including first product manager for the CUDA parallel programming platform. Previously, he managed a game studio and developed advanced technology for the entertainment industry as a product manager and software engineer. He holds a degree in computer science from Willamette University and completed the Japan Studies Program at Tokyo International University. Outside of work, Will enjoys learning piano, playing pickleball, and open-water swimming.

speakers.name: Corentin Lapeyre
speakers.bio: Corentin Lapeyre is a strategic researcher engagement manager at NVIDIA, where he empowers scientists to achieve breakthrough discoveries through accelerated computing and AI. With an academic background in computational physics and data-driven physics across aerospace, energy engineering, and geosciences applications, he supports researchers in leveraging accelerated computing and AI for science.

speakers.name: Judit Szulagyi
speakers.bio: Judit Szulagyi is the director of higher education and research in Europe, Middle East and Africa. As such, she is responsible for setting the strategy on how NVIDIA is engaging with academia in the region. Before joining NVIDIA, she was the professor of computational astrophysics at ETH Zurich. She holds a Ph.D. in the same field from University of Nice, France.

speakers.name: Chelsea Cropper
speakers.bio: Chelsea Cropper is a senior program manager for developer programs at NVIDIA. Her work centers on driving adoption of NVIDIA platforms among the higher education and research community, including researchers, educators, and students. Prior to joining NVIDIA, she spent several years working in EdTech and teaching English abroad.

speakers.name: Andrea Pilzer
speakers.bio: Andrea Pilzer is a HER solution architect at NVIDIA with the NVIDIA AI Technology Center in Italy. He was a postdoc at Aalto University, working on uncertainty estimation for deep learning. He worked at Huawei Ireland and got his Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Trento with professors Nicu Sebe and Elisa Ricci.

speakers.name: Mitesh Patel
speakers.bio: Mitesh Patel is a product marketing manager at NVIDIA, where he works with researchers in higher education to execute their ideas using NVIDIA SDKs and platforms. Before joining NVIDIA, he was a senior research scientist at Fuji Xerox Palo Alto Laboratory. Mitesh received his Ph.D. in robotics from the Center of Autonomous Systems at the University of Technology Sydney, Australia in 2014.

speakers.name: Steve Davey
speakers.bio: Steve Davey is UK& I Director for Higher Education, Research and Supercomputing at NVIDIA. He is responsible for NVIDIAs engagement strategy and enabling the community to  benefit from NVIDIAs programmes and technologies. Steve joined NVIDIA through the acquisition of Mellanox Technologies in 2020, having spent the previous 5 years managing UK High Performance Computing. Previously to that he specialized in computer networking, holding with roles at Brocade, NCR and 3Com. Steve Graduated from the University of Portsmouth with a BSc (Hons) in Computer Science and Statistics.

speakers.name: Kyla Wilkes
speakers.bio: Kyla is a technology leader dedicated to the intersection of education and innovation. Before joining NVIDIA, she served as the VP of Product Partnerships at Dreamscape Learn, and prior to that, she led Public Sector Go-to-Market Programs at Google. Kyla is deeply committed to developing ecosystem initiatives that foster learning and scientific discovery.

speakers.name: Joseph Fullop
speakers.bio: Joshi Fullop is a Sr. Solutions Architect in Higher Education and Research focused on AI and HPC infrastructure. His career spans nearly a decade as an HPC admin, engineer and researcher at Los Alamos National Laboratory and almost two decades prior at the National Center for Supercomputing Applications.  His specialties center on monitoring and analytics to understand how leadership class clusters scale as well as implementing practical system policy and management methods on today's supercomputers.

sessions.title: Accelerate Your AI Infrastructure at Scale
sessions.abstract:
This session will be an extension to the session led by NVIDIA senior solutions architect Kristof De Brouwer, titled "Mastering AI Infrastructure At Scale — a DevOps Playbook." You can ask questions about your scaled AI deployments of our technical experts, explore deployment strategies in depth, and engage in discussions on optimizing NVIDIA AI infrastructure for your specific environments.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Kristof De Brouwer
speakers.bio: Kristof De Brouwer focuses on supporting networking for AI workloads. With over 20 years of experience in the industry, he specializes in data center switching and accelerated computing. In his current role, Kristof is a subject matter expert in designing and supporting scalable, low-latency network architectures that power modern AI and GPU-based compute environments.

speakers.name: Vasileios Karakasis
speakers.bio: Vasileios is a senior systems software engineer at the Applied Systems Engineering Team at NVIDIA, focusing on systems performance and validation. He is the creator and lead developer of the open-source regression testing and validation framework ReFrame. Before joining NVIDIA, Vasileios served various positions in leading supercomputing centers in Europe, including the Swiss National Supercomputing Center.

speakers.name: Nagendra Kumar Nainar
speakers.bio: Nagendra Kumar Nainar is a Solution Architect supporting NCP customers in building and operating AI factories. Nagendra specialize in networking technologies, with deep experience designing, deploying, and operating high-performance InfiniBand and RoCE network fabrics. He bring 20+ years of industry experience helping teams deliver scalable, reliable infrastructure for AI and accelerated computing.

speakers.name: Andre Melkoumian
speakers.bio: Technical Leader at NVIDIA specializing in AI networking and AI fabric for global CSPs. A 20-year networking veteran, he holds a PhD in CECS from the National Academy of Sciences of Armenia.

speakers.name: Arts Yang
speakers.bio:
Arts is a principal hardware architect for Datacenter Systems Engineering and product architect for DGX products.
She is the main author of DGX SuperPOD reference architectures and is one of the original architects for the NVIDIA Cloud Partner reference design program.

sessions.title: Build Industrial Digital Twins for the Era of Physical AI
sessions.abstract:
Want to learn how to get started building industrial facility digital twins with NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and OpenUSD? Join this interactive support session with NVIDIA technical product managers and solutions architects to get direct help and surface your questions, issues, and ideas specific to your use case.

Please Note: Brian Klobucher will be joining us for the Tuesday session only. Adam Brasic and Rama Darbha will be joining us for the Wednesday session only. We encourage you to attend the session accordingly if you'd like to hear from one of them directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: James McKenna
speakers.bio: James McKenna is global product marketing manager at NVIDIA, responsible for the go-to-market strategy for Omniverse for digital twins. Prior to NVIDIA, James worked for Gartner, where he partnered with business and technology leaders from the Fortune 500 on digital transformation and technology strategy.

speakers.name: Adam Brasic
speakers.bio: Adam Brasic is a senior solutions architect for NVIDIA Omniverse, with a primary focus on virtual facility integration. Previously, Adam worked in the drilling segment of the oil industry, instrumenting and visualizing drill floor operations in digital twins.

speakers.name: Mike Geyer
speakers.bio: Mike Geyer leads manufacturing industry marketing for NVIDIA. Having held positions at Caterpillar, Autodesk, Fictiv, and Kaiser Aluminum, he's helped many companies solve a wide range of business challenges throughout the product development process.

speakers.name: Rama Darbha
speakers.bio: Rama Darbha focuses on data center builds. He has broad and deep expertise in data center infrastructures. He guides customers in deploying the largest, most performant, and most scalable data centers.

speakers.name: David Marshall
speakers.bio: David Marshall spends his time with customers designing and building massive, performant, and scalable data centers. He has a plethora of knowledge in networking and specifically data center scale out networking.

speakers.name: Cobus Bothma
speakers.bio: Cobus Bothma works at NVIDIA on Omniverse, focused on the architecture, engineering, construction, & operations industries. With a background in product and architectural design, he specializes in design computation, simulation, and visualization. Prior to NVIDIA, he was the director of applied research at Kohn Pedersen Fox, a global architectural firm. His work included the design of large architectural projects, developing design functions, and progressing technology adoption for advancing the design and construction process.

speakers.name: Esther Lee
speakers.bio: Esther Lee is a global product marketing manager for NVIDIA Metropolis, a platform for building Vision AI agents and applications. Prior to joining NVIDIA, she was a product growth leader focused on accelerating the adoption of AWS AI services across industries.

speakers.name: Brian Klobucher
speakers.bio: Brian Klobucher leads Omniverse ecosystem at NVIDIA, focusing on supercharging partners with technologies for scalable, collaborative, and AI-enabled 3D digital worlds. Brian works closely with business leaders and technology developers to foster strong relationships and bring innovations to life.

speakers.name: Edward Chan
speakers.bio: Edward Chan is a Senior Product Manager on the Omniverse Team at NVIDIA, where he works on furthing digital twin and robotics use cases in industrial applications. Previously, Edward had developed robotic applications and products at Kawasaki Robotics, Keyence, Schneider Electric, and Universal Robots, gaining deep empathy for manufacturers and enterprises employing new automation technology. He holds an MBA from UC Berkeley Haas School of Business and a BS Mechanical Engineering from SJSU.

speakers.name: Florian Gantert
speakers.bio:
Florian Gantert drives NVIDIA’s global business development and strategy for Omniverse and is a leader in metaverse solutions, artificial intelligence, digital twins, and immersive experiences. Prior to his current role, he led the Automotive Enterprise business in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa.

Before joining NVIDIA in 2017, Florian worked at various sales and engineering roles at Texas Instruments, BMW, and Amatek. Florian holds a M.S. in electrical engineering from the Technical University of Munich.

sessions.title: Ask Us Anything About NVIDIA Mission Control
sessions.abstract:
What is Mission Control? What goes into operating a high performance AI factory? How does Mission Control integrate with other tools and processes? This session is the place to get those types of questions answered!

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Salah Chaou
speakers.bio: Salah Chaou is a GPU MLOps solutions architect at NVIDIA, helping customers to use NVIDIA's innovative solutions for accelerating their AI workloads. He studied computer science and mathematics at the University of Paris, and holds an M.S. in computer science advanced networking from the University of Troyes (France). Salah delivers over 20 years of experience architecting and designing advanced solutions in multiple fields across enterprises and service providers.

speakers.name: Jeff Weiss
speakers.bio: Jeff Weiss is a senior director in North America, working on the Solution Architecture & Engineering team at NVIDIA. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Jeff had a seven-year stint at VMware as an end-user computing staff engineer, as well as time at Symantec and Sun Microsystems. His focus is AI infrastructure computing. Jeff is based in Los Angeles.

speakers.name: Chad Chapman
speakers.bio: Chad Chapman has more than two decades of hands-on infrastructure leadership, building, and optimizing large-scale systems at Run:AI and NVIDIA. He has helped customers improve workflow efficiency, GPU utilization, and overall reliability. Starting in the early 2000s with virtualization and data center infrastructure, he now leverages this foundation to architect and tune modern AI systems in cloud-native environments.

speakers.name: Robert Stober
speakers.bio: Robert Stober is a solutions architect at NVIDIA in Santa Clara, California, who specializes in planning and implementing high performance computing clusters and has broad experience integrating applications and workload management systems. Prior to NVIDIA, Robert spent more than 10 years as a solutions architect, director of solutions architects, and finally product manager at Bright Computing.

speakers.name: Charu Ramachandran
speakers.bio: Charu Ramachandran is a technical expert on AI factory focused on AI Infrastructure software. As a senior solutions architect, she helps manufacturers and partners design, build, and deploy AI factories using NVIDIA advanced hardware and software platforms. She is passionate about bringing NVIDIA’s AI factory vision to life by sharing best practices and insights for NVIDIA customers to be successful.

speakers.name: Scott Ellis
speakers.bio: A technology leader with over 25 years of experience, Scott specializes in architecting the high-performance infrastructure that powers modern artificial intelligence. He possesses deep expertise in designing massively parallel GPU clusters optimized for deep learning and generative AI workloads. Leading cross-functional engineering teams, he delivers scalable, first-to-market AI compute stacks. His work bridges the gap between hardware capabilities and algorithmic demands, transitioning complex AI systems from experimental concepts to production-grade reality.

AI factories are hard, but automation can make life easier. Join us to get your questions answered about how to use industry standard and product-specific tools to automate the deployment and operation of your AI factory.

Jeff is based in Los Angeles.

speakers.name: Vallard Benincosa
speakers.bio: Vallard is a crusty, grumpy industry veteran who's enjoyed working on all aspects of the HPC stack, from the hardware to the AI applications that run on top. He enjoys watching machines boot up from bare metal and automatically deploy. He calculates that he's spent at least five years of his life doing that.

speakers.name: Chad Chapman

Starting in the early 2000s with virtualization and data center infrastructure, he now leverages this foundation to architect and tune modern AI systems in cloud-native environments.

speakers.name: David Dean
speakers.bio: David has over 35 years of systems engineering experience with expertise in Department of Defense modeling and simulation, IBM hardware, and high performance computing. He joined NVIDIA as part of the acquisition of Bright Computing, where he had a pre-sales role for seven years, including managing the pre-sales team as the director of systems engineering and pre-sales.

speakers.name: Scott Ellis
speakers.bio: A technology leader with over 25 years of experience, Scott specializes in architecting the high-performance infrastructure that powers modern artificial intelligence. He possesses deep expertise in designing massively parallel GPU clusters optimized for deep learning and generative AI workloads. Leading cross-functional engineering teams, he delivers scalable, first-to-market AI compute stacks. His work bridges the gap between hardware capabilities and algorithmic demands, transitioning complex AI systems from experimental concepts to production-grade reality.

speakers.name: Martin Piercy
speakers.bio: Martin Piercy is a solution architect focused on helping NVIDIA customers succeed with various technologies such as DGX Cloud, NIMs, BCM, and overall AI infrastructure design.

sessions.title: Next-Gen Data Systems: GPU Acceleration for SQL and Vector Databases
sessions.abstract:
AI applications increasingly rely on fast, scalable access to both structured data and high-dimensional vector embeddings. Traditional CPU-based systems struggle to meet these demands at scale. We'll explore how to accelerate SQL databases and vector search engines with GPUs, enabling rapid index builds, low-latency queries, and seamless integration of analytical and AI workloads. Learn how GPU-accelerated data systems unlock new performance levels for modern AI pipelines.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Felipe Aramburu
speakers.bio: Felipe has been working on accelerated SQL engines and CUDA computational primitives for over a decade. At NVIDIA, he works on accelerating hardware-accelerated execution engines with a focus on larger-than-memory problems, distributed computing, and working on components that can be leveraged by other libraries and engine builders.

speakers.name: Manas Singh
speakers.bio: Manas Singh is the technical product manager for Vector Search and Databases at NVIDIA. He has worked as an ML engineer at companies including Amazon and PwC building deep-learning based customer support chatbots training and fine-tuning mutlilingual LLM models. He studied Mechanical and Computer Science Engineering at Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Bombay and holds an MBA from UC Berkeley.

speakers.name: Tanmay Gujar
speakers.bio: Tanmay Gujar works on accelerating data analytic workloads on GPU-accelerated computing systems. He earned his master's degree in computer science at University of California, San Diego in 2023. He's interested in parallel computation, distributed systems, and most problems close to hardware.

speakers.name: Corey Nolet
speakers.bio: Corey is a distinguished engineer at NVIDIA, leading engineering for unstructured data processing acceleration. Prior to NVIDIA, Corey spent over a decade building massive-scale exploratory data science & real-time analytics platforms for big-data and HPC environments in the defense industry. Corey holds Bs. & Ms. degrees in Computer Science. He is also a Ph.D candidate in the same discipline, focused on the acceleration of algorithms at the intersection of graph and machine learning. Corey has a passion for using data to make better sense of the world.

speakers.name: Benjamin Karsin
speakers.bio: Ben Karsin is a Senior developer technology engineer at NVIDIA focused on vector search applications and workloads. Before joining NVIDIA, he was a postdoctoral researcher at the Université libre de Bruxelles, working on parallel and cache-efficient algorithms. He received his Ph.D. in 2018 from the University of Hawaii, where he developed GPU-efficient algorithms and performance models for GPU architectures.

speakers.name: Greg Kimball
speakers.bio: Gregory Kimball is a software engineering manager at NVIDIA working on the RAPIDS team. Gregory leads development for libcudf, the CUDA/C++ library for columnar data processing that powers RAPIDS cuDF. Gregory holds a PhD in applied physics from the California Institute of Technology.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

sessions.title: Accelerated Computing in Financial Services for Banking
sessions.abstract:
Join this interactive session with NVIDIA experts to learn best practices and latest techniques for deploying AI applications and accelerated computing to banking use cases. We'll answer your questions, share implementation insights, and discuss architectural patterns for deploying AI reliably across on-premises, cloud, and hybrid environments. Gain practical guidance on navigating regulatory, data, and governance challenges, and learn how to design robust, efficient, and scalable solutions that unlock new products, services, and operational efficiencies for banks and financial institutions.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: David Williams
speakers.bio: David Williams is an NVIDIA solutions architect on the Financial Services team. He spends his days engaging with data science teams and their wider organizations to foster the adoption of AI and GPU acceleration. Prior to NVIDIA, David obtained his master’s degree in computer engineering from Northwestern University in Chicago.

speakers.name: Jessica Clark
speakers.bio: Jessica specializes in Gen AI. After earning her M.S. in information services, she started her career as a data science consultant, collaborating with diverse companies to tackle complex challenges. As a senior machine learning engineer at Capital One, she created reusable software for ML and delivered technical training. Her passion for learning drove her to Stack Overflow, where she focused on developing and implementing Gen AI solutions.

speakers.name: Roman Yokunda Enzmann
speakers.bio: Roman studied economics in his undergraduate and graduate studies. He specialized in machine learning and dynamic optimization, graduating with his master’s thesis on the topic of explainable AI. He has a background in data science and risk modeling within the financial services industry. Roman concentrates on accelerated data science using RAPIDS, with a focus on fraud detection.

speakers.name: Benika Hall
speakers.bio: Benika Hall is a senior solutions architect in the financial services industry at NVIDIA. She focuses on Gen AI and graphDS. She received her Ph.D. and master's degrees in bioinformatics from the University of North Carolina at Charlotte. Benika graduated from North Carolina A&T State University with degrees in industrial engineering and physics. Benika and her husband have two amazing kids. She enjoys cooking, daily fantasy sports, photography, and fitness.

speakers.name: Flora Huang
speakers.bio: Flora previously worked as the quantitative data science manager in Vanguard’s Investment Management Group, where she led machine learning and generative AI research in quantitative trading and investment strategies. Her experience will continue to help clients leverage NVIDIA’s cutting-edge technology to build scalable and impactful AI solutions across various financial use cases.

speakers.name: Anass Majji
speakers.bio: Anass Majji is a solution architect for the financial services industry at NVIDIA, where he supports banking leaders through their AI adoption journey, helping accelerate transformation in financial services. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he spent six years developing and deploying AI solutions for banks, including BNP Paribas and Crédit Agricole.

speakers.name: Marcus Manos
speakers.bio: Marcus works with deep learning and natural language processing. He has experience in solving complex problems relating to deep learning, machine learning, statistics, and visualization. Marcus holds a master's degree in information and data science from the University of California, Berkeley.

speakers.name: Emanuel Scoullos
speakers.bio: Emanuel Scoullos is a data scientist on the AI Factory team at NVIDIA, where he focuses on GPU applications. Previously, he worked at a startup in the anti-money laundering space, applying data science techniques to construct machine learning pipelines. He earned his Ph.D. and master's degrees in chemical engineering from Princeton University and an undergraduate degree in chemical engineering from Rutgers University.

speakers.name: Yongming Shi
speakers.bio: Yongming is the solution architect manager for NVIDIA China financial services industry, retail, smart spaces, and manufacturing.

speakers.name: Alex Stephens
speakers.bio: Alex is a senior solutions architect covering the financial services industry at NVIDIA.

speakers.name: James Sutton
speakers.bio: James specializes in real-time inference and optimization for enterprise AI. Based in Canada, James leads strategic initiatives in the financial services sector, helping organizations like RBC leverage Gen AI to solve complex challenges. Over the past decade, he's guided industry leaders JPMC, CapitalOne, and Farmers Insurance in AI adoption. Previously, James gained expertise in MLOps and machine learning engineering at Algorithmia, one of the first MLOps platforms, and DataRobot, where he was a principal ML engineer. Passionate about bridging cutting-edge AI technology with practical enterprise solutions, James is committed to empowering businesses to unlock the full potential of AI.

speakers.name: Bogdan Vioreanu
speakers.bio: Bogdan is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, focused on AI solutions for the financial services industry. Previously, he was a quant at Goldman Sachs in the Risk and Asset Management divisions. His background is in quantitative modeling and scientific computing, with academic experience as a postdoctoral assistant professor at the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. He holds a Ph.D. in applied mathematics from Yale University.

speakers.name: Benjamin Wu
speakers.bio: Benjamin Wu is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, focusing on GPU-accelerated applications for the financial services industry. Previously, he was a data science manager at Capital One, leading work on recommender systems. His background is in machine learning and astrophysics, having spent time at NVIDIA Research, NASA, and the National Astronomical Observatory of Japan. He holds Ph.D. and master’s degrees in physics from the University of Florida, and a bachelor’s in physics and mathematics from Duke University.

sessions.title: Connect With Cybersecurity, Agentic AI, and Threat Hunter Experts
sessions.abstract:
Join NVIDIA cybersecurity and agentic AI experts to learn how NVIDIA technology and agentic systems can be applied to strengthen your cybersecurity posture.

Please Note: Rich Harang will be joining us for the Tuesday 3PM session only. We encourage you to attend this session if you'd like to hear from him directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Neha Hudait
speakers.bio: Neha Hudait is a senior security product manager at NVIDIA, leading the development and execution of cybersecurity strategies in collaboration with leadership. With a focus on advancing security research and strategy, Neha plays a key role in shaping the future of security in AI and accelerated computing. Neha holds degrees in electrical engineering & computer science and business administration from the University of California, Berkeley, as part of the Management, Entrepreneurship, and Technology (M.E.T.) program. Her work emphasizes technical innovation, best practices, and driving diversity and inclusion within the cybersecurity community.

speakers.name: Ofir Arkin
speakers.bio: Ofir Arkin is a renowned information security expert with a career spanning academia, consulting, and executive roles. He's passionate about creating innovative products that address customer needs, and has introduced several industry-first technologies. Known for his dedication to mentoring, Ofir has authored numerous influential research papers and articles, and is a recognized speaker in the information security community.

speakers.name: Hsin Chen
speakers.bio: Hsin Chen is a senior data scientist at NVIDIA, advancing cybersecurity with AI-driven solutions. She builds automated threat detection systems, develops open-source AI for security, and applies LLM agentic systems for deep analysis. Previously, she was a tech lead at Vectra AI, designing deep learning and anomaly detection models for XDR platforms. She holds an M.S. in mechanical engineering from Stanford University, specializing in computational modeling and finite element analysis for complex material simulations.

speakers.name: Marcela Denniston
speakers.bio: Marcela Denniston leads Cyber Defense Operations and Engineering at Nvidia focusing on building proactive, threat informed cyber defense. Marcela began her career serving in the United States Navy cyber operations analyst, primarily working in cyber intelligence and threat operations for the National Security Agency. Marcela went on to help domestic and international governments build stream- lined cyber operations and teams, providing groundbreaking aid in national security initiatives against critical war-grade cyberattacks. In 2019, Marcela co-founded Cyber Lantern )acquired by Fortscale), a start-up focused on helping small to mid-size businesses defend themselves against cyber attacks.

speakers.name: Leon Derczynski
speakers.bio: Leon Derczynski is principal research scientist in LLM security at NVIDIA and a professor in natural language processing at ITU Copenhagen. He contributes to leading LLM security bodies, being on the OWASP LLM Top 10 core team, ML Commons, and founder of the ACL SIG on NLP Security. He has published more than 100 papers including recent talks at DEF CON, demonstrating large language model security tech developed at NVIDIA. Leon heads up garak, NVIDIA's LLM vulnerability scanner, in the NeMo Guardrails team.

speakers.name: Rich Harang
speakers.bio: Rich Harang is a principal security architect at NVIDIA, specializing in ML/AI systems, with over a decade of experience at the intersection of computer security, machine learning, and privacy. Rich's work has been presented at USENIX, BlackHat, IEEE S&P workshops, and DEF CON AI Village, among others, and has also been featured in The Register and KrebsOnSecurity.

speakers.name: Becca Lynch
speakers.bio: Becca Lynch is an offensive security researcher on the NVIDIA AI Red Team, where she works to secure AI models and model infrastructure. Her prior work includes applied machine learning for anomaly detection at Duo Security, and leveraging data science expertise to build robust and actionable processes for threat hunting and intelligence. Becca’s work has been presented at Black Hat, DEF CON AI Village, and CAMLIS.

speakers.name: Daniel Rohrer
speakers.bio: Daniel Rohrer has held a variety of technical and leadership roles in his 24 years at NVIDIA. Daniel has taken his integrated knowledge of "everything NVIDIA" to hone security practices through the delivery of advanced technical solutions, reliable processes, and strategic investments to build trustworthy security solutions. He has a master’s degree in computer science from the University of North Carolina.

speakers.name: Laura Seletos
speakers.bio:
Laura Seletos secures AI and cloud workloads at scale. With 10-plus years of experience spanning threat intelligence, security automation, and cloud transformation, Laura specializes in aligning complex technical solutions with regulatory frameworks like NIST 800-53, FedRAMP, and HIPAA. 

Previously, as a principal security consultant at AWS, Laura guided Fortune 500 companies and U.S. government agencies in architecting and deploying secure cloud infrastructures at scale. Before that, she held key roles at Qualys, where she led hybrid-cloud security strategies and large-scale digital transformation initiatives.

speakers.name: Julien Soriano
speakers.bio: Julien Soriano oversees the adoption of AI-fueled security defenses across NVIDIA and the strategic incorporation of security fundamentals at scale within NVIDIA portfolio of AI solutions and products. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Julien was Global Chief Security Officer at technology firms such as Box and MongoDB. He also served as Chief Security Architect at eBay/Paypal and other leadership positions at Cisco and PricewaterhouseCoopers. Julien has dedicated his 25+ years career in building programs specializing in security, risk management, and privacy. He holds a French MS degree in Telecommunications from IMT Atlantique and a BSc in Physics and Quantum Mechanics from Université de Provence.

speakers.name: Emily Sakata
speakers.bio: Emily Sakata is a senior product manager for data center GPUs at NVIDIA. Her work focuses on bringing security features on accelerated computing to deliver trustworthy solutions to market. She has held cybersecurity and security product management roles in software and industrial product companies.

speakers.name: Hema Shankar Bontha
speakers.bio: Focused on bringing confidential AI through frontier model portability to Enterprises with trusted security and scalable SW stack. Focused on Confidential Computing (CC)/ Confidential Containers (CoCo), CC GTM and Licensing aspects.

speakers.name: Rob Nertney
speakers.bio: Rob Nertney is a senior software architect for confidential computing. He has spent nearly 15 years architecting the features and deployment of accelerator hardware into hyperscale environments for both internal and external use by developers. He has several patents in processor design relating to secure solutions that are in production today. In his spare time, he loves golfing when the weather is nice, and gaming (on RTX hardware of course!) when the weather isn’t.

sessions.title: CUDA Developer Best Practices
sessions.abstract:
Join this live Q&A session with some of NVIDIA’s own CUDA developers to demystify the process of building real-world CUDA applications. From utilizing CUDA accelerated libraries, profilers, and debugging tools to setting up CI pipelines, packaging, and builds—this session covers the entire development life cycle. If you’re looking for practical guidance on integrating GPU acceleration into your software, this is the perfect opportunity to get answers and advice from engineers who develop and maintain CUDA software every day.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Jake Hemstad
speakers.bio: Jake Hemstad leads development of the CUDA Core Compute Libraries. He's focused on making CUDA more delightful by making it easier to write high-quality, high-performance modern CUDA software in both Python and C++. Before that, Jake led development of the RAPIDS libcudf CUDA C++ library, where he developed his passion for building high-quality, high-performance software.

speakers.name: Georgii Evtushenko
speakers.bio: Georgii is a member of the CUDA Core Compute Libraries Team at NVIDIA. His core interest has been high-performance computing ever since the beginning of his career. After developing various production HPC applications, his interest turned into delivering speed-of-light performance through high-level Python/C++ abstractions.

speakers.name: Trent Nelson
speakers.bio: Trent Nelson is a Principal Software Engineer on NVIDIA’s CUDA Core Compute Libraries team, where he focuses on delivering a first-class CUDA experience for Python through projects such as cuda.coop and cuTile. A Python core committer since 2008, he brings over two decades of systems experience spanning GPU computing, Windows kernel development, and high-performance infrastructure in finance and large-scale production systems.

speakers.name: Leo Fang
speakers.bio: Leo Fang is a computational scientist and a theoretical physicist by training. He has extensive experience in computational projects involving Python, CUDA, and C/C++. His expertise encompasses code optimization, GPU programming, software engineering, Monte Carlo simulation, partial differential equation solving, numerical integration, and data analysis.

speakers.name: Jaydeep Marathe
speakers.bio: Jaydeep Marathe is a principal software engineer who holds an M.S. and Ph.D. in computer science from North Carolina State University.

speakers.name: Jonathan Bentz
speakers.bio: Jonathan Bentz leads the CUDA technical marketing engineering team at NVIDIA, where his team focuses on creating and delivering engaging content and connecting with CUDA developers. Jonathan holds a Ph.D. in chemistry and a master’s degree in computer science from Iowa State University.

speakers.name: Vyas Ramasubramani
speakers.bio: Vyas leads the development of Python tabular data processing in RAPIDS, including libraries like cudf-polars and cudf.pandas. Vyas has extensive experience across the CUDA development stack in Python, C, and C++, as well as with packaging CUDA-accelerated libraries.

speakers.name: Jonathan Dekhtiar
speakers.bio: Jonathan Dekhtiar has been contributing to many deep learning libraries, compilers, and frameworks since 2019. At NVIDIA, he played a key role in designing Python software and libraries release mechanisms and solving packaging challenges for GPU-accelerated Python code. As a founding member of the WheelNext initiative, Jonathan has dedicated significant energy to advance this open-source work with the community, and is actively working on proofs of concept, demos, and on the writing of PEPs.

speakers.name: Rafael Campana
speakers.bio: Rafael Campana is a senior software engineering director at NVIDIA, where his teams develop several compute-focused software-development products that enable developers tap into the power of the NVIDIA GPUs and embedded platforms. Previously, Rafael shipped tools for multiple gaming consoles and also helped customers ship games and products for these devices. Rafael holds a B.S. in computer science from the University of Texas at Austin.

speakers.name: Ashwin Srinath
speakers.bio: Ashwin Srinath is a Senior Software Engineer at NVIDIA, where he works on the CUDA Core Compute Libraries (CCCL) for Python. Previously, he worked on the cuDF Python library, as part of RAPIDS team at NVIDIA.  He’s passionate about making the experience of using CUDA from Python delightful.

speakers.name: Bryce Lelbach
speakers.bio: Bryce Adelstein Lelbach has spent over a decade developing programming languages, compilers, and libraries. He leads programming language efforts and drives the technical roadmap for NVIDIA's HPC compilers and libraries. He's passionate about C++ and is one of the leaders of the C++ community, having served as chair of the C++ Library Evolution group. Outside of work, Bryce is passionate about airplanes and watches.

speakers.name: Nader Al Awar
speakers.bio: Nader Al Awar is a senior software engineer at NVIDIA and a member of the CUDA Core Compute Libraries (CCCL) team, where he focuses on the development of CUB and cuda.compute. He earned his PhD in Electrical and Computer Engineering from The University of Texas at Austin, specializing in high-performance computing for Python. Nader is passionate about bridging the gap between high-level languages and hardware by accelerating Python code using GPUs.
