sessions.title: Multi-Modal RAG: Build Agentic AI Systems With Video, Audio, and Text
sessions.abstract:
Enterprise developers are moving beyond text-based retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to harness multi-modal data—video, audio, and text. This Connect with the Experts session explores best practices for designing and optimizing multi-modal agentic RAG systems. Learn to combine speech recognition, visual understanding, and LLMs to enable context-rich, dynamic enterprise AI applications. The discussion covers real-world implementation including vector indexing of multi-modal embeddings, latency optimization for large-scale deployments, and fine-tuning for domain-specific retrieval. Gain insights into integrating NVIDIA Nemotron models and cuVS accelerated vector search to create unified RAG pipelines that reason across diverse data types. Engage with NVIDIA experts and fellow developers to exchange design patterns and best practices for powering next-generation enterprise intelligence.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Maryam Najafian
speakers.bio: Maryam is a principal applied AI scientist (technical lead), MIT Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory and Institute for Data, Systems, and Society faculty, and two-time startup founder/CEO with 15 years of hands-on R&D, shipping AI solutions to millions of users. She specializes in delivering transformative AI research and building cutting-edge AI products at scale, focusing on safe, scalable Gen AI, agentic AI, and multi-modal explainable/interpretable AI systems.

speakers.name: Michael Demoret
speakers.bio: Michael Demoret is the lead engineer and architect for NVIDIA Morpheus, an application framework that allows developers to build high-performance pipelines integrated with AI capabilities for the cybersecurity domain. Michael has been with NVIDIA for over seven years and focused on building production-ready inferencing pipelines for large-scale, multi-model AI deployments across multiple sectors. Prior to NVIDIA, Michael spent seven years as a software developer, producing commercial off-the-shelf software for the aerospace industry. His past projects include collision avoidance for the International Space Station and building the first GPU-accelerated ground system at NASA.

speakers.name: Tanay Varshney
speakers.bio: Tanay Varshney is a deep learning developer advocate engineer at NVIDIA responsible for a wide range of deep learning software products. He has a master's degree in computer science from New York University focused on the cross section of computer vision, data visualization, and urban analytics.

speakers.name: Ruchika Kharwar
speakers.bio: Ruchika Kharwar is a product manager at NVIDIA with extensive experience in cloud computing and systems engineering. Before this, she was a customer engineer at Google, driving customer success and the adoption of Google Cloud Platform for nearly six years. Her background also includes roles at RedHat as a cloud success architect and at Texas Instruments in software development. Ruchika has earned certifications like Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect, Data Engineer, and others. She excels at bridging technical and customer-facing roles to ensure successful technology adoption and customer satisfaction.

speakers.name: Edward Li
speakers.bio: Edward Li is a technical marketing engineer with NVIDIA Enterprise Computing. He is a recent graduate of the University of Pennsylvania School of Engineering and Applied Science. He holds a bachelor’s degree and a master’s degree in computer science with a concentration in data science. At NVIDIA, Edward is passionate about data science, AI, and ML, and is working on solutions to bring generative AI to enterprises.

speakers.name: Nave Algarici
speakers.bio: Nave Algarici is an AI and deep learning product manager at NVIDIA, responsible for NeMo Retrieval and a member of the NeMo-LLM product team. Before joining NVIDIA, Nave managed the AI research and development at Voca, acquired by Snap. He holds an M.B.A. from the University of California Berkeley Haas School of Business and master’s and bachelor’s degrees in electrical engineering from Ben Gurion University, Israel.

speakers.name: Annie Surla
speakers.bio: Annie Surla is responsible for developing and presenting a wide range of deep learning software products. She comes with experience working in deep learning applications including vision and natural language processing. She holds a master’s degree in engineering management from Duke University.

speakers.name: Sean Sodha
speakers.bio: Sean Sodha is a deep learning senior product manager at NVIDIA, responsible for multi-modal retrieval and document ingestion for NeMo Retriever. Before joining NVIDIA, Sean ran his own AI venture and was formerly at IBM Watson. He holds an M.B.A. from the Wharton School of Business, an M.S. in engineering from Cornell University, and a B.S. in electrical engineering from Purdue University.

speakers.name: Daniel Fatade
speakers.bio: Daniel Fatade is a senior solutions architect for generative AI at NVIDIA based in Houston, Texas. With over three years of experience as a machine learning Engineer at Microsoft, he specializes in enterprise AI solutions and generative AI implementation. Daniel holds a bachelor's degree in computer engineering from Syracuse University and is an active member of the National Society of Black Engineers.

speakers.name: Jacob Ioffe
speakers.bio: Jacob Ioffe is a software engineer at NVIDIA building GPU-accelerated document extraction and retrieval for LLM applications. A member of the NeMo Retriever team, he focuses on optimizing high-performance data ingestion and RAG-based systems. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Jacob completed his Master’s degree at Cornell Tech with a focus on machine learning and neuroscience. He also holds a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science from Vanderbilt University.

speakers.name: Chantal D Gama Rose
speakers.bio: Chantal D Gama Rose is a Senior Software Engineer working on Agentic AI at NVIDIA. She focuses on building and optimizing enterprise-ready, multi-modal agentic AI systems and enabling their large-scale deployment. Her experience includes advancing the optimization and adoption of GPU-accelerated inference pipelines and agent workflows for real-world environments. Prior to joining NVIDIA, she spent three years at Cisco building production-ready software systems. She holds a Master’s degree in Computational Data Science from Carnegie Mellon University.

sessions.title: Scale MoE Training With Megatron Core: Features, Optimizations, and Best Practices
sessions.abstract:
Engage directly with the Megatron Core MoE development team to explore mixture of experts (MoE) training at scale. This interactive session offers a unique opportunity to discuss the latest advancements in MoE training framework, roadmap, and best practices with the engineers who build it. We'll cover how to train state-of-the-art models like DeepSeek-V3 and Qwen, and share strategies for achieving optimal performance on the latest Grace Blackwell hardware. We'll dive into key features such as leveraging FP8 for accelerated training, implementing efficient expert parallelism with DeepEP/HybridEP, maximizing communication efficiency with 1F1B EP Overlap, and minimizing memory footprint. Whether you're building the next foundation model or optimizing an existing training pipeline, this session delivers actionable insights to maximize throughput and harness the full power of Megatron Core.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Yigong Qin
speakers.bio: Yigong Qin is a developer technology engineer who earned his Ph.D. from the University of Texas at Austin in 2025. He received his B.S. from the University of Science and Technology of China in 2019.

speakers.name: Kunlun Li
speakers.bio: Kunlun focuses on performance optimization for CUDA programming and LLM training. He is currently working on development and optimization in Megatron-Core and Transformer-Engine.

speakers.name: Zijie Yan
speakers.bio: Zijie Yan is a Senior DevTech Engineer and technical leader at NVIDIA, specializing in LLM training systems. He currently leads the Megatron-Core MoE project, driving development and innovation for large-scale MoE training system. Prior to NVIDIA, he researched communication optimization for distributed deep learning at Sun Yat-sen University.

speakers.name: Yuzhong Wang
speakers.bio: Yuzhong is a developer technology engineer at NVIDIA, specializing in large-scale deep learning optimization. He's a core developer of Megatron-Core MoE, where he focuses on accelerating training performance for next-generation, long-context LLMs.

speakers.name: Xin Yao
speakers.bio: Xin Yao is a senior engineer on the DevTech Team at NVIDIA focusing on FP8 training and mixture-of-experts models. He holds a Ph.D. degree in computer science from Tsinghua University, and joined NVIDIA in 2021.

speakers.name: Jack Chang
speakers.bio: Jack is a software engineer at NVIDIA, specializing in deep learning framework development and large-scale model training optimization. He contributed to the early development of Megatron-MoE data parallelism and, since 2024, has been developing Megatron-FSDP within Megatron-Core. Jack's research interests focus on enhancing the performance and usability of large-scale distributed training systems.

speakers.name: Hongxiao Bai
speakers.bio: Hongxiao Bai is a DevTech engineer at NVIDIA who specializes in the performance optimization of large-scale LLM training. He contributes key features to Megatron-Core and Transformer-Engine frameworks.

speakers.name: Zhongbo Zhu
speakers.bio: Zhongbo Zhu joined NVIDIA in July 2024 as an AI developer technology engineer who's been focusing on FP8 training recipes, CUDA kernel optimizations, and related profiling and benchmarking. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Zhongbo earned his M.S. in electrical and computer engineering from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, with a focus on parallel computing and distributed systems.

speakers.name: Yan Bai
speakers.bio: Yan joined NVIDIA in 2024, focusing on LLM training optimization and reinforcement learning optimization.

speakers.name: Dennis Liu
speakers.bio: Dennis Liu is a senior DevTech engineer at NVIDIA, having joined the DevTech team in 2023. He specializes in large-scale deep learning and LLM training optimization. He is an active developer for Megatron-Core MoE and contributed advanced features like MoE Parallel Folding.

speakers.name: Hongbin Liu
speakers.bio: Hongbin joined NVIDIA in 2020 to focus on large-scale distributed training. He also has experience on CUDA parallel computing and HPC.

sessions.title: What's in Your Developer Toolbox? CUDA, AI, and Graphics Profiling, Optimization, and Debugging Tools
sessions.abstract:
Several experts from the tools development and management teams will be available to talk about getting started, best practices, and advanced techniques for application debugging and optimization with NVIDIA developer tools. You can ask questions about anything from high-level concepts to specific tools details and also discuss plans and suggestions for improving the tools in the future. We can provide a brief overview of the NVIDIA Nsight family of design, profiling, and debugging tools, including an introduction to the latest features and copilot integrations for developing CUDA compute, AI, and graphics applications on the newest platforms.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Rafael Campana
speakers.bio: Rafael Campana is a senior software engineering director at NVIDIA, where his teams develop several compute-focused software-development products that enable developers tap into the power of the NVIDIA GPUs and embedded platforms. Previously, Rafael shipped tools for multiple gaming consoles and also helped customers ship games and products for these devices. Rafael holds a B.S. in computer science from the University of Texas at Austin.

speakers.name: Magnus Strengert
speakers.bio: Magnus Strengert is the engineering manager of the Nsight Compute team at NVIDIA. Magnus obtained his Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Stuttgart, Germany in 2010. He joined NVIDIA in 2008.

speakers.name: Holly Wilper
speakers.bio: Holly Wilper's team develops tools that help you be more efficient and effective at harnessing the power of GPUs. For the last 24 years, she's worked on a wide variety of developer tools focused on parallel computing and HPC environments including compilers, profilers, and correctness tools. Holly is a graduate of the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign.

speakers.name: Gaoyan Xie
speakers.bio: Gaoyan Xie is a senior manager for deep learning developer tools at NVIDIA, where he leads a team creating tools for designing high-performance deep neural networks. He is also actively involved with building coding assistants with LLMs. Prior to NVIDIA, Gaoyan worked on 3D graphics drivers at AMD for eight years.

speakers.name: Jackson Marusarz
speakers.bio: Jackson Marusarz is the product manager for Compute Developer Tools at NVIDIA. He focuses on how tools can be used to enable all developers to profile, debug, and optimize CUDA code easily and efficiently. Jackson holds a master’s degree in computer engineering from the University of Colorado, Boulder.

speakers.name: Aurelio Reis
speakers.bio: Aurelio spends his days at NVIDIA working diligently to ensure that real-time graphics developers worldwide have the best tools possible. As the director of graphics tools, Aurelio works with customers inside and outside of NVIDIA to help guide the vision and development of tools like Nsight Graphics and Nsight Aftermath. Prior to his life at NVIDIA, Aurelio was a game developer for 16 years, having worked at most of the big publishers on legendary titles.

speakers.name: Victor da-Cruz-Ferreira
speakers.bio: Victor is a senior software engineer at NVIDIA, focused on GPU software and ML/HPC systems. Over the past several years, he has worked across research and production environments, helping translate machine learning research into reliable, scalable software products. Victor holds a Ph.D. and M.Sc. in Computer Architectures and Machine Learning from the Federal University of Rio de Janeiro.

speakers.name: Zbigniew Kondolewicz
speakers.bio: Zbigniew is a SW engineer with wide optimization/porting experience. He has spent 2 years working for NVIDIA in Compute Sanitizer team.

sessions.title: Real-World Federated Learning With NVIDIA FLARE
sessions.abstract:
Federated learning is transforming data-centric industries such as healthcare, life sciences, finance, and transportation — domains where privacy, compliance, and data governance are essential. NVIDIA FLARE enables collaborative model training across institutions without moving sensitive data, unlocking insights from distributed datasets that would otherwise remain siloed. Today, organizations worldwide are deploying federated learning in production, using NVIDIA FLARE to build more accurate diagnostic models, accelerate drug discovery, improve financial risk modeling, and optimize transportation systems. With Confidential Computing on NVIDIA GPUs, FLARE supports secure, decentralized pipelines that maintain data protection and execution integrity across every site, even when running complex multi-party workflows. Meet the experts behind NVIDIA FLARE and join the discussion.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Holger Roth
speakers.bio: Holger Roth develops distributed and collaborative software and models using federated learning and analytics, exploring the topic in both theory and practice. During the COVID-19 pandemic, he led the experimentation of a federated learning study involving 20 hospitals around the globe to train more generalizable models for predicting clinical outcomes in symptomatic patients. His other research interests include computer-assisted annotation, active learning, and natural language processing. He's an associate editor for IEEE Transactions of Medical Imaging and holds a Ph.D. from University College London. In 2018, he was awarded the MICCAI Young Scientist Publication Impact Award.

speakers.name: Yuan-Ting Hsieh
speakers.bio: Yuan-Ting is a senior software engineer at NVIDIA. He is working on NVIDIA Flare, an application runtime environment designed for NVIDIA federated learning initiatives.

speakers.name: Chester Chen
speakers.bio: Chester works on NVIDIA's NVFLARE team. Chester has 20-plus years of engineering experience in roles ranging from chief architect to director of engineering at various startup and big companies. Before he joined NVIDIA, Chester was the director of data science engineering at GoPro for almost six years.

speakers.name: Isaac Yang
speakers.bio: Isaac Yang is focused on building tools to solve problems related to deep learning. He was the main contributor of DIGITS, Clara Train SDK, and NVIDIA FLARE. Prior to NVIDIA, Isaac worked at Sharp Laboratories of America, designing and developing large-scale cloud-based information management systems, and authored several patents on signal processing on healthcare devices.

speakers.name: Zhijin Li
speakers.bio: Zhijin Li is a Senior Solutions Architect at NVIDIA EMEA, focusing on MedTech. With a Ph.D. in Applied Mathematics from Université Paris-Saclay and extensive experience in the Medical Devices industry, Zhijin has developed expertise in medical imaging, machine learning, federated learning, healthcare digital twins, and healthcare robotics. Zhijin's role involves assisting MedTech customers and academic partners in leveraging various NVIDIA technologies, including Holoscan, IGX, MONAI, NVFLARE, and Isaac for Healthcare, for algorithm development, software engineering, and applied research.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

sessions.title: Leverage AI and Accelerated Computing for Digital Biology: Parabricks, BioNeMo and Clara Open Models
sessions.abstract:
Join NVIDIA experts to explore the field of digital biology. Discuss the latest advancements in accelerated computing and foundation models for drug discovery and genomics — from bioinformatics tooling to protein structure prediction, molecular dynamics to molecular generation, and much more. Learn how NVIDIA BioNeMo, Parabricks, and RAPIDS-singlecell, together with NVIDIA NIM, empower developers, researchers, and enterprises to quickly create generative AI solutions across chemistry, biology, and genomics. Engage directly with our product managers, developer relations, and solution architects to address your challenges and answer your questions.

Please Note: Kushal Shah will be joining us for the Tuesday 11AM session only. We encourage you to attend this session if you'd like to hear from him directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Severin Dicks
speakers.bio: Severin Dicks is a Product Architect for Genomics at NVIDIA and an scverse core member. He helps researchers accelerate single-cell analysis by developing GPU-accelerated workflows and is the primary developer behind rapids-singlecell. Before joining NVIDIA, Severin worked as a software developer in Fabian Theis’ lab at Helmholtz Munich.

speakers.name: Daniel Puleri
speakers.bio: Daniel is an HPC engineer on the NVIDIA Parabricks team, where he focuses on genomics — specifically, accelerating our secondary analysis tools on both GPU and CPU. Daniel holds a Ph.D. in biomedical engineering from Duke University. His work at NVIDIA is driven by a passion for leveraging cutting-edge technology to advance genomic research.

speakers.name: Kyle Gion
speakers.bio: Kyle Gion is a product manager for research at NVIDIA, translating R&D in digital biology and molecular science into impactful products. He focuses on guiding research applying computational biology, computational chemistry, and AI to life sciences, drawing on experience building scientific software and developing cystic fibrosis therapies. Kyle earned his bachelor's and master's degrees in chemical engineering from Brown University.

speakers.name: Neha Tadimeti
speakers.bio: Neha has spent the past eight years making significant strides in both deep learning and digital biology. Neha began her journey at NVIDIA in 2016 as a deep learning software engineer in the Self-Driving Cars department and has since transitioned to the Digital Biology department, applying her extensive expertise in machine learning and computer vision. Neha holds a master’s degree in electrical and computer engineering from Rutgers University, where her research focused on machine learning and computer vision. Her unique blend of technical expertise and strategic vision underscores her commitment to advancing digital biology through cutting-edge AI solutions.

speakers.name: T.J. Chen
speakers.bio: T.J. leads genomics products at NVIDIA and is responsible for leveraging NVIDIA’s expertise in AI, high performance computing, and data analytics stacks to address genomics workflows with accelerated high-accuracy solutions. Prior to NVIDIA, T.J. led teams focused on cloud platforms, data analysis, data interpretation, and visualization, supporting R&D through to clinical applications. She received a Ph.D./M.S. in biomedical informatics from Stanford University and B.S. in computer science/biology from Duke University.

speakers.name: Peter St. John
speakers.bio: Peter is currently a machine learning engineer working on the BioNeMo team at NVIDIA, where he is working on accelerating digital biology workflows in the protein and genomics space. Prior to joining NVIDIA in 2022, he worked for seven years as a project lead at the National Renewable Energy Lab, where his research investigated new fuel chemistries and microbial biomass conversion. He received his Ph.D. in chemical engineering from the University of California, Santa Barbara in 2015.

speakers.name: Pankaj Vats
speakers.bio: Pankaj Vats Ph.D. is senior bioinformatics scientist for NVIDIA Clara Parabricks. He specializes in the research and development of innovative applications for analyzing large complex genomic datasets leveraging NVIDIA's AI, high performance computing, and data analytics capabilities to accelerate high-accuracy genomics workflows. He operates as a distinguished expert in genomics and has contributed significantly to the scientific community with his outstanding research in the field of cancer genomics and precision medicine, where his research has significantly impacted the field.

speakers.name: Xin Yu
speakers.bio: Xin Yu is a Solution Architect Manager at NVIDIA.  She leads a team of solution architects working on AI/ML for healthcare and life sciences, focusing on foundation models, AI scientist, and autonomous labs. When she's not working, she likes gardening and spending time with family.

speakers.name: Kushal Shah
speakers.bio: Kushal is a Senior Solutions Architect within Healthcare and Life Sciences at NVIDIA, primarily focused on the applications of AI within drug discovery. Prior to that, he completed his PhD in Biostatistics at the University of North Carolina (Chapel Hill), where his research involved the development of reinforcement learning methodologies for personalized medicine. He now partners with pharmaceutical and biotech companies to architect and deploy AI solutions that accelerate drug development pipelines.

speakers.name: Darren Hsu
speakers.bio: Darren Hsu is a solutions architect focusing on healthcare and the life sciences. His research interests are structural biology, molecular dynamics simulation, large language models for biochemistry, supercomputing, and synchrotron radiation. He holds a Ph.D. in chemistry from Northwestern University, and worked at AstraZeneca and Oak Ridge National Laboratory as a postdoctoral researcher.

speakers.name: Isabel Wilkinson
speakers.bio: Dr Isabel Wilkinson is the EMEA Solution Architect Manager for Digital Biology at NVIDIA, helping pharma and biotech customers accelerate their workloads using NVIDIA software. She originally trained as a chemist, starting with a Natural Sciences degree from the University of Cambridge and then a PhD from the University of Oxford. Isabel joined NVIDIA in 2024 after a stint as Principal Data Scientist at molab.ai, developing deep learning models for predicting activity and ADME/Tox molecular properties.

speakers.name: John St John
speakers.bio: John St. John is an expert in the distributed training of genomic foundation models, having led collaborative efforts in the development, training, and maintenance of the BioNeMo variant of Arc's Evo2 as well as Basecamp Research's EDEN model. He also brings expertise in single-cell foundation models through his prior work with Geneformer, and in functional genomics models from earlier industry roles. Additionally, John has deep expertise in building validated WGS diagnostic tests from leadership roles at Freenome and Driver.

sessions.title: Harness the Potential and Navigate the Challenges of Agentic AI Across Colleges and Universities Worldwide
sessions.abstract:
Discover how to design, deploy, and scale agentic AI systems that transform research acceleration, student support, and campus operations while navigating governance, safety, and infrastructure challenges unique to higher education. Join NVIDIA's Higher Education and Research team to explore proven architectures, deployment patterns, and integration strategies that help you move from experimentation to production-ready AI agents across your institution.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Amanda Butler
speakers.bio: Amanda Butler is a solutions architect on the Higher Education and Research team, where she focuses on HPC and AI. With a strong educational background and diverse professional experience, Amanda has established herself as a key figure in astronomy, AI, LLMs, and accelerated HPC.

speakers.name: Mike O'Keeffe
speakers.bio: Mike O’Keeffe is a senior solutions architect on the Higher Education and Research team at NVIDIA. He supports researchers, research computing, and university leadership in accelerating computational solutions bridging AI, ML, physics, and quantum computing for scientific applications. Mike received his Ph.D. in physics from the City University of New York.

speakers.name: Mahsa Lotfollahi
speakers.bio: Mahsa works closely with researchers to leverage NVIDIA's technologies for advancing artificial intelligence and machine learning applications. With a strong background in image analysis, machine learning, and deep learning, Mahsa has established herself as a key figure in enabling researchers to tackle complex computational challenges in various fields.

speakers.name: Zoe Ryan
speakers.bio: Zoe is a solutions architect at NVIDIA who's worked in embedded systems/simulations and GPU-accelerated high performance computing, specifically targeting higher education institutions and researchers to adopt and fully utilize GPU technology.

speakers.name: Huiwen Ju
speakers.bio: Huiwen is a solutions architect on the Higher Education and Research team at NVIDIA. She helps researchers leverage NVIDIA's healthcare AI platform CLARA (which offers various GPU-accelerated tools, e.g., MONAI as the high-performance medical imaging PyTorch) to accelerate their research. She received her Ph.D. in neuroscience at Georgia State University in biological neural networks in the mathematical and computational neuroscience field.

speakers.name: Kaleb Smith
speakers.bio: Kaleb Smith is an AI and deep learning expert currently serving as the higher education and research lead for NVIDIA AI Technology Center (NVAITC) for all of North and Latin America. He also serves as the site lead for the first NVAITC at the University of Florida. Kaleb's educational journey is rooted in Florida Institute of Technology, earning his Ph.D. focused in generative AI.

speakers.name: Zahra Ronaghi
speakers.bio: Zahra Ronaghi is a distinguished professional in the field of artificial intelligence and high performance computing, currently serving as the manager of solution architecture at NVIDIA. Her extensive background and expertise in AI, machine learning, and data science have been instrumental in driving innovation and advancing research in various industries.

sessions.title: Build Smarter Robots Using NVIDIA Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab
sessions.abstract:
Next-generation robots need the ability to sense, plan, and act with full autonomy. A simulation-first approach is key to building robots that are adaptable and efficient — supporting rapid development of robust policies and smooth transfer to new tasks across robot embodiments. Connect with NVIDIA experts to learn how NVIDIA Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab can accelerate your robotics pipeline. Explore how to generate synthetic data, train advanced robot policies, and run software-in-the-loop testing to validate your entire stack and close the gap between simulation and real-world deployment.

Please note: Karthik Sachdev, Alex Millane and Hammad Mazhar will be joining us for the Monday 5PM session only. Akul Santosh, Peter Du and Ted Hromadka will be joining us for the Tuesday 2PM session only. We encourage you to attend the session accordingly if you'd like to hear from a particular expert directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Teresa Conceicao
speakers.bio: Teresa leads the Robotics Solutions Architect team at NVIDIA, helping accelerate adoption of NVIDIA's Physical AI technologies. She holds a Master’s degree in Electrical and Computer Engineering from the University of Porto and has previously worked on agricultural robotics localization and navigation, free‑flying space robots, and AI medical imaging. At NVIDIA, she specializes in the Isaac and Cosmos platforms, working hand in hand with customers to make Physical AI a reality.

speakers.name: Edith Llontop
speakers.bio: Edith Llontop is a Robotics Technical Marketing Engineer at NVIDIA. She has a degree in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences from UC Berkeley and has experience doing robotics research both at Nvidia's Generalist Embodied Agent Research Lab and Berkeley's Artificial Intelligence Research Lab. She now works on projects that help support researchers, customers, and the broader robotics community leverage Nvidia's robotics technology.

speakers.name: Akul Santhosh
speakers.bio: Akul Santhosh is a solutions architect on NVIDIA’s Robotics SA team, where he supports customers building on the Isaac and Cosmos platforms. Before joining NVIDIA, he earned a master’s degree in computer science from New York University, where he also conducted research in the Robot Perception Lab.

speakers.name: Kartik Sachdev
speakers.bio: Kartik is a solutions architect for Robotics and AI at NVIDIA. He leads customer engagements across a range of AI driven autonomous machines and robotics with command in topics such as robot planning, computer vision, reinforcement learning and ROS1/2. He brings expertise in NVIDIA platforms such as Isaac Sim, Isaac Lab, and Omniverse among others and has a demonstrated history of working in different geographies - India, Germany and Japan.

speakers.name: Ruben D'Sa
speakers.bio: Ruben D'Sa is the Product Manager for Isaac Lab, bringing over a decade of experience in simulation, robot learning, and ML infrastructure at organizations such as 1x and OpenAI. He holds a Ph.D. from the University of Minnesota where he led the development of shape-shifting and high-endurance UAVs.

speakers.name: Austin Klein
speakers.bio: Austin Klein is a Product Manager for Isaac Sim at NVIDIA, where he focuses on building a simulation platform to accelerate robotics development. He has extensive experience with sim-to-real workflows, particularly related to perception model training and software-in-the-loop testing. Austin holds a B.S. in Mechanical Engineering and a M.S. in Computer Science, both from the University of Southern California.

speakers.name: Rishabh Chadha
speakers.bio: Rishabh Chadha is an engineer at NVIDIA, where he focuses on integrating deep learning and robotics frameworks for the NVIDIA Jetson platforms. He has a master's degree in robotics from Worcester Polytechnic Institute. His interests primarily include deep learning, medical imaging, and robot perception.

speakers.name: Sangeeta Subramanian
speakers.bio: Sangeeta Subramanian is a senior product manager at NVIDIA, focusing on simulation software for robotics learning and evaluation. She previously worked on autonomous vehicle platform software and has a background in system software engineering leadership for NVIDIA DRIVE platforms. She holds a master’s degree in Computer Science from North Carolina State University.

speakers.name: Alex Millane
speakers.bio: Alexander Millane is a senior robotics engineer at NVIDIA, where he works at the intersection of machine perception, robot learning, and simulation. Alex holds a PhD from ETH Zurich, where his research focused on real-time 3D reconstruction for robotics.

speakers.name: Asawaree Bhide
speakers.bio: Asawaree Bhide is a technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA, working on AI and robotics on the Jetson platform. She did her master’s studies in computer science at Georgia Tech and is interested in solving complex perception tasks for autonomous embodied agents.

speakers.name: Gavriel State
speakers.bio: Gavriel State (he/him) is a Senior Director, System Software at NVIDIA, based in Toronto, where he leads efforts involving applications of AI technology to gaming and vice versa, in addition to work in remastering games for NVIDIA's SHIELD TV platform. Previously, Gavriel founded TransGaming Inc, and spent 15 years focused on games and rendering technologies.

speakers.name: Kelly Guo
speakers.bio: Kelly Guo works on projects at the intersection of machine learning and simulation technology. She's an active developer on the Isaac Gym team, with a focus on reinforcement learning frameworks and environments. She also spends a lot of her time in Omniverse, working on projects around robotics and animation.

sessions.title: AI for Payments
sessions.abstract:
Learn from the NVIDIA financial services Solution Architecture team how artificial intelligence is being applied in the payments industry. We'll discuss techniques like tabular transformers, graph neural networks, and NVIDIA NIMs for large language models, and how they apply to critical payments workflows like fraud detection, anti-money laundering, and customer experience.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Benjamin Wu
speakers.bio: Benjamin Wu is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, focusing on GPU-accelerated applications for the financial services industry. Previously, he was a data science manager at Capital One, leading work on recommender systems. His background is in machine learning and astrophysics, having spent time at NVIDIA Research, NASA, and the National Astronomical Observatory of Japan. He holds Ph.D. and master’s degrees in physics from the University of Florida, and a bachelor’s in physics and mathematics from Duke University.

speakers.name: Marcus Manos
speakers.bio: Marcus works with deep learning and natural language processing. He has experience in solving complex problems relating to deep learning, machine learning, statistics, and visualization. Marcus holds a master's degree in information and data science from the University of California, Berkeley.

speakers.name: Roman Yokunda Enzmann
speakers.bio: Roman studied economics in his undergraduate and graduate studies. He specialized in machine learning and dynamic optimization, graduating with his master’s thesis on the topic of explainable AI. He has a background in data science and risk modeling within the financial services industry. Roman concentrates on accelerated data science using RAPIDS, with a focus on fraud detection.

speakers.name: David Williams
speakers.bio: David Williams is an NVIDIA solutions architect on the Financial Services team. He spends his days engaging with data science teams and their wider organizations to foster the adoption of AI and GPU acceleration. Prior to NVIDIA, David obtained his master’s degree in computer engineering from Northwestern University in Chicago.

speakers.name: Anass Majji
speakers.bio: Anass Majji is a solution architect for the financial services industry at NVIDIA, where he supports banking leaders through their AI adoption journey, helping accelerate transformation in financial services. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he spent six years developing and deploying AI solutions for banks, including BNP Paribas and Crédit Agricole.

speakers.name: Flora Huang
speakers.bio: Flora previously worked as the quantitative data science manager in Vanguard’s Investment Management Group, where she led machine learning and generative AI research in quantitative trading and investment strategies. Her experience will continue to help clients leverage NVIDIA’s cutting-edge technology to build scalable and impactful AI solutions across various financial use cases.

speakers.name: Jessica Clark
speakers.bio: Jessica specializes in Gen AI. After earning her M.S. in information services, she started her career as a data science consultant, collaborating with diverse companies to tackle complex challenges. As a senior machine learning engineer at Capital One, she created reusable software for ML and delivered technical training. Her passion for learning drove her to Stack Overflow, where she focused on developing and implementing Gen AI solutions.

speakers.name: Alex Stephens
speakers.bio: Alex is a senior solutions architect covering the financial services industry at NVIDIA.

sessions.title: Connect With the Experts on NVIDIA's Cloud Technologies
sessions.abstract:
Join NVIDIA's cloud team to learn about NVIDIA's portfolio of technologies for building and managing AI factories at scale, including components for monitoring GPU health, automated remediation and resiliency, provisioning and managing multi-tenancy, inferencing and deployment platforms, and more.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Andrew Liu
speakers.bio: Andrew is the manager of the DGX Cloud Technical Marketing Engineering team, focusing on showcasing the power and capabilities of DGX Cloud by creating technical assets and collateral. His goal is to demonstrate how DGX Cloud empowers NVIDIA and the ecosystem to create world-class AI solutions. In his free time, Andrew enjoys being outdoors and going mountain biking and skiing.

speakers.name: Christian Shrauder
speakers.bio: Christian Shrauder leads NVIDIA’s GPU Health product, unifying health and optimization capabilities into a single, portfolio-wide cloud service. Over the past year, he has partnered across engineering, product, and customer teams to drive initial deployments and translate real-world feedback into roadmap priorities that maximize value for enterprises and partners. In addition to GPU optimization, Christian heads product for nvTrust services, delivering cloud capabilities that enable GPU integrity validation and confidential computing. He brings a customer-first mindset and operational rigor to building scalable, trusted solutions.

speakers.name: Ed Balduf
speakers.bio: Ed Balduf is a Principal Cloud Integration Architect at Nvidia focusing on cloud systems design and customer cloud integrations.  Prior to Nvidia, Ed worked at Google as a Cloud Principal Architect supporting customers. Prior to Google, Ed held architecture positions at HammerSpace, SolidFire/NetApp and Fusion-io/SanDisk. Ed contributed to OpenStack Cinder and was on the SolidFire Docker storage driver team. Ed has been working over 20+ years in technology, consulting and sales. Ed is highly technical but has always chosen to work in roles involving interaction with customers as he excels at solving new problems and knowledge sharing.

speakers.name: Peter Cross
speakers.bio: Peter is a Sr Technical Marketing Engineer at NVIDIA, where he creates cutting-edge AI demos for DGX Cloud, showcasing Omniverse, Lepton, Cosmos Curator, and NVCF workloads for major global events. With deep expertise in developer relations and technical marketing, he previously served as Principal Member of Technical Staff at AMD, focusing on AI developer collaterals for data center GPU, and held senior positions at Intel Corporation spanning fab equipment automation software engineering as well as graphics and AI developer enablement. His work bridges advanced AI technologies with practical industry applications.

speakers.name: Yuze Ma
speakers.bio: Yuze Ma is a Product Manager at NVIDIA focused on AI infrastructure and large-scale model deployment. He previously founded Lepton AI, an AI inference platform later acquired by NVIDIA. Yuze specializes in bridging deep technical systems with practical product strategy, helping enterprises build and scale modern AI applications.

speakers.name: Sowmyan Soman
speakers.bio: Sowmyan is a Principal Cloud Integration Architect at NVIDIA and part of the DGX Cloud team. He focuses on enabling large-scale AI infrastructure and empowering AI development by working closely with GPU providers and cloud platforms. His core technology interests include Kubernetes, AI/ML model deployment, GPU computing, and optimizing cloud-based training and inference workloads.He is deeply passionate about AI development enablement, helping teams and customers build, deploy, and scale enterprise-grade AI solutions using secure, efficient, and cloud-native architectures. Sowmyan holds a degree in Information Technology and brings over 15 years of experience in cloud and AI infrastructure.

speakers.name: Andrew Thappa
speakers.bio: Andrew Thappa is a product leader at NVIDIA focused on cloud-scale AI inference platforms. He works at the intersection of infrastructure and developer platforms, shaping how enterprises and sovereign AI initiatives deploy and operate large-scale AI workloads. Prior to NVIDIA, he worked across analytics and observability startups, building systems for operating distributed platforms at scale.

speakers.name: Zoey Zhang
speakers.bio: Zoey Zhang is a technical marketing engineer on DGX Cloud at NVIDIA. She works on integrating machine learning models into large-scale compute clusters on cloud, and uses her technical expertise to bring NVIDIA products to market. Before NVIDIA, her focus was in medical imaging research. Zoey earned her bachelor's degree in engineering at the University of Waterloo.

speakers.name: Jared Conway
speakers.bio: Jared leads a team of product managers focused on cloud and cloud infrastructure. He's a 7-year veteran of NVIDIA having worked across the stack from hardware with EGX through to cloud services with DGX Cloud and Fleet Command.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)

sessions.title: Tile Programming for GPUs
sessions.abstract:
Bring your questions about performance portability, integration with existing frameworks, and migrating from traditional SIMT kernels for an in-depth discussion of when and how to use each model effectively. Connect with CUDA experts who can help you navigate the new generation of tile-based GPU programming models, including OpenAI’s Triton, NVIDIA’s CuTe DSL in CUTLASS, and the recently announced cuTile tile programming model for CUDA. Come explore how these array- and tile-centric abstractions automate memory movement, pipelining, and Tensor Core utilization while still letting you write high-performance kernels in Python or C++ for AI, scientific computing, and HPC workloads.

Please note: Stephen Jones, Rob Armstrong, and Jaydeep Marathe will be joining us for the Tuesday 9AM session only and Nikhil Gupta will be joining us for the Wednesday 3PM session only. We encourage you to attend this session if you'd like to hear from a particular expert directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Bryce Lelbach
speakers.bio: Bryce Adelstein Lelbach has spent over a decade developing programming languages, compilers, and libraries. He leads programming language efforts and drives the technical roadmap for NVIDIA's HPC compilers and libraries. He's passionate about C++ and is one of the leaders of the C++ community, having served as chair of the C++ Library Evolution group. Outside of work, Bryce is passionate about airplanes and watches.

speakers.name: Jaydeep Marathe
speakers.bio: Jaydeep Marathe is a principal software engineer who holds an M.S. and Ph.D. in computer science from North Carolina State University.

speakers.name: Andy Terrel
speakers.bio: Andy leads CUDA Python from the product management team. His research focused on domain-specific languages to generate high-performance code for physics simulations with the PETSc and FEniCS projects. Andy is a leader in the Python open-source software community. He's most notably a co-creator of the Dask distributed computing framework, the Conda package manager, the SymPy symbolic computing library, and NumFOCUS foundation.

speakers.name: Stephen Jones
speakers.bio: Stephen is one of the architects of CUDA, working on defining the language, the platform, and the hardware that it runs on to span the needs of parallel programming, from high performance computing to artificial intelligence. Previously, he led the Simulation & Analytics group at SpaceX, working on large-scale simulation of rocket engines. Stephen has been a part of CUDA since 2008.

speakers.name: Rob Armstrong
speakers.bio: Rob Armstrong is a principal technical product manager for the CUDA toolkit. For over 20 years, he's focused on accelerating software with heterogeneous hardware platforms, and has particular interest in computer architecture and hardware/software interaction.

speakers.name: Nikhil Gupta
speakers.bio:
Nikhil Gupta is an engineering manager on NVIDIA’s CUDA Compiler team, where he leads development of key components of the CUDA C++ toolchain, including NVCC, the NVRTC runtime compiler, and the CUDA device linker and object tools used to build GPU applications.

He has worked on the CUDA compiler stack for over seven years, helping improve compile-time performance, modern C++ support, and the scalability of large CUDA builds. His work spans the CUDA C++ front end, compilation infrastructure, and device linking technologies that power GPU software across AI, high-performance computing, and accelerated data platforms.

Nikhil is passionate about making CUDA C++ more powerful and productive for developers, and works closely with teams across NVIDIA to evolve the CUDA programming model and support the next generation of GPU computing.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)
