sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Bridging the Real-to-Sim Gap with 3D Gaussian Splatting
sessions.abstract:
Advances in 3D Gaussian-based reconstruction are transforming simulation for autonomous vehicles and robotics. This interactive session offers an opportunity to connect directly with experts on cutting-edge techniques for indoor and outdoor physical AI simulation. Learn how Gaussian splatting enables real-time rendering of complex, photorealistic environments with greater computational efficiency than traditional 3D reconstruction methods. Whether you’re a researcher, developer, or industry professional, this session provides the technical grounding and networking opportunities needed to apply 3D Gaussian splatting in simulation pipelines that accelerate autonomy development.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Zoe LaLena
speakers.bio: Zoë LaLena is a solutions architect at NVIDIA with a focus on imaging science and computer vision. Since joining NVIDIA in 2025, she's been working on creating virtual three-dimensional environments from two-dimensional imagery. She earned an M.S. in computer vision from Carnegie Mellon University and a B.S. in imaging science from the Rochester institute of Technology.

speakers.name: Brent Bartlett
speakers.bio: Brent Bartlett is a senior solutions architect at NVIDIA with expertise in geospatial applications, with an emphasis on remote sensing with airborne and spaceborne imagery. He has worked on various projects applying accelerated processing techniques to large-scale geospatial imagery analysis. Brent has over 13 years of experience in the remote sensing field, and earned his Ph.D. in imaging science from the Rochester Institute of Technology.

speakers.name: Matt Cragun
speakers.bio: Matt Cragun is the director of the Autonomous Vehicle Infrastructure product at NVIDIA. For the past decade, he has worked in the automotive and racing industries. Matt holds master’s degrees in business and mechanical engineering from MIT.

speakers.name: Itai Zadok
speakers.bio: Itai Zadok is a senior product manager at NVIDIA, leading neural reconstruction and world foundation model products for autonomous vehicle simulation. Itai holds a bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering from Tel Aviv University and a joint M.B.A.-AI degree from the Kellogg School of Management and the McCormick School of Engineering at Northwestern University.

speakers.name: Harel Omer
speakers.bio: Harel Omer is a senior product manager at NVIDIA with over 13 years of experience in autonomous systems development, including robotics and autonomous vehicles. He specializes in simulation technologies and leads scalable world reconstruction and simulation workflows that advance physical AI. Harel holds a master's degree in system engineering from Tel Aviv University.

speakers.name: Riccardo de Lutio
speakers.bio: Riccardo is a research scientist in NVIDIA's Spatial Intelligence Lab, specializing in neural reconstruction and simulation. He is focused on building NVIDIA Omniverse NuRec with the mission of developing AI methods to reconstruct simulation environments from sensor data in which end-to-end policy models can be tested, evaluated, and trained in closed loop. His initial work was on developing neural reconstruction-based solutions and generative models for simulation.

speakers.name: Mohamed Hosny
speakers.bio: Mohamed is a system software engineering manager at NVIDIA, focusing on simulation solutions. With over 16 years of experience in automotive software, his expertise lies in architecting advanced simulation solutions tailored to diverse customer needs. His work encompasses the integration of various vehicle dynamics and sensor models, scenario creation and reconstruction, and ensuring seamless closed-loop integration between simulators and different autonomous vehicle stacks. Mohamed holds a bachelor of engineering degree from Helwan University, Cairo, and a postgraduate diploma in embedded systems from the Information Technology Institute in Cairo.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

sessions.title: Inter-GPU Communication Techniques and Libraries for HPC and AI
sessions.abstract:
Discuss with experts everything related to inter-GPU communication through NVLink, Infiniband, or other networks. We'll cover all communication libraries: MPI, NCCL, NIXL, NVSHMEM and UCX. This is the perfect place to discuss performance benefits of CUDA stream/graph aware communication, GPU/Kernel initiated communication using device APIs, GPU Direct, NVLink, Infiniband, and SHARP to accelerate your deep learning training workload or your HPC application.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Jiri Kraus
speakers.bio: Jiri Kraus works as a performance expert for GPU high performance computing applications. Jiri has over 10 years of experience in scientific computing and HPC. Multi-GPU programming models are a primary focus.

speakers.name: Jim Dinan
speakers.bio: Jim Dinan is a distinguished engineer at NVIDIA who leads the GPU Communications Software Architecture team. Jim was a James Wallace Givens postdoctoral fellow at Argonne National Laboratory and earned a Ph.D. in computer science from the Ohio State University. Jim’s work focuses on hardware/software co-design, high-speed networking, and scalable communications software.

speakers.name: Arnav Goel
speakers.bio: Arnav Goel has been working on NVIDIA's GPU Communications team since 2023. He graduated from the University of Southern California with a master’s degree in electrical engineering in 2016. Before joining NVIDIA, he worked on Data Plane Development Kit (DPDK) for data center networking solutions at Cisco and Oracle, and accelerating ML workloads on Reconfigurable Dataflow (RDA) Runtime technologies at SambaNova Systems.

speakers.name: Oded Green
speakers.bio: Oded works on a wide range of HPC problems. Previously, Oded was a senior solutions architect and a senior graph software engineer (part of the NVIDIA RAPIDS team). Oded received his Ph.D. in computational sciences and engineering at the Georgia Institute of Technology. Oded is currently an associate editor for the ACM Transaction on Parallel Computing.

speakers.name: Sylvain Jeaugey
speakers.bio: Sylvain has been working on optimizing communication libraries for large-scale HPC/AI systems for 20 years. He's been the main contributor to the NCCL library since its inception in 2016.

speakers.name: Pak Markthub
speakers.bio: Pak Markthub leads the research and development of GPUDirect Async — Kernel Initiate and Kernel Submit technologies, in addition to assisting the development of other core GPUDirect technologies. Pak received a Ph.D. in mathematical and computing sciences from the Tokyo Institute of Technology, Japan. He has spent nearly a decade in the HPC field related to GPU communication technologies.

speakers.name: Akshay Venkatesh
speakers.bio: Akshay Venkatesh is a senior software engineer who's been working with the GPU Communications team at NVIDIA since 2017. His main focus areas include identifying communication requirements for CUDA entities for use with intra-node and inter-node networking transports, and design of CUDA-aware communication protocols in OpenMPI/UCX.

speakers.name: Thomas Gillis
speakers.bio: Thomas Gillis is a senior software engineer at NVIDIA. He contributes to the NCCL library with a focus on new architectures and all-to-all performance. He graduated from UCLouvain (Belgium) with an PhD in Mechanical Engineering in 2019, and a master’s degree in Applied Mathematics in 2015. Thomas previously held postdoctoral research positions at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) and the Argonne National Laboratory.

speakers.name: Benjamin Glick
speakers.bio: Benjamin Glick is a Senior Software Engineer at NVIDIA specializing in GPU communication libraries, high-performance computing, and scalable system software. He focuses on NVSHMEM, enabling high-performance, GPU-aware distributed programming for developers. Ben has extensive experience designing distributed operating systems, memory and I/O architectures, collective communication libraries, and virtualization frameworks for advanced accelerators. He has contributed to software–hardware co-design efforts spanning multiple product generations, from pre-silicon through deployment.

speakers.name: Ke Wen
speakers.bio: Ke Wen works on distributed features in PyTorch, including symmetric memory, model parallel APIs, irregular communication primitives, etc

speakers.name: Katie Gioioso
speakers.bio: Katie Gioioso is a senior software engineer at NVIDIA. She works on the NCCL library, with a focus on the Device API and GPU-Initiated Networking (GIN). Prior to Nvidia, Katie worked on networking and distributed systems at Clockwork Systems.

speakers.name: Davide Rossetti
speakers.bio: Davide Rossetti is currently a senior engineer at NVIDIA. His research focuses on high performance computing (parallel computing, high-speed networking architectures, numerical simulations), while his interests span HPC, computer graphics, operating systems, I/O technologies, GPGPU, embedded systems, and real-time systems. Davide has a degree in theoretical physics from Sapienza Rome University.

speakers.name: Taru Doodi
speakers.bio: Taru Doodi is a Senior HPC Performance Engineer on NVIDIA’s GPU Communications team, where she focuses on NCCL performance. She holds a Master’s degree in Electrical and Computer Engineering from the University of Florida. Before joining NVIDIA, Taru worked at Intel, contributing to MPICH for the Aurora Supercomputer at Argonne National Laboratory with an emphasis on collective algorithms and performance.  Her expertise is in debugging large scale performance bottlenecks, and she enjoys performance tuning to drive peak performance across different networks and platforms.

speakers.name: Harry Xie
speakers.bio: Harry Xie works as a performance expert for high performance inference and networking. Harry has over 10 years of experience in systems and networking.

speakers.name: Moein Khazraee
speakers.bio: Moein Khazraee serves as a senior networking and systems architect at NVIDIA, driven by a fascination with the intricacies of end-to-end systems. He completed his PhD at UC San Diego, followed by a postdoctoral position at MIT. His research centered on the software and hardware co-design of frameworks for networked systems. At NVIDIA, Moein’s work spans AI workload modeling, co-leading the architecture of the NVIDIA NIXL library, and network architecture for NVQLink.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

sessions.title: From Sandbox to Scale: Build Production-Ready AI Agents for Your Business
sessions.abstract:
Join our experts for a candid, face-to-face discussion on the critical steps needed to take your AI agents from proof-of-concept to production. We'll go beyond the basics, diving into real-world challenges like building for scale, ensuring robust security, and maintaining performance in enterprise environments. This is your chance to get practical advice, ask your toughest questions, and learn what it takes to build agents that truly deliver value for the business.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Hsin Chen
speakers.bio: Hsin Chen is a senior data scientist at NVIDIA, advancing cybersecurity with AI-driven solutions. She builds automated threat detection systems, develops open-source AI for security, and applies LLM agentic systems for deep analysis. Previously, she was a tech lead at Vectra AI, designing deep learning and anomaly detection models for XDR platforms. She holds an M.S. in mechanical engineering from Stanford University, specializing in computational modeling and finite element analysis for complex material simulations.

speakers.name: Nic Borensztein
speakers.bio: Nic Borensztein is a generative AI-focused software engineer, leader, and serial entrepreneur based in the San Francisco Bay Area. He is the lead solution architect for Astra, NVIDIA's internal enterprise AI factory. Previously, he was the founder/CTO of CrowdAI, a no-code computer-vision platform.

speakers.name: JB Blair
speakers.bio: JB Blair leads a team of solution architects who focus on accelerated data processing, cybersecurity, and agentic AI. He helps bring innovative NVIDIA technologies in GPU acceleration to product development and engineering teams.

speakers.name: Michael Demoret
speakers.bio: Michael Demoret is the lead engineer and architect for NVIDIA Morpheus, an application framework that allows developers to build high-performance pipelines integrated with AI capabilities for the cybersecurity domain. Michael has been with NVIDIA for over seven years and focused on building production-ready inferencing pipelines for large-scale, multi-model AI deployments across multiple sectors. Prior to NVIDIA, Michael spent seven years as a software developer, producing commercial off-the-shelf software for the aerospace industry. His past projects include collision avoidance for the International Space Station and building the first GPU-accelerated ground system at NASA.

speakers.name: Dhruv Nandakumar
speakers.bio: Dhruv specializes in helping customers harness the power of NVIDIA AI Enterprise for building advanced, scalable, GPU-accelerated pipelines tailored for cybersecurity data processing and AI workloads. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Dhruv was a published deep learning researcher, developing advanced methods for cyberthreat detection, adversarial simulation, and automated security response.

speakers.name: Matt Penn
speakers.bio: Matt Penn is a senior data scientist and leader of cybersecurity solutions architecture at NVIDIA. His focus is the application of GPU-accelerated AI and high-performance data analytics to cybersecurity challenges across industries. ‌He holds a master’s degree in information systems from Indiana University and a bachelor’s degree in aerospace engineering from the University of Central Florida.

speakers.name: Colt McAnlis
speakers.bio: Colt McAnlis is a thought leader in the space of GenAI agents and data compression, as well as an industry expert on developing performant applications across web, mobile, and cloud platforms. He's written books, published hordes of online videos/blogs, and was a college professor a few times. When he's not working to make applications faster, cheaper, and smarter, Colt spends his time preparing for invasions of giant ants from outer space.

speakers.name: Meghana Puvvadi
speakers.bio: Meghana is director of engineering to build AI products and platforms.

speakers.name: Rachel Allen
speakers.bio: Rachel is an engineering manager for cybersecurity AI at NVIDIA. Her team’s focus is the research and development of GPU-accelerated machine learning, deep learning, and GenAI applications to solve cybersecurity challenges. Prior to NVIDIA, Rachel was a lead data scientist at Booz Allen Hamilton, where she designed a variety of capabilities for advanced threat hunting and network defense. She holds a bachelor’s degree in cognitive science and a Ph.D. in neuroscience from the University of Virginia.

speakers.name: Sean Lopp
speakers.bio: Sean Lopp is an engineer focused on developer tooling and examples for enterprise AI. He has led production deployments of AI applications within NVIDIA. Prior to NVIDIA, Sean led teams at various startups and at Google Cloud, where he helped organizations adopt data solutions. He's passionate about open source, especially within the Python and R data ecosystems.

speakers.name: Ryan Angilly
speakers.bio: Ryan is a principal engineer focusing on building agents and agentic platforms. With nearly two decades of experience building business-to-business and business-to-customer software-as-a-service platforms, he specializes in bridging deep technical expertise with strategic product vision.

speakers.name: Rich Harang
speakers.bio: Rich Harang is a principal security architect at NVIDIA, specializing in ML/AI systems, with over a decade of experience at the intersection of computer security, machine learning, and privacy. Rich's work has been presented at USENIX, BlackHat, IEEE S&P workshops, and DEF CON AI Village, among others, and has also been featured in The Register and KrebsOnSecurity.

speakers.name: Ajay Thorve
speakers.bio: Ajay Thorve is a Senior Software Engineer on the Agentic AI team at NVIDIA, where he builds end-to-end applications and reusable system blueprints across the full stack. Previously a core maintainer of RAPIDS cuXfilter and JupyterLab-nvdashboard, Ajay has a deep background in GPU-accelerated data science and visualization. He currently helps drive the development of the AI-Q Research Assistant, an NVIDIA AI Blueprint for deep research and autonomous report generation.

speakers.name: Abhishek Sawarkar
speakers.bio: Abhishek Sawarkar is a product manager in the NVIDIA AI Enterprise team working on Agentic AI. He focuses on product strategy and roadmap of integrating Agentic AI library in partner platforms & enhancing user experience on accelerated computing for AI Agents.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Next-Generation Discovery: Agentic AI for Science, AI-Driven Simulation, and GPU-Accelerated Chemistry
sessions.abstract:
Accelerate your scientific discovery pipeline by connecting directly with experts spanning autonomous AI agents for science, AI for molecular and materials simulation, and high-performance traditional atomistic simulation. You will explore how to leverage the latest GPU-optimized tools — including NVIDIA ALCHEMI Toolkit, cuEquivariance, VASP, and GROMACS — to maximize accuracy and computational throughput for your specific research challenges.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Justin Smith
speakers.bio:
Justin obtained a B.S. in math and Ph.D. in chemistry from the University of Florida, then was a postdoc at Los Alamos National Laboratory, where he was promoted to staff scientist. He was a ML scientist in drug design at Relay Therapeutics.

Justin developed the ANI machine learning interatomic potential (MLIP) and pioneered the development of general-use machine learning interatomic potentials for small molecule drug design. He later contributed to the HIP-NN and AIMNet MLIPs, and active learning methodologies for training MLIPs for materials science and reactive chemistry applications. Now he helps the ML chemistry and materials science community fully utilize NVIDIA hardware.

speakers.name: Evan Weinberg
speakers.bio: Evan Weinberg is a DevTech compute engineer with NVIDIA, focusing on algorithm development and performance optimization in the LAMMPS molecular dynamics application and in the QUDA library for lattice quantum chromodynamics calculations. He received his Ph.D. in physics from Boston University in Massachusetts, and worked on the algorithmic development of multi-scale linear solvers as part of the Exascale Computing Project.

speakers.name: Yutong Zhao
speakers.bio: Yutong Zhao is currently principal product manager of biomolecular simulations in the Digital Biology team. He spent the past decade of his life working at the intersection of free energy methods, molecular dynamics, and machine learning. He was previously senior director of the physics platform at Relay Therapeutics, and before that, technical lead on the FEP+ team and technical co-founder of the MLIP team at Schrodinger. He started his career working on CUDA kernels for OpenMM and Folding@home in Vijay Pande’s lab at Stanford University.

speakers.name: Stefan Maintz
speakers.bio: Stefan Maintz manages development technology engineers for computational chemistry and life sciences based in Zurich, Switzerland. Before he switched to management, Stefan led NVIDIA efforts on VASP and other HPC codes in computational chemistry and materials science. Stefan studied chemistry with a focus on theory and programming and graduated from RWTH Aachen University, Germany.

speakers.name: Logan Ward
speakers.bio: Logan is an application engineer in the AI for Science team at NVIDIA. He helps teams in the public and private sectors build applications that weave together advanced AI and simulation techniques. Logan has a Ph.D. in materials science and engineering from Northwestern University and was a staff scientist at Argonne National Laboratory for six years before joining NVIDIA.

speakers.name: Robert Parrish
speakers.bio: Rob Parrish is a quantum chemist whose favorite topic is finding accelerations for Gaussian-basis electronic stucture on NVIDIA GPUs. Rob has a Ph.D. in quantum chemistry from Georgia Tech, where he was a senior author of the CPU-based PSI4 project. Subsequently, he was a postdoc and associate staff scientist as Stanford / SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory, and spent six years at the startup QC Ware, working on quantum chemistry software on both quantum processing units (for the future) and GPUs (for right now).

speakers.name: Alan Gray
speakers.bio: Alan specializes in HPC and AI application optimization, including GPU-accelerated supercomputer scaling. Previously, he worked as an HPC consultant at EPCC (The University of Edinburgh). His career began in the area of theoretical particle physics, for which he received a Ph.D. from the University of Glasgow, followed by a postdoctoral fellowship at The Ohio State University.

speakers.name: Atul Thakur
speakers.bio: Atul Thakur is the Senior Application Engineer for AI in Chemistry and Materials Science at NVIDIA. He is a computational materials scientist by training and earned his PhD from Rutgers University in 2024. He then completed his postdoctoral work at the University of California San Diego, developing accelerated machine learning models for materials science and discovery. He now brings this expertise to NVIDIA, where he helps advance AI-driven approaches in chemistry and materials research.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science

sessions.title: MedTech: Physical AI for Imaging, Intervention, and Robotics
sessions.abstract:
Physical AI is redefining how next-generation medical devices perceive, decide, and act in the real world. From advanced imaging systems and autonomous interventional workflows to intelligent surgical robots, developers now require a unified stack that bridges data, simulation, and real-time runtime and control. NVIDIA’s platforms — such as Holoscan, Isaac for Healthcare, Omniverse, Cosmos and Metropolis — deliver an end-to-end foundation for building and scaling physical AI: model training with domain-specific foundation models, high-fidelity digital twins for procedure-aware simulation, and deterministic low-latency deployment at the edge. Join NVIDIA experts to learn how to leverage our full-stack technologies to accelerate your development — from imaging and interventional systems to next-generation robotics — and bring safer, smarter MedTech to market faster.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Jay Carlson
speakers.bio: Jay leads product for Holoscan and Holoscan Sensor Bridge at NVIDIA, driving real-time, ultra-low-latency, software-defined data pipelines for healthcare, robotics, industrial, and scientific applications. He previously led hardware and software efforts in surgical robotics and has deep expertise in embedded systems and high-performance sensing.

speakers.name: Mikael Brudfors
speakers.bio: Mikael works as a solution architect at NVIDIA, focusing on healthcare applications in the Europe, Middle East, and Africa region. He specializes in the integration of NVIDIA software and hardware in both medical device and medical imaging applications.

speakers.name: Mostafa Toloui
speakers.bio: Mostafa leads product management for NVIDIA Healthcare Robotics, overseeing the NVIDIA Holoscan and Isaac for Healthcare platforms. His passion is to enable medtech companies to deliver intelligent, patient-centric solutions and disrupt the way diseases are prevented, diagnosed, and treated. Before NVIDIA, Mostafa worked in the medtech industry in various capacities from strategy manager to scientist, bringing products (software and hardware) from concept to launch. Mostafa holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering and an MBA from the University of Minnesota.

speakers.name: Rahul Choudhury
speakers.bio: Rahul leads the Holoscan SDK and MONAI Deploy teams that shape NVIDIA’s strategy for real-time AI on multi-modal sensor data. He builds GPU-accelerated, low-latency platforms powering healthcare imaging, robotic automation, life sciences, and scientific computing. Working at the intersection of AI, real-time systems, and developer platforms, he develops foundations that transform high-bandwidth sensor streams into actionable intelligence. He's led advances in GPU-resident execution, edge-to-cloud orchestration, and simulation/visualization workflows that support NVIDIA’s streaming AI ecosystem. He previously led Clara Applications and holds an M.S. in biomedical informatics from Stanford.

speakers.name: Zhijin Li
speakers.bio: Zhijin Li is a Senior Solutions Architect at NVIDIA EMEA, focusing on MedTech. With a Ph.D. in Applied Mathematics from Université Paris-Saclay and extensive experience in the Medical Devices industry, Zhijin has developed expertise in medical imaging, machine learning, federated learning, healthcare digital twins, and healthcare robotics. Zhijin's role involves assisting MedTech customers and academic partners in leveraging various NVIDIA technologies, including Holoscan, IGX, MONAI, NVFLARE, and Isaac for Healthcare, for algorithm development, software engineering, and applied research.

speakers.name: Anas Abidin
speakers.bio: Anas leads the team that focuses on integration of GPU technology, particularly AI into medical devices, directly working with some of the largest healthcare companies in the world. A core area of focus for him is the optimization of deep learning workloads training and inference. He holds a Ph.D. in biomedical engineering from the University of Rochester, where he conducted research on computational methods for medical image analysis.

speakers.name: Daguang Xu
speakers.bio: Daguang Xu is now a senior research manager at NVIDIA who leads an applied research team in healthcare AI, developing world-class deep learning-based methods to solve challenging problems in the medical domain. His current research interest includes, but is not limited to, medical imaging analysis, foundation models, multi-modality LLM, generative models, and physical AI in healthcare. He has published more than 100 papers on top journals and conferences and has 50-plus granted or in-application patents. Daguang received a bachelor's degree from Tsinghua University, China in 2009. He received M.S.E and Ph.D. degrees from Johns Hopkins University in 2014.

speakers.name: Mahdi Azizian
speakers.bio: Mahdi Azizian is a senior director of engineering at NVIDIA, focusing on development of the Holoscan Platform, Holoscan for Medical, ISAAC for Healthcare, and MONAI. Mahdi holds a Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from Western University in Canada. Prior to joining NVIDIA in 2021, he spent almost a decade at Intuitive, bringing image guidance and intelligence to surgical robots. Mahdi’s technical interests involve intelligent medical devices, medical imaging, medical robotics, and real-time systems.

speakers.name: Bogdan Mitrea
speakers.bio: Bogdan Mitrea is a Senior Solutions Architect on the NVIDIA Healthcare team, where he specializes in edge AI deployment and healthcare AI workflow optimization using NVIDIA’s platform. With extensive expertise in computer vision technologies, he architects solutions that bridge the gap between complex technical requirements and practical healthcare applications. Throughout his career, Bogdan has demonstrated versatility across multiple domains, from developing advanced imaging solutions in radiology and medical devices to pioneering computer vision applications in consumer robotics.

speakers.name: Nadim Daher
speakers.bio: A medtech and health informatics industry expert, Nadim's passion for innovation has led to his current role at NVIDIA, helping develop and diffuse accelerated computing and artificial intelligence in the healthcare field.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.title: AI For Science: Driving Innovation in Research Labs and Academia
sessions.abstract:
AI is revolutionizing the way researchers approach their work and accelerating the pace of discovery. Join this interactive session to discuss the latest methods and models in AI for science, including domain-specific foundation models, research copilots, and autonomous science workflows with NVIDIA engineers and solutions architects. Come chat with us about NVIDIA’s latest research and enterprise products, including collaborations with partners. Some examples include training Nemotron models for data science tasks with NeMo-Gym and NeMo-RL, building agentic research assistants, developing hybrid quantum-classical algorithms with CUDA-Q, accelerating traditional simulations with NVIDIA Apollo and PhysicsNeMo, and AI-driven chemical and materials discovery using NVIDIA ALCHEMI.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Justin Smith
speakers.bio:
Justin obtained a B.S. in math and Ph.D. in chemistry from the University of Florida, then was a postdoc at Los Alamos National Laboratory, where he was promoted to staff scientist. He was a ML scientist in drug design at Relay Therapeutics.

Justin developed the ANI machine learning interatomic potential (MLIP) and pioneered the development of general-use machine learning interatomic potentials for small molecule drug design. He later contributed to the HIP-NN and AIMNet MLIPs, and active learning methodologies for training MLIPs for materials science and reactive chemistry applications. Now he helps the ML chemistry and materials science community fully utilize NVIDIA hardware.

speakers.name: Xin Yu
speakers.bio: Xin Yu is a Solution Architect Manager at NVIDIA.  She leads a team of solution architects working on AI/ML for healthcare and life sciences, focusing on foundation models, AI scientist, and autonomous labs. When she's not working, she likes gardening and spending time with family.

speakers.name: Savitha Pareek
speakers.bio: A senior solutions architect at NVIDIA specializing in HPC/AI performance, large-scale benchmarking, and system design, Savitha is an inventor on multiple NVIDIA patents and author of 30-plus publications, with strong community and conference engagement.

speakers.name: John Linford
speakers.bio: Dr. John Linford leads NVIDIA's industrial engineering product team.  John's technical experience spans high-performance physics simulation, extreme-scale software optimization, software performance analysis, and semiconductor pre-silicon simulation and design.  John received his Ph.D from Virginia Tech with the Outstanding Dissertation Award for his work on accelerated numerical methods for atmospheric chemical kinetics.

speakers.name: Daniel Burkhardt
speakers.bio: Daniel Burkhardt is a developer relations manager at NVIDIA, where he manages product strategy and partnerships with organizations building foundation models and AI agents for the life sciences. Previously, he led a machine-learning research group at Cellarity, an AI drug-discovery company. Daniel received his doctorate from Yale University by developing representation-learning algorithms for biomedical data.

speakers.name: Christian Munley
speakers.bio: Christian Munley is a solutions architect at NVIDIA in the New College Grad rotation program, where he supports various cutting-edge projects for AI in enterprise and healthcare. His background includes research in HPC, machine learning, and biophysics. He graduated from the University of Delaware with bachelor’s degrees in applied physics and computer science.

speakers.name: Ramya Gurunathan
speakers.bio: Ramya works with research teams around the world to convert high-bandwidth sensor data into real-time scientific insights. Her work on the Edge Supercomputing team sits at the intersection of metrology, AI-driven sensor processing, high-performance networking, and edge-deployed GPU compute. Ramya holds a PhD in Materials Science and Engineering from Northwestern University and an MPhil in Scientific Computing from the University of Cambridge. Prior to joining NVIDIA, she developed AI for Materials Science models at NIST and implemented AI-based quality control at Wolfspeed, a leader in wide-bandgap semiconductor manufacturing.

speakers.name: Huiwen Ju
speakers.bio: Huiwen is a solutions architect on the Higher Education and Research team at NVIDIA. She helps researchers leverage NVIDIA's healthcare AI platform CLARA (which offers various GPU-accelerated tools, e.g., MONAI as the high-performance medical imaging PyTorch) to accelerate their research. She received her Ph.D. in neuroscience at Georgia State University in biological neural networks in the mathematical and computational neuroscience field.

sessions.title: Build End-to-End Medical AI Workflows With NVIDIA Clara Open Models
sessions.abstract:
Connect with experts on NVIDIA Clara Open Models enabling end-to-end medical AI workflows — from real-time segmentation and synthetic data generation to explainable clinical reasoning and healthcare robotics. Explore deployment strategies across imaging, surgical simulation, and autonomous systems.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Michael Zephyr
speakers.bio: Michael Zephyr is a technical product manager for healthcare AI at NVIDIA. He leads both NVIDIA MONAI Services and the open-source MONAI Outreach Working Group. With a focus on enhancing annotation, fine-tuning, and training through API Services, Michael is dedicated to transforming how organizations use AI in healthcare. He sees API Services as the essential tool for enterprises to seamlessly adopt cutting-edge solutions.

speakers.name: Andriy Myronenko
speakers.bio: Andriy Myronenko is a senior research scientist at NVIDIA. His research interests include computer vision, deep learning, and medical image analysis. At NVIDIA, his focus is on innovative AI algorithms for medical imaging applications, such as 3D MRI/CT organ and tumor segmentation. He also works on enabling AI technologies to be a part of clinical workflow  through collaborations with hospitals, including the University of California, San Francisco, and Stanford University.

speakers.name: Maximilian Ofir
speakers.bio: Maximilian Ofir is a technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA. His work focuses on AI-enhanced medical imaging applications with Holoscan and Isaac Lab. Max completed his M.S. in robotics, cognition, and intelligence at the Technical University Munich.

speakers.name: Sean Huver
speakers.bio: Sean is a senior manager and principal ML engineer, working to enable the adoption of AI for streaming applications among startups, researchers, and industry partners. Previously, Sean was a startup founder in the computer vision space, as well as a research scientist on numerous DARPA and other Department of Defense projects. Sean holds a Ph.D. in quantum optics and information theory from Louisiana State University, and a bachelor’s degree in physics from UCLA.

speakers.name: Daguang Xu
speakers.bio: Daguang Xu is now a senior research manager at NVIDIA who leads an applied research team in healthcare AI, developing world-class deep learning-based methods to solve challenging problems in the medical domain. His current research interest includes, but is not limited to, medical imaging analysis, foundation models, multi-modality LLM, generative models, and physical AI in healthcare. He has published more than 100 papers on top journals and conferences and has 50-plus granted or in-application patents. Daguang received a bachelor's degree from Tsinghua University, China in 2009. He received M.S.E and Ph.D. degrees from Johns Hopkins University in 2014.

speakers.name: Ahmed Harouni
speakers.bio: Ahmed Harouni is a technical marketing engineer for medical imaging at NVIDIA. Ahmed received his Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from Johns Hopkins University, focusing on magnetic resonance image acquisition and image processing. Recently, he's been using deep learning for image segmentation, registration, disease classification, and localization.

speakers.name: Nigel Nelson
speakers.bio: Nigel Nelson is a machine learning engineer at NVIDIA, focusing on the research and application of AI in healthcare. His research interests include vision-language models, vision-language-action models, and visuomotor robotics policies. He holds a bachelor’s degree in computer science from the Milwaukee School of Engineering.

speakers.name: Walter Simson
speakers.bio: Walter is a senior AI engineer at NVIDIA working on advanced ultrasound perception. He received a Ph.D. in deep learning for medical imaging under the supervision of Professor Nassir Navab at the Technical University of Munich, and most recently worked on advanced ultrasound reconstruction with Professor Jeremy Dahl at Stanford University.

speakers.name: Pengfei Guo
speakers.bio: Pengfei is an applied research scientist who received a Ph.D. from the Department of Computer Science, Johns Hopkins University, where he was member of the Vision and Image Understanding Lab, advised by Vishal M Patel. Pengfei worked closely with the Magnetic Resonance Research Division, Johns Hopkins Medicine, to develop clinical applications.

Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Pre-Trained / Foundation Models

sessions.title: Develop World Foundation Models With NVIDIA Cosmos
sessions.abstract:
Learn how world foundation models generate physics-based data to advance physical AI development. Explore open tools for video processing, tokenization that accelerates training, and frameworks to customize models for autonomous vehicles, robotics, and humanoids. Get help from experts to optimize your physical AI development pipelines with NVIDIA Cosmos.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Joshua Bapst
speakers.bio: Joshua Bapst is product manager at NVIDIA, leading development and ecosystem engagement for NVIDIA Cosmos.

speakers.name: Alexander Schwarz
speakers.bio: Alexander Schwarz is a Robotics Solutions Architect at NVIDIA, where he guides developers building next-generation Robot Brains and World Foundation Models with NVIDIA Cosmos and Isaac. He holds an M.Sc. in Robotics, Cognition, Intelligence from TU Munich and conducted research at Johns Hopkins University, TU Delft, and ETH Zurich.

speakers.name: Pranjali Joshi
speakers.bio: Pranjali Joshi is the product marketing manager for NVIDIA Omniverse, focusing on core technologies and visual generative AI. She holds an M.Sc. in data science and marketing strategy from the University of Maryland and a B.Sc. in electronics engineering. Previously, she worked at Accenture and Hitachi Vantara in software development and technology marketing roles.

speakers.name: Stefanie Manzinger
speakers.bio: Stefanie Manzinger is a senior solutions architect for Automotive and Manufacturing at NVIDIA. She helps customers accelerate physical AI development with NVIDIA Cosmos and drives the adoption of generative AI for industrial applications. Stefanie holds a Ph.D. from the Technical University of Munich, where her doctoral research specialized in decision-making and trajectory planning for autonomous vehicles.

speakers.name: Akul Santhosh
speakers.bio: Akul Santhosh is a solutions architect on NVIDIA’s Robotics SA team, where he supports customers building on the Isaac and Cosmos platforms. Before joining NVIDIA, he earned a master’s degree in computer science from New York University, where he also conducted research in the Robot Perception Lab.

speakers.name: Chhavi Nijhawan
speakers.bio: Chhavi Nijhawan is a senior product marketing manager at NVIDIA on the Metropolis team, focused on advancing Cosmos Reason and physical AI applications. She previously held technical marketing roles across AI, cloud, observability, and networking at Microsoft, VMware, New Relic, and Cisco.

speakers.name: Asawaree Bhide
speakers.bio: Asawaree Bhide is a technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA, working on AI and robotics on the Jetson platform. She did her master’s studies in computer science at Georgia Tech and is interested in solving complex perception tasks for autonomous embodied agents.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: Maximize Memory Bandwidth on Modern GPUs
sessions.abstract:
Struggling to fully utilize GPU memory bandwidth on modern architectures? In this interactive session, NVIDIA engineers will work with you in real time to diagnose and optimize memory-bound kernels. Bring your own code, performance traces, or ideas and get hands-on guidance on loop unrolling, vectorization, prefetching, and asynchronous Tensor Memory Accelerator (TMA) pipelines. Learn to recognize common pitfalls, explore TMA patterns, and apply producer–consumer pipelines to maximize throughput. Walk away with practical, actionable solutions tailored to your workloads.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Matthew Martineau
speakers.bio: Matthew Martineau has been a developer technology engineer since 2018, focusing primarily on the performance of future NVIDIA GPU architectures and the optimization of sparse iterative solvers. He holds a Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Bristol, U.K.

speakers.name: Benedikt Dorschner
speakers.bio: Benedikt Dorschner is a computational scientist and senior development technology engineer at NVIDIA, specializing in numerical methods and high‑performance scientific computing. He earned his Ph.D. in computational fluid dynamics from ETH Zurich and subsequently held positions as a postdoctoral scholar at Caltech and a senior research scientist at ETH.

speakers.name: Kate Clark
speakers.bio: Kate Clark works at the interface of applications, algorithms, and parallel computation. Before joining NVIDIA, Kate was a researcher in radio astronomy signal-processing algorithms at Harvard University and a postdoc at Boston University in Massachusetts, with a focus on multi-grid solver algorithm. She received her Ph.D. from the University of Edinburgh, Scotland, where her doctoral research focused on Monte Carlo algorithms for Lattice QCD.

speakers.name: Francis Tseng
speakers.bio: Francis Tseng is an accelerated computing architect at NVIDIA. Previously, Francis spent two decades at Intel and Microsoft as a hardware architect with a strong emphasis on performance optimizations. He earned a Ph.D. in computer engineering from The University of Texas in 2007.

speakers.name: Sam Mish
speakers.bio: Sam is a developer technology engineer at NVIDIA who earned a Ph.D. at UC Davis in civil engineering, specializing in finite element analysis, then went on to work at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, writing software for differentiable physics simulations. After that, he joined NVIDIA to help accelerate HPC applications. In his spare time, Sam enjoys playing video games and Irish traditional music.

speakers.name: Andrew Briand
speakers.bio: Andrew Briand is a senior developer technology engineer at NVIDIA, where he optimizes GPU kernels for RecSys deep learning workloads. He also accelerates end-to-end training and inference for RecSys and LLMs. He holds a master's degree in Computational Linguistics and a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science from the University of Washington.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

sessions.title: Turbocharge Your LLM Inference: Expert Strategies for Lightning-Fast, Scalable AI Deployment
sessions.abstract:
Explore cutting-edge methods for accelerating large language model (LLM) inference with NVIDIA technologies. This interactive session dives into deploying and scaling models using TensorRT-LLM with PyTorch integration, alongside modern speculation and quantization techniques that boost efficiency. Learn from NVIDIA experts how to fine-tune inference pipelines, optimize GPU performance, and build scalable, high-throughput deployments across advanced systems like the GB200 NVL72.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Nikita Korobov
speakers.bio: Nikita is an AI developer technology engineer who works on performance optimizations of deep learning pipelines for computer vision, large language models, and graphics. Nikita holds a bachelor's degree from Moscow Institute of Physics and Technology and a master's degree from the Technical University of Munich.

speakers.name: Martin Marciniszyn Mehringer
speakers.bio: Martin is a senior DevTech manager at NVIDIA. He holds a master’s degree in computer science from TU Munich and a Ph.D. in discrete mathematics at ETH Zurich. After graduating, Martin spent 15 years in the high-frequency trading industry, focusing on quantitative research and low-latency strategy development. At NVIDIA, he now focuses on optimizing deep neural network training and inference on NVIDIA GPUs for financial services applications.

speakers.name: Haohang Huang
speakers.bio: Haohang Huang is a senior AI developer technology engineer at NVIDIA. He works on accelerating GenAI applications on GPUs, with the focus on computer vision and large language models.

speakers.name: Robin Kobus
speakers.bio: Robin Kobus is a AI developer technology engineer at NVIDIA. His work focuses on optimizing large language model inference in TensorRT-LLM. Robin studied math and computer science at the Johannes Gutenberg University in Mainz, Germany. In his Ph.D. thesis, he investigated the acceleration of bioinformatics algorithms on multi-GPU systems.

speakers.name: Julien Debache
speakers.bio: Julien Debache is a DevTech engineer at NVIDIA, specializing in the optimization of large language model inference across a range of frameworks. Julien holds both a bachelor's and a master's degree in mathematics from the University of Fribourg.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Unlock Peak Performance and Stability: End-to-End Benchmarking of AI Infrastructure
sessions.abstract:
Delivering peak performance and stability in large-scale AI infrastructure is increasingly challenging as workloads and hardware continuously evolve. This requires rapid, end-to-end benchmarking and performance debugging across every layer of the hardware and software stack. Join this interactive session to connect with NVIDIA experts and learn about the benchmarks, tools, and methodologies we use internally at NVIDIA — and make available to you — to measure, optimize, and operate AI infrastructure at scale.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Vasileios Karakasis
speakers.bio: Vasileios is a senior systems software engineer at the Applied Systems Engineering Team at NVIDIA, focusing on systems performance and validation. He is the creator and lead developer of the open-source regression testing and validation framework ReFrame. Before joining NVIDIA, Vasileios served various positions in leading supercomputing centers in Europe, including the Swiss National Supercomputing Center.

speakers.name: Pramod Kumbhar
speakers.bio: Pramod Kumbhar is a senior solutions architect in HPC and AI at NVIDIA. He focuses on resiliency and performance of large-scale training and AI infrastructure. Pramod collaborates with partners building models and deploying AI infrastructure. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he spent 15 years in the HPC and scientific computing domain, developing and optimizing scientific applications and also operation of large-scale supercomputing systems.

speakers.name: Michal Marcinkiewicz
speakers.bio: Michal is an engineering manager at the Deep Learning Algorithms division at NVIDIA. He is responsible for optimizing training performance at small to very large scales and MLPerf Training submissions. Prior to NVIDIA, he worked on deep learning-based solutions for brain tumor segmentation. Michal holds a Ph.D. in condensed matter physics from University of Montpellier, France.

speakers.name: Patrick Atkinson
speakers.bio: Patrick Atkinson specializes in HPC. He focuses on advancing distributed linear algebra for GPU architectures and optimization of the NVIDIA HPC Benchmarks package (including HPL, HPCG, and HPL-MxP). Patrick has assisted with Top500 and Green500 benchmarking runs on several of the world's top supercomputing systems. He holds a Ph.D. in high-performance computing from the University of Bristol.

speakers.name: Sriharsha Niverty
speakers.bio: Sriharsha Niverty focuses on AI infrastructure at NVIDIA, optimizing systems-level performance for large-scale LLM inference and training workloads. Previously, he worked on graphics application performance and architecture exploration, with an emphasis on efficient work scheduling inside the GPU.

speakers.name: Mohak Chadha
speakers.bio: Mohak Chadha is based in Munich, where he focuses on designing solutions for enterprise AI applications. Before this, he was at Firebolt, a unicorn startup, where he managed their cloud infrastructure and worked on improving security. With a strong foundation in distributed systems, Mohak brings expertise in cloud computing, parallel processing, and high-performance computing. He earned a Ph.D. in computer science from TU Munich, graduating summa cum laude.

speakers.name: Giovanni Mascari
speakers.bio: Leading HPC efforts within NVIS, I help organizations build the foundational AI Factories that define the modern era of supercomputing. I thrive on the technical adventure of field-delivering massive-scale infrastructure that pushes the boundaries of what AI and HPC can achieve together.

speakers.name: Anshul Jindal
speakers.bio: Anshul is on NVIDIA's DGX Cloud team, where he specializes in helping customers deploy their workloads at scale. Anshul has a strong background in site reliability engineering and has extensive experience in managing production-grade Kubernetes clusters across various cloud service providers. He has a Ph.D. in computer science from TU Munich, graduating summa cum laude.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.title: The Vision AI Toolbox
sessions.abstract:
Visit us and discuss ways to get started or deepen your understanding of NVIDIA tools that support vision AI applications. Our topic experts are offering one-on-one dialogue on topics related to training, fine-tuning, and deploying a range of vision AI models. Ask about NVIDIA Metropolis, Cosmos Reason VLM, DeepStream, TAO, deploying on edge through cloud, and more.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Khoa Ho
speakers.bio: Khoa Ho focuses on vision AI platforms and making AI more broadly accessible. Before joining his current team, he supported NVIDIA’s system and solution partners as a deep learning solutions architect. And before that, he had different technical and customer-facing roles as part of an NVIDIA’s rotation program, including solutions architecture, product management, and system software engineering.

speakers.name: Sammy Ochoa
speakers.bio: Samuel Ochoa is a technical marketing engineer with the Metropolis team at NVIDIA, focusing on bringing AI to industrial applications. Prior to NVIDIA, he graduated from the University of Texas at Austin with a B.S. and M.S. in computer engineering, with a specialty in machine learning for edge devices.

speakers.name: Chintan Shah
speakers.bio: Chintan is a senior product manager at NVIDIA, focusing on AI products. He manages NVIDIA TAO and vision AI solutions for smart spaces, retail, industrial, and other industry verticals. His focus is on simplifying and democratizing AI for all developers and enterprises.

speakers.name: Adam Ryason
speakers.bio: Adam Ryason is a Product Manager on the Metropolis team and leading the AI Blueprint for Video Search and Summarization. An experienced startup founder and researcher, his background consists of vision-based AI, digital twins, and human-computer interaction. He holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute.

speakers.name: Louise Huang
speakers.bio: Louise is a product manager on the NVIDIA Metropolis team. She leads efforts in computer vision and vision-language models and their fine-tuning. Over the years, she has built a strong technical foundation in computer vision, large-scale data processing, and creative design software. Louise holds her degree in computer science from University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.

speakers.name: Varun Praveen
speakers.bio: Varun Praveen is an engineering manager in the TAO Toolkit team, creating novel deep learning solutions and deploying them to the edge. Before joining NVIDIA in 2017, he obtained his M.S. in electrical engineering from Clemson University. His research interests are digital signal processing, computer vision, and machine learning.

speakers.name: Carlos Garcia-Sierra
speakers.bio: Carlos is a product manager at NVIDIA, focusing on making AI accessible to all. Carlos manages Metropolis AI Workflows, enabling new uses across a diverse set of markets. He's held multiple roles at NVIDIA, including strategic partnerships for the Metropolis business unit, business development for the Automotive BU, and technical marketing. In his free time, Carlos enjoys hiking with his family, mountain biking, reading, and growing organic food.

speakers.name: Nakul Arora
speakers.bio: Nakul is a Director of Product Management at NVIDIA, working on Metropolis - a platform that helps businesses deploy AI that sees and understands the physical world through cameras. He drives product strategy across NVIDIA's three-computer architecture - from synthetic data generation and simulation to training and real-world inference at the edge and cloud - turning physical infrastructure into intelligent, responsive systems.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: A Deep Dive Into Omniverse Libraries for OpenUSD and Physical AI Development
sessions.abstract:
Join this interactive Q&A session to get direct, actionable advice from NVIDIA's core engineering teams on overcoming the critical developer friction points in building large-scale, physically accurate digital twins for robotics, autonomous vehicles, and manufacturing. This is your unique opportunity to discuss your specific use cases and technical challenges directly with the experts who develop and maintain the core Omniverse libraries and technologies for world simulation, including OpenUSD, PhysX, RTX, and more.

Please Note: Mitesh Patel, Ernesto Pacheco, and Jay Axe will be joining us for the Tuesday 10AM session only. Neelakantan Mani and Natasha Anisimova will be joining us for the Tuesday 2PM session only. We encourage you to attend this session if you'd like to hear from a particular expert directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Neelakantan Mani
speakers.bio: Neelakantan is a senior product manager at NVIDIA focused on physics in Omniverse, including PhysX, Clash Detection, USD Physics, and FMI. He has been with NVIDIA for over three years, and before that had over 12 years of development and product experience in high fidelity simulations, CAD, and computer-aided engineering with Siemens. Neelakantan has a masters in engineering from Arizona State University and an M.B.A. from Michigan Ross.

speakers.name: Brian Harrison
speakers.bio: Brian Harrison is senior director of software product management of digital twins for Omniverse. He’s been with NVIDIA for over 10 years, working in a variety of areas involving the manufacturing market, as well as tech marketing, in addition to his work with the Omniverse team. Previously, Brian worked for a number of software companies in the CAD arena: CADKEY, PTC, and SOLIDWORKS.

speakers.name: Stephanie Rubenstein
speakers.bio: Stephanie Rubenstein leads product marketing for Omniverse core libraries and Open USD at NVIDIA, responsible for driving developer adoption and enablement for industrial and physical AI workflows. She has over a decade of experience helping both early-stage startups and global tech leaders successfully build and launch innovative products across spatial computing, cloud, and AI.

speakers.name: Mitesh Patel
speakers.bio: Mitesh Patel is a product marketing manager at NVIDIA, where he works with researchers in higher education to execute their ideas using NVIDIA SDKs and platforms. Before joining NVIDIA, he was a senior research scientist at Fuji Xerox Palo Alto Laboratory. Mitesh received his Ph.D. in robotics from the Center of Autonomous Systems at the University of Technology Sydney, Australia in 2014.

speakers.name: Ashley Goldstein
speakers.bio: Ashley Goldstein develops content that guides developers through integrating NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD into their workflows. With a colorful background in areas such as academia, fine art, and 3D workflows, Ashley reaches far across the community in media & entertainment; architecture, engineering, construction & operations; and education. She enjoys popping into the NVIDIA Omniverse livestreams and community forums, where her dogs make an occasional cameo.

speakers.name: Justine Lin
speakers.bio: Justine Lin focuses on Omniverse and cloud developer workflows. Prior to this, she held various roles across the industry, such as software engineer, solutions engineer, and technical program manager. She holds a B.S. in computer science from University of California, San Diego.

speakers.name: Ernesto Pacheco
speakers.bio:
Ernesto is a Senior Product Manager at NVIDIA, where he focuses on AI Factories. With more than twenty-five years of experience in the AECO industry, he has played a key role in shaping data ingestion pipelines and advancing immersive, real-time technologies for design collaboration and visualization.
At NVIDIA, Ernesto helps define industry workflows, tooling, infrastructure, and processes to deliver a high-performance, real-time platform powered by Omniverse. As part of the Customer Success team, he supports OpenUSD workflows for AI Factories, bridging cutting-edge simulation and visualization with intelligent, scalable solutions.

speakers.name: Jay Axe
speakers.bio: Jay is a technical product manager at NVIDIA focusing on Omniverse Virtual Factories. He found his way to NVIDIA as a freelance computer-generated lighting/simulation consultant about 10 years ago. At that time, he created photorealistic rendering for Endeavor, NVIDIA's new headquarters building in Santa Clara, California. Day to day at NVIDIA, Jay demonstrates architecture, engineering, & construction workflows for real-time ray tracing and photorealistic path-tracing.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Next-Gen Digital Health Agents for Language, Speech, and Analytics
sessions.abstract:
The future of digital health is agentic. Join NVIDIA experts to discuss how AI agents are transforming the ecosystem by bridging the gap between perception and reasoning. Discuss the implementation of advanced pipelines that utilize conversational AI and speech for patient interaction, computer vision for diagnostics, and deep analytics for decision support. Whether you are a researcher or an enterprise developer, engage with our team to understand how NVIDIA NIM, blueprints, and SDKs can help you deploy robust, multi-modal agents that drive real-world clinical, research, revenue cycle, and administrative outcomes.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Brad Genereaux
speakers.bio: Brad Genereaux is the global lead for healthcare alliances at NVIDIA, focused on developer relations in digital health and medical imaging. With over 22 years in healthcare IT, he advocates for integrating AI into clinical practice at enterprise scale. Brad is also a leader in healthcare interoperability, contributing to standards like HL7, FHIR, DICOM, and IHE to help make healthcare a seamless experience for all.

speakers.name: Jinhan Wang
speakers.bio: Jinhan Wang is an Engineer at NVIDIA focusing on development of channel robust Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR). He specializes in building reliable and robust ASR systems across various real-word scenarios and deliver cutting-edged product to customers.

speakers.name: Cedric Steenbeke
speakers.bio: Cedric leads NVIDIA HCLS (Health Care and Life Sciences) Startups Developer Relations and works with key startups across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. Driving technology adoption and implementation is at the core of what Cedric does.

speakers.name: Chelsea Sumner
speakers.bio:
Chelsea is a healthcare innovator and the North and Latin American and Africa healthcare AI startups lead at NVIDIA. With a background in the pharmaceutical industry and clinical settings, she manages relationships for over 1,500 healthcare startups. Her passion for healthcare and technology led to her current position, where she strives to democratize AI and advance digital health.
 
Chelsea is committed to uplifting underrepresented communities. She founded the nonprofit Dose of Melanin, Inc., connecting thousands of Black pharmacists and student pharmacists. Her multifaceted background, dedication to healthcare innovation, and efforts in community service make her an influential figure.

speakers.name: Arun Venkatesan
speakers.bio: Arun is a group product manager at NVIDIA and leads product management for generative AI Inference. Prior to this, Arun was an engineering lead at Samsung Research America and has broad experience building conversational AI applications, and other enterprise and consumer software. Arun graduated with an M.B.A. from the Yale School of Management and a master’s degree in electrical and computer engineering from the University of Maryland, College Park.

speakers.name: Reza Negahdar
speakers.bio: Reza Negahdar is a senior solutions architect at NVIDIA, focused on transforming digital health through agentic AI. He helps independent software vendors leverage the NVIDIA NeMo framework and open models like Nemotron to build scalable and intelligent clinical systems. Previously, as a director and senior principal AI scientist at Genentech, Reza led clinical AI strategies and the development of multi-modal foundation models. His expertise extends to his time at IBM Research, where he built clinical AI assistants. Reza holds a Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from the University of Louisville and completed a postdoctoral fellowship at the Stanford School of Medicine.

speakers.name: Abood Quraini
speakers.bio: Abood Al-Quraini is a technical marketing engineering manager for healthcare AI at NVIDIA. He leads a team focused on reference application development and developer integrations of NVIDIA AI NIMs and Blueprints for Digital Health. Abood holds a master’s degree in electrical engineering from McGill University and an M.B.A. from Santa Clara University.

speakers.name: Ben Randoing
speakers.bio: Ben Randoing works on applied AI at NVIDIA, where he contributes to conversational AI systems, real-time multi-modal agents, and emerging digital health technologies. Prior to NVIDIA, he contributed to discovery-stage research in Apple’s Health Technologies group, exploring future consumer health tech capabilities. He has also conducted research in Stanford’s Neuromuscular Biomechanics Laboratory, studying human movement and computational modeling. Ben holds a bachelor’s degree in biomedical engineering from Duke University and a master’s degree in artificial intelligence from Stanford University.

speakers.name: Jin Li
speakers.bio: Jin Li is a healthcare technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA, working on product solutions in digital health and more. She focuses on utilizing NVIDIA Blueprints and NVIDIA NIM microservices in creating generative AI-enabled sample applications, demos, tutorials, and reference workflows to address real-world use cases. She holds an M.S. in machine learning from Carnegie Mellon University, and a bachelor's degree in electrical engineering with a minor in computer science from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.

speakers.name: Ignacio Sarasua
speakers.bio: Ignacio joined NVIDIA in 2022, where he leads the NVIDIA AI Technology Center for Iberia, supporting local research groups to accelerate their work using NVIDIA’s solutions. He is also involved in initiatives around AI applications for mental health. Ignacio completed a Ph.D. at Ludwig Maximilian University of Munich, focusing on deep learning for Alzheimer’s detection, and holds engineering degrees from UPC Barcelona and TU Munich.

speakers.name: Jiahui Guan
speakers.bio: Jiahui Guan is a solution architect at NVIDIA, collaborating closely with healthcare providers and payers. She aids in developing and deploying AI using NVIDIA hardware and tools. Her recent MONAI projects include a continuous learning platform at the Mayo Clinic, AI-assisted pathology annotation at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, and NVFlare deployment in top academic medical centers.

speakers.name: Myungjong Kim
speakers.bio: Myungjong Kim is an Engineering Manager at NVIDIA specializing in the development of Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) and Speech Translation models. He focuses on building and customizing robust, multilingual models designed to perform reliably across diverse, real-world customer applications.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Quantum Computing and AI
sessions.abstract:
Join us to discuss how AI is driving key breakthroughs in quantum computing. From discovering new quantum algorithms with generative AI and building AI-based quantum error correction decoders to enabling the low-latency integration of QPUs and AI supercomputing with NVQLink — whatever your interest in quantum computing, we'll show you how AI can accelerate your work. We’ll answer your questions about realizing these capabilities by architecting hybrid workflows with CUDA-Q, and show you how to leverage CUDA-Q Academic to prepare the next generation of experts to drive this convergence.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Jin-Sung Kim
speakers.bio: Jin-Sung Kim is a developer relations manager for quantum computing at NVIDIA who leads strategic partnerships and alliances. Prior to NVIDIA, he was a research staff member at IBM Quantum. Jin-Sung holds a Ph.D. in electrical engineering and materials science from Princeton University, and is based out of San Francisco.

speakers.name: Monica Van-Dieren
speakers.bio: Monica leads the CUDA-Q Academic initiative at NVIDIA. Previously, she created quantum training materials for IBM. She holds a Ph.D. in mathematical sciences from Carnegie Mellon University. Monica has over 20 years of academic experience at institutions like Stanford, the University of Michigan, and Robert Morris University. She has taught mathematics, launched an interdisciplinary honors program, organized STEM outreach activities, and conducted both theoretical research in logic and pedagogical research in educational technology. Monica is passionate about bridging the gap between classical and quantum computing, enabling practical solutions for complex real-world problems.

speakers.name: Mark Wolf
speakers.bio: Mark Wolf serves as a technical marketing engineer on NVIDIA's quantum computing team, where he focuses on bridging the gap between advanced research and user education. Mark works closely with researchers at NVIDIA and across the quantum ecosystem to develop CUDA-Q Academic content. Previously, Mark worked at Quantinuum, and holds a Ph.D. in physical chemistry from the Center for Computational Quantum Chemistry at the University of Georgia.

speakers.name: Justin Lietz
speakers.bio: Justin Lietz is a senior quantum computing software architect on the NVIDIA HPC and Quantum Computing team. He is focused on quantum error correction and integrating quantum computers with classical HPC systems. Prior to NVIDIA, Justin was on the research staff at the National Center for Computational Sciences at Oak Ridge National Laboratory. Justin received his Ph.D. in computational nuclear physics in 2019 from Michigan State University.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

sessions.title: How to Run and Optimize Your Workloads on the NVIDIA Grace and Vera CPUs
sessions.abstract:
Join us to explore how NVIDIA's latest generation of ARM-based CPUs can boost data center performance and energy efficiency. We'll help you optimize your applications for the current NVIDIA Grace architecture while providing insights into NVIDIA Vera CPU, the next-generation processor based on a custom NVIDIA core — available in the second half 2026. Learn practical strategies to enable your code to run efficiently on these CPUs and understand how to take advantage of the next architectural improvements. We welcome participants interested in evaluating and deploying Grace today, as well as those considering adopting Vera in the future. You should have a foundational understanding of CPU programming. Bring your application-specific and general questions — we're here to help you prepare for both current and future generations of NVIDIA's data center processors.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Mathias Wagner
speakers.bio: Mathias Wagner is a member of the European developer technology team at NVIDIA, where he works on high performance computing and scientific applications. Before joining NVIDIA, he worked as a postdoc in high-energy physics in Europe and the United States, focusing on lattice quantum chromodynamics simulations using GPUs. Mathias holds a Ph.D. in theoretical physics from Darmstadt University of Technology.

speakers.name: Holly Wilper
speakers.bio: Holly Wilper's team develops tools that help you be more efficient and effective at harnessing the power of GPUs. For the last 24 years, she's worked on a wide variety of developer tools focused on parallel computing and HPC environments including compilers, profilers, and correctness tools. Holly is a graduate of the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign.

speakers.name: Yuzhong Wen
speakers.bio: Yuzhong Wen is a senior developer technology engineer at NVIDIA. Since joining the DevTech team in 2024, he primarily works on optimizing software for NVIDIA CPU platforms, including optimization operating system kernels, concurrent algorithms, database workloads, and video transcoding. He holds a master of science degree in computer science from Virginia Tech and has nine years of experience of performance-critical system software on ARM system-on-chips.

speakers.name: Matthias Langer
speakers.bio: Matthias Langer is a Senior AI & Technology Engineer at NVIDIA based in Melbourne. His work primarily focuses on workload distribution, algorithm parallelization, and performance optimization to help customers maximize the performance of their HPC applications on NVIDIA hardware. Matthias holds a Ph.D. in Artificial Intelligence from La Trobe University in Melbourne and has over 15 years of experience as a software engineer and machine learning researcher.

speakers.name: Lukas Alt
speakers.bio: Lukas Alt is a developer technology engineer who joined NVIDIA in 2024 and has been working on performance optimization of data center applications and libraries for NVIDIA’s data center CPU platforms, with a strong focus on compression and serialization frameworks. Lukas holds a master’s degree in computer science from RWTH Aachen University.

speakers.name: Hans Mortensen
speakers.bio: Hans Mortensen is a senior solutions architect at NVIDIA, where he focuses on pioneering NVIDIA Grace solutions for the data center. With a lifetime of experience, Hans masterfully balances the compute, storage, and networking components of HPC solutions to seamlessly align with software requirements. Hans has extensive experience in migrating code to new CPU architectures including MIPS, Itanium, Xeon, and NVIDIA Grace.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

sessions.title: Master Immersive Real-Time Visual Process Understanding and Guidance
sessions.abstract:
Extended reality (XR) applications powered by agentic AI will revolutionize how frontline workers do their jobs. Join NVIDIA experts for a deep-dive discussion on how to design and train agentic AI systems for enterprise use cases. In this session, we'll share strategies and best practices for: leveraging the Nemo Agent Toolkit to build powerful, scalable, and optimized agents for XR use cases, optimizing agentic AI to accurately interpret and guide complex workflows, and deploying AI to the edge using NVIDIA Cosmos for low-latency feedback.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Greg Barbone
speakers.bio: Greg Barbone leads extended reality (XR) product and partner management at NVIDIA, with a mission of helping software developers and enterprise partners harness NVIDIA’s graphics and AI technologies to build cutting-edge XR solutions that deliver real value to end users. With a background in immersive media and spatial computing, Greg has worked across the XR ecosystem to bring innovative tools and platforms to market.

speakers.name: Chao-Yeh Chen
speakers.bio: Chao-Yeh Chen is a software engineer at NVIDIA XR, specializing in the convergence of artificial intelligence and extended reality (XR). Building on his Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Texas at Austin, he develops cutting-edge applications designed to push the boundaries of immersive experiences. His current work focuses on creating sophisticated agentic AI systems, often powered by large language models and vision language models, for next-generation XR environments.

speakers.name: Tiffany Chen
speakers.bio: Tiffany Chen is a distinguished software engineer and manager at NVIDIA, leading AI and robotics for spatial computing. Her team advances robot teleoperation for physical AI, enabling high-fidelity imitation learning and optimized human-machine interaction.
