Tiffany also initiated NVIDIA’s XR-AI efforts, bridging foundation models with spatial devices via agentic workflows to transform industrial productivity. Previously, she led multi-modal perception and map-robust perception at Waymo, advancing autonomous vehicle safety. She holds a Ph.D. from MIT's Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory, specializing in machine learning systems.

speakers.name: Katherine Huang
speakers.bio: Katherine Huang is a solutions architect who works with customers to design agentic AI workflows for specific enterprise use cases. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Katherine was a researcher working at the intersection of wearable technology and human-computer interaction. Her work examined how interaction interface design drives adoption of augmented reality, and how agentic AI can be leveraged as such an interface.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)

sessions.title: IGX and Jetson: Autonomous Robots and Physical AI Applications
sessions.abstract:
NVIDIA IGX and Jetson are the world's leading computing platform for AI at the edge. It offers high-performance compute and advanced NVIDIA AI software stack, as well as an extensive ecosystem to help you create and take products to market. Join with NVIDIA engineers and experts to ask your questions and join discussion on topics ranging from hardware design to system software, from the latest AI models to application frameworks like Metropolis, Isaac, and Holoscan.

Please Note: Martin Doe, Dipen Patel, Anubhav Rai, Kaustubh Purandare, Ilies Chergui, Shashank Maheshwari, Aayush Pathak, Kiril Stoynov, Alexander Yu, Michael Silverman, Kenneth MacLean, Thejas Shivashankar, and Neel Patel, will be joining us for the Tuesday 3PM session only. Phil Lawrence, Amulya Yarlagadda, Mahdi Azizian, Suhas Hariharapura Sheshadri, David Barkin, Bernie Yip, Chris Bareng, Dac Tran, Gian Luca Storti, and Chitoku Yato will be joining us for the Thursday 2PM session only. We encourage you to attend the corresponding session if you'd like to hear from a particular expert directly.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Shashank Maheshwari
speakers.bio: Shashank Maheshwari is the product manager for Jetson and IGX software at NVIDIA. He holds an MBA from Duke University, The Fuqua School of Business, and a B.E. in electronics from BITS Pilani.

speakers.name: Suhas Hariharapura Sheshadri
speakers.bio: Suhas Sheshadri focuses on Jetson and Isaac robotics software. He previously worked with the autonomous driving team at NVIDIA, optimizing system software for the NVIDIA DRIVE platform. In his free time, Suhas likes to read books on quantum physics and game theory.

speakers.name: Aayush Pathak
speakers.bio: Aayush Pathak is a hardware product manager at NVIDIA specializing in embedded edge and autonomous machines. He has worked extensively in the semiconductor industry, designing supercomputer system-on-chips and advancing low‑power, energy‑efficient hardware. He holds a master’s degree in electrical engineering from the University of Southern California and an M.B.A. from the University of Chicago.

speakers.name: Chitoku Yato
speakers.bio: Chitoku Yato is senior technical product marketing manager of the NVIDIA Jetson Edge AI platform, where he's responsible for ensuring the best developer experience on the platform. He also works closely with the vibrant NVIDIA developer community to help evangelize the use of pre-trained AI models, developer SDKs, and support for cloud-native technologies on Jetson to help customers build, deploy, and manage AI-based autonomous machines.

speakers.name: Thejas Shivashankar
speakers.bio: Thejas Boggaram Shivashankar is the Jetson partner ecosystem manager at NVIDIA, responsible for expanding and enabling the collaborative Jetson partner ecosystem. Prior to NVIDIA, Thejas worked as the business development manager for an embedded product design house. He also co-founded a cloud-based hiring platform to provide job opportunities for students in India. Outside of work, he loves playing basketball, discovering new places to eat, meeting new people, and building communities.

speakers.name: Neel Patel
speakers.bio: Neel Patel is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, working on the Jetson embedded platform and the IGX platform. He's responsible for helping Jetson and IGX customers with their board bring-up and software solutions using NVIDIA's stack. Neel holds a master's degree in computer engineering from San Jose State University. He's an avid embedded systems enthusiast and interested in creating solutions that change the world.

speakers.name: Winnie Hsu
speakers.bio: To be updated

speakers.name: Martin Doe
speakers.bio: Martin Doe is a Senior Manager of Technical Program Management at NVIDIA, focusing on JetPack and IGX-OS SW support for NVIDIA Edge Platforms, which include Jetson and IGX HW products.

speakers.name: Phil Lawrence
speakers.bio: Phil Lawrence is a senior program manager focused on edge AI platforms. He operates at the intersection of go-to-market efforts with Jetson and IGX ecosystem partners and customers, translating real-world needs into concrete product improvements. Phil helps build the process and collaboration structures that keep complex edge programs on track, bridging product, engineering, marketing, and other teams. A former Jetson program manager, he brings deep experience in BSP and OS architecture, genuine love for developers, and a moderate appetite for technical marketing.

speakers.name: Amulya Yarlagadda
speakers.bio: Amulya Yarlagadda is a Manager of Embedded Platform Software focusing on Board Support Package (BSP) releases for Jetpack and IGX-OS releases for Jetson and IGX HW products.

speakers.name: Dipen Patel
speakers.bio: Managing the platform and firmware team which overseas the platform bringup, board support package development for Jetson, IGX and DGX Spak, Realtime kernel enablement, safety extension package ccplex enablement and firmware development.

speakers.name: Anubhav Rai
speakers.bio: Anubhav works for the camera L4T team at NVIDIA with a 5+ year experience in this team. He primarily works in development of camera architecture, associated hw/ sw interaction and application programming interfaces.

speakers.name: Kaustubh Purandare
speakers.bio: Kaustubh Purandare is Senior Director of Software Engineering – Metropolis, DeepStream, VSS, NIMS at NVIDIA.

speakers.name: Mahdi Azizian
speakers.bio: Mahdi Azizian is a senior director of engineering at NVIDIA, focusing on development of the Holoscan Platform, Holoscan for Medical, ISAAC for Healthcare, and MONAI. Mahdi holds a Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from Western University in Canada. Prior to joining NVIDIA in 2021, he spent almost a decade at Intuitive, bringing image guidance and intelligence to surgical robots. Mahdi’s technical interests involve intelligent medical devices, medical imaging, medical robotics, and real-time systems.

speakers.name: Ilies Chergui
speakers.bio:
Ilies Chergui is a Senior System Software Engineer at NVIDIA and a member of the Holoscan team, working on system software for edge AI platforms.
He is also an open-source maintainer for the OpenEmbedded for Tegra project, contributing to the Tegra meta layer and focusing on Linux BSP development and platform security.

speakers.name: David Barkin
speakers.bio: David Barkin is the Director of Hardware Product Management for NVIDIA’s Jetson and IGX Edge AI platforms. Prior to NVIDIA, David led product management for a portfolio of medical devices at Alphabet’s Verily, and earlier held design, product, and strategy leadership positions at National Semiconductor and Texas Instruments.

speakers.name: Kiril Stoynov
speakers.bio: Kiril is an Engineering Program Manager within the Systems Products Team at NVIDIA. He leads hardware development programs for the Jetson AGX and IGX platform families. Because Jetson and IGX are horizontal, multi-market platforms, Kiril partners closely with cross-functional teams across NVIDIA, as well as with external ecosystem and OEM partners. His work supports deployments across robotics, healthcare and medtech, smart factories, and Physical AI infrastructure, with a focus on delivering scalable, production-ready platforms that accelerate customers from prototype to high-volume deployment.

speakers.name: Alexander Yu
speakers.bio: Alexander Yu is the Go-To-Market Product Manager for Jetson and IGX platform at NVIDIA, leading cross‑functional go‑to‑market planning and execution. He has extensive background in the semiconductor industry. He holds a Master’s Degree in Computer Engineering from University of Southern California.

speakers.name: Chris Bareng
speakers.bio: Chris Bareng is a Senior Applications Engineer at NVIDIA with over 20 years of experience supporting embedded and automotive customers and partners. He has worked on NVIDIA’s Tegra, Jetson, DRIVE, and automotive GPU products, including Nintendo's Switch and Switch 2 projects. Chris focuses on design support, platform bring-up, customer enablement, and technical documentation. He holds a bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences from the University of California, Berkeley.

speakers.name: Dac Tran
speakers.bio: Dac Tran is a senior hardware and platform engineer at NVIDIA supporting embedded and automotive solutions. His work spans high‑speed I/O, signal integrity, power/thermal analysis, board-level design, and system deployment for NVIDIA's Jetson/IGX/DRIVE platforms. Dac focuses on helping customers translate advanced SoC capabilities into practical, real-world applications.

speakers.name: Michael Silverman
speakers.bio: With over a decade of experience in robotics and autonomous vehicles, Michael Silverman is a systems and mechatronics engineer working across autonomous drones, humanoid robots, subsea vehicles, space systems, industrial automation, and augmented reality. His work centers on robust mechanical design, tight integration of hardware with sensing and control, and hands‑on validation of high‑reliability platforms. Michael holds both bachelor’s and master’s degrees in mechanical engineering from Embry‑Riddle Aeronautical University.

speakers.name: Kenneth MacLean
speakers.bio: The original creator of NVIDIA’s Nova Carter robot, Ken brings a wealth of experience working with and designing robots from the ground up. His expertise spans the entire development lifecycle—from mechanical and electrical design to motor controls, sensors and software integration. Ken has dual bachelor's degrees in Physics and Mechanical Engineering, a Master’s from UBC, and scars from years in the trenches at startups.

speakers.name: Gian Luca Storti
speakers.bio: Gian Luca Storti is a systems engineer with over 15 years of international experience designing and deploying complex systems. Specializing in autonomous vehicles and industrial robotics, his work focuses on critical systems integration, time synchronization, and  verification and validation (V&V). Gian Luca earned his PhD in Electronics Engineering from Sapienza University of Rome.

speakers.name: Shobek Attupurath
speakers.bio: Shobek is a Senior System Software engineer focused on wired/wireless connectivity for Jetson, IGX and DGX Spark platforms.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod E (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.title: Best Practices in Building Your AI Factory: Connect With NVIDIA MLOps and AIOps Experts
sessions.abstract:
As organizations shift from traditional data center to AI factories, building a full-stack AI infrastructure can be challenging. Connect with NVIDIA experts on how to quickly deploy your AI factories, and learn best practices for your ML/AI workflows.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Nic Borensztein
speakers.bio: Nic Borensztein is a generative AI-focused software engineer, leader, and serial entrepreneur based in the San Francisco Bay Area. He is the lead solution architect for Astra, NVIDIA's internal enterprise AI factory. Previously, he was the founder/CTO of CrowdAI, a no-code computer-vision platform.

speakers.name: Erik Bohnhorst
speakers.bio: Erik Bohnhorst is a Director of Product Management at NVIDIA, specialized in building infrastructure software that powers modern AI. Erik has been at NVIDIA since 2014, and currently focuses on making AI workloads run effortlessly and efficiently on Kubernetes and virtualized platforms.

speakers.name: Nik Spirin
speakers.bio: Nik is a hands-on technology leader and Ph.D. with over 15 years of experience applying AI/ML to drive business results as a startup founder, executive, consultant, and advisor. He's currently a director at NVIDIA working on generative AI and LLMOps Platform. In the past, he worked with Fortune 500 corporations and venture capital-funded startups on AI strategies, products, and platforms, and built high-performance AI organizations up from zero.

speakers.name: Michael Balint
speakers.bio: Michael is focused on cluster management, orchestration, and scheduling of NVIDIA DGX servers. Previously, Michael was a White House Presidential Innovation Fellow, where he brought his technical expertise to projects like then-Vice President Biden's Cancer Moonshot and Code.gov.

speakers.name: William Benton
speakers.bio: William works on data science product architecture at NVIDIA. He's passionate about making it easier for machine learning practitioners to benefit from advanced infrastructure and making it possible for organizations to manage ML systems. Will has also conducted research and development related to static program analysis, language runtimes, cluster configuration management, and music technology.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.title: Advance Physical AI Safety With NVIDIA Halos
sessions.abstract:
Discover how NVIDIA’s Halos safety tools, methods, and principles, from chip to cloud, accelerate safe physical AI development. Understand how safety innovation and standards compliance converge to enable the next generation of autonomous vehicles and robotics.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Khoa Ho
speakers.bio: Khoa Ho focuses on vision AI platforms and making AI more broadly accessible. Before joining his current team, he supported NVIDIA’s system and solution partners as a deep learning solutions architect. And before that, he had different technical and customer-facing roles as part of an NVIDIA’s rotation program, including solutions architecture, product management, and system software engineering.

speakers.name: Suhas Hariharapura Sheshadri
speakers.bio: Suhas Sheshadri focuses on Jetson and Isaac robotics software. He previously worked with the autonomous driving team at NVIDIA, optimizing system software for the NVIDIA DRIVE platform. In his free time, Suhas likes to read books on quantum physics and game theory.

speakers.name: Riccardo Mariani
speakers.bio: Riccardo Mariani is the vice president of industry safety at NVIDIA and leads the NVIDIA Halos initiative. He's responsible for developing cohesive safety strategies and cross-segment safety processes, architecture, and products that can be leveraged across NVIDIA’s artificial intelligence-based hardware and software platforms.

speakers.name: Ekram Mukbil
speakers.bio:
Ekram leads the growth and engagement of the automotive developer community worldwide. She oversees strategic technical partnerships and drives initiatives to educate and empower partners on NVIDIA’s latest automotive and AI technologies.

Ekram has over 20 years of experience in the semiconductor industry that includes ASIC design, business process improvement, product engineering, technical program management, and business development. Prior to joining NVIDIA in 2013, she worked at AMD. Before that, Ekram worked for Hewlett-Packard and Nortel Networks. 

Ekram holds an M.B.A. from Haas School of Business at UC Berkeley and a B.S. in computer engineering from the University of Ottawa.

speakers.name: Apoorva Sharma
speakers.bio: Apoorva is a research scientist in the Autonomous Vehicle Research Group at NVIDIA. His research interests focus on both building data-driven models that can be safely and reliably deployed and exploring how machine learning models can aid safety assurance of complex autonomous systems. Apoorva received his Ph.D. and M.S. in aeronautics and astronautics from Stanford University in 2022.

speakers.name: Viola Wu
speakers.bio: Viola Wu is a senior product marketing manager at NVIDIA, handling the positioning, messaging, and branding of the end-to-end platform for autonomous vehicle development and NVIDIA Halos initiative. She produces and hosts the NVIDIA DRIVE Labs video series. Viola holds engineering degrees from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute and University College London, with a background in research, engineering, consulting, and product development. She was named one of Business Insider’s 2022 Top 31 Self-Driving Industry Power Players.

speakers.name: Sarah Todd
speakers.bio: Sarah Todd is a product marketing manager focused on the go-to-market strategy for NVIDIA Metropolis solutions, promoting application integration across industries. Previously, Sarah worked as a marketing communications manager for the autonomous machines team supporting NVIDIA products like Jetson, Isaac, DeepStream, TAO Toolkit, and others.

speakers.name: Akshay Sharan
speakers.bio: Akshay Sharan works at the intersection of high-reliability AI systems and Security. At NVIDIA, Akshay has spent the past decade building ‘chip-to-cloud’ security architectures for cyber-physical AI systems. Leveraging his experience in both traditional embedded security and adversarial Al/ML security, Akshay helps ensure the world’s most complex, safety-relevant systems are trustworthy and robust against adversaries.

speakers.name: Jack Zhang
speakers.bio: Jack Zhang focuses on robotics perception, sensors, and functional safety. He serves as the functional safety manager for NVIDIA Halos Outside In Safety and participates in different physical AI safety standardization groups. Previously he worked on many NVIDIA projects from DNN development, to different robotic platforms, to the AV Hyperion platform. Jack received his Ph.D. in Materials Science from UCSB in 2016.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

sessions.title: The Developer’s Roadmap to Physical AI: Bridging Digital Models and Real-World Systems
sessions.abstract:
The era of generative AI has mastered digital content, but the next frontier lies in the physical world. For developers comfortable with LLMs and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), the leap into physical AI—robotics, autonomous systems, and edge computing—can feel like starting from zero. This session will bridge that gap. We will deconstruct physical AI workflows, showing you how to translate digital reasoning into physical action. We'll explore how to move beyond text-based inputs to incorporate multi-modal sensing and spatial intelligence, turning static models into interactive, real-world agents.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Stephanie Rubenstein
speakers.bio: Stephanie Rubenstein leads product marketing for Omniverse core libraries and Open USD at NVIDIA, responsible for driving developer adoption and enablement for industrial and physical AI workflows. She has over a decade of experience helping both early-stage startups and global tech leaders successfully build and launch innovative products across spatial computing, cloud, and AI.

speakers.name: Cobus Bothma
speakers.bio: Cobus Bothma works at NVIDIA on Omniverse, focused on the architecture, engineering, construction, & operations industries. With a background in product and architectural design, he specializes in design computation, simulation, and visualization. Prior to NVIDIA, he was the director of applied research at Kohn Pedersen Fox, a global architectural firm. His work included the design of large architectural projects, developing design functions, and progressing technology adoption for advancing the design and construction process.

speakers.name: Ashley Goldstein
speakers.bio: Ashley Goldstein develops content that guides developers through integrating NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD into their workflows. With a colorful background in areas such as academia, fine art, and 3D workflows, Ashley reaches far across the community in media & entertainment; architecture, engineering, construction & operations; and education. She enjoys popping into the NVIDIA Omniverse livestreams and community forums, where her dogs make an occasional cameo.

speakers.name: Brian Harrison
speakers.bio: Brian Harrison is senior director of software product management of digital twins for Omniverse. He’s been with NVIDIA for over 10 years, working in a variety of areas involving the manufacturing market, as well as tech marketing, in addition to his work with the Omniverse team. Previously, Brian worked for a number of software companies in the CAD arena: CADKEY, PTC, and SOLIDWORKS.

speakers.name: Chhavi Nijhawan
speakers.bio: Chhavi Nijhawan is a senior product marketing manager at NVIDIA on the Metropolis team, focused on advancing Cosmos Reason and physical AI applications. She previously held technical marketing roles across AI, cloud, observability, and networking at Microsoft, VMware, New Relic, and Cisco.

speakers.name: Mitesh Patel
speakers.bio: Mitesh Patel is a product marketing manager at NVIDIA, where he works with researchers in higher education to execute their ideas using NVIDIA SDKs and platforms. Before joining NVIDIA, he was a senior research scientist at Fuji Xerox Palo Alto Laboratory. Mitesh received his Ph.D. in robotics from the Center of Autonomous Systems at the University of Technology Sydney, Australia in 2014.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Computational Imaging

sessions.title: CUDA on Windows and WSL
sessions.abstract:
Join us for a CWE session with the CUDA Windows and WSL engineering group to ask any questions you might have about performance, best practices, and how to leverage some of our new features and new platforms. Bring your questions/requests for customized answers on any topics: WSL containers, tuning programs for MCDM/WDDM, graphics interoperability (DirectX, Vulkan, OpenGL) and AI in games—even some of the most niche memory allocation and optimizations problems, and much more.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Jalpa Patel
speakers.bio: Jalpa Patel is a senior systems software engineer at NNVIDIA who works in the CUDA Windows Driver team, optimizing graphics interoperability. Previously, she worked at Microsoft, enabling the Microsoft Compute Driver Model on NPUs for AI-powered experiences. She holds a master's degree in computer science from the University of Southern California.

speakers.name: Raphael Boissel
speakers.bio: Raphael Boissel leads CUDA engineering for the Windows platform. He holds a master's degree in computer science from the French engineering school EPITA.

speakers.name: Mike Delorme
speakers.bio: Mike's a senior system software engineer and engineering manager skilled in C, C++, CUDA, OpenCL, and system software. He obtained his master of applied science degree, focused in computer engineering, from the University of Toronto.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

sessions.title: Accelerate Data Compression and Decompression on GPUs with nvCOMP
sessions.abstract:
Join NVIDIA experts to discuss GPU-accelerated compression and decompression with nvCOMP and dedicated hardware decompress engines for data analytics, genomics, deep learning, and more. We’ll walk through best practices for offloading decompression to Blackwell’s dedicated engine, show how the latest nvCOMP APIs (including Python APIs) simplify integration into existing CPU and GPU pipelines, and talk through real-world performance tuning patterns.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Balazs Nagy
speakers.bio:
Balazs is a senior software engineer working on lossless and lossy data compression algorithms. He has been working with GPUs (CUDA specifically) for over six years.

He started in robotics with visual-inertial odometry/SLAM for airborne and free-moving devices, then transitioned toward highly accurate 3D volume reconstruction (CBCT) in the domain of radiotherapy. Lately, he has been working on data compression on GPUs.

Balazs is trying to bring his enthusiasm for hardware and low-level programming to address present challenges.

speakers.name: Naveen Himthani
speakers.bio: Naveen Himthani is a senior CUDA math library engineer at NVIDIA who primarily focuses on new features and performance improvement of image and signal processing (NPP) and image codecs (nvJPEG, nvJPEG2000, nvImgcodec and nvTiff). Naveen holds a Ph.D. in computational science and engineering from the University of Texas at Austin.

speakers.name: Makan Taghavi Dilamani
speakers.bio: Makan focuses on image processing and data compression technologies at NVIDIA for high performance computing applications. Previously, as a senior product manager at Qualcomm, Makan led the Low Power AI and Audio/Voice AI software roadmap, driving over 100 design wins and helping establishing Qualcomm's Low Power AI platform as one of the leaders in AI on the edge.

speakers.name: Eric Schmidt
speakers.bio: Eric Schmidt is a lead engineer on the nvCOMP team, working on accelerating compression routines on GPUs. Prior to joining NVIDIA in 2021, Eric spent 11 years in the aerospace industry, developing software and researching algorithms in applied mathematics.

speakers.name: Eugene Zhidkov
speakers.bio: Eugene is a Compute DevTech Manager for Emerging Workloads at NVIDIA. His team brings Speed-Of-Light acceleration to large CSP and enterprise workloads with advances in compression and GPU data processing, striving for what wasn’t possible before. Prior to NVIDIA, Eugene helped Apple evolve their GPU HW and SW to strong-scale from phones to Ultra chips with the professional applications and ML focus, co-designing new Metal features together with top-tier ISVs. Eugene holds MSc at Computer Science and Applied Math, with his 2007 thesis focused on large procedural terrain generation and visualization and worked in games and simulation industries for 15 years, creating marine and air simulation systems for Kongsberg Maritime and for his startup.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

sessions.title: AI Grids: Why Telcos Hold the Keys to Distributed Intelligence
sessions.abstract:
Discover how telcos are leveraging NVIDIA’s platforms and partner ecosystem to transform their networks, AI factories, and infrastructure assets into a unified platform for distributed intelligence. Whether you're focused on sovereign AI, distributed inference, autonomous networks, or 6G innovation, connect with experts shaping how telcos are becoming the intelligent backbone of the AI era.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Sree Sankar
speakers.bio: Sree is a visionary AI product and business executive and global head at NVIDIA, where she leads the strategy and vision for AI-GRID, the company’s distributed inference platform. With two decades of experience and eight years of AI/ML product leadership, she has delivered transformative impact across computer vision, natural language processing, recommendation systems, and search in ecommerce, retail-tech, software as a service, and advertising. Previously, as SVP at Grabango, she helped disrupt retail with computer-vision-powered checkout-free tech, scaling enterprise adoption, securing multiple 100-store expansion deals, and cutting operational costs through Gen AI-driven automation.

speakers.name: Chris Penrose
speakers.bio: As the global head of business development for telco at NVIDIA, Chris works across the telecoms ecosystem to drive the growth of NVIDIA’s hardware and software solutions. Coordinating across NVIDIA and partners, Chris helps to create telco-specific offerings that leverage AI/ML, generative AI, 5G, edge/cloud computing, and advanced networking. Previously, as the COO of FogHorn, Chris led the go-to-market efforts across business development, technical sales, strategic partnerships, third-party distribution, marketing, advertising, and public relations globally. He holds a B.S. in marketing and an M.B.A. from Indiana University. His personal passions are family, skiing, dining out, and travel.

speakers.name: Joao Kluck Gomes
speakers.bio:
Joao is Senior Director of Business Development for AI Factories at NVIDIA, where he leads global initiatives helping telecom operators evolve into providers of sovereign AI infrastructure, platforms and services. With more than two decades of experience,  Joao has led product management, business development and technical sales across Juniper Networks, Nokia and Siemens in AI, cloud and edge spaces.

He specializes in creating new solution businesses end to end — from concept and ecosystem development to go-to-market design and large-scale sales execution with strategic partners and operators worldwide. Joao is passionate about the transformational impact of technology, and his proposed innovations include a granted U.S. patent (US 9,439,106) in the areas of mobile user experience and QoS. Joao holds a B.Eng. degree and an Executive MBA from the University of São Paulo.

speakers.name: Elad Blatt
speakers.bio: Elad Blatt is global head of business development telco networking at NVIDIA. He is an innovative and driven executive, with 17 years of experience of leading sales and business development teams in domestic and international marketplaces. Tech-savvy and technology-driven, he builds fruitful partnerships with hundreds of clients, delivering multimillion-dollar bottom-line growth.

speakers.name: Kanika Atri
speakers.bio: Kanika Atri is the senior director for telco marketing at NVIDIA, where she evangelizes AI adoption into telecoms, across generative AI for operations, sovereign AI factories, and 5G and 6G wireless networks. She brings deep industry experience from leading product management, technology strategy, and marketing functions globally over the last 20 years for various telecom companies. She has been a powerful influencer in accelerating new technologies and innovations from inception to adoption.

speakers.name: Waleed Badr
speakers.bio: Waleed Badr is a technology leader with deep experience across product GoTM,, focused on building and scaling AI and security solutions from cloud to edge with hyperscalers and global service providers. He specializes in developing joint AI solutions from ideation and integration through global scale, enabling production-grade AI platforms. He works with NVIDIA Cloud Partners to build Token Factories and Sovereign AI infrastructure, enabling large-scale AI training, inference, and data pipeline architectures. He activates ecosystems of foundation model providers, MLOps platforms, and industrial AI ISVs within the Sovereign AI Grid to accelerate enterprise and Sovereign AI adoption worldwide.

speakers.name: Amogh Dendukuri
speakers.bio: Amogh Dendukuri is a Product Marketing Manager for Telco AI at NVIDIA, where he drives go‑to‑market strategies that evangelize and accelerate telco AI adoption across their businesses, networks, and services. He focuses on uniting AI-powered operations with telco-led AI infrastructure, empowering operators to evolve into AI-native platforms for local innovation. Prior to NVIDIA, Amogh worked as a product manager in the networking industry, collaborating with telcos, cloud providers, and enterprises to build solutions at the intersection of AI, cloud, and network transformation.

speakers.name: Cindy Goh
speakers.bio: Cindy leads Product Marketing for AI-RAN and 6G at NVIDIA, driving the strategy for the next generation of intelligent wireless networks. With a deep foundation in semiconductor innovation, she previously led Technical Marketing and IP Product Management at Intel and Altera. Cindy holds both an M.S. and B.S. in Electrical Engineering from the University of Southern California.

speakers.name: Adnan Boustany
speakers.bio: Adnan Boustany is the principal product manager for Aerial in the Telco & AI group at NVIDIA. Adnan is focused on bringing the benefits of full-stack acceleration to the telco world, covering all network elements using both classic and AI/ML approaches. Before NVIDIA, Adnan worked at Intel NEX BU on vRAN product definition (FlexRAN) and market adoption, and at companies such as Mindspeed and Picochip. He worked on SoCs for cellular infrastructure covering WiMAX, 4G, and 5G. Adnan received an executive MBA from Imperial College London and an M.Sc. in engineering from the University of Limerick.

speakers.name: Shuvo Chowdhury
speakers.bio: Shuvo Chowdhury is a Principal Product Manager at NVIDIA, where he drives innovation at the intersection of AI, 6G, and cloud technologies. He leads the development of the AI-RAN and AI Grid platforms, focusing on enabling AI services for telcos by transforming networks into hubs for connected Intelligence. Before joining NVIDIA in 2022, Shuvo held leadership roles at Casa Systems, Huawei, and AT&T Labs, developing in cloud-native wireless core, RAN, and orchestration platforms. He holds an Executive MBA from the University of Texas at Austin and an MSc in Engineering from the University of Windsor.

speakers.name: Jean-Francois Lacasse
speakers.bio: Jean‑Francois Lacasse is a DevRel and AI‑RAN Evangelist in NVIDIA’s Telco & AI group, focused on full‑stack acceleration across RAN and edge and shaping early core concepts behind AI‑RAN and the AI‑RAN Alliance. Before NVIDIA, he held technical leadership roles spanning from pre‑WiMAX to 5G, with hands‑on expertise in system design. He led wireless SoCs designs, served as Product Line Manager at Marvell Technology, and holds a B.Sc. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from McGill University and a Master’s in Engineering Management from Université de Sherbrooke.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod D (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.title: GPU-Accelerated Image Processing and Data Loading With NVIDIA Image Processing Libraries
sessions.abstract:
Explore how to build high-performance, GPU-accelerated image pre- and post-processing pipelines using nvImageCodec, NPP, DALI, and CV-CUDA across domains such as semiconductors, medical imaging, geospatial, and more. See how nvImageCodec (with nvTIFF, nvJPEG, nvJPEG2000, HTJ2K) accelerates decode/encode, how NPP provides low-level image primitives, and how CV-CUDA and DALI enable scalable, model-ready pipelines. We’ll share recent optimizations and roadmap highlights, and use this session to gather your feedback on requirements across these libraries.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Mahesh Khadatare
speakers.bio: With an illustrious career spanning more than two decades, Mahesh Khadatare leads initiatives aimed at improving features and optimizing performance in crucial libraries like Image and Signal Processing (NPP), CV-CUDA, and image codecs (nvJPEG/2000/nvTIFF).

speakers.name: Makan Taghavi Dilamani
speakers.bio: Makan focuses on image processing and data compression technologies at NVIDIA for high performance computing applications. Previously, as a senior product manager at Qualcomm, Makan led the Low Power AI and Audio/Voice AI software roadmap, driving over 100 design wins and helping establishing Qualcomm's Low Power AI platform as one of the leaders in AI on the edge.

speakers.name: David Lesage
speakers.bio: David Lesage is the engineering manager for CV-CUDA at NVIDIA, helping the team fulfill its mission of building reference primitives for accelerated computer vision. Before NVIDIA, David worked extensively within the medical image processing field. First as a research scientist and then as an engineering manager, he's helped R&D software teams develop 2D and 3D ML and computer vision solutions.

speakers.name: Zoheb Khan
speakers.bio: Zoheb is part of the CUDA Math Libraries team at NVIDIA. He works on CUDA accelerated image codec libraries, such as nvJPEG, nvJPEG2000, and nvTIFF.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: Reinforcement Learning in the Modern AI Stack
sessions.abstract:
Explore new solutions for navigating the expanding landscape of reinforcement learning (RL) — know when to use RL, which flavor fits your domain, and what tooling accelerates your path to production. Get practical guidance on stacks, environments, and implementation strategies from hands-on practitioners.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Chris Alexiuk
speakers.bio: Chris Alexiuk is a Sr. Product Research Engineer at NVIDIA, working on creating technical assets that help developers use the incredible suite of AI tools available at NVIDIA. Chris comes from a machine learning and data science background, and he is obsessed with everything and anything about large language models.

speakers.name: Shashank Verma
speakers.bio: Shashank Verma is a deep learning technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA who's responsible for developing and presenting developer-focused content on various deep learning frameworks, specializing in generative AI and inference optimization. He obtained his master's in electrical engineering from the University of Wisconsin-Madison, where he focused on computer vision, security aspects in data science, and HPC.

speakers.name: Chris Wing
speakers.bio: Chris Wing is a Senior Product Manager for NeMo at NVIDIA, where he focuses on reinforcement learning for large language models. He has over a decade of experience in the AI industry, including prior roles at Palantir Technologies and Sinovation Ventures. Chris graduated magna cum laude in Computer Science from Georgetown University and earned his MBA from the Wharton School as a Palmer Scholar. He is also a recipient of the U.S. Department of State Critical Language Scholarship for advanced Mandarin.

speakers.name: Wenwen Gao
speakers.bio: Wenwen Gao is a senior product manager for NeMo at NVIDIA, focusing on LLM training framework and microservices. Her past experience include LLM inference (NIM) and recommender systems (Merlin). She holds a B.S. in computer science from the University of Toronto and an M.B.A. from the MIT Sloan School of Management.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Best Practices for Accelerating LLM and VLM Inference With SGLang
sessions.abstract:
Join this interactive session to ask questions and deepen your understanding of serving LLMs (and vision language models, VLMs) with SGLang. Our experts are offering one-on-one guidance on efficiently deploying models with SGLang, optimizing throughput/latency, and leveraging key runtime features. Whether you're exploring best practices for deploying SGLang via its OpenAI-compatible API on Kubernetes, or tuning configs (speculative decoding, kernel integration and fused ops, Tensor/pipeline/expert parallelism, request scheduling, prefix/prompt caching, and quantization) to accelerate inference, this session will cover it all.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Trevor Morris
speakers.bio: Trevor Morris is a senior deep learning software engineer on the SGLang team at NVIDIA, where he focuses on accelerating LLM inference. His recent work includes optimizing GPU communication, quantization, kernel integration, and prefill-decode disaggregation. Previously, he contributed to TensorFlow and improved distributed training with Jax and XLA. Trevor received his master's degree in computer science from the University of California at Santa Barbara.

speakers.name: Ian Wang
speakers.bio: Shu Wang is a senior engineer with the DLFW team at NVIDIA, where he focuses on model performance optimization and kernel tuning for inference. He received his Ph.D. in electrical engineering from the University of New Mexico.

speakers.name: Yangmin Li
speakers.bio: Yangmin Li is a Senior System Software Engineer at NVIDIA working on large-scale LLM inference systems. His work focuses on optimizing long-context inference and distributed serving for modern models such as DeepSeek and Qwen using frameworks like SGLang. He is particularly interested in system-level optimization for large-scale AI infrastructure.

speakers.name: Kaixi Hou
speakers.bio: Kaixi Hou is an engineer in the Deep Learning Frameworks group at NVIDIA, focusing on GPU-accelerated software for AI and inference workloads. He works on sglang inference frameworks and collaborates with internal product teams and customers to build, optimize, and deploy high-performance solutions on NVIDIA platforms.

speakers.name: Meenakshi Venkataraman
speakers.bio: Meenakshi Venkataraman is a Senior Deep Learning Software Engineer at NVIDIA focused on bringing LLM inference frameworks such as vLLM and SGLang to production on new GPU platforms. Her work spans hardware enablement, framework integration, and containerized releases across the NVIDIA AI software stack.

speakers.name: Serge Panev
speakers.bio: Serge Panev is a Senior Software Engineer on NVIDIA’s Deep Learning Frameworks team, where he works on NVIDIA DALI. He holds an M.Sc. in Computer Science from EPITA (France). His work centers on building and optimizing deep learning frameworks and tooling—including NVIDIA DALI, TensorFlow, MXNet, and PyTorch Geometric—and more recently on research in graph learning and graph neural networks (GNNs).

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

sessions.title: NVIDIA 101: From Chips to Software—How NVIDIA's AI Platform Enables the AI Revolution
sessions.abstract:
This session is a continuation of the "NVIDIA 101: From Chips to Software — How NVIDIA's AI Platform Enables the AI Revolution" session led by Rambo Jacoby. In this session, you'll get the opportunity to touch and feel NVIDIA's hardware and ask any questions you may have about NVIDIA's platform, and to ask follow up questions from Rambo's earlier session, directly from Rambo himself.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Rambo Jacoby
speakers.bio: Rambo Jacoby is a principal engineer with a 24-year tenure at NVIDIA, and one of NVIDIA’s most dynamic and influential speakers. He currently contributes to NVIDIA’s Executive Briefing Center, where he's responsible for educating world leaders and Fortune 500 executives on NVIDIA’s AI platform and the transformative impact of graphics, digital twins, high-performance computing, and agentic and physical AI on the world’s major industries.

speakers.name: Bogdan Vioreanu
speakers.bio: Bogdan is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, focused on AI solutions for the financial services industry. Previously, he was a quant at Goldman Sachs in the Risk and Asset Management divisions. His background is in quantitative modeling and scientific computing, with academic experience as a postdoctoral assistant professor at the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. He holds a Ph.D. in applied mathematics from Yale University.

speakers.name: Jonathan Bentz
speakers.bio: Jonathan Bentz leads the CUDA technical marketing engineering team at NVIDIA, where his team focuses on creating and delivering engaging content and connecting with CUDA developers. Jonathan holds a Ph.D. in chemistry and a master’s degree in computer science from Iowa State University.

speakers.name: Chelsea Sumner
speakers.bio:
Chelsea is a healthcare innovator and the North and Latin American and Africa healthcare AI startups lead at NVIDIA. With a background in the pharmaceutical industry and clinical settings, she manages relationships for over 1,500 healthcare startups. Her passion for healthcare and technology led to her current position, where she strives to democratize AI and advance digital health.
 
Chelsea is committed to uplifting underrepresented communities. She founded the nonprofit Dose of Melanin, Inc., connecting thousands of Black pharmacists and student pharmacists. Her multifaceted background, dedication to healthcare innovation, and efforts in community service make her an influential figure.

speakers.name: Vallard Benincosa
speakers.bio: Vallard is a crusty, grumpy industry veteran who's enjoyed working on all aspects of the HPC stack, from the hardware to the AI applications that run on top. He enjoys watching machines boot up from bare metal and automatically deploy. He calculates that he's spent at least five years of his life doing that.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Maximize GPU Resources in Your Virtualized Environment: Best Practices for Sizing and Deployment
sessions.abstract:
Join this interactive session for direct insights and recommendations from NVIDIA specialists on fully utilizing your virtualized GPUs to deploy graphic-intensive apps, digital twin simulations, AI development, and more. Engage with experts who've navigated virtualization complexities, offering practical advice and proven methodologies. Bring your questions and leave with actionable strategies to enhance your virtualized data center operations.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Kelly Siggers
speakers.bio: Kelly Siggers is a solution architect at NVIDIA, specializing in vGPU solutions for cloud service providers. With a background rooted in data center networking from an architectural customer perspective, Kelly supports a range of vGPU solutions, focusing on virtual desktop infrastructure environments that leverage GPUs to accelerate applications in cloud-based settings.

speakers.name: Jimmy Rotella
speakers.bio: Jimmy Rotella's dynamic career spans IT, architecture, engineering, and construction. Jimmy excels in designing and implementing cutting-edge technology that is purpose-driven, and strives to empower others to harness GPU innovation to enhance workflows, foster collaboration, and increase efficiencies across industries.

speakers.name: Anshul Fadnavis
speakers.bio: As a senior product manager, Anshul leads a range of GPU virtualization products at NVIDIA. He brings valuable engineering experience from his former role as a systems software engineer for vGPU, and he now shapes the future of the technology in fulfilling its mission to help customers extract maximum value from NVIDIA GPUs.

speakers.name: Manvender Rawat
speakers.bio: Manvender is a Sr. Manager of Product Management for NVIDIA Enterprise Platform & Solutions group with more than 20 years’ experience and a background in kernel programming, Storage and GPU virtualization. He is responsible for NVIDIA’s software Enterprise reference architectures and AI workload benchmarking & sizing for large-scale clusters designed for high performance. Manvender has a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science from University of Pune.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

sessions.title: Agentic Edge AI: Nemotron Training & Alignment Best Practices
sessions.abstract:
Discover how NVIDIA developed the Nemotron family of edge-ready models, setting new benchmarks for accuracy and performance. This session explores standard pre-training to advanced pruning and distillation for edge-scale efficiency. We will also detail the alignment techniques used to create state-of-the-art multimodal reasoning models. Finally, learn best practices for domain adaptation and the deployment of customized Nemotron models for gaming and AI applications on GeForce RTX platforms.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Ameya Sunil Mahabaleshwarkar
speakers.bio: Ameya Sunil Mahabaleshwarkar is a senior deep learning scientist at NVIDIA working on pre-training and post-training of language models for edge devices. He previously received his M.S. from the Language Technologies Institute in Carnegie Mellon University's School of Computer Science.

speakers.name: Yoshi Suhara
speakers.bio: Yoshi Suhara is an applied research manager at NVIDIA, leading a team focused on developing small language models, task-specific large language models, and synthetic data generation. He received his Ph.D. in engineering from Keio University, Japan.

speakers.name: Zijia Chen
speakers.bio: Zijia Chen is senior deep learning scientist at NVIDIA with a strong focus on the development and optimization of large and small language models (LLMs/SLMs). His expertise spans the entire model life cycle, from multi-stage pruning and fine-tuning to comprehensive evaluation and deployment. Zijia was a senior software engineer at Ping An U.S. Research Lab and holds a master's degree in computer science from UC San Diego.

speakers.name: Aditya Malte
speakers.bio: Aditya Malte is a deep learning software engineer at NVIDIA. He works on building NeMo edge language models through a variety of pre-training, distillation, and post-training techniques. Aditya received his M.S. in computer science from the University of Southern California's Viterbi School of Engineering.

speakers.name: Jani Joki
speakers.bio: Jani Joki leads NVIDIA’s benchmarking and technical marketing teams for client computing. With more than 20 years of experience, he has helped create and define industry-standard benchmarks used to evaluate computer performance across a broad range of applications.

speakers.name: Oluwatobi Olabiyi
speakers.bio: Tobi is a senior engineering manager at NVIDIA, currently leading teams in delivering state-of-the-art multi-modal, multilingual conversational AI solutions for customers. Previously, Tobi was a senior manager of AI research at Capital One and a senior research scientist at Toyota Research Institute. He holds a Ph.D. in electrical engineering from Prairie View A&M University.

speakers.name: Vinay Raman
speakers.bio: Vinay Raman is a senior deep learning scientist at NVIDIA. He spearheads projects spanning edge-ready LLMs for agentic and gaming applications, text-based and multi-modal retrieval-augmented generation, and synthetic data generation, along with building generative AI solutions for a variety of enterprise use cases in different domains. He has an academic background in applied math with a Ph.D. from MIT in computational physics.

speakers.name: Hayley Ross
speakers.bio: Hayley Ross is a senior deep learning scientist at NVIDIA, working on the post-training of edge language models. Her work focuses on instruction following and robustness, primarily using reinforcement learning. Hayley holds a Ph.D in linguistics from Harvard University.

speakers.name: Negar Habibi
speakers.bio: Negar is a senior manager at NVIDIA leading the Edge Models and Data Engineering PMO team. She is also responsible for program management of NV agentic solutions and foundational blueprints. She has more than 15 years of industry experience and received her electrical and computer engineering degree from the University of Toronto.

speakers.name: Bilal Kartal
speakers.bio: Bilal Kartal is a senior deep learning scientist at NVIDIA, specializing in learning- and planning-based decision-making systems. He has cross-domain experience spanning a variety of areas, including automated trading, autonomous driving technologies, and, more recently, large language models with reasoning capabilities. Bilal holds a Ph.D. in computer science and engineering from the University of Minnesota.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Best Practices for Accelerating LLM and VLM Inference With vLLM
sessions.abstract:
Meet with the experts at NVIDIA to learn how to accelerate vLLM inference performance on NVIDIA GPUs. Our experts at NVIDIA help make vLLM more performance and scalable. We cover a diversity of workloads running from DGX spark to NVIDIA GB200 NVL72. Join us for a deep dive in various optimization techniques, including speculative decoding, kernel performance, deployment recipes, reinforcement learning, and open-source software collaborations. Connect with us and share your vLLM use case, and together we'll explore how we can help improve the framework!

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Xin Li
speakers.bio: Xin Li is a software engineering manager at NVIDIA, where he contributes to vLLM to improve its performance and usability on NVIDIA GPUs. He is generally interested in the intersection of machine learning and system level optimization.

speakers.name: Pavani Majety
speakers.bio: Pavani is a senior deep learning engineer focused on inference in the Deep Learning Frameworks team at NVIDIA, where she works on accelerating open-source inference frameworks such as vLLM and SGLang for NVIDIA Blackwell and next-generation GPUs. Her work spans end-to-end inference stacks, from kernel-level performance and low-precision numerics to distributed serving at scale. Previously, she contributed to ML compiler technologies like MLIR-TensorRT at NVIDIA and worked on Embedded Coder at MathWorks. Pavani is excited to chat about inference tech stacks, kernel DSLs, low-precision hardware, ML compilers, and ways to contribute effectively to open-source ecosystems

speakers.name: Shang Wang
speakers.bio: With NVIDIA's acquisition of CentML in June 2025, Shang Wang rejoined NVIDIA as an engineering manager for AI Systems Software, leading a team that contributes to MLPerf Inference and vLLM. Shang was a co-founder and CTO of CentML from 2022-25, leading company-wide research and engineering efforts and various administrative responsibilities. Before that, Shang was a deep learning software engineer at NVIDIA from 2020-22. During his Ph.D. study, Shang was a finalist of the 2023 Meta Research PhD Fellowship. Shang received his M.Sc. in computer science in 2020, and his B.A.Sc. as the valedictorian graduate of the computer engineering class in 2018, both from University of Toronto.

speakers.name: Duncan Moss
speakers.bio: Duncan Moss is a Senior Developer Technology Engineer at NVIDIA, specializing in the optimization of customer workloads for large-scale LLM inference systems, including the NVIDIA GB200 NVL72. He has a long track record of developing high-performance, real-time inference systems, ranging from FPGA-based deep learning accelerators to low-latency GPU video analytics. Having designed both high-performance hardware architectures for low-precision GEMMs and distributed software inference systems, he offers a unique perspective on hardware-software co-design to achieve exceptional results on cutting-edge hardware.

speakers.name: Siyuan Fu
speakers.bio: Siyuan Fu is an AI Developer Technology Engineer at NVIDIA. He has been contributing to vLLM’s kernel enablement efforts, with a focus on FlashInfer and CUTLASS kernels. He has also worked on generating kernel trace lines and fine-tuning the PD disaggregation configurations for vLLM.

speakers.name: Alec Flowers
speakers.bio: Alec Flowers is a Senior Deep Learning Algorithms Engineer at NVIDIA, where he focuses on accelerating LLM inference at datacenter scale and is a core contributor to Dynamo, an open-source inference library. He holds a M.S. in Data Science from École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL). His expertise lies at the intersection of deep learning, optimization, and high-performance computing, driving advancements in model efficiency and deployment.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Voice Agent Applications and Models Development
sessions.abstract:
In this session you will learn how NVIDIA’s reference voice applications and models are developed to establish new state-of-the-art accuracy numbers on multiple benchmarks and user experience. This session covers the standard voice application pipeline configuration consisting of Nemotron Speech Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR), language models (LLM), and speech synthesis (TTS), and advanced configurations for end-of-utterance detection and barge-in or backchannelling handling. It will also cover how end-to-end voice models are making voice application development more user friendly and easy to deploy and maintain. Voice model development including pre-training techniques as well as advanced speech alignment techniques to turn base models into state-of-the-art instruct models will also be covered. We will also share best practices for voice agent and model development for various domains and applications.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Slim Essid
speakers.bio:
Slim Essid is a senior scientist who joined NVIDIA in June 2025 to work on conversational AI, especially audio/speech language models. His wider research interests are in AI, ML, and signal processing applied to multi-modal language models and multi-modal perception, as well as speech processing, machine listening, and music information research.

Previously, he was full professor of Télécom Paris. He received an engineering degree from the École Nationale d’Ingénieurs de Tunis in 2001; an M.Sc. (D.E.A.) from the École Nationale Supérieure des Télécommunications in 2002; a Ph.D. from the Université Pierre et Marie Curie in 2005; and the habilitation (H.D.R.) degree from UPMC in 2015.

speakers.name: Nikhil Srihari
speakers.bio: Nikhil Srihari is a senior deep learning scientist at NVIDIA whose primary work is in the development of Full-Duplex ALM and AVLM models. He has also worked in the development of LLMs and the evaluation systems for large models. Nikhil previously worked at Amazon and Fidelity Investments. His educational background includes a master's degree in computer science from the State University of New York Buffalo, and a bachelor's degree from the National Institute of Technology Karnataka, India.

speakers.name: Viet Anh Trinh
speakers.bio: Viet Anh Trinh is a senior applied scientist working on full-duplex multi-modal systems at NVIDIA. He was previously an applied scientist at Amazon, focusing on speech systems, and held research internships at Microsoft Research and Amazon. He earned his Ph.D. at City University of New York Graduate Center, where he worked on automatic speech recognition, text-to-speech, and speech enhancement, and later completed a postdoctoral position at Worcester Polytechnic Institute, researching half-duplex multi-modal systems.

speakers.name: Dharmendra Choudhary
speakers.bio: Dharmendra Choudhary is a conversational AI expert with eight years of experience. His primary areas of focus are building digital human experiences and improving user experience for speech-to-speech assistants. He holds a bachelor's degree from the Indian Institute of Technology, Delhi.

speakers.name: Nourchene Ferchichi
speakers.bio: Nourchene Ferchichi is a deep learning language engineer at NVIDIA, focusing on the development of advanced speech models. Her work spans automatic speech recognition and automatic speech translation, with an emphasis on end-to-end, streaming speech-to-speech and full-duplex systems for real-time voice agents. Nourchene holds a master’s degree in applied mathematics from ENIT, Tunisia.

speakers.name: Negar Habibi
speakers.bio: Negar is a senior manager at NVIDIA leading the Edge Models and Data Engineering PMO team. She is also responsible for program management of NV agentic solutions and foundational blueprints. She has more than 15 years of industry experience and received her electrical and computer engineering degree from the University of Toronto.

speakers.name: Lily Lee
speakers.bio: Yi-Hui (Lily) Lee is a deep learning scientist working on full-duplex multi-modal systems. She previously interned for four summers at NVIDIA in Oluwatobi's team, contributing to several projects, including prompt-tuning LLMs for dialogue and fine-tuning a small language model for gameplay. Most recently, she worked on the half-duplex Omni project this summer, focusing on audio understanding. She is also a Ph.D. candidate in computer science at the University of Texas at Dallas, working in the Natural Language Processing Lab. Her research focuses on mitigating hallucinations in LLMs and multi-modal LLMs.

speakers.name: Adi Margolin
speakers.bio: Adi Margolin is the product manager for Riva SDK and Speech NIM. With 16 years of product management experience, Adi has built high-impact speech technology solutions across enterprise software companies, developing expertise in bringing ASR and TTS innovations to market. Based in San Jose, Adi brings a unique perspective to speech AI development, having successfully navigated the transition from legacy systems to modern AI-driven platforms while addressing complex requirements of real-time media applications.

speakers.name: Tripti Singhal
speakers.bio: Tripti Singhal is a senior solutions architect in the Worldwide Field Organization at NVIDIA. During her nine years at NVIDIA, she has specialized in real-time inference using NVIDIA Riva Speech AI SDK, Speech NIM, and voice agent pipelines. She works hands-on with enterprise customers and partners to design, deploy, and optimize applications for automatic speech recognition (ASR), text-to-speech (TTS) and machine translation. She previously spent a year and a half on the NVIDIA product management team focusing on inference, gaining deep insight into inference serving architectures, scalability, and performance optimization that she applies directly to customer deployments through Speech AI.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod E (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation

sessions.title: Optimize NVFP4 Training and Inference at Scale Without Losing Quality
sessions.abstract:
NVFP4 and MXFP8 are the new numerical formats in Blackwell to significantly speed up LLM training and inference versus previous formats based on FP8, BF16, or FP16. In this session, we’ll discuss how to achieve this speedup without compromising the stability of the training and model accuracy. We’ll bring experts with deep knowledge on both the hardware and the software side, so that you learn how to enable NVFP4 and MXFP8 on your GPU to reduce memory footprint and accelerate training and inference.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Sergio Perez
speakers.bio: Sergio Perez is a solutions architect at NVIDIA working on training and inference of LLMs. Sergio collaborates with developers of supercomputing centers, startups, and large corporations to accelerate their AI workloads. Sergio focuses on model compression, leveraging pruning, distillation, and numerical formats like FP8 or NVFP4 to speed up AI. Sergio holds a Ph.D. in computational fluid dynamics from Imperial College London.

speakers.name: Kirthi Shankar Sivamani
speakers.bio: Kirthi Shankar Sivamani is a senior performance engineer on the Model Performance Team at NVIDIA. He holds a bachelor's degree in computer engineering from Purdue University, West Lafayette. Since joining NVIDIA in 2021, he has co-started the Transformer Engine project and focuses on deep learning using the PyTorch framework.

speakers.name: Asma Beevi Kuriparambil Thekkumpate
speakers.bio: Asma Beevi K. T. is a senior deep learning engineer at NVIDIA, where she develops the Model Optimizer library. Her work focuses on algorithmic methods for improving inference performance and accuracy in deep learning models, particularly large language models. She holds an M.S. from Georgia Tech, and previously worked at OmniML and Intel Labs.

speakers.name: Chenjie Luo
speakers.bio: Chenjie Luo is a manager in the Deep Learning Algorithm and Software team at NVIDIA, leading the initiative of the NVIDIA Model Optimizer quantization and performance optimization. Chenjie joined NVIDIA through the acquisition of OmniML, Inc. as an early team member. Before that, he was a software manager at Nuro, developing self-driving robots, and a SW engineer at Google on edge acceleration. He received his master’s degree in electrical engineering from Stanford, and bachelor’s degree from Zhejiang University.

speakers.name: Zhiyu Cheng
speakers.bio: Zhiyu Cheng is a manager at NVIDIA, where he focuses on driving algorithms and software development to optimize large-scale inference for generative AI workloads, including LLMs/vision language models, and diffusion models on NVIDIA’s latest platforms. He has 12 years of industry experience in efficient deep learning across his career from NXP, Xilinx, Baidu, and OmniML (acquired by NVIDIA). Zhiyu has over 30 published papers and patents. He holds a Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from the University of Illinois with a thesis in the field of information theory.

speakers.name: Karin Sevegnani
speakers.bio: Karin is a solutions architect leading the NVIDIA AI Technology Center U.K. initiative. Karin’s expertise lies in exploring topic transitions within conversational systems and devising effective strategies for introducing items in recommendation settings; she completed a Ph.D. in natural language processing at Heriot-Watt University, U.K., in July 2023, where she focused on conversational AI and recommendation systems.

speakers.name: Leo Du
speakers.bio: Leo Du is a senior generative AI solutions architect at NVIDIA, with prior experience as an algorithm engineer at ASML. He has rich experience in the semiconductor industry. He helps partners design AI infrastructure, and architect and implement generative AI enterprise software and products. His expertise spans LLMs, domain-adaptive pre-training, post-training, reinforcement learning, and computer vision. Outside work, he enjoys cycling and hiking.

speakers.name: Liana Mikaelyan
speakers.bio: Liana Mikaelyan is a senior solutions architect at NVIDIA, specializing in model compression and efficient training and inference for LLM deployment. Previously, she was an applied scientist at Microsoft, developing methods for injecting knowledge into LLMs and enabling on-device capabilities. She holds an M.Sc. in machine learning from University College London, with a background in mathematics.

speakers.name: Rachel Oberman
speakers.bio: Rachel Oberman is an AI solutions architect at NVIDIA who specializes in optimizing and productizing AI applications for the consumer internet and AI research industries. Her primary focus is on speeding up end-to-end AI pipelines with techniques such as lower precision format training (FP8, NVFP4) and optimized inference strategies. She holds a master's degree in computer science from Columbia University, and a bachelor's degree in computer science and data science from the College of William & Mary.

speakers.name: River Liu
speakers.bio:
River Liu, holding master’s degree from Nanjing University, is the solutions architect manager leading low-precision training enablement in China. River collaborates with leading open-source model builders in China to accelerate practical adoption of low-precision training. With expertise in LLM training using DeepSeek-V3-style FP8, MXFP8, and NVFP4, River focuses on preserving model quality while improving training efficiency. 

Previously, River worked on blockwise FP8 recipe validation, best-practice development, and performance tuning; today, leads NVFP4 training-recipe validation on top open-source models, along with performance profiling and optimization.

speakers.name: Sagar Desai
speakers.bio: Sagar's a generative AI solutions architect specializing in the design and deployment of large-scale machine learning systems, who has deep expertise in optimizing model performance for both training and inference workloads. With a strong foundation in MLOps and LLMOps, he architects resilient infrastructure using distributed computing and orchestration principles to deliver high-impact, enterprise-grade AI solutions.

speakers.name: Sandro Cavallari
speakers.bio: Sandro Cavallari is a solutions architect specializing in generative AI at NVIDIA. His primary focus is assisting customers in adopting NVIDIA technologies and embracing the AI revolution. He holds a Ph.D. in computational intelligence from Nanyang Technological University.

speakers.name: Utkarsh Uppal
speakers.bio: Utkarsh Uppal is a senior applied deep learning solutions architect at NVIDIA, where he specializes in building high-performance deep learning pipelines across domains like language and speech. His primary focus is on developing end-to-end conversational AI systems, including training LLMs from scratch, particularly for Indic languages, and building domain-specific models with enterprises. He also has deep expertise in designing and optimizing inference architectures for production, with a focus on low-precision formats (FP4, FP8), decoding strategies, and KV-cache optimizations.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

sessions.title: How to Design and Build Gigawatt Scale AI Factories
sessions.abstract:
Designing gigawatt-scale AI factories requires a shift from bespoke construction to standardized, modular architecture. This session introduces DSX, NVIDIA’s modular reference design for large-scale AI infrastructure. We move beyond traditional data center design to a purpose-built framework optimized for the latest liquid-cooled compute.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Robert Cervellione
speakers.bio: Robert contributes to the Omniverse Platform, focusing on AI factories and manufacturing. With 20-plus years of experience in developing 3D-centric workflows for digital twins, Robert has built his career on digital transformation in manufacturing and industry, bridging advanced simulation, interoperability, and real-time digital twin technologies. Using cutting-edge platforms and tools, he enables organizations to unify design, production, and operational data. As both a registered architect and a technology leader, Robert implements advanced digital twin and manufacturing workflows, driving transformation across large-scale projects.

speakers.name: Jay Axe
speakers.bio: Jay is a technical product manager at NVIDIA focusing on Omniverse Virtual Factories. He found his way to NVIDIA as a freelance computer-generated lighting/simulation consultant about 10 years ago. At that time, he created photorealistic rendering for Endeavor, NVIDIA's new headquarters building in Santa Clara, California. Day to day at NVIDIA, Jay demonstrates architecture, engineering, & construction workflows for real-time ray tracing and photorealistic path-tracing.

speakers.name: James Mills
speakers.bio: James is a product marketing manager at NVIDIA, focused on go-to-market strategy for Omniverse. James has been bringing new products to market since 2012. He has held positions at early-stage startups and global brands including Adobe, Amazon Web Services, and the BBC, prioritizing a focus on cloud-based products for 3D content creation. James begin his career in physical infrastructure as a signaling engineer, helping maintain the U.K.'s rail signaling network.

speakers.name: Constant Sanciaume
speakers.bio: Constant leads data center engineering at NVIDIA, where he oversees teams of infrastructure engineers, solutions architects, and software engineers. He and his organization design and deploy NVIDIA’s AI factories and drive the development of NVIDIA’s DSX AI Factory reference design, which helps organizations standardize and rapidly roll out AI factories at scale across the world. He is based in Santa Clara, California.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

sessions.title: Speech AI Model and Services Development and Customization
sessions.abstract:
Get direct answers to your implementation challenges. This open Q&A session is your chance to connect with the experts responsible for Nemotron Speech to discuss how to maximize the performance of automatic speech recognition (ASR) and text-to-speech (TTS) in your own projects. Whether you are building for a noisy industrial environment, need better multilingual support, or are trying to create intelligent voice agents, our team is here to troubleshoot and share actionable strategies.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Yitagessu Gebremedhin
speakers.bio: Yitagessu is a senior speech AI engineer at NVIDIA, where he has been contributing to multilingual automatic speech recognition (ASR) development since 2022. His work focuses on large-scale ASR model training, improving noise robustness, and enhancing data and label quality across diverse languages. He worked on an efficient tokenization algorithm, which boosted multilingual modeling efficiency and accuracy. Most recently, he has been working on scaling ASR architectures, improving code-switch performance, and advancing real-world robustness for production-grade multilingual ASR systems.

speakers.name: Mayank Jain
speakers.bio: Mayank is a senior software engineer at NVIDIA specializing in speech AI. He focuses on developing and optimizing high-performance inference pipelines for automatic speech recognition (ASR), with a strong emphasis on achieving best-in-class end-to-end latency and throughput. He works to enable production-ready deployment of state-of-the-art ASR models.

speakers.name: Sanjay Singh Chauhan
speakers.bio: As a senior engineering manager at NVIDIA, Sanjay architects the engines that make AI conversational at a global scale. He leadd engineering teams building high-performance inference services for automatic speech recognition, text-to-speech, neural machine translation, and LLMs. His focus is the entire life cycle of AI production—from rigorous data curation and model development to the final mile of deployment. Sanjay is obsessed with milliseconds, optimizing for ultra-low latency and maximum throughput to solve the complex challenges of serving population-scale AI.

speakers.name: Mikyas Desta
speakers.bio: Mikyas Desta is a senior deep learning scientist at NVIDIA whose work focuses on developing multilingual, high-quality, and scalable speech synthesis models, spanning data curation, research, model development, training, and evaluation. Most recently, he has been working on a state-of-the-art, transformer-based multilingual speech synthesis model powering NVIDIA Riva. The model integrates classifier-free guidance and group relative policy optimization to enhance output quality and fine-grained control.
