Prior to NVIDIA, he worked on multi-modal AI development at IBM Research, focusing on visual question-answering.

speakers.name: Harishchandra Dubey
speakers.bio: Hari Dubey is a senior ML scientist in the Riva speech team contributing to consumer-centric products and experiences. His work is focused on robust speech recognition for a variety of real-world edge AI use-cases. He has experience in interdisciplinary projects involving speech, language, and video processing.

speakers.name: Rahul Mittal
speakers.bio: As an engineering manager at NVIDIA, Rahul focuses on optimizing state-of-the-art speech AI models for best latency and throughput, enabling these optimized models in NVIDIA's speech inference engine, and creating production-ready NIMs. Rahul's team designs end-to-end speech inference solutions spanning recognition, synthesis, and translation, accessible through multiple API interfaces like gRPC, HTTP, and web sockets, and easily integrable in a conversational AI agent pipeline.

speakers.name: Adi Margolin
speakers.bio: Adi Margolin is the product manager for Riva SDK and Speech NIM. With 16 years of product management experience, Adi has built high-impact speech technology solutions across enterprise software companies, developing expertise in bringing ASR and TTS innovations to market. Based in San Jose, Adi brings a unique perspective to speech AI development, having successfully navigated the transition from legacy systems to modern AI-driven platforms while addressing complex requirements of real-time media applications.

speakers.name: Tripti Singhal
speakers.bio: Tripti Singhal is a senior solutions architect in the Worldwide Field Organization at NVIDIA. During her nine years at NVIDIA, she has specialized in real-time inference using NVIDIA Riva Speech AI SDK, Speech NIM, and voice agent pipelines. She works hands-on with enterprise customers and partners to design, deploy, and optimize applications for automatic speech recognition (ASR), text-to-speech (TTS) and machine translation. She previously spent a year and a half on the NVIDIA product management team focusing on inference, gaining deep insight into inference serving architectures, scalability, and performance optimization that she applies directly to customer deployments through Speech AI.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation

sessions.title: Boost Data Science Pipelines With Accelerated Libraries
sessions.abstract:
Drop into these walk-up office hours to meet the experts building NVIDIA’s CUDA-X data science libraries and talk through your end-to-end pipeline needs. ​Get practical guidance on where cuDF, cuML, cuGraph, and GPU-accelerated Spark can boost productivity in your workflow. ​Discuss how to experiment and scale from DGX Spark to the largest supercomputers, and share feedback that can help shape upcoming releases.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Greg Kimball
speakers.bio: Gregory Kimball is a software engineering manager at NVIDIA working on the RAPIDS team. Gregory leads development for libcudf, the CUDA/C++ library for columnar data processing that powers RAPIDS cuDF. Gregory holds a PhD in applied physics from the California Institute of Technology.

speakers.name: Alexandria Barghi
speakers.bio: Alexandria (Alex) Barghi is the RAPIDS Graph team lead, directing the development of core graph packages including cuGraph, cuGraph-PyG, and WholeGraph.  Her background is in accelerated graph analytics with a focus on improving graph processing for downstream machine learning tasks and retrieval-augmented generation. Alex received her PhD in Electrical and Computer Engineering from the University of Maryland.

speakers.name: Divye Paresh Gala
speakers.bio: Divye Gala is a Senior Software Engineer on the RAPIDS team at NVIDIA, developing fast & scalable algorithms for machine learning and graph analytics on the GPU. Divye holds Ms. and Bs. degrees in Computer Science. In his free time, Divye enjoys playing soccer and watching cricket.

speakers.name: Vyas Ramasubramani
speakers.bio: Vyas leads the development of Python tabular data processing in RAPIDS, including libraries like cudf-polars and cudf.pandas. Vyas has extensive experience across the CUDA development stack in Python, C, and C++, as well as with packaging CUDA-accelerated libraries.

speakers.name: Bobby Evans
speakers.bio: Bobby Evans has worked on open-source big data for well over a decade. He is a PMC member and committer for the Apache Spark, Hadoop, Storm and TEZ projects. Most recently he has worked on the RAPIDS Accelerator for Apache Spark to reduce cost of ETL processing using GPUs.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.title: Robotics Teleoperation From the Web
sessions.abstract:
Discover how to break the hardware bottleneck in industrial AI with CloudXR.js, the critical web-based extension of the NVIDIA CloudXR platform. This session will explore the plug-and-play nature of CloudXR.js, enabling effortless integration of diverse immersive devices—including Meta Quest and PICO—into high-performance streaming pipelines.
 
Learn how to operationalize this universal interface for large-scale robotics data collection and teleoperation. We will demonstrate how CloudXR.js bridges Isaac Lab simulations with wireless headsets to accelerate sim-to-real workflows. Beyond robotics, we will examine the platform’s impact on enterprise extended reality (XR) streaming in the automotive and manufacturing sectors, where browser-native accessibility is redefining how teams collaborate in immersive environments.

speakers.name: Yanzi Zhu
speakers.bio: Yanzi Zhu is a principal software engineer and manager at NVIDIA, at the forefront of building the next generation of spatial computing infrastructure. His team is engineering the backbone of modern immersive technology, enabling robots and AI to stream, perceive, and interact with the physical world, and humans to immerse in the digital world. His expertise is rooted in streaming frameworks, sensor data pipelines, and cloud systems that power data capturing and immersive experiences for industrial applications. Previously, he led teams on Kubernetes-based distributed infrastructure at Google.

speakers.name: Max Bickley
speakers.bio: Max Bickley is a product manager and designer for spatial computing on the Omniverse team. He graduated from the Visual Effects program at the Savannah College of Art and Design. Before joining NVIDIA, he was a lighting artist and supervisor at Pixar. He resides in Oakland, California with his partner Katie and their son Otto.

speakers.name: Harel Omer
speakers.bio: Harel Omer is a senior product manager at NVIDIA with over 13 years of experience in autonomous systems development, including robotics and autonomous vehicles. He specializes in simulation technologies and leads scalable world reconstruction and simulation workflows that advance physical AI. Harel holds a master's degree in system engineering from Tel Aviv University.

speakers.name: Gareth Morgan
speakers.bio: Gareth Morgan is a veteran of over a quarter of a century of graphics, rendering and mixed-reality development. Since 2024 he has been building NVIDIA's spatial compute platform and exploring new frontiers for the technology. Prior to NVIDIA, he worked at companies including Autodesk, Activision and Silicon Graphics.

speakers.name: Sergey Grizan
speakers.bio: Sergey Grizan is a Software Engineer and technical lead at NVIDIA, focusing on XR‑driven real‑robot teleoperation and humanoid whole‑body control. He leads development of teleoperation integrations across multiple application surfaces, enabling end‑to‑end teleoperation and data collection workflows for platforms such as Unitree G1 and GR00T VLA. Previously, he led teams working on Android Automotive OS at Google and contributed to Google’s interactive cloud streaming technologies.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

sessions.title: Unlock the Power of AI-Ready Data: Transform Your Enterprise Storage Systems With NVIDIA AI Data Platform
sessions.abstract:
Data is the hardest part of AI—a majority AI initiatives fail to reach production due to concerns around data availability and security issues. Join NVIDIA experts to explore how the NVIDIA AI Data Platform unlocks AI-ready data pipelines for training, inference, and next-generation AI applications.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. You can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Matheen Raza
speakers.bio: Matheen is the product marketing lead for Enterprise AI Products at NVIDIA. Prior to NVIDIA, Matheen has experience across multiple companies, including Amazon Web Services, Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Qubole, Infosys, and Intel. You can follow him on twitter @razatweets

speakers.name: John Fragalla
speakers.bio: John Fragalla has been with NVIDIA since March 2024. With over two decades of experience in the high performance computing industry, John has established himself as an expert in system architectures, storage solutions, and performance optimization.

speakers.name: Daniel Glogowski
speakers.bio:
Daniel Glogowski is a senior technical product manager in the NVIDIA Enterprise product group, working on NVIDIA’s AI Data Platform and Blueprints. 
Previously, he was VP of Product at Robust Intelligence (acquired by Cisco), focusing on AI risk and security. 
He has worked on data-driven platforms, with experience in business intelligence and AI Ops. Daniel holds an MBA from the MIT Sloan School of Management.

speakers.name: Ruchika Kharwar
speakers.bio: Ruchika Kharwar is a product manager at NVIDIA with extensive experience in cloud computing and systems engineering. Before this, she was a customer engineer at Google, driving customer success and the adoption of Google Cloud Platform for nearly six years. Her background also includes roles at RedHat as a cloud success architect and at Texas Instruments in software development. Ruchika has earned certifications like Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect, Data Engineer, and others. She excels at bridging technical and customer-facing roles to ensure successful technology adoption and customer satisfaction.

speakers.name: Jacob Liberman
speakers.bio: Jacob Liberman is a manager in the Enterprise Product group. His team builds NVIDIA AI blueprints, agentic AI platforms, and open-source connectors to leading generative AI programming framworks. Previously, he held product management and engineering roles at Red Hat, AMD, and Dell.

speakers.name: Janaki Vamaraju
speakers.bio: Janaki Vamaraju is a senior deep learning architect and scientist developing generative and large language models for IT enterprise use-cases. Before NVIDIA, she was an AI researcher at Meta and Shell Global Research. Janaki has a Ph.D. in computational sciences and applied math from the University of Texas at Austin. She also holds two master’s degrees, in mathematics and computational mechanics.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod B (L2)

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Building L4 Autonomy
sessions.abstract:
Connect with NVIDIA experts to explore a seamless cloud-to-car pipeline—from training and simulation to real-world deployment. Discover the latest breakthroughs in scalable Level 2++ to L4 autonomy and get direct access to the engineers building the future of driving.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Matt Cragun
speakers.bio: Matt Cragun is the director of the Autonomous Vehicle Infrastructure product at NVIDIA. For the past decade, he has worked in the automotive and racing industries. Matt holds master’s degrees in business and mechanical engineering from MIT.

speakers.name: Boris Ivanovic
speakers.bio: Boris Ivanovic is a senior research scientist and manager in NVIDIA’s Autonomous Vehicle Research Group, developing novel end-to-end AV architectures, training strategies, sensor and traffic simulation, AI safety, and the integration of foundation models in AV development. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he received his Ph.D. in aeronautics and astronautics and an M.S. in computer science, both from Stanford University.

speakers.name: Riccardo Mariani
speakers.bio: Riccardo Mariani is the vice president of industry safety at NVIDIA and leads the NVIDIA Halos initiative. He's responsible for developing cohesive safety strategies and cross-segment safety processes, architecture, and products that can be leveraged across NVIDIA’s artificial intelligence-based hardware and software platforms.

speakers.name: Itai Zadok
speakers.bio: Itai Zadok is a senior product manager at NVIDIA, leading neural reconstruction and world foundation model products for autonomous vehicle simulation. Itai holds a bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering from Tel Aviv University and a joint M.B.A.-AI degree from the Kellogg School of Management and the McCormick School of Engineering at Northwestern University.

speakers.name: Bill Veenhuis
speakers.bio: Bill Veenhuis, principal solutions architect in the Enterprise Automotive Group at NVIDIA, focuses on integrating NVIDIA technologies with vehicles to provide driver assistance to self-driving vehicles. Prior involvements included multi-touch panel software, graphics and animation, and high performance computing, all leveraging NVIDIA GPU acceleration technologies. Bill serves on the Steering Committee of Michigan Modeling, Simulation, Visualization and Immersive Technologies in southeast Michigan and is an active member in both the Society of Automotive Engineers and the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics. He holds a B.S. in aerospace engineering from University or Arizona.

speakers.name: Nikhil Hoskeri
speakers.bio: Nikhil Hoskeri is a Principal Product Manager for NVIDIA Hyperion, leading the product definition of the Hyperion architecture—a set of foundational platform architectures for building autonomous vehicles. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he spent the last decade and a half at General Motors, working on architecture for autonomous systems and software-defined vehicles. Nikhil holds an M.Eng. in Global Automotive and Manufacturing Engineering from the University of Michigan.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation

sessions.title: Everything You Want to Ask About NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architectures
sessions.abstract: Engage directly with NVIDIA specialists to discuss the NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architecture program in this informal "Connect With Experts" session. This is a unique opportunity to ask questions, share insights, and gain a deeper understanding of how these reference architectures and accompanying deployment guides for Networking, Infrastructure Software, Observability, and Sizing help our global systems partners to streamline enterprise AI deployments. This session is ideal for IT professionals, AI system architects, and anyone interested in leveraging NVIDIA's cutting-edge technologies to deploy AI in the enterprise. Don’t miss this chance to connect with experts and deepen your understanding of NVIDIA's innovative enterprise AI solutions!

speakers.name: Satinder Nijjar
speakers.bio: Satinder is a technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA with over 25 years of experience in solution architecture and infrastructure design. His expertise spans an array of data center technologies, including high-speed I/O virtualization, networking, and cloud infrastructure. In the Enterprise Platforms group, he eagerly leads his team to develop scalable solutions in the data center, an effort enhanced by prior experience in senior roles at Oracle working on virtual networking and private cloud appliance.

speakers.name: Peter Lillian
speakers.bio: Peter Lillian is a director of product management in the NVIDIA Enterprise Platforms and Solutions organization. Leveraging his 20 years of data center solutions experience, he has a passion for combining hardware, software, and infrastructure to create compelling, customer-driven solutions. Past experience with Acceleration Technologies, HPC, Linux, High Performance Storage, Networking, and numerous product management roles has provided him with a strong foundation to establish creative ways to solve customer challenges.

speakers.name: Matthew Hausmann
speakers.bio: Matt Hausmann is a product marketer for enterprise platforms at NVIDIA. He focuses on the enterprise go-to-market strategy and solutions for NVIDIA's data center portfolio, which includes accelerated computing solutions, networking platforms, and AI software. Matt brings to NVIDIA experience in product marketing, analytics consulting, and applications engineering from tech companies including HPE, Dell, and Teradata. He holds an electrical engineering and computer science degree from MIT.

speakers.name: Richard Hastie
speakers.bio: Richard is senior director for NVIDIA Enterprise Platforms. He has over 25 years of experience in IT, with a current focus on accelerated computing and NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architectures. He works with global system partners to help enterprise customers deploy AI infrastructure at scale—while ensuring high performance, faster time-to-market, and lower costs. He is passionate about generative AI and its potential to transform industries.

speakers.name: Anita Tragler
speakers.bio: Anita Tragler is a product manager in the NVIDIA Enterprise Product Group, focused on scaling and sizing AI workloads for the NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architecture program. Her passion and expertise are in performance benchmarking and scaling of AI/ML and HPC workloads on cloud-native (Kubernetes) infrastructure to meet enterprise and telco needs. Prior to NVIDIA, Anita worked as a product manager at NetApp and Red Hat, scaling cloud-native applications on-premises and in the cloud. In her free time, she enjoys the outdoors, hiking, biking, and traveling.

speakers.name: Abhishek Prabhakar
speakers.bio: Abhishek is a senior architect with 19 years of expertise in designing, implementing, and managing sophisticated data center solutions. Abhishek is skilled in virtualization technologies, cloud computing, and infrastructure design, with a deep understanding of architectural principles and best practices.

speakers.name: Leigh Engel
speakers.bio: Leigh Engel serves as a senior product manager within the NVIDIA Enterprise Platforms team, overseeing the development of products designed to accelerate data center computing, including the NVIDIA Enterprise Reference Architectures. Previously, she worked as a product manager on AI teams at Apple (AI/ML) and WPP (EDG), specializing in large language model customization.

speakers.name: Shashank Sabhlok
speakers.bio: Shashank Sabhlok is a senior product manager in the NVIDIA Enterprise Product Group, where he leads initiatives around AI factory design and enterprise adoption to drive scalable, high-performance AI across organizations. Prior to this, he was the lead product manager for IBM watsonx.governance, IBM’s generative AI governance solution, where he successfully led the product from inception to launch, and beyond. He holds an M.B.Ai. (MBA + AI) degree from Northwestern University’s Kellogg School of Management and McCormick School of Engineering, and a B.A.Sc. in electrical engineering with distinction from the University of Waterloo.

speakers.name: John Fragalla
speakers.bio: John Fragalla has been with NVIDIA since March 2024. With over two decades of experience in the high performance computing industry, John has established himself as an expert in system architectures, storage solutions, and performance optimization.

speakers.name: Manvender Rawat
speakers.bio: Manvender is a Sr. Manager of Product Management for NVIDIA Enterprise Platform & Solutions group with more than 20 years’ experience and a background in kernel programming, Storage and GPU virtualization. He is responsible for NVIDIA’s software Enterprise reference architectures and AI workload benchmarking & sizing for large-scale clusters designed for high performance. Manvender has a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science from University of Pune.

speakers.name: Zoe Huczok
speakers.bio: Zoe specializes in enabling the value of full AI stacks for a broad range of enterprise use cases. Her strong interest in process design and productivity levers  is informed by her experience advising industry leaders on growth, particularly in telecommunications and retail. She champions enterprise AI adoption through partnerships with GSIs, ISVs, and OEMs.

speakers.name: Boskey Savla
speakers.bio: Boskey Savla is a Product Manager at NVIDIA focusing on defining benchmarks and architectures for LLMs, Agentic flows for Enterprise customers, she has 18 years of experience in systems and operations. She started as a Linux Sys Admin and moved on to solution engineering and system engineering role focusing on Virtual , PaaS, Cloud and Kubernetes based solutions. She is the Author of the book ‘Kubernetes on vSphere for Dummies’ and has spoken and conducted workshops at various events like VMworld, AWS Re:Invent, and Kubecon.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.title: Accelerate Video Workloads Using NVENC/NVDEC Across Gaming, Pro-Viz, Streaming, and the Data Center
sessions.abstract:
Stop by our session to explore video workflows accelerated by NVIDIA's dedicated video encoders (NVENC) and decoders (NVDEC). GPUs with dedicated video codecs power AI video pipelines, training, and inference alongside gaming, professional video production, streaming, and data center applications. 

Product managers, engineers, solution architects, and DevTech experts will be on hand to dive into our video feature sets, troubleshoot pipelines, or answer any video-related questions you have.

speakers.name: Abhijit Patait
speakers.bio: Abhijit Patait is the senior director of software codec technology and AI software at NVIDIA, responsible for audio/video technologies, algorithms, SDKs, drivers, and cloud applications. Prior to NVIDIA, Abhijit worked in senior management roles at Motorola, Ericsson, and Ditech Networks (acquired by Nuance/Microsoft). Abhijit is a regular speaker at GTC. He holds an M.S. in electrical engineering from Missouri S&T University and an M.B.A. from the University of California, Berkeley.

speakers.name: Prathap Muthana
speakers.bio: Prathap Muthana focuses on video technologies and data center products. He's worked at NVIDIA for over 15 years. Before becoming a product manager, Prathap worked in the hardware engineering group, focusing on signal integrity, power distribution, and substrate designs. He holds a graduate degree in engineering from Georgia Tech and an M.B.A. from Cornell University.

speakers.name: Hiba Yousef
speakers.bio: Hiba Yousef is a Senior Solutions Architect at NVIDIA, where she works with media and entertainment organizations to design and deploy next-generation video solutions at the intersection of video, AI, and real-time performance. Her expertise spans broadcast and OTT media systems, adaptive streaming, CDN architectures, video transcoding, and industry standards. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Hiba held roles across research and industry in R&D, broadcast architecture, and software development. She holds a PhD in video streaming optimization, a Master’s in Multimedia Networking, and an engineering degree in Telecommunications and Electronics.

speakers.name: Aryan Yaghoubian
speakers.bio: Aryan Yaghoubian is a Senior Video Performance Engineer at NVIDIA with 10 years of experience in video technology. He specializes in evaluating and optimizing the performance and quality of NVIDIA video encode and decode across a broad range of products and workloads, spanning data center/server GPUs to consumer GPUs.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: Agentic AI II: Production Deployment, Safety and Scale
sessions.abstract: This session focuses on the deployment of agents in production, and hard engineering required to run models and agents at scale. Our experts will discuss the considerations of hosting open models using high-performance inference engines on your own infrastructure, as well as incorporating frontier models in your workflows. We discuss optimizing, inference speed, and reducing costs as well as implementing rigorous safety guardrails, along with evaluating and auditing models in production.

speakers.name: Aditi Bodhankar
speakers.bio: Aditi Bodhankar is a developer advocate for deep learning at NVIDIA, where she focuses on building and advancing security for generative AI applications. She holds a master’s degree from the University of Southern California and is passionate about empowering enterprises to harness the potential of Gen AI technologies in innovative ways.

speakers.name: Chris Alexiuk
speakers.bio: Chris Alexiuk is a Sr. Product Research Engineer at NVIDIA, working on creating technical assets that help developers use the incredible suite of AI tools available at NVIDIA. Chris comes from a machine learning and data science background, and he is obsessed with everything and anything about large language models.

speakers.name: Neal Vaidya
speakers.bio: Neal Vaidya is a technical marketing engineer for deep learning software at NVIDIA. He's responsible for developing and presenting developer-focused content on deep learning frameworks and inference solutions. He holds a bachelor’s degree in statistics from Duke University.

speakers.name: Aastha Jhunjhunwala
speakers.bio: Aastha is a solutions architect in the NVIDIA AI Enterprise team, helping customers build optimized end-to-end large language model applications by leveraging NVIDIA's hardware and software stack. She collaborates across product and engineering teams to help a wide variety of customers develop and optimize their LLM workflows.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Accelerate the AI Factory With NVIDIA-Certified Systems
sessions.abstract:
NVIDIA-Certified Systems provide the essential foundation for the modern AI factory, offering performance-optimized hardware validated to run demanding workloads from the desktop to the data center and edge. This session explores how the program’s rigorous testing—now including NVIDIA-Certified Storage for massive data demands—ensures enterprise-grade reliability, security, and scalability for generative AI and industrial digitalization. Join us to learn how these certified servers, workstations, and edge systems provide a trusted, best-practice pathway for enterprises to confidently deploy accelerated computing at scale.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Tiffany Xie
speakers.bio: Tiffany Xie is a Product Manager on NVIDIA‑Certified Systems (NV Cert) team, serving as a primary point of contact for OEM partners and internal stakeholders across certification operations. She owns high‑volume NV Cert bug triage, leads technical log review and weekly debug forums, and drives end‑to‑end certification workflows from system submission through final publication. Tiffany also maintains core NV Cert documentation and tracking, helping align engineering, lab operations, product, and marketing teams so partners can bring NVIDIA‑Certified platforms to market reliably and on schedule.

speakers.name: Steve Stein
speakers.bio: Steve is a product marketing manager with a decade of experience selling AI and HPC systems, as well as developing next-generation space technologies for the U.S. Air Force. He collaborates with global system partners to assist enterprise customers in deploying AI infrastructure at scale, ensuring high performance, faster time-to-market, and reduced costs. Steve is passionate about generative AI and its transformative potential across industries.

speakers.name: Greg Crider
speakers.bio: Greg is the lead product manager for the NVIDIA-Certified Systems program, where he oversees program definition, partner system assessment, and quarterly release management. With nearly 40 years of experience, Greg has a record of introducing cutting-edge technology to the market through diverse partners and channels.

speakers.name: Manjeet Bhatia
speakers.bio: Manjeet Bhatia is a Technical Product Manager on NVIDIA’s NVIDIA-Certified Systems (NV Cert) team. He focuses on scaling the NV Cert program to rack-level systems and expanding system performance testing to accelerate certification and improve repeatability. He has 10+ years of experience in data center systems and has worked on systems and infrastructure initiatives including SR-IOV enablement in OpenStack, CI/CD automation for hardware testing, Kubernetes enablement for CPU offload accelerators and end-to-end performance optimization.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod F (L2)
Connect With the Experts Pod A (L2)

sessions.title: Optimize Deep Learning Kernels on NVIDIA GPUs With FlashInfer and cuDNN
sessions.abstract:
Modern AI workloads live or die by the performance of a handful of critical GPU kernels. In this Connect With the Experts session, there are no slides—just open, in-depth discussions with NVIDIA engineers about FlashInfer, cuDNN, and the optimized kernels that power today’s models. Bring your hardest performance questions, unusual model shapes, and kernel-level challenges to talk through how choices around attention, Tensor operations, memory access patterns, fusion, and precision affect behavior on current GPU architectures. The conversation will center on your real-world scenarios: what limits you’re hitting, what you’ve already tried, and which directions are most promising to explore next. You’ll walk away with concrete ideas and heuristics you can apply later with your own tools and workflows, plus pointers to resources for going deeper.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Yang Xu
speakers.bio: Yang Xu graduated from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign with a Ph.D. in medical imaging and image processing in August 2017. He's the engineering manager of the cuDNN Frontend and Fuser Team at NVIDIA. His team specializes in runtime high-performance CUDA kernel generation and fusion for various networks, including large language models and convolutional neural networks.

speakers.name: Brian Ryu
speakers.bio: Brian is a senior software engineer in the cuDNN team at NVIDIA. His primary focus within the team is profiling and improving the accuracy of heuristic recommendations, as well as developing timing models for predicting kernel execution times. Brian graduated with a Ph.D. from Stanford University.

speakers.name: Rajas Mhaskar
speakers.bio: Rajas is a senior software engineer with the cuDNN team at NVIDIA. He focuses on performance optimizations for memory bound fusion graphs across different GPU architectures and works on their integration into popular deep learning frameworks and compilers.

speakers.name: Anerudhan Gopal
speakers.bio: Anerudhan is a senior software engineer with the cuDNN team at NVIDIA. Anerudhan develops and maintains the github.com/NVIDIA/cudnn-frontend/ repository to ease the adoption of cudnn into your application.

speakers.name: Joe Halabi
speakers.bio: Joe is a senior software engineer in the cuDNN group at NVIDIA. Joe develops and maintains the cuDNN build and packaging infrastructure with a focus on enhancing the library’s end-user and developer experience. He graduated with a master’s degree in computer science from UCLA.

speakers.name: Vedaanta Agarwalla
speakers.bio: Vedaanta is a senior software engineer at NVIDIA in the cuDNN team. He focuses on generating efficient kernels at runtime for deep learning primitives and their integration into popular frameworks and compilers.

speakers.name: Yanqin Zhai
speakers.bio: Yanqin is a senior systems software engineer at NVIDIA. Yanqin develops cuDNN runtime fusion engines to create the most efficient GEMM and LLM kernels at runtime for NVIDIA's Ampere, Ada Lovelace, Hopper, Blackwell, and future GPU architectures.

speakers.name: Yunzhe Qiu
speakers.bio: Yunzhe Qiu is working on cuDNN heuristic and flashinfer model performance optimization.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

sessions.title: Claws: How to Build Safe and Secure Long Running Agents
sessions.abstract:
Running today’s self-evolving AI agents is fundamentally insecure. Agents such as OpenClaw operate with real system access but lack a robust, infrastructure-level security model. Tool-level restrictions can be bypassed when agents discover alternate execution paths, and container or prompt-based guardrails are insufficient for long-running systems. In this session, experts will discuss infrastructure-level security and runtime platforms for deploying autonomous agents more securely by default.

Important: Connect With the Experts sessions are interactive sessions that give you a unique opportunity to meet, in either a group or one-on-one setting, with the minds behind NVIDIA’s products and research to get your questions answered. Attendees can meet with experts on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: Alex Watson
speakers.bio: Alex Watson is senior director of AI software at NVIDIA, leading product teams to deliver agentic AI solutions. He joined from Gretel, where as co-founder and CPO he led a 50-plus person team pioneering synthetic data generation for AI model training. Earlier, he founded harvest.ai, acquired by AWS, where he helped grow Amazon Macie into one of its fastest-growing AI services. He began his career in applied research at the U.S. National Security Agency.

speakers.name: Nader Khalil
speakers.bio: Nader Khalil is director of developer tech, building great developer experiences across NVIDIA products. He was co-founder and CEO of Brev.dev, which was acquired by NVIDIA in July 2024. Nader is a second-time founder, having gone through YC W20 for his first startup, and has a degree in computer engineering from the University of California, Santa Barbara.

speakers.name: Carter Abdallah
speakers.bio: Carter Abdallah is a senior developer technology engineer at NVIDIA, creating generative AI developer tooling and infrastructure. He has a degree in computer engineering from Georgia Tech and was the founding engineer of startup Brev.Dev, which was acquired by NVIDIA in July 2024. Carter also gives advice to the future developers of the world with his social media platform of over 800,000 aspiring or new software engineers.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Ask the NVCC Experts: CUDA Compiler Language, Performance, and Tuning
sessions.abstract:
Join us for an interactive Connect with Experts session focused on the NVCC CUDA Compiler Toolchain—the core engine behind CUDA C++ language support, compiler functionality, and performance optimization on NVIDIA platforms.

NVCC compiler experts will be available to discuss topics including CUDA language features, compilation workflows, optimization strategies, and performance tuning considerations across different workloads and architectures. Bring real-world questions, challenges, or areas of curiosity: from understanding compilation decisions and generated code to navigating advanced optimization and debugging scenarios.

speakers.name: Nikhil Gupta
speakers.bio:
Nikhil Gupta is an engineering manager on NVIDIA’s CUDA Compiler team, where he leads development of key components of the CUDA C++ toolchain, including NVCC, the NVRTC runtime compiler, and the CUDA device linker and object tools used to build GPU applications.

He has worked on the CUDA compiler stack for over seven years, helping improve compile-time performance, modern C++ support, and the scalability of large CUDA builds. His work spans the CUDA C++ front end, compilation infrastructure, and device linking technologies that power GPU software across AI, high-performance computing, and accelerated data platforms.

Nikhil is passionate about making CUDA C++ more powerful and productive for developers, and works closely with teams across NVIDIA to evolve the CUDA programming model and support the next generation of GPU computing.

speakers.name: Vatsa Santhanam
speakers.bio: Vatsa Santhanam serves as a senior director in NVIDIA’s Compiler Organization. The organization he leads is responsible for the CUDA C++ and OpenCL compute compilers, the NVVM high-level optimizer (based on the LLVM optimization framework), as well as the Vulkan/SPIR-V and DirectX/DXIL  graphics shader compilers. He has been with NVIDIA since 2011, and previously served as the director of the Java, Compilers and Tools R&D Lab at Hewlett-Packard Corp.

speakers.name: Howard Chen (SW-GPU)
speakers.bio: Howard is a compiler engineering manager at NVIDIA.  He holds a Ph.D. in computer science and engineering from the University of Minnesota.

speakers.name: Anshu Das Gupta
speakers.bio: Anshu is a senior director of engineering at NVIDIA. He leads teams focused on CUDA C++ and compiler front‑end technologies, including NVVM, Clang, and MLIR, with active engagement in the LLVM open‑source community. Anshu's work and interests span compiler front-end and back-end architecture, language front ends, and intermediate representation design, with an emphasis on enabling high‑performance and portable programming models across NVIDIA platforms. Previously, Anshu led the teams responsible for compilers and development tools at Qualcomm supporting CPUs, neural signal processors, and custom microcontrollers.

speakers.name: Jerry Zheng
speakers.bio: Jerry Zheng is an Engineering Director in NVIDIA’s Compiler Organization. He holds a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from McGill university, Canada. He leads the compiler team responsible for optimizing GPU kernels and performing device code generations.

speakers.name: Mike Murphy
speakers.bio: Mike Murphy leads the work on linkers, fatbins, and ELF at NVIDIA.  He helped launch the initial CUDA compiler and has been working on it ever since.

schedule.room: Connect With the Experts Pod C (L2)

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: How to Run AI-Powered Computer-Aided Engineering Simulations
sessions.abstract:
In this lab, we explore how to build AI surrogate models for crash simulations, one of the most computationally intensive tasks in computer aided-engineering (CAE). Learn how modern AI architectures can reproduce high-fidelity physics simulations at a fraction of the cost, enabling faster design exploration and decision-making. We'll walk through the full workflow for developing an AI surrogate for CAE applications, including data preparation and analysis, training and optimization, loading and running inference with pre-trained models, and evaluating model accuracy and uncertainty. We'll also discuss how AI-accelerated workflows are transforming CAE across various industries and areas of research, and how these tools can integrate seamlessly into existing engineering pipelines.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of engineering or scientific modeling workflows and the associated technical terminology
  • Basic programming knowledge in Python (data structures, functions, basic libraries)
  • Understanding of deep learning concepts (neural networks, training, inference)
  • Experience with PyTorch or similar ML frameworks
  • Experience with Linux command line operations
  • Basic understanding of data preprocessing techniques
  • Basic knowledge of parallel computing concepts
  • Understanding of model validation and verification methods

speakers.name: Mark Hobbs
speakers.bio: Mark works as a solutions architect at NVIDIA, specializing in energy and computer-aided engineering. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he worked as a technical specialist in the Future Methods group at Rolls-Royce. He previously held a postdoctoral research fellowship at The Alan Turing Institute, and earned his Ph.D. in computational mechanics from the University of Cambridge.

speakers.name: Pablo Hermoso-Moreno
speakers.bio: Pablo is a senior solutions architect focused on CAE and AI for physical simulation, surrogate modeling, and design optimization. He graduated in aerospace engineering from Imperial College London and completed his master’s thesis at Caltech on physics-informed machine learning. He later worked at Mercedes F1 as an aerodynamics engineer before joining AWS as a senior data scientist delivering AI/ML solutions for large enterprises.

speakers.name: Ira Shokar
speakers.bio: Ira works on CAE and Earth-2 at NVIDIA, focusing on AI for physical simulation, surrogate modeling, and design optimization with PhysicsNeMo. Before joining NVIDIA, he completed a Ph.D. in applied mathematics at the University of Cambridge, where he developed probabilistic deep learning emulators for chaotic and turbulent fluid flows.

sessions.title: Hands-On With Earth-2: Building Local and National Weather Resilience With AI
sessions.abstract:
We explore the life cycle of AI-based high-resolution regional weather forecasting applications, which are used by businesses and governments to achieve independent local weather and climate prediction capabilities at high accuracy and unprecedented speed. We'll start by creating a custom high-resolution model, guiding you through preparing a training dataset, training a downscaling diffusion model with NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo, and packaging it for real-time use. We then use NVIDIA Earth2Studio to integrate our custom model with a global AI-based weather forecasting model, such as FourCastNet 3, to build a full regional forecasting workflow, enabling sharper and more actionable local forecasts. We'll then discuss leveraging custom weather observations to improve the accuracy of the predictions, and deploying the workflow in operations so it can be productized as an inference service.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Previous DLI: https://learn.nvidia.com/courses/course-detail?course_id=course-v1:DLI+S-FX-31+V1
  • Basic Python and Deep learning experience.

speakers.name: Georg Ertl
speakers.bio: Georg Ertl works as a solutions architect on the NVIDIA Earth-2 project. He holds degrees in meteorology from the University of Hamburg and in industrial engineering from the Karlsruhe Institute of Technology. He's worked for a commodity analytics business at the intersection of weather, data science, and its applications to energy markets. Georg enjoys helping clients make use of the powerful suite of Earth-2 tools.

speakers.name: Ira Shokar
speakers.bio: Ira works on CAE and Earth-2 at NVIDIA, focusing on AI for physical simulation, surrogate modeling, and design optimization with PhysicsNeMo. Before joining NVIDIA, he completed a Ph.D. in applied mathematics at the University of Cambridge, where he developed probabilistic deep learning emulators for chaotic and turbulent fluid flows.

speakers.name: Nicholas Geneva
speakers.bio: Nicholas Geneva is a senior software engineer for NVIDIA Modulus and related packages. Nicholas received his Ph.D. from the University of Notre Dame, researching the integration of deep learning with the prediction of physical systems. His present research interests are deep learning methods for modeling physics, AI for weather/climate prediction, and uncertainty quantification.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling

sessions.title: Build Surgical and Medical Robotics With NVIDIA Isaac for Healthcare: From Simulation to Deployment
sessions.abstract:
Gain hands-on experience in setting up simulation environments, generating and converting synthetic medical data, modifying scenes and assets, collecting and curating datasets via state-machine demonstrations, fine-tuning the GR00T N1 Vision-Language-Action (VLA) model, and testing robotic ultrasound tasks in simulation. We emphasize safe, efficient AI model development and deployment for healthcare applications.

Prerequisite(s):

  • IsaacSim and IsaacLab foundational experience.

  • Python and terminal experience.

  • General understanding of deep learning, and vision language action models.

speakers.name: Maximilian Ofir
speakers.bio: Maximilian Ofir is a technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA. His work focuses on AI-enhanced medical imaging applications with Holoscan and Isaac Lab. Max completed his M.S. in robotics, cognition, and intelligence at the Technical University Munich.

sessions.title: Profiling Python and AI workloads with Nsight Compute
sessions.abstract:
Learn how to use the Nsight Compute tool to profile CUDA and AI GPU kernels in the Python framework of your choice: Numba, Triton or PyTorch. Understand the optimization workflows provided by NVIDIA Nsight Compute UI and its ncu command line interface. Use the new scoreboard dependency analysis view and source page improvements like opcode to metric pipeline associations. Learn how to use the Nsight Jupyterlab Extension for profiling and streaming reports directly in Jupyter. Try the latest Nsight Python decorators for profiling from within any Python script.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate knowledge of CUDA

speakers.name: Felix Schmitt
speakers.bio: Felix Schmitt is a Principal System Software Engineer on NVIDIA's Developer Tools team. He's developing the CUDA performance analysis tool Nsight Compute. Prior projects include performance APIs and correctness checkers for GPUs. Before joining NVIDIA, he worked as a research associate investigating novel performance analysis tools and techniques. He holds a master's degree in computer science from Dresden University of Technology, Germany.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Profilers / Debuggers / Code Analysis

sessions.title: Supercharge Tabular ML Models With GPU-Accelerated Feature Engineering
sessions.abstract:
Fast experimentation in feature engineering is essential to quickly discover the most valuable features that improve model performance. In this tutorial, we leverage NVIDIA cuDF and cuML libraries to accelerate the experimentation pipeline on GPUs with their zero-code change features, enabling faster feature engineering and quicker development of more accurate models.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of Python and structured data.

speakers.name: Chris Deotte
speakers.bio: After earning a B.A. in mathematics, Chris worked as a graphic artist, photographer, carpenter, and teacher. He earned a Ph.D. in computational science and mathematics with a thesis on optimizing parallel processing, and now works as a senior data scientist with NVIDIA. Chris is a quadruple Kaggle Grandmaster.

speakers.name: Ronay Ak
speakers.bio: Ronay Ak's current focus is information retrieval systems and retrieval augmented generation applications. She was part of the teams that won past RecSys competitions: WSDM WebTour Workshop Challenge 2021 by Booking.com and the SIGIR eCommerceWorkshop Data Challenge 2021 by Coveo. Ronay's authored over 20 technical publications published in prestigious conferences and journals. She received her Ph.D. in energy and power systems engineering from CentraleSupelec in France.

sessions.title: Build Domain AI Agents that Accurately Use Tools with Reinforcement Learning
sessions.abstract:
In this hands-on lab, we will create adaptive, tool-using AI agents that learn through trial, error, and feedback, moving beyond static prompting. We’ll work with NVIDIA NeMo Gym and NeMo RL open libraries, and post-train Nemotron open models to build domain-specific agents capable of mastering complex workflows and tool-use using reinforcement learning (RL).

As a practical and engaging challenge, we’ll build and train an agent to play a “Wordle” game. While the game is lighthearted, attendees experience the same enterprise-grade stack and development patterns that can scale to real-world applications such as autonomous coding, applied reasoning, scientific discovery, and to any custom use case. Together, we’ll walk through the full RL lifecycle - from building custom simulations/ RL environments and designing verifiable rewards to iterative training and performance evaluation - developing the foundations needed to build high-performance, domain AI agents that continually learn and adapt.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic experience with Python

  • A general understanding of LLMs or agent workflows or Generative AI concepts (no deep ML background required)

  • Familiarity with REST APIs or function-calling concepts is helpful but not mandatory

  • Optional but beneficial: Exposure to NVIDIA NeMo, containerized development (Docker), or cloud workflows

  • No prior experience with Nemotron models or NeMo is required.

speakers.name: Shashank Verma
speakers.bio: Shashank Verma is a deep learning technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA who's responsible for developing and presenting developer-focused content on various deep learning frameworks, specializing in generative AI and inference optimization. He obtained his master's in electrical engineering from the University of Wisconsin-Madison, where he focused on computer vision, security aspects in data science, and HPC.

speakers.name: Yang Yu
speakers.bio: Yang specializes in Gen AI development at NVIDIA. He leverages NVIDIA technologies to help enterprise customers build and deploy GPU-accelerated Gen AI platforms/applications at scale. He started his career as an applied AI researcher focusing on physics-informed deep learning. Prior to NVIDIA, he worked as an applied scientist at Raytheon Technologies.

speakers.name: Sylendran Arunagiri
speakers.bio: Sylendran is a Technical Marketing Manager on the Agentic AI team at NVIDIA. With a background in AI/ML and data science, he specializes in creating technical assets and activations for the global AI developer community. Before moving into product management and marketing, Sylendran worked as a software developer, giving him a hands-on perspective on building and deploying AI systems. Today, he focuses on designing go-to-market strategies and working with the broader AI ecosystem to help developers implement agentic capabilities.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models

sessions.title: The Kaggle Grandmasters Playbook: Battle-Tested Modeling Techniques for Tabular Data
sessions.abstract:
Our workshop highlights proven strategies for tabular data developed by NVIDIA’s Kaggle grandmasters, who have earned top honors in hundreds of international data science competitions. You'll practice rapid electronic design automation (EDA), large-scale feature engineering, model building, ensembling, and pseudo-labeling — all accelerated with GPUs for faster experimentation and better accuracy.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic Python knowledge and basic understanding of machine learning models and tabular data.

speakers.name: Chris Deotte
speakers.bio: After earning a B.A. in mathematics, Chris worked as a graphic artist, photographer, carpenter, and teacher. He earned a Ph.D. in computational science and mathematics with a thesis on optimizing parallel processing, and now works as a senior data scientist with NVIDIA. Chris is a quadruple Kaggle Grandmaster.

speakers.name: Gilberto Titericz Junior
speakers.bio: Gilberto Titericz is a senior data scientist at NVIDIA and a triple Kaggle grandmaster.

sessions.title: Accelerating LLM Training and Inferencing With Reduced Precision Format
sessions.abstract:
Large-scale LLMs require enormous computational resources, making efficiency crucial for training. Learn how cutting-edge reduced-precision formats such as FP8, MXFP4 and NVFP4 can dramatically accelerate pre-training, post-training, and inference while maintaining accuracy close to full-precision baseline models. You’ll learn how NVIDIA's innovative NVFP4 format better captures dynamic range and reduces quantization error compared to MXFP4. We'll cover techniques including Random Hadamard Transforms, two-dimensional block quantization, stochastic rounding, and strategic placement of higher-precision layers to stabilize training. Gain practical skills with the software tools necessary for quantization-aware training and efficient inference deployment, helping your applications retain accuracy while benefiting from reduced precision.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Understanding of LLM training and inference
  • Basic understandings of reduced precision formats
  • Python, docker and shell scripting skills

speakers.name: Leo Du
speakers.bio: Leo Du is a senior generative AI solutions architect at NVIDIA, with prior experience as an algorithm engineer at ASML. He has rich experience in the semiconductor industry. He helps partners design AI infrastructure, and architect and implement generative AI enterprise software and products. His expertise spans LLMs, domain-adaptive pre-training, post-training, reinforcement learning, and computer vision. Outside work, he enjoys cycling and hiking.

speakers.name: Oleg Rybakov
speakers.bio: Oleg is principal software engineer at NVIDIA, working on deep learning models training and inference optimization. He has industry experience in computer vision, recommender systems, and speech recognition with Samsung Electronics, Amazon, and Google.

speakers.name: Sergio Perez
speakers.bio: Sergio Perez is a solutions architect at NVIDIA working on training and inference of LLMs. Sergio collaborates with developers of supercomputing centers, startups, and large corporations to accelerate their AI workloads. Sergio focuses on model compression, leveraging pruning, distillation, and numerical formats like FP8 or NVFP4 to speed up AI. Sergio holds a Ph.D. in computational fluid dynamics from Imperial College London.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)

sessions.title: Powering Specialized Agents: Architecting Synthetic Data Pipelines with NVIDIA Data Designer
sessions.abstract:
Building a truly performant LLM agent means fine-tuning for your unique use-case. This requires training data that span multiple specialized domains or tools; for most real-world applications, the data you need simply don't exist. This hands-on workshop leverages NVIDIA Data Designer, our recently open-sourced, state-of-the-art framework, to bridge this gap. We'll cover the latest techniques developed at NVIDIA for producing high-quality reasoning data, including STEM and tool-calling workflows used to scale Nemotron models. By the end of this session, you will gain the skills to speed up data experimentation and drive innovation in your own AI projects.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic Python and Jupyter notebooks

  • Training or fine-tuning LLMs (conceptually)

  • Using datasets for supervised fine-tuning and evaluation (classification, generation, or instruction-following tasks)

  • Optional: prior exposure to NVIDIA NeMo, Hugging Face datasets/models, or other LLM fine-tuning stacks.

speakers.name: Johnny Greco
speakers.bio: Johnny is an AI research scientist at NVIDIA, where he leads the development of the NeMo Data Designer library. He specializes in synthetic data generation, with a focus on translating complex research into products that are practically useful and a joy to use. Previously, Johnny was a professional astronomer, and he holds a Ph.D. in astrophysics from Princeton University.

speakers.name: Yev Meyer
speakers.bio: Yev Meyer is a principal research scientist at NVIDIA, focusing on applied research on data generation models and synthetic dataset creation. Prior to NVIDIA, he was the chief scientist at Gretel, the synthetic data platform for developers. Yev also led AI/ML teams at enterprise software-as-a-service startups Guru, Curalate, and RJMetrics (precursor to dbt Labs, Stitch). He holds a Ph.D. in computational neuroscience.

speakers.name: Kirit Thadaka
speakers.bio: Kirit Thadaka is a product manager at NVIDIA focused on eliminating data bottlenecks in enterprise AI adoption. With over a decade of AI/ML experience across startups and large enterprises, Kirit has deep expertise in technical leadership, core research and development, and solution architecture. He specializes in building innovative AI/ML platforms that help organizations harness the full potential of artificial intelligence.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Optimize PyTorch Models for High-Performance Inference with Nsight Deep Learning Designer
sessions.abstract:
Learn how to use a graphical user interface-based integrated development environment (IDE) purpose-built for deep neural network developers to manage the end-to-end process of going from PyTorch to a deployment-ready model that yields the best inference performance. Learn how to use a GPU-based performance profiler to guide sound decision-making for model optimizations.

Prerequisite(s):

  • The core concepts of PyTorch and Deep Learning are needed to get through this course.

speakers.name: Manoj Kumar-Yennapureddy
speakers.bio: Manoj Kumar Yennapureddy is a senior deep learning engineer at NVIDIA, where he contributes to the DL Designer platform. Before joining NVIDIA, he worked as a perception engineer at Motional, an autonomous vehicle startup. Manoj holds a Ph.D. in physics from the University of Arizona.

sessions.title: Accelerated Omics and Single-cell Analysis Using NVIDIA GPUs
sessions.abstract:
The explosion of biological data generation and innovations in AI have opened new frontiers in scientific discovery and biomedicine. With the ability to process vast amounts of biological data, accelerated analysis and foundation model approaches are transforming digital biology. This workshop provides hands-on experience for how accelerated libraries and deep learning models can be leveraged to process and analyze complex biological data. Gain an understanding of the end-to-end process, from how NVIDIA libraries support data generation, pre-processing, and analysis of single-cell data, to how foundation models are being used and evaluated in the life sciences. Get an overview of relevant solutions in digital biology, such as Parabricks, RAPIDS-singlecell (developed by scverse), NVIDIA CUDA-X Data Science Libraries, and Clara Open Models.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic familiarity with Python, Jupyter, and Bioinformatics workflows.

speakers.name: Gary Burnett
speakers.bio: Gary Burnett is an application engineer at NVIDIA who helps customers use CLARA Parabricks software to accelerate their genomics pipelines. Gary received his master's degree in biomedical informatics from Stanford University.

speakers.name: Severin Dicks
speakers.bio: Severin Dicks is a Product Architect for Genomics at NVIDIA and an scverse core member. He helps researchers accelerate single-cell analysis by developing GPU-accelerated workflows and is the primary developer behind rapids-singlecell. Before joining NVIDIA, Severin worked as a software developer in Fabian Theis’ lab at Helmholtz Munich.

speakers.name: T.J. Chen
speakers.bio: T.J. leads genomics products at NVIDIA and is responsible for leveraging NVIDIA’s expertise in AI, high performance computing, and data analytics stacks to address genomics workflows with accelerated high-accuracy solutions. Prior to NVIDIA, T.J. led teams focused on cloud platforms, data analysis, data interpretation, and visualization, supporting R&D through to clinical applications. She received a Ph.D./M.S. in biomedical informatics from Stanford University and B.S. in computer science/biology from Duke University.

sessions.title: Advancing Multimodal Systems: Alignment, Information Typology, Reasoning, and Cross-Modal Flow
sessions.abstract:
This lab explores key principles for advancing multi-modal systems, focusing on multi-modal information typology, alignment, multi-step reasoning, and cross-modal flow. We analyze redundant, unique, and synergistic information, and examine multi-modal alignment through coarse-grained versus fine-grained behavior. The lab then investigates multi-step multi-modality reasoning with external knowledge, where agents integrate images, text, tables, and diagrams to solve complex tasks. Finally, we study multi-modal information flow through cross-modal translation, cross-modal editing, and cross-modal querying, supported by NIM examples for text-to-image generation, semantic image editing, and visual question-answering.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of machine learning and deep learning concepts, including embeddings and neural network architectures.

  • Familiarity with multimodal AI concepts, such as combining text, image, and other data modalities.

  • Experience with Python programming and standard ML libraries (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow).

  • Basic knowledge of data visualization techniques (e.g., PCA, t-SNE) for interpreting embeddings.

speakers.name: Andrea Pilzer
speakers.bio: Andrea Pilzer is a HER solution architect at NVIDIA with the NVIDIA AI Technology Center in Italy. He was a postdoc at Aalto University, working on uncertainty estimation for deep learning. He worked at Huawei Ireland and got his Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Trento with professors Nicu Sebe and Elisa Ricci.

speakers.name: Mireille Fares
speakers.bio: Mireille specializes in developing multi-modal generative AI models, particularly for synthesizing and animating gestures in embodied conversational agents. Her expertise spans human behavior modeling, signal processing, natural language processing, sentiment analysis, and deep learning, with a focus on Transformers. Mireille holds a Ph.D. in computer science and artificial intelligence from Sorbonne University.

speakers.name: Karin Sevegnani
speakers.bio: Karin is a solutions architect leading the NVIDIA AI Technology Center U.K. initiative. Karin’s expertise lies in exploring topic transitions within conversational systems and devising effective strategies for introducing items in recommendation settings; she completed a Ph.D. in natural language processing at Heriot-Watt University, U.K., in July 2023, where she focused on conversational AI and recommendation systems.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: Build End-to-End Medical AI Workflows with NVIDIA MedTech Open Models and NeMo Data Designer
sessions.abstract:
This lab provides a hands-on deep dive into medical AI workflows for both clinical text and imaging, with a focus on building a synthetic patient record to address data scarcity and privacy challenges in healthcare.

You will first employ NeMo Data Designer to generate synthetic electronic health records (EHR), including patient visits, doctor notes, and follow-up reports. From there, you will use NVIDIA open models to handle segmentation, reasoning, and synthetic image generation for CT, MR, and X-ray data.

The lab provides an interactive viewer for immediate clinical validation and hands-on notebooks for deep technical customization. Whether you are exploring the provided samples or uploading your own images, we also provide ways to download sample data from the NIH to test on real-world data.

Each model contributes a different piece of the synthetic patient record, and by the end of the lab, you will understand how these tools fit together and be ready to explore the growing landscape of NVIDIA MedTech open models

speakers.name: Ahmed Harouni
speakers.bio: Ahmed Harouni is a technical marketing engineer for medical imaging at NVIDIA. Ahmed received his Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from Johns Hopkins University, focusing on magnetic resonance image acquisition and image processing. Recently, he's been using deep learning for image segmentation, registration, disease classification, and localization.

sessions.title: Automate 3D Data Pipelines With USD Exchange SDK
sessions.abstract:
Learn how to build robust OpenUSD-based data pipelines using USD Exchange SDK, to leverage existing 3D software and tools for scalable physical AI workflows. Start from common 3D source formats and write Python-based code to extract, transform, and load assets into reusable, modular USD representations suitable for large-scale workflows. Through guided, hands-on exercises, structure 3D content into composable USD assets, apply simulation-ready (“SimReady”) standards, and enrich assets with rigid-body physics that support downstream robotics and simulation workloads. By the end, you'll have automated an asset conversion pipeline capable of processing large 3D datasets, for a practical blueprint to adapt to own production environment.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate experience with Python: working with packages, virtual environments, basic scripting.

  • Familiarity with content concepts: meshes, materials, transforms, scene hierarchies common to 3D modeling applications.

  • Understand command-line usage for running scripts, managing project files.

Recommended:

  • Completed Learn OpenUSD curriculum, covering fundamentals of USD-based scene representation and concepts (stages, layers, prims, composition).

  • Basic familiarity with robotics, simulation or general 3D content workflows (simulation tools, physics-enabled assets).

speakers.name: Andrew Kaufman
speakers.bio: Andrew Kaufman is a Principal Software Engineer at NVIDIA. He is the Engineering Lead for NVIDIA's OpenUSD Exchange SDK, which helps new OpenUSD developers design, build, and test their own USD I/O solutions. He has additionally helped design and develop several USD ecosystem products at NVIDIA, including a MuJoCo to USD data converter for robotics simulation, USD data converters for Generative AI, and NVIDIA's USD Asset Validation framework. Prior to NVIDIA, Andrew was engineering manager and lead at Image Engine Design and a key contributor to the open source frameworks Cortex and Gaffer.

speakers.name: Aaron Luk
speakers.bio: Aaron Luk leads product management for the Universal Scene Description (OpenUSD) ecosystem across NVIDIA Omniverse. Previously, Aaron co-developed and deployed OpenUSD into production pipelines at Pixar.

sessions.title: Find the Bottleneck: Optimize AI Pipelines With Nsight Systems
sessions.abstract:
Learn about using NVIDIA Nsight Systems for bottleneck detection in AI pipelines and explore new features to optimize and scale the execution of your applications. This lab will guide you through the performance analysis process of GPU-accelerated AI applications that run across multiple, possibly containerized, compute instances. In addition to profiling of CPU, GPU, network and I/O activity, we'll show how NVTX and plugin mechanisms enable the collection of custom data to support an even more comprehensive and tailored analysis on the target platform. We'll also show how the recipe system can be used to identify bottlenecks in applications running across multiple nodes. This lab is for both beginners and experienced developers who want to learn about the latest features, tips, and tricks for performance analysis to get the most out of their hardware.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with GPUs and CUDA
  • Up to date Chrome or IE browser

speakers.name: Sneha Kottapalli
speakers.bio: Sneha Latha Kottapalli is a senior systems software engineer at NVIDIA. Her team develops NVIDIA Nsight Systems, a system-wide performance analysis tool. Prior to joining NVIDIA, she was an engineer at Microsoft and Intel. Sneha obtained her M.S. in electrical engineering from Clemson University and a B.Tech. degree in electronics and communication engineering from National Institute of Technology Karnataka Surathkal, Mangalore, India.

sessions.title: Accelerate Apache Spark With GPU and AI: A Hands-On Workshop
sessions.abstract:
In this training lab, we'll introduce Project Aether — a set of automation and AI tools that speed up and reduce the cost of accelerated extract, transform, load and analytics workloads on the RAPIDS Accelerator for Apache Spark, built on NVIDIA cuDF. Aether combines automation along with advanced AI tools to automatically test and optimize your Apache Spark workloads with GPU.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Some experience with Linux and the command line needed.
  • An understanding of large-scale analytics and Apache Spark is not required, but would be helpful.

speakers.name: Hirakendu Das
speakers.bio: Hirakendu Das is a principal software engineer at NVIDIA developing AI-based tools for optimizing ETL workloads when using RAPIDS for Spark. He has over 12 years of experience building distributed ML systems for search advertising platforms at Yahoo and Amazon. An early adopter of Apache Spark, he contributed to the development of GBDT algo in MLlib. He holds a Ph.D. from UC San Diego, specializing in information theory.

speakers.name: Navin Kumar
speakers.bio: Navin is a distributed systems software engineer at NVIDIA, where he primarily concentrates on building systems that support the migration to accelerated Spark workloads. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he was the technical architect at several startups and focused on building large-scale distributed applications and data pipelines.

speakers.name: Rishi Chandra
speakers.bio: Rishi Chandra is a systems software engineer at NVIDIA focusing on machine learning and deep learning for GPU-accelerated Apache Spark. He holds a bachelor’s degree in computer science from Johns Hopkins University.

sessions.title: Generate Synthetic Data for Physical AI with NVIDIA Cosmos World Foundation Models
sessions.abstract:
This hands-on lab introduces roboticists, AI developers, and pipeline engineers to NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models (WFMs) for synthetic data generation in robot learning pipelines. Participants will learn how to leverage simulation scenes from Isaac Sim and real-life images and videos to provide initial datasets and augment them with Cosmos Transfer, then evaluate data quality with Cosmos Reason—all within a production-ready robotics pipeline. Through this session, you'll experience an end-to-end data pipeline workflow, leveraging Cosmos WFMs to generate synthetic data from real-world and physically-accurate simulated data, and using Cosmos Reason as an intelligent critic to validate data quality. By completing this lab, you'll unlock the ability to generate high-quality training datasets, accelerating your action model development.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with robotics simulation concepts (what is synthetic data, why it matters)
  • Basic Python scripting experience (reading/writing scripts, understanding function calls)
  • Understanding of machine learning fundamentals (training, fine-tuning, evaluation)
  • Familiarity with Cosmos (recommended course: An Introduction to NVIDIA Cosmos for Physical AI)

speakers.name: Stefanie Manzinger
speakers.bio: Stefanie Manzinger is a senior solutions architect for Automotive and Manufacturing at NVIDIA. She helps customers accelerate physical AI development with NVIDIA Cosmos and drives the adoption of generative AI for industrial applications. Stefanie holds a Ph.D. from the Technical University of Munich, where her doctoral research specialized in decision-making and trajectory planning for autonomous vehicles.

speakers.name: Alexander Schwarz
speakers.bio: Alexander Schwarz is a Robotics Solutions Architect at NVIDIA, where he guides developers building next-generation Robot Brains and World Foundation Models with NVIDIA Cosmos and Isaac. He holds an M.Sc. in Robotics, Cognition, Intelligence from TU Munich and conducted research at Johns Hopkins University, TU Delft, and ETH Zurich.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation

sessions.title: Develop AI-Powered Operational Dashboards for Digital Twin Applications
sessions.abstract:
Create intelligent operational dashboards for digital twin applications using NVIDIA Omniverse. Build compact, real-time monitoring interfaces with React to track factory metrics, robot states, and sensor data streams. Embed custom panels into Kit applications and deploy dashboards across local and cloud environments. Learn to integrate LLM agents for natural language interaction with your digital twin data. Through hands-on exercises, learn the workflow for dashboard creation for streaming deployment, enabling intelligent monitoring and control of complex industrial operations.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with REST APIs, data streaming, and real-time visualization concepts
  • Basic understanding of factory operations, IoT metrics, and monitoring workflows
  • Experience with Python scripting for backend integration and automation
  • Knowledge of LLM concepts and natural language processing

speakers.name: Justine Lin
speakers.bio: Justine Lin focuses on Omniverse and cloud developer workflows. Prior to this, she held various roles across the industry, such as software engineer, solutions engineer, and technical program manager. She holds a B.S. in computer science from University of California, San Diego.

speakers.name: Ashley Goldstein
speakers.bio: Ashley Goldstein develops content that guides developers through integrating NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD into their workflows. With a colorful background in areas such as academia, fine art, and 3D workflows, Ashley reaches far across the community in media & entertainment; architecture, engineering, construction & operations; and education. She enjoys popping into the NVIDIA Omniverse livestreams and community forums, where her dogs make an occasional cameo.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Data to Decisions: GPU-Accelerated Decision Optimization
sessions.abstract:
Learn to optimize decision optimization problems with GPU-accelerated workflows using NVIDIA cuOpt, RAPIDS, CUDA-X libraries, and Nemotron models. This hands-on lab dives into advanced LP/MIP solver integration, accelerated primal heuristics, cutting-plane methods, and deployment patterns for large-scale, real-time optimization.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Foundational knowledge of optimization: Familiarity with Linear Programming and Mixed Integer Programming concepts (constraints, relaxations, heuristics, cutting planes).

  • Basic Python proficiency: Ability to read and modify Python scripts used in optimization workflows.

  • Some experience with GPUs or CUDA-based frameworks: Prior exposure to GPU computing (e.g., RAPIDS, CUDA-X libraries) is helpful but not mandatory.

  • Comfort with OR tooling: Experience with at least the optimization solver ecosystem

speakers.name: Adi Yanovsky
speakers.bio: Adi Geva brings AI solutions for enterprises using various NVIDIA GPU-accelerated frameworks across different AI applications. She received her bachelor's degree in computer science and applied mathematics at the University of Massachusetts, Amherst.

speakers.name: Burcin Bozkaya
speakers.bio: Burcin focuses on NVIDIA AI software, including NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails.

sessions.title: Harness NVIDIA AI Advanced Tools for Generative AI in Digital Health
sessions.abstract:
Explore the NVIDIA Data Flywheel, which uses agent deployment artifacts from production applications to increase the overall accuracy and reduce the latency/cost of agentic AI systems. We'll explore how to utilize the NeMo Microservices Platform for programmatic control of datasets, fine-tuning, evaluation, and inference.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with Python, basic understanding of how agentic systems work, hands-on knowledge is encouraged but not required.

speakers.name: Abood Quraini
speakers.bio: Abood Al-Quraini is a technical marketing engineering manager for healthcare AI at NVIDIA. He leads a team focused on reference application development and developer integrations of NVIDIA AI NIMs and Blueprints for Digital Health. Abood holds a master’s degree in electrical engineering from McGill University and an M.B.A. from Santa Clara University.

speakers.name: Ben Randoing
speakers.bio: Ben Randoing works on applied AI at NVIDIA, where he contributes to conversational AI systems, real-time multi-modal agents, and emerging digital health technologies. Prior to NVIDIA, he contributed to discovery-stage research in Apple’s Health Technologies group, exploring future consumer health tech capabilities. He has also conducted research in Stanford’s Neuromuscular Biomechanics Laboratory, studying human movement and computational modeling. Ben holds a bachelor’s degree in biomedical engineering from Duke University and a master’s degree in artificial intelligence from Stanford University.

speakers.name: Jin Li
speakers.bio: Jin Li is a healthcare technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA, working on product solutions in digital health and more. She focuses on utilizing NVIDIA Blueprints and NVIDIA NIM microservices in creating generative AI-enabled sample applications, demos, tutorials, and reference workflows to address real-world use cases. She holds an M.S. in machine learning from Carnegie Mellon University, and a bachelor's degree in electrical engineering with a minor in computer science from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.

sessions.title: Accelerate Drug Discovery With NVIDIA Libraries and Platforms
sessions.abstract:
The field of drug discovery is increasingly benefiting from AI, helping researchers build efficient predictive and generative workflows that drastically reduce time-to-clinic. NVIDIA accelerates this process with open-source solutions like BioNeMo Recipes, NIM, and Clara Open Models for Digital Biology, providing easy-to-adopt solutions for training and deploying foundation models and agentic AI systems for drug discovery. In this session, we'll demonstrate how the latest NVIDIA accelerated libraries and models streamline AI workflows, accelerate drug discovery, and enable more efficient biomolecular design and analysis.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of Python, Machine Learning, and Docker

speakers.name: Kristopher Kersten
speakers.bio: Kristopher Kersten is focused on AI, working to scale ML and deep learning solutions to solve today's most pressing problems in healthcare.
