Prior to NVIDIA, Kristopher worked at Cray Supercomputers, studying hardware and software performance characteristics from low-level cache benchmarking to large-scale parallel simulation.

speakers.name: Neel Patel
speakers.bio: Neel Patel is a drug discovery scientist focusing on cheminformatics and computational structural biology. Before joining NVIDIA, Neel was a computational chemist at Takeda Pharmaceuticals. He holds a Ph.D. from the University of Southern California.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Biology - Generative AI

sessions.title: Configure and Tune Robot Assets With OpenUSD and PhysX
sessions.abstract:
Learn from NVIDIA experts about OpenUSD best practices and physics tuning of mechanisms, using the example of a robotic hand. Starting from a URDF file, see how to structure, assemble, and optimize USD. Then learn about the physics tuning process, to enable dexterous hands to behave realistically in simulation. We will focus on best practices that make complex hand models performant, controllable, and ready for downstream learning and control tasks.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate experience with Python, including working with packages, virtual environments, and basic scripting.

  • Familiarity with 3D content concepts such as meshes, materials, transforms, and scene hierarchies from tools like Blender, Maya, or similar DCC applications.

  • Understand command-line usage for running scripts and managing project files.

  • Have familiarity with robotics or simulation workflows (for example, using simulation tools or working with physics-enabled assets).

speakers.name: Alexandra Kissel
speakers.bio: Alexandra Kissel is a robotics simulation software engineer at NVIDIA, where she develops physics-based simulation workflows, asset validation tools, and robotics features for Isaac Sim. She holds M.S. degrees in mechanical engineering and computer sciences from the University of Wisconsin–Madison, specializing in high-performance scientific computing, numerical methods, and multi-physics simulation software development.

speakers.name: Ji Yuan (Steven) Feng
speakers.bio: Ji Yuan "Steven" is a robotics engineer specializing in simulation development, building high-fidelity virtual environments to accelerate robotic testing and deployment. His work contributes to bridging the sim-to-real gap by creating more accurate and efficient simulation frameworks. Steven is particularly interested in reinforcement learning and imitation learning, exploring novel control policies that unlock new robotic capabilities and enable safer, more human-like interactions.

sessions.title: Scalable Environment Setup and Policy Evaluation in Simulation with Isaac Lab-Arena and GR00T
sessions.abstract:
In this hands-on lab, you’ll gain expertise on workflows for simulation-based policy evaluation, including how to setup simulation environments with Isaac Lab Arena, how to post-train GR00T models for custom tasks and how to evaluate trained policies in simulation at scale. NVIDIA Isaac Lab Arena is a framework for scalable policy evaluation in simulation. NVIDIA Isaac GR00T provides robot foundation models for cognition and control, built on NVIDIA Omniverse™ and Cosmos™.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate experience with Python, including working with packages, virtual environments, and basic scripting.
  • Understand command-line usage for running scripts and managing project files.
  • Have familiarity with robotics or simulation workflows (for example, using simulation tools or working with physics-enabled assets).

speakers.name: Asawaree Bhide
speakers.bio: Asawaree Bhide is a technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA, working on AI and robotics on the Jetson platform. She did her master’s studies in computer science at Georgia Tech and is interested in solving complex perception tasks for autonomous embodied agents.

speakers.name: Edith Llontop
speakers.bio: Edith Llontop is a Robotics Technical Marketing Engineer at NVIDIA. She has a degree in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences from UC Berkeley and has experience doing robotics research both at Nvidia's Generalist Embodied Agent Research Lab and Berkeley's Artificial Intelligence Research Lab. She now works on projects that help support researchers, customers, and the broader robotics community leverage Nvidia's robotics technology.

speakers.name: Sangeeta Subramanian
speakers.bio: Sangeeta Subramanian is a senior product manager at NVIDIA, focusing on simulation software for robotics learning and evaluation. She previously worked on autonomous vehicle platform software and has a background in system software engineering leadership for NVIDIA DRIVE platforms. She holds a master’s degree in Computer Science from North Carolina State University.

speakers.name: Alex Millane
speakers.bio: Alexander Millane is a senior robotics engineer at NVIDIA, where he works at the intersection of machine perception, robot learning, and simulation. Alex holds a PhD from ETH Zurich, where his research focused on real-time 3D reconstruction for robotics.

sessions.title: Building and Testing Multi‑Robot Scenarios with Isaac Sim and ROS 2
sessions.abstract:
Gain practical experience in orchestrating multi-robot collaboration, automating simulation workflows, and executing autonomous navigation and object manipulation tasks in a test scenario, using ROS 2, Simulation Interfaces, and Isaac Sim. These skills will empower you to deploy and test advanced robotics solutions.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of ROS (Robot Operating System).
  • Intermediate experience with Python, including working with packages, virtual environments, and basic scripting.
  • Familiarity with 3D content concepts such as meshes, materials, transforms, and scene hierarchies from tools like Blender, Maya, or similar DCC applications.
  • Understand command-line usage for running scripts and managing project files.
  • Have familiarity with robotics or simulation workflows (for example, using simulation tools or working with physics-enabled assets).

speakers.name: Ayush Ghosh
speakers.bio: Ayush Ghosh is a robotics systems engineer at NVIDIA, specializing in robotics simulation and digital twin environments. He plays a crucial role in developing the ROS Bridge for NVIDIA Isaac Sim. Ayush holds a bachelor's degree in mechatronics engineering and AI from the University of Waterloo, where he gained experience in swarm robotic navigation systems. His diverse experience includes work on autonomous underwater robots, Canadian spaceflight cameras, and Canada's first lunar rover.

speakers.name: Rishabh Chadha
speakers.bio: Rishabh Chadha is an engineer at NVIDIA, where he focuses on integrating deep learning and robotics frameworks for the NVIDIA Jetson platforms. He has a master's degree in robotics from Worcester Polytechnic Institute. His interests primarily include deep learning, medical imaging, and robot perception.

sessions.title: Accelerate Robot Learning With NVIDIA Isaac Lab and Newton
sessions.abstract:
This hands-on lab introduces Newton, a GPU-accelerated physics engine. Then we'll integrate Newton with Isaac Lab, showing how they can work together in robot-learning workflows. You'll configure tasks to run on Newton, and train and evaluate policies in Isaac Lab with Newton providing high-fidelity, high-throughput physics. You'll leave knowing how to use Newton for more realistic robotics simulation.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate experience with Python, including working with packages, virtual environments, and basic scripting.
  • Familiarity with 3D content concepts such as meshes, materials, transforms, and scene hierarchies from tools like Blender, Maya, or similar DCC applications.
  • Understand command-line usage for running scripts and managing project files.
  • Have familiarity with robotics or simulation workflows (for example, using simulation tools or working with physics-enabled assets).

speakers.name: Eric Heiden
speakers.bio: Eric Heiden is a research scientist at NVIDIA, where he develops the open-source Python library Warp. His research interests lie in the intersection of simulation and robotics, particularly differentiable simulators that can be used to reduce the reality gap and control dynamical systems through optimization. He received his Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Southern California under the supervision of Professor Gaurav Sukhatme.

speakers.name: Akul Santhosh
speakers.bio: Akul Santhosh is a solutions architect on NVIDIA’s Robotics SA team, where he supports customers building on the Isaac and Cosmos platforms. Before joining NVIDIA, he earned a master’s degree in computer science from New York University, where he also conducted research in the Robot Perception Lab.

speakers.name: Mohammad Mohajerani
speakers.bio: Mohammad Mohajerani is a senior product manager at NVIDIA, where his work enables high-performance simulation and real-time physics across physical AI, CAE, and AI-driven digital twin applications through Warp and Newton. Prior to NVIDIA, Mohammad held product and engineering leadership roles in the startup world at Sanctuary AI and Haply Robotics and spent several years advancing physics engines and skills training simulators at CM Labs Simulations. He holds a master's degree in mechanical engineering from Concordia University, with a focus on aerial robotics, system identification, and control optimization.

sessions.title: Develop Production Agents with Eval-Driven Design
sessions.abstract:
This training lab will explore new techniques for building robust, production-ready AI agents by focusing on an evaluation-driven design workflow. You'll learn how we use human-created datasets for iterative improvement and how to apply continuous eval and iterate cycles, including prompt changes and experimentation, to significantly improve agent performance.

Prerequisite(s):

  • The prerequisites for this course are practical familiarity with AI/ML fundamentals, including Large Language Models (LLMs), and programming proficiency in Python.

  • Attendees should also have a working understanding of basic prompt engineering and experience with the overall lifecycle of building an AI application, as the lab is focused on the advanced, production-ready stage of evaluation and iterative design.

speakers.name: Dhruv Nandakumar
speakers.bio: Dhruv specializes in helping customers harness the power of NVIDIA AI Enterprise for building advanced, scalable, GPU-accelerated pipelines tailored for cybersecurity data processing and AI workloads. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Dhruv was a published deep learning researcher, developing advanced methods for cyberthreat detection, adversarial simulation, and automated security response.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Deploy and Optimize LLMs and VLMs on NVIDIA Jetson Thor
sessions.abstract:
Learn to deploy, optimize, and accelerate large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs) on NVIDIA Jetson Thor through hands-on exercises with real hardware. We'll demonstrate production-ready inference frameworks including vLLM and SGLang, apply NVIDIA ModelOpt for quantization, showcase systematic methods to achieve inference performance improvements, and explore advanced capabilities like tool calling and live VLM inference — all running on edge devices you can touch and interact with.

Prerequisite(s):

Required:

  • Basic understanding of Large Language Models and transformer architectures

  • Familiarity with Python programming and container technologies (Docker)

  • Experience with command-line interfaces and SSH for remote device access

  • Laptop with network connectivity (Ethernet preferred, WiFi acceptable)

Recommended:

  • Prior exposure to PyTorch or inference frameworks
  • Basic understanding of model quantization concepts
  • Familiarity with REST APIs and web services

speakers.name: Chitoku Yato
speakers.bio: Chitoku Yato is senior technical product marketing manager of the NVIDIA Jetson Edge AI platform, where he's responsible for ensuring the best developer experience on the platform. He also works closely with the vibrant NVIDIA developer community to help evangelize the use of pre-trained AI models, developer SDKs, and support for cloud-native technologies on Jetson to help customers build, deploy, and manage AI-based autonomous machines.

sessions.title: From Ingestion to Inference: Mastering the High-Performance GPU Data Science Pipeline
sessions.abstract:
Get a comprehensive guide to constructing a complete, high-performance data science pipeline that leverages GPU acceleration throughout its life cycle. We'll sequentially cover the pipeline's core components: data ingestion, exploration, processing, and model training. For the initial stages of data ingestion and exploration, we'll demonstrate the use of GPU-accelerated frameworks, specifically Polars and/or cuDF pandas, to achieve significant speedups in data manipulation and analysis. Then we'll showcase the power of the cuML library (part of the RAPIDS ecosystem) for training and testing various machine learning models entirely on the GPU.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Attendees need basic Python skills and introductory data science knowledge, but no prior GPU experience is required.

speakers.name: Allison Ding
speakers.bio: Allison is a developer advocate for GPU-accelerated AI APIs, libraries, and tools at NVIDIA, with a specialization in large language models and advanced data science techniques. She brings over eight years of hands-on experience as a data scientist, focusing on managing and delivering end-to-end data science solutions. Her academic background includes a strong emphasis on natural language processing and generative AI. Allison holds a master’s degree in applied statistics from Cornell University and a master’s degree in computer science from San Francisco Bay University.

sessions.title: Advance World Simulation With 3D Gaussian Splatting for Large-Scale Environment Reconstruction
sessions.abstract:
Learn how to reconstruct a large scene for robotics testing using NVIDIA Omniverse NuRec Gaussian-based reconstruction technologies to perform multi-GPU training, object segmentation, and extraction. This lab will walk through core concepts with a step-by-step workflow for data capture, reconstruction, and object level-integration for robotics simulation in NVIDIA Isaac Lab.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Omniverse NuRec, Isaac Sim / Lab

speakers.name: Zoe LaLena
speakers.bio: Zoë LaLena is a solutions architect at NVIDIA with a focus on imaging science and computer vision. Since joining NVIDIA in 2025, she's been working on creating virtual three-dimensional environments from two-dimensional imagery. She earned an M.S. in computer vision from Carnegie Mellon University and a B.S. in imaging science from the Rochester institute of Technology.

sessions.title: Unlock Real-Time Financial Decisions With GPU-Accelerated Portfolio Optimization
sessions.abstract:
Discover how to resolve the trade-off between computational speed and model complexity in financial portfolio management. This session combines NVIDIA’s Quantitative Portfolio Optimization developer example with exclusive insights from Kendall Square Capital, demonstrating how to accelerate large-scale, complex portfolio optimization problems without compromising result quality. Learn how to formulate data-driven models to leverage cuOpt and CUDA-X Data Science libraries to unlock accelerations up to triple-digit speed-ups compared to CPU-based solutions. Gain insights into how these innovations transform portfolio optimization from a slow batch process into a dynamic workflow, unlocking new frontiers in automated investing research and real-time rebalancing.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Python programming, basic knowledge of numerical optimization

speakers.name: Ioana Boier
speakers.bio: Ioana Boier is the global head of capital markets strategy at NVIDIA, where she defines and leads the strategy to support institutional trading firms and ecosystem partners in building AI factories for scalable alpha generation. She holds a Ph.D. in computer science from Purdue University and has deep experience spanning quantitative research and AI systems, with senior roles at Alphadyne Asset Management, Citadel LLC, BNP Paribas, and IBM T.J. Watson Research.

speakers.name: Peihan Huo
speakers.bio: Peihan Huo is a solutions architect on the Financial Services Industry team at NVIDIA. He has expertise in mathematical optimization, reinforcement learning, and financial modeling. Currently, his work focuses on accelerating capital markets workflows by leveraging NVIDIA advanced computing technologies. Peihan holds a Ph.D. in applied mathematics from Cornell University.

speakers.name: Jianchi Chen
speakers.bio: Jianchi "JC" Chen is the chief investment officer at Kendall Square Capital, a quantitative investment management company that applies cutting-edge AI research and technology to the financial markets. Before joining Kendall Square, he worked as a quant researcher at Jump Trading LLC. He holds a bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering from Caltech.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries

sessions.title: Build a Video Analytics AI Agent With Vision Language Models
sessions.abstract:
New vision language models (VLMs) are transforming computer vision with scalable, reasoning zero-shot solutions. Learn about Cosmos Reason — a new open and fully customizable reasoning VLM for physical AI and robotics — that lets robots and vision AI agents reason like humans, using prior knowledge, physics understanding, and common sense to understand and act in the real world. In this lab, you'll build a video analytics AI agent using the NVIDIA Blueprint for Video Search and Summarization (VSS) with Cosmos Reason VLM.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of computer vision models.

speakers.name: Sammy Ochoa
speakers.bio: Samuel Ochoa is a technical marketing engineer with the Metropolis team at NVIDIA, focusing on bringing AI to industrial applications. Prior to NVIDIA, he graduated from the University of Texas at Austin with a B.S. and M.S. in computer engineering, with a specialty in machine learning for edge devices.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: Adding Safety and Perception to Industrial Robots
sessions.abstract:
Learn to implement functional safety for robots using an "outside-in" approach. You will gain deep insight into building collaborative, AI-powered factory environments where vision systems and robotics converge to deliver operational intelligence and safety. We will connect industrial robots to factory-wide sensor infrastructure using NVIDIA Metropolis to build intelligent systems dedicated to robot safety. Through practical exercises, you will create a video analytics AI agent that extends a robot's real-time perception. This agent will enable critical safety features, such as automatic speed adjustments (slowing down or speeding up) and risk mitigation, essential for safe co-bot environments. This lab showcases how NVIDIA Halos for Outside-In Safety Agents, powered by NVIDIA Metropolis technology, enable an outside-in approach to achieving advanced functional safety.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate experience with Python, including packages, virtual environments, and scripting

  • Familiarity with computer vision concepts, object detection, and AI inference pipelines

  • Experience with robotics workflows and sensor integration

  • Knowledge of data streaming, real-time analytics, and message-passing architectures

speakers.name: Sammy Ochoa
speakers.bio: Samuel Ochoa is a technical marketing engineer with the Metropolis team at NVIDIA, focusing on bringing AI to industrial applications. Prior to NVIDIA, he graduated from the University of Texas at Austin with a B.S. and M.S. in computer engineering, with a specialty in machine learning for edge devices.

speakers.name: Edward Chan
speakers.bio: Edward Chan is a Senior Product Manager on the Omniverse Team at NVIDIA, where he works on furthing digital twin and robotics use cases in industrial applications. Previously, Edward had developed robotic applications and products at Kawasaki Robotics, Keyence, Schneider Electric, and Universal Robots, gaining deep empathy for manufacturers and enterprises employing new automation technology. He holds an MBA from UC Berkeley Haas School of Business and a BS Mechanical Engineering from SJSU.

sessions.title: Building and Deploying Digital Twin Applications With Omniverse Kit App Streaming
sessions.abstract:
Unlock the power of real-time 3D digital twin applications with NVIDIA Omniverse Kit App Streaming. In this lab, you'll build, containerize, deploy, and extend interactive 3D applications that stream directly to web clients, enabling scalable, remote simulation and control for digital twin solutions. We’re going to create a streaming-ready OpenUSD application and a front-end client, then package and deploy a solution using industry-standard tools and APIs. You’ll explore how to implement custom messaging between your app and client, allowing for rich, bi-directional interaction and integration with live data sources. You'll gain practical experience with the full development and deployment workflow for Omniverse Kit App Streaming and develop a solid foundation for building operational digital twins that deliver real-time insights and collaboration across your organization.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate Python experience, including working with packages and basic scripting
  • Familiarity with cloud platforms and IoT concepts
  • Understanding of 3D scene concepts: transforms, hierarchies, and basic visualization
  • Basic knowledge of REST APIs and data streaming concepts

speakers.name: Sebastian Misiurek
speakers.bio: Sebastian Misiurek is a product manager on the Omniverse team at NVIDIA. He has worked as a design technologist and educator who specializes in sustainable, high-performance, and data-driven design. Sebastian has a bachelor’s degree in architecture from Pratt Institute and resides in New York.

speakers.name: Andrew Wrenn
speakers.bio: Andrew Wrenn is a Cloud Software Engineer at NVIDIA, working as a Developer Technologist to support critical customers for Omniverse Streaming and NVCF workloads.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Assembling Industrial Digital Twins with OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse Libraries
sessions.abstract:
In this hands-on lab, participants will explore how to build industrial digital twins from manufacturing facilities to AI factories, using OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse libraries, drawing on real-world collaboration with industry leaders. The session walks through an end-to-end workflow, from CAD models to full digital twin assembly, demonstrating how manufacturers are transforming design and production through simulation and interoperability.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate experience with 3D content concepts: meshes, materials, transforms, scene hierarchies, and USD fundamentals
  • Familiarity with manufacturing workflows, factory layouts, or industrial automation processes
  • Basic Python scripting experience for automation and integration tasks
  • Knowledge of data integration concepts and structured data formats

speakers.name: Jay Axe
speakers.bio: Jay is a technical product manager at NVIDIA focusing on Omniverse Virtual Factories. He found his way to NVIDIA as a freelance computer-generated lighting/simulation consultant about 10 years ago. At that time, he created photorealistic rendering for Endeavor, NVIDIA's new headquarters building in Santa Clara, California. Day to day at NVIDIA, Jay demonstrates architecture, engineering, & construction workflows for real-time ray tracing and photorealistic path-tracing.

speakers.name: Ernesto Pacheco
speakers.bio:
Ernesto is a Senior Product Manager at NVIDIA, where he focuses on AI Factories. With more than twenty-five years of experience in the AECO industry, he has played a key role in shaping data ingestion pipelines and advancing immersive, real-time technologies for design collaboration and visualization.
At NVIDIA, Ernesto helps define industry workflows, tooling, infrastructure, and processes to deliver a high-performance, real-time platform powered by Omniverse. As part of the Customer Success team, he supports OpenUSD workflows for AI Factories, bridging cutting-edge simulation and visualization with intelligent, scalable solutions.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Real-Time Simulation for Real-Time Results: How AI Physics Can Accelerate Your AI Factory
sessions.abstract:
This hands-on lab introduces mechanical and simulation engineers to GPU-accelerated, real-time CFD workflows that combine interactive visualization with physics-AI models. Participants will use NVIDIA technologies to connect CFD simulations, PhysicsNeMo, and digital twin environments for faster iteration and more reliable design insights. We will configure and run real-time CFD cases, integrate PhysicsNeMo for accelerated prediction, and visualize results interactively for design exploration. The session will show how to move from traditional batch-style CFD to responsive, GPU-driven workflows that support rapid concept evaluation and collaborative design reviews.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with CAE workflows and core computational fluid dynamics concepts.
  • Experience with engineering simulation principles, including mesh, boundary conditions, and solver workflows.
  • Understanding of 3D scene composition, transforms, and visualization in simulation or 3D environments.
  • Basic knowledge of physics-based simulation and design optimization processes.

speakers.name: Abigail Breazeale
speakers.bio: Abigail is a senior technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA, where she specializes in integrating cutting-edge products like Warp, CUDA-X, and Omniverse into the computer-aided engineering field. With a strong background in computational fluid dynamics, she has a deep expertise in external aerodynamics, workflow optimization, and handling large-scale data for post-processing.

speakers.name: Robert Cervellione
speakers.bio: Robert contributes to the Omniverse Platform, focusing on AI factories and manufacturing. With 20-plus years of experience in developing 3D-centric workflows for digital twins, Robert has built his career on digital transformation in manufacturing and industry, bridging advanced simulation, interoperability, and real-time digital twin technologies. Using cutting-edge platforms and tools, he enables organizations to unify design, production, and operational data. As both a registered architect and a technology leader, Robert implements advanced digital twin and manufacturing workflows, driving transformation across large-scale projects.

sessions.title: OpenUSD Asset Integration and Data Review With Clash Detection
sessions.abstract:
Integrate clash detection into your OpenUSD-based digital twin workflows using NVIDIA Omniverse libraries. In this lab, we’ll ensure ingested assets are run through conflict analysis using the Clash Detection SDK. You’ll create and execute collision detection queries, inspect and visualize results in an Omniverse viewport, and implement robust overlap detection in complex 3D scenes. We will learn the fundamentals of simulation for digital twins, engineering validation, and automated design verification workflows.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with CAD workflows, 3D modeling concepts
  • Basic understanding of simulation concepts and collision detection principles
  • Experience navigating 3D viewports and interactive visualization environments
  • Intermediate scripting experience with Python

speakers.name: Martin Vovk
speakers.bio: Martin is a senior solutions architect at NVIDIA, where he focuses on OpenUSD, Omniverse, and developing scalable digital-twin solutions. With more than a decade of experience in interactive 3D graphics, he brings deep expertise in real-time visualization, simulation workflows, and collaborative virtual environments. He holds a master’s degree in mechanical engineering and is dedicated to helping customers translate complex real-world systems into accurate, high-fidelity digital representations. His work centers on enabling developers and enterprises to build, iterate, and deploy advanced digital-twin workflows that accelerate innovation and streamline decision-making.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Train and Deploy Contact-Rich Robot Manipulation Skills With Isaac Lab and Isaac ROS
sessions.abstract:
Learn how to develop contact‑rich manipulation policies in Isaac Lab, and deploy them using NVIDIA Isaac ROS. In this hands‑on lab, you will walk through a gear-assembly workflow that uses Isaac Lab to train an insertion policy, and then deploy it in ROS 2, together with perception models (segmentation and 3D pose estimation) and cuMotion for GPU‑accelerated planning. You'll see how to configure an Isaac for Manipulation workflow that begins with a simulated UR robot and depth cameras for training the policy, and then tune to achieve robust, repeatable sim‑to‑real performance on contact‑rich tasks.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of ROS (Robot Operating System).
  • Intermediate experience with Python, including working with packages, virtual environments, and basic scripting.
  • Familiarity with 3D content concepts such as meshes, materials, transforms, and scene hierarchies from tools like Blender, Maya, or similar DCC applications.
  • Understand command-line usage for running scripts and managing project files.
  • Have familiarity with robotics or simulation workflows (for example, using simulation tools or working with physics-enabled assets).

speakers.name: Ashwin Varghese Kuruttukulam
speakers.bio: Ashwin Varghese Kuruttukulam is a senior systems software engineer at NVIDIA with over three years of experience in robotics platform software. He began by working on Isaac ROS packages, contributing to Nova Carter, Isaac Manipulator, and maintaining multiple core Isaac ROS components. He later worked on Isaac Lab Mimic and data generation pipelines. Over the past year, his work has focused on reinforcement learning training and deployment using Isaac Lab and ROS, enabling the transition of learned policies from simulation to real-world robotic systems.

speakers.name: Ayusman Saha
speakers.bio: Ayusman Saha is a senior systems software engineer at NVIDIA. He has worked on platform software for robotics including Isaac SDK, GXF, and Isaac ROS. Before joining NVIDIA, he completed his master’s degree in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at University of California, Berkeley with a focus on robotics and embedded software.

sessions.title: Build Agentic Workflows for Financial Applications
sessions.abstract:
We'll walk you through how to turn financial services workflows into end‑to‑end AI agents, covering orchestration, tools, retrieval, and guardrails tailored to domain-specific applications in the financial services industry (FSI). Using NVIDIA’s NeMo Agent Toolkit and Blueprints, you'll get hands on to building multi‑agent systems that support use cases such as investment research and agentic commerce.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Participants should be comfortable with basic Python and familiar with core AI/ML or LLM concepts so they can follow the code and agent patterns efficiently.
  • Experience working with financial services data or workflows (e.g., banking, insurance, payments) is recommended but not required; the material is designed for technical practitioners such as data scientists, ML engineers, and developers who want to build or own FSI agentic AI solutions.

speakers.name: Benjamin Wu
speakers.bio: Benjamin Wu is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, focusing on GPU-accelerated applications for the financial services industry. Previously, he was a data science manager at Capital One, leading work on recommender systems. His background is in machine learning and astrophysics, having spent time at NVIDIA Research, NASA, and the National Astronomical Observatory of Japan. He holds Ph.D. and master’s degrees in physics from the University of Florida, and a bachelor’s in physics and mathematics from Duke University.

speakers.name: David Williams
speakers.bio: David Williams is an NVIDIA solutions architect on the Financial Services team. He spends his days engaging with data science teams and their wider organizations to foster the adoption of AI and GPU acceleration. Prior to NVIDIA, David obtained his master’s degree in computer engineering from Northwestern University in Chicago.

speakers.name: Flora Huang
speakers.bio: Flora previously worked as the quantitative data science manager in Vanguard’s Investment Management Group, where she led machine learning and generative AI research in quantitative trading and investment strategies. Her experience will continue to help clients leverage NVIDIA’s cutting-edge technology to build scalable and impactful AI solutions across various financial use cases.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Build Robot-Ready Assets for Physically Accurate Simulations With Lightwheel
sessions.abstract:
In this hands-on lab, Lightwheel, a leading provider of simulation-ready assets, platforms, and synthetic data for embodied AI, guides you through the complete SimReady asset preparation workflow. You’ll learn to generate high-fidelity synthetic data using OpenUSD, configure accurate physics properties (mass, friction, collision geometry) with PhysX and Newton, and add semantic metadata along with manipulation affordances. You’ll validate your work by deploying trained robot agents inside NVIDIA Isaac Sim. By the end of the lab, you’ll know how to build simulation-ready scenes that support real-to-sim calibration and sim-to-real transfer, enabling robots to learn robustly in simulation and perform reliably in the real world.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic familiarity with 3D content concepts (meshes, materials, transforms) and modeling software
  • Understanding of physics simulation fundamentals (mass, friction, collision)
  • Familiarity with robotics terminology (end-effector, gripper, kinematics)

Recommended:

  • Familiarity with Isaac Sim (recommended course: Getting Started With Isaac Sim).

speakers.name: Frank Chen
speakers.bio: Frank Chen is a leading expert in simulation and synthetic data generation for embodied AI. He has extensive experience building scalable, physically accurate SimReady asset pipelines, along with physical solver optimization, reinforcement learning environments, and large-scale synthetic data generation systems.

speakers.name: Siyi Lin
speakers.bio: Siyi Lin is the technical artist lead at Lightwheel, focusing on physics-accurate SimReady asset development for embodied AI. With experience spanning gaming, self-driving, and robotics simulation, Siyi leads R&D on high-fidelity physics and material models calibrated to real-world conditions and sets standards for SimReady asset and reinforcement learning environment production.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Tabular Foundation Models for Financial Services
sessions.abstract:
Tabular data powers entire industries, but building state-of-the-art models for structured data at scale remains complex. In this session, we’ll unveil a comprehensive workflow for building transformer-based tabular foundation models. Each stage of our reference architecture is GPU-accelerated: tabular synthetic data generation, feature engineering and tokenization, training large-scale tabular transformers, and deploying inference pipelines. We apply this workflow to the real-world problem of fraud detection in credit card transactions, highlighting best practices and NVIDIA’s ecosystem for scalable tabular AI.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Participants should be comfortable with basic Python and familiar with core AI/ML and Foundation Model concepts (tokenization, pre-training, fine tuning).
  • Experience working with financial services data or workflows (e.g., banking, payments) is recommended but not required; the material is designed for technical practitioners such as data scientists, ML engineers, and developers.

speakers.name: Benjamin Wu
speakers.bio: Benjamin Wu is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, focusing on GPU-accelerated applications for the financial services industry. Previously, he was a data science manager at Capital One, leading work on recommender systems. His background is in machine learning and astrophysics, having spent time at NVIDIA Research, NASA, and the National Astronomical Observatory of Japan. He holds Ph.D. and master’s degrees in physics from the University of Florida, and a bachelor’s in physics and mathematics from Duke University.

speakers.name: Flora Huang
speakers.bio: Flora previously worked as the quantitative data science manager in Vanguard’s Investment Management Group, where she led machine learning and generative AI research in quantitative trading and investment strategies. Her experience will continue to help clients leverage NVIDIA’s cutting-edge technology to build scalable and impactful AI solutions across various financial use cases.

speakers.name: Roman Yokunda Enzmann
speakers.bio: Roman studied economics in his undergraduate and graduate studies. He specialized in machine learning and dynamic optimization, graduating with his master’s thesis on the topic of explainable AI. He has a background in data science and risk modeling within the financial services industry. Roman concentrates on accelerated data science using RAPIDS, with a focus on fraud detection.

sessions.title: How to use NVIDIA Warp to Build GPU-Accelerated Computational Physics Simulations
sessions.abstract: Discover how NVIDIA Warp enables the next generation of GPU-accelerated physics, geometry processing, and differentiable programming. This training lab introduces Warp’s core capabilities and modules, then moves into a hands-on notebook where participants build a high-performance physics solver entirely in Python. This is demonstrated through a practical computational fluid dynamics example based on a 2-D Navier–Stokes formulation. You'll leave with an understanding of what Warp is designed for, how its core building blocks work, and how it can be used alongside deep learning frameworks like PyTorch and JAX in modern computational engineering workflows.

speakers.name: Sheel Nidhan
speakers.bio: Sheel Nidhan is a senior technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA, working at the intersection of CUDA-X libraries and computer-aided engineering applications. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Sheel was a senior R&D engineer at Ansys, focusing on application of deep learning to Ansys’ simulation technology. He obtained his Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from the University of California, San Diego, where he worked on high-fidelity simulations and data-driven analysis of turbulent wakes.

speakers.name: Eric Shi
speakers.bio: Eric Shi is the software engineering manager for the Warp team, overseeing engineering standards and technical execution. He facilitates collaboration across internal and external teams and develops training content for researchers and developers. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he was a computational physicist at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory. Eric holds a Ph.D. in plasma physics from Princeton University.

speakers.name: Mohammad Mohajerani
speakers.bio: Mohammad Mohajerani is a senior product manager at NVIDIA, where his work enables high-performance simulation and real-time physics across physical AI, CAE, and AI-driven digital twin applications through Warp and Newton. Prior to NVIDIA, Mohammad held product and engineering leadership roles in the startup world at Sanctuary AI and Haply Robotics and spent several years advancing physics engines and skills training simulators at CM Labs Simulations. He holds a master's degree in mechanical engineering from Concordia University, with a focus on aerial robotics, system identification, and control optimization.

sessions.title: Compress, Cut, and Distill: The Latest Gen AI Model Compression Techniques in Practice
sessions.abstract:
This training lab explores the art and engineering of compressing large language models to make them cheaper, faster, and easier to deploy while preserving practical capability. Designed for a broad audience that spans beginners to advanced practitioners, the workshop will introduce foundational concepts for newcomers, share implementation patterns and pitfalls for experienced engineers, and highlight cutting-edge research directions for specialists.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with Python/PyTorch: Proficiency in Python programming and basic hands-on experience using deep learning frameworks.
  • Conceptual Knowledge of Deep Learning: Basic understanding of neural networks, backpropagation, and the model fine-tuning process.
  • Familiarity with LLM Architectures: Conceptual understanding of the Transformer architecture, including components like attention mechanisms and feed-forward networks.

speakers.name: Harshita Seth
speakers.bio: Harshita is a senior partner solutions architect who joined NVIDIA in May 2024. Her work focuses on applied AI research and deriving the adoption of AI-driven technologies among global systems integrator partners. Before joining NVIDIA, she worked on building and deploying large language models for on-device application.

speakers.name: Lavinia Ghita
speakers.bio: Lavinia is a Solutions Architect Manager at NVIDIA, leading the EMEA technical team for the Financial Services Industry (FSI). Her work focuses on large‑scale AI systems, with emphasis on distillation methods, domain-adaption techniques and model compression methods for efficient AI pipelines. Her research also covers foundation‑model architectures for time‑series analysis and the development of general methods for modelling structure and latent dynamics in temporal processes. Prior to NVIDIA, Lavinia worked on research and applications in this space at a quantitative hedge fund and in big tech. She holds advanced degrees in Mathematics from the École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL).

speakers.name: Sergio Perez
speakers.bio: Sergio Perez is a solutions architect at NVIDIA working on training and inference of LLMs. Sergio collaborates with developers of supercomputing centers, startups, and large corporations to accelerate their AI workloads. Sergio focuses on model compression, leveraging pruning, distillation, and numerical formats like FP8 or NVFP4 to speed up AI. Sergio holds a Ph.D. in computational fluid dynamics from Imperial College London.

speakers.name: Liana Mikaelyan
speakers.bio: Liana Mikaelyan is a senior solutions architect at NVIDIA, specializing in model compression and efficient training and inference for LLM deployment. Previously, she was an applied scientist at Microsoft, developing methods for injecting knowledge into LLMs and enabling on-device capabilities. She holds an M.Sc. in machine learning from University College London, with a background in mathematics.

sessions.title: Create Vision AI Applications With Generative AI Coding Agents
sessions.abstract:
Computer vision is a cornerstone of the generative AI revolution, providing the essential visual metadata required by vision language models (VLMs) to extract actionable insights. In this two-hour instructor-led workshop, you will learn how to GPU-accelerate your video analytics workflow by combining the NVIDIA DeepStream SDK with the power of prompt engineering. Discover how to generate complete, GPU-accelerated DeepStream pipelines using simple natural language prompts. Whether you are a developer new to DeepStream or an advanced user looking to accelerate your prototyping, this course demonstrates how to produce intuitive, readable Python applications without the manual overhead. Join us to bridge the gap between traditional video analytics and modern GenAI development.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Understanding of Python and basic knowledge of computer vision models.

speakers.name: Carlos Garcia-Sierra
speakers.bio: Carlos is a product manager at NVIDIA, focusing on making AI accessible to all. Carlos manages Metropolis AI Workflows, enabling new uses across a diverse set of markets. He's held multiple roles at NVIDIA, including strategic partnerships for the Metropolis business unit, business development for the Automotive BU, and technical marketing. In his free time, Carlos enjoys hiking with his family, mountain biking, reading, and growing organic food.

speakers.name: Monika Jhuria
speakers.bio: Monika Jhuria is a technical marketing engineer with the NVIDIA Metropolis team and has over ten years of experience in the tech industry. Previously, she worked as a computer vision engineer in the automotive sector, designing and deploying vision models optimized for vision SoC. She holds an M.Sc. in Electrical Engineering.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: Scaling Robotic AI Development from Prototype to Production
sessions.abstract:
In this hands-on lab, General Robotics will guide you through a complete, end-to-end solution development workflow built on the NVIDIA ecosystem. You'll learn to:
  • Capture teleoperated demonstrations of real manual tasks
  • Augment, vary, and scale those tasks in simulation using NVIDIA Isaac
  • Generate high-volume synthetic datasets to expand coverage
  • Fine-tune modern robotics models using NVIDIA’s training infrastructure
  • Test policies and reinforcement learning models in advanced simulation, leveraging NVIDIA AI models like GraspGen, CuMotion, and Foundation Stereo
  • Deploy learned behaviors to real robots running on Jetson
  • Analyze system performance and iterate using the latest LLMs and agentic tools
  • Bridge the human–robot gap across development, validation, and deployment

    • Prerequisite(s):

    • Enthusiasm to learn the latest techniques in AI and robotics.

speakers.name: Sai Vemprala
speakers.bio: Sai Vemprala is the CTO and co-founder of General Robotics. As a leading researcher and engineer at the forefront of robotics, AI, and embodied intelligence, he drives the development of scalable, general-purpose robot intelligence systems. Prior to this, Sai was a principal researcher at Microsoft, where he helped shape the future of autonomous systems and was a key contributor to the open-source simulator AirSim.

speakers.name: Ashish Kapoor
speakers.bio: Ashish Kapoor is the Co-Founder and CEO of General Robotics, on a mission to become the fastest way to deploy AI on robots in production with GRID. Previously, Ashish spent over a decade at Microsoft, leading the robotics and autonomous systems group. He is the inventor of AirSim, the industry-standard for autonomous vehicle research.  With over 30 patents and extensive publications in machine learning and aerial informatics, Ashish is leading General Robotics to make physical AI production-ready at scale.

sessions.title: Fine-Tune a Telco Reasoning Model: A Guide to Synthetic Data, Tool Calling, and Evaluation
sessions.abstract:
Network operation centers (NoC) are the central nervous system of telecommunications, but they are often overwhelmed by "alarm storms." In a traditional setup, engineers manually validate alarms, swivel between multiple dashboards, check topologies, and perform root-cause analysis. This manual process is time-consuming and prone to fatigue. To solve this, we are moving toward zero-touch, self-healing networks. In this tutorial, we'll walk through creating a fine-tuning playbook that integrates synthetic data generation, training, and evaluation pipelines. We'll demonstrate how to build an AI-driven reasoning model capable of autonomously performing NoC engineer workflows — detecting issues, calling tools, and remediating incidents without human intervention.

Prerequisite(s):

  •  Familiarity with Python, LLM fine-tuning concepts, and basic containerization.

speakers.name: Amparo Canaveras
speakers.bio: Amparo boasts over two decades in telecommunications. Her roles provided profound insights into the telecom industry and AI's growing presence. She has an M.Sc. from MIT, an M.B.A. from EAE Business School, a bachelor's degree from Valencia's Polytechnical University, and a master's from Stevens Institute of Technology. She's currently working toward a Ph.D. at Colorado State University.

speakers.name: Aiden Chang
speakers.bio: Aiden Chang is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, focusing on enterprise applications of generative AI, robotics, and reasoning systems. He earned his master’s in computer science from the University of Southern California. Outside of work, he enjoys skiing, aviation, and building robots.

speakers.name: Ari Uskudar
speakers.bio: Ari has 20-plus years of experience in AI-driven network automation, RAN intelligence, and large-scale telecom architecture across NVIDIA, VMware, Ericsson, Verizon, Turkcell, Vodafone, and Motorola. Her expertise spans agentic AI systems, autonomous network design, LLM-based telco reasoning, ML-powered observability, and end-to-end optimization. Ari has authored multiple patents in autonomous networks, 6G core architecture, and telco blueprints, etc. Known for bridging deep engineering with strategic product thinking, she designs advanced architectures, leads complex technical collaborations, and develops industry-adopted innovations that shape the future of AI-native telecom systems.

speakers.name: Rajeshwari Devaramani
speakers.bio: Rajeshwari Devaramani is a solutions architect at NVIDIA as part of the company's new college grad program. She is working with the CUDA technical marketing engineering team. Rajeshwari holds a Master’s degree in computational science and engineering from the Georgia Institute of Technology. Her background includes GPU programming, high-performance computing, and deep learning.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Faster Together: Train and Deploy a Speculative Decoding Model for Low-Latency LLM Inference
sessions.abstract:
Dive deep into theory and practice of low-latency inference by deploying NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM with advanced speculative decoding techniques. You'll train an Eagle-3 draft head to propose candidate tokens efficiently, serve it, and benchmark it using AIPerf to quantify how these strategies minimize latency.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with bash and Python.
  • Familiarity with the concept of token.

speakers.name: Mireille Fares
speakers.bio: Mireille specializes in developing multi-modal generative AI models, particularly for synthesizing and animating gestures in embodied conversational agents. Her expertise spans human behavior modeling, signal processing, natural language processing, sentiment analysis, and deep learning, with a focus on Transformers. Mireille holds a Ph.D. in computer science and artificial intelligence from Sorbonne University.

sessions.title: Create Generative AI Workflow for Design and Visualization in ComfyUI
sessions.abstract:
The latest image and video generation models, combined with LLM, are fantastic tools to build powerful agentic AI workflows, but they're hard to access through too many cloud access points to efficiently iterate on concepts and stay up to date. In this two-hour instructor-led workshop, you'll learn how to use GPU acceleration for ComfyUI on local workstation or servers, pulling the latest models to build complex generative workflows. Workflows will range from image generation and separation, artefact removal, 3D generation, Physically based rendering (PBR) material generation, 360 and HDR Image Based Lighting (IBL) generation, as well as video generation. Whether you are a developer willing to learn what models work best for what tasks, or an industrial designer or digital artist willing to understand how to drive AI with your inputs, this course demonstrates how ComfyUI can be used to power design and visualization workflows with Gen AI.

Prerequisite(s):

  • A desire to learn. Whether you are an expert in models, ComfyUI or a beginner, you should learn something if you are ready to drink the AI from the firehose.

speakers.name: Alessandro La Tona
speakers.bio:
Alessandro La Tona is a compositor supervisor and passionate FX artist who specializes in film and TV shows. After working at CoSA VFX until 2023, and contributing to popular TV shows like "Westworld," "Lucifer," and "Gotham," and movies titles like "The Prom," "The Unbearable Weight of Massive Talent," and "Togo," he joined NVIDIA to master the latest generative AI technology applied to design and art.
 
Alessandro is familiar working with all visual effects departments. He has a passion for dynamic simulations and procedural effects, and enjoys the challenges of R&D.

speakers.name: Ashlee Martino-Tarr
speakers.bio: Ashlee is a 3D workflow specialist based in California. She has a passion for designing and creating creatures and specializes in 3D materials, texturing, and lighting to accomplish her designs. Recently, she became a main driver in NVIDIA's initiatives with generative AI, through third-party models or NVIDIA Picasso.

speakers.name: Daniela Flamm Jackson
speakers.bio: Daniela is a visual effects artist with a background in traditional oil and acrylic painting. Now working in 3D tools such as Maya, UE, Houdini, Nuke, and Blender, she has a passion for lighting and compositing, as well as creature designs.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: Deploy State-of-the-Art Gen AI on Your RTX PRO Workstation
sessions.abstract:
This session guides you through the end-to-end deployment of generative image models on NVIDIA RTX PRO workstations by leveraging ONNX Runtime and TensorRT-RTX. We'll demonstrate how to ship a multi-stage model pipeline across a wide set of hardware without sacrificing performance. This workflow will be powered by classic graphics APIs, ONNX Runtime, and the TensorRT-RTX Execution Provider. You'll leave with the skills to convert raw models into high-performance, offline-capable applications that run seamlessly across diverse hardware architectures.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Foundational knowledge of Python and GenAI models

speakers.name: Julius Tischbein
speakers.bio: Julius Tischbein is a developer technology engineer for CUDA and AI on ARM platforms at NVIDIA. His passion for optimizing AI workloads helps external partners and internal teams to best leverage the performance of NVIDIA accelerators on ARM architectures. Before joining NVIDIA, he studied computer science at RWTH Aachen.

speakers.name: Maximilian Mueller
speakers.bio: Maximilian Müller is a developer technology engineer for professional visualization at NVIDIA. His passion for computer vision and deep learning helps external partners and internal teams to best leverage the performance of NVIDIA accelerators using CUDA. Before joining NVIDIA, he acquired an M.Sc. in electrical and computational engineering at RWTH Aachen.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: Split and Win: Dynamo's Prefill-Decode Disaggregation and Smart KV Routing for Extreme LLM Throughput
sessions.abstract:
Master the deployment of massive mixture-of-expert architectures by configuring Dynamo’s PD Disaggregation with Large Expert Parallelism to maximize GPU efficiency. Learn to optimize distributed data flows including KV Cache Aware Routing to achieve an order-of-magnitude increase in serving throughput while meeting strict production latency demands.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with LLM inference pipelines (prefill-decode, kv-cache, etc) and compilers including TensorRT-LLM, vLLM, or SGLang

speakers.name: Arun Raman
speakers.bio: Arun Raman is a senior solution architect at NVIDIA specializing in AI applications in edge, cloud, and on-prem for the consumer internet industry. In his current role, he works on end-to-end AI pipelines including preprocessing, training, and inference. In addition to his AI work, he has also worked on a wide range of products including network routers and switches, multi-cloud infrastructure, and services. He holds a master’s degree in electrical engineering from the University of Texas at Dallas.

speakers.name: Kyle Huang
speakers.bio: Kyle Huang is a Gen AI solutions architect specializing in LLM inference optimization. He expertly leverages NVIDIA NIM and Dynamo to accelerate model performance. Kyle’s technical toolkit includes TensorRT-LLM,  Kubernetes, and Ray, enabling him to build highly efficient, scalable AI deployments. He is dedicated to reducing latency and computational costs for production-grade generative AI.

speakers.name: Utkarsh Uppal
speakers.bio: Utkarsh Uppal is a senior applied deep learning solutions architect at NVIDIA, where he specializes in building high-performance deep learning pipelines across domains like language and speech. His primary focus is on developing end-to-end conversational AI systems, including training LLMs from scratch, particularly for Indic languages, and building domain-specific models with enterprises. He also has deep expertise in designing and optimizing inference architectures for production, with a focus on low-precision formats (FP4, FP8), decoding strategies, and KV-cache optimizations.

sessions.title: Efficient Workload Management for AI Factories: What Every Admin Needs to Know
sessions.abstract:
In this hands-on lab you'll learn, as an AI factory administrator, how real workloads are run on a cluster. Intended for IT types (not data scientists!), this lab will have you configuring Run:ai, allocating and monitoring resources, and building a workload with NVIDIA NIMS to understand just what the user experience is, and what tools are at your disposal to make the AI factory workloads actually work.

Prerequisite(s):

  • To be successful, participants should have an understanding of AI clusters, Linux, and containers.
  • Bring a laptop as this session is very interactive!

speakers.name: Max Steele
speakers.bio: Max Steele develops and delivers training on DGX systems, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, and Base Command Manager. Max received his Ph.D. from the University of Texas at Dallas, where he currently serves as an adjunct assistant professor in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department. His research focused on applying deep learning in a clinical setting to automatically assess patient mobility.

speakers.name: Terrell Bennett
speakers.bio: Terrell is a technical content developer on the Education Services team at NVIDIA. Previously, Terrell worked as a faculty member in the Jonsson School of Engineering and Computer Science at the University of Texas at Dallas. Terrell obtained his Ph.D. in electrical engineering from U-T Dallas with research focused on algorithms for wearable devices.

sessions.title: Build Cost-Effective, Scalable RAG Pipelines: From Ingestion to Response Generation
sessions.abstract: We'll walk you through the full life cycle of a RAG pipeline: data ingestion, chunking and embedding, retrieval orchestration, and response generation, emphasizing robustness techniques such as hybrid retrieval, fallback strategies, caching, and constraint-aware prompting, along with patterns for monitoring quality and detecting drift over time. You'll also explore scaling concerns, including index sharding, cost-aware architecture choices, and aligning RAG design with product requirements and service-level agreements. By the end, you'll have implemented and evaluated a RAG pipeline that balances answer quality, latency, and reliability, providing a template for deploying similar systems in production.

speakers.name: Ruchika Kharwar
speakers.bio: Ruchika Kharwar is a product manager at NVIDIA with extensive experience in cloud computing and systems engineering. Before this, she was a customer engineer at Google, driving customer success and the adoption of Google Cloud Platform for nearly six years. Her background also includes roles at RedHat as a cloud success architect and at Texas Instruments in software development. Ruchika has earned certifications like Google Cloud Certified Professional Cloud Architect, Data Engineer, and others. She excels at bridging technical and customer-facing roles to ensure successful technology adoption and customer satisfaction.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Deploy and Customize Your AI Factory: Make Your AI Factory Feel Like Home!
sessions.abstract:
This hands-on lab will get you down and dirty with customizing an AI factory. Experience how typical administrator tasks are made easier with NVIDIA Mission Control, and explore the interfaces and tools that NVIDIA Mission Control provides for doing "Day 1 and beyond" work.

Prerequisite(s):

  • To be successful, participants should have an understanding of AI clusters, Linux, and containers.
  • Bring a laptop as this session is very interactive!

speakers.name: Max Steele
speakers.bio: Max Steele develops and delivers training on DGX systems, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, and Base Command Manager. Max received his Ph.D. from the University of Texas at Dallas, where he currently serves as an adjunct assistant professor in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department. His research focused on applying deep learning in a clinical setting to automatically assess patient mobility.

speakers.name: Terrell Bennett
speakers.bio: Terrell is a technical content developer on the Education Services team at NVIDIA. Previously, Terrell worked as a faculty member in the Jonsson School of Engineering and Computer Science at the University of Texas at Dallas. Terrell obtained his Ph.D. in electrical engineering from U-T Dallas with research focused on algorithms for wearable devices.

sessions.title: Ultra Scale Runbook for PyTorch on NVIDIA GPUs for Training and Inference
sessions.abstract:
In this lab, we'll walk through large-scale training and deployment of a well-known mixture-of-experts (MoE) LLM. You'll learn how to scale MoE pre-training by composing several parallelism techniques (FSDP, Tensor Parallel, Pipeline Parallel), memory saving techniques (Activation Checkpointing, CPU Offloading), and torch.compile driven optimizations. We'll use TorchTitan as our pre-training framework. We'll also demonstrate how to deploy such a model using the vLLM framework. You'll learn how to apply CUDA-accelerated inference-specific optimizations available in vLLM.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Knowledge of PyTorch concepts and modules.
  • Knowledge of MoE LLM models like DeepSeek-V3 or LLAMA4-Scout.
  • Knowledge of system concepts like docker, CUDA, distributed processes.

speakers.name: Syed Ahmed
speakers.bio: Syed Ahmed is a senior software engineer on the PyTorch Core team at NVIDIA, focused on keeping PyTorch fast and numerically stable on current NVIDIA platforms, and making PyTorch more expressive on future NVIDIA platforms. He holds a master’s degree in electrical engineering from the University of Pennsylvania, where he worked on accelerating field-programmable gate array compilation.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Deep Learning Frameworks

sessions.title: MLOps Best Practices: Build an AI Agent
sessions.abstract:
Learn practical ways to implement MLOps first principles as you create autonomous AI agents using LangGraph and NVIDIA NIM by building an agentic system that demonstrates tool integration, multi-step reasoning, and adaptive planning. Master the foundational agent architecture that MLOps engineers use to automate tedious workflows like incident investigation, experiment documentation, and model health monitoring — then customize it for your own use cases.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic programming knowledge

speakers.name: Edward Li
speakers.bio: Edward Li is a technical marketing engineer with NVIDIA Enterprise Computing. He is a recent graduate of the University of Pennsylvania School of Engineering and Applied Science. He holds a bachelor’s degree and a master’s degree in computer science with a concentration in data science. At NVIDIA, Edward is passionate about data science, AI, and ML, and is working on solutions to bring generative AI to enterprises.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: High-Performance LLM Serving and Training with SGLang
sessions.abstract:
This training lab shows you how to optimize and scale LLM workflows with SGLang. We walk through practical performance tuning using the SGL-Cookbook, dive into profiling and bottleneck analysis for developers, and demonstrate its deep integration into reinforcement learning (RL) training frameworks by showing how to use SGLang in a real RL run with the Miles RL framework.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Comfortable with Python and command-line workflows
  • Curious about how LLM systems are optimized and scaled in practice
  • No prior experience with SGLang, performance tuning, or RL training is required

sessions.title: Accelerate Video Frame Extraction and Labeling
sessions.abstract:
Learn how to reduce the cost associated with frame extraction for computer vision post-processing using free and publicly-accessible American Sign Language data. Use a novel, open-source framework and contribute to the world's largest dataset.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Have GitHub Account
  • Understand Python and Poetry
  • Have AWS Command Line Interface Configured (for S3 Operations)

speakers.name: Suseella Panguluri
speakers.bio: Suseella Panguluri is a senior engineering manager at NVIDIA specializing in LLMOps. She leads the development of software tools and workflows for end-to-end Gen AI model life cycle that includes large-scale training data acquisition, model fine-tuning, and performing LLM evaluations. She has more than 19 years of experience working as an engineering manager and as an engineer. She previously worked at Amazon, PayPal, and IBM, and holds an engineering degree from the National Institute of Technology, Warangal, India.

speakers.name: Khanh Nguyen
speakers.bio: Khanh Nguyen is a machine learning software engineer at NVIDIA, focused on applying AI models to accelerate data annotation and improve dataset quality for training. Their work enhances efficiency in generating high-quality labeled data, advancing NVIDIA’s mission to build smarter and faster AI systems.

speakers.name: Radha Sri-Tharan
speakers.bio: Radha Sri-Tharan is a data engineer at NVIDIA specializing in human-in-the-loop annotation operations. They design and automate end-to-end workflows that optimize dataset ingestion, validation, and processing, ensuring scalable and efficient data pipelines. Their contributions advance the development of domain-specific large language models for autonomous vehicles, robotics, content safety, and trustworthy AI.

sessions.title: How to Accelerate AI Workflows With NVIDIA Run:ai
sessions.abstract:
Unlock faster, smarter AI development in this hands-on introduction to the NVIDIA Run:ai platform. In this lab, you’ll explore how Run:ai streamlines machine learning operations, removes infrastructure bottlenecks, and simplifies workload management to accelerate outcomes. Learn how dynamic resource allocation transforms efficiency across enterprise teams, ensuring workloads run faster, smoother, and at scale. Then apply what you learn directly in your lab environment as instructors guide you through real-world examples of accelerated ML operations. You’ll leave with practical skills and a strong foundation for optimizing performance with Run:ai.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of machine learning workflows and terminology.
  • General familiarity with containerized environments (Docker or Kubernetes preferred).
  • No prior Run:ai experience required.

speakers.name: David Whitehouse
speakers.bio:
David is a lab manager and technical specialist within NVIDIA Education Services, where he designs and delivers hands-on technical training experiences. He brings deep expertise in data engineering, infrastructure, and DevOps, with practical knowledge across Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, data pipelines, and the broader HPC ecosystem.

Before recently joining Education Services, David spent two years with the NVIDIA Run:AI team as a post-sales solutions architect, working with enterprise customers to plan, deploy, and optimize large-scale AI and ML environments. His career in technology follows a distinguished tenure in the Royal Navy.

sessions.title: Advanced Workload Orchestration With NVIDIA Run:ai
sessions.abstract:
NVIDIA Run:ai is a comprehensive platform that brings advanced, AI-native scheduling and resource management to GPU infrastructure, enabling enterprises to accelerate and scale AI operations efficiently, reduce costs, and accelerate innovation. In this lab, you will learn the basics of NVIDIA Run:ai and how to create AI workloads. Designed for AI practitioners, this lab guides you through the practical steps of creating, launching, and monitoring interactive, inference, and training workloads using NVIDIA Run:ai.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with the NVIDIA Run:ai platform
  • Familiarity with containerized environments (e.g., Docker)
  • General knowledge of AI/ML workflows
  • Experience working with machine learning libraries and tools, such as PyTorch and Jupyter Lab

speakers.name: Juan Delgado
speakers.bio: Juan is currently a Senior Technical Instructor for AI and Data Science at NVIDIA. Before joining NVIDIA, Juan worked at Coursera and Udacity developing AI/ML courses.

sessions.title: How to Provision and Install NVIDIA Run:ai
sessions.abstract:
Get grounded in the practical fundamentals of deploying NVIDIA Run:ai from the ground up. This lab walks you through every stage of installation—from environment preparation to final validation—so you can confidently stand-up Run:ai within your own organization. Follow a guided, instructor-led deployment in your provided lab environment, learn how to verify installations, and practice diagnosing and resolving common setup issues. Whether you’re preparing for full production rollout or building a proof of concept, this session delivers the skills to deploy successfully and maintain stability.

Prerequisite(s):

  • General experience with Kubernetes cluster administration.
  • Basic understanding of containerized workloads and GPU infrastructure.

speakers.name: David Whitehouse
speakers.bio:
David is a lab manager and technical specialist within NVIDIA Education Services, where he designs and delivers hands-on technical training experiences. He brings deep expertise in data engineering, infrastructure, and DevOps, with practical knowledge across Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, data pipelines, and the broader HPC ecosystem.

Before recently joining Education Services, David spent two years with the NVIDIA Run:AI team as a post-sales solutions architect, working with enterprise customers to plan, deploy, and optimize large-scale AI and ML environments. His career in technology follows a distinguished tenure in the Royal Navy.

sessions.title: Scalable and Resilient Training with NeMo Megatron-Bridge
sessions.abstract: As modern data centers evolve into AI factories, training foundational models becomes critical. But scaling these training pipelines across tens of thousands of GPUs introduces significant challenges—such as node failures, network errors, checkpointing overhead—that can quickly turn occasional anomalies into frequent production issues. In our hands-on lab, learn about the challenges of running training pipelines and how to integrate resiliency and fault-tolerance features. We'll present various fault-tolerance mechanisms provided in frameworks like NVRx and PyTorch, and demonstrate how to achieve robust, scalable training in modern AI factories using NVIDIA NeMo. Gain practical insights into techniques for measuring I/O and overall  infrastructure performance. You should be familiar with Python and deep learning frameworks, understand LLM training concepts, and be comfortable with Linux.

speakers.name: Mohak Chadha
speakers.bio: Mohak Chadha is based in Munich, where he focuses on designing solutions for enterprise AI applications. Before this, he was at Firebolt, a unicorn startup, where he managed their cloud infrastructure and worked on improving security. With a strong foundation in distributed systems, Mohak brings expertise in cloud computing, parallel processing, and high-performance computing. He earned a Ph.D. in computer science from TU Munich, graduating summa cum laude.

speakers.name: Oleg Sudakov
speakers.bio: Oleg Sudakov concentrates on LLM training and conversational AI. Previously, he worked as a machine learning engineer and data scientist at Apple, Huawei, and Rakuten. He has a strong background in natural language processing and speech processing. Oleg is based in Germany.

speakers.name: Pramod Kumbhar
speakers.bio: Pramod Kumbhar is a senior solutions architect in HPC and AI at NVIDIA. He focuses on resiliency and performance of large-scale training and AI infrastructure. Pramod collaborates with partners building models and deploying AI infrastructure. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he spent 15 years in the HPC and scientific computing domain, developing and optimizing scientific applications and also operation of large-scale supercomputing systems.

speakers.name: Shreya Gupta
speakers.bio: Shreya is an AI architect building resiliency products to make large-scale AI training fault-tolerant, including open-source tools like NVIDIA Resiliency Extension. Previously, she fine-tuned LLM software for enterprises. Shreya holds a master’s degree in symbolic systems from Stanford University, where she conducted research at the Stanford Vision and Learning Lab on robot learning and vision neuroscience. Her experience spans model training, natural language processing, and product development. She's co-authored papers in EACL and CoRL and received the Google Women Techmaker Scholar award. She loves mentoring, technical writing, and supporting innovation through hackathons and roundtables.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models

sessions.title: Teach AI to Code in Every Language With NVIDIA NeMo
sessions.abstract: “Teaching AI to Code in Every Language with NVIDIA NeMo” is a hands-on training lab for machine learning practitioners, data scientists, and developers who want to build state-of-the-art coding language models on NVIDIA’s platform. Participants will learn how to use NVIDIA NeMo and NVIDIA GPU-accelerated infrastructure to train multilingual LLMs for code, from dataset preparation and cleaning to large-scale distributed training and optimization. The session will cover key challenges of multilingual code modeling, such as balancing languages, handling noisy repositories, and designing evaluation strategies that reflect real-world developer workflows. Attendees will implement practical techniques for scaling training across large compute environments, apply best practices for performance tuning, and explore how these models can power developer assistants, code review tools, and enterprise codebase management solutions. By the end of the lab, participants will have a clear roadmap to experiment with, train, and deploy advanced multilingual coding LLMs using NVIDIA’s end-to-end ecosystem.

speakers.name: Meriem Bendris
speakers.bio: Meriem is a deep learning solutions architect manager, leading a team that helps European, Middle Eastern, and African partners build neural networks for speech, natural language processing, and computer vision. Her areas of expertise include conversational AI and multi-modality. Meriem holds a Ph.D. in signal and image processing from Telecom ParisTech, where she focused on machine learning applied to speech and computer vision.

speakers.name: Miguel Martinez
speakers.bio: Miguel Martínez is a senior applied deep learning researcher at NVIDIA, where he focuses on training large language models as part of the ADLR team. He specializes in generative AI and machine learning, with deep expertise in GPU-accelerated, end-to-end data science workflows. Previously, he mentored students in Udacity’s Artificial Intelligence Nanodegree program, helping practitioners build practical skills in modern AI.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Hands-On NVIDIA DGX Spark (Sponsored by GCL)
sessions.abstract: NVIDIA is teaming up with GCL for a hands-on lab on NVIDIA DGX Spark. In this lab, you will build an AI agent using the latest open-source NVIDIA and community model, and explore enhancing and extending the agent abilities.
sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Train and Test End-to-End Autonomous Vehicles with Alpamayo
sessions.abstract: This workshop will help you get started with the Alpamayo suite of models, frameworks, and data for building reasoning-based autonomous vehicles. Learn how to run neural reconstructions on the AlpaSim simulator with the NVIDIA Physical AI Open Datasets and connect it to the Alpamayo 1 end-to-end stack. By the end of the session, you'll have hands-on experience running neural simulations and will be more familiar working with end‑to‑end stacks.

speakers.name: Itai Zadok
speakers.bio: Itai Zadok is a senior product manager at NVIDIA, leading neural reconstruction and world foundation model products for autonomous vehicle simulation. Itai holds a bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering from Tel Aviv University and a joint M.B.A.-AI degree from the Kellogg School of Management and the McCormick School of Engineering at Northwestern University.

speakers.name: Stefanie Manzinger
speakers.bio: Stefanie Manzinger is a senior solutions architect for Automotive and Manufacturing at NVIDIA. She helps customers accelerate physical AI development with NVIDIA Cosmos and drives the adoption of generative AI for industrial applications. Stefanie holds a Ph.D. from the Technical University of Munich, where her doctoral research specialized in decision-making and trajectory planning for autonomous vehicles.

sessions.title: Transform Massive Raw Data Into Diverse High-Quality Post-Training Datasets
sessions.abstract: Struggling to gather high-quality data for your vision language models? This hands-on training lab walks you through a complete synthetic data generation pipeline—from curation and augmentation to pseudo-annotation and quality control. You’ll learn how to transform any data into a high-quality dataset to post-train your vision language models (VLMs) for your domain-specific use cases.

speakers.name: Aidan Ladenburg
speakers.bio: coming soon

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation

sessions.title: Inspecting Multimodal Data with Nemotron and Gemma 3
