sessions.abstract: This workshop introduces a multi-agent approach to solving complex, visual-heavy enterprise tasks. You will learn to use Google Gemma 3 as an agentic orchestrator to plan tasks, call tools, and leverage Nemotron for high-fidelity visual reasoning.

speakers.name: Sandeep Sadasivuni
speakers.bio: Sandeep is an independent software vendor customer engineer with a focus on driving technical go-to-market for build partners on Google Cloud. Previously, Sandeep developed Gen AI cloud partnerships at Google by guiding service partners to illustrate how Google Cloud services can help customers innovate, bring Gen AI solutions to market faster, differentiate, and create new revenue streams.

speakers.name: Chuck Freeman
speakers.bio: Chuck serves Google Cloud GTM at NVIDIA, focusing on helping organizations accelerate their AI, machine learning, and high-performance computing workloads through NVIDIA's cutting-edge chips and software solutions on Google Cloud. Prior to NVIDIA, Chuck spent six years at Google, holding various roles ranging from managing partnerships to driving go-to-market solution strategies for Startups across North America.

speakers.name: Juan Pablo (JP) Guerra
speakers.bio: JP is an experienced software solutions architect specializing in designing and deploying innovative applications in cloud environments. With a strong focus on Google Cloud Platform (GCP), he excels in integrating NVIDIA software solutions to unlock the power of AI, machine learning, and GPU-accelerated computing. Their expertise bridges cutting-edge technology with real-world implementation, helping organizations maximize performance and scalability in the cloud.

sessions.title: Always-On Autonomous AI Agents: Exploring the OpenClaw Abstraction
sessions.abstract:
As agents evolve from basic conversational interfaces to personal complex task navigators, the primary challenge shifts from writing context strategies to designing robust systems. This hands-on workshop explores the engineering needed to manage persistent agent state, secure tool execution, and complex workflow creation in a low-code, high-capacity context. 

Using OpenClaw, a popular open-source agent gateway, we illustrate how a single daemon can manage sessions across multiple applications, maintain long-term interpretable memory, and safely execute tools in a virtual user-centric environment. We emphasize low-code abstractions and out-of-the-box capabilities while maintaining enough rigor to motivate effective and secure best practices. You'll be able to expand on these ideas and improve your agents' capabilities as new innovations arrive and the field continues to evolve.

speakers.name: Kevin Lee
speakers.bio: Kevin Lee is a senior technical content developer on the Deep Learning Institute Team at NVIDIA. Kevin’s work focuses on raising awareness and driving adoption for GPU-accelerated technologies with an emphasis on data science, computer vision, and large language models. Prior to NVIDIA, Kevin led a risk analytics team at Morgan Stanley and taught data science and machine learning at the University of California, Berkeley.

speakers.name: Vadim Kudlay
speakers.bio: Vadim works as a senior content developer at the NVIDIA Deep Learning Institute with focus in agentic orchestration and generative AI education. He spearheads and enables course development/design, and helps to progress frameworks for inclusion in such presentations. Prior to NVIDIA, Vadim specialized in deep learning and computer vision (B.S. in computer science from University of Richmond, Class of 2021; M.Sc. in computer science at Brown University, Class of 2023).

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Accelerated Networking for AI Infrastructure
sessions.abstract:
Gain a holistic understanding of AI factory networking, exploring how key communication methods between GPUs, racks, and storage shape overall AI performance and scalability.


Bring your own laptop and experience AI factory networking in action. In AI factories, the network is critical to maximizing performance. You’ll see how networking choices shape AI workload performance and scalability, then configure key pieces yourself in guided labs.

We start with a level set session that walks through essential communication paths in an AI factory deployments: east-west GPU-to-GPU communication across racks with InfiniBand, north south and storage connectivity over Spectrum-X Ethernet, and low latency, high bandwidth NVLINK communication between GPUs within the same rack.


By the end of the session, you’ll be familiar with the key networking technologies that underpin AI factories and have a clear view of how network design decisions translate directly into faster, more efficient AI workloads.

    Prerequisite(s):

    • Basic Linux administration skills, including directory navigation, file management, and text editing with nano or vim are required.
    • Intermediate TCP/IP networking experience is expected, with familiarity in L2/L3 protocols, IP networking concepts, and proficiency using tools such as iperf, wireshark, and the command line.

speakers.name: Nawar Nawar
speakers.bio: Nawar is a technical instructor on the Education Services team at NVIDIA and an electrical engineer by training, with over two decades of experience designing, operating, and teaching large-scale networking systems for cloud, service provider, and enterprise environments. With multiple CCIE certifications in routing and switching, service provider, security, and wireless, Nawar helps practitioners build high-performance, secure, and scalable networking foundations for modern AI infrastructure.

sessions.title: Fundamentals of GPU-Accelerated Workflows with CUDA Python
sessions.abstract:
This course delivers a hands-on introduction to GPU-accelerated computing in Python, empowering developers to build fast, scalable applications using NVIDIA’s CUDA ecosystem. Through guided notebooks, participants master CuPy for array acceleration, cuDF for GPU DataFrames, and the cuda-python API to write custom kernels—all without leaving Python. Real-world exercises in data science, machine learning, and scientific computing emphasize performance, interoperability, and end-to-end efficiency.

Learners progress from drop-in speedups to fully integrated GPU pipelines, mastering data movement, asynchronous execution, and profiling with Nsight tools. By the end, attendees can transform CPU-bound Python code into production-grade GPU solutions, bridge prototyping to deployment, and apply best practices for performance and reproducibility in modern accelerated computing.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic Python competency including familiarity with variable types, loops, conditional statements, functions, and array manipulations.
  • NumPy competency including the use of ndarrays and ufuncs.
  • No previous knowledge of CUDA programming is assumed.

speakers.name: Bryce Lelbach
speakers.bio: Bryce Adelstein Lelbach has spent over a decade developing programming languages, compilers, and libraries. He leads programming language efforts and drives the technical roadmap for NVIDIA's HPC compilers and libraries. He's passionate about C++ and is one of the leaders of the C++ community, having served as chair of the C++ Library Evolution group. Outside of work, Bryce is passionate about airplanes and watches.

speakers.name: Katrina Riehl
speakers.bio: Katrina is an experienced data science and software development manager with a history of building data mining, machine learning, visualization, and artificial intelligence technologies. She's a strong engineering professional skilled in Python, NumPy, SciPy, Scikit-learn, R, C++, C, Java, Spark, and data science.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Adding New Knowledge to LLMs
sessions.abstract:
In today's AI landscape, even powerful Large Language Models (LLMs) face limitations when confronted with specialized business knowledge, technical domains, or cultural contexts absent from their training data. While retrieval-augmented generation can mitigate some gaps, true domain mastery requires deeper model adaptation.

This comprehensive workshop equips developers with hands-on skills to transform open-source LLMs into domain-specialized AI assets. Through five interconnected modules, you'll master the complete lifecycle of model customization. By workshop completion, you'll possess the complete technical skillset to develop, deploy, and operate sovereign AI systems tailored to your specific requirements - from data preparation to production scaling. Bring your laptop; we'll provide the GPUs.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.


Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate Python programming skills.
  • Previous work with LLM-based applications and understanding of prompt engineering principles.
  • Experience with data processing pipelines and text preprocessing techniques.
  • Understanding of fine-tuning, training/validation splits, and basic ML metrics.
  • Basic knowledge of GPU acceleration for ML workloads (CUDA experience helpful but not required).
  • Familiarity with containerization and basic Kubernetes concepts (Optional but helpful).

speakers.name: John Jahanipour
speakers.bio: John Jahanipour is a Senior Solutions Architect on the Worldwide Field Operations team at NVIDIA. His work focuses on enabling enterprise adoption of accelerated computing platforms, particularly in the healthcare and manufacturing industries, with an emphasis on generative AI. John's technical expertise spans AI model optimization, GPU infrastructure sizing, and deployment strategies for both cloud and on-premises environments. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he contributed to AI research and development in both academic and industrial settings. He holds a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering with a specialization in applied machine learning and AI.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)

sessions.title: Building Observable and Scalable Multi-Agent Workflows for Asset Lifecycle Management
sessions.abstract:
Learn to develop multi-agent workflows tailored for Asset Lifecycle Management use cases within industrial process applications. You'll be introduced to the NVIDIA open-source NeMo Agent Toolkit (NAT) and its key features, enabling the creation of a comprehensive and customizable reference solution for this use case. You'll also be guided in effectively utilizing other components of NVIDIA AI stack, including NVIDIA Inference Microservices (NIM), cloud endpoints, and NVIDIA Tesseract time series foundational models. These components are used to design agents capable of executing frequently performed tasks such as data querying and analysis, Forecasting, Remaining useful life prediction, and Anomaly detection. Furthermore, you will gain insight into NAT’s marquee features, such as workflow observability and sizing, prompt optimization, and third-party connections.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.


Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic familiarity with Python.

speakers.name: Viraj Modak
speakers.bio: TBD

speakers.name: Vineeth Kalluru Srinivas
speakers.bio: Vineeth Kalluru is a senior solutions architect in the NVIDIA Azure team. He focuses on integrating NVIDIA software with Azure services and helping enterprises with deploying distributed LLM inference and Gen AI applications. He previously worked on developing deep learning compiler technologies at SambaNova systems and Tensor Processing Unit architecture design at Google. Recently he contributed to designing solutions for deploying NIMs on AzureML and other Azure services.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Building AI Agents with Multimodal Models
sessions.abstract:
Just like how humans have multiple senses to perceive the world around them, computers have a variety of sensors to help perceive the human world. In the health industry, computed tomography (CT) scans provide a 3D representation used to detect potentially dangerous abnormalities. In the robotics industry, lidars are used to help robots see depth and navigate the complex topology around them. In this course, learners will develop neural network based multimodal models that can understand many different data types by exploring different fusion techniques.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.


Prerequisite(s):

  • Basic understanding of Python (including classes, objects, and decorators)
  • Basic understanding of Neural Networks such image convolution and sequential models (covered in Fundamentals of Deep Learning)

speakers.name: Mark Moyou
speakers.bio: Mark Moyou is on the Retail team, working with North America's top retailers on scaling ML and Gen AI workloads. On the side, Mark is the host of the AI Portfolio Podcast, Caribbean Tech Pioneers Podcast, and the Optimized AI Conference in Atlanta.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: Building LLM Applications with Prompt Engineering
sessions.abstract:
With the incredible capabilities of large language models (LLMs), enterprises are eager to integrate them into their products and internal applications for a wide variety of use cases, including (but not limited to) text generation, large-scale document analysis, and chatbot assistants. The fastest way to begin leveraging LLMs for diverse tasks is by using modern prompt engineering techniques. These techniques are also foundational for more advanced LLM-based methods such as Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT). In this workshop, learners will work with an NVIDIA language model NIM, powered by the open-source Llama-3.1 large language model, alongside the popular LangChain library. The workshop will provide a foundational skill set for building a range of LLM-based applications using prompt engineering.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.


Prerequisite(s):

  • Familiarity with basic programming fundamentals such as functions and variables.

speakers.name: Matt Linder
speakers.bio: Matt Linder is a senior solutions architect on NVIDIA's Developer Programs Solutions Engineering team, currently focusing on retrieval-augmented generation and large language model operations. Prior to his work at NVIDIA, Matt worked on recommender systems and habit formation for a popular consumer app, and prior to that, he was a touring professional classical musician. Matt loves learning in all its forms, and he enjoys teaching humans and machines alike.

sessions.title: OpenUSD Crash Course: Build 3D Data Pipelines for Physical AI
sessions.abstract:
Discover how OpenUSD principles and Python scripting can build scalable 3D data pipelines for manufacturing, robotics, and physical AI applications. Throughout this full-day workshop, you'll learn composition arc techniques, asset hierarchy design, and performance optimization strategies essential for production pipelines. Develop practical skills in automating 3D asset workflows, managing complex scene data, and preparing simulation environments for physical AI training. This workshop directly prepares you for the NVIDIA-Certified Professional: OpenUSD Development exam.

Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.

Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate Python experience, including packages, virtual environments, and scripting fundamentals.
  • Familiarity with 3D modeling concepts: meshes, materials, transforms, scene hierarchies, and asset organization.
  • Understanding of data pipeline concepts and file-based workflows.
  • Basic knowledge of composition and layering concepts in content creation tools.

speakers.name: Daniel Roizman
speakers.bio: As CEO of UME.Studio, Daniel specializes in AI technology enablement and secure virtual platforms for enterprise customers. He helps accelerate the adoption of complex systems like digital twins and Physical AI.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: How to Simulate, Train, Validate, and Deploy an End-to-End Robotics Workflow with NVIDIA Isaac
sessions.abstract:
In this full-day workshop, we'll guide you through an end-to-end robotic workflow—from simulation-based training to real-world robot deployment. You'll simulate the robot in NVIDIA Isaac Sim™, train the policies in Isaac Lab, validate the trained skills through software-in-the-loop testing, and deploy them to physical robots. Through practical exercises, you'll gain end-to-end experience in the techniques that bridge the sim-to-real gap: synthetic data generation, policy training and refinement, hardware testing on edge devices, and real-robot deployment. This workshop demonstrates how simulation accelerates robot learning and reduces the time and cost of bringing AI-powered automation to production environments.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.


Prerequisite(s):

  • Intermediate experience with Python, including working with packages, virtual environments, and basic scripting.
  • Familiarity with 3D content concepts such as meshes, materials, transforms, and scene hierarchies from tools like Blender, Maya, or similar DCC applications.
  • Understand command-line usage for running scripts and managing project files. Understand basics of world foundation models.
  • Have familiarity with robotics or simulation workflows (for example, using simulation tools or working with physics-enabled assets).

speakers.name: Maycon Carvalho
speakers.bio: Maycon Carvalho is the solutions architect for robotics and embedded systems at NVIDIA. Maycon leads customer engagements in robotics development, simulation, and deployment, with particular focus on simulation pipelines for validation of AI-based systems through software-in-the-loop and hardware-in-the-loop workflows, and eventual deployments at the edge.

speakers.name: Kartik Sachdev
speakers.bio: Kartik is a solutions architect for Robotics and AI at NVIDIA. He leads customer engagements across a range of AI driven autonomous machines and robotics with command in topics such as robot planning, computer vision, reinforcement learning and ROS1/2. He brings expertise in NVIDIA platforms such as Isaac Sim, Isaac Lab, and Omniverse among others and has a demonstrated history of working in different geographies - India, Germany and Japan.

speakers.name: Lior Ben Horin
speakers.bio: Lior Ben Horin is a Senior Developer Relations Manager at NVIDIA, specializing in Autonomous Robotics, SDG, and Simulation. As a seasoned CGI expert, prior to joining NVIDIA, he served as CTO and Pipeline Engineer in international VFX and SDG studios. Lior brings over a decade of academic experience, having served as a Professor at the Bezalel Academy of Arts and Design. He is passionate about assisting engineers and product teams in developing workflows using state-of-the-art technologies for AI-driven innovation.

speakers.name: Zhijin Li
speakers.bio: Zhijin Li is a Senior Solutions Architect at NVIDIA EMEA, focusing on MedTech. With a Ph.D. in Applied Mathematics from Université Paris-Saclay and extensive experience in the Medical Devices industry, Zhijin has developed expertise in medical imaging, machine learning, federated learning, healthcare digital twins, and healthcare robotics. Zhijin's role involves assisting MedTech customers and academic partners in leveraging various NVIDIA technologies, including Holoscan, IGX, MONAI, NVFLARE, and Isaac for Healthcare, for algorithm development, software engineering, and applied research.

speakers.name: Jieming Cui
speakers.bio: Jieming Cui is a third-year Ph.D. student at the Institute of Artificial Intelligence, Peking University. Her research interests span embodied AI, robotics, and computer vision. She is currently an intern at NVIDIA, working on bridging sim-to-real gaps and robustness/consistency issues in real-world robotics deployment. She has published at leading venues including CVPR, CoRL, and NeurIPS, with Oral/Highlight recognitions, and serves as a reviewer for conferences such as RSS and CVPR.

speakers.name: Steven Palma
speakers.bio: Established engineer with over 6 years of experience ready to deliver end-to-end robotics projects with a proven track record of blending technical and management expertise in an interdisciplinary and intercultural environment. My professional experience spans over various fields, including: OSS & Certified Software, Autonomy, Perception, Networking, Electromechanics and Robotics AI Learning.

speakers.name: Jade Choghari
speakers.bio: Jade Choghari is part the LeRobot team at Hugging Face where he works on large-scale robot learning, simulation, and transformer-based policies. His research focuses on building scalable environments, datasets, and transformer-based control systems that enable robots to perform complex manipulation and real-world tasks. His education is in Computer Science at the University of Waterloo. His goal is to understand robotics datasets at scale and to build foundation models for robotics and physically actuated devices.

sessions.title: Deploying and Optimizing AI Inference at Scale
sessions.abstract:
As foundation models move toward deeper test-time computation, inference becomes the dominant scaling constraint. Latency, throughput, and cost are governed by a small set of forces: autoregressive decoding, KV-cache growth, memory bandwidth, and scheduling under contention. This workshop frames large-scale inference through these emerging laws of inference, starting from first principles and building toward real systems. Learners begin with monolithic and gateway-based vLLM deployments on Kubernetes to establish baseline behavior, then transition to NVIDIA Dynamo to operate aggregated and disaggregated inference architectures using built-in KV-aware routing and scheduling. A core emphasis is observability: attendees will deploy a full stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Tempo) to monitor metrics, capture structured logs, and perform distributed tracing. The outcome is a principled understanding of where inference time and money go - and how architectural choices bend those curves in production.


Certificate: Upon successful completion of the assessment, you’ll receive an NVIDIA certificate to recognize your subject matter competency and support your professional career growth.


Prerequisite(s):

  • Command-line proficiency.
  • Experience working with configuration files (e.g., YAML, JSON).
  • Familiarity with containers and container-based workflows (e.g., Docker) and some basic knowledge of Kubernetes.
  • Basic understanding of large language model (LLM) inference concepts.
  • Familiarity with distributed computing concepts, such as data or model parallelism.

speakers.name: Anshul Jindal
speakers.bio: Anshul is on NVIDIA's DGX Cloud team, where he specializes in helping customers deploy their workloads at scale. Anshul has a strong background in site reliability engineering and has extensive experience in managing production-grade Kubernetes clusters across various cloud service providers. He has a Ph.D. in computer science from TU Munich, graduating summa cum laude.

speakers.name: Mohak Chadha
speakers.bio: Mohak Chadha is based in Munich, where he focuses on designing solutions for enterprise AI applications. Before this, he was at Firebolt, a unicorn startup, where he managed their cloud infrastructure and worked on improving security. With a strong foundation in distributed systems, Mohak brings expertise in cloud computing, parallel processing, and high-performance computing. He earned a Ph.D. in computer science from TU Munich, graduating summa cum laude.

speakers.name: Severine Habert
speakers.bio: Séverine Habert is a senior solutions architect at NVIDIA for Supercomputing, where she helps European research centers and universities develop and deploy cutting-edge AI workloads on supercomputing infrastructure. She holds a Ph.D. from the Technical University of Munich.

sessions.title: Transform Data Center Economics With NVIDIA Grace- and Vera-Powered Edge Compute Servers (Presented by MiTAC)
sessions.abstract: Dive into the MiTAC R1917GC, featuring the NVIDIA Grace C1 CPU and PCIe Gen 5 I/O in an MGX 1U form factor. Discover how its industry-leading energy efficiency significantly reduces total cost of ownership (TCO) for edge data center workloads.

speakers.name: Raymond Huang
speakers.bio: Raymond Huang is general manager at MiTAC Computing Technology, where he oversees business growth initiatives in server and cloud computing solutions. With a strong background in enterprise technology, he has held leadership roles at ASUS, Inspur, and QCT, bringing deep expertise in data center infrastructure, AI platforms, and channel development. Raymond is recognized for building strategic alliances, expertise in go-to-market execution, strategic partnerships, and scaling enterprise solutions globally, driving innovation in the IT hardware space.

sessions.title: Why AI Requires Vertically Integrated, AI-Native Cloud Infrastructure (Presented by Lightning AI)
sessions.abstract: AI workloads aren’t web applications, and they’re pushing traditional hyperscaler clouds beyond the assumptions they were built on. Learn why full-stack, AI-native cloud delivers measurable gains in performance, cost, and iteration speed.

speakers.name: William Falcon
speakers.bio: William Falcon is the creator of PyTorch Lightning, a deep learning framework. He's also the founder and CEO of Lightning AI, and was previously a co-founder and CTO of NextGenVest. He began working on these projects while completing a Ph.D. at New York University that was funded by Google DeepMind and the National Science Foundation. He also worked as a researcher at Facebook AI and at Goldman Sachs.

sessions.title: Building the Next Frontier for the World’s Largest Sovereign AI Factories (Presented by Yotta)
sessions.abstract: India’s AI future will be defined by mass-scale inferencing, not just training—and sovereign AI factories will be the nation’s strategic engine for that shift. This session explores how Yotta, powered by NVIDIA, is building India’s first AI-inferencing-optimized cloud, and what the country must prepare for as demand accelerates.

speakers.name: Sunil Gupta
speakers.bio: Sunil Gupta, co-founder, managing director, and CEO of Yotta Data Services—known as the “Data Center Man of India”—is a prominent figure in India’s data center and cloud industries, with over 30 years of experience. An alumnus of Columbia University and NIT Kurukshetra, he leads the development of hyperscale data center parks and the NVIDIA-powered Shakti-Cloud platform, enhancing AI and HPC capabilities for enterprises.

sessions.title: Why the Data Center is Becoming the new Control Plane for Agentic AI (Presented by Equinix)
sessions.abstract: Data centers are no longer just about high‑density power and advanced liquid cooling—they’ve become the central gateway connecting private and public data, open and closed models, and an expansive ecosystem of AI service providers. Ultra‑reliable, low‑latency networks now link AI agents to distributed datasets while ensuring continuous sovereignty, security, and cost optimization through choice. As business needs evolve rapidly, the ability to adapt starts with the data center networking fabric. This session will explore how flexible, scalable infrastructure gives organizations a significant advantage when running distributed AI across hybrid multi-cloud environments.

speakers.name: Kevin Egan
speakers.bio: As senior director of Equinix technical marketing, Kevin is instrumental in defining Equinix’s distributed AI strategy.  His 25-plus years of experience with Cisco, HPE, and VMware give him unique insight into the market.

sessions.title: From Kernels to Clusters: How PyTorch Powers High-Performance AI (Presented by Meta)
sessions.abstract: Discover how PyTorch is redefining high-performance AI from the ground up. This session explores PyTorch’s latest advances—from custom kernel authoring and the Helion compiler stack to Monarch’s next-generation distributed runtime for reinforcement learning to Torchcomms’ vendor-neutral, scalable communication layer. We’ll highlight how PyTorch’s robust fault tolerance and elasticity enable reliable training at massive scale, and how these innovations together create a unified, production-ready stack for both research and deployment. Learn why PyTorch is the open foundation for AI innovation across clusters, clouds, and devices.

speakers.name: Alban Desmaison
speakers.bio: Alban Desmaison has been working on PyTorch since nearly the beginning, first during his Ph.D. at the University of Oxford and now at Meta. He's focused on maintaining core components, designing a wide breadth of features and fostering the PyTorch community.

sessions.title: Synergy at Scale: Unify Compute, Power and Cooling (Presented by COMPAL)
sessions.abstract: High-density AI workloads demand a paradigm shift, from siloed infrastructure to integrated ecosystems. This presentation reveals how a combined entity is leveraging its expanded scale and expertise to deliver the first truly total solution for next-gen data centers. We will explore how unifying compute, power, and liquid cooling into a single, validated solution stack eliminates integration risk, slashes deployment time, and ensures optimal performance for NVIDIA's GPU architecture. Learn how to transition from fragmented purchasing to a seamless, factory-integrated, plug-and-play AI infrastructure solution.

speakers.name: John Leung
speakers.bio: As the senior director for USA Products & Operations, John is responsible for the technical planning and operational execution of AI and high-performance computing server platforms in the U.S. market. He focuses on GPU-accelerated systems, platform architecture, thermal and power integration, and data center–scale deployment requirements. John leads cross-functional alignment across product roadmaps, manufacturing, and supply chain to deliver scalable, high-power AI platforms with performance, stability, and reliability for production environments.

sessions.title: A Modular Blueprint for Next-Generation AI Servers: Inside the MGX Architecture (Presented by MSI)
sessions.abstract: Explore how the MGX architecture is reshaping AI server design with its modular, building-block approach engineered to maximize performance for GPU-driven AI workloads, while also simplifying hardware reuse across multiple processor platforms to provide a scalable, future-ready foundation for evolving enterprise AI infrastructure. This session also showcases how optimized resource allocation, integrated internal cabling, and MGX-powered MSI AI platforms accelerate AI deployment, enhance efficiency, and support both on-premises and cloud environments.

speakers.name: Philip Maher
speakers.bio: Philip Maher, director of product marketing at MSI Enterprise Platform Solutions, has over 20 years of server industry experience. For his early years, Philip mastered his craft in enterprise IT support before graduating to field application engineering for original design manufacturer servers. He's spent his later years working in server product planning and architecture with Tier One cloud service providers.

sessions.title: Powering the AI Factory: From Platform Integration to Scalable AI Data Center Solutions (Presented by Pegatron)
sessions.abstract:
As AI workloads continue to grow in size, power density, and complexity, data centers must evolve beyond traditional architectures. This session explores how PEGATRON is enabling next‑generation AI factories through automated manufacturing and tightly integrated server platforms.

From accelerated rack‑level deployment to end‑to‑end infrastructure integration, PEGATRON delivers a comprehensive solution spanning compute, networking, and storage—engineered to interconnect racks and clusters in modern AI data centers.

speakers.name: Christopher Liang
speakers.bio: Christopher has over 20 years’ experience in the server and data center industry, and for the past five years has focused on server thermal optimization in terms of air cooling, direct liquid cooling, and immersion for different usage scenarios and workloads. He's also been working with cross-region partners to enhance material and component sourcing to provide the best total cost of ownership.

sessions.title: Liquid-Cooled AI Server Platforms for Next-Generation Performance (Presented by ASRock Rack, Inc.)
sessions.abstract: Explore ASRock Rack AI server solutions from edge to cloud and discover how ASRock Rack adopts liquid-cooling approaches in next-generation AI platforms to unlock higher performance and scalability. Learn how these liquid-cooled server designs are validated in ASRock Rack’s Liquid Cooling Lab to ensure reliability, efficiency, and readiness for modern AI workloads.

speakers.name: Charlotte Li
speakers.bio: Charlotte Li is the director of product marketing at ASRock Rack, with over a decade of experience in the server hardware and network appliance industry. With deep insight across x86 and ARM platforms, she is passionate about driving product innovation, positioning, and go-to-market strategy for advanced server and networking solutions from edge to cloud, collaborating with ecosystem partners and engaging with customers to address evolving needs and technical challenges.

sessions.title: Practical Applications of NVIDIA 3 Computers in Vision, Robotics, and Edge (Presented by EDOM Technology)
sessions.abstract: This session explores how NVIDIA 3 Computers—Train, Simulate, and Deploy—enable enterprises to turn AI models into real-world applications across computer vision, robotics, and edge AI. Learn how this end-to-end architecture accelerates AI deployment, reduces risk, and helps organizations scale AI from experimentation to production.

speakers.name: Wilson Yen
speakers.bio: Wilson Yen is product director at EDOM Technology, with over nine years of experience in semiconductor and product development sales. His focus is on networking and data center product development and sales, using his rich professional expertise to deeply understand customer design and development requirements and provide effective solutions for swift product development.

sessions.title: Building Sovereign AI: Scaling 100B+ Model Training on NVIDIA Blackwell Infrastructure (Presented by Lablup, Inc.)
sessions.abstract:
Dive deep into the real-world challenges of training 100 billion-plus parameter foundation models from scratch on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. We'll share hands-on experiences from a national-scale sovereign AI initiative, covering fault-tolerant orchestration, storage I/O optimization, and NVIDIA Collective Communication Library (NCCL) tuning strategies that keep massive training jobs running reliably across more than 500 B200 GPUs.

At this scale, GPU errors, NCCL timeouts, and storage slowdowns begin to appear. Learn how we built a resilient stack with automated failure recovery, resolved 10x storage degradation from NFS driver issues, tackled MXFP8 precision stability on Blackwell, and tuned RoCE and NVIDIA InfiniBand networks through cross-vendor collaboration.

speakers.name: Jeongkyu Shin
speakers.bio: Jeongkyu Shin, founder of Lablup, has a two-decade track record as a lead developer of open-source projects like Textcube, and as a AI/cloud expert at Google Developers Experts. Holding a Ph.D. in statistical physics and computational neuroscience from POSTECH, he now leads the development of Backend.AI, a scalable AI infrastructure operating platform.

sessions.title: Design an Enterprise-Grade Platform for Generative and Agentic AI at Scale (Presented by EXL Service)
sessions.abstract:
In this session we explore EXLerate.ai, EXL’s enterprise-grade platform for developing, orchestrating, and running Gen AI and agentic AI solutions at scale. We will illustrate how EXLerate.ai enables capabilities such as domain-aware copilots, agentic workflow orchestration, intelligent automation, and decision intelligence in enterprise environments.

While generative AI presents significant opportunities to transform how work gets done, it also introduces complex challenges related to enterprise data integration, contextual relevance, governance, security, cost efficiency, and operational reliability. EXLerate.ai addresses these challenges through a modular platform architecture, enterprise data and knowledge grounding, flexible model orchestration, and built-in guardrails designed to support responsible, scalable, and production-ready AI systems.

speakers.name: Aidan McGowran
speakers.bio: As vice president of AI engineering at EXL, Aidan oversees the development of their AI platform, EXLerate.ai, and ensures the quality of AI solutions as chair of the Architecture Review Board.

sessions.title: Scaling the AI Factory: Eliminating Power and Thermal Barriers with Two-Phase Cooling (Presented by ZutaCore)
sessions.abstract:
As AI infrastructure evolves toward AI factories and megawatt-scale deployments, thermals are emerging as a primary barrier to sustaining GPU performance. Rising chip power and continuous AI workloads are pushing traditional air and facility-water cooling beyond their practical limits, leading to throttling, energy inefficiency, and infrastructure constraints.

In this session, My D. Truong, CTO of ZutaCore, will explain how waterless two-phase direct-to-chip cooling removes heat at the silicon through phase-change heat transfer, enabling stable GPU temperatures and sustained performance under dynamic AI workloads. The presentation will highlight two-phase cooling architectures designed for NVIDIA HGX™ B300 systems, introduce support for PCIe servers built with NVIDIA RTX PRO™ 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs, and discuss a modular waterless data center architecture developed with Munters for scaling next-generation AI infrastructure.

speakers.name: My Truong
speakers.bio: My D. Truong brings a wealth of experience in scaling innovative data center technologies. Previously serving as field CTO at Equinix, he played a pioneering role in integrating liquid cooling across the company's extensive network, enabling sustainable strategies for its global operations. His background also includes leadership at companies like Uber, where he led the development of large-scale data center infrastructure, and as Chairperson for the Linux Foundation's Sustainable Scalable Infrastructure Alliance.

sessions.title: Leverage the Power of Thor in Industrial Applications (Presented by Advantech)
sessions.abstract: As physical AI becomes more prevalent in industrial applications, performance, longevity, and reliability are critical. Enter NVIDIA Jetson Thor solutions from Advantech. In this session, Advantech will provide an overview of both NVIDIA IGX and Jetson Thor, discuss use cases in a variety of industries, and showcase how NVIDIA Thor platform and Advantech together solve some of the biggest challenges facing you today.

speakers.name: Jason Waldman
speakers.bio: Jason Waldman manages Advantech’s ecosystem partnerships in North America, working with a variety of silicon and software partners, with a particular focus on NVIDIA. In his role, Jason collaborates closely with NVIDIA on commercial and product strategies for the North American market. Based at Advantech’s North American headquarters in Orange County, California, Jason has been with Advantech since 2023 and holds an M.B.A. from the UCLA Anderson School of Business.

sessions.title: Robotics in the Wild: What’s Real, What’s Not, and What’s Coming Next (Presented by HCLTech)
sessions.abstract: Enterprise robotics is moving fast, driven by advancements in AI and accelerated by NVIDIA’s ecosystem. But what is actually working in production today, and what is still stuck in prototypes and labs? In this session, HCLTech will take a practical look at real-world deployments of AI-powered robotics deployments across industries. We will discuss how we transformed operations for one of the largest mining companies with simulations built on Omniverse, robots trained with Cosmos and Isaac Sim, and video feeds processed with HCLTech IPs, alongside NVIDIA's Isaac robotics platform. We will share our lessons learned, perspective on what is working, what is promising but not quite ready, and where we are seeing the most momentum going into 2026.

speakers.name: Tamas Foldi
speakers.bio: Tamas Foldi is a technology leader and data expert helping enterprises become more data-driven. He currently serves as the vice president and head of robotics at HCLTech. Tamas is recognized internationally for his expertise in data engineering, analytics, and driving practical business value from data.

speakers.name: Gaurav Manchanda
speakers.bio:
Gaurav Manchanda is the Director and Head of the NVIDIA Ecosystem at HCLTech, where he leads the company’s global business with NVIDIA across strategic markets and industry verticals. With more than 22 years of experience, including over a decade at HCLTech, Gaurav drives joint GTM initiatives, co‑innovation programs, and large-scale AI, GenAI, and Physical AI transformation engagements for Fortune 500 clients.

In his leadership role, Gaurav manages a global charter spanning solution development, ecosystem expansion, field alignment, and the scaling of NVIDIA-powered offerings such as AI Factories, SmartTwin, VisionX, and HCLTech’s AI Force platform. His work is deeply integrated with HCLTech’s engineering, cloud, and digital business units across North America, Europe, and Asia.

Earlier at HCLTech, Gaurav successfully managed several high-impact global alliances, including HCLTech-Dell, HCLTech-VMWare and HCLTech-IBM enabling multimillion‑dollar pipeline creation and accelerated enterprise cloud adoption.

sessions.title: Unlocking AI Performance​ with CXL-Powered KV Cache​ (Presented by Penguin Solutions)
sessions.abstract: This session will explain how KV cache technology improves AI inference performance and scalability. CXL memory can play a transformative role by expanding local and shared infrastructure to save processed results, enabling GPUs to focus on more high-value tasks. Learn how sharing cached work across multiple GPUs not only accelerates response times, but also unlocks new functionalities, driving higher system efficiency and reducing the total cost of inference. Attend this talk to explore the future of AI inference optimization through the benefits of KV cache.

speakers.name: Phil Pokorny
speakers.bio:
Phil brings a wealth of engineering experience and customer insight to the design, development, support, and vision for technology solutions. He joined Penguin Computing in 2001 as an engineer and progressed steadily, influencing key tech and design decisions. Before that,, he spent 14 years in various roles with Cummins, where he developed internal network standards, deployed and managed a multi-site network of multi-protocol routers, and supported diverse office and engineering workers with a variety of server and desktop operating systems.

Phil graduated from Rose-Hulman Institute of Technology with B.S. degrees in math and electrical engineering, with a second major in computer science.

sessions.title: Maximize Tokens-per-Watt With AI Factory Digital Twins, From Chip to Grid (Presented by Siemens)
sessions.abstract:
AI factories are becoming mission‑critical, gigawatt‑scale infrastructure that must be engineered as a single, connected system. This session presents a “chip‑to‑grid” workflow focused on two key performance indicators: time‑to‑token and watts‑per‑token. 

It begins at the silicon layer, where AI‑native, GPU‑accelerated electronic design automation that utilizes Fuse and NVIDIA Nemotron accelerates design cycles. The workflow then extends to the factory, where Siemens Digital Twin Composer with NVIDIA Omniverse enables decision‑grade digital twins across power, cooling, and layout before construction. The session will conclude with an operational reference architecture that improves efficiency, resilience, and uptime. Join us to explore how digital twins redefine AI factory design, construction, and operations—creating a new level of operational excellence.

speakers.name: Ales Alabegovic
speakers.bio: Ales Alajbegovic, Siemens’ global project manager for industrial metaverse, drives the industrial AI revolution. He utilizes digital threads for seamless product-to-performance flow. His work emphasizes customer success, implementing industrial metaverse tech to deliver tangible value and set new standards for efficiency and innovation globally.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Securing Intelligence: A Practical Framework for AI and LLM Security (Presented by TrendAI)
sessions.abstract:
As AI and large language models move from experimentation into mission-critical systems, organizations are building intelligent platforms to power decision-making, automation, and innovation at scale, creating a new security reality. From data pipelines and model development to deployment, inference, and real-world impact, AI systems expand the attack surface in ways traditional security can't address. 

We'll explore a practical Security for AI Framework, aligned with the OWASP Top 10 for LLMs, and see how modern threats like adversarial manipulation, data poisoning, prompt injection, model abuse, and synthetic media attacks are reshaping the landscape. Learn how organizations are embedding governance, access control, validation, monitoring, and response into their AI platforms and AI factory architectures, turning security into a foundation for trusted, production-ready intelligence.

speakers.name: Fernando Cardoso
speakers.bio: Fernando Cardoso has over 13 years of experience in cybersecurity. Starting as a network and sales engineer, he developed expertise in data centers, cloud, DevOps, and cybersecurity. Over the past six years, he's led cloud security, DevSecOps, and AI security projects, collaborating globally with customers and key cloud and AI ecosystem partners.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: From Silicon to Solution: Building a World-Class Inference Engine for the AI Builder (Presented by GMI Cloud)
sessions.abstract:
As NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs power production inference stacks originally built for previous generations, developers may observe unexpected bottlenecks. Higher peak compute and faster interconnects do not automatically translate to lower latency or better cost efficiency at scale.

This session examines how inference system behavior changes when transitioning from NVIDIA Hopper to NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. We’ll focus on concrete architectural shifts and show how the architecture improves. Rather than benchmarking peak performance, we’ll analyze observable patterns that emerge under real inference traffic and discuss how to redesign scheduling, batching, and monitoring strategies to fully benefit from NVIDIA Blackwell architecture.

speakers.name: Yujing Qian
speakers.bio:
As the head of engineering at GMI Cloud, Yujing leads global engineering strategy and product development for high-performance GPU cloud infrastructure. With deep expertise in distributed systems, large-scale compute, and AI infrastructure, Yujing scales GMI Cloud’s platform to support cutting-edge workloads across generative AI, ML, and data-intensive research.

Before joining GMI Cloud, Yujing built and managed engineering teams at top technology companies, driving innovation in cloud computing and accelerating AI adoption at scale. He's passionate about empowering developers, researchers, and enterprises to unlock new possibilities with accessible and cost-effective compute.

sessions.title: Architecting AI-Ready Data Centers: Lessons From Leading-Edge Deployments (Presented by Hyve Solutions)
sessions.abstract:
As organizations accelerate AI initiatives, infrastructure has become a critical competitive differentiator. This session examines how next-generation data centers are architected for exponential AI workload demands, featuring insights from leading-edge deployments.

You'll explore technical foundations of large-scale AI infrastructure, advanced cooling, high-performance networking, and validation protocols ensuring reliability at scale. We’ll address real-world challenges: site preparation, capacity planning, and operational considerations separating successful implementations from costly missteps.

Drawing on global projects, learn practical guidance for leaders transitioning to AI-optimized facilities. Whether you're expanding existing infrastructure or designing greenfield sites, gain actionable perspectives on architecture, deployment strategies, and operational best practices.

speakers.name: Rami Khouri
speakers.bio: As senior vice president of global engineering, Khouri leads Hyve Solutions’ global engineering strategy and guides next-generation compute, AI, and data center solutions. Khouri brings 24 years of experience from Intel, where he held senior leadership roles in the data center business. Most recently, he served as VP of the Data Center Group and general manager of Data Center Hardware Engineering, where he oversaw comprehensive platform development across technologies including electrical signaling, power delivery, packaging, mechanical and thermal engineering, and board, system, and rack design. He also played a key role in advancing industry standards and fostering an open ecosystem.

sessions.title: Advance AI Data Center Infrastructure Design and Construction to Accommodate GW Scale (Presented by EdgeConnex)
sessions.abstract:
Today’s data center industry is evolving faster than ever, with record demand for gigawatt (GW)-scale designs now paired with the accelerated delivery intervals needed to meet today’s ever-changing technology demands. This race to build large-scale data center infrastructure has fundamentally shifted the industry’s approach, from traditional design and construction methods to ones that are highly optimized for accelerated delivery at GW scale. 

In this session, explore the drivers and overall impacts of designing and building large-scale GW data center infrastructure that serves as the building blocks for AI factories of the future. Better understand the new challenges faced by the industry and the general path forward for designing and constructing large-scale data center architectures at accelerated delivery intervals to accommodate the technology innovations of tomorrow.

speakers.name: Aron Smith
speakers.bio: Aron currently leads the Global Product Management function for EdgeConneX and is responsible for the ongoing support and development of existing and new products within their data center portfolio. He's recently focused on bringing high-density solutions into the data center, specifically supporting new liquid-cooling architectures.

sessions.title: Accelerating Rack-Scale Connectivity for NVIDIA Blackwell and Rubin Platforms (Presented by Astera Labs)
sessions.abstract: Learn how advanced rack-scale connectivity solutions are revolutionizing data center infrastructure for next-generation AI workloads. We'll explore the technical innovations enabling seamless multi-GPU scaling and the performance breakthroughs achieved when integrating Astera Labs' purpose-built connectivity solutions with NVIDIA's Blackwell and upcoming Rubin architectures.

speakers.name: Mike Hendricks
speakers.bio: Mike has over 20 years of experience driving innovation in AI infrastructure, semiconductors, and automotive electronics. As associate VP solutions and ecosystems at Astera Labs, he spearheads the development of cutting-edge connectivity solutions that power next-generation AI and cloud infrastructure. Mike has held senior leadership roles at Amazon, Intel, and Texas Instruments, where he led product management, business development, and corporate strategy initiatives. He brings deep expertise in system architecture, memory, and storage solutions, helping to accelerate the transformation of cloud-scale data centers. Mike holds a B.S. in electrical engineering from UC Santa Cruz.

sessions.title: Power Solution for NVIDIA Vera Rubin and 800 VDC AI Rack Architecture (Presented by LITEON)
sessions.abstract: The great performance leap of the Vera Rubin servers also brings great challenges for power design. With the power increase of the rack versus GB300, the power shelf, after being upgraded, needs to stay precisely regulated under the increased load dynamics of AI workloads and be friendly to the utility. Under close cooperation with the NVIDIA team, LITEON jointly developed a 3RU 110kW power shelf with three-phase AC input PSUs and control system, which can meet the rigorous power management requirements at both the input and output ends. When pushing the IT rack power level to 1MW and beyond, the 800 VDC solutions prevail, and LITEON has several different power levels under developing (480Vac to 800 VDC,330kW, 660kW and 1.2MW). For the 800 VDC step-down, LITEON also provides options including DC/DC power shelf and power brick modules, with boosted power density.

speakers.name: Eric Lee
speakers.bio: Eric Lee, technical director at LITEON, has nearly 20 years of experience in power management, specializing in high-efficiency conversion design, advanced power topologies, digital control, and high-density architectures for AI and cloud systems. He is known for solving complex, high-performance power challenges and driving innovative power solutions for data center platforms.

sessions.title: Redefining Vector DB Management: Uniting Massive Scalability With GPU-Accelerated Indexing (Presented by KIOXIA America)
sessions.abstract: As generative AI reshapes enterprise workloads, the rapid growth of vector databases introduces new challenges for scalability and performance. Kioxia Corp. addresses these challenges with AiSAQ, an innovative approximate nearest neighbor search (ANNS) algorithm that breaks traditional DRAM limitations by leveraging SSDs for search operations.  Now, AiSAQ adds powerful GPU utilization to vector indexing, transforming a time-consuming bottleneck into a highly accelerated workflow. In this session, discover how AiSAQ’s combination of SSD scalability and NVIDIA cuVS dramatically boosts throughput for demanding generative AI applications such as retrieval-augmented generation and semantic search. Gain insights into the benefits of integrating SSDs and GPUs, enabling robust, cost-effective solutions to meet the demands of today's AI-driven workloads.

speakers.name: Rory Bolt
speakers.bio: Rory is a senior fellow at KIOXIA America and leads the forward-looking technology and storage pathfinding group for SSDs. He has more than 25 years of experience in data storage systems, data protection systems, and high performance computing, with a pedigree from marquee storage companies. Rory has been granted over 12 storage-related patents and has several pending. Rory has a B.S. in computer engineering from the University of California, San Diego.

sessions.title: Network Automation Lessons Learned From Launching 20+ Multi-Tenant Clouds and AI Factories (Presented by Netris)
sessions.abstract: Both enterprises and NVIDIA Cloud Partners (NCPs) must establish consistent tenant isolation across NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet, NVIDIA Quantum InfiniBand, NVLink Multi-Node, DPU, virtual, and edge networks. This requires hard isolation across bare metal, virtual machines (VMs), and container workloads on all involved fabrics to support data sovereignty and compliance requirements.

speakers.name: Alex Saroyan
speakers.bio: Alex Saroyan is the CEO and co-founder of Netris, the leading network automation platform for AI cloud operators. With over 25 years of experience in large-scale data center and telecom networking, Alex brings deep expertise to AI infrastructure challenges. Before Netris, he held engineering leadership roles at Lycos, Orange (France Télécom) and Ucom, where he architected a global transit network and scaled carrier infrastructure nearly 100x. Alex co-founded Netris in 2018 to bring cloud-provider-grade network automation and multi-tenancy to every AI infrastructure operator.

sessions.title: High-Performance Inference for Frontier AI Models (Presented by Baseten)
sessions.abstract: How do you go from a state-of-the-art foundation model to a globally available usage-based API? This session provides an overview of the roadmap and architecture from model performance optimization to multi-region GPU-based infrastructure setup to the precise mechanics of request prioritization, token accounting, and rate limiting. Building on an inference stack that includes technologies like NVIDIA Dynamo, TensorRT-LLM, and CuTe, we'll trace the path from model weights to a robust production service.

speakers.name: Philip Kiely
speakers.bio: Philip Kiely leads developer relations at Baseten. Prior to joining Baseten in 2022, he worked across software engineering and technical writing for a variety of startups. Outside of work, you'll find Philip practicing martial arts, reading a new book, or cheering for his adopted Bay Area sports teams.

sessions.title: Build AI Factories for Always-On Throughput and Maximum Cost Efficiency (Presented by WEKA)
sessions.abstract: Learn how to architect efficient AI factories by eliminating I/O and storage bottlenecks with sustained throughput across AI training and inference. Dive into how WEKA and NVIDIA enable enterprises to build AI factories with reference architectures, featuring WEKA NeuralMesh and NVIDIA accelerated computing and networking, that deliver AI-ready data with NVIDIA AI Data Platform to keep resources fully utilized, simplify operations at scale, and maximize performance, power, and cost efficiencies. Walk away with a practical plan to evolve your organization’s AI infrastructure from initial pilots to secure, resilient, and scalable enterprise deployments.

speakers.name: Shimon Ben David
speakers.bio: Shimon is WEKA’s chief technology officer, responsible for engaging with the company’s customers and partners to track emerging trends and bring actionable feedback and insights to its engineering and product management teams. He previously held leadership positions in the company’s customer success and sales engineering functions. Shimon brings deep IT domain expertise to WEKA, having previously run IT and support services at Primary Data, XtremIO (acquired by EMC), IBM, and XIV Storage, where he met WEKA’s founding team. He studied computer science and philosophy at Ramat Gan University.

sessions.title: Simply Build, Run, and Optimize Your NVIDIA AI Factories With Nutanix (Presented by Nutanix)
sessions.abstract: Learn how Nutanix integrates infrastructure orchestration, security, and AI platform services into a single, turnkey environment. This approach replaces fragmented point solutions with an optimized stack designed to accelerate the deployment of production-grade agentic AI applications while simplifying day-to-day operations.

speakers.name: Mikey Barmonde
speakers.bio: Mike Barmonde is an American from Seattle, handling product marketing for AI, networking, and security. He can speak French poorly, but loves to try. Mike loves to laugh and has a LOT of energy. When Mike isn't Nutanix-ing, he's working on his old van, gardening, pretending he's a handyman, wrestling his children, or trying new AI tools. Mike loves to engage, and hopes you come say hi at the Nutanix booth on the expo floor!

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: A Safe, Efficient, and Scalable Approach to 800 VDC Architecture (Presented by Eaton)
sessions.abstract:
The growing demand for high‑performance computing and dense GPU deployments is accelerating adoption of DC power distribution in next‑generation data centers. By eliminating rack‑level AC conversion, DC architectures improve efficiency, reduce space overhead, and enable higher‑voltage distribution (800 VDC), increasing overall system scalability and lowering losses. 

Within this landscape, Eaton plays an active leadership role in the Open Compute Project (OCP) community, and in the development of Underwriters Laboratories (UL) and International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) standards, helping shape industry frameworks that enable the safe deployment of high‑voltage DC architectures at scale. This standards‑driven approach ensures that emerging DC ecosystems are interoperable, fault‑tolerant, and aligned with real‑world operational requirements for AI and HPC environments. 

Building on its deep power management expertise, Eaton focuses on enabling higher power densities safely. By combining active standards leadership, and a laser focus on safety, Eaton is helping establish the foundational power architectures required for the next generation of AI and high‑performance computing data centers.

speakers.name: Joshua Buzzell
speakers.bio: JP Buzzell is Eaton's data center chief architect, driving global strategy, reference designs, and full-stack deployments across power, cooling, networking, and software. A leading voice in liquid cooling, he develops scalable solutions for AI and quantum growth. Previously, he led data center operations at Meta and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, advancing cooling technologies and GPU cluster scaling. His career began in nuclear power, with deep expertise in reactor operations and maintenance. JP holds a B.S. in nuclear energy engineering technology (ABET-accredited) and an M.S. in technology management.

sessions.title: Build and Productize an AI Factory: From Design to Deployment (Presented by Inventec)
sessions.abstract: This presentation explores the dual pillars of the AI factory: robust infrastructure, and transformative applications. We examine how high-performance compute environments enable digital employees and agentic AI to redefine industrial efficiency. A primary focus is placed on video-recognition-based agents deployed directly onto production lines. These intelligent systems automate anomaly detection and cycle-time control while providing sophisticated post-process insights. By synthesizing raw visual data, these agents generate automated event summaries and perform deep root-cause analysis, shifting manufacturing from reactive monitoring to proactive, data-driven optimization.

speakers.name: Roger Cheng
speakers.bio: Roger is Inventec's director for engineering and product management.

sessions.title: Al + Quantum: Unlocking the Next Era of Scientific and Industrial Breakthroughs (Presented by Quantinuum)
sessions.abstract: The convergence of AI and quantum computing is not a distant vision—it’s unfolding now. Generative Quantum AI (GenQAI) is at the forefront of this shift, creating a feedback loop where quantum computers generate novel data that can enhance classical AI models, while AI systems learn from and guide quantum exploration. Quantinuum has been using the NVIDIA CUDA-Q platform to bring these two technologies together. Their convergence unlocks more than technical achievements—it redefines what’s possible. In pharmaceuticals, it makes the acceleration of drug discovery possible by exploring chemical spaces classical computing cannot reach. In materials science, it makes breakthroughs in superconductors and batteries possible that can transform energy and mobility. In finance and logistics, it opens the door to solving optimization problems that define competitiveness.

speakers.name: Vishal Shrotriya
speakers.bio: Vishal Shrotriya is a high-impact commercial leader with 20 years of experience driving revenue in deep tech and quantum computing. Vishal has a distinguished record of scaling high-growth ventures, having led go-to-market strategies for two quantum computing unicorns and co-founded a successful semiconductor venture. Currently leading business development at Quantinuum, Vishal orchestrates global partnerships to align scientific roadmaps with market demand. Beyond his commercial success, he is a respected technical authority with 25 peer-reviewed publications and over 17,000 citations. He holds a Ph.D. in materials science from UCLA and a management of technology certification from Caltech.

sessions.title: Own Your AI: Beat Frontier Models With Your Own Data (Presented by Fireworks AI)
sessions.abstract: The best AI companies aren't renting intelligence from closed model APIs—they're building their own. In this session, Roberto from Fireworks AI busts three common myths about fine-tuning open models: that closed models are always better, that fine-tuning is hard, and that you need massive labeled datasets. Drawing on real production examples from real-world examples from clients, he'll show how supervised fine-tuning, direct preference optimization, and reinforcement fine-tuning turn your production data into a compounding moat—and why the best model for your product is the one you own. Powered by NVIDIA hardware.

speakers.name: Roberto Barroso-Luque
speakers.bio: Roberto is a data scientist/ML engineer who's passionate about building AI solutions that create real business value—from natural language processing systems and business-to-business sales optimization to leading Gen AI transformations.

sessions.title: How AI Coding Augments IT Teams Without Replacing Them (Presented by CDW)
sessions.abstract:
This session shows how AI coding can be used today by IT teams with the tools and systems they already have. You will see how common IT work such as maintaining legacy code and scripts, reducing technical debt, and creating tests and documentation can be done faster with AI, without changing platforms or processes.

Through real-world examples and a short demo, we will show how AI coding fits into existing IT workflows instead of requiring new tools or large integrations. The focus is on practical and low-risk use cases that deliver quick value, reduce ongoing maintenance work, and help teams spend more time on higher-impact efforts like modernization and optimization.

speakers.name: Jeff Myers
speakers.bio: Jeff Myers serves as a lead on the Advanced Computing Strategy Team at CDW. He brings expertise in driving innovative technology solutions for enterprise clients and spearheads initiatives within CDW. Leveraging his deep understanding of market dynamics and technical architectures, he and his team empower organizations with cutting-edge AI and high performance computing solutions.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: From Foundation Models to SLMs: Powering Vertical AI Factories on NVIDIA (Presented by Tech Mahindra, Ltd.)
sessions.abstract:
Enterprises and governments are rethinking how they deploy large foundation models alongside smaller, domain-specific language models to achieve accuracy, security, and cost efficiency at scale. While foundation models provide broad intelligence, domain-tuned small language models (SLMs) are increasingly critical for embedding AI into vertical, regulated, and low-latency operational workflows. 

In this session, we explore how Tech Mahindra is leveraging NVIDIA-accelerated platforms to support the evolution from foundation models to production-grade AI factories—systems that continuously train, fine-tune, deploy, and operate both LLMs and SLMs. Drawing on real-world experience, including telecom-specific language models delivering measurable operational outcomes—such as AI-led handling of over 50% of network operations tickets across autonomous and human-approved workflows—and India’s Indus AI mission focused on education at national scale, we show how sovereign AI, private AI, and edge AI can be integrated to deliver secure, scalable, and vertical-ready AI solutions, leveraging NVIDIA technologies such as NVIDIA AI Enterprise, RAPIDS, and NeMo-based data tooling.

speakers.name: Nikhil Malhotra
speakers.bio: Nikhil Malhotra is the chief innovation officer, the global head of AI and emerging technologies, a World Economic Forum AI and quantum fellow, and the creator and principal researcher of Project INDUS (India’s LLM) at Tech Mahindra. Nikhil leads the growth of AI and quantum computing research within Tech Mahindra. He's won numerous awards, including the 2020, 2021, and 2023 Innovation Congress awards for the most innovative leader.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: AI-Accelerated Design: From Hyperscale Data Centers to Molecular Discovery (Presented by Cadence)
sessions.abstract:
The pace of AI-driven design innovation has accelerated dramatically since early 2025. With the introduction of Cadence’s AI-optimized Millennium M2000 Supercomputer built on NVIDIA, engineers and scientists now have the unprecedented capability to tackle the hardest problems in semiconductor design, system integration, and life sciences.
 
In this session, discover how Cadence and NVIDIA are shaping a unified AI design ecosystem—integrating agentic AI workflows, physics-based models, and simulation-driven approaches to accelerate breakthroughs in chip design, advanced packaging, physical AI, and molecular discovery. Learn how next-generation infrastructure and tools are driving continuous innovation across every scale—from silicon and systems to data centers and molecules.

speakers.name: Rob Knoth
speakers.bio: Rob is a technologist focusing on corporate strategy, and the interfaces between domain-specific solutions. He has extensive experience in both semiconductor design and electronic design automation. Rob holds a B.S. in electrical engineering from Purdue University. Outside of work, he's an author, speaker, and advocate to get more families adventuring in the outdoors.

sessions.title: Scale AI Data Pipelines With High-Capacity JBODs and NVIDIA BlueField-4 DPUs (Presented by Seagate Technology)
sessions.abstract: Learn how to move data-intensive applications from proof of concept (PoC) to production scale using a reference architecture that pairs high-capacity JBOD storage with NVIDIA BlueField 4 DPUs to accelerate data access, reduce CPU overhead, and improve end-to-end efficiency. This session presents a practical blueprint for solution architects designing cost-effective, high-throughput storage systems to support modern, data-driven and AI-enabled applications at scale.

speakers.name: Mohamad El-Batal
speakers.bio: Mohamad El-Batal shapes Seagate’s Cloud Systems Group (CSG) strategy and storage product roadmap. He holds a B.S. in electrical and computer engineering from the University of Colorado Boulder and has worked at companies like Tandem, EMC, IBM, LSI, and NetApp. Mohamad holds over 50 patents, with at least 20 more pending, focusing on high-availability storage, memory solutions, SSD controllers, and systems architecture.

sessions.title: Tailor-Made Inference: Building SLA-Focused AI Stacks for Enterprise Demands (Presented by Simplismart)
sessions.abstract: One size doesn’t fit all for inference, and black-box APIs break down when real workloads demand clear service-level agreements (SLAs) around latency, cost, reliability, and output quality. This session will walk you step by step through how you can choose the right inference stack for your use case, build it yourself, and operate it in production, using practical techniques to balance performance trade-offs across real-time workloads.

speakers.name: Amritanshu Jain
speakers.bio: Amritanshu Jain is a co-founder at Simplismart. He previously worked at Oracle Cloud and Capillary. Amritanshu has been breathing machine learning and building AI stacks for enterprises to enable inference at scale for more than seven years.

sessions.title: From Imagination to Experimentation to Full-Scale Adoption: Ensure a Successful Generative AI Project (Presented by SHI)
sessions.abstract: Join us as we explore SHI's approach to supporting customers' AI journies and adoption of generative AI within their existing enterprise environments. We’ll share best practices and challenges encountered in identifying, prototyping, building, and scaling AI applications.

speakers.name: Jack Hogan
speakers.bio: As vice president of advanced growth technologies, Jack Hogan brings more than 30 years of experience as a technology executive, including 23 years as a customer-side co-founder, CTO, and IT operator at LifeScript, and seven years as a vendor-side strategist at Pure Storage. An “entrepreneurial technologist” and early AI adopter, Jack leads SHI’s 160-plus member AI overlay team, and built SHI’s AI & Cyber Labs. He focuses on executive engagement with global customers, advising on data center optimization, hybrid cloud, and AI solutions, drawing on his deep expertise as both a technology strategist and customer advocate.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Gesture-Based Control of Heavy Equipment Using Simulation-First Robotics Workflows (Presented by Marvik)
sessions.abstract: Marvik presents a real-world use case demonstrating how computer vision, simulation, and NVIDIA Isaac Sim can be used to design more intuitive and safer human–machine interfaces for heavy equipment. The project explores gesture-based control of an excavator, using a standard camera and real-time pose estimation to translate operator movements into machine commands inside a high-fidelity simulated environment. By validating control logic, latency, and usability in simulation before touching physical hardware, this approach reduces development risk, shortens iteration cycles, and lowers costs. The session highlights how simulation-first workflows enable scalable foundations for future applications such as remote operation, semi-autonomous machinery, and intelligent operator assistance, while improving safety and operational efficiency in industrial settings.

speakers.name: Paula Martinez
speakers.bio:
Paula Martínez is the Co-Founder and CEO of Marvik, a hands-on AI consulting firm specializing in computer vision, agents and LLMs, predictive analytics, data engineering, generative AI, and robotics and automation.

With over ten years of experience in the technology sector, Paula is an electrical engineer with a master’s degree in technology management. She has been recognized as a Google Developer Expert for her technical leadership and contributions to the community. She also co-organizes artificial intelligence meetups and has been an invited speaker at international conferences such as Nvidia GTC and Google Cloud Next.

Since its early days, she has led Marvik with a strategic and hands-on vision, driving its growth and positioning it as a leading AI company in the region. The company works with Fortune 500 enterprises and innovative startups worldwide.

Together with her partner, Rodrigo Beceiro, she firmly believes in the power of technology to solve real-world problems. Under her leadership, Marvik leverages cutting-edge artificial intelligence to deliver innovative, high-impact solutions.

sessions.title: Accelerate Physical AI with Simulation-Centric Data Engine (Presented by Lightwheel)
sessions.abstract: Physical AI is moving from research demos into real-world deployment. However, scaling Physical AI systems remains constrained by low-fidelity simulation without physics, limited data generation capacity, and the lack of large-scale, industrial-grade evaluation infrastructure. Lightwheel was built to close this gap, and trusted by 80% of leading Physical AI teams. Lightwheel is a simulation-driven Physical AI infrastructure company, providing the simulation, data, and evaluation platform required to train, scale, and deploy Physical AI systems in the real world.

speakers.name: Myles Liu
speakers.bio: Myles leads business development and operations at Lightwheel, working at the intersection of simulation, data, and embodied AI. In close collaboration with partners including NVIDIA, he focuses on how the simulation platform, large-scale egocentric human data, and evaluation can be operationalized to accelerate robotics development. His work spans simulation systems, data pipelines, and evaluation frameworks that bridge research and real-world deployment.

sessions.title: Scale a Thinking Enterprise on BlueVerse (Presented by LTM)
sessions.abstract: This session introduces how BlueVerse, built on the NVIDIA stack and powered by Nemotron models, enables AI factories to reliably move from idea to impact. Scaling a thinking enterprise requires more than powerful models—it requires an architecture where intelligence, context, governance, and outcomes work together. Learn how agentic workflows use Nemotron‑driven reasoning, unified guardrails, and continuous evaluation to improve with every outcome—creating an enterprise that learns, adapts, and scales safely.

speakers.name: Prakash Pasupathy
speakers.bio: Prakash Pasupathy is assistant vice president—enterprise AI and BlueVerse at LTIMindtree, leading the Americas AI business across strategy, solutioning, productization, and go-to-market. He works to reimagine business operations and elevate customer satisfaction through agentic automation, data readiness, and multi-agent orchestration—grounded in clear value propositions and outcome-driven delivery. Over 16-plus years, Prakash has launched over 50 market-facing products and led multiple end-to-end AI and digital transformations. He was a founding member of Mindtree’s advanced analytics and AI practice and holds an executive M.B.A. from London Business School.

sessions.title: Why AI Platforms are Reclaiming Their GPU Infrastructure (Presented by Hydra Host)
sessions.abstract:
As AI platforms scale from pilots to production, infrastructure decisions become strategic choices that affect cost, reliability, and long-term competitiveness. This session explores why an increasing number of AI platforms are moving away from fully centralized cloud models and toward owning dedicated GPU infrastructure on bare metal.

We will discuss how AI platforms are using AI factory models to improve performance predictability, reduce unit costs, and monetize compute directly, while maintaining control over where and how their models run.

speakers.name: Aaron Ginn
speakers.bio:
