Aaron Ginn is a serial technology entrepreneur, author, speaker, Forbes honoree, and international policy advisor on AI and data center infrastructure. He's also the CEO and co-founder of Hydra Host, a Colorado-based AI data center services and management company that operates over 50 data centers in more than 24 countries. As NVIDIA’s premier AI factory platform, Hydra is powering sovereign AI deployments worldwide and is Lepton’s go-to-market partner. 

Aaron has written extensively on AI, trade policy, and technology competition. In 2025, he played a pivotal role in shaping U.S. GPU export policy and was selected by NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang to represent the company in Washington, D.C.

sessions.title: Your Agent is Expensive and Wrong (Let's Fix Both!) (Presented by Databricks)
sessions.abstract: More than 80% of AI projects fail. The reason? Customers are not ready to put AI in production in areas where the agent can cause reputational or financial harm. Agent Bricks solves this by automatically generating domain‑specific synthetic data and task‑aware benchmarks, then using those benchmarks to auto‑optimize the agents for accuracy, latency and expense—eliminating the tedious trial‑and‑error cycle. In this session, you’ll see how to design and scale multi‑agent systems that combine retrieval, reasoning, and generation to answer complex, enterprise‑grounded queries, while tracking experiments, fine‑tuning models, and building governed, repeatable workflows that can be deployed at scale. By the end you’ll be equipped to launch production‑ready AI agents with confidence, speed, and budget‑control from Day 1.

speakers.name: Nicolas Pelaez
speakers.bio: Nicolas Pelaez is a technical marketing engineer at Databricks, where he leverages AI to create engaging, data-driven demos. With a background in aerospace engineering and data science, Nicolas previously worked as a turbine performance engineer, monitoring military jet engines, and developing advanced control models to ensure safety and performance.

He spearheads the groundbreaking initiative behind BharatGPT, India’s premier large language model-based secure generative AI. Ankush's mission is to harness technology to transform businesses and enrich human lives. ​​ ​​Honored as the Innovator of the Year (2024) by Entrepreneur and recognized among the Top 10 Tech Entrepreneurs and the Top 10 AI Influencers globally, Ankush brings to the table over two decades of profound IT experience spanning India, America, and Europe. His academic pursuits include an MS in software engineering from BITS, Pilani, complemented by an MBA, reflecting his commitment to excellence in both technology and business realms.

sessions.title: Supercharging Postgres for Agentic Analytics with Rapids Accelerator and Apache Iceberg (Presented by EDB)
sessions.abstract:
As data volumes increase, the primary bottleneck for high-performance AI agents will shift from the model to the data. This increases the importance of the underlying data engine’s ability to process massive enterprise datasets in real time. This scaling problem is further amplified by the desire to make the latest transactional business data seamlessly available to agentic processing.  

Join the experts from EDB for a technical deep dive into how to overcome these scaling and transactional integration hurdles using the world's most popular open-source database. We will showcase the architecture behind GPU acceleration in EDB Postgres AI, specifically how offloading complex analytical workloads to an NVIDIA RAPIDS Accelerator for Apache Spark eliminates traditional CPU bottlenecks. Through a review of TPC-DS benchmarks, we will demonstrate how to transform Postgres into a high-throughput engine capable of powering autonomous agentic analytics for real-time business decision-making. We will also showcase how EDB Postgres makes all transactional data available to GPU processing in real time through Apache Iceberg. This establishes a GPU-accelerated Hybrid Analytics and Transactional Processing (HTAP) stack, which at the same time avoids vendor lock-in by being fully compatible with the modern open analytics ecosystems.

Note: All attendees will receive a complimentary copy of the new O’Reilly business guide, “Building a Data and AI Platform with PostgreSQL."

speakers.name: Quais Taraki
speakers.bio:
Quais Taraki is focused on using technology to solve real problems, deliver meaningful value to customers, and build durable businesses. He has built organizations that integrate software engineering, AI research, cloud infrastructure, platform operations, and customer-facing functions.

Quais most recently served as senior vice president and general manager for Oracle Health and AI, where he was responsible for modernizing clinical systems, advancing applied AI, and improving platform reliability for some of the world’s largest healthcare providers, including the federal government. Before that, he led Oracle’s Data and AI organization and guided company-wide data platforms and applied AI initiatives. He previously ran the Compute organization at Oracle Cloud Infrastructure and delivered the hyperscale compute products that power enterprise workloads worldwide, including virtual machines, bare-metal servers, high-performance computing clusters, and GPU infrastructure. 

Earlier in his career, Quais founded a machine learning startup and served as an engineering advisor to Microsoft Ventures. He also held senior technical leadership roles at Amazon Web Services and Microsoft.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Building AI Factories for High-Assurance, Mission-Critical Environments (Presented by Astris AI)
sessions.abstract: As AI systems move from experimentation to operational deployment, organizations in aerospace, energy, manufacturing, and the public sector face a common challenge: how to deploy AI reliably in environments where failure, data leakage, or downtime is unacceptable. This session explores how AI factory architectures are being designed to support high-assurance, mission-critical workloads across on-premises, edge, disconnected, and regulated environments.

speakers.name: Greg Forrest
speakers.bio: Greg Forrest leads the development and execution of Astris AI and Lockheed Martin's corporate artificial intelligence strategy, creating an enterprise AI ecosystem for MLOps and generative AI, and leading teams that develop transformational AI and machine learning capabilities in support of digital transformation and customer missions.

sessions.title: Simplifying high-density fiber cabling for AI PODs, improving site efficiency for Day 2 (Presented by CommScope)
sessions.abstract: True innovation has arrived for cabling AI PODs! Navigating cabinet depth, accessibility, cable volume and performance is simplified. Explore options that can save time on site!

speakers.name: Ken Hall
speakers.bio: Ken Hall is data center architect responsible for technology, thought leadership, and optical infrastructure planning for AI and cloud data centers. He's been instrumental in developing high-speed, ultra-low-loss fiber optic solutions to efficiently enable network migration for data center operators. Focused on cabling efficiencies for AI design and implementation, he continues to drive solutions for these rapidly evolving networks.

sessions.title: Red Hat & NVIDIA: Driving Mainstream Enterprise AI Solutions to Unlock Wide-Scale Adoption of AI (Presented by Red Hat)
sessions.abstract: Delivering a mainstream enterprise AI solution requires an ecosystem that not only works, but is engineered as a cohesive set of components that fit seamlessly to drive success. To truly provide a seamless enterprise solution, you start in the open, where the components can be visualized, vetted, and cleanly integrated. Red Hat and NVIDIA have taken those steps to begin Day 0 support for Red Hat Enterprise Linux and Red Hat OpenShift on NVIDIA systems, starting with Vera Rubin. In this talk, we will look at the work being done behind the scenes to deliver what is a fundamental change in how open moves faster to deliver an enterprise experience.

speakers.name: Scott Herold
speakers.bio: Scott is a product management leader with over 20 years of industry experience, managing the team that is concentrating on the future of Red Hat Enterprise Linux. The team has an outside-in focus on customer experiences as adoption and maturity of Linux usage increases from data center to cloud, and extends to the edge.

sessions.title: Who Owns Intelligence When AI Runs the World? (Presented by Global AI)
sessions.abstract: As AI systems scale from megawatts to gigawatts, the center of gravity is shifting from models to infrastructure. Join three leaders operating at the intersection of compute, systems, and national platforms as they examine how power, cooling, architecture, and deployment choices now determine performance, sovereignty, and control of intelligence.

speakers.name: Sami Issa
speakers.bio: Sami Issa is co-founder, director, and CEO of Global AI, the world’s first sovereign AI hyperscaler. With more than 35 years of experience across semiconductors, cloud, and artificial intelligence, he has helped build national-scale technology platforms in the United States and the Middle East. Previously an executive director at Mubadala, Issa played a central role in creating GlobalFoundries, serving eight years on its board and helping drive landmark developments including Fab 8 in New York. He chaired multiple international semiconductor working groups, led major R&D investments, and co-founded Cognit, the Mubadala–IBM venture behind the first Arabic AI platform. Earlier roles span Intel, Broadcom, Texas Instruments, and National Semiconductor. He holds over 30 patents and an M.S. in electrical engineering from Arizona State University.

speakers.name: Charles Liang
speakers.bio:
Charles Liang founded Supermicro in 1993 and has been president, chief executive officer, and chairman of the board. A Silicon Valley entrepreneur, Charles has been developing server system architectures and technologies for over three decades. With a passion for green IT and protecting the environment, Charles has been the driving force behind Supermicro’s transformation into a total IT solutions provider for enterprise, cloud, AI, and 5G/edge markets. 

Under Charles’ guidance, Supermicro has evolved from a motherboard and subsystem company to a multi-billion-dollar business optimizing complete rack and data center-level solutions customers can deploy and bring online within hours. In addition, the company has expanded globally well beyond its San Jose, California headquarters to encompass significant manufacturing and operations campuses in Asia and Europe with over 4,000 employees globally. 

Charles has been granted many server technology patents. He holds an M.S. in electrical engineering from the University of Texas at Arlington and a B.S. in electrical engineering from the National Taiwan University of Science & Technology in Taiwan.

sessions.title: The Agentic AI Data Factory: Why Agents Need a GPU-Native Data Platform to Create Real Value (Presented by Capgemini)
sessions.abstract:
Agentic AI is evolving fast—but most organizations can’t fully capitalize on it, because their data foundations weren’t built for real‑time, AI‑driven decision-making.

In this session, we introduce InsightGrid—a GPU‑accelerated data factory built on NVIDIA technologies that unifies all enterprise data into a single, AI‑ready foundation. InsightGrid brings together batch, streaming, structured, and unstructured data, and embeds unstructured content alongside core business entities to give agents a complete, contextual view of your business. The result: agentic systems that don’t just retrieve information—they reason, decide, and automate with confidence, transparency, and auditability.  

Join us for a live demonstration of how this new architecture expands what agentic AI can do across the enterprise, enabling smarter decisions, faster operations, and more resilient workflows at scale.

speakers.name: Rajesh Iyer
speakers.bio: Rajesh has 30-plus years of FinServ experience. He holds top Capgemini industry certifications and served as a member of the Forbes Technology Council. Rajesh serves on Harvard Business Review's Advisory Council, as a contributor to Evident AI’s AI Benchmark for Banks, and advisor to the startup ecosystem through a marquee venture capital firm. He has led successful deployments of GPU-powered foundation model-based solutions, including an agent assist solution with a $40 million net present value.

sessions.title: A Practical Playbook for Making AI Real: Bring Physical and Agentic AI to Life (Presented by SoftServe, Inc.)
sessions.abstract:
As enterprises move beyond AI experimentation and into production, they face the growing complexity of modern AI across capabilities, technologies, and use cases. This session presents a practical, outcome-driven playbook for making AI real by translating that complexity into a clear, actionable framework.

By leveraging physical AI and agentic AI, organizations can deliver step-change improvements in efficiency, safety, and profitability. We’ll explore how enterprises connect real-time simulation, robotics, intelligent collaborative agents, and computer vision into scalable architectures that transform the end-to-end value chain.
Through real-world business use cases, you'll gain a clear framework to simplify AI adoption, articulate value to stakeholders, and accelerate the deployment of physical and agentic AI in production environments.

speakers.name: Taras Bachynskyi
speakers.bio: Taras is vice president of technology at SoftServe, where he leads initiatives focused on NVIDIA technologies and strategic projects. He works closely with NVIDIA to drive innovation and help customers translate advanced AI into real-world business outcomes. With extensive experience partnering with global enterprises, Taras has helped organizations address complex business challenges by applying technologies such as digital twins, real-time simulation, agentic AI, computer vision, AI/ML platforms, big data, and internet of things. Taras brings a practical, outcome-driven perspective to AI adoption, helping enterprises move from experimentation to scalable, production-ready solutions.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Build Self-Learning Agents on Cloudflare (Presented by Cloudflare)
sessions.abstract: Cloudflare provides a serverless platform for building agents that can run code, execute tools, and persist state without managing servers or backend infrastructure. In this session, we’ll show how workers, durable objects, and Cloudflare’s Agents SDK can power a self-learning agent by having the agent control a car to go around obstacles in a simulated environment. This illustrates how Cloudflare enables agents that can learn, adapt, and evolve over time, rather than operate as stateless chat interfaces.

speakers.name: Maximo Guk
speakers.bio: Maximo is a software engineer on Cloudflare's Developer Platform working on edge computing and AI-related products (e.g. MCP).

sessions.title: AI No Longer Benefits From High-Capacity SSDs—It Requires Them (Presented by Solidigm)
sessions.abstract: Huge training datasets are not new. But massive context windows, extended workflows, and rapid iteration are causing a data explosion on the inference side that analysts have dubbed the “memory apocalypse.” Demand vastly outpaces supply, putting AI users in a crunch. The solution: modern SSDs that are fast enough to relieve the pressure while also offering storage in the hundreds of terabytes, enabling unprecedented scale and efficiency.

speakers.name: Ace Stryker
speakers.bio: Ace Stryker focuses on AI workloads, data infrastructure, and the role of SSDs in improving real-world outcomes. A Day 1 Solidigm employee, he previously worked as a solution architect and technical marketing engineer in Intel's memory group. Ace has an M.B.A. from Cornell University and lives in the Sacramento area.

sessions.title: Eliminate the Data Bottleneck: Accelerate Enterprise AI at Scale (Presented by Elastic)
sessions.abstract: Successful enterprise AI deployments require context from your petabytes of unstructured, proprietary company data. Yet indexing and vectorizing this data at enterprise speed and scale remains one of the biggest bottlenecks to implementing AI in production, resulting in stalled deployments and high overhead costs. Elastic’s GPU-accelerated search with NVIDIA cuVS now delivers 12x faster indexing speeds and enables enterprise-scale AI deployments that accelerate your path from proof of concept to production.

speakers.name: James Williams
speakers.bio: As an Elastic senior principal solutions architect, James helps customers evaluate and implement enterprise search solutions. He specializes in Gen AI use cases that leverage a combination of lexical, hybrid, and vector search techniques. Drawing on a 20-plus year background in enterprise application architecture, James has navigated pivotal tech movements including virtualization, containerization, enterprise open source, and distributed data platforms.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: The Data Storage Platform That Powers AI Factories (Presented by Everpure)
sessions.abstract:
Ready to move your AI pilot from proof of concept to production scale? Your success depends on an AI factory architecture that continuously ingests data, refines models, and delivers inference at scale. And while GPUs power the AI factory engine, your data storage platform determines how fast and consistently your AI factory actually runs.

This session explores why your storage strategy is critical to AI success—and how Everpure delivers the performance, scalability, and reliability AI Factories demand. Learn how the Everpure AI Data Platform, including FlashBlade, FlashStack for AI, and Data Stream, accelerates every stage of the pipeline from data preparation through training and inference.

speakers.name: Erin Stevens
speakers.bio: Erin Stevens is a senior product marketing manager at Everpure, focused on helping enterprises modernize their data infrastructure for AI and cloud. Before joining Everpure, Erin worked at Google Cloud and Tableau, where she built and advised data analytics teams to improve performance and scalability. Earlier in her career, she worked as an actuary, building early predictive models for insurance.

sessions.title: No One Will Use Your AI Agents if They Don’t Feel Human (Presented by ElevenLabs)
sessions.abstract: AI agents are getting smarter—but many still fail to earn real usage because they don’t feel human in live interaction. In this session, we’ll explore why agents need a conversational interface, what it takes to handle real-time interactions at scale, and how to build brand trust and repeat usage.

speakers.name: Ben Budde
speakers.bio: Ben Budde has spent over 15 years at the intersection of leading AI research and Fortune 500 organizations. He currently leads revenue at ElevenLabs, the leading voice agent platform. A battle-tested business leader and decorated Marine Corps officer, Ben has a proven track record of improving workflows for developers and executives—bridging innovation and trust to unlock new levels of efficiency and experience in the evolving AI landscape.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation

sessions.title: How AI-Infused Platforms Redefine Modernization, Delivery, and Value (Presented by Wipro)
sessions.abstract: In the AI era, competitiveness will be determined not by how fast enterprises modernize once, but by how effectively they institutionalize change itself. AI platforms illustrate this shift by productizing modernization intelligence, embedding AI across the software life cycle, and enabling enterprises to reduce the cost of change while accelerating AI readiness.

speakers.name: Shivakumar Krishnan
speakers.bio:
Shiv brings over 24 years of experience in Consulting, Technology transformation and building large teams. As the Global Head of Application Engineering, Shiv leads a team of 20,000+ engineers who help clients deliver their Application and platform modernisations from shaping the vision to realizing the business benefits.
Shiv is passionate about translating complex transformation goals into actionable strategies, solution components, driving engineering excellence and contextual AI implementation. His collaborative leadership style and deep commitment to execution inspires excellence, creating an environment where talent are able to deliver innovative solutions and value.
Shiv is product managing the development of new AI led assets and accelerators as part of WEGA platform, leveraging Wipro experience coupled with wider partner ecosystem solutions.
Shiv brings wealth of hands-on transformation experience across number of industries including Banking, Telecom, Retail, Insurance and Technology firms.
Shiv holds double masters degree with MBA and MSc in Computer Science. This enables him to bring balanced perspectives of how technology and Business teams can collaborate to deliver value.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Capital Group, Global Asset Manager, Transforms Investor onboarding with NVIDIA and Infosys AI Next platform (Presented by Infosys)
sessions.abstract:
Capital Group Investor Onboarding Transformation:

Infosys modernized and deployed a portfolio guidelines analysis solution on Infosys AI Next platform, powered by NVIDIA NIM for Capital Group. This AI-based solution streamlines the review of compliance language to speed up client onboarding, enable analysis and research, and reduce reputational and financial risk.

The solution automatically extracts compliance language from investment guidelines documents, categorizes it, and maps the language to applicable compliance rules. This implementation reduces manual work and enables portfolio guidelines team to review and approve onboarding of clients with significantly higher speed and precision.

The platform, in production today, has increased the efficiency of review and processing volume significantly across customers’ onboarding workflows.

speakers.name: Badri Devalla
speakers.bio: Badri Devalla heads AI & Automation Solutions for Infosys EdgeVerve. His expertise and focus is to bring the latest in predictive/document/generative/agentic AI to enterprises as they evolve from automation to autonomy. Badri has scaled AI in enterprises to drive touchless operations, deepen customer engagement, and amplify employee potential. His global team of forward-deployed solution engineers design AI-first solutions that drive business outcomes for our clients using EdgeVerve AI Next platform. Badri is also responsible for strategic initiatives with go-to-market partners for EdgeVerve. Badri has a Ph.D. in computer science from Texas A&M University.

speakers.name: Yuriy Suzdaltsev
speakers.bio: Yuriy Suzdaltsev is a technology leader at Capital Group with more than 25 years of experience building large-scale financial and enterprise software platforms. He leads engineering initiatives focused on cloud transformation, scalable architecture, and modern development practices supporting investment management systems. Over his career, he has held engineering and technical leadership roles at organizations including Thomson Reuters, Mantara/UNX and other technology firms, working on complex, high-availability systems in regulated environments. Yuriy holds a Master of Business Administration from the Marshall School of Business at the University of Southern California and a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science from California State University, Northridge, combining deep technical expertise with a strategic business perspective.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Liquid Cooling Readiness for Vera-Rubin Reference Architectures (Presented by nVent)
sessions.abstract: Vera-Rubin deployments extend the performance envelope of direct-to-chip liquid cooling while remaining compatible with the same core liquid-cooling architecture used for Grace-Blackwell systems. This session defines what it means to be “liquid-cooling ready” for NVIDIA SuperPOD reference architectures by examining coolant distribution unit (CDU) and technology cooling system (TCS) network design across Grace-Blackwell and Vera-Rubin platforms. A properly designed TCS manifold with common CDU and rack-drop interfaces enables scalability through CDU count and rack-drop configuration, allowing systems to scale from GB200 to VR300 without changes to the underlying TCS architecture.

speakers.name: Matthew Archibald
speakers.bio: Matt Archibald is director of technical architecture at nVent, where he leads new product and technology strategy for data center and networking solutions, with a strong emphasis on advanced liquid cooling. With a background spanning power electronics, data center engineering, and software architecture at IBM and Lenovo, he brings deep expertise in bridging IT and facility infrastructure. At nVent, Matt focuses on unified infrastructure management, monitoring, and automation to help customers deploy efficient, scalable, and reliable data center environments worldwide.

sessions.title: Reducing the Sim2Real Gap for Industrial Robotics (Presented by Synopsys)
sessions.abstract: Robotics and physical AI have reached their “ChatGPT” moment. The reliability for industrial use requires precision dexterity, beginning with precision simulation and high sim2real fidelity, where robots learn and reason in simulation before deployment. This session highlights the collaboration between Synopsys and Analog Devices (ADI) to augment the fidelity of simulation environments for dexterity in industrial robotics. The collaboration includes an industry-standard benchmark for tactile manipulation, led by ADI and enabled by Synopsys.

speakers.name: Rich Goldman
speakers.bio: Will update once I get from Rich-

speakers.name: Philip Sharos
speakers.bio: Philip Sharos is a Principal Engineer at Analog Devices, leading simulation and digital‑twin efforts to close the sim‑to‑real gap in robotics. He leads a cross‑disciplinary team spanning robotics, machine learning, and physics‑based simulation, developing AI‑accelerated simulation assets for robot learning that transfer reliably to physical systems. He collaborates closely with partners across the NVIDIA and robotics ecosystems.

sessions.title: Designing Modular Infrastructure for the AI Factory Era (Presented by Intelliflex)
sessions.abstract:
AI workloads are scaling faster than traditional data center models were designed to support, particularly as power density requirements increase and expansion timelines compress.

In this session, we explore the infrastructure requirements of AI factories—including power density, modular deployment approaches, and direct liquid cooling readiness—and examine architectural strategies that enable phased scalability while avoiding the constraints of retrofitting legacy, low-density facilities for accelerated computing at scale.

speakers.name: Charles L’Ecuyer
speakers.bio: Chuck L’Ecuyer, founder and CEO of CES Corp., has over two decades of experience driving innovation in engineering and construction. Since founding CES in 2011, he has led the creation of Intelliflex—a groundbreaking modular platform designed to accelerate AI and high-performance computing. Intelliflex empowers organizations to scale faster, deploy smarter, and unlock the future of data infrastructure.

sessions.title: Opentrons: Close the Gap Between Intent and Execution in the Lab
sessions.abstract: AI is rapidly reshaping laboratory automation, moving beyond static scripting toward systems that can reason, perceive, and act in the physical world. In this session, James Atwood (Opentrons) explores what’s next for AI-enabled labs as large language models, computer vision, and robotics converge. He'll examine how LLMs are evolving from tools that translate scientific intent into executable workflows into components of adaptive systems that plan experiments, interact with robotic platforms, and respond to changing requirements. He'll also highlight the growing role of vision-based and multi-modal models for verification, real-time monitoring, and in-run adjustment. Looking ahead, James introduces emerging vision-language-action approaches that unify planning, perception, and execution, connecting digital intelligence with physical lab automation.

speakers.name: James Atwood
speakers.bio: James Atwood is the chief executive officer of Opentrons, where he leads a new era of AI-driven laboratory automation that brings advanced robotics and intelligent software within reach of every scientist. With a career spanning genomics, analytical instrumentation, and life-science platforms, James has built and scaled technologies that turn complex scientific workflows into accessible, repeatable systems. He is focused on redefining how humans and machines collaborate in the lab—using AI to close the gap between scientific intent and physical execution, and accelerating the pace of discovery worldwide.

sessions.title: Building Proprietary Multi-Agentic AI Workflows for a Consumer Banking Operational Use Case (Presented by Capital One)
sessions.abstract: Capital One leverages its own proprietary, multi-agentic AI framework to help several hundred bank customer service agents that specialize in complex fraud resolution to understand, analyze, respond to real-time customer queries with more speed/quality than ever before. Explore how Capital One uses multi-agent collaboration to process long-form conversational audio between customers and service agents—or across agents—to generate accurate, structured, and low-latency summaries. These summaries enhance the human agent experience—especially by reducing the large cognitive load required to accurately recap long-form discussions, which can last up to an hour for complex fraud scenarios—as well as the customer experience by advancing quality reviews, speed, and agent productivity, while meeting rigorous business standards around accuracy, reliability, and risk management.

speakers.name: Milind Naphade
speakers.bio: Milind Naphade, senior vice president for technology, leads the AI Foundations team within the Intelligent Foundations and Experiences organization at Capital One. Milind partners with product, tech, and business leaders to advance AI efforts, such as generative AI, at Capital One.

speakers.name: Ritesh Soni
speakers.bio:
Ritesh Soni is the Divisional Data and AI Officer for Capital One’s  Retail Bank. In this role he leads the application of statistical, machine learning and AI methods to uncover opportunities and enable optimal decision making across various functions in the Retail Bank.

Ritesh’s career began with academic research in symbolic AI in the mid-90s followed by his nearly two decades leading engineering and machine learning teams at Sapient building capabilities centered on applications in Retail, Telecom and Security.

In his current role, Ritesh leads the transformation of Consumer Banking by leveraging the combined power of classical statistical learning methods and emerging capabilities of large reasoning models in support of robust, optimal and better decisions that enable customer trust and bring simplicity to Banking.

Ritesh holds a Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science and Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (Bombay) and a Masters of Science in Computer Science from Indiana University. He also holds a Masters in Information and Data Science from University of California, Berkeley.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - 3D Model Generation

sessions.title: NVIDIA Inception Presents: Startup Pitches
sessions.abstract: Join us for an engaging hour of startup pitches showcasing groundbreaking solutions across diverse fields, including media & entertainment, climate, energy, retail, and more. These dynamic presentations are part of NVIDIA Inception, a free program designed to help startups evolve faster through cutting-edge technology, opportunities to connect with venture capitalists, and access to the latest technical resources from NVIDIA.

speakers.name: Chris Brown
speakers.bio:
Chris is a part of the Inception Ecosystem team where he helps support the NVIDIA Inception startup ecosystem through the development of scaled community support and evangelism. 
In his career, Chris has been helping communities all over the globe at groups like 500 Global, the NYU Data Future Lab, Startup Wise Guys, and now NVIDIA. He’s deeply passionate and curious about using innovation to drive opportunity for founders around the world.

speakers.name: Alex Momand
speakers.bio: Alex Momand is the founder of Firescore, where he leads the charge in fighting fires with data. From shaping sustainable waste policies in developing countries to pioneering AI-driven geospatial intelligence with U.S. fire departments, he’s a frontline innovator redefining wildfire prevention shoulder to shoulder with firefighters.

sessions.title: Life Sciences: Scale Multi‑Agent for Accelerated Clinical Operation Systems With NVIDIA and AWS (Presented by Cognizant)
sessions.abstract: Discover how life sciences leaders are transforming enterprise‑scale clinical operations through multi‑agent AI systems built on NVIDIA’s accelerated computing and AWS’ cloud‑native intelligence. This session brings AbbVie and Clario to the stage to share how organizations can strategically modernize workflows, unify data and automation platforms, and unlock new operating leverage—positioning their enterprises for faster innovation, responsible AI adoption, and measurable business impact.

speakers.name: Derek Debe
speakers.bio: Derek is the director for innovation and disruptive technology at Abbvie.

speakers.name: Gaurav Josan
speakers.bio: With more than 20 years’ experience leading technology initiatives, including transformation and launch of innovative products, Gaurav is passionate about accelerating clinical trials by solving complex problems using technology. Adept at optimization and new service/product launch, she thrives in implementing global team structure utilizing bright talent across the globe. Gaurav is recognized for strategic vision, along with pragmatic execution.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Making AI Agents Explainable for Manufacturing at Scale With Dataiku and NVIDIA (Presented by Dataiku)
sessions.abstract: As manufacturers accelerate toward autonomous AI agents to reduce costly unplanned downtime, trust, transparency, and safety have become the critical blockers to adoption in high-stakes environments. This session shows how Dataiku and NVIDIA, together with the Puffin Inspector framework from Dataiku’s Open Source Lab, make AI-driven scheduling explainable, governed, and ready for real-world industrial orchestration today.

speakers.name: Hannes Hapke
speakers.bio: Hannes Hapke leads Dataiku's new Open Source Lab, with a focus on responsible AI, privacy, and AI explainability. His work centers on advancing open and trustworthy AI practices within the Dataiku ecosystem.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models

sessions.title: The Velocity of Volts: Hitachi and NVIDIA Bring Physical AI to Energy Infrastructure (Presented by Hitachi)
sessions.abstract: U.S. electricity demand is rising fast. But connecting new energy sources to the grid is challenging, with twice the total generated power capacity (1.28 terawatts) in the United States sitting in a "queue," waiting for interconnection. This backlog is largely due to the exhaustive, manual simulation studies required to ensure that new power sources do not destabilize the existing grid. This session highlights how Hitachi and NVIDIA are using physical AI to reduce the time taken to connect new energy sources to the grid by up to 80%.

speakers.name: Roslyn Stuart
speakers.bio:
Roslyn Stuart serves as the vice president of synergy creation at Hitachi Digital, where she leads and accelerates the establishment of advanced digital solutions across the green energy and mobility sectors. In this role, Roslyn works closely with Hitachi Energy and Hitachi Rail, uniting the technological strengths of Hitachi Digital Services and GlobalLogic to co-create next-generation innovations that strengthen Hitachi’s global digital ecosystem. This ecosystems includes building external partnerships working very closely with NVIDIA, and has launched HMAX accelerated by NVIDIA.

Prior to joining Hitachi Digital, Roslyn successfully ran Hitachi Rail in Australia. Recognized for deep cross-industry expertise, Roslyn was invited by Hitachi Ltd. to help shape the One-Hitachi business strategy, fostering collaboration between Hitachi Energy, Hitachi Rail, and the broader Digital & Services organizations—including Hitachi Digital Services, Hitachi Vantara, and GlobalLogic—to accelerate digital transformation for sustainable, profitable growth.

With over 20 years of global experience in systems engineering and digital transformation, Roslyn has delivered industry-defining programs—including leading Rio Tinto’s AutoHaul, the world’s first fully autonomous heavy-haul rail system, which was recognized in the 2020 Guinness Book of Records as the world’s heaviest robot.

Earlier in Roslyn’s career, she developed advanced through-water communication systems for naval customers across Australia, the U.K., and the United States. Roslyn is recognized for authentic leadership and a commitment to uniting global teams to drive innovation and societal impact.

speakers.name: Adrian Timbus
speakers.bio: Adrian Timbus is the head of portfolio and market strategy at Hitachi Energy. He has been with the company since 2007, focusing on developing technology and solutions for the energy transition. Adrian also chairs the European Technology and Innovation Platform (ETIP Wind), advising European institutions on the future direction of wind and renewable power.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Energy Exploration & Generation

sessions.title: Transform Clinical and Commercial Workflows with AI Agents That Deliver Immediate Impact
sessions.abstract: Explore how a unified agentic AI platform, optimized for life sciences, can automate complex clinical and commercial workflows to accelerate research, improve patient outcomes, and drive sustainable growth. With organizations moving quickly toward an "agent-first" operating model, now is the moment to understand where automation can deliver immediate, transformational impact.

speakers.name: Raja Shankar
speakers.bio:
Raja is focused on transforming life sciences with applied AI. As the vice president for machine learning, Raja is determined to change healthcare with the power of AI. He believes that imagination is the biggest barrier, and motivates his team to create new narratives that fully leverage AI's potential to reshape the industry, from R&D through to commercialization. 

Raja brings together a diverse set of technical and strategic capabilities, including ML, deep learning, generative AI, product development, life sciences expertise, and business consulting skills. His team combines data science, engineering, domain expertise, and consulting skills to drive impactful change by applying AI to life sciences and healthcare decisions. Currently, his focus is on leveraging data and AI to help transform clinical development. Raja is a trusted adviser to global life sciences companies, public sector clients, and international organizations.

speakers.name: Sandeep Burugupalli
speakers.bio:
Sandeep Burugupalli is Head of Data Science in BioPharmaceuticals R&D at AstraZeneca, where he leads data science and AI initiatives supporting R&D and enterprise operations. His work focuses on applying advanced AI approaches to clinical development, evidence generation, and scientific execution at scale. 

Prior to joining AstraZeneca, he served as Vice President, Data Science External Affairs at Novo Nordisk, where he helped shape the company’s approach to AI and external innovation partnerships in clinical development during a period of rapid growth in a new class of medicines for obesity and cardiometabolic disease. Before that, he led enterprise data initiatives at Pfizer, bringing AI and real‑world data into R&D, including during the height of the COVID‑19 pandemic. He also served as a White House Presidential Innovation Fellow, supporting the national launch of the NIH All of Us Research Program, a landmark precision‑medicine initiative.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Biology - Generative AI

sessions.title: From Smart Factory to Lights-Out Factory: Operationalizing Industrial AI With LTTS and NVIDIA (Presented by LTTS)
sessions.abstract:
Manufacturing is advancing automation, yet factories still depend on human input for planning, quality, and operations. The next step is autonomous, self-optimizing factories powered by industrial AI.

L&T Technology Services (LTTS) introduces its Lights-Out Factory (LoF) solution, built on NVIDIA’s Industrial AI platform, Omniverse, Isaac, and CUDA-accelerated AI. LoF integrates digital twins, robotics, and AI-driven decision-making into a closed-loop system, enabling factories to move from assisted automation to autonomy.

This session shows how LTTS combines manufacturing expertise with NVIDIA’s AI to create learning factories. Discover how virtual twins simulate workflows, AI adapts to real-time conditions, and robotics improve yield, safety, and throughput. Real-world results include reduced cycle times, improved yields, and faster ramp-ups, with a roadmap for scaling LoF deployments

speakers.name: Umashankar KA
speakers.bio: Umashankar K A is a seasoned business leader with over 34 years of experience, including deep expertise in Consulting, IT, and Digital Manufacturing. He specializes in AI‑driven manufacturing and Plant, Process, and Product Digital Twins, with a strong track record in building high‑performing Competency Units and Centers of Excellence. Throughout his career, he has advised Fortune 500 clients across Automotive, Energy, Industrial Products, and Medical sectors. Umashankar is also the pioneer of a patented Digital Twin methodology at LTTS, successfully deployed across global enterprises.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Private AI: Run Modern Models On-Prem Without Moving Sensitive Data (Presented by Cirrascale)
sessions.abstract: Explore why more enterprises are shifting AI workloads closer to protect sensitive company data and how to operationalize Private AI without sacrificing performance. In this session, we’ll break down the real-world drivers behind on-prem and private deployments (data sovereignty, IP protection, regulated workloads, and risk reduction), then walk through a practical blueprint for running modern models with full control of data. Attendees will leave with a clear framework for model selection, secure deployment design, and operational controls so teams can confidently deploy high-performance inference in private environments while keeping sensitive data in place and minimizing exposure.

speakers.name: Dave Driggers
speakers.bio: Dave Driggers is the founder and CEO of Cirrascale Cloud Services, a leading provider of purpose-built AI infrastructure. With decades of experience in accelerated computing, cloud, and high-performance systems, Dave has built Cirrascale to help enterprises deploy AI at scale without the cost, complexity, or constraints of hyperscalers. Under his leadership, Cirrascale delivers secure, high-performance GPU infrastructure with white-glove service, enabling customers to train, fine-tune, and run production AI with confidence and speed.

sessions.title: Building Dawn: An AI Agent for Mental Health With Clinically-Grounded Safety
sessions.abstract: We'll share how we built Dawn, an AI agent for mental health support, addressing the unique challenges of deploying LLMs in clinical settings. Learn how we developed clinically-grounded evaluation frameworks and specialized guardrails through close collaboration with Ph.D.-level psychologists, moving beyond generic safety measures to systems designed specifically for therapeutic conversations.

speakers.name: Ricardo Rei
speakers.bio:
As the head of AI research at Sword Health, Ricardo Rei leads efforts to transform healthcare through artificial intelligence, with a specific focus on LLMs for mental health and multi-modal architectures for physical therapy.
Previously, he created the standard machine translation evaluation metric COMET during his Ph.D. at at Instituto Superior Técnico and Carnegie Mellon University. In the generative space, he was a core contributor to the multilingual EuroLLM and the lead researcher behind TowerLLM (70B), the model that secured first place in the WMT2024 translation competition.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Make AI Factories Production-Ready: Inference, Networking, and DPUs (Presented by F5, Inc.)
sessions.abstract:
As AI factories move from experimentation to production, teams quickly learn that GPU performance alone does not determine inference capacity, latency, or user experience. Inference workloads produce long-lived, bursty, stateful traffic that strains traditional networking and security architectures, reducing token throughput, increasing time-to-first-token, and introducing instability at scale.

This session explores how AI factories built on NVIDIA accelerated computing benefit from BlueField DPU-accelerated networking and cloud-native traffic management. We examine the unique demands of AI inference, how NVIDIA DPUs offload and isolate critical data paths, and how inference-aware traffic control improves performance, reduces latency, and enables consistent multi-tenant inference services in production environments.

speakers.name: Gregory Dalle
speakers.bio:
Gregory Dalle is a senior director of product management at F5, where he focuses on infrastructure platforms that support AI inference and application workloads at scale. He works closely with platform engineers, infrastructure architects, and operators to address the networking, security, and traffic management challenges that emerge as AI systems move from experimentation into production.

With deep experience in cloud-native architectures, networking, and distributed systems, Greg brings a practical, production-first perspective on how infrastructure design impacts inference performance, latency, and reliability.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: When Surgical Intelligence Becomes Embodied: The Agentic Operating Room and the Partners Behind It
sessions.abstract:
AI in healthcare is moving beyond perception and prediction toward real-time action in the physical world. Nowhere is this shift more demanding than the operating room, where conditions change continuously and coordination across systems is critical.

This session introduces the agentic operating room—a physical AI architecture where distributed agents across imaging, robotics, sensors, and clinical applications run locally, reason independently, and synchronize through shared context and orchestration. See how NVIDIA’s physical AI stack and partner network enables this workflow, from simulation and digital twins to accelerated training and low-latency edge inference.

Join Pertias.ai to explore how physical AI is turning the operating room into a coordinated, software-defined system—unlocking safer, more adaptive, and more autonomous surgical workflows.

speakers.name: Courtney Knoll
speakers.bio:
Cortney Knoll is a technology executive and company builder who specializes in turning emerging technologies into category-defining platforms. Across Fortune 100 enterprises, she has built and scaled AI-driven businesses—defining enterprise AI strategies and leading the platforms that generated more than $900 million in growth. As president and CEO of peritasAi, Cortney is building the orchestration layer for embodied AI in healthcare—connecting intelligent software, physical systems, and human teams into a real-time operating platform for the perioperative enterprise.

Previously, Cortney founded and scaled IBM Garage globally, and led first-of-their-kind digital platform initiatives at HSBC and Veritas, driving large-scale transformation at the intersection of data, automation, and intelligent systems.

speakers.name: Shaun Braun
speakers.bio: Shaun Braun is Chief Information and Technology Officer at Zimmer Biomet, leading IT, AI, and Digital & Technology Solutions to drive growth, agility, and efficiency, including Surgical Robotics and Connected Health solutions. Previously, he held senior leadership roles at Intuitive Surgical, 3M, and Stryker, and began his career in software development at Oracle, PeopleSoft, and JD Edwards. Shaun holds a bachelor’s degree in Neurobiology and an MBA from UC Berkeley, and has been recognized by Fortune as a top 10 Service Leader.

sessions.title: GPU‑Accelerated Performance Gains With AI-RAN powered by Nokia and NVIDIA (Presented by Nokia)
sessions.abstract: Get a look at how Nokia’s co‑innovation with NVIDIA marks a deep integration of GPUs in the radio access network (RAN), bringing high‑performance computing to the edge and accelerating the path to 6G leadership. This session will discuss the latest performance benchmarks and new use cases for AI‑RAN.

speakers.name: Aji Ed
speakers.bio:
As head of AI-RAN, Aji is driving AI-RAN products and solutions, as well as leading strategic initiatives in collaboration with partners. His scope includes product strategy, business development, and market making for AI RAN (radio access network) solutions. Previously, Aji served as the CTO / head of technology for mobile networks in the Middle East/Africa region, creating technology thought leadership for Nokia. 
 
Aji is an active, intuitive leader with a multicultural background.  He is passionate about empowering and coaching his team members to achieve ever-higher goals, beyond the imaginable.

sessions.title: The AI Cloud Behind the Breakthroughs: How Core42 Accelerates MBZUAI Research (Presented by Core42)
sessions.abstract: Discover how a sovereign, multi‑accelerator AI cloud is enabling Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence (MBZUAI) researchers to train frontier‑level models faster, more securely, and at larger scale, removing compute bottlenecks and unlocking new breakthroughs in language, climate, and scientific AI. This session explores how purpose-built infrastructure can accelerate research velocity and empower institutions to compete at a global level.

speakers.name: Raghu Chakravarthi
speakers.bio:
Raghu Chakravarthi is Core42 EVP, Engineering and GM, Americas leading the development of scalable AI-enabled analytics, cloud applications, and big data platforms across on-premises and public cloud environments. He oversees the architecture, development, and deployment of Core42’ AI Cloud, including Compass, its flagship platform that enables developers to build and scale GenAI workloads with seamless infrastructure integration. He is a seasoned engineering and product leader with over three decades of experience building large-scale AI, data analytics, and cloud platforms.​

​At GTC, he will anchor Core42's technical presence, engaging directly with NVIDIA's engineering and product teams on platform integration, AI infrastructure architecture, and the evolution of Compass to power enterprise-scale GenAI deployments.

sessions.title: Prioritize Accelerated Compute: Maximize Impact in a Supply-Constrained World (Presented by PwC)
sessions.abstract:
In today’s economy, accelerated computing is the engine behind AI, industrial optimization, and next-generation digital platforms. Yet demand for advanced chips continues to outpace supply, forcing enterprises to make sharper decisions about where their computing resources create the greatest impact. 

In this forward-looking conversation, sector leaders from industrial products, energy, and technology will explore how organizations can prioritize their accelerated computing resources across competing use cases. Rather than relying on static forecasts, the discussion will introduce a practical framework for aligning GPU deployment with business value, operational resilience, and broader societal needs. The goal is not simply to manage existing resources, but to ensure that compute capacity is directed toward the most transformative and economically meaningful applications. 

Designed to be timely and actionable, this session will help you move from reactive allocation to strategic prioritization in an environment defined by rapid technological change.

speakers.name: Dallas Dolen
speakers.bio:
Dallas leads PwC's Technology, Media, and Telecom industry, which includes overseeing service delivery, thought leadership, and marketing in this space. Dallas is also the global client partner for PwC’s largest technology client, where he delivers consulting and compliance services and oversees the company's commercial relationship with the market. 

Dallas has nearly 20 years of experience working with Fortune 500 software, internet, and entertainment & media clients, leading deals, divestitures, business expansion, and global technology transformation. Dallas also serves
on the PwC Network’s Global Board. He enjoys supporting his children's sports, and trail running.

speakers.name: Ryan Hawk
speakers.bio:
Ryan is a partner with PwC’s Operations practice, based in Chicago. He has more than 32 years of professional experience. He has served some of the firm’s largest, multinational clients in areas such as cost reduction, manufacturing and operations improvement, and deals. He is one of the firm's leaders around the future of industrials and industry convergence, and recently coauthored "Can smart tech, robots, and bold policy rebuild American manufacturing?" 

Ryan holds a B.S. in mechanical engineering from Rose-Hulman Institute of Technology and an M.S. in industrial engineering from the University of Michigan.

sessions.title: The Next Era of Surgery: AI-Driven Innovation in the Operating Room
sessions.abstract: Explore how Barco’s newest AI-based surgical products, powered by NVIDIA AI infrastructure and the NVIDIA Holoscan platform, transform the operating room into a software-defined space. You will discover how generative AI improves surgical precision and patient safety, and simplifies the clinical workflow. Combined with NVIDIA edge computing solutions, we can run real-time AI models that act as a surgical copilot.

speakers.name: Stephanie Dupont
speakers.bio: Stephanie Dupont is the VP of Product Management at Barco Healthcare, where she accelerates innovation at the intersection of technology, healthcare, and AI. She holds a PhD in Engineering from Stanford University and brings a decade of experience in the healthcare sector. Stephanie has led high‑impact initiatives across global corporations like Bain, HP, and Barco, as well as dynamic startups. Known for her strategic drive and ability to turn complex ideas into breakthrough solutions, she empowers teams to deliver products that elevate surgical care and push the boundaries of what’s possible in healthcare.

sessions.title: Transforming Industry With Physical AI (Presented by Agile Robots)
sessions.abstract: Agile Robots is driving a shift in industrial automation—from systems that follow predefined rules to machines that perceive, learn, and act in the physical world through AI. This talk explores how Agile Robots builds on deep experience in industrial automation and combines it with advanced AI to realize physical AI as a new paradigm for designing work, manufacturing, and production, where intelligence is embodied in robotic systems, rather than confined to software. The presentation highlights how Agile Robots is developing today’s and the next generation's robotic systems—including humanoid robots—by integrating NVIDIA Isaac open frameworks and libraries to bring AI-driven flexibility, autonomy, and collaboration to the factory floor, and outlines the technological trajectory ahead.

speakers.name: Dmitrij Forssilow
speakers.bio: Dmitrij Forssilow is head of strategic accounts at Agile Robots. He is a co-founder of Franka Emika, a pioneer in collaborative robotics, where he was the lead engineer behind the development of the award-winning Franka Research 3 robot arm, now one of the most widely used robotic platforms in academia and research. At Agile Robots, he focuses on business development and strategic account management.

sessions.title: Detect Windshield Damage Using Physically Realistic Synthetic Data
sessions.abstract: Explore a practical pipeline that uses Omniverse synthetic data and Cosmos-Transfer to measure windshield damage with pixel-level masks and produce clear repair versus replace calls. You gain tools to iterate faster, boost model quality without real data, and harden claims with trusted, auditable outputs.

speakers.name: Diego Torres
speakers.bio: Diego's an engineer with deep expertise in low-level development, performance optimization, and hardware design. He has a proven track record in software and video game development.

speakers.name: Jose Castro
speakers.bio: Jose has over 20 years of experience in 3D/VFX leading procedural generation, simulation, technical art, and synthetic data pipelines for global productions. He's an expert at bridging art and tech.

speakers.name: Miguel Clavijo
speakers.bio: Miguel is a Ph.D. engineer with deep expertise in AI for autonomous vehicles, 3D mapping, and scene understanding, with a proven R&D track record across academia and industry.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation

sessions.title: LESS: Large Language Model Enhanced Semi-Supervised Learning for Speech Foundational Models Using In-the-Wild Data​
sessions.abstract: Although state-of-the-art speech foundation models (SFM) can produce high-quality text pseudo-labels, applying semi-supervised learning (SSL) for in-the-wild, real-world data remains challenging due to its richer and more complex acoustics. We therefore introduce LESS (Large Language Model Enhanced Semi-supervised Learning), a versatile framework that uses LLMs to correct pseudo-labels generated on in-the-wild data. In LESS, pseudo-labeled text from automatic speech recognition (ASR) or automatic speech translation (AST) of the unsupervised data is refined by an LLM, and further improved by a data filtering strategy. Across Mandarin ASR and Spanish-to-English AST evaluations, LESS delivers consistent gains, with an absolute Word Error Rate reduction of 3.8% on WenetSpeech, and BLEU score increase of 0.8 and 0.7, achieving 34.0 on Callhome and 64.7 on Fisher test sets, respectively.

speakers.name: Fan Qian
speakers.bio: Fan is a senior engineer on the Riva team, working on various speech AI-related projects. She graduated from Carnegie Mellon University with an M.S. in computational data science.

speakers.name: Wen Ding
speakers.bio: Wen Ding enhances speech recognition models and develops solutions in the speech processing field. She is the main developer of Riva Mandarin automatic speech recognition models. Wen has a master’s degree in computer science from Shanghai Jiao Tong University.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Audio / Speech Foundation Models

sessions.title: LightRV: An End-to-End Lightweight Training Solution for Open-Source Reasoning Vision-Language Models
sessions.abstract: We introduce a fully open-source, end-to-end training solution for reasoning-capable vision-language models, enabling efficient, low-cost construction of such VLMs without building from scratch. Through collaborative explorations with Zeekr, the pipeline has demonstrated promising applicability to cockpit entertainment scenarios.

speakers.name: Ann Zhao
speakers.bio: Ann Zhao is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, currently responsible for data center GPU optimization for automotive industry customers. Ann focuses on LLM inference deployment and vision-language model/visual-language-action training, with extensive experience in model acceleration and optimization as well as application areas.

speakers.name: Liangmin Wu
speakers.bio: Liangmin Wu is a large model algorithm engineer at Zeekr.

speakers.name: Han Yang
speakers.bio: Han Yang is the director of large model algorithm development at Zeekr, and holds a Ph.D. from Peking University.

speakers.name: Shawn Zhang
speakers.bio: Before joining NVIDIA, Shawn worked for several years at Baidu as a senior natural language processing algorithm engineer. His personal research interests in the field of LLMs include code generation, math, and retrieval-augmented generation.

speakers.name: Shuang Yu
speakers.bio: Shuang Yu is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, specializing in natural language processing, LLMs, and algorithm engineering. She holds a master's degree in computer science from Tsinghua University.

speakers.name: Qiang Tang
speakers.bio: Qiang Tang is a large model algorithm engineer at Zeekr.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Dual Engines of Thought: A Dual-Engine Architecture With Breadth–Depth Coordination for Open-Ended Reasoning in LLMs
sessions.abstract: Dual engines of thought (DEoT) — an adaptive reasoning framework that dynamically orchestrates breadth and depth reasoning modules to enable stable, efficient, and context-sensitive inference in large language models. DEoT employs an entropy-regulated controller to judiciously navigate the reasoning space, mitigating both informational sparsity and computational overhead. This dynamic coordination facilitates robust multi-source integration and enhances the model's capacity for deep causal inference. Empirical results on the open-ended News-to-Question benchmark reveal that DEoT significantly outperforms standard LLMs, delivering responses that are not only more analytically nuanced, but also more logically coherent. Our architecture demonstrates broad applicability across high-impact domains, including biomedicine, economics, geopolitics, industrial systems, and advanced technologies.

speakers.name: Teng-Ruei Chen
speakers.bio: A former engineer at the Industrial Technology Research Institute, Teng-Ruei led multiple projects funded by the Industrial Development Bureau and the Ministry of Science and Technology. He's received of several AI and digital technology awards and holds over 10 patents in intelligent vehicles, energy systems, and machine learning. Teng-Ruei holds a Ph.D. in bioinformatics and systems biology from National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University.

sessions.title: Accelerate Topological Property Simulation of SSH–Hubbard Model via DMET-VQE With CUDA-Q
sessions.abstract: We demonstrate how the Density Matrix Embedding Theory with Variational Quantum Eigensolver (DMET–VQE) can be applied to uncover topological properties in the one-dimensional Su–Schrieffer–Heeger–Hubbard (SSHH) model, and how its computational performance can be enhanced through asynchronous simulation across multiple GPUs using NVIDIA CUDA-Q. In our simulations, the open-boundary edge-state signatures — that is, the signals of the topological states splitting away from half filling to one-and-three-quarters filling as onsite interaction increases — depend on the fragment size, which determines the number of qubits, revealing the significance of computational acceleration in high-qubit simulations.

speakers.name: Ssu-Yi Chen
speakers.bio:
Ssu-Yi Chen is a graduate student at Graduate Institute of Electronics Engineering, National Taiwan University, specializing in Electronic Design Automation and quantum computing. Her research focuses on developing GPU-accelerated Density Matrix Embedding Theory–Variational Quantum
Eigensolver (DMET–VQE) workflows using NVIDIA CUDA-Q to study correlated and topological quantum systems.

speakers.name: Yu-Cheng Chung
speakers.bio: Yu-Cheng Chung is a graduate student in the Department of Physics at National Taiwan Normal University. His research focuses on quantum computing applications in physics and chemistry, including quantum machine learning and hybrid quantum–classical algorithms. He is particularly interested in developing scalable computational frameworks that bridge quantum and classical information theory.

speakers.name: Dah-Wei Chiou
speakers.bio: Dah-Wei Chiou received his B.S. and M.S. degrees in Physics from National Tsing Hua University in 1996 and 1998, respectively, and his Ph.D. in Physics from the University of California, Berkeley, in 2006. From 2020 to 2022, he served as an assistant professor at National Sun Yat-sen University. Since 2023, he has been a postdoctoral researcher at the Graduate Institute of Electronics Engineering at National Taiwan University.

sessions.title: AI-Orchestrating Platform for Drug Repurposing
sessions.abstract: We'll introduce a multi-model AI-orchestrated platform that integrates structure-based screening, knowledge-graph reasoning, and gene-expression analysis to accelerate systematic drug repurposing. By unifying multi-modal data and diverse AI models, the system enables cross-domain reasoning from molecular binding prediction to disease-gene inference. A case study on Bremelanotide illustrates how this approach identifies new therapeutic indications through end-to-end computational orchestration.

speakers.name: YIN-HSONG HSU
speakers.bio: Yin-Hsong Hsu is a pioneer in Taiwan’s healthcare technology, with 20 years of experience bridging innovation and medicine. He is the president of Repurgenesis, a company specializing in AI-driven drug repurposing, and a visiting professor at the AI School of National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University. Previously, he served as president of Acer Medical, where he led Taiwan’s smart healthcare transformation and achieved the nation’s first approved SaMD — now used in over 200 hospitals.

sessions.title: AI-Driven Rapid Diagnosis of Lymph Node Metastasis During Cancer Surgery
sessions.abstract: We developed an advanced intraoperative lymph node diagnostic framework that integrates rapid fresh pathology imaging with an AI-assisted analytical pipeline. The demonstrated approach enables accurate intraoperative diagnosis of lymph nodes, markedly improving diagnostic sensitivity and reducing the false-negative rate of intraoperative frozen section analysis.

speakers.name: Chi-Cheng Fu
speakers.bio: Chi-Cheng Fu is a senior developer relations manager of strategic researcher engagement at NVIDIA, helping scientists advance breakthrough discoveries using accelerated computing and AI. He supports researchers in harnessing these technologies for scientific innovation. Chi-Cheng holds a Ph.D. in bioengineering from UC Berkeley.

speakers.name: Chi-Kuang Sun
speakers.bio: Chi-Kuang Sun is Garmin Distinguished Professor at National Taiwan University. He received his S.B. in electrical engineering from NTU in 1987 and Ph.D. in applied physics from Harvard University in 1995. He founded Molecular Imaging Center, NTU and mesoView Ltd. Chi-Kuang served as deputy dean of NTU Electrical Engineering and Computer Science College, chair of the Photonics Program, Taiwan Ministry of Science and Technology, and president of Taiwan Photonics Society. He is a fellow of IEEE, OPTICA, and SPIE.

speakers.name: Andrew Kuo
speakers.bio: Andrew Kuo is a master’s student at the Graduate Institute of Photonics and Optoelectronics, National Taiwan University. He is currently an exchange student in the Graduate Institute of Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Kobe University. He received his bachelor’s degree in optics and photonics, with a minor in computer science and information engineering, from National Central University.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging

sessions.title: Learn Skill Abstraction From Action-Free Videos
sessions.abstract: Learning from videos is a path toward generalist robots by leveraging diverse visual data beyond traditional datasets. Videos often contain recurring skills (e.g., grasping, lifting) across tasks and environments. While skill-based methods can learn reusable behaviors, they usually require clean, action-labeled data. Conversely, existing video-learning methods often train monolithic models or focus on single-step dynamics, hindering skill composition and long-horizon planning. We propose Skill Abstraction from Optical Flow (SOF), which learns skills directly from action-free videos. SOF uses optical flow as a surrogate for action and adapts skill learning to operate on flow-based representations. It plans in skill space and translates these plans into actions. SOF improves multitask and long-horizon performance, enabling skill acquisition and composition directly from raw visual data.

speakers.name: Shao Hua Sun
speakers.bio: Shao-Hua Sun is an assistant professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at National Taiwan University. He received his Ph.D. from the University of Southern California. His research spans machine learning, robot learning, reinforcement learning, and program synthesis. His work has been published at leading venues, including NeurIPS, ICML, CoRL, ICLR, etc. He has organized tutorials and workshops at NeurIPS, ICML, RLC, and CoRL.

speakers.name: Hung-Chieh Fang
speakers.bio: Hung-Chieh Fang is an undergraduate student at National Taiwan University and a research intern at Stanford University. His research interests include robot learning, machine learning, representation learning, and reinforcement learning. His work has been published at top conferences such as ICML and ICCV.

speakers.name: Yu-Chiang Wang
speakers.bio: Yu-Chiang Frank Wang received his B.S. in electrical engineering from National Taiwan University in 2001, and his M.S. and Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from Carnegie Mellon University in 2004 and 2009. He joined Academia Sinica in 2009, leading the Multimedia and Machine Learning Lab, and later became a professor at National Taiwan University in 2019. Since 2022, he has served as research director of deep learning and computer vision at NVIDIA Research Taiwan.

speakers.name: Kuo-Han Hung
speakers.bio: Kuo-Han Hung is a first-year MSCS student at Stanford specializing in AI and robotics, with four years of experience in machine learning, including large language models, multi-modal models, and policy learning. Kuo-Han published at ICLR 2025, NAACL 2025, and NeurIPS 2024, and interned at IBM Research and Microsoft, working on LLM systems and applied AI for real-world tasks.

speakers.name: Min-Hung Chen
speakers.bio: Min-Hung Chen is a senior research scientist at NVIDIA, specializing in multi-modal AI, including vision-language, video understanding, and efficient deep learning. He has published extensively at top conferences like CVPR, ICCV, ICML, and NeurIPS, and received outstanding reviewer awards. He also co-organized workshops at leading conferences and mentors students from NTU, National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University, and National Tsing Hua University for academic collaborations.

sessions.title: Accelerated Quantum Supercomputing: Build a Multi-GPU, Multi-QPU, Multi-User Environment for Hybrid Algorithms
sessions.abstract: Quantum computers can run some calculations faster than classical computers, but integration with classical computing is required. Our work is the first-ever demonstration of a multi-QPU and multi-GPU environment fully integrated into a data center.

speakers.name: James Fletcher
speakers.bio: As head of solutions architecture for ORCA Computing, James works with customers and partners to break new ground on how photonic quantum processors can integrate with high performance compute, especially for AI/ML workloads in science and industrial research.

sessions.title: Make Federated Learning Robust When Devices Join and Leave
sessions.abstract: This work introduces the first open-source framework for benchmarking federated learning (FL) under dynamic client participation, demonstrating that this practical scenario causes significant performance degradation. To address this challenge, we propose knowledge-pool federated learning (KPFL), a generic plugin that uses a shared knowledge pool and generative knowledge distillation to improve model robustness and prevent knowledge loss.

speakers.name: Chi-Cheng Fu
speakers.bio: Chi-Cheng Fu is a senior developer relations manager of strategic researcher engagement at NVIDIA, helping scientists advance breakthrough discoveries using accelerated computing and AI. He supports researchers in harnessing these technologies for scientific innovation. Chi-Cheng holds a Ph.D. in bioengineering from UC Berkeley.

speakers.name: Yan-Ann Chen
speakers.bio: Yan-Ann Chen is currently an assistant professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Yuan Ze University, Taiwan. His research focuses on pervasive intelligence, artificial intelligence of things, internet of things, and cyber-physical systems. He has published over 20 papers on topics including intelligent sensing, edge AI, and multimodal learning for smart environments. He received his Ph.D. in Computer Science from National Chiao Tung University in 2016.

speakers.name: Yu-Chee Tseng
speakers.bio: Yu-Chee Tseng (Ph.D., Ohio State University, 1994) is an IEEE Fellow and National Chiao Tung University (NCTU) Chair Professor, holding National Chair Professorship (2020-23). He directs NCTU's Microelectronics and Information Research Center and MOST's Pervasive AI Research Labs, having formerly served as NCTU computer science dean. His research in mobile computing, wireless, and internet of things has earned him an h-index > 60, the TWAS Prize (2018), and key editorial roles (e.g., IEEE TMC, IEEE TPDS).

speakers.name: Cheng-Kuan Lin
speakers.bio: Cheng-Kuan Lin (Ph.D., National Chiao Tung University, 2011) is an associate professor at National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University. He previously held positions at Soochow University (2014) and Fuzhou University (2018). He has published over 190 papers, secured multiple patents, and earned a WOS highly cited paper (2022). His research interests include deep learning, computer vision, smart medicine, algorithms, and graph theory.

speakers.name: Ming-Lun Lee
speakers.bio: Ming-Lun Lee received his M.S. in computer science from National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University in 2024. He is currently a graduate research assistant in NYCU PAIR (Pervasive Artificial Intelligence Research) Labs. His research interests focused on robust federated learning systems, multi-modal models for robotics, and the artificial intelligence of things.

speakers.name: Fu-Shiang Yang
speakers.bio: Fu-Shiang Yang is a master's student in computer science and engineering focusing on federated learning and its use in large-scale AI systems. Driven by curiosity, he also explores transformers and generative AI. His goal is to contribute to developing intelligent, efficient, and privacy-preserving AI systems that can broadly benefit society.

sessions.title: Accelerating High-Fidelity Generative Models: Entangled Quantum Walks With CUDA-Q
sessions.abstract:
We introduce a Quantum Walk-based Adaptive Distribution Generator (QW-ADG), a hybrid quantum-classical framework designed for high-efficiency generative modeling. The key is using an entangled two-qubit coin within a variational quantum walk, generating complex 2D spatial correlations for tasks like image creation.

Implemented on the CUDA-Q platform and accelerated on an NVIDIA GPU, our model demonstrates a significant performance advantage over alternative quantum approaches. In a direct benchmark generating 8x8 MNIST digits, our QW-ADG is over 10x faster than a standard quantum generative adversarial network (QGAN) while achieving comparable or superior accuracy (L1 Error).

We establish the QW-ADG as a highly efficient and practical framework for near-term quantum generative modeling, highlighting the power of structured quantum dynamics combined with high-performance simulation.

speakers.name: Yen Jui Chang
speakers.bio: Yen Jui is developing applications of quantum algorithms in finance and machine learning. He's currently a researcher at the Quantum Information Center of Chung Yuan Christian University.

speakers.name: Wei-Ting Wang
speakers.bio: Wei-Ting is researching quantum walks and their applications.

speakers.name: Ching-Ray Chang
speakers.bio: Ching-Ray is a fellow of both the American Physical Society and the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, and a member of the Russian International Academy of Engineering. In recent years, he has been a key promoter of quantum technology development in Taiwan, founding the NTU-IBM Quantum Computer Hub as its first director and leading Taiwan into the new era of the quantum revolution.

sessions.title: Generative Antibody Factory: GPU-Accelerated Diffusion Model and Protein Language Model for Nanobody Discovery
sessions.abstract: We present a GPU-accelerated generative AI pipeline that designs and evaluates nanobody candidates in days instead of months — combining diffusion-based structure generation with protein language models to improve developability and experimental success.

speakers.name: Jhih-Wei Jian
speakers.bio:
Jhih-Wei is a seasoned professional with over 10 years of experience in biomedical research and management,
specializing in protein function prediction, synthetic antibody library design, and next-generation
sequencing data analysis for single-cell RNAseq and cancer genomics. He has demonstrated excellence in
team leadership and cross-functional collaboration, committed to optimizing antibody design and
enhancing R&D efficiency through AI technology.

speakers.name: Hom-Ming Yeh
speakers.bio: Alan Yeh specializes in viral vector engineering and bioprocess development for gene and cell therapy. His work focuses on designing and optimizing lentiviral vectors — covering pseudotyping and safety modifications — to support CAR-T and in vivo CAR applications. He integrates molecular virology and process development to advance next-generation therapeutic platforms at ARCE Therapeutics.

speakers.name: Kao-Jean Huang
speakers.bio: Kao-Jean Huang is an immunologist and an expert in viral infectious diseases, who has more than 15 years of experience in new drug discovery and development, including the drug modalities of nature compounds, biologics, and gene-modified immune cells. Dr. Huang led the team in developing the upstream process (USP) and downstream process (DSP) of chemistry, manufacturing, and controls for biologics, as well as gene-modified immune cells.

speakers.name: Chien-Tsun Kuan
speakers.bio: Chien-Tsun Kuan, president/CEO and co-founder of ARCE Therapeutics, leads development of next-generation cell and gene therapies, including CAR-engineered cells for cancer and immune diseases.
