Formerly deputy executive director at DCB and professor at Duke University, he led R&D in antibodies and gene therapies. An expert in immunology and molecular biology, he holds more than 50 patents, 45-plus papers, and the 2014 AACR Team Science Award.

speakers.name: Yiching Chen
speakers.bio: Yiching Chen received B.E. and M.E.degrees in computer science and information technology from National Taiwan University, Taipei, Taiwan, in 1995 and 1997, respectively. Currently, he is director of RD center at Compal Electronics, Inc., Taipei. His research interests include artificial intelligence and quantum computing.

sessions.title: Create Noninvasive Diagnoses: Adapting Digital-Twin Model for Hemodynamic Simulation
sessions.abstract: We are developing noninvasive diagnostic solutions for CAD to replace risk-bearing, high-cost invasive angiography with safe, efficient, and data-rich alternatives. By combining coronary computed tomography angiography (CCTA), AI-based lumen segmentation, and hemodynamics simulation, clinicians can assess both the anatomical and functional significance of coronary lesions — improving patient safety, healthcare efficiency, and clinical precision.

speakers.name: Shih-Pei Huang
speakers.bio: Shih-Pei Huang is a distinguished physician and healthcare executive with extensive leadership experience across Taiwan’s medical and biotech sectors. He currently serves as board member at EBM Corp. (TPEX: 8409). He previously held the position of deputy CEO at YongLin Healthcare Foundation (Foxconn Group) and chief medical officer at Yourgene Bioscience, where he led the company through a cross-border acquisition by Premaitha Health (U.K.).

speakers.name: Chien-Yuan Huang
speakers.bio: Chien-Yuan Huang is a senior cardiologist specialized in coronary artery disease intervention, with abundant experience in clinical practice and patient care. He also serves as the general secretary at the Taipei Hospital, Ministry of Health and Welfare, Taiwan.

speakers.name: Wen-Yang Chu
speakers.bio: A data scientist with experience at Philips and as co-founder and CTO of Virtonomy.io, Wen-Yang holds a master's degree from the Université catholique de Louvain and has skills in areas like Matlab, LabVIEW, and Simulink. He is also a critical project member for the minimal viable product for the clinical application in Taiwan, co-working with Virphysio Medtech.

speakers.name: Simon Sonntag
speakers.bio: Virtonomy introduces v-Patients, enabling medical device developers to perform development and testing in a virtual environment, thereby accelerating development, reducing risks, expenses, and regulatory burden. The end-to-end digital twin and simulation solution is based on an ever-expanding database of real clinical data to reflect anatomical variability, demographic diversity, and pathological conditions.

speakers.name: Yu-Chung Liao
speakers.bio: Yu-Chung Liao is a computational scientist specializing in patient-specific cardiology simulation with mesh-free Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics (SPH). He advances cardiovascular digital twins centered on hemodynamics and explores fluid–structure interaction to improve clinical relevance.

speakers.name: Ming-Chen Hsiao
speakers.bio: Ming-Chen holds a Ph.D. in nuclear engineering and science from Tsing Hua University in Taiwan, complemented by an executive master’s degree in health sociology and health economics at National Taiwan University. His training includes in-depth experience with Boron Neutron Capture Therapy at NTHU, the Heavy Ion Therapy at the DKFZ, and the healthcare business model at NTU, with a strong command in AI-driven healthcare solutions.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Continuous-Time NODE in Transformers and Physics-Informed Loss as Soft Physical Constraints in Weather Forecasting
sessions.abstract: We develop a novel approach to integrate continuous-time Neural ODE dynamics to transformers and implement physics-informed loss as soft physical constraints to learn the spatio-temporal mapping of weather variables in the embedding space of the vision transformer. As climate volatility grows, this approach offers timely improvements in forecast quality without sacrificing transformer's learning capacity.

speakers.name: Hira Saleem
speakers.bio: Hira is a Ph.D. candidate at UNSW holding the SmartSat CRC scholarship. Her research interests include AI for weather and climate, explainable AI, spatio-temporal forecasting, and computer vision in general.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling

sessions.title: AI-Powered Digital Twin Transforms 7-Eleven Retail Store Design and Training
sessions.abstract: See how interactive 3D digital twins reduce retail store setup time by 70% and display costs by 40%, replacing expensive physical mock-ups with fully virtual environments. This self-improving platform integrates real-time inventory management, AI-driven planogram optimization, and demand forecasting — enabling over 7,200 stores to validate layouts, train employees, and test “what-if” scenarios safely before physical implementation.

speakers.name: Shashika Dharmasena
speakers.bio: Shashika Dharmasena is an AI researcher from Sri Lanka, currently based in Taiwan at President Information Corporation (PIC). He specializes in AI for retail, focusing on conversational AI (automatic speech recognition, text-to-speech, natural language processing), generative AI (large language models), and vision-based robotics. Shashika has led projects like smart search engines and chatbots across Asia.

speakers.name: Areoll Wu
speakers.bio: Areoll Wu is from Taiwan, where PIC (President Information Corp.) is located now. Their main R&D focus is on conversational AI (automatic speech recognition, text-to-speech, and natural language processing), generative AI (large language models), micro cloud services, vision systems, and robotics that handle AI projects in Asia, such as smart call centers, chatbot services, smart speakers, service robots, and object/facial recognition systems.

speakers.name: Tracy Huang
speakers.bio: As the manager of the AI Center, Tracy leads AI projects from development to mass production. She has successfully launched a smart speaker with Chunghwa Telecom/MediaTek and a music robot with Yamaha/Toshiba in the Japanese market. She excels in product planning and cross-departmental coordination to ensure projects meet customer goals on schedule.

speakers.name: James Andrew Presidente
speakers.bio: James Andrew Presidente is an AI engineer with a background in developing conversational AI and digital twin technologies, specializing in 3D simulation and the intersection of AI with virtual environments. James is currently focused on developing intelligent systems using Python and Omniverse tools to create immersive, real-time digital experiences.

sessions.title: BrainRAD: Dementia Diagnosis and Prognosis Prediction With Retrieval-Assisted Vision–Language Framework
sessions.abstract:
Dementia affects over 55 million people worldwide and is expected to double by 2050. Diagnosis and prognosis are challenging due to reliance on expert interpretation of neuroimaging and clinical data. Large vision-language models (VLMs) offer a promising solution by integrating MRI images and textual information to generate clinically meaningful image captions and diagnostic reasoning, but their potential in brain imaging is limited by the scarcity of large-scale, paired MRI-report datasets. While public datasets such as ADNI [2] provide extensive imaging and clinical data for Alzheimer’s disease study, they often lack detailed radiological reports linking visual and linguistic features. 

We develop Brain Retrieval-Assisted Diagnosis (BrainRAD), an AI-driven framework designed to advance precision diagnosis in dementia through integrating quantitative MRI biomarkers and large VLMs.

speakers.name: Geng Zhan
speakers.bio: Geng Zhan is a Ph.D. research associate at the Brain and Mind Centre, University of Sydney. He's also a research engineer at the Sydney Neuroimaging Analysis Centre. His focus is on cutting-edge deep learning application in medical research and clinical practice.

speakers.name: Chenyu Tim Wang
speakers.bio: Chenyu (Tim) Wang is a postdoctoral researcher at the Brain and Mind Centre and the director of operations at the Sydney Neuroimaging Analysis Centre. With a background in electronics and information engineering and neuroimaging, Tim has particular interest in developing novel MRI and electrophysiological biomarkers of myelin and axonal integrity in neurological disorders, particularly for subclinical disease progression.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging

sessions.title: GPU Efficiency Optimization for Multi-Trillion Video Recommendation Models
sessions.abstract: Large-scale recommendation systems, critical for Facebook Video, face significant computational and memory challenges due to inherent scale, imbalanced sparse embeddings, and increased dense computation. We've advanced multifaceted optimizations for GPU resources. Specifically, we implement 60-plus custom forward and backward kernels with 2-100X speedup, including rowwise FP8 for GEMM, custom Flash Attention masks, feature encoder operators, and Tensor memory/compute operation on jagged Tensors. We also explore various techniques to eliminate stream sync points and CPU blocking operations and demonstrate the effectiveness of overlapping communication with compute, distributed inference/caching, and AOTI lowering. These combined optimizations result in >4X QPS gain for both training and inference, significantly lowering down infra cost and enabling model innovations.

speakers.name: Chuanwei Yi
speakers.bio: Chuanwei Yi has been working as a software engineer at Meta in Singapore since May 2025. In his current role, Chuanwei focuses on optimizing recommendation systems, covering both training and inference processes. He is passionate about enhancing recommendation architecture, advancing AI infrastructure, and building robust distributed systems. Chuanwei holds a master’s degree in computer science from Shanghai Jiao Tong University.

speakers.name: Yongxiong Ren
speakers.bio: Yongxiong Ren received a Ph.D. in electrical engineering from the University of Southern California and is currently staff software engineer at Meta Platform, where he leads the AI infrastructure optimization for Facebook video recosys. Previously, he worked as principal engineer at Kuaishou Technology on heterogeneous computing for video recommendation and content understanding. He has more than 160 publications, including two book chapters, 15 U.S. patents, 78 journals, and 83 conference papers.

speakers.name: Kai Ren
speakers.bio: Kai Ren obtained his Ph.D in computer science from Carnegie Mellon University and he is currently a senior engineering manager at Meta. Previously, he worked at Kuaishou Technology and led the entire recommendation infrastructure at Kuaishou with extensive experience and expertise in short-form video infra.

speakers.name: Zheng-Yong (Arsa) Ang
speakers.bio: Arsa

speakers.name: Jing Ma
speakers.bio: Jing Ma is a staff research scientist leading the ML inference optimizations for Meta’s RecSys models. She has rich experience in distributed systems, and particularly in large-scale model inference and model-parallel optimization. She also has vast research experience in distributed optimizations, especially under the scenario of federated learning. Jing received a Ph.D in computer science from Emory University.

sessions.title: LeHome: A Simulation Environment for Deformable Object Manipulation in Household Scenarios
sessions.abstract:
Our goal is to deliver a visually realistic and physically authentic simulation that enables robots to handle daily tasks in ordinary homes (especially deformable objects), and promote large-scale home adoption of robots by bridging the sim2real gap. Challenges include interacting with diverse, non-standard objects and adapting to dynamic environments; expensive and labor-intensive household data collection; the complexity of modeling diverse deformable objects; and the high cost, large size, and expertise of industrial robots.

We contribute diverse high-fidelity assets across four room types via NVIDIA Newton; accurate, fine-grained, and causally interactions with objects through Isaacsim Features; support for multiple robotic embodiments, especially low-cost robots; and efficient and extensive teleoperation methods for data collection with Leader & Follower, Xbox, etc.

speakers.name: Zeyi Li
speakers.bio: Zeyi Li is an AI researcher at Lightwheel. His research interests include high-fidelity simulation platforms and deformable-object manipulation. He is the first author of LeHome, the latest benchmark for deformable simulation using LeRobot.

speakers.name: Steve Xie
speakers.bio: Steve is founder and CEO of Lightwheel, a company leading simulation infrastructure for embodied AI and a pioneer in generative AI-powered simulation for robotics. He holds a B.S. from Peking University and a Ph.D. from Columbia University. Steve has led simulation efforts at NVIDIA and Cruise, where he built end-to-end synthetic data pipelines that set industry benchmarks for realism, scalability, and sim-to-real transfer.

speakers.name: Ruihai Wu
speakers.bio: Ruihai Wu obtained his Ph.D. at Peking University and will be a postdoc scholar at UC Berkeley. His research focuses on deformable object simulation and manipulation. As the main author of GarmentLab, DexGarmentLab and UniGarmentManip, he is familiar with the simulation technique of deformable objects in Isaac Sim/Lab, and manipulation. He is among the main organizers of the Representing and Manipulating Deformable Objects (RMDO) workshop.

sessions.title: Fast and Accurate Virtual Drug Screening Powered by GPU-Annealer and CUDA-Q
sessions.abstract: This work presents a fast and accurate GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening pipeline. In the first stage, a GPU-annealer was built to simulate a quantum annealer and used for molecular docking, achieving faster performance with competitive AUC and EF1. In the second stage, CUDA-Q VQE refined binding energies for reliable ranking and better EF1. During the entire pipeline, GPU delivers up to four orders of magnitude speedup over CPU execution, accelerating early drug discovery.

speakers.name: Yen-Chu Lin
speakers.bio: Former CEO of Insilico Medicine Taiwan, expert in AI-driven and computer-aided drug design. Proven track record in leading new drug development from 0 to PCC and pioneering AI-Quantum integration for molecular discovery. Opinion leader in Asia’s AIDD field and adjunct assistant professor at NYCU. PhD from ETH Zurich, with postdoctoral research at Harvard and A*STAR.

speakers.name: Kuo-Wei Huang
speakers.bio: Kuo-Wei Huang holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from the University of California, Los Angeles, and an M.S. in computer science from Georgia Tech. With over a decade of industry experience, he drives interdisciplinary innovation across nanotechnology, MEMS, biotechnology, and AI — bridging advanced computing and life science applications.

speakers.name: Yiching Chen
speakers.bio: Yiching Chen received B.E. and M.E.degrees in computer science and information technology from National Taiwan University, Taipei, Taiwan, in 1995 and 1997, respectively. Currently, he is director of RD center at Compal Electronics, Inc., Taipei. His research interests include artificial intelligence and quantum computing.

speakers.name: Yu-Hsuan Tung
speakers.bio: Tung is a researcher in quantum computing, focusing on the application of quantum algorithms to chemical and pharmaceutical problems. She has been dedicated to exploring how quantum computers can accelerate molecular simulations and drug discovery. Tung is currently affiliated with the Center for Quantum Science and Engineering at National Taiwan University, where she also contributes to quantum computing education and public engagement.

speakers.name: Jian-bin Lee
speakers.bio: Jian-bin has over 10 years of industrial experience as a senior computational chemist in drug design. Skilled in integrating molecular modeling with AI and GPU acceleration to enhance virtual screening and protein–ligand interaction analysis, he has strong communication and teamwork abilities across interdisciplinary research environments. Jian-bin holds a Ph.D. in computational chemistry from NCU, specializing in molecular dynamics simulations and force field construction.

speakers.name: Yi Chou
speakers.bio: Yi Chou is a research engineer at Compal Electronics, Inc., specializing in GPU-accelerated annealing and its applications. She holds degrees in atmospheric science and physics from National Taiwan University, and in computer science (M.S., Ph.D.) from the University of Colorado Boulder. She has published research in quantum control, ultrafast laser science, verification, and Bayesian inference.

sessions.title: BenchOverflow: Measuring Overflow in Large Language Models via Plain-Text Prompts
sessions.abstract: We propose BenchOverflow, a new benchmark that exposes how large language models silently waste GPU time, energy, and cost by generating unnecessarily long outputs during normal use. We show how to measure, compare, and mitigate overflow, helping you build faster, more sustainable, and compute-efficient AI systems.

speakers.name: Raz Lapid
speakers.bio: Now a chief scientist at DeepKeep, Raz earned his Ph.D. in computer science from Ben-Gurion University, Israel.

speakers.name: Erin Feiglin
speakers.bio: Erin's a bioinformatics student at the Hebrew University in Jerusalem and an ML researcher at DeepKeep.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Disaggregated Inference Experiments With NVIDIA Dynamo for Telecoms Reasoning, AI Agent Use Cases
sessions.abstract:
We explore how disaggregated inference optimizes GPU resource utilization in real-world reasoning and AI agent use cases within telco environments. We also analyze the workload characteristics of Wikipedia-based AI agents, which increase prefill inputs. To handle such large inputs, we experimented with the NVIDIA Dynamo framework on NVIDIA H100 GPUs in a disaggregated setup. Our results show an approximate 16% overhead in time-to-first-token and confirm superior stability under high loads, compared to aggregated inference.

From a telecom perspective, the disaggregated inference could represent a significant breakthrough to enable flexible, on-demand GPU allocation across multi–data center architectures and supports scalable AI agent deployment. Future work will focus on developing cost models for geo-distributed GPU resources to ensure efficient resource planning for our customers.

speakers.name: Kota Tsuyuzaki
speakers.bio: 2010年 日本電信電話株式会社に入社、分散オブジェクトストレージOpenStack Swiftの研究開発に従事し、コアデベロッパーとしてOSS開発を主導。2018年より、AI計算基盤の開発プロジェクトに参画し、構築、運用をリード。2023年NTTドコモビジネス株式会社(旧NTTコミュニケーションズ株式会社)に入社し、ハイブリッドクラウド、データマネジメントプロジェクト、及び、LLM をはじめとした生成 AI 関連の技術検証に携わる。

speakers.name: Sotetsu Suzugamine
speakers.bio:
2020年にNTTコミュニケーションズ株式会社に入社し、機械学習システムの研究開発に従事。パブリッククラウドおよびハイブリッドクラウド環境でのML実装、映像解析技術を活用したAIエッジソリューションの開発、IOWNによるGPUクラスタ上での大規模言語モデルの構築検証などに取り組む。これらの実績を通じ、NTTにおける機械学習システムの実用化推進に貢献。

Since joining NTT Communications in 2020, Sotetsu Suzugamine has been involved in research and development of machine learning systems. His work encompasses ML implementations in public and hybrid cloud environments, AI edge solutions using video analytics technology, and large language models on GPU clusters. He contributes to NTT's efforts in advancing practical ML systems.

sessions.title: Interactive 2D–3D Visualization Interface for Digital Pathology
sessions.abstract: Explore an interactive GPU-accelerated interface that links 2D level-by-level images with 3D component renderings, making it easier for researchers and clinicians to navigate complex 3D spatial structures in dense tissue datasets, such as digital pathology.

speakers.name: Renao Yan
speakers.bio: Renao Yan is a Ph.D. student in mechanical engineering at the University of Washington and a visiting student at Stanford University, advised by Prof. Jonathan T.C. Liu. His research focuses on computational pathology and deep learning for 3D medical imaging, particularly open-top light-sheet microscopy. He received his M.S. from Tsinghua University and B.E. from Hunan University.

speakers.name: Huai-Ching Hsieh
speakers.bio: Huai-Ching Hsieh is a Ph.D. student in bioengineering at Stanford University, with research interests at the intersection of bioimaging, computation, and cancer biology. With a dual background in life sciences and electrical engineering, her work focuses on developing hardware (3D microdissection) and software tools (3D visualization and simulation) to leverage 3D morphology for spatial biology, particularly in studying tumor phylogeny.

speakers.name: Gan Gao
speakers.bio: Gan Gao is a final-year Ph.D. candidate in mechanical engineering at the University of Washington. His research focuses on computational vision, image processing, and optical imaging for biomedical applications. He develops multi-GPU, high-performance, weakly and self-supervised learning models to assist in evaluating pathology images, and designs CUDA-accelerated algorithms for light-sheet microscopy to enable rapid surgical specimen assessment.

speakers.name: Yang-Hsien Lin
speakers.bio: Yang-Hsien Lin is currently with the NVIDIA AI Technology Center, where his research focuses on healthcare and agentic AI to accelerate innovation in medical applications. His recent work explores the integration of advanced artificial intelligence with biomedical technologies to translate research into practical healthcare solutions.

speakers.name: Jonathan Liu
speakers.bio: Jonathan Liu is a professor of pathology and bioengineering at Stanford University, where his lab develops optical-imaging devices and AI-driven computational pipelines to guide treatment decisions. He received his B.S.E. from Princeton University and his M.S. and Ph.D. in mechanical engineering at Stanford. Jonathan is a co-founder and board member of Alpenglow Biosciences, which commercialized the non-destructive 3D pathology technologies from his lab.

sessions.topic: Content Creation / Rendering - Rendering Engines / Pipelines / Tools

sessions.title: Agentic AI for Medical Discharge: Combining Machine Learning with Language Models for ICD Code Prediction- Department of Computer Engineering, Faculty of Engineering, Mahidol University
sessions.abstract: We show a new AI system that helps hospitals code medical records automatically. Our system combines machine learning with language models to make coding more accurate and save doctors' time.

speakers.name: Lalita Narupiyakul
speakers.bio: Lalita Narupiyakul got her bachelor’s and master’s degrees in electrical engineering from King Mongkut's Institute of Technology Ladkrabang, Thailand. She also got her Ph.D. from the University of Missouri in 2005. She is now an associate professor at Mahidol University. Her research interests are computer networks, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and healthcare informatics.

speakers.name: Kitti Akkhawatthanakun
speakers.bio: Kitti Akkawatthanakul received his Doctor of Pharmacy degree from Silpakorn University, Thailand, in 2019, where he studied health informatics. He is currently pursuing a master’s degree in computer engineering at Mahidol University. His interests include data science, software engineering, and cybersecurity.

speakers.name: Paisarn Muneesawang
speakers.bio: Paisarn Muneesawang got his bachelor’s degree from Mahanakorn University of Technology, Thailand, in 1996. He got his master’s degree from the University of New South Wales, Australia, and Ph.D. from the University of Sydney in 2004. He is now a professor at Mahidol University. His research interests are AI, machine learning, and computer vision.

speakers.name: Konlakorn Wongpatikaseree
speakers.bio: Konlakorn Wongpatikaseree got his bachelor’s degree in computer science and master’s degree in information technology from Thammasat University, Thailand. He got his Ph.D. from Japan Advanced Institute of Science and Technology in 2013. He is now an assistant professor and head of the Computer Engineering Department at Mahidol University. He studies smart systems, human behavior, and smart home technology.

speakers.name: Narit Hnoohom
speakers.bio: Narit is the assistant to the president for information technology and digital transformation at Mahidol University.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)

sessions.title: GreenMind: A LLM for Complex Reasoning — Chain-of-Thought Data and Self-Correction Cycles on NVIDIA NEMO
sessions.abstract: We introduce GreenMind, a large language model designed for complex reasoning through chain-of-thought data and self-correction cycles. By leveraging the GRPO strategy from NVIDIA NEMO for fine-tuning, we guide the model to generate logically coherent responses, deliver informative answers, and offer intuitive explanations. These features provide value not only to end users, but also to ongoing research aimed at improving data quality and sampling techniques.

speakers.name: vo thu
speakers.bio: Vo Trong Thu is the head of AI Lab at GreenNode, driving user-centric AI for Vietnam’s top tech firms. With over 10 years in AI, data, system intelligence, and architecture, he leads projects that boost automation and business value. His legal background ensures AI development stays ethical and complies with regulations.

speakers.name: Bao Loc Pham
speakers.bio: Loc Pham, an AI engineer at GreenNode, creates platforms that help developers, researchers, and companies advance their experiments and streamline production processes.

speakers.name: Viet Hoang
speakers.bio: As lead AI engineer at GreenNode, Viet leads the development of cutting-edge platforms that drive innovation, enabling developers, researchers, and companies to advance their experiments and streamline production processes.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: AI Solves GPS Outages: Making Vehicle Navigation Robust
sessions.abstract: Kalman filters (KF) are the most successful tracking algorithm for vehicle navigation. Its accuracy drops when the process noise covariance matrix Q is fixed. Conventional approaches for updating the Qmatrix have stability issues due to aggressive updates. We propose a reinforcement learning (RL)-guided adaptive Q-update for robust vehicle navigation. The proposed RL-guided KF achieves stable state estimation under GPS loss and tough terrain.

speakers.name: KUANG HAO LIU
speakers.bio: Kuang-Hao Liu is a professor and the director of the Institute of Communications Engineering at National Tsing Hua University, Taiwan. His recent research focuses on wireless energy harvesting, millimeter-wave communications, and intelligent radio access technologies.

sessions.title: Glider: A Unified Platform Architecture for Resource Placement and Coordinated Scheduling of Dynamic AI Workloads
sessions.abstract: Dive deep into new solutions for the complex challenge of scheduling dynamic AI workloads. We present Glider, a unified platform that improves resource utilization by using workload-centric orchestration, fragmentation-aware placement, and native gang scheduling to efficiently coordinate parallel jobs.

speakers.name: PERMPOON BOONYARIT
speakers.bio: Permpoon is a system engineer and Glider project lead at CMKL University.

speakers.name: Akkarit Sangpetch
speakers.bio: Akkarit Sangpetch works as the director of AI and Computer Engineering Program at CMKL University. He is also the director of AI Engineering Institute, which oversees AI research and education programs for leading universities in Thailand. With expertise in cloud computing and AI infrastructure, he researches and develops scalable AI platforms with local cloud service providers for various applications from education to smart cities.

sessions.title: MANGO : Thai Multi-Modal Adaptive Neural Generative Orchestrator
sessions.abstract: We explore new techniques for fine-tuning open-source multi-modal models like Qwen, LLaMA, and Gemma to understand Thai language and culture. By combining curated Thai data from the internet, university courses, and volunteer contributions, we aim to improve model performance and enable AI applications for education, travel, call-center services, and research
sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation

sessions.title: Interactive Digital Twin of the Left Atrium Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
sessions.abstract: We created an interactive digital twin of the heart's left atrium that allows researchers to simulate and study atrial arrhythmias, and practice treating them through virtual ablation. This gives physicians the ability to test ablation techniques without performing these invasive procedures on live patients.

speakers.name: Philip Alcorn
speakers.bio: Phil Alcorn is an electrical engineering student at Tarleton State University. He is interested in computer architecture and intends to pursue graduate studies upon graduation, with the intent to specifically focus on parallel systems such as GPUs and accelerators. He is also the chair of Tarleton’s IEEE branch and spends his time working with their robotics competition team.

speakers.name: Mason Bane
speakers.bio: Mason Bane is pursuing a B.S. in computer science with a concentration in computer engineering and a minor in mathematics at Tarleton State University. With a passion for solving complex problems, he is planning to pursue an advanced degree in computer science. Mason enjoys exploring new ideas, constantly learning, and applying his knowledge in diverse areas both academically and creatively.

speakers.name: Kyla Moore
speakers.bio: Kyla Moore is a student researcher at Tarleton State University currently pursuing a degree in mathematics with a concentration in data analysis. In addition to her research work, she is an active member of the Math Club and the Presidential Honors Program, reflecting her commitment to academic excellence. With plans to pursue a master’s degree in mathematics, she is focused on applying mathematical concepts to address real-world challenges.

speakers.name: Bryant Wyatt
speakers.bio: Bryant Wyatt founded Tarleton State University's HPC lab in 2011 with an NVIDIA grant, turning it into a collaboration hub. He and his students utilized NVIDIA GPUs, presenting at hundreds of conferences and winning awards for their innovative work in fields like astrophysics, engineering, biology, chemistry, and mathematics. In 2018, Bryant was named Texas' distinguished university teacher by the Mathematical Association of America.

speakers.name: Leah Rogers
speakers.bio: Leah Rogers is a student researcher at Tarleton State University and serves as the president of the Math Club. Majoring in physics with a concentration in mathematics, she’s passionate about computational modeling and applying advanced math to solve intricate problems. With plans for graduate school, Leah aims to further her expertise by combining mathematics, physics, and computing in her research.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Accelerate Minimal Residual Disease Detection: Real-World Performance and Clinical Impact
sessions.abstract: We implemented an minimal residual disease (MRD) detection pipeline optimized with NVIDIA Parabricks to accelerate computation and enable faster, scalable molecular analysis without compromising accuracy.

speakers.name: Zhuosheng Gu
speakers.bio: Zhuosheng is a transformational bioinformatics leader with 10-plus years of experience in pharmacogenomics, cancer diagnostics, and digital health. He has extensive experience in assay development, pipeline build, panel design, and assay submission under CLIA, CAP, NYSDOH, and FDA regulations.

sessions.title: GPU-Accelerated Multi-Agent Large Language Models  for Enhanced Recovery After Surgery (ERAS) Protocols
sessions.abstract:
ERAS protocols need real-time, personalized support; CPU rule engines have high latency and low throughput. We quantify performance and clinical utility of GPU-accelerated LLM inference for ERAS decision support by building a multi-agent LLM system on a single RTX 5090 with hybrid RAG, benchmarked against a CPU rules baseline across three ERAS use cases: PONV prevention, POD checklists, and chest-tube removal. Evaluations included microbenchmarks, concurrency stress tests, and task-level accuracy.

Results: Mean latency fell 2.9× (1900→650 ms); P95 fell 2.4× (2050→850 ms); max throughput rose 7× (0.5→3.5 QPS). Under 16-patient concurrency, P95 remained ~2.1 s with GPU utilization 60–80% and VRAM 16–18 GB. Task accuracy reached 95%.

A single RTX 5090 enables bedside, sub-second responses and supports 8–16 concurrent patients, offering a practical solution with a favorable cost profile.

speakers.name: Hsiang Wei Hu
speakers.bio: Hsiang Wei is the generative AI senior technical software program manager.

speakers.name: Hsing-hsien Wu
speakers.bio: Hsing-hsien is the chief of thoracic surgery at Tainan Municipal Hospital.

speakers.name: Joyce Chen
speakers.bio: Joyce is experienced in medical devices, biotechnology, regulatory management, and product development, having secured multiple government grants, including sci-tech and AI pilot programs. She holds a master's degree in life sciences and bachelor's degrees in chemistry and information technology. She's also active in the Taiwan AI Academy and AI Association, promoting generative AI services.

speakers.name: Dawn Chen
speakers.bio: An analytical, certified, self-motivated IT Professional (BI Architect & Data Engineer) with technical experience in software database design and development which covers Microsoft Power Platform, BI, AI, Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Mi-SQL, Azure SQL, ADF, COSMOS, DM, ETL/ELT, EDW, Big Data, MPP, OLAP, OLTP, IoT system in the industry or business domain areas of start-up companies dream, enterprise wide applications, legacy system migration, retail POS system, power plant energy system industry, call center, payment gateway industry, express ordering system, merchandizing, telecom, operational log, financial accounting, sales marketing, and HR. Performance tuning, production support, and trouble shooting.

sessions.title: Hybrid Inference With SLMs on Devices and NVIDIA NIM Microservices in the Cloud
sessions.abstract: We explore how small language models (SLMs) running on small devices integrate with NVIDIA NIM microservices running in the cloud or local GPU environments to enable real-time, hybrid AI inference. Our hybrid local–cloud inference approach combines on-device processing for instant interaction with GPU-accelerated reasoning in the cloud. This solution achieves latency under 200 ms and is expected to significantly reduce bandwidth usage — potentially by up to 10× — while scaling across industries from consumer electronics to healthcare and retail. Built with NVIDIA technologies including NeMo, TensorRT, and Triton, we demonstrate how hybrid inference brings AI closer to the user, powering natural, multi-modal experiences directly on devices.

speakers.name: Dmytro Dudchenko
speakers.bio: Dmytro Dudchenko leads R&D initiatives at SoftServe’s Hardware Lab, focusing on bridging hardware engineering and applied AI. His work explores hybrid inference, edge intelligence, and AI-enabled devices — from embedded systems to augmented reality and retail automation. With a background in product strategy and innovation, Dmytro drives collaborations with partners like NVIDIA, Qualcomm, and AWS to advance next-gen “physical AI” solutions.

speakers.name: Marian Petruk
speakers.bio: Marian Petruk is an R&D lead specializing in efficient and generative AI, deep learning, and computer vision. His research spans diffusion models, Gaussian splatting, neural architecture search for low-latency vision, and panoptic segmentation using satellite imagery. He’s also explored generative adversarial networks for high-res synthesis and applied ML to digital signal processing and biosignal analysis in HCI research.

sessions.title: In-Storage ETL: Fast In-Place Dataset Transformation for Machine Learning
sessions.abstract:
Most extract, transform, load (ETL) workflows pull data out of object storage into client machines or servers for preprocessing, augmentation, or custom transformations. This lacks scalability, and unnecessary data movement often degrades performance significantly. Unlike other ETL tools, in-storage ETL performs transformations on the same machines where your data lives, minimizing redundant transfers by exploiting data locality.

We introduce an in-storage ETL system that runs I/O-intensive transformations close to the data. It supports inline transformations for real-time training, and offline batch processing where results are stored in a destination bucket. We’ll explore its architecture and benefits, highlighting linear scalability and high performance. Learn how to accelerate ETL pipelines, streamline workflows, and scale seamlessly from a real-world example of data preprocessing.

speakers.name: Abhishek Gaikwad
speakers.bio: Abhishek Gaikwad is a senior software engineer at NVIDIA, holding an M.S. in computer science from San José State University. As a core contributor and maintainer of AIStore, he has driven its architecture and implementation. Abhishek also manages large-scale AIStore deployments at NVIDIA, oversees its full life cycle, authors deployment automation, maintains the Python SDK, and continuously tunes performance for maximum efficiency.

speakers.name: Tony Chen
speakers.bio: Tony Chen is a software engineer at NVIDIA and a core contributor to the AIStore project. He focuses on the ETL (extract, transform, load) subsystem, which leverages Kubernetes to enable high-performance, scalable dataset transformation. His contributions include designing, developing, and maintaining the AIS-ETL subsystem from the ground up, integrating the Python SDK client, and supporting production deployment and maintenance across clusters.

sessions.title: Agentic UI in XR Solutions With NVIDIA and Apple Vision Pro
sessions.abstract:
We’re at a pivotal moment to reimagine how we interact in extended reality (XR), shifting from complex, traditional interfaces to intuitive, conversational experiences powered by voice and AI. This evolution lets us focus on creativity and exploration, not learning mechanics. 

The Agentic UI architecture reshapes the traditional interaction model by introducing intelligent, conversational interfaces accelerated by NVIDIA's AI and rendering stack using the following design pillars:  

•	Natural Language Interface: Replace traditional user interface (UI) controls with conversational AI 
•	Context Awareness: Maintain rich understanding of user intent and application state 
•	Proactive Assistance: Anticipate user needs and provide intelligent guidance

speakers.name: Taras Kovalchuk
speakers.bio: Taras Kovalchuk is head of the extended reality group at SoftServe. Taras has a background in game development and has over 15 years of experience working in 3D graphics, including XR.

speakers.name: Roman Abramovych
speakers.bio: Roman Abramovych is an XR solution architect at SoftServe with over 20 years of expertise in software development, including more than a decade specializing in extended reality technologies. He holds a Ph.D. in computer science and is a co-author of more than 15 scientific papers. His work bridges academic research and industry innovation in immersive technology.

sessions.title: Agentic Engineering Development: Planning, Coding, Deployment
sessions.abstract:
Our Prometheus framework for agentic engineering coordinates planning, coding, and deployment agents to automate software development. Explore the unification of human oversight and AI agents, in which we offer a new approach to the software development life cycle. Discover the benefits of our framework and the lessons we learned while working on it. 

The system consists of a multi-pronged approach. Planning involves gathering project requirements, business insights, and creating epics and tasks. We combine this with coding, frontend, backend and testing efforts, deployment, and verification.

speakers.name: Nazarii Drushchak
speakers.bio: Nazarii Drushchak is a senior data scientist at SoftServe, where he focuses on natural language processing, generative AI, and agentic AI. He is also a Ph.D. candidate and lecturer at the Ukrainian Catholic University, teaching courses on generative and responsible AI. His research has been published at conferences/journals such as Nature Communication, ACM FAccT, ICSE (RAIE Workshop) and UNLP.

speakers.name: Maryna Bautina
speakers.bio: Maryna is a lead data scientist at SoftServe with over seven years of experience and a master’s degree in software engineering, specializing in predictive analytics and Gen AI. She has extensive experience leading internal research initiatives and managing data science projects across a variety of industries, transforming complex data into actionable insights that fuel innovation and drive business growth.

speakers.name: Taras Hnot
speakers.bio: Taras, principal AI consultancy lead at SoftServe, brings 11-plus years of expertise in data science, ML, and AI. He’s led AI solution design for top clients across retail, manufacturing, and energy, driving innovation and core service development. Now, he heads GenAI Lab, advancing Gen AI solutions and IP while shaping SoftServe’s AI consultancy direction.

speakers.name: Nataliya Polyakovska
speakers.bio: Nataliya bridges technical excellence through strategic partnerships and her expertise in AI/ML and generative AI, with a successful track record leading complex data science projects across AI/ML life cycles. She's a public speaker on AI topics, presenting at industry events and teaching machine learning courses as a university lecturer. Nataliya has her master’s in mathematics and computer science from V. N. Karazin Kharkiv National University.

speakers.name: Dmytro Zikrach
speakers.bio: Dmytro is AI competence manager and Gen AI technical leader at SoftServe with over 10 years in AI product development. He blends deep math knowledge with state-of-the-art data science techniques, specializing in predictive analytics, digital signal processing, natural language processing, Gen AI, and time series. He holds a Ph.D. in mathematics, has 20-plus publications, and actively participates in conferences, competitions, and hackathons.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: A Collaborative Multi-Agent AI System for Scalable High School Transcript Processing
sessions.abstract: College admissions offices face a major challenge: processing millions of high school transcripts with varying formats, grading systems, and layouts. In this paper, we present a multi-agent AI system that automates this process through intelligent coordination among specialized agents. The architecture includes a Pattern Recognition Agent, Semantic Analysis Agent, Visual Intelligence Agent, and an Orchestration Agent that manages communication and result integration. Using GPA extraction as a coordination signal, the system ensures reliable collaboration and prevents information loss. Tested on 40 real-world transcripts from 13 U.S. states, it achieved 96.7% accuracy versus expert review, processing each document in 45 seconds. This demonstrates a scalable, collaborative AI solution that preserves human-level accuracy while greatly reducing processing time.

speakers.name: Jun Zhou
speakers.bio: Jun Zhou is a research scientist at the University of South Carolina, specializing in computer vision, deep learning, machine learning, and data science. With over 20 years of academic and industry experience, her interdisciplinary work spans public health informatics, automated document processing, and AI for libraries and digital collections, advancing intelligent, data-driven systems for real-world impact.

speakers.name: Behzad Torkian
speakers.bio: Behzad Torkian is an expert in AI, large language models, high performance computing, and scalable architectures. He holds multiple patents and has developed advanced platforms bridging research and real-world AI applications. Collaborating with NVIDIA on generative and agentic AI certifications, he also leads AI strategy across startups, turning innovation into impactful, scalable solutions.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: A Multi-Task Deep Learning Framework for Reliable Estimated Time of Arrival Prediction
sessions.abstract: Explore how we leverage deep learning to build a scalable, multi-task estimated time of arrival (ETA) prediction system that improves delivery time accuracy and reliability across millions of DoorDash orders.

speakers.name: Jaydeep Thik
speakers.bio: Jaydeep Thik is a machine learning engineer in the Logistics Team at DoorDash, working on modeling consumer-facing ETAs for marketplace and restaurants.

speakers.name: Jason Kim
speakers.bio: Jason Kim is a machine learning engineer on DoorDash’s Logistics team. He develops and productionizes deep learning models in order fulfillment, including ETA prediction and related applications.

speakers.name: Ziqi Jiang
speakers.bio: Ziqi Jiang is a machine learning engineer on the Dasher Logistics team at DoorDash. She concentrates on enhancing models to capture defect risk in the delivery process.

speakers.name: Pierre Counathe
speakers.bio: Pierre Counathe is a machine learning engineer on the ETA ML team at DoorDash. His focus is on applying state-of-the-art machine learning to optimize large-scale decision-making under uncertainty.

speakers.name: Gayatri Iyengar
speakers.bio: Gayatri Iyengar is the head of engineering and applied sciences for logistics and dasher growth at DoorDash, overseeing everything from dasher onboarding to core algorithms, apps, maps, and foundational platforms.

sessions.title: Cut Engineering Time With a Digital Twin Framework: Case Study on Vision Inspection and Depalletizing
sessions.abstract: Since industrial robots in real factories perform a variety of tasks — including welding, inspection, and pick-and-place — the recently developed digital twin framework should be applicable to all tasks for practical on-site deployment. In this study, we collaborated with Hyundai Motors to verify the scalability of the digital twin framework by applying it not only to the welding shop line, but also to vision inspection and depalletizing tasks. The motion planning results for each task were deployed onsite, and it was verified that the engineering time can be reduced by over 88% without compromising productivity. These results confirm that the proposed framework can quickly adapt to diverse robotic workflows, improving performance and scalability across real production lines.

speakers.name: Banseok Kim
speakers.bio: Kim Banseok is a senior manager at Hyundai Motor Co.’s Electrification Manufacturing Technology Center, where he leads initiatives focused on smart factory transformation for powertrain and electrification plants, as well as the strategic adoption of digital twin technologies. He joined Hyundai in 2014 as an engine production technology engineer, beginning his career by managing engine machining lines. He later spent six years overseeing manufacturing execution systems and quality systems across Hyundai and Kia manufacturing sites worldwide, contributing significantly to the advancement of manufacturing efficiency and quality performance.

speakers.name: Nick Hong
speakers.bio: Nick Hong is the founder and CEO of ROAI, a company pursuing fully autonomous manufacturing through AI and robotics technologies. Before founding ROAI, he spent over 10 years at Hyundai Motor Co. as a manufacturing technology engineer, leading the design and implementation of robot-driven production systems. Now, he is focused on applying intelligent robotic technologies to real manufacturing environments to overcome labor dependency.

speakers.name: Junbyoung Park
speakers.bio: Junbyoung is a research engineer at ROAI, specializing in robot simulation.

speakers.name: Changhoon Lee
speakers.bio: Lee Changhoon is an employee of Hyundai Motor's Advanced Electrification Manufacturing Engineering Team 2, which specializes in process 3D simulation and virtual commissioning. In his recent project, he worked on a project to build an electrified digital twin factory using NVIDIA Omniverse and process simulation.

speakers.name: Yusung Kim
speakers.bio: Yusung Kim is the chief research officer at ROAI, where he leads R&D in robotics and AI research for building smart factories. His team is realizing the vision of a “One-Click Factory” by integrating factory design (bill of process, bill of materials) with advanced motion planning and cycle-time optimization in a single platform. Prior to ROAI, he earned a B.S. in mechanical engineering from POSTECH and an M.S. in mechanical engineering from KAIST.

speakers.name: Hyeonsu Oh
speakers.bio: Hyeonsu is a robotics engineer at ROAI Robotics Lab, focusing on industrial robot process optimization and intelligent automation. He works on applying AI-based motion planning and NVIDIA Isaac Sim to improve efficiency and reliability in factory environments. Hyeonsu completed his M.S. in information and communication engineering at Sejong University, where he researched digital-twin simulation and visual servo control.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: GPU-Accelerated Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials With cuEquivariance and MILAP
sessions.abstract: Explore new techniques to extend the spatial and temporal limits of machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIAP). Leverage MLIAP for distributed inference in molecular dynamics and cuEquivariance for accelerated performance, achieving 26× faster training and 12× faster inference.

speakers.name: Hyuntae Cho
speakers.bio: Hyuntae Cho is a staff researcher at Samsung, exploring efficient and scalable machine learning approaches for molecular simulations.

speakers.name: Geonu Kim
speakers.bio: Staff Researcher at Samsung AI Center specializing in scalable ML interatomic potentials for atomistic and multiscale materials modeling.

speakers.name: Yongdeok Kim
speakers.bio:
Yongdeok Kim leads the Materials AI part at Samsung Electronics’ AI Center, working on machine learning for atomistic simulations, including Machine Learning Force Fields.
Prior to this role, he led large-scale AI training initiatives with MLPerf benchmark results demonstrating leading performance,
as well as deep learning projects in automatic defect classification for TSV processes and a mobile GPU-based inference library deployed on Galaxy devices.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science

sessions.title: Deploying 8B Models on Edge GPUs: 1.58-Bit Quantized LLM on CUDA Environment
sessions.abstract: Explore our edge-optimized quantization approach that enables full 8B-scale reasoning on Jetson Orin NX. By combining 1.58-bit and INT4 quantization with custom CUDA kernels and fused attention/Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) layers, we achieve 8B inference in just 2 GB VRAM — bringing large-scale generative AI fully offline.

speakers.name: jaeyoon yoo
speakers.bio: Jaeyoon Yoo is the chief technology officer at ENERZAi, leading research and development in efficient deep learning and edge AI deployment. His expertise spans large-scale model compression, ML compiler optimization, and sustainable AI infrastructure. With a strong background in both research and engineering leadership, he drives teams building high-performance inference systems for constrained hardware.

speakers.name: Seongju Lee
speakers.bio: Seongju Lee is a machine learning engineer specializing in efficient deep learning and model optimization for real-world deployment. His work bridges algorithm design and systems engineering, focusing on quantization, CUDA acceleration, and on-device inference. With experience spanning academic research and applied AI projects, he explores ways to make large-scale neural networks more lightweight and accessible across edge platforms.

speakers.name: changbeom kang
speakers.bio: Changbeom Kang is head of AI research at ENERZAi, leading efforts in efficient deep learning and high-performance model deployment. His work focuses on low-bit quantization, CUDA optimization, and scalable inference systems for real-time applications.

speakers.name: Jinwoo Lee
speakers.bio: Jinwoo Lee is a machine learning engineer with five years of experience bridging research and real-world AI deployment. He began his career in the gaming industry, developing large-scale data-driven systems to improve player experience, and later co-founded SpriteAI, an AI animation software-as-a-service for creative production. Currently, he focuses on efficient deployment of large models on edge GPUs, exploring low-bit quantization and CUDA-level optimization.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: AI-Powered Digital Twin for Rapid Verification in Robotic Manufacturing: Accelerate Motion Planning With Isaac Sim
sessions.abstract: We present an AI-driven offline programming (OLP) and digital-twin framework for factory-level automation in automotive manufacturing. By integrating GPU-accelerated motion generation with NVIDIA Isaac Sim for simulation-based validation, we demonstrate how manufacturers can rapidly verify safety, cycle time, and precision before physical deployment, accelerating the digital transformation of production lines.

speakers.name: jaekyoun park
speakers.bio: Jaekyoun Park currently leads digital twin development at Kia. He has extensive experience in new vehicle development projects and now focuses on smart manufacturing innovations. Using NVIDIA Omniverse Isaac Sim, Jaekyoun is advancing technologies for industrial robot and fixture control, integrated simulations, and real-time bidirectional communication to enhance production efficiency and flexibility.

speakers.name: Nick Hong
speakers.bio: Nick Hong is the founder and CEO of ROAI, a company pursuing fully autonomous manufacturing through AI and robotics technologies. Before founding ROAI, he spent over 10 years at Hyundai Motor Co. as a manufacturing technology engineer, leading the design and implementation of robot-driven production systems. Now, he is focused on applying intelligent robotic technologies to real manufacturing environments to overcome labor dependency.

speakers.name: Junbyoung Park
speakers.bio: Junbyoung is a research engineer at ROAI, specializing in robot simulation.

speakers.name: Hyungi Kim
speakers.bio: Hyungi Kim, currently working in Kia’s Body Engineering Team, has experience in new model launch projects for body plants and now focuses on digital twin development. His work includes developing AI path planning technologies using Robot Control System with ROAI, as well as creating advanced digital twin interfaces. He's committed to integrating robotics and simulation to drive smart manufacturing and enhance production efficiency.

speakers.name: Yusung Kim
speakers.bio: Yusung Kim is the chief research officer at ROAI, where he leads R&D in robotics and AI research for building smart factories. His team is realizing the vision of a “One-Click Factory” by integrating factory design (bill of process, bill of materials) with advanced motion planning and cycle-time optimization in a single platform. Prior to ROAI, he earned a B.S. in mechanical engineering from POSTECH and an M.S. in mechanical engineering from KAIST.

speakers.name: Hyeonsu Oh
speakers.bio: Hyeonsu is a robotics engineer at ROAI Robotics Lab, focusing on industrial robot process optimization and intelligent automation. He works on applying AI-based motion planning and NVIDIA Isaac Sim to improve efficiency and reliability in factory environments. Hyeonsu completed his M.S. in information and communication engineering at Sejong University, where he researched digital-twin simulation and visual servo control.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Glasses-Free 3D Smart Educational Training and Decision Support Platform for Radiation Oncology Treatment
sessions.abstract: We present a light field-based 3D planning platform that enhances visualization, precision, and workflow efficiency in radiation therapy planning. The system integrates advanced eye-tracking, light-field rendering, and GPU-accelerated computation to provide real-time, glasses-free 3D visualization of anatomical structures and radiation dose distributions. By performing automated image segmentation and continuous ray-tracing calculations, the platform enables clinicians to instantly identify underdosed or overdosed regions and make rapid adjustments. Clinical trials demonstrate significant improvements in both accuracy and efficiency, reducing the experience-based planning gap from 67 minutes to 16 minutes. Integrating powerful GPU computing not only ensures real-time performance, but also establishes a new standard for intelligent, interactive, and precise radiation therapy planning.

speakers.name: Dai-Wei Liu
speakers.bio: Dai-Wei is a radiation oncologist and Ph.D. in molecular virology, currently serving as director of radiation oncology at Hualien Tzu Chi Hospital and professor at Tzu Chi University, Hualien, Taiwan. His expertise lies in radiotherapy for breast, esophageal, and lung cancers. Dai-Wei is advancing precision oncology through innovative radiotherapy techniques, including particle therapy, boron neutron capture therapy, and adaptive RT.

speakers.name: Chun An Wang
speakers.bio: Chun An is a software engineer at Lixel whose research interests include computer graphics, medical image processing, and 3D visualization systems. Her current work focuses on developing advanced computational methods and interactive 3D applications to enhance the analysis and visualization of medical imaging data.

speakers.name: ShengYao Huang
speakers.bio: A radiation oncologist with a Ph.D. in medical science, Sheng-Yao's doctoral research applied deep learning–based computer vision to identify metastatic lymph nodes in head and neck cancer. He continues to explore the integration of artificial intelligence and clinical practice, focusing on medical imaging analysis. Sheng-Yao is dedicated to bridging clinical expertise with advanced computational methods to improve diagnosis, treatment planning, and patient outcomes.

speakers.name: Chun Hao Chan
speakers.bio: Sheng Yao Huang is an optical R&D engineer specializing in 3D floating display systems and semiconductor device development. With a Ph.D. candidacy in electro-optical engineering at National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University and experience at Lixel Inc., he has contributed to multiple international patents and papers. His expertise spans optical system integration, micro-optical design, and imaging optimization.

speakers.name: Alex Yang
speakers.bio: Alex, a photonic engineering graduate, worked in semiconductor equipment before founding Cheray Co., Ltd. and Lixel. At Lixel, he leads development of glasses-free 3D displays and air-touch systems, targeting medical and education markets. Collaborations with Taiwan’s top hospitals and schools have driven growth. His innovative human-machine interface reshapes behaviors and business models, with plans for global expansion.

speakers.name: Ren-Jun Hsu
speakers.bio: Highly accomplished Ph.D. Cancer Biologist currently directing both Radiobiology and a Cancer Center. Doctoral research specialized in gene-cancer relationships. Ongoing work focuses on applying evolutionary learning to genomic and clinical data for optimized patient selection in clinical trials, advancing precision oncology.

sessions.title: End-to-End FP8 Training for Large Language Models: Higher Accuracy at Lower Cost
sessions.abstract:
Explore a plug-and-play FP8 training recipe that lets you train reasoning LLMs faster and with less memory—while matching BF16 accuracy—so you can scale context, batches, and experiments on the same budget. See how you can adopt it across pre-training, fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning (RL) to unlock more results per GPU hour. 

Our end-to-end FP8 pipeline improves speed and memory use without changing your model or schedule. Learn how to apply it today to do more with fixed compute budgets and deliver stronger downstream results.

We'll also share an FP8-first workflow that maintains accuracy while cutting compute and memory overhead. You get practical steps to drop it into your pre-training and post-training stacks.

Dive deep into an inclusive, end-to-end FP8 approach that turns training efficiency into real task gains.

speakers.name: Zhen LI
speakers.bio: Zhen Li is currently a Ph.D. student in the Department of Computing at The Hong Kong Polytechnic University. Before that, he received his M.S. degree from the University of Science and Technology of China. His primary research interests lie in low-resource pre-training and efficient inference for foundation models. Zhen has previously worked at several leading technology companies, including Tencent, Ant Group, AMD, and iFlytek.

speakers.name: Kejing Yang
speakers.bio: As a software engineer, Kejing Yang has been conducting research and working in the field of code representation and code large language models since 2021, when she worked as an algorithm engineer in Alibaba's Group Security Department. Kejing is responsible for the supervised fine-tuning of code LLMs, including full-parameter fine-tuning and LoRA fine-tuning.

speakers.name: Congkai Xie
speakers.bio: Congkai Xie is currently a member of technical staff at Reallm Labs, where he focuses on foundation model research and development. His work encompasses various aspects including efficient AI infrastructure, pre-training, post-training, and multi-modal technologies.

speakers.name: Zhen LI
speakers.bio: Prof. Hongxia Yang, Ph.D. from Duke University, has published over 150 top conference and journal papers, and held more than 50 patents. She has been awarded the highest prize of the 2019 World Artificial Intelligence Conference, Super AI Leader, the second prize of the 2020 National Science and Technology Progress Award. She used to work as the ByteDance U.S. LLM head, AI scientist, and director in Alibaba Group.

speakers.name: Pengkai Wang
speakers.bio: Pengkai Wang is a Ph.D. student in the Department of Computing at The Hong Kong Polytechnic University. His research focuses on agentic LLM post-training, particularly for applications in the medical domain now. He received his master's degree from Zhejiang University and bachelor's from Xiamen University. Additionally, his broader research interests span large language models, control theory, and scientific machine learning.

speakers.name: Zhijie Sang
speakers.bio: Zhijie Sang has eight years of experience in natural language processing, deep learning, and LLM, working with Bing Search at Microsoft for five years. He contributed to the research and development of Bing's generative search, enabling Microsoft Copilot to support summarization and Q&A for long-context documents, and introduced generative answers to the Bing China market.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models

sessions.title: Closing the Sim-to-Real Gap in Physical AI With Real-Time Control Stack
sessions.abstract: We explore a real-time execution stack that unifies NVIDIA Isaac Robot Foundation Models, ROS2, and software-based motion control to achieve sub-millisecond, deterministic performance — enabling precise, synchronized actions in the real world and closing the sim-to-real gap in physical AI. We also introduce sensor-driven fine-tuning methods to improve real-world adaptability through residual policy learning and LoRA-based adaptation.

speakers.name: Fikih Muhamad
speakers.bio: Fikih Muhamad is an associate researcher at MOVENSYS R&D, South Korea. His work focuses on combining optimal control, robotics, and AI to develop reliable, deployable robotic systems. He received his B.S. in electrical engineering from Universitas Indonesia (2020) and M.S. in electrical and information engineering from Seoul National University of Science and Technology (2024).

speakers.name: Junho Heo
speakers.bio: Junho Heo is a senior engineer on the Engine Team at MOVENSYS Inc. He develops optimal trajectory generation, robot dynamics analysis, collision detection, and real-time motion control. Previously, he worked on vibration measurement and analysis, anti-vibration technology deployment, and AI-based predictive maintenance using vibration data. He received an M.S. in mechanical systems engineering from Kumoh National Institute of Technology in 2018.

speakers.name: Boo-Ho Yang
speakers.bio: Boo-Ho Yang is the chair and founder of MOVENSYS Inc., a leader in software-based real-time motion control. He holds a Ph.D. and M.S. in mechanical engineering (robotics) from MIT. Formerly a research scientist at MIT, his current focus is integrating real-time motion intelligence with NVIDIA Isaac frameworks to advance physical AI and close the gap between simulation and real-world execution.

speakers.name: Kota Weaver
speakers.bio: Kota is the CTO of the U.S. office of MOVENSYS. He has a B.S. from Rennselaer Polytechnic Institute in electrical engineering, and has 10 years of robotics experience. Prior to his role at MOVENSYS, Kota was co-founder and CTO at Skylla Technologies, which was acquired by MOVENSYS in 2022. His work primarily focuses around autonomous mobile robots and mobile manipulator control and perception.

speakers.name: Daniel Hong
speakers.bio: Daniel Hong is a robotics software engineer at MOVENSYS Inc., where he develops real-time motion control software and integrates ROS 2–based systems for industrial robots. He received an M.Eng. in aerospace engineering from Seoul National University, with a focus on guidance, navigation, and control. His interests include robot perception, computer vision, state estimation, and feedback control.

sessions.title: AI-Accelerated Materials Discovery: High-Throughput Virtual Screening of Novel Materials With NVIDIA ALCHEMI
sessions.abstract: Leveraging NVIDIA ALCEMI, which confers unprecedented AI-enabled batched simulation capabilities, we performed ultra-fast high-throughput virtual screening of catalysts for hydrogen generation and immersion coolant for GPUs. Our newly discovered material candidates are poised to unlock the potential for hydrogen-driven and AI-driven societies.

speakers.name: Kawami Yoichiro
speakers.bio: Yoichiro is a chief staff member specializing in materials informatics and machine learning applications at ENEOS Holdings, Inc. He obtained his Ph.D. at the University of Queensland in Australia, with his primary focus on materials for energy generation. After his Ph.D., he underwent his postdoc at Kyushu University, where he investigated catalysts for carbon dioxide conversion. He's expert in electron microscpy and computational chemistry.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science

sessions.title: Autonomous AI Agent for End-to-End Component Data Extraction
sessions.abstract: Improve component data extraction performance by 6x (from two hours to 20 minutes) and achieve 100% accuracy using an autonomous generative AI agent. We detail our "Intelligent Processing Pipeline" and the "Data-Oriented Hybrid Cloud" (Wiwynn OCP Grand Teton servers + 8x NVIDIA H100s) that powers this solution.

speakers.name: Albert Lee
speakers.bio: Albert Li is an integral member of the Wiwynn Engineering Intelligence Team, contributing to software development for AI/automation applications since 2019. He holds a graduate degree from the National Taiwan Ocean University.

speakers.name: Shao Hung Yeh
speakers.bio: Will Yeh is a new member of the Wiwynn Engineering Intelligence Team (joining in 2023) specializing in AI and automation software development. He earned his graduate degree from National Taipei University.

speakers.name: Ted Pang
speakers.bio: Ted has led Wiwynn's software engineering team since 2017. He's responsible for strategy, architecture, and software development, covering data center management, testing, and AI. With over 30 years in the IT industry—spanning server, storage, and cloud computing—Ted brings extensive experience. He earned his graduate degree from the University of Florida.

speakers.name: Yujou Nieh
speakers.bio: Yujou Nieh just joined Wiwynn engineering intelligence team in 2024. She's responsible for software development for AI/automation applications. She earned her graduate degree from the National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University.

speakers.name: PJ Lin
speakers.bio: PJ is the leader of the AI software engineering team at Wiwynn since 2018. His core responsibilities include software development leadership for data center management and AI/automation applications. He brings over 15 years of experience in software development, covering both system and application domains.

speakers.name: Karl Chiang
speakers.bio: Karl Chiang joined Wiwynn and has led engineering intelligence team since 2021. He's responsible for software development for data center management and AI/automation applications, with over 20 years in the server, storage, and cloud computing realms.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)

sessions.title: CudaqToEinsum: Efficient Tensor-Network Conversion From CUDA Quantum for Large-Scale Quantum Circuit Simulation
sessions.abstract: We present CudaqToEinsum, a high-performance framework for converting quantum circuits into Tensor-network representations. Integrated tightly with CUDA Quantum, our method achieves 30–50× faster translation than the Qiskit + cuQuantum CircuitToEinsum route on 85,000-qubit BPS circuits. With native support for batched contraction, it processes over 150,000 samples simultaneously, achieving up to 200–300× speedups, paving the way for large-scale quantum simulations on HPC platforms.

speakers.name: Louis Chen
speakers.bio: Kuan-Cheng Chen's research focuses on distributed quantum computing and devices. He works at Jij Europe; previously, he was a R&D engineer at Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. Kuan-Cheng was first in Deloitte Quantum Challenge 2024 and third in Pascal Blaise; he won 2025 IEEE QTC QCE25 Distinguished Technical Paper; Best Paper (Application & Photonic). He organized Infocom’s workshop on quantum networking. Interests are distributed quantum computing, quantum ML/AI, and optimization. He earned his M.Sc. and Ph.D. at Imperial College London.

speakers.name: Tai-Yue Li
speakers.bio: Tai-Yue Li is an assistant researcher at the National Center for High-Performance Computing. He earned his Ph.D. in physics from National Dong Hwa University and was a postdoctoral fellow at the National Synchrotron Radiation Research Center, Taiwan. His research covers large-scale quantum-circuit simulation, quantum machine learning, hybrid quantum-classical architectures, quantum-inspired algorithms, and deep learning for X-ray imaging.

speakers.name: Yu-Chen Yeh
speakers.bio: Yu-Chen Yeh is a senior undergraduate in computer science at National Tsing Hua University, Taiwan. He is passionate about high-performance and GPU-accelerated computing, with hands-on experience from the ISC and APAC-HPC Student Competitions.

speakers.name: Nan-Yow Chen
speakers.bio: Nan-yow Chen is a research fellow at the National Center for High-Performance Computing. He received his Ph.D. in physics from National Tsing Hua University, Taiwan. He has published over 30 SCI-indexed journal papers and holds three U.S. patents. His current research interests include deep learning, image informatics, large-scale parallel computing, and quantum computing.

speakers.name: Pika Wang
speakers.bio: Pika is a quantum solution architect at the NVIDIA AI Technology Center. His prior work concentrated on quantum algorithms for very large-scale integration design and hardware development aimed at accelerating quantum-inspired and machine learning algorithms. Pika holds a bachelor’s degree in physics from the National Tsing Hua University and a master’s degree in the College of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from National Taiwan University.

sessions.title: Bioactivity Prediction via Spatial Emptiness Representation of Protein-Ligand Complex and Uniting Multiple Pockets
sessions.abstract:
Discover how spatial intelligence and pocket integration advance bioactivity prediction. We present DTIGN + LigoSpace, a next-gen framework that combines GeoREC — a geometric representation of spatial emptiness — and the Union-Pocket, a unified 3D structure merging multiple binding pockets of the same protein. Together, they capture how ligands truly fill and influence complex protein environments.

By integrating GeoREC for spatial representation, Union-Pocket for global context, and a pairwise learning strategy for accurate ligand ranking, our model delivers significant gains in bioactivity prediction. It learns not only if a ligand binds, but how completely it occupies its target, revealing the link between pocket filling and real-world potency. Accelerated on NVIDIA H200 GPUs, this approach achieves up to 96% faster inference.

speakers.name: Adams Wai Kin Kong
speakers.bio: Adams Wai-Kin Kong, Ph.D. (University of Waterloo), is an associate professor at Nanyang Technological University, Singapore, where he also serves as the director of the Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence program. Renowned for his contributions to artificial intelligence and biometrics, Adams Wai Kin has been recognized among Stanford University's Top 2% Scientists, reflecting the significant impact of his research.

speakers.name: Hoi Yeung Li
speakers.bio: Li Hoi Yeung integrates cancer biology, natural compound drug discovery, and AI-driven modeling to develop targeted cancer therapies. His patented Drug-Target Interaction Graph Neural Network accelerates therapeutic discovery through spatial and bioactivity prediction. Founder of NYB-AI and member of NVIDIA Ignition AI, Hoi Yeung bridges academia and industry with over 50 papers and multiple patents.

speakers.name: Giang Nguyen
speakers.bio: Giang Nguyen is an AI engineer with extensive experience in deploying AI solutions at scale. He previously founded a company that provided production-ready data services for AI companies across Asia. Currently, as the CTO of Nanyang Biologics AI, he focuses on building and commercializing AI-driven platforms for drug discovery, translating innovative algorithms into impactful real-world applications.

speakers.name: Duy Trieu
speakers.bio: Duy Trieu is a seasoned tech entrepreneur with a background in software engineering and startup leadership. A Duke University graduate in electronic and computer engineering, he blends technical expertise with experience scaling innovative products. Duy has led engineering teams at Nook, Sway Commerce, and FatTail, driving the development of scalable, high-impact technology solutions.

sessions.title: Enhance Production of Synthetic Radar Images From Geostationary Satellite Observations With Generative Diffusion Models
sessions.abstract: Explore how generative diffusion models can transform geostationary satellite and lightning observations into realistic, high-resolution radar reflectivity imagery. This work introduces a probabilistic approach that improves key metrics and reproduces fine-scale storm structures, helping extend radar-like coverage to regions without operational radar systems.

speakers.name: Yuguang Hu
speakers.bio: Yuguang Hu is an M.Phil. student in computer science at the University of Sydney. His research focuses on generative models.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: Emotion-Aware Voice Agent
sessions.abstract: Our goal is to develop emotion-aware voice agents capable of understanding and responding with appropriate emotion. Traditional agents, relying solely on text-based sentiment analysis, miss critical emotional cues conveyed through speech. Our research integrates multi-modal sentiment analysis, processing both the semantic content (text) and the prosodic features (speech) to create a holistic emotional understanding. This allows for more natural, empathetic, and engaging human-computer interactions.

speakers.name: Shing Ling Goh
speakers.bio: Shing Ling is a software engineer finding innovative solutions.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation

sessions.title: A Multi-Agent Debate Framework for Automated NVIDIA Cosmos Optimization in Surgical Robot Training
sessions.abstract: We present HMARCF-HILFOF, the first multi-agent agentic AI system integrated with NVIDIA Cosmos Physical AI for automated surgical robot training. By combining multi-expert debate reasoning with physics-based world simulation, we automate scenario design while ensuring clinical quality. HMARCF coordinates four agents — Thoracic Surgery, Robot Learning, Cosmos Engineering, and Imaging/Radiology — through structured debates to produce auditable, versioned Cosmos configurations (angles/tissue/dynamics/physics). HILFOF provides human-in-the-loop quality assurance with quantitative metrics (VACS >0.85, SBF >0.90) and feedback-driven regeneration. In preliminary phantom-based validation of thoracic needle insertion, the system expanded scenario diversity from 10 (manual baseline) to 30+ (5 angles × 3 tissues × 2 dynamics) while reducing prompt engineering time from hours to minutes.

speakers.name: Joyce Chen
speakers.bio: Joyce is experienced in medical devices, biotechnology, regulatory management, and product development, having secured multiple government grants, including sci-tech and AI pilot programs. She holds a master's degree in life sciences and bachelor's degrees in chemistry and information technology. She's also active in the Taiwan AI Academy and AI Association, promoting generative AI services.

speakers.name: Hsiang Wei Hu
speakers.bio: Hsiang Wei is the generative AI senior technical software program manager.

speakers.name: Hsing-hsien Wu
speakers.bio: Hsing-hsien is the chief of thoracic surgery at Tainan Municipal Hospital.

sessions.title: Design and Optimization of GPU-Aware MPI Allreduce Using Direct Sendrecv Communication
sessions.abstract: Explore new techniques for accelerating GPU-aware MPI Allreduce using direct send/receive communication with throttling. Our design improves scalability and overlaps computation with communication, achieving up to 7% faster nanoGPT training while maintaining strong performance across diverse workloads.

speakers.name: Dhabaleswar Panda
speakers.bio: DK Panda is a professor and distinguished scholar of computer science at Ohio State University.
