His research interests include parallel computer architecture, high-performance networking, InfiniBand, network-based computing, exascale computing, deep learning, programming models, GPUs and accelerators, high-performance file systems and storage, virtualization and cloud computing, and big data (Hadoop—HDFS, MapReduce, and HBase—and Memcached).

speakers.name: Chen-Chun Chen
speakers.bio: Chen-Chun Chen is a sixth-year Ph.D. student in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at The Ohio State University. His research focuses on designing and optimizing modern message-passing interface libraries, particularly MVAPICH, for heterogeneous GPU-based high performance computing and deep learning workloads. He specializes in developing advanced collective algorithms and integrating next-generation accelerators to achieve scalable, high-performance communication.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries

sessions.title: AI-Based Software Quantifies Fatty Liver Disease From MRI-PDFF Images Using Feature Extraction to Reduce False Positives
sessions.abstract: Our system integrates neural networks with SVM-based feature extraction to automatically detect liver regions in MRI-PDFF images while minimizing false positives. By employing whole-liver quantification instead of traditional ROI sampling, it objectively computes mean PDFF values for more accurate and consistent fatty liver assessment and follow-up.

speakers.name: Jimmy Lin
speakers.bio: Jimmy Lin is deputy manager at FindingsTech, overseeing the software development team. He is responsible for managing projects, coordinating workflows, and ensuring the delivery of high-quality software solutions.

speakers.name: Yueh-Hsun Lu
speakers.bio: Yueh-Hsun is a radiologist at Shuang Ho Hospital, specializing in interventional neurovascular procedures and acute ischemic stroke thrombectomy. His expertise includes X-ray and MRI interpretation, CT diagnostics, tumor embolization and ablation, and acute hemorrhage management. He is also actively involved in AI research to advance medical imaging and treatment.

speakers.name: Michael Pan
speakers.bio: Michael Pan is an AI engineer at FindingsTech, specializing in AI and physics simulation engines. FindingsTech helps transform real-world data into actionable insights for the semiconductor and digital healthcare industries, driving innovation and smarter solutions.

speakers.name: Lu Liu
speakers.bio: Lu Liu is chief designer at FindingsTech, responsible for user interface/user experience, product, and visual design. She leads the creation of intuitive interfaces and engaging user experiences, ensuring that the company’s products are both functional and visually compelling.

speakers.name: Chun-Peng Owen Hsu
speakers.bio: Owen Hsu is CEO and co-founder of FindingsTech. He has more than 20 intelligent patents in terms of algorithm and mechanics design.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging

sessions.title: Conversational AI Agent for Transportation Planning
sessions.abstract: We present a hybrid AI agent that combines retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and LoRA-tuned models to achieve 95.7% accuracy in generating transportation planning commands from natural language, reducing technical barriers and letting planners focus on analytical insights instead of memorizing command-line interface (CLI) syntax.

speakers.name: Tomasz Bednarz
speakers.bio: Tomasz's team at NVIDIA builds active collaborations with top researchers and premier research institutions that do compelling, computationally intense work to solve some of the world’s most challenging scientific problems.

speakers.name: Travis Waller
speakers.bio: Travis has held tenured professorial roles in America, Australia, and Germany (currently at TU Dresden). He co-founded two startups and was founding director of multiple university research centers. University admin roles include deputy head, deputy dean, and head of school. He has over 40 industry sponsors, 200 peer-reviewed journal publications, and 43 graduated Ph.D. students with multiple awards, including MIT Technology Review's TR100 list, U.S. NSF CAREER Award, TRB’s Fred Burggraf & Pyke Johnson Awards, and being named a fellow of Engineers Australia.

speakers.name: Surendra Reddy Kancharla
speakers.bio: Surendra Reddy Kancharla is a senior research associate at TU Dresden's Faculty of Transport and Traffic Sciences, specializing in sustainable logistics and urban mobility. With a Ph.D. from IIT Madras and 12 years of research experience, he develops computational models that integrate machine learning and optimization for vehicle routing and transportation planning.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Build AI Libraries for NVIDIA GPUs in Pure Modern Java With TornadoVM
sessions.abstract:
Discover how TornadoVM opens the NVIDIA GPU ecosystem to millions of Java developers, a community where GPU adoption has traditionally been extremely low. We demonstrate how TornadoVM’s hybrid API enables pure Java code to compile to PTX and interoperate with NVIDIA libraries like cuDNN, bridging GPU compute with the broader JVM ecosystem through frameworks such as GraalVM, LangChain4j, and Quarkus.

As an open-source framework, TornadoVM provides a seamless bridge between Java applications and NVIDIA’s developer tooling, allowing profiling, debugging, and optimization of GPU kernels directly from Java. This work highlights how GPU acceleration can become a first-class capability for the JVM community, unlocking new opportunities for AI and high-performance computing in pure modern Java.

speakers.name: Mary Xekalaki
speakers.bio: Mary is a postdoctoral research associate at the University of Manchester and a core contributor to TornadoVM. Her research focuses on heterogeneous computing for managed runtime systems and compilers.

speakers.name: MIchail Papadimitriou
speakers.bio: Michalis Papadimitriou is a research fellow at the University of Manchester and a staff software engineer on the TornadoVM team. His core expertise includes open-source software development, hardware abstractions for high-level programming languages, compiler optimizations for GPU computing, and enabling large language model inference on GPUs for the Java Virtual Machine.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: LFQUIAD: Lookup-Free Quantized Autoencoder for Few-Shot Unsupervised Industrial Anomaly Detection
sessions.abstract: Explore how we generate realistic synthetic anomalies using diffusion-based inpainting to train a quantized autoencoder without relying on large codebooks. Learn how our LFQUIAD framework improves anomaly detection and segmentation with minimal real data, offering a scalable and plug-and-play solution for industrial and medical inspection.

speakers.name: SHIHCHIH LIN
speakers.bio: With 20-plus years in AI and smart manufacturing, Shih-Chih focuses on computer vision and anomaly detection for semiconductor automation. A PMP, PMI-ACP, and PMI-PBA professional and AIA Executive Program graduate, he bridges industry and research, with papers published in ECCVW, ICCVW, CVPRW, BMVC, and ICIP.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: Galaxea Open-World Dataset and G0 Dual-System VLA Model
sessions.abstract: We present the Galaxea Open-World Dataset, a large and diverse collection of robotic behaviors captured in real human environments. Demonstrations are collected with a consistent robotic embodiment and annotated with precise subtask-level language descriptions for both training and evaluation. Building on this dataset, we introduce G0, a dual-system framework combining a vision-language model (VLM) for multi-modal planning and a vision-language-action (VLA) model for fine-grained execution. G0 is trained through a three-stage curriculum: cross-embodiment pre-training, single-embodiment pre-training, and task-specific post-training. Extensive benchmarks on tabletop manipulation, few-shot learning, and long-horizon mobile manipulation validate the approach, showing that single-embodiment pre-training, together with the Galaxea Dataset, is crucial for strong overall performance.

speakers.name: Hang Zhao
speakers.bio: Hang is the chief technology officer of GALAXEA. The former Waymo research scientist, now an assistant professor at Tsinghua University, has a Ph.D. from MIT. He has published more than 70 papers with over 21,000 citations (Google Scholar). Awards include Top 3 Best Paper at CoRL 2023, sole Chinese Area Chair at ICLR 2023, and multiple area chair roles at leading conferences including CVPR.

speakers.name: Tao Jiang
speakers.bio: Tao is an expert engineer at Galaxea, with key contributions to the development of their model and dataset.

sessions.title: AI-Driven Discovery of Novel JAK3 Inhibitors Using NVIDIA NIM-Accelerated Computational Pipeline
sessions.abstract: We accelerated JAK3 inhibitor discovery from months to hours using NVIDIA NIM microservices with GenMol and DiffDock on RTX Ada 6000 GPUs. Our pipeline identified novel drug candidates with better binding than FDA-approved treatments. This end-to-end workflow runs on a single GPU and combines NIM-accelerated AI generation with physics-based validation. Pharmaceutical companies and academic researchers can deploy this modular NIM-powered framework to dramatically reduce drug development timelines and costs for any therapeutic target.

speakers.name: Nhung Duong
speakers.bio: Nhung Duong is CEO of N2TP Technology Solutions, driving AI integration in drug discovery through SmartDoseAI for precision dosing, LynxOmics for biomarker discovery, and LynxDetect for biosensor detection. Her work has generated intellectual property in personalized medicine and early cancer detection. As a Ph.D. candidate at Hanoi University of Pharmacy, she focuses on pharmacometrics, biomarker discovery, and precision oncology.

sessions.title: Efficient On-Policy Distillation for Transferring Reasoning Capabilities ​via Parallel and Sparse Training​
sessions.abstract: We present an efficient on-policy distillation framework enhanced by the novel Zero-trick, which mitigates logit alignment gaps in sparse distillation. Our work fills the gap of open-source on-policy distillation tools and drastically reduces computational overhead compared with full-logit distillation, making advanced reasoning capabilities more accessible for practical small-scale models.

speakers.name: Zhaopeng Qiu
speakers.bio: Alex Qiu is focused on LLMs, recommender systems, and medical data mining. Before joining NVIDIA, he worked as a senior researcher at Jarvis Lab of Tencent. He obtained a master's degree in computer software and theory from Peking University in 2018.

speakers.name: Shuang Yu
speakers.bio: Shuang Yu is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, specializing in natural language processing, LLMs, and algorithm engineering. She holds a master's degree in computer science from Tsinghua University.

sessions.title: Benchmarking CUDA-Q Emulators Across Multiple GPU Generations
sessions.abstract: Explore how the performance of CUDA-Q emulators scales across the latest NVIDIA GPU generations and between single and multi-GPU setups to guide more efficient quantum-classical workloads.

speakers.name: Simone Rizzo
speakers.bio: Simone Rizzo has been a software engineer at E4 Computer Engineering since May 2024, where he has participated in projects funded by Italian and European research initiatives in the field of quantum computing. These projects mainly investigate the integration between quantum computing and high performance computing. He has also been focusing on benchmarking GPU-accelerated quantum emulators across various architectures.

speakers.name: Daniele Gregori
speakers.bio: Daniele Gregori has collaborated with the LHCb experiment at CERN, operationally managed the INFN-CNAF Tier1 computing center, and taught general physics at the University of Bologna. Currently, he leads the  European Projects and Quantum Computing Team as chief scientific officer at E4 Computer Engineering. Daniele has a Ph.D. in physics.

sessions.title: LoBE-GS: Load-Balanced and Efficient 3D Gaussian Splatting for Large-Scale Scene Reconstruction
sessions.abstract: Explore new techniques for load-balanced 3D Gaussian splatting that make large-scale scene reconstruction faster and more accessible. We introduce LoBE-GS, a framework that balances computation across partitions to achieve up to 2x faster training while maintaining quality—enabling real-time visualization and interactive exploration through Omniverse streaming.

speakers.name: Ting-Yu Yen
speakers.bio: Ting-Yu Yen is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in computer science at National Tsing Hua University under the supervision of Prof. Hung-Kuo Chu. His research interests include computer graphics, computer vision, and Gaussian splatting.

speakers.name: Shih-Hsuan Hung
speakers.bio: Shih-Hsuan Hung is an assistant professor at National Tsing Hua University, where he leads the Visualization and Modeling Lab (VMLab). His research focuses on computer graphics, including visualization, geometric modeling, physics AI, and computer vision. Shih-Hsuan earned his Ph.D. in computer science with a minor in mathematics from Oregon State University in 2024, and M.S./B.S. degrees from NTUST.

speakers.name: Jeng Wen Joshua Lean
speakers.bio: Joshua Lean is currently an undergraduate student in electrical engineering and computer science at National Tsing Hua University, advised by professors Hung-Kuo Chu and Shih-Hsuan Hung. Current interests include computer graphics and large system design.

speakers.name: Johnson Sun
speakers.bio: Wei-Fang Sun is currently a solutions architect at NVIDIA AI Technology Center, where his main research areas include reinforcement learning, generative modeling, and robotics applications and research.

speakers.name: Sheng-Hsiang Hung
speakers.bio: Sheng-Hsiang Hung is currently a master’s student in computer science at National Tsing Hua University, advised by Prof. Hung-Kuo Chu, where his research interests lie in deep learning for 3D reconstruction and large-scale novel-view synthesis.

speakers.name: HungKuo Chu
speakers.bio: Hung-Kuo Chu, professor of computer science at National Tsing Hua University and director of the Center for Sports Technology, focuses on computer graphics, vision, 3D reconstruction, extended reality, and sports tech. With 80-plus papers and 20 patents, he has led NSTC flagship projects and co-founded NeuinX, an AI sports startup. His virtual reality and motion capture systems won the 2024 Future Tech Award and NTHU’s 2024 Collaboration Excellence Award.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: A Diffusion Model Framework for Solving Scientific Inverse Problems
sessions.abstract: A key challenge in many scientific tasks is inferring the underlying scientific phenomenon from sparse and corrupted measurements, also known as inverse problems. A canonical example is imaging a black hole using the Event Horizon Telescope, which collects sparse radio waves that have been corrupted by the Earth's atmosphere. We have developed a framework based on diffusion models that can solve a broad range of scientific inverse problems, with applications that span medical imaging, astronomy, seismology, and climate science.

speakers.name: Hongkai Zheng
speakers.bio: Hongkai Zheng is a final-year Ph.D. student at Caltech. His research interests lie in the realm of deep generative modeling and inverse problems. He develops scalable and efficient generative models and design algorithms to solve ill-posed problems in a probabilistic framework. Hongkai is supported by PIMCO Data Science Graduate Fellowship.

speakers.name: Katie Bouman
speakers.bio: Katie Bouman is a professor of computing and mathematical sciences at Caltech. Her research interests are in computational imaging and machine learning. She is best known for leading the imaging team that produced the first image of a black hole using the Event Horizon Telescope.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: GPU-Accelerated Simulations of Flow-Structure Interaction at Extreme Scales
sessions.abstract: Numerical simulation of the Navier-Stokes equation is among the most complex problems in computational sciences. The complexity increases tremendously when incorporating moving boundaries and modeling interactions with multiple physics, such as structural mechanics, acoustics, and more. We present a sharp-interface immersed boundary method and accelerate it using GPUs to simulate some of the most complex problems currently beyond the capabilities of many industrial solvers.

speakers.name: Sushrut Kumar
speakers.bio: Sushrut Kumar is a doctoral researcher in the Flow Physics and Computation Lab at Johns Hopkins University. His research expertise is in high-performance computing, sparse linear algebra, and geometric deep learning with applications in computational fluid dynamics and computational structural mechanics.

sessions.title: HyperSack: Resource-Aware Distributed Hyperparameter Optimization for Lightweight Vision and Language Models on GPUs
sessions.abstract: Hyperparameter optimization (HPO) can unlock the full potential of deep learning (DL) models, but it's one of the most compute-intensive tasks in DL. Existing HPO parallelization strategies underutilize powerful GPUs, especially for training lightweight deep neural networks. Resource-sharing mechanisms such as multi-process service can improve GPU utilization, but naive adaptations for HPO workloads lead to poor performance. We present HyperSack: a distributed HPO framework for dynamic and resource-aware scheduling on GPU-based HPC systems. HyperSack cuts the execution time of HPO workloads by orchestrating efficient placement of DL training jobs. Our tests on vision and language models HPO workloads show up to 2.8x performance improvement in execution time on AI00s, 4.0x on HI00s, and 3.9x on a combination of AI00s and HI00s using HyperSack over standard HPO parallelization methods.

speakers.name: Dhabaleswar Panda
speakers.bio: DK Panda is a professor and distinguished scholar of computer science at Ohio State University. His research interests include parallel computer architecture, high-performance networking, InfiniBand, network-based computing, exascale computing, deep learning, programming models, GPUs and accelerators, high-performance file systems and storage, virtualization and cloud computing, and big data (Hadoop—HDFS, MapReduce, and HBase—and Memcached).

speakers.name: Nawras Alnaasan
speakers.bio: Nawras Alnaasan is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in computer science and engineering at NOWLAB, The Ohio State University. His research interests lie at the intersection of deep learning and high performance computing. He's actively involved in several projects such as HiDL (high-performance deep learning) and ICICLE (Intelligent Cyberinfrastructure with Computational Learning in the Environment).

sessions.title: Accelerate Robotic Grinding With Physics-Based Simulation and Synthetic Data
sessions.abstract: To solve the slow, manual tuning and costly physical trials hindering robotic grinding, we present HolonCloud: a physics-based digital twin powered by NVIDIA Isaac Sim. This framework leverages synthetic data and material simulation to accurately predict results, eliminating physical validation and enabling an autonomous, reinforcement learning-driven sim-to-real workflow.

speakers.name: YI-ZHE Yang
speakers.bio: As the chief marketing officer and co-founder of Holo Robotics, Yi Zhe is driven by using aesthetics and logic to create a positive value cycle, specializing in turning abstract needs into concrete solutions. As the former software R&D director at Da Shiang Automation (2019-23), he led an eight-person team, managing business-to-business product/system integration projects from market research to deployment and maintenance. Yi Zhe holds an M.S. in mechanical engineering from National Taiwan University.

speakers.name: YuanChieh Lo
speakers.bio: YuanChieh is the founder/CEO of Holo Robotic, an ITRI spinoff. As a former ITRI project manager (2017-24), he integrated 20-plus robotic systems and led numerous collaborations. He holds an M.S. in applied mechanics from National Taiwan University. YuanChieh's expertise includes robot control, sensor tech, and full-stack development, and his team won the 2024 TREE Award (First Prize) and 2024 EE Awards Asia (Best Smart Manufacturing Platform).

sessions.title: Manufacturing AI Agent and Explainable AI for Human-in-the-Loop Knowledge Transfer
sessions.abstract: Explore a Multi-Tool ReAct AI Agent that integrates XGBoost, GPU-accelerated SHAP, and LLM reasoning on NVIDIA NIM to preserve and scale tacit manufacturing expertise. We demonstrate how this agentic AI framework improves optimization, interpretability, and operational decision-making, helping manufacturers accelerate digital transformation through explainable, data-driven intelligence.

speakers.name: CT Cheng
speakers.bio: CT is the chief technology officer of Morale AI. With expertise in backend architecture, natural language processing, and applied machine learning, he leads the development of scalable AI systems integrating AI agent frameworks and machine learning.

speakers.name: Yun Lee
speakers.bio: Yun Lee is a staff technical program manager (TPM) and data scientist of Morale AI, and an alumna of the University of Michigan. She specializes in bridging advanced AI platforms with industrial operations, enabling companies to adopt real-time decision-making and scalable digital transformation. Yun is passionate about leveraging practical AI solutions to strengthen competitive advantage, improve efficiency, and foster sustainable growth across sectors.

speakers.name: CHIU CHUNG CHEN
speakers.bio: Wade Chen is an ML engineer at Morale AI, interested in building scalable data pipelines and deploying AI models for industrial automation, quality prediction, and environmental, social, and governance applications.

speakers.name: Eddie Shih
speakers.bio: Eddie is the chief operations officer and co-founder of Morale AI. He conducts industrial user analysis to guide the design of intelligent manufacturing solutions and model development. His work bridges machine learning, industrial engineering, and applied analytics to enhance productivity and sustainability across production environments.

speakers.name: Phill Kao
speakers.bio: Morale AI was established in September 2023, originating from National Tsing Hua University with a wealth of AI-driven smart manufacturing case studies. The company primarily offers advanced AI solutions for smart manufacturing and environmental, social, and governance concerns, aiming to utilize cutting-edge AI technology to become a savior for corporate performance and sustainable development.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: GLARE: GPU-Accelerated 3D CT Vision-Language Reasoning via Efficient Anatomical Boundary Guidance
sessions.abstract: Interpreting volumetric CT scans using vision-language models requires significant GPU resources and robust spatial focus. GLARE introduces slice-wise visual-instruction prompting, overlaying explicit blue contours generated by NVIDIA VISTA-3D on every axial CT slice to direct model attention to clinically relevant regions. Combined with a compact Qwen-2.5 decoder, this design enables high-performance medical reasoning with reduced VRAM footprint and inference time, delivering state-of-the-art VQA accuracy on medical benchmarks.

speakers.name: NAMJOON KIM
speakers.bio: Nam-Joon is a visiting associate professor in electrical and computer engineering at Seoul National University, focusing on AI and hardware technologies. With over 13 years of industry experience at LG Electronics and Samsung, he specializes in neural processors, computer vision, and AI applications. Nam-Joon's research covers hardware and software for vision, conversational AI, and biomedical imaging, with an emphasis on optimizing hardware and firmware.

speakers.name: Ginny Wong
speakers.bio: Ginny Wong is a data scientist at NVIDIA with over four years of experience, previously working at HSBC and ASM in data science and software engineering roles. She holds a Ph.D. in electronic and information engineering from The Hong Kong Polytechnic University. She is skilled in data mining, research, and machine learning, and is proficient in Cantonese, English, and Mandarin.

speakers.name: Soo Yong Kim
speakers.bio: Soo Yong Kim is a machine learning engineer based in Seoul with over two years of experience. His strengths include deep understanding and rapid learning. He specializes in diffusion and vision-language models with recent interests in video applications and speech processing, including text-to-speech, speech-to-text, and speech-to-face modeling. He holds a master's degree in AI from Seoul National University and a bachelor's from Korea University.

speakers.name: Junsu Lim
speakers.bio: Junsu is a data science and big data major at Sangmyung University who's currently conducting AI research at Imsilab and OUTTA, Seoul National University. At Imsilab, he studies medical AI domains, exploring innovative solutions in healthcare. Junsu is broadly interested in artificial intelligence and vision-language models, aiming to advance both fundamental research and practical applications.

speakers.name: Eunseob Choi
speakers.bio: Eunseob Choi is a GIST (Gwangju Institute of Science and Technology) undergraduate double-majoring in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and AI Convergence, focused on computer vision for medical imaging and generative/foundation models. He studied at UC Berkeley (Visiting) and Seoul National University (Visiting), where he conducts research at the IMSI (Imaging-driven Medical SuperIntelligence) group in collaboration with NVIDIA Taiwan, HK Healthcare, and Samsung Medical Center, developing MAE-based generative foundation models for high-fidelity CT/MRI reconstruction. He currently works as an AI Research Intern at JB Woori Capital, applying deep learning to financial engineering.

speakers.name: Youngung Han
speakers.bio: Youngung Han is a Ph.D. candidate in computer science and engineering at Seoul National University, specializing in AI and medical imaging research. He has served as a research engineer at AIDEEP and held leadership and mentoring roles in various academic initiatives. His work covers retrieval-augmented generation, vision-language models, and efficient AI for healthcare.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Biology - Generative AI

sessions.title: Low-Cost Motion Learning Pipeline for Industrial Humanoids on NVIDIA Jetson Platforms
sessions.abstract: In real factories, safety regulations, security restrictions, and a lack of skilled operators make it difficult to collect and deploy motion data at scale. To address these challenges, we developed an intuitive teleoperation pipeline using a single consumer-grade head-mounted display (HMD), removing the need for external trackers or multi-camera systems. The system integrates Jetson-based inference with an RTOS-driven dual-network control architecture—EtherCAT for ALICE-M1 and RS-485 for ALICE4—enabling deterministic real-time operation. A GR00T-Mimic-inspired data augmentation method further diversifies robot trajectories without additional data collection, enhancing robustness across manipulation tasks. Experiments in industrial scenarios show stable autonomous execution and confirm the feasibility of deploying Jetson-based humanoid systems for real-world industrial applications.

speakers.name: Junhee Won
speakers.bio: Junhee is a robotics engineer at AeiROBOT, where he works on the development of the ALICE humanoid series. Junhee's focus is on actuator design and advanced control systems for humanoid robots, exploring how precise hardware and intelligent software can merge to achieve human-like motion. He's excited to share AeiROBOT's progress and connect with others shaping the future of embodied AI.

speakers.name: Yuhyun Kim
speakers.bio: Yuhyun specializes in large language model-based motion reasoning and teleoperation feedback systems. Yuhyun contributed to the design of human-intuitive interfaces and to the development of the data augmentation pipeline inspired by GR00T-Mimic for improving humanoid task generalization.

speakers.name: kim uijin
speakers.bio: An AI Engineer at AeiROBOT, Kim's currently working on applying reinforcement learning to enhance humanoid locomotion and control capabilities.

speakers.name: Gihun Kang
speakers.bio: Gihun specializes in dynamic stability, thermal design, and mechanical safety optimization. He has developed lightweight, vibration-isolated frames and modular structures ensuring safe operation and maintainability in industrial environments.

speakers.name: YONG JUNWOO
speakers.bio: JunWoo is an AI engineer at AeiROBOT, currently working on integrating NVIDIA GR00T into humanoid robots to explore advanced learning and control capabilities.

speakers.name: Sihyeong Park
speakers.bio: Sihyeong specializes in real-time embedded control, power, and communication system design, and the integration of NVIDIA Jetson platforms into humanoid robots. He's contributed to establishing the dual-network control structure using EtherCAT and RS-485, enabling deterministic and synchronized operation across distributed actuators.

speakers.name: Han Jeakweon
speakers.bio: Han leads the overall R&D strategy for humanoid robot development and industrial AI integration. With extensive experience in embedded systems, real-time control, and hardware–software co-design, he supervises the convergence of mechanical, electrical, and AI subsystems across the ALICE humanoid series.

sessions.title: ColonVR: Enhancing Colonography Through Artificial Intelligence and Virtual Reality
sessions.abstract:
Colorectal cancer is a leading cause of cancer-related deaths. While virtual colonoscopy provides a noninvasive alternative, current approaches depend on manual segmentation and lack immersive exploration. We present ColonVR, a hybrid AI and virtual reality framework that combines NVIDIA MONAI with TotalSegmentator for automated colon reconstruction from unprepared CT scans. Our hybrid pipeline was benchmarked against NVIDIA VISTA 3D, MONAI, CLEAR, and TotalSegmentator, showing improved anatomical accuracy and surface continuity.

In a study with 30 participants, users searched for polyps inside a reconstructed colon in VR using three navigation modes: Automatic, Flythrough, and Teleport. Automatic navigation delivered the best trade-off between efficiency, accuracy, and comfort, highlighting how AI-driven reconstruction and immersive VR can advance diagnostic training.

speakers.name: Afonso Águas
speakers.bio: Afonso is a dynamic and assertive professional with experience in software development and data analysis, combined with strong leadership and team management skills. Currently, he's furthering his knowledge through a master’s degree in computer science and engineering at Instituto Superior Técnico.

speakers.name: Joaquim Jorge
speakers.bio: Joaquim Jorge is a full professor of computer science at Instituto Superior Técnico, University of Lisboa, and heads the Graphics and Interaction Group at INESC-ID. The editor-in-chief of "Computers & Graphics" and "Graphics and Visual Computing," he has chaired major conferences including IEEE VR and Eurographics. A Fellow of Eurographics and ACM Distinguished Member, he received the IFIP Silver Core Award in 2014.

speakers.name: Catarina Moreira
speakers.bio: Catarina Moreira is an associate professor in machine learning at the Data Science Institute, University of Technology Sydney, and UNESCO co-chair on AI and extended reality. Her research spans human-centered AI, multi-modal deep learning, and probabilistic models. She co-founded the XAMI Lab, advancing fair and transparent AI. She is recognized with the 2024 Women in AI Award (AI in Quantum) and 2021 WiT Award.

sessions.title: GRAVEX: GPU-Accelerated GAN-Diffusion Representation for Counterfeit Pattern Analysis With MONAI-Based Validation
sessions.abstract: We demonstrate how systematically reusing and fine-tuning discriminators from generative models yields robust deepfake detection across diverse synthesis methods. By integrating scale-adaptive features from ProGAN discriminators with temporal-consistency features from diffusion models, our GRAVEX framework achieves 74.33% F1-score across nine state-of-the-art generators—outperforming conventional ViT-based detectors by over 30% F1, on average. Implemented using NVIDIA MONAI for streamlined training and deployment, this approach addresses the critical challenge of detecting AI-generated faces as generative AI becomes increasingly sophisticated. On challenging datasets like CIFAKE, where baseline detectors fail completely, GRAVEX maintains 88% F1-score, demonstrating exceptional robustness and validating discriminator reuse as a promising path toward production-ready detection systems.

speakers.name: Induk Um
speakers.bio: Induk Um is an undergraduate researcher at Seoul National University's IMSI Lab under Professor Nam-Joon Kim, majoring in mechanical engineering at Chung-Ang University. He specializes in deep learning and medical image analysis, contributing to research on GPU-accelerated AI pipelines for clinical applications.

speakers.name: Kyeonghun Kim
speakers.bio: Kyeonghun Kim is an AI researcher at OUTTA specializing in medical AI and computer vision, currently conducting research at Seoul National University on GPU-accelerated deep learning pipelines for clinical healthcare applications.

speakers.name: Ken Liao
speakers.bio: Ken Ying-Kai Liao is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, specializing in AI and GPU-accelerated computing for healthcare and medical imaging applications. He works closely with research institutions and hospitals to deploy NVIDIA technologies including TensorRT, Triton Inference Server, and Clara platform for clinical AI solutions.

speakers.name: Junsu Lim
speakers.bio: Junsu is a data science and big data major at Sangmyung University who's currently conducting AI research at Imsilab and OUTTA, Seoul National University. At Imsilab, he studies medical AI domains, exploring innovative solutions in healthcare. Junsu is broadly interested in artificial intelligence and vision-language models, aiming to advance both fundamental research and practical applications.

speakers.name: Hyuk-jae Lee
speakers.bio: Hyuk-jae is a professor of electrical and computer engineering at Seoul National University, where he's been a faculty member since 2001 and served as department chair from 2019-23. Before joining SNU, he was a senior engineer at Intel and an assistant professor at Louisiana Tech University. Hyuk-jae also served as program director at Korea Evaluation Institute of Industrial Technology from 2013-15. He holds a Ph.D. from Purdue University and M.S. and B.S. degrees from SNU.

speakers.name: Eunseob Choi
speakers.bio: Eunseob Choi is a GIST (Gwangju Institute of Science and Technology) undergraduate double-majoring in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and AI Convergence, focused on computer vision for medical imaging and generative/foundation models. He studied at UC Berkeley (Visiting) and Seoul National University (Visiting), where he conducts research at the IMSI (Imaging-driven Medical SuperIntelligence) group in collaboration with NVIDIA Taiwan, HK Healthcare, and Samsung Medical Center, developing MAE-based generative foundation models for high-fidelity CT/MRI reconstruction. He currently works as an AI Research Intern at JB Woori Capital, applying deep learning to financial engineering.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: AI Inference on NVIDIA Jetson Orin AGX GPU for Real-Time Arthroscopic Surgery Assistance
sessions.abstract:
AI is transforming medical imaging and surgical navigation by enhancing precision, reducing human error, and improving patient outcomes. Yet, its integration in operating rooms is challenging due to stringent real-time and safety demands.

In minimally invasive procedures such as arthroscopy, limited maneuverability and indirect visualization make tasks like ACL reconstruction technically demanding. AI-driven video-based surgical navigation in edge computing systems offer improved accuracy and efficiency without high-end workstations.

Our recent collaboration with Smith & Nephew features an edge AI surgical assistance pipeline built on the NVIDIA Holoscan framework and Jetson AGX Orin hardware. We optimized three AI subsystems — segmentation, classification, and lens mark detection — validated on cadaveric datasets, achieving real-time performance with dozens of frames per second.

speakers.name: Gabriel Falcao
speakers.bio: Gabriel Falcão is an associate professor of ECE at the University of Coimbra and a researcher at Instituto de Telecomunicações. He earned his Ph.D. in 2010 on parallel computer architectures for LDPC codes. His research focuses on low-power HPC for signal, image, and data processing. A former visiting professor at EPFL and ETHZ, and in 2025/26 at NYU Abu Dhabi, he received a Google Faculty Research Award and leads a CUDA Research Center.

sessions.title: GPU-Accelerated SIP/RTP Real-Time Voice Customer Service System
sessions.abstract: We present a GPU-accelerated real-time voice interaction system built on NVIDIA NeMo, TensorRT-LLM, and Riva, capable of running directly over standard SIP/RTP telephony. Using only one RTX 5070 Ti or RTX 5090 GPU, the system achieves sub-600 ms end-to-end conversational latency, performing automatic speech recognition (ASR), LLM inference, and text-to-speech (TTS) fully on-device. This work demonstrates how small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) can deploy on-premises AI voice agents using consumer-grade GPUs, reducing cloud costs and ensuring secure, low-latency communication.

speakers.name: Lin Yin Ma
speakers.bio: Lin Yin Ma, CTO of The Barking Dog (Barkingdog AI), specializes in the convergence of extended reality and generative AI technologies. She currently spearheads the development and deployment of the company's core product lines, including AI AMAZE (intelligent customer service platform) and the entire AI agent product matrix.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation

sessions.title: Characterizing Communication Patterns in Distributed LLM Inference
sessions.abstract: This work investigates communication dynamics in distributed LLM serving—analyzing how various parallelization approaches coordinate data exchange between GPU workers during inference. We study transformer-based models as representative examples of contemporary architectures widely used in operational deployments. Our work combines detailed profiling measurements with predictive analytical models to characterize communication behavior across different parallelization configurations.

speakers.name: Lang Xu
speakers.bio: Lang is a third-year Ph.D. student at The Ohio State University, supervised by DK Dhbaleswar Panda. He contributes to the development of MVAPICH—a high-performance, GPU-aware message-passing interface (MPI) library. He's also worked on compression-assisted MPI collectives. Lang's research lies in high-performance deep learning and large-scale training optimization.

speakers.name: Quentin Anthony
speakers.bio: Quentin Anthony is a Ph.D. student at The Ohio State University whose research is primarily focused on the intersection of deep learning frameworks and high performance computing. He actively contributes to the MVAPICH2 project and its sub-projects, such as MVAPICH2-GDR (high-performance message passing interface for GPU clusters), and HiDL (High Performance Deep Learning).

speakers.name: Nawras Alnaasan
speakers.bio: Nawras Alnaasan is currently pursuing a Ph.D. in computer science and engineering at NOWLAB, The Ohio State University. His research interests lie at the intersection of deep learning and high performance computing. He's actively involved in several projects such as HiDL (high-performance deep learning) and ICICLE (Intelligent Cyberinfrastructure with Computational Learning in the Environment).

speakers.name: Dhabaleswar Panda
speakers.bio: DK Panda is a professor and distinguished scholar of computer science at Ohio State University. His research interests include parallel computer architecture, high-performance networking, InfiniBand, network-based computing, exascale computing, deep learning, programming models, GPUs and accelerators, high-performance file systems and storage, virtualization and cloud computing, and big data (Hadoop—HDFS, MapReduce, and HBase—and Memcached).

sessions.title: An Ecosystem for End-to-End Multi-Modal Data Curation
sessions.abstract:
Multi-modal data from diverse domains is the lifeblood of the Gen AI revolution, but curating and discovering datasets is expensive and time consuming. With the scarcity of efficient tools to unify data discovery, debiasing, and blending multi-modal human-labeled datasets for reuse in dynamic downstream model development tasks, there is a tremendous opportunity to drastically reduce data annotation cost and data pre-processing compute.

We present inventions utilizing technologies such as agentic AI, synthetic data generation, and multi-modal embedding models closely integrated with the NVIDIA tool suite. Our end-to-end ecosystem exhibits downstream performance improvements on multiple task types (pre-training, post-training). These tools can rapidly cut down cycle times for foundation model teams, and power smarter and more aligned Gen AI models across numerous industries.

speakers.name: Sarah Amiraslani
speakers.bio: Sarah Amiraslani is a data scientist at NVIDIA focusing on applied AI. She has over five years of experience working in data analytics across diverse industries including manufacturing automation, quantitative modeling, AI model development, and academic research. She earned an M.S. in applied data science from the University of Michigan.

speakers.name: Ramani Akkati
speakers.bio: Ramani Akkati is a NeMo generative AI analyst at NVIDIA focusing on semantic data curation for annotated datasets. She earned a bachelor of arts in English language and literature at San Jose State University.

speakers.name: Pratyush Muthukumar
speakers.bio: Pratyush Muthukumar is a generative AI data scientist at NVIDIA with a focus on applied research for large-scale generative AI. His research interests include multi-modal embedding models, agentic AI, and synthetic data generation. He earned an M.S. in computer science with a specialization in AI from Stanford University.

speakers.name: Shaan Jain
speakers.bio: Shaan Jain is a generative AI analyst at NVIDIA focusing on agentic data curation tools and AI platforms. He has earned credentials in machine learning from MIT Professional Education, statistics from NYU Stern School of Business, and finance from San Jose State University.

speakers.name: Tomohiro Kanazawa
speakers.bio: Tomo Kanazawa is a data scientist at NVIDIA focusing on multi-modal semantic search. He earned an M.S. in computer science from Santa Clara University and an M.S. in physics and astronomy from Kyoto University.

speakers.name: Harshil Kotamreddy
speakers.bio: Harshil Kotamreddy is a Gen AI engineer at NVIDIA focusing on applied AI for multi-modal data curation. He earned a master's degree in computing science from the University of Alberta with a focus on eigenoptions.

speakers.name: Andrew Mathau
speakers.bio: Andrew Mathau is a Generative AI/ML Engineer, focused on designing/scaling ML solutions and building tooling   for Generative AI data infrastructure. His research interests include virtually embodied AI agents, unsupervised learning in neural networks, neuroscience, and agentic AI. He holds an MSc in Cognitive Neuroscience from University of York.

sessions.title: Agentic AutoML with RAPIDS-Accelerated AutoGluon and NeMo Agent Toolkit
sessions.abstract:
We introduce NeMo-RAPIDS AutoML Agent, an agentic AutoML framework designed for tabular and time-series tasks. Unlike prior LLM-based AutoML systems (e.g., AutoML-Agent), our system integrates NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit for multi-agent orchestration and RAPIDS for GPU-accelerated AutoML.

Through tool-augmented reasoning, LLM agents invoke high-level APIs (AutoGluon, RAPIDS) instead of writing raw code, enabling both autonomy and performance. Experiments on public datasets demonstrate up to 13% improvement in quality and 5–10x faster runtime, proving that agentic orchestration plus GPU acceleration can make LLM-driven AutoML efficient, scalable, and enterprise-ready.

speakers.name: Hoang Nguyen
speakers.bio: Hoang Nguyen is an AI engineer at Viettel Solutions who works on developing LLM applications. He holds a bachelor's degree in data science and artificial intelligence from Hanoi University of Science and Technology.

speakers.name: Tuan Nguyen
speakers.bio: Tuan Nguyen is the head of the large language model development and smart traffic solutions at Viettel Solutions, where he also serves as a data analytics project manager. He holds a master’s degree in computer science from the Vietnam Post and Telecommunications Institute of Technology.

speakers.name: Binh Nguyen
speakers.bio: Binh Nguyen is an AI engineer at Viettel Solutions, focusing on developing various natural language processing models and solutions. He earned both his bachelor’s degree in information terchnology and master’s degree in computer science from the University of Engineering and Technology, Vietnam National University Hanoi.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: A Multi-Step Approach to Customize Small Language Models for Enterprise AI Applications
sessions.abstract: Many enterprise generative AI use cases could benefit from more effectively utilizing user preference data. In this poster, we demonstrate an affective architecture for fine-tuning small language models using this preference data, built on NVIDIA NeMo, and share results from the model performance gained.

speakers.name: Ben Cochran
speakers.bio: Ben is EY's generative AI product manager. His background is in financial services, but he's been focused on generative AI for the past several years.

speakers.name: Ben Wyatt
speakers.bio: Ben's an EY generative AI researcher with a background in physics.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Agentic AI for DAG Reasoning: Build Deterministic Data Engineering Pipelines Through Human-Machine Collaboration
sessions.abstract: Data engineering can dramatically benefit from agentic assistance. However, agents are not well suited to many standard data integration tasks, as hallucinations during pipeline execution can produce non-deterministic outcomes, and operations rarely merit the expense of AI at scale. We demonstrate a system that leverages agents to help design abstract data pipelines as Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs), then translate those pipelines into code that can be deployed in existing data platforms. This ensures deterministic, traceable, and affordable data workflows, while still benefiting from the huge advantages agents offer in accelerating building pipelines. A combination graph and chat based user interface is demonstrated allowing efficient human-machine teaming for pipeline build-out.

speakers.name: Christopher Morris
speakers.bio: Chris is a senior engineering leader with more than a decade of experience directing multidisciplinary teams in machine learning, computer vision, and autonomous systems. Before Clear Fracture, he led large-scale AI initiatives at Saab, BigBear.ai, and Jacobs, delivering for organizations including DARPA, Air Force Research Laboratory, the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency, and the U.S. Navy. Chris holds a Ph.D. in physics and has authored more than 15 peer-reviewed research publications.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Accelerate Predictive Water Safety via Synthetic Data and Continuous Learning
sessions.abstract: We demonstrate how synthetic data generation and continuous learning solve the critical deployment barriers in water safety AI—reducing detection latency by 37% and cutting multi-site onboarding from two months to five days. Explore how generative AI mitigates model drift across diverse aquatic environments where labeled real-world data is scarce and costly.

speakers.name: Chin Ju Chen
speakers.bio: Chin Ju Chen is the founder and CEO of Fortune AI, builder of SAFE SWIM, an edge-AI platform that turns CCTV into real-time water-safety monitors. He deploys from pools to coasts with partners across Asia and Europe. With a background in data and software, he delivers fast installs and measurable safety gains.

speakers.name: LIN SHENGJIE
speakers.bio: Sheng Jie Lin is head of engineering (chief technology officer) at Fortune AI. He builds the edge-AI stack for SAFE SWIM, covering model training, low-latency inference, and camera integrations for reliable, real-time detection.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: Can Large Language Models Explain Themselves? A Study of LLM-Generated Self-Explanations
sessions.abstract: LLMs excel at diverse natural language processing (NLP) tasks, from sentiment analysis to reasoning and summarization. Because they are instruction-tuned to produce helpful responses, they often generate self-explanations — rationales accompanying their outputs. For instance, when analyzing a movie review, an LLM may identify sentiment-laden words like “fantastic” or “memorable.” We investigate such self-explanations in sentiment analysis, focusing on feature attribution explanations. We explore different elicitation strategies, assess their faithfulness, and compare them with traditional methods like occlusion and LIME. Our experiments show that LLM self-explanations are comparably faithful, differ notably from traditional ones, and are far cheaper to obtain. These findings invite a reconsideration of current interpretability practices in the era of instruction-tuned LLMs.

speakers.name: Shiyuan Huang
speakers.bio: Shiyuan Huang is a Ph.D. student in computer science and engineering at UC Santa Cruz, advised by Leilani Gilpin and Ian Lane. His research focuses on improving the explainability and trustworthiness of large language models by uncovering their internal mechanisms to help users better understand and trust them.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Automate Enterprise Day-to-Days With AI Personal Assistant
sessions.abstract:
NVIDIA employees face the real problem of information overload on a daily basis. Communication, project updates, knowledge sharing, and cross-functional collaborations are spread across multiple third-party enterprise cloud providers such as Teams, Outlook, Confluence, Jira, Slack, etc. People spend a lot of time sifting through noisy information.

We propose an AI-based "Personal Assistant" to automatically connect to people's information sources, leveraging AI to identify people's ongoing tasks, to-dos, recent accomplishments, etc. We propose an MCP-based architecture for our app, where the Personal Assistant operates as an MCP client, while leveraging native or partner-hosted MCP servers to tackle various daily task that requires various data access. We also set ourselves as an example of standard MCP-based agentic architecture with a high standard on security.

speakers.name: Lei Yang
speakers.bio: Lei is a staff software engineer on the NVIDIA Enterprise AI and Automation Team.

speakers.name: Di Xu
speakers.bio: Di works on the NVIDIA Enterprise AI and Automation team.

speakers.name: Tan Yu
speakers.bio: Tan Yu is on the Enterprise AI team at NVIDIA. Before joining NVIDIA, He was a staff machine learning engineer in TikTok Advertisements Recommendation and a senior research scientist at Baidu USA. His research interests are information retrieval, recommendation systems, and multi-modal understanding.

speakers.name: Rushang Karia
speakers.bio: Rushang is a machine learning engineer on the NVIDIA Enterprise AI and Automation Team.

speakers.name: Lu An
speakers.bio: Lu works on NVIDIA's Enterprise AI and Automation team.

speakers.name: Anbang Xu
speakers.bio: Anbang Xu is a senior manager at NVIDIA who leads a machine learning team to develop enterprise AI solutions. His research is a mix of applied machine learning and human-computer interaction. He's the associate editor of ACM Transactions on Interactive Intelligent Systems. He has published more than 50 research articles and 25-plus patents, and has received over 3,600 citations. Anbang received his Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Building Industrial Automation Systems, From Simulation to Production
sessions.abstract: Our framework accelerates development, training, and deployment of intelligent robotic systems by integrating digital model creation, synthetic data generation, real-time scene adaptation, LLMs, and vision language models (VLMs). Uing NVIDIA technologies for high-performance simulation and inference enables realistic training and testing of vision models and overall system performance. LLM and VLM integration allows the system to interpret user commands, generate executable code, and enhance scene understanding for intuitive human-machine collaboration. A custom vision system provides 3D data coordinates for robot manipulation in the simulation. The system is already used for industrial purposes, including high-fidelity perception for manufacturing, quality control, and surveillance. Results from simulated and real-world deployments demonstrate the framework's adaptability and potential.

speakers.name: Nikolina Roso
speakers.bio: Nikolina's an academic assistant and Ph.D. student at the University of Zagreb, where her research focuses on human-robot interaction and AI, including evaluating robot control requests, conducting user experience studies, integrating simulated and real robotic systems, and developing human-robot communication with virtual reality and AI. Nikolina also studies large language model-based conversational agents in gaming, performing user testing and analyzing latency effects.

speakers.name: Mirko Sužnjević
speakers.bio: Mirko Sužnjević is working as an associate professor at the Department of Telecommunications, University of Zagreb Faculty of Electrical Engineering and Computing. He has led and participated in over 20 research projects funded by public bodies and private companies. His current research focuses on enhanced interfaces, user interactions, applications of immersive technologies, geolocation, and digital twins. He is a lecturer at several courses at bachelor- and master-level studies covering topics of communication networks, virtual environments, and game development. He is an author of over 50 research papers, and a stakeholder in startups in the areas of computer vision and green energy.

speakers.name: Tommy Jonsson
speakers.bio: Tommy Jonsson is a co-founder of Byte Motion AB, and a researcher at Byte Motion R&D with over 25 years of experience in software engineering and system architecture within the finance, telecommunications, and industrial domains. His expertise spans computer vision, AI-based perception and decision systems, robotics and autonomous systems, embedded and edge computing, and high-performance real-time platforms for industrial automation.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Groove Intelligence: Reviving Vinyl Through Artificial Intelligence
sessions.abstract: Explore how AI and computer vision can read sound visually. This project presents a digital turntable that plays vinyl records using only a 4K camera and deep learning to reconstruct audio from microscopic groove images in real time. Using NVIDIA GPU acceleration, we merge art and technology to preserve fragile analog media and reveal new possibilities for sound restoration, cultural heritage, and multi-modal AI — where image and sound become one expressive language.

speakers.name: Nilton Guedes
speakers.bio: Nilton is an electronic engineer who works for ARTEONIC, developing projects and studies using AI and deep learning for different industries. He graduated from the Polytechnic School of the University of Sao Paulo, with specialties in visual computing/computer graphics and AI/deep learning, and most of his work was dedicated to technology and product development.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: Auto Help: Transforming NVIDIA IT Support Through AI-Powered Ticket Resolution
sessions.abstract: Thousands of inquiry tickets received annually by IT support can be resolved using existing knowledge bases but require manual effort, prolonging resolution times. The Auto Help System automates this process using an LLM-powered architecture with intelligent classification agent and a context-aware, retrieval augmented generation (RAG)-based generator that crafts human-like, accurate responses. A multi-layered guardrail ensures safe and relevant outputs, while an automated workflow updates tickets, notifies users via Slack and email, and escalates when needed. We achieved 89% accuracy where the system delivers a median time savings of 39 minutes per ticket, and on average two days of fulfillment time for customers, and is projected to self-resolve over 8,000 tickets annually, resulting in an estimated $880,000 in savings while enhancing user satisfaction and operational efficiency.

speakers.name: Vinodh Kumar Thanigachalam
speakers.bio: Vinodh Kumar Thanigachalam leads NVIDIA’s IT Service Desk, driving automation, AI-powered service delivery, and operational excellence. He spearheads initiatives like Auto-Help and agentic AI, reducing resolution times from days to seconds while sustaining 99%+ customer satisfaction scores. Passionate about data-driven innovation, he focuses on scaling AI automation and delivering world-class user experiences.

speakers.name: Hridhay Mehta
speakers.bio: Hridhay Mehta, a technology leader in AI at NVIDIA, specializes in leveraging AI and machine learning to enhance IT service management. His expertise includes building conversational AI solutions with LangGraph, LangChain, and ServiceNow’s generative AI tools. Passionate about customer experience, he drives process automation and delivers AI-powered solutions to optimize IT workflows and improve customer engagement.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: AI-Driven Industrial Safety Monitoring: Real-Time Detection and Physics-Informed Safety Risk Reasoning
sessions.abstract: We present an AI-based industrial safety monitoring system that performs real-time personal protective equipment (PPE) detection and worker action recognition. Using physics-informed risk reasoning, the system generates automated safety summaries and actionable recommendations, supporting evidence-based decision-making. Deployed on server and edge platforms, it significantly reduces PPE noncompliance and provides timely insights for operators and managers in complex industrial environments.

speakers.name: Sangho Yoon
speakers.bio: Sangho Yoon is an AI researcher at Guardian AI and holds a master's degree in artificial intelligence. His research interests are reinforcement learning and autonomous driving, and he is currently involved in developing industrial safety systems and robots using computer vision.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: Accessible Quantum Reinforcement Learning for Finance: Benchmarking on NVIDIA RTX GPUs
sessions.abstract: Explore how we make quantum reinforcement learning for finance truly accessible using everyday GPUs. This poster demonstrates how you can train hybrid quantum–classical financial agents on NVIDIA RTX 3090s in just 20 hours, benchmarked on 18 years of Taiwan stock data, revealing how GPU-accelerated quantum simulation unlocks faster, fairer, and more inclusive financial AI research.

speakers.name: Hsiang Wei Hu
speakers.bio: Hsiang Wei is the generative AI senior technical software program manager.

speakers.name: Chisheng Chen
speakers.bio: Chi-Sheng (Michael) Chen is an AI research assistant at Harvard Medical School and Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center, focusing on biomedical AI and EEG-based neuroscience. He is also an AI trainer at OpenAI, contributing to generative AI model training. His research integrates deep learning, quantum machine learning, and GPU-accelerated AI pipelines using NVIDIA CUDA, TensorRT, and cuQuantum.

speakers.name: Joyce Chen
speakers.bio: Joyce is experienced in medical devices, biotechnology, regulatory management, and product development, having secured multiple government grants, including sci-tech and AI pilot programs. She holds a master's degree in life sciences and bachelor's degrees in chemistry and information technology. She's also active in the Taiwan AI Academy and AI Association, promoting generative AI services.

sessions.title: Deploy One-Click Agent Blueprints With NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit on On-Prem AI Infrastructure
sessions.abstract: Astra Blueprints enables one-click deployment of agentic AI workflows on on-premises NVIDIA infrastructure using the NeMo Agent Toolkit. The framework provides blueprint-based templates to standardize workflow creation and configuration while supporting customization across use cases. With continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) and GitOps automation, Astra orchestrates code, image, and deployment life cycles via the Astra Console, integrating with Vault, ArgoCD, and JFrog Artifactory. OpenTelemetry ensures unified observability with real-time metrics, traces, and logs. By abstracting infrastructure complexity and ensuring enterprise-grade scalability, privacy, and compliance, Astra Blueprints accelerates the transition from prototype to production—empowering teams to deploy and monitor multi-agent AI systems efficiently.

speakers.name: Abhinav Balasubramanian
speakers.bio: Abhinav Balasubramanian is a senior full stack software engineer with expertise in building AI-driven chatbot platforms and enterprise applications. He holds an M.S. in computer engineering from San Jose State University, with a focus on data science and distributed systems. Abhinav enjoys tackling complex problems and contributing to impactful projects.

speakers.name: Francis Obiagwu
speakers.bio: Francis is a full stack software engineer at NVIDIA.

speakers.name: Vineeth Kalluru Srinivas
speakers.bio: Vineeth Kalluru is a senior solutions architect in the NVIDIA Azure team. He focuses on integrating NVIDIA software with Azure services and helping enterprises with deploying distributed LLM inference and Gen AI applications. He previously worked on developing deep learning compiler technologies at SambaNova systems and Tensor Processing Unit architecture design at Google. Recently he contributed to designing solutions for deploying NIMs on AzureML and other Azure services.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: GPU-Accelerated Chinese Brain-to-Text Interface Powered by NVIDIA H100 80GB
sessions.abstract: This poster presents the first GPU-accelerated EEG-to-Chinese text generation framework, enabling brain-to-text communication for Chinese speakers with speech disorders. By leveraging the NVIDIA H100’s Transformer Engine and mixed-precision training, the system achieves threefold faster training and 60 TFLOPS performance, making real-time decoding feasible. The model decodes 128-channel EEG signals into open-vocabulary Chinese text, addressing a long-standing gap in multilingual brain-computer interface (BCI) research. This work empowers inclusive assistive communication and sets a new foundation for large-scale, clinically deployable Chinese brain-computer interfaces.

speakers.name: Hsiang Wei Hu
speakers.bio: Hsiang Wei is the generative AI senior technical software program manager.

speakers.name: Chisheng Chen
speakers.bio: Chi-Sheng (Michael) Chen is an AI research assistant at Harvard Medical School and Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center, focusing on biomedical AI and EEG-based neuroscience. He is also an AI trainer at OpenAI, contributing to generative AI model training. His research integrates deep learning, quantum machine learning, and GPU-accelerated AI pipelines using NVIDIA CUDA, TensorRT, and cuQuantum.

speakers.name: Joyce Chen
speakers.bio: Joyce is experienced in medical devices, biotechnology, regulatory management, and product development, having secured multiple government grants, including sci-tech and AI pilot programs. She holds a master's degree in life sciences and bachelor's degrees in chemistry and information technology. She's also active in the Taiwan AI Academy and AI Association, promoting generative AI services.

sessions.title: A Vision Language Foundation Model for MRI Standarization
sessions.abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is widely used in both clinical and research settings. However, there is no standard system for naming or categorizing MRI sequences. This inconsistency is due to differences in imaging protocols, scanner vendors, and institutional practices. As a result, MRI sequences often vary in appearance, and DICOM metadata can be unreliable or conflicting. Clinician oversight is frequently required to verify the correct sequence type. This manual process is time-consuming and prone to error, especially when dealing with large datasets. The goal of this study is to create an efficient streamlined method to standardize the MRI sequences. In this project, we introduce the first foundation model that standardize MRI naming convention across nearly all sequence and anatomy in routine scans.

speakers.name: Dayang Wang
speakers.bio: Dayang Wang is a research scientist in Subtle Medical, as well as an associate editor of Physics in Medicine & Biology and a researcher specializing in medical imaging, artificial intelligence, and multi-modal data analysis. His work bridges deep learning, MRI/CT reconstruction, and image standardization, with publications recognized by international awards for innovation and citation impact.

speakers.name: Thomas Campbell Arnold
speakers.bio: Campbell currently serve as a research scientist at Subtle Medical, where he helped lead multiple AI-based MRI tools from research to U.S. Food and Drug Administration clearance. Campbell also serves as the managing editor of Radiology Access, a biweekly newsletter highlighting the people, research, and technologies advancing global imaging access.

speakers.name: Ajit Shankaranarayanan
speakers.bio: Ajit is chief product officer at Subtle Medical. He received his Ph.D. in biomedical engineering from Case Western Reserve University. Before Subtle, Ajit was COO at HealthLytix, and spent 16 years with GE Healthcare as the global manager for neurology in the Magnetic Resonance division, where he guided the overall vision and goals of GE MR.

speakers.name: Greg Zaharchuk
speakers.bio: Greg Zaharchuk is a radiologist and professor in radiology at Stanford University and a neuroradiologist at Stanford Hospital. His research interests include deep learning applications in neuroimaging, imaging of cerebral hemodynamics with MRI and CT, noninvasive oxygenation measurement with MRI, clinical imaging of cerebrovascular disease, MR/PET in neuroradiology, and resting-state fMRI for perfusion imaging and stroke.

speakers.name: Enhao Gong
speakers.bio: Enhao Gong is a founder and CEO at Subtle Medical, a leading AI and healthcare company from Stanford, California, and the winner of the 2018 NVIDIA Inception AI Award in Healthcare. Subtle Medical aims to significantly improve medical imaging workflows using AI and is one of the few companies with U.S. Food and Drug Administration clearance on AI software. His work on AI that enables faster and ultra-low-dose medical imaging has been featured by top radiology journals.

sessions.title: Efficient SubtleHD-Enhanced MR Images for Improved Dementia Classification
sessions.abstract: Our poster demonstrates new solutions for robust, accelerated dementia classification by training a 3D ResNet34 model on U.S. Food and Drug Administration-cleared SubtleHD enhanced MR images, and explores how to improve performance by accelerating the ConvNeXt enhancement pipeline using NVIDIA TensorRT on various GPUs. This work is timely because it provides a validated, high-efficiency pipeline that resolves the critical trade-off between rapid MR scanning and reliable AI-guided diagnosis in neurology.

speakers.name: Zechen Zhou
speakers.bio: Zechen is a deep learning scientist working to improve medical imaging speed and quality.

speakers.name: Dayang Wang
speakers.bio: Dayang Wang is a research scientist in Subtle Medical, as well as an associate editor of Physics in Medicine & Biology and a researcher specializing in medical imaging, artificial intelligence, and multi-modal data analysis. His work bridges deep learning, MRI/CT reconstruction, and image standardization, with publications recognized by international awards for innovation and citation impact.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging

sessions.title: Efficient Iterative QPE Simulations for Quantum Chemistry Using Distributed State-Vector Methods
sessions.abstract: We demonstrate Iterative quantum phase estimation (QPE) simulations for quantum chemistry up to 31 qubits on 64 GPUs using chemqulacs-gpu, our distributed simulator with NVIDIA cuQuantum backend. Through memory-efficient algorithms and optimized communication strategies, we achieve strong scalability for evaluating quantum algorithms toward fault-tolerant quantum computing.

speakers.name: Sho Nishida
speakers.bio: Sho Nishida is currently an undergraduate student at Osaka University, doing research and development in quantum programming.

speakers.name: Shoma Hiraoka
speakers.bio: Shoma Hiraoka obtained a bachelor’s degree in engineering from Osaka University in Japan. He's currently pursuing graduate studies, focusing on research in algorithms.

speakers.name: Yusuke Teranishi
speakers.bio: Yusuke Teranishi earned a master’s degree in computer science from Osaka University in Japan, specializing in high performance computing. He currently works as a software engineer in Japan, optimizing and accelerating computer programs.

speakers.name: Wataru Mizukami
speakers.bio: Wataru Mizukami is a chemist who's actively developing new algorithms for computational chemistry. He's been developing his research in quantum computing since moving to Osaka University, where the Center for Quantum Information and Quantum Biology, the largest institute of its kind in Japan, hosts diverse research groups.

sessions.title: Automated, DL-Powered Edge-Based, Point-of-Care Ultrasound Tool for Improved CVD Risk Screening
sessions.abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVDs) contribute to over one-fourth of all deaths in India, making early and equitable risk screening a priority. Screening methods relying on blood pressure and risk charts have low sensitivity and detect disease late, while advanced ultrasound tools are confined to tertiary centers by high cost, complexity, and legal constraints. We propose a low-cost, ultrafast A-mode ultrasound edge system powered by automated deep learning (DL) algorithms for arterial wall detection and motion tracking. The system enables multi-marker vascular assessment — including arterial stiffness, endothelial function, and calibration-free blood pressure — at the point of care. Compact, intelligent, and easy to use, the technology promotes field-deployable cardiovascular screening while building indigenous vascular phenotyping datasets to drive personalized and preventive healthcare.

speakers.name: Mohanasankar Sivaprakasam
speakers.bio: Mohanasankar Sivaprakasam is a professor of electrical engineering at IIT Madras and heads the Sudha Gopalakrishnan Brain Center at IIT Madras, where he leads a large-scale multidisciplinary effort to image and map whole human brains at a cellular level. He also heads IITM’s Healthcare Technology Innovation Center that develops and deploys affordable healthcare technologies at scale through strong collaborations with hospitals and industry.

speakers.name: Jayaraj Joseph
speakers.bio: Jayaraj Joseph is an associate professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Madras. With a Ph.D. in electrical engineering from IIT Madras, he leads the Advanced Cardiovascular Technologies Lab and is CTO-in-residence at the Healthcare Technology Innovation Center, IIT Madras. He co-founded VascRisk LLC in the United States, focusing on AI-driven vascular health technologies.

speakers.name: Amit Kumar Gupta
speakers.bio: Amit is a project engineer at the Healthcare Technology Innovation Center, IIT Madras, with over a year of experience in deep learning, large language models, healthcare application development, and designing data processing pipelines. He also has a strong background in data analysis and mentoring early-career professionals.

speakers.name: Sujay Shanthakumar
speakers.bio: Sujay is a research associate at the Healthcare Technology Innovation Center, IIT Madras, with over a year of experience in firmware engineering, healthcare application development, and building performance-constrained edge systems. He has also won multiple national and international hardware-based hackathons.

speakers.name: Raj Kiran V
speakers.bio: Raj Kiran V received his Ph.D. degree from the Electrical Engineering Department of Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Madras, Chennai, with a Best Interdisciplinary Thesis award at the institute level. Raj has over a decade of experience in R&D and two years in product development. His scientific contributions are reflected in the scope and quality of over 70 peer-reviewed publications and seven issued patents (30-plus filed).

speakers.name: Ajay C
speakers.bio: Ajay is an AI researcher at the Healthcare Technology Innovation Center, IIT Madras, with over a year of experience in developing advanced computer vision, natural language processing, and agentic AI systems for healthcare applications. He holds a bachelor’s degree in electrical and computer science engineering and has a strong background in GPU optimization and high-performance deep learning deployment.

sessions.title: GPU-Accelerated Scalable Domain-Specific Data Curation Using VLM
sessions.abstract: We will explain how to implement data curation specialized for a specific domain (e.g., autonomous driving) by linking NVIDIA NeMo Curator and vision language models and utilizing image caption information. Solving the duplicate data problem, which is a bottleneck in AI development, and creating highly accurate datasets that are impossible to do manually are essential to improving model performance. This poster provides cutting-edge, practical insights that will dramatically improve AI learning efficiency and quality.

speakers.name: 山田 直
speakers.bio: Nao engaged in research in the robotics and AI during graduate school, submitting a peer-reviewed paper to an international conference. After gaining development experience as a backend engineer, he now handles AI model development and various technical validations in the image domain at FastLabel.

sessions.title: CAPA: Cross-View Automated Part Alignment via SAM-Guided Point Sampling for 3D Part Segmentation
sessions.abstract: The limited part-level understanding capability of existing 2D semantic segmentation models is a bottleneck in 2D-to-3D part segmentation. So we introduce cross-view automated part alignment (CAPA), a novel zero-shot multi-view framework that systematically unifies state-of-the-art 2D and 3D models to achieve superior, class-agnostic 3D part segmentation. It utilizes ControlNet and Semantic-SAM to generate and segment 36 geometrically consistent, high-fidelity 2D part masks across multiple views. Next, it employs PTv3 for feature extraction and a novel Wasserstein distance-based clustering approach for cross-view integration, ensuring consistent 3D part definition. CAPA achieved 15% class-agnostic mIoU improvement over existing methods on challenging benchmarks: PartObjaverse-Tiny and PartNetE. And it only requires 5-9 minutes per object on an RTX 3090 (vs. 15-20 min for other models).

speakers.name: Yuan-Ming Chang
speakers.bio: Yuan-Ming Chang is currently a student at National Tsing Hua University in Taiwan. He is a master's student in the department of Computer Science, and is interested in developing methods for better 3D part segmentation and object understanding. Previously, he also worked in Siemens as a summer intern.

speakers.name: Che Rung Lee
speakers.bio: Che-Rung Lee joined the Department of Computer Science in National Tsing Hua University in 2008. Che-Rung's research focus includes numerical methods, cloud computing, high-performance computing, heterogeneous computing, and performance optimization. He's a principal investigator (PI) or co-PI of many academic and industry projects, and has published many papers in different areas, such as IPDPS, CloudCom, BigData, ECCV, ACCV, and AAAI.

speakers.name: Che-Rung Lee
speakers.bio: Iuan-Kai Fang has been engaged in interior design for nearly 20 years. His works have won numerous awards including Red Dot, IF, and more. However, he just started to touch the field of deep learning. He's studying the technology of single-image generates to a 3D model. Iuan-Kai received his master’s degree in lighting design from Parsons School of Design, New York and another master’s in interior design from Chung Yuan University, Taiwan.

speakers.name: Bo-hao Zhang
speakers.bio: A master's student at National Tsing Hua University, Taiwan, Bo-hao specializes in computer graphics and deep learning. He's focused on 3D modeling, rendering, and real-time graphics, and passionate about integrating neural networks with graphical applications. Bo-hao aims to develop intelligent systems that revolutionize visual computing across industries, and is committed to advancing how we interact with digital visual information.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - 3D Model Generation

sessions.title: Agility With the Speed of Light, Powered by NeMo Agentic Toolkit
sessions.abstract:
The Agentic Scrum Master (ASM) is a multi-service platform using the NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit to orchestrate workflows autonomously.

Its intelligent core integrates five applications/systems that provide proactive insights like predictive risk assessment and Smart assignee recommendations.
