ASM achieves true portfolio alignment via full bidirectional Jira-Jira Align linking. This autonomously manages epic-to-capability linking and feature hierarchies, solving a major enterprise coordination problem. ASM's development was lead by a Scrum master in the Agentic AI Hackathon at NVIDIA in 2025. A non-developer picked it up and enhanced it further, inspiring others use it to reduce the admin churn and move from reactive scrum mastering to proactive strategic leadership to deliver highest value to customers.

speakers.name: Smitha SL
speakers.bio: TBD

speakers.name: Jahquay Hyles
speakers.bio: Jahquay is a program manager at NVIDIA.

sessions.title: Latency Optimization in BugNeMo
sessions.abstract: High query latency hindered NVIDIA’s BugNeMo Text2SQL co-pilot operating on millions of records. We identified and resolved three key bottlenecks—table hint injection, inefficient LIKE searches, and missing indexes—through AST-based query rewriting, full-text indexing, and targeted index creation. These optimizations reduced median latency from 26.9 to 7.8 seconds, vastly improving responsiveness and scalability of the Gen AI-powered platform.

speakers.name: Jiaxiang Ren
speakers.bio: Jiaxiang works on agentic AI, Gen AI, and Text2SQL for NVIDIA.

sessions.title: From Data to Decisions: Accelerate Supply Chain Planning With Agentic AI (Talk to Your Supply Chain)
sessions.abstract: This poster presents an agentic AI system that enables supply chain planners to ask complex operational questions in natural language and receive accurate, validated answers sourced directly from enterprise datasets. By combining entity normalization, Text2SQL generation, and a reflection-based self-correction loop, the system eliminates manual data stitching across SAP/HANA/Databricks tables and significantly reduces time-to-insight. Deployed across NVIDIA’s Operations Data Platform, the solution has already delivered measurable value with over 3,800 invocations and 1,900-plus planner-hours saved in just three months, demonstrating strong business adoption and scalability. The architecture is designed for extensibility, enabling future multi-retriever reasoning across bill of materials (BOM), logistics, supplier risk, and material readiness workflows.

speakers.name: Jiaxiang Ren
speakers.bio: Jiaxiang works on agentic AI, Gen AI, and Text2SQL for NVIDIA.

speakers.name: Aaditya Shukla
speakers.bio: Aaditya Shukla is a machine learning engineer with several years of experience in the tech industry. Before joining NVIDIA, he worked at IBM and Meta, focusing on various domains like healthcare, legal, AIOps, and recommendation systems. Aaditya enjoys building applications with generative AI to tackle complex problems.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: DeepFrame: Scalable Long-Context Video Understanding With Omnimodal Fusion
sessions.abstract: We present DeepFrame, an end-to-end framework for processing multi-hour videos using omnimodal fusion (video+audio+text) across 128k-256k tokens. Our hybrid GPU infrastructure leverages L40S/H100/B200 for data generation and training, with NVIDIA NeMo Curator for automated video annotation. The system employs Flash Attention 2 (40% VRAM reduction) and mixed precision training (2-3x speedup) on AWS ParallelCluster. We evaluate on LVBench, WorldSense, and custom NARU benchmarks for narrative intelligence. Applications include media moderation, education, security, and retail analytics. Results were announced in January 2026. This work demonstrates breakthrough capabilities in long-form video understanding using NVIDIA's hybrid GPU infrastructure for large-scale AI development.

speakers.name: Aza Kai
speakers.bio: GoogleでクラウドインフラやMLオペレーションに従事し、Google Researchで大規模推薦システムとTPU上でのML研究を行った。現在は企業向け大規模動画検索エンジンプラットフォームSDioを創業。

speakers.name: Vincent Markert
speakers.bio: Vincent is a lead AI engineer in natural language processing, vision language models, and LLMs.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: AI4S Machine Learning Potential: Integrated Workflow With Active Learning, Model Distillation, and Multi-GPU Simulation
sessions.abstract: Our GPU-accelerated pipeline bridges first-principles data generation, neural potential training, and large-scale atomistic simulation. Starting from automated data generation, representative chemical systems are selected and explored through DFT calculations under an active-learning strategy. Then neural interatomic potentials are trained from scratch or refined via multi-head fine-tuning and model distillation, leveraging GPU-optimized cuequivariant architectures for efficient learning. The trained models are deployed in LAMMPS-MLIAP for massive-scale molecular dynamics, achieving 300,000 atom simulations with full GPU acceleration. This framework unifies data, training, and simulation within a single reproducible ecosystem—transforming traditional materials modeling into a scalable, automated workflow capable of continuously improving its accuracy through iterative GPU-based learning.

speakers.name: Siteng Ma
speakers.bio: NVIDIA solutions architect Siteng Ma (马四腾) is responsible for GPU computing solution design and research. His focus areas include the application of GPUs in accelerated computing, deep learning, and data science.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science

sessions.title: Accelerate Speed to Insight: Harness BERTopic and NVIDIA Technology
sessions.abstract: Explore how we accelerated insight extraction on Adoreboard by combining BERTopic with NVIDIA GPU technology. This approach reduces analysis time by 83% and theme overload by two-thirds, delivering faster, clearer, and more actionable insights. Using CUDA, RAPIDS, and containerized pipelines, we enable real-time topic modeling across massive datasets—empowering teams to move from hours of manual analysis to minutes of automated understanding.

speakers.name: Zamena Jaffer
speakers.bio: Zamena is a ‘Google Deepmind Artificial Intelligence’ Scholar working as a senior AI engineer at the cutting edge of emotion AI. A multi-disciplined and enthusiastic individual, Zamena focuses on developing a narrative around AI and ML, with a passion for uncovering compelling stories within data that have the potential to drive real change, blending advanced statistical methods with data-led storytelling for insight actionability and consumption.

sessions.title: AI Planner: An Agentic Workflow for Supply Chain Optimization
sessions.abstract: AI Planner demonstrates an agentic framework for supply chain optimization and is aimed at democratizing operations research to business supply chain planners. We combined discrete optimization with the power of agentic AI and created an end-to-end decision management solution.

speakers.name: Suhas Maddali
speakers.bio: Suhas Maddali is a data and AI professional at NVIDIA, specializing in enterprise forecasting and LLM-driven analytics. He focuses on building intelligent agentic systems to enhance business decision-making.

speakers.name: Anthony Noguera
speakers.bio: Anthony is currently director of AI for business applications at NVIDIA, with over 25 years of experience leading cross-functional teams to develop and deploy business-centric AI, operations research, and SAP solutions. With a special focus on supply chain optimization, he leads high performing teams to leverage quantitative analysis, predictive modeling, and simulation to drive strategic decision-making.

speakers.name: Jing Lu
speakers.bio: Jing Lu is a senior machine learning engineer at NVIDIA, where she works on the design and development of AI-powered planner that leverages large language models and generative AI to orchestrate real-time decision-making across complex, dynamic systems. She holds a Ph.D. in operations research from MIT.

speakers.name: Sandeep Reddy Muddireddy
speakers.bio: With over eight years at NVIDIA, Sandeep serves as a subject matter expert for end-to-end supply planning of advanced semiconductor (chip) products. He has played a key role in designing and implementing complex optimizer solutions in SAP APO, incorporating co-production, die binning, and other cutting-edge planning logics that bridge supply, demand, and manufacturing execution seamlessly across global sites.

speakers.name: Alvaro Sierra Altamiranda
speakers.bio: Alvaro Sierra-Altamiranda is a senior operations research scientist on the AI for Business Applications team at NVIDIA, where he applies optimization and business intelligence to solve complex problems. He earned his Ph.D. in industrial engineering from the University of South Florida, specializing in multiobjective optimization and data-driven decision-making.

speakers.name: Saif Ali Khan
speakers.bio: Saif Ali Khan is a senior operations research scientist at NVIDIA in the AI for Business Applications team. He has a background in supply chain consulting to employ operations research for delivering custom optimization solutions across automotive, food, and semiconductor industries. Saif holds a master's degree from the University of Michigan in Ann Arbor specializing in industrial and operations engineering.

sessions.title: Benchmarking GDS: From Synthetic Loads to Real AI Workflows
sessions.abstract: This poster emphasizes performance benefits of GPU direct storage. We ran the TRT LLM and compared it with and without GPUDirect Storage (GDS), and got better performance with GDS. This solves the major problem of loading data into GPU memory and avoids bounce buffer through CPU. Other GPU-accelerated applications can make use of a similar approach.

speakers.name: Savitha Pareek
speakers.bio: A senior solutions architect at NVIDIA specializing in HPC/AI performance, large-scale benchmarking, and system design, Savitha is an inventor on multiple NVIDIA patents and author of 30-plus publications, with strong community and conference engagement.

speakers.name: Yogendra Sharma
speakers.bio: Yogendra Sharma specializes in AI infrastructure with a focus on large‑scale GPU systems, InfiniBand, and data‑center networking, and high‑performance storage. His work spans end‑to‑end stack design and optimization, from low‑level fabric and storage integration up through AI frameworks and distributed training workloads.

sessions.title: AI-Augmented RFP Benchmarking Suite: Intelligent Performance Prediction for Heterogeneous Supercomputing Clusters
sessions.abstract: We present an AI-assisted benchmarking method that predicts multi-node HPL performance using minimal measurements. The model achieves <3% error up to 32 nodes and detects uncertainty at higher scales, enabling faster and more efficient cluster sizing and request for proposal (RFP) evaluation.

speakers.name: Savitha Pareek
speakers.bio: A senior solutions architect at NVIDIA specializing in HPC/AI performance, large-scale benchmarking, and system design, Savitha is an inventor on multiple NVIDIA patents and author of 30-plus publications, with strong community and conference engagement.

sessions.title: Dynamic Rate Limiting for Multi-Tenant GPU Inference in AI Factories
sessions.abstract: This poster shows how dynamic, priority‑aware rate limiting turns AI factories into efficient, shared GPU inference plants that keep critical workloads fast while boosting overall utilization.

speakers.name: Nic Borensztein
speakers.bio: Nic Borensztein is a generative AI-focused software engineer, leader, and serial entrepreneur based in the San Francisco Bay Area. He is the lead solution architect for Astra, NVIDIA's internal enterprise AI factory. Previously, he was the founder/CTO of CrowdAI, a no-code computer-vision platform.

speakers.name: Collen Roller
speakers.bio: Collen Roller is a senior generative AI solutions architect at NVIDIA, focused on bringing secure, practical generative AI capabilities to the U.S. Department of Defense. Based in the Greater Syracuse–Auburn area, he specializes in natural language processing, conversational AI, and modern DevSecOps for defense environments.

speakers.name: Raghu Nuggehalli Ramesha
speakers.bio: Raghu Ramesha is a solutions architect manager, Gen AI at NVIDIA who helps enterprises design and scale advanced generative and agentic AI applications. He has deep expertise in AI/ML systems, distributed LLM inference, and production-grade architectures for large-scale workloads.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: LAMMPS at Mixed Precision: Speeding Up Material Science Simulations
sessions.abstract: By implementing reduced precision, we improved end-to-end simulation throughput by factors ranging from 1.8x to ~12x across a variety of interatomic potentials in the LAMMPS molecular dynamics code, as measured on an RTX Pro 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPU.

speakers.name: Stan Moore
speakers.bio: Stan Moore is a computational scientist at Sandia National Laboratories specializing in particle-based simulations methods — molecular dynamics and direct simulation Monte Carlo; extending particle-based codes to use the Kokkos performance portability library; and running the codes at extreme scale on HPC platforms.

speakers.name: Evan Weinberg
speakers.bio: Evan Weinberg is a DevTech compute engineer with NVIDIA, focusing on algorithm development and performance optimization in the LAMMPS molecular dynamics application and in the QUDA library for lattice quantum chromodynamics calculations. He received his Ph.D. in physics from Boston University in Massachusetts, and worked on the algorithmic development of multi-scale linear solvers as part of the Exascale Computing Project.

sessions.title: Fast High-Fidelity Urban Airflow Emulator With Explainable Optimized Sensor Placement
sessions.abstract: Explore how we enable real-time, near-DNS-quality reconstruction of urban airflow using only sparse, strategically optimized sensors, along with interpretable sensor layouts that cities can actually deploy. Our approach offers new tools for understanding how wind moves pollutants and heat through dense urban spaces, helping communities make faster and better-informed decisions. This work is especially timely as the World Health Organization reports that air pollution contributes to 7 million premature deaths every year, underscoring the urgent need for accurate, scalable monitoring solutions that support healthier and more resilient cities.

speakers.name: Abhijeet Vishwasrao
speakers.bio: Abhijeet is a highly motivated researcher who's passionate about physics, fluid mechanics, and data-driven modeling. He's pursuing a PhD in engineering mechanics at the University of Michigan, with prior degrees from École Polytechnique and Pune University. His work focuses on computational fluid dynamics, turbulence modeling, and deep generative AI to develop scalable, interpretable tools for complex urban and environmental flow problems.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling

sessions.title: Audio Flamingo: Advancing Audio Intelligence With Open and Powerful Large Audio-Language Models
sessions.abstract: We present new audio-first AI techniques that help language models truly listen—improving how they understand sounds, speech, music, and complex real-world scenes. You’ll discover how large audio-language models can boost perception, reasoning, and creative applications, offering timely solutions for building more natural, multi-modal AI systems.

speakers.name: Sreyan Ghosh
speakers.bio: Sreyan Ghosh is a fourth-year Ph.D. student in computer science at the University of Maryland, College Park, advised by professors Dinesh Manocha and Ramani Duraiswami. His research focuses on enhancing AI models’ ability to understand and reason about audio, with an emphasis on multi-modal and low-resource learning. He earned his master’s degree from the University of Maryland and his bachelor’s degree from Christ University in India.

sessions.title: Generate Rare Driving Scenarios Using World Foundational Models Plus Omniverse
sessions.abstract: Design a world‑model‑driven framework that automatically generates diverse, realistic, and targeted rare driving scenarios, and quantify its impact on downstream perception and planning performance.

speakers.name: Anchal Gupta
speakers.bio: Anchal works as a senior solutions architect at NVIDIA, with over nine years' experience in the domain of deep learning and generative AI. His concentration is in leading automotive applications, with regard to both generative AI and computer vision. With a distinguished track record in deep learning engineering, Anchal brings advanced expertise in developing and deploying AI-driven solutions for automotive industries.

speakers.name: Amit Kumar
speakers.bio: Amit Kumar is an AI leader at NVIDIA, driving agentic and generative AI adoption across industries. With 15 years of experience, he helps organizations build impactful intelligent systems. An IIT Guwahati alumnus and Stanford scholar, he holds six patents and blends deep technical expertise with strategic leadership.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation

sessions.title: PMPP Edition 5 Unveiled: Ask Dr. Wen Mei Your Questions
sessions.abstract:
Join Dr. Wen-Mei Hwu and NVIDIA experts for a live Q&A exploring key updates in the CUDA ecosystem since the last edition of his book Programming Massively Parallel Processors (PMPP). This session offers a rare opportunity to engage directly with Wen-Mei and his team of CUDA engineers, who will answer technical questions, share new features introduced in PMPP Edition 5, and discuss emerging topics under consideration for the sixth edition.

Important:Q&A with NVIDIA Experts are live, interactive sessions where attendees can ask questions directly to a panel of NVIDIA experts in a group setting. The experts address one question at a time for everyone in the room, so each answer is shared with all participants. This format is designed to deliver broadly relevant insights, turning every question into a chance for collective learning.

speakers.name: Wen-Mei Hwu
speakers.bio: Wen-mei Hwu joined NVIDIA in February 2020 as senior distinguished research scientist after spending 32 years at the University of Illinois, where he was a professor, endowed chair, and chief scientist of the Parallel Computing Institute. In 2010, Wen-mei co-authored the popular textbook "Programming Massively Parallel Processors: a Hands-on Approach," thereby setting the foundation for programming and compiler techniques for modern GPU computing.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Ask the Experts: Applications Built for Scale With cuPyNumeric
sessions.abstract:
Want to understand how Python scales efficiently across GPUs and clusters? Join NVIDIA experts for a live, classroom-style Q&A on cuPyNumeric, the technology that extends NumPy to distributed GPU environments. In this session, you’ll have the opportunity to ask questions and discuss how cuPyNumeric maps familiar array operations onto distributed memory layouts, task schedules, and communication patterns. Our engineers will walk through real-world examples from the Linac Coherent Light Source, the Vera Rubin Observatory, and Australia National University, illustrating how these teams built and tuned distributed GPU applications from prototype to production. Bring your questions and dive deep into the engineering that bridges Python productivity with raw GPU performance.

Important:Q&A with NVIDIA Experts are live, interactive sessions where attendees can ask questions directly to a panel of NVIDIA experts in a group setting. The experts address one question at a time for everyone in the room, so each answer is shared with all participants. This format is designed to deliver broadly relevant insights, turning every question into a chance for collective learning.

speakers.name: Manolis Papadakis
speakers.bio: Manolis joined NVIDIA after completing his Ph.D. in computer science at Stanford University, with a focus on distributed and scientific computing. He has since been working on making project Legate/cuNumeric a reality.

speakers.name: Shriram Jagannathan
speakers.bio: Shriram Jagannathan is an engineer at NVIDIA who enjoys solving problems in scientific computing, HPC, and fluid mechanics. Prior to working at NVIDIA, Shriram developed scientific computing tool chains for CPUs and GPUs across multiple scientific disciplines at Microsoft and Ansys.

speakers.name: Andy Terrel
speakers.bio: Andy leads CUDA Python from the product management team. His research focused on domain-specific languages to generate high-performance code for physics simulations with the PETSc and FEniCS projects. Andy is a leader in the Python open-source software community. He's most notably a co-creator of the Dask distributed computing framework, the Conda package manager, the SymPy symbolic computing library, and NumFOCUS foundation.

speakers.name: Irina Demeshko
speakers.bio: Irina Demeshko is a senior software engineer at NVIDIA, working on cuNumeric and Legate projects. Before NVIDIA, Irina was a research scientist and team leader of the Co-Design team at the Los Alamos National Laboratory. Her work and research interests are in the area of new HPC technologies and programming models. Irina received her Ph.D. in mathematical and computer science from the Tokyo Institute of Technology in 2013.

speakers.name: Quynh Nguyen
speakers.bio: Quynh Nguyen is a high performance computing and AI alliance manager at NVIDIA, partnering with national labs, academia, and industry to advance large-scale computing and AI for science. Previously a physicist focusing on X-ray and fusion energy sciences at SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory, she brings deep expertise in HPC workflows, AI-accelerated research, and community engagement to help organizations adopt next-generation computing technologies.

speakers.name: Bo Dong
speakers.bio: Bo Dong is a principal technical product manager on the CUDA team who's responsible for NVIDIA’s go-to-market strategies for distributed computing, including Legate and other technologies/products. He started his career as a C/C++ developer, then moved to product management in various areas like Lightweight Directory Access Protocol, search, Android, big data, virtualization, cloud, etc. He's experienced in compute architecture, system software, distributed system, and cloud compute.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries

sessions.title: What's in Your Developer Toolbox? CUDA, AI, and HPC Profiling, Optimization, and Debugging Tools
sessions.abstract:
Several experts from the tools development and management teams will be available to talk about getting started, best practices, and advanced techniques for application debugging and optimization with NVIDIA developer tools. You can ask questions about anything from high-level concepts to specific tools details, and also discuss plans and suggestions for improving the tools in the future. We can provide a brief overview of the NVIDIA Nsight family of design, profiling, and debugging tools, including an introduction to the latest features and copilot integrations for developing CUDA compute, AI, and HPC applications on the newest platforms.

Important:Q&A with NVIDIA Experts are live, interactive sessions where attendees can ask questions directly to a panel of NVIDIA experts in a group setting. The experts address one question at a time for everyone in the room, so each answer is shared with all participants. This format is designed to deliver broadly relevant insights, turning every question into a chance for collective learning.

speakers.name: Rafael Campana
speakers.bio: Rafael Campana is a senior software engineering director at NVIDIA, where his teams develop several compute-focused software-development products that enable developers tap into the power of the NVIDIA GPUs and embedded platforms. Previously, Rafael shipped tools for multiple gaming consoles and also helped customers ship games and products for these devices. Rafael holds a B.S. in computer science from the University of Texas at Austin.

speakers.name: Holly Wilper
speakers.bio: Holly Wilper's team develops tools that help you be more efficient and effective at harnessing the power of GPUs. For the last 24 years, she's worked on a wide variety of developer tools focused on parallel computing and HPC environments including compilers, profilers, and correctness tools. Holly is a graduate of the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign.

speakers.name: Mahender Hari
speakers.bio: Mahender Hari leads product management team for developer tools at NVIDIA. He helps guide the creation of tools for efficient and effective development on NVIDIA GPUs and systems-on-a-chip. Prior to developer tools, he worked on NVIDIA SHIELD, Microsoft Surface, and Windows.

speakers.name: Sebastien Domine
speakers.bio: Sébastien is a software engineering VP, developer tools, at NVIDIA. He oversees the development of software products dedicated to ease the developer's life and foster the adoption of accelerated computing via world-class developer tools. Prior to NVIDIA, he worked on PC games at GameFX/THQ and 3D digital content creation tools at Katrix and Nichimen Graphics. He holds a Diplôme d'Ingénieur in computer science from EPITA, Paris, France.

speakers.name: Matt Frazier
speakers.bio: Matt Frazier empowers the next generation of experts in accelerated computing by creating coding, profiling, and development assistants and positive feedback loops for developer expertise and productivity. He's built AI solutions for over 20 years, from foundational work in computer vision to most recently productizing video intelligence and code healing products at Google.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Profilers / Debuggers / Code Analysis

sessions.title: Robust LLM Training Infrastructure at ByteDance
sessions.abstract: Large language model training has scaled to tens of thousands of GPUs, and keeps expanding. As cluster sizes continually scale, training runs can incur failures, interrupting the run and requiring mitigation. Examples may include NaN values, CUDA errors, and job hangs that challenge stability. High-efficiency LLM training infrastructure requires minimal interruption, fast diagnosis, and strong fault tolerance—we present ByteRobust, a GPU infrastructure management system tailored for robust LLM training. Leveraging LLM training’s parallelism and characteristics, it achieves large-capacity fault tolerance, prompt fault demarcation, and localization via a data-driven approach to ensure continuous efficient training. Deployed on a production GPU platform, ByteRobust sets a new level in training robustness, with 97% effective training time ratio over a three-month training job on 9,600 GPUs.

speakers.name: Zuquan Song
speakers.bio: Zuquan Song is a software engineer specializing in large-scale distributed training and AI infrastructure. His work focuses on training reliability, observability, and performance optimization across GPU clusters. He is particularly interested in end-to-end trace analysis, debuggability, distributed systems efficiency, and building tools that make large-model training more transparent and dependable.

sessions.title: Master AI Infrastructure At Scale: A DevOps Playbook
sessions.abstract:
Your AI factory is ready for action—now it’s time to put it to work.

In this session, two NVIDIA senior solutions architects will walk you through the essential steps for deploying and managing your AI infrastructure. Aiming at teams beginning their AI factory journey, we'll focus on real-world deployment practices and insights drawn from leading customer environments. Together, we’ll discuss how to streamline cluster onboarding, configuration, and monitoring, laying the foundation for efficient and resilient operations from Day One.

speakers.name: Kristof De Brouwer
speakers.bio: Kristof De Brouwer focuses on supporting networking for AI workloads. With over 20 years of experience in the industry, he specializes in data center switching and accelerated computing. In his current role, Kristof is a subject matter expert in designing and supporting scalable, low-latency network architectures that power modern AI and GPU-based compute environments.

sessions.topic: Data Center / Cloud - Software-Defined Data Center

sessions.title: Achieve Truly Serverless GPUs With libfuse, CRIU, and CUDA-Checkpoint
sessions.abstract: Unpredictable workloads make inference a natural fit for serverless computing (a.k.a. "functions-as-a-service"). But serverless execution only works if new function replicas spin up quickly. Learn the techniques we've developed at Modal to speed up the boots of GPU containers and achieve truly serverless execution for workloads running on thousands of GPUs.

speakers.name: Charles Frye
speakers.bio: Charles Frye builds applications of neural networks and teaches people to do the same. Charles received his Ph.D. from the University of California, Berkeley in 2020 for work on the geometry of neural network optimization. He has since worked at Weights & Biases (acquired by CoreWeave), Full Stack Deep Learning, and Modal. Charles is the author of "The GPU Glossary" and "The LLM Engineer's Almanac."

sessions.title: cuTile Programming
sessions.abstract:
Express your algorithm; cuTile and Tile IR will figure out the threads, data movement, and Tensor cores.

In this talk, we'll present cuTile, NVIDIA's new block-based programming abstraction, and Tile IR, the compiler stack that it is built with. We'll explore examples in C++ and Python from a variety of domains, including inference of a mixture-of-experts large language model and a sparse conjugate gradient solver.

CUDA tile programming divides inputs into local arrays that are processed concurrently by groups of threads. Users write sequential array-centric code, and the framework handles parallelization, synchronization, and data movement behind the scenes. Architecture-specific details are abstracted away—you express your algorithm once in a high-level way, and the system lowers it to the right implementation for any NVIDIA GPU architecture.

speakers.name: Bryce Lelbach
speakers.bio: Bryce Adelstein Lelbach has spent over a decade developing programming languages, compilers, and libraries. He leads programming language efforts and drives the technical roadmap for NVIDIA's HPC compilers and libraries. He's passionate about C++ and is one of the leaders of the C++ community, having served as chair of the C++ Library Evolution group. Outside of work, Bryce is passionate about airplanes and watches.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Building Frontier AI Models for eCommerce with the NVIDIA AI Factory
sessions.abstract:
Large-model technologies are rapidly transforming ecommerce. We'll discuss how we successfully overcome major challenges in large-scale data processing and training efficiency to develop a 245 billion-parameter mixture-of-experts (MoE) model that achieves state-of-the-art performance across Southeast Asian languages and key ecommerce tasks—a critical milestone for AI-native ecommerce. 

See how our model powers traffic distribution, search, content generation, and customer service to enhance efficiency and user experience, while our AI Merchant Bot improves seller efficiency and drives tangible growth. We'll highlight breakthrough search capabilities of large models in ecommerce search, including query rewriting, attribute enrichment, semantic retrieval, and multi-modal search, and how they significantly improve search quality and user intent understanding.

speakers.name: Anxiang Zeng
speakers.bio: Rex, a tech lead at Shopee, specializes in AI-driven transformation and large-model applications across ecommerce platforms. He’s led major AI-native initiatives like search understanding, the AI Merchant Bot, and automated customer service systems, with measurable impact. With a Ph.D. from Nanyang Technological University and 40-plus papers (NeurIPS, AAAI), he combines deep technical expertise with business insights to scale AI innovations.

speakers.name: Peter Wu
speakers.bio: Peter Zhang leads Shopee’s large language model innovation, building Compass, the multilingual AI foundation powering Southeast Asia’s ecommerce ecosystem. He is an active AI researcher with publications in top conferences such as ICLR, ACL, AAAI, and EMNLP, advancing state-of-the-art methods in LLM scaling, alignment, and multi-modal understanding.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Solve the Day 1 Challenge of Landing LLM Inference in Enterprise
sessions.abstract: Alibaba Cloud ACK RBG is a management tool for the full life cycle of large model inference deployment on Kubernetes, designed to address the challenges of large-scale deployment and operations of prefill-decode (PD) disaggregation architecture within Kubernetes clusters. It not only provides features such as multi-role management/collaboration, auto service discovery, topology-aware placement, elastic scaling, and atomic rollout/failure recovery, but also tightly integrates with NVIDIA Dynamo's AIConfigurator and Planner to achieve performance profiling and smarter scaling in complex PD disaggregation and mixture-of-experts (MoE) scenarios. This aims to further improve inference efficiency and reduce costs while meeting service level agreement (SLA) requirements, representing one of the effective ways for large-scale practical deployment of NVIDIA Dynamo.

speakers.name: Kai Zhang
speakers.bio: Kai Zhang is a senior staff engineer at Alibaba Cloud Intelligence who leads the development of Alibaba Cloud container products and cloud-native AI solutions.

speakers.name: Tongyu Guo
speakers.bio: Tongyu Guo is a software engineer at Alibaba Cloud Intelligence who focuses on building cloud-native AI platforms and solutions by extending Kubernetes.

sessions.title: Achieve Peak Tensor Core Performance for GEMM on Blackwell via CUTLASS Python
sessions.abstract: We'll walk you, step by step, through how to achieve peak Tensor Core performance for GEMM. We'll provide tutorial examples to help you get started with the CUTLASS Python, and also demonstrate how to further optimize performance using the latest Blackwell hardware and software features.

speakers.name: Vicki Wang
speakers.bio: Vicki Wang is a software engineer in compute arch, working on developing speed of light deep learning operations, mega kernel fusions, and exploring efficient code generation technology on GPUs.

speakers.name: Manas Sahni
speakers.bio: Manas Sahni is a Senior Systems Software Engineer at NVIDIA, where he works on enabling high performance deep learning operations in AI SW like PyTorch, cuDNN, and TensorRT. Previously, he has worked on Neural Architecture Search, mobile inference, and model optimization. He holds a Master's degree from Georgia Tech.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Medical Speech and Transcription Frontiers: Transforming European Healthcare Delivery
sessions.abstract: This panel explores how AI technologies designed and developed in Europe are revolutionizing the healthcare sector, particularly in the realms of speech recognition and transcription. Panelists will discuss the challenges and opportunities of building robust, privacy-focused, and multilingual AI tools for clinical and patient-facing applications across the continent. Gain insights into cutting-edge advancements, collaboration models, and ethical considerations shaping the future of AI-driven speech solutions in European healthcare.

speakers.name: Cedric Steenbeke
speakers.bio: Cedric leads NVIDIA HCLS (Health Care and Life Sciences) Startups Developer Relations and works with key startups across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. Driving technology adoption and implementation is at the core of what Cedric does.

speakers.name: Alessandro De Sario
speakers.bio: Alessandro is the chief executive officer and a co-founder of Therapyside, an online therapy platform offering mental health support across Spain, Italy, and the U.K. Before this, he held managerial roles at Rocket Internet, working across multiple regions and market stages in the online food delivery industry. He began his career in mergers and acquisitions at HSBC in London after obtaining a master’s degree in management from ESCP Business School Europe.

speakers.name: Alex Brown
speakers.bio: Alex is the strategic alliance healthcare director at PolyAI, partnering with organizations to use voice AI for better patient experience and operational efficiency. Prior to joining Poly, Alex was a VP at 24Haymarket, leading healthcare and life science venture capital investments across the care continuum. Alex holds a biochemical engineering degree from Queen’s and an M.B.A. from London Business School. She serves on the boards of two health companies and advises organizations focused on improving access to care.

speakers.name: María González Manso
speakers.bio: María González Manso is CEO & Co-founder of Tucuvi, a healthcare AI company building autonomous clinical voice agents that help health systems scale patient care safely and efficiently. A biomedical engineer and healthcare entrepreneur, she focuses on deploying responsible, evidence-based AI in real clinical workflows. Tucuvi has already partnered with more than 70 healthcare organizations, and María regularly speaks on healthcare transformation and the safe adoption of AI at scale.

speakers.name: Ricardo Herreros-Symons
speakers.bio: Ricardo Herreros-Symons is a Founding Member & Chief Strategy and Revenue Officer at Speechmatics

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Graduate Fellowship Fast Forward Talks
sessions.abstract: Join a special presentation from our 2025-26 Graduate Fellowship recipients to learn "what's next" from the world of research and academia. Sponsored projects involve a variety of technical challenges, including machine learning, humanoid robotics, computer vision, speech and audio, reliability, drug discovery, agentic AI and trustworthy AI. These minds lead the future in our industry, and we're proud to support the 2025-26 NVIDIA Graduate Fellows. We'll also announce the 2026-27 Graduate Fellows at this session. For more information on the NVIDIA Graduate Fellowship program, visit www.nvidia.com/en-us/research/graduate-fellowships

speakers.name: Jiayi (Eris) Zhang
speakers.bio: Jiayi (Eris) Zhang is a fourth-year Ph.D. candidate at Stanford University, advised by Prof. Doug James. She earned her undergraduate degree in computer science and mathematics from the University of Toronto, under the supervision of Prof. Alec Jacobson. She is a Stanford Reed-Hodgson Fellow, a Young Researcher at the Heidelberg Laureate Forum, an MIT EECS Rising Star, and a recipient of the Adobe Women in Technology Scholarship and the Roblox Graduate Fellowship.

speakers.name: Zhiqiang Xie
speakers.bio: Zhiqiang is a Ph.D. student at Stanford University, advised by Prof. Christos Kozyrakis and collaborating closely with Prof. Kayvon Fatahalian. Before Stanford, he spent a year at Max Planck Institute for Software Systems in Germany as a research intern, and earned his M.S. and B.S. from ShanghaiTech University.

speakers.name: Xiaogeng Liu
speakers.bio: Xiaogeng Liu is a third-year Ph.D. student at Johns Hopkins University, where he is advised by Chaowei Xiao. He received his master's degree in cybersecurity from Huazhong University of Science and Technology in 2023, where he had the privilege of working with Shengshan Hu in the TAI group. During his Ph.D., he has focused on research in trustworthy AI and robust machine learning systems, and has contributed to top international conferences.

speakers.name: Jiawei Yang
speakers.bio: Jiawei Yang is a second-year Ph.D. student in computer science at the University of Southern California, advised by Yue Wang. His research interests lie in developing vision-centric and physics-aware AI systems through self-supervised learning. Before his Ph.D., he earned an M.S. from the University of California, Los Angeles, and a bachelor's degree from Southeast University in China.

speakers.name: Sreyan Ghosh
speakers.bio: Sreyan Ghosh is a fourth-year Ph.D. student in computer science at the University of Maryland, College Park, advised by professors Dinesh Manocha and Ramani Duraiswami. His research focuses on enhancing AI models’ ability to understand and reason about audio, with an emphasis on multi-modal and low-resource learning. He earned his master’s degree from the University of Maryland and his bachelor’s degree from Christ University in India.

speakers.name: Anish Saxena
speakers.bio: Anish Saxena is a fourth-year Ph.D. student working with Prof. Moinuddin Qureshi at Georgia Tech. He obtained his Bachelor of Technology in mechanical engineering from IIT Kanpur in 2021, advised by Prof. Biswabandan Panda. His professional experience includes internships at Intel Labs, Micron, AMD Research, NVIDIA Research, and collaborative projects with Qualcomm and NXP Semiconductors.

speakers.name: Ruisi Cai
speakers.bio: Ruisi Cai is a third-year Ph.D. student in the VITA Group of Electrical and Computer Engineering Department, the University of Texas at Austin, under the supervision of Prof. Zhangyang (Atlas) Wang. Prior to that, she obtained her B.E. from University of Science and Technology of China.

speakers.name: Bill Dally
speakers.bio: Bill Dally joined NVIDIA in 2009 as chief scientist after spending 12 years at Stanford University, where he chaired the computer science department. Bill and his Stanford team developed the system architecture, network architecture, signaling, routing, and synchronization technology that's found in most large parallel computers today.

speakers.name: Seul Lee
speakers.bio: Seul Lee is a third-year Ph.D. student at the Graduate School of AI at the Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology, advised by Prof. Sung Ju Hwang. Prior to her Ph.D., she received a B.S. in biological sciences from KAIST and an M.S. from KAIST Graduate School. During her Ph.D., she has interned at the NVIDIA Fundamental Generative AI Research (GenAIR) team.

speakers.name: Yunze Man
speakers.bio: Yunze is a Ph.D. student in computer science at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, advised by Yu-Xiong Wang and Liangyan Gui. His research interests lie at the intersection of vision, machine learning, and robotics. He received an M.S. in robotics at Carnegie Mellon University, advised by Kris Kitani. He received his B.S. in computer science from Zhejiang University.

speakers.name: Tairan He
speakers.bio: Tairan is a second-year Ph.D. student at Carnegie Mellon University’s Robotics Institute, advised by Guanya Shi and Changliu Liu. He is also a member of NVIDIA’s GEAR group, led by Jim Fan and Yuke Zhu. He received his bachelor’s degree in computer science from Shanghai Jiao Tong University.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: Accelerate Native Sparse Attention Kernels on Blackwell by CUTLASS Python
sessions.abstract: We dive deep into new techniques for accelerating native sparse attention (NSA) kernels on Blackwell using CUTLASS Python, showing how precise control over data movement and sparsity scheduling unlocks dramatic performance gains. Together, we'll explore practical strategies you can apply to push your model training efficiency to the next level.

speakers.name: Akash Mehra
speakers.bio: Akash is a key contributor to the NeMo team, specializing in the LLM training stack. His key focus areas include the critical intersection of algorithmic efficiency and systems performance. Akash's expertise spans both the modeling and the systems aspects of LLM training. He is focused on enhancing the underlying infrastructure for high-throughput, efficient model training, including hands-on experience writing specialized kernels using CuteDSL.

speakers.name: Vincent Zhang
speakers.bio: Vincent focuses on pushing GPU efficiency for modern AI workloads, from custom kernels to production LLM inference. He founded and leads core development of CV-CUDA, and contributes to Torch-TensorRT, TensorRT-LLM, Cutlass-IR, and CuteDSL across the NVIDIA optimization stack.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Supercharging AI With Multi-Gigawatt AI Factories
sessions.abstract: Join NVIDIA experts and industry leaders for a deep dive into the hard-won lessons from building massive AI infrastructure, the newest strategies for scaling network performance across clusters, and forward-looking insights into the next generation of AI networking. This session cuts through the noise with practical guidance and bold predictions shaped by real-world deployments at hyperscale.

speakers.name: Gilad Shainer
speakers.bio: Gilad Shainer focuses on high performance computing and artificial intelligence. He holds multiple patents in the field of high-speed networking. Gilad Shainer holds an M.S. and a B.S. in electrical engineering from the Technion Institute of Technology in Israel.

speakers.name: Peter Salanki
speakers.bio: Peter is the chief technology officer at CoreWeave and the architect of CoreWeave Cloud's Kubernetes-native infrastructure. Peter leads the engineering team in the maintenance and management of CoreWeave Cloud while working closely with teams and clients to help them set up their Kubernetes clusters.

speakers.name: Omar Baldonado
speakers.bio: Omar supports the DC Networking team that designs, develops, and operates Meta’s data center networks and their underlying technologies. This includes the fabric topologies, switch platforms, and control and management software. These DC networks support all Meta applications and services, including large-scale AI clusters for ranking, recommendation, and generative AI workloads.

speakers.name: Yuya Kawakami
speakers.bio: Yuya Kawakami is the Principal Network Architect at Next-Gen Technology Development Division and responsible for the technical strategy and architecture design of AI infrastructure.

speakers.name: Alexis Bjorlin
speakers.bio: Alexis Bjorlin is vice president and general manager for DGX Cloud at NVIDIA. Prior to NVIDIA, Alexis was VP for infrastructure at Meta, where her team was responsible for infrastructure hardware/software co-designed systems, including AI systems, compute and storage platforms, and custom silicon. Before that, she served as SVP and GM of the Broadcom Optical Systems Division.

sessions.title: How to Build End‑to‑End Physical AI Systems for Humanoid Robots
sessions.abstract:
Physical AI brings intelligence to machines, industrial arms, autonomous mobile robots (AMRs), and humanoids, enabling them to perceive, reason, and act in the real world in real time. These robots go beyond scripted behavior to make intelligent decisions on the fly: moving materials, assembling parts, and collaborating safely with people in dynamic environments.

In this session, experts will walk through practical, end-to-end workflows for physical AI development using NVIDIA Isaac and Omniverse technologies. You’ll learn workflows spanning data collection and curation, digital twin creation, model training and scaling, high-fidelity simulation, and deployment to real hardware. We’ll highlight proven toolchains and real-world examples that developers can apply to accelerate the path to smarter, more adaptable robots.

speakers.name: Akul Santhosh
speakers.bio: Akul Santhosh is a solutions architect on NVIDIA’s Robotics SA team, where he supports customers building on the Isaac and Cosmos platforms. Before joining NVIDIA, he earned a master’s degree in computer science from New York University, where he also conducted research in the Robot Perception Lab.

speakers.name: Edith Llontop
speakers.bio: Edith Llontop is a Robotics Technical Marketing Engineer at NVIDIA. She has a degree in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences from UC Berkeley and has experience doing robotics research both at Nvidia's Generalist Embodied Agent Research Lab and Berkeley's Artificial Intelligence Research Lab. She now works on projects that help support researchers, customers, and the broader robotics community leverage Nvidia's robotics technology.

sessions.title: Physical AI for the Real World: A Vision From NVIDIA Robotics Research
sessions.abstract: One of the hardest problems in physical AI is transforming robots from task-specific machines to general-purpose, intelligent collaborators. NVIDIA Research is pioneering a unified AI- and simulation-driven approach to solve this challenge, working from theoretical advances all the way to high-performance deployment. Join this session to learn about research breakthroughs in sim-to-real, real-world learning, model architecture, and model training, as well as key milestones on the path to generalist embodied intelligence for real-world applications. Gain perspectives about where robotics is headed, what technical hurdles remain, and how the next wave of research innovation could unlock entirely new markets.

speakers.name: Yashraj Narang
speakers.bio: Yashraj “Yash” Narang is a research manager in NVIDIA’s robotics research lab. His work lies at the intersection of robotics, AI, and simulation, with recent projects in sim-to-real transfer, deep reinforcement learning, and neural physics models. Prior to NVIDIA, he completed a Ph.D. in materials science and mechanical engineering from Harvard University and a master’s in mechanical engineering from MIT.

speakers.name: Jim Fan
speakers.bio: Jim Fan is the co-lead of the Generalist Embodied Agent Group at NVIDIA. GEAR’s mission is to build generally capable physical AI. Jim's team is spearheading Project GR00T, NVIDIA’s moonshot initiative at building the AI brain for humanoid robotics. Previously, he obtained his Ph.D. at Stanford Vision Lab. His publications have won awards and nominations, including Outstanding Paper at NeurIPS 2022. He spearheaded multiple works on robot foundation models, proficient Minecraft players, and general-purpose agents that learn to operate a computer. Jim's works have been widely featured in news media such as the New York Times, Forbes, MIT Technology Review, TechCrunch, WIRED, and VentureBeat.

speakers.name: Yuke Zhu
speakers.bio: Yuke Zhu co-leads the Generalist Embodied Agent Research (GEAR) Lab at NVIDIA Research. He focuses on developing intelligent algorithms for generalist robots and embodied agents to reason about and interact with the real world. His research spans robotics, computer vision, and machine learning. He received his Master's and Ph.D. degrees from Stanford University.

sessions.title: An Introduction to Robot Simulation
sessions.abstract:
Physical AI is transforming how robots interact with the world. Building these advanced systems requires three computers: compute for training, advanced simulation for validation, and efficient edge deployment. With a simulation-first approach, robots can train across millions of scenarios, generalize faster, and adapt to real-world complexity. 
 
 In this technical deep-dive session, learn core robotics simulation workflows, from building virtual environments to configuring robots, sensors, and physics, using NVIDIA Isaac Sim and Isaac Lab, built with NVIDIA Omniverse libraries. You'll also explore how to generate and leverage synthetic data with NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models to train and validate robot perception and control policies before deploying them in the real world.

speakers.name: Rishabh Chadha
speakers.bio: Rishabh Chadha is an engineer at NVIDIA, where he focuses on integrating deep learning and robotics frameworks for the NVIDIA Jetson platforms. He has a master's degree in robotics from Worcester Polytechnic Institute. His interests primarily include deep learning, medical imaging, and robot perception.

speakers.name: Asawaree Bhide
speakers.bio: Asawaree Bhide is a technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA, working on AI and robotics on the Jetson platform. She did her master’s studies in computer science at Georgia Tech and is interested in solving complex perception tasks for autonomous embodied agents.

sessions.title: A New Paradigm: Verifiable AI
sessions.abstract:
Autonomous AI requires a new security model. From AI agents to robots, we need to rethink how we should trust these systems to deliver the full promise of their automation. Can we verify their operations, security, and alignment as we grant them access to our most coveted data and delegate them critical tasks?.

As multi-agent AI systems span boundaries from cloud to edge, the challenge of maintaining trust and security across every layer of the stack—digital and physical—has never been greater. This session introduces EQTY’s verifiable AI runtime, a new approach that continuously ensures verified integrity from model training and inference to deployment in real-world, physical AI environments, including the verification of AI sovereignty.

speakers.name: Ofir Arkin
speakers.bio: Ofir Arkin is a renowned information security expert with a career spanning academia, consulting, and executive roles. He's passionate about creating innovative products that address customer needs, and has introduced several industry-first technologies. Known for his dedication to mentoring, Ofir has authored numerous influential research papers and articles, and is a recognized speaker in the information security community.

speakers.name: Jonathan Dotan
speakers.bio: EQTY Lab builds tools to reinvent trust in AI. As a serial founder, Jonathan has spent over 25 years navigating the intersection of technology and governance as a C-suite leader, investor, and researcher. He is also the founding director of The Starling Lab at Stanford University, where he leads pioneering applied research on hardware-accelerated cryptography. He teaches the first course on digital authenticity and provenance, and has co-written standards for Adobe and the Linux Foundation that are used across civil society and industry, including foundational models from OpenAI and Google.

speakers.name: Caroline Yap
speakers.bio:
Caroline Yap is a pioneering force in the field of artificial intelligence, recognized globally for her expertise in applied AI. As the CTO of Sovereign AI, Caroline is at the helm of a visionary initiative that harnesses the power of AI to drive human progress, business change and societal advancement.

Drawing on her extensive background in AI integration, networking, security, and cloud infrastructure, Caroline's work at Sovereign AI focuses on developing innovative AI solutions that align technological advancements with human-centric goals. Her approach combines cutting-edge AI applications with a deep understanding of business dynamics and societal needs with data privacy and security at the forefront.

Prior to joining, Caroline held the position of Global Managing Director for AI Business and Applied Engineering at Google, where she led transformative AI initiatives for enterprises worldwide.

A respected voice in the AI community, Caroline continues to serve as a trusted advisor to governments and policymakers on ethical AI innovation and contributes to education via her mentorship role at the University of Cambridge. Her unwavering commitment to responsible AI development has solidified her position as a thought leader, influencing the trajectory of AI in both business and society.

Caroline's unique blend of technical acumen, strategic vision, and ethical leadership continues to shape the future of AI, ensuring that technological progress moves in lockstep with human advancement.

sessions.topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI

sessions.title: Building the Next Wave of AI-Enabled Robots
sessions.abstract: Building generalist robots is a complex AI challenge—developers must ensure these machines can safely adapt across industries, from manufacturing floors to hospital corridors. Join this lightning session featuring founders and technologists from robotics startups in the NVIDIA Inception program as they share real-world lessons from tackling these problems at scale. Hear how they're leveraging NVIDIA CUDA-accelerated libraries, foundation AI models, and simulation frameworks built with Omniverse to build and deploy AI-driven robotics products—what worked, what didn't, and why. Gain insights on architectures, deployment patterns, and measurable customer impact across manufacturing, healthcare, retail, and food service.

speakers.name: Akhil Docca
speakers.bio: Akhil Docca is a senior product marketing manager for Omniverse at NVIDIA. Akhil has an M.B.A. from the University of California Los Angeles Anderson School of Business and a bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering from San Jose State University.

speakers.name: Steve Xie
speakers.bio: Steve is founder and CEO of Lightwheel, a company leading simulation infrastructure for embodied AI and a pioneer in generative AI-powered simulation for robotics. He holds a B.S. from Peking University and a Ph.D. from Columbia University. Steve has led simulation efforts at NVIDIA and Cruise, where he built end-to-end synthetic data pipelines that set industry benchmarks for realism, scalability, and sim-to-real transfer.

speakers.name: Ken Macken
speakers.bio:
Ken Macken is the CEO and founder of Workr Labs, where he is focused on deploying the world’s largest robotic
workforce. He has over 20 years of international experience in manufacturing, robotics, and machine vision, with work spanning diverse sectors including photonics, photovoltaics, automotive, and defense across the United States, China, and Australia.

speakers.name: Rajesh Radhakrishnan
speakers.bio: Rajesh Radhakrishnan is an AI and robotics leader with 15 years of experience across agricultural robotics, biotech, human–computer interaction, and mobility. As the first co-founding engineer at Blue River Technology, he built pioneering AI and computer-vision systems that helped drive its landmark acquisition by John Deere. He now leads last-mile delivery innovation at Serve Robotics (Nasdaq: SERV) and advises and invests in early-stage robotics startups globally.

speakers.name: Kevin Peterson
speakers.bio: Kevin Peterson is co-founder and CTO of Bedrock Robotics, where he leads the development of autonomous systems for heavy construction equipment. Previously, he was head of perception for Waymo's commercial trucking division. He also co-founded Marble, a robotics company he led as CEO through its acquisition by Caterpillar. Earlier in his career, he served as CTO at Astrobotic, developing lunar landers for NASA. Kevin holds an M.S. in electrical and computer engineering from Carnegie Mellon University.

speakers.name: Yao Lu
speakers.bio: Yao is Head of ML Infra at physical intelligence. His mission at PI is to build the next generation physical AI infra. Before that he was a distinguished research scientist at NVIDIA Research. He worked on vision language model and published a serious papers including VILA, NVILA, LongVILA, VILA-U, VILA^2, SparseVILA etc. Before joining NVIDIA, he was a TLM at Google Deepmind where he worked on reinforcement learning, imitation learning on embodied AI. He co-led the SayCan, RT-1, RT-2, works that laid foundation of VLA models.

sessions.title: Security for the AI-First Enterprise
sessions.abstract: As AI transforms every layer of modern computing, security must evolve in parallel—shifting from a separate function to an embedded capability woven into every stage of innovation. Today’s CISOs and security leaders are moving beyond reactive defense, redefining their roles as proactive business enablers who accelerate safe adoption of AI, cloud, and emerging technologies. This talk explores how that shift is reshaping security architectures, operating models, and organizational influence. Finally, it highlights why meaningful collaboration across industry, academia, and government is no longer optional. Addressing global cyber threats at AI scale demands shared intelligence, coordinated standards, and partnerships that blend cutting-edge research with real-world deployment. Together, these forces define the future of secure innovation.

speakers.name: David Reber
speakers.bio: David Reber is a security executive with experience directing cybersecurity and product security for technology companies and the U.S. public sector. David currently serves as the chief security officer for NVIDIA. Previously, David served as senior director of cybersecurity for Nutanix, director of security and solutions for Frame, and spent more than 10 years providing formation technology and security leadership to the U.S. government.

sessions.topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI

sessions.title: Disney’s Robotic Characters: From the Screen to Reality via Physical AI
sessions.abstract: The translation of animated characters like Olaf, or the design of characters like our BDX Droids, requires balancing artistic fidelity with the constraints of the physical world. This talk explores how these robotic characters achieve that balance through the integration of modular mechatronics and deep reinforcement learning. Leveraging GPU-accelerated simulation to handle complex mechanisms, we train control policies that enable our characters to self-balance and self-regulate temperature. We further demonstrate progress in believable, autonomous navigation and human-robot interaction driven by onboard perception.

speakers.name: Moritz Baecher
speakers.bio: Moritz Bächer is the associate lab director of Disney’s Zurich-based robotics team, where he leads a strategic program focusing on the development of novel model- and learning-based tools for the design and control of believable robotic characters. His core expertise is the optimal design and control of both soft and rigid systems, using a combination of differentiable simulation and reinforcement learning. Prior to joining Disney, Moritz received his Ph.D. from the Harvard School of Engineering and Applied Sciences and his master’s degree from ETH Zurich.

sessions.title: Securing Coding Assistants
sessions.abstract: As AI-powered coding assistants become more capable—and begin interacting directly with developer environments—their security risks grow alongside their utility. This session offers a practical demonstration of how vulnerabilities can emerge when these assistants gain computer-use capabilities, exposing new attack surfaces inside the software development life cycle. We then dive into Cursor’s real-world mitigation strategies, showing how hard security controls around critical functions create a flexible balance between capability and risk. Finally, we connect lessons from AI security research, secure application design, and red-teaming exercises to illustrate how organizations can safely unlock higher levels of automation. Together, these practices chart a path toward powerful AI development tools with far lower operational risk.

speakers.name: Rich Harang
speakers.bio: Rich Harang is a principal security architect at NVIDIA, specializing in ML/AI systems, with over a decade of experience at the intersection of computer security, machine learning, and privacy. Rich's work has been presented at USENIX, BlackHat, IEEE S&P workshops, and DEF CON AI Village, among others, and has also been featured in The Register and KrebsOnSecurity.

speakers.name: Tom Daniels
speakers.bio: Tom Daniels is a seasoned cybersecurity professional currently serving as the chief information security officer (CISO) at Cursor, where he leads efforts in safeguarding sensitive financial data. With over a decade of experience in various security roles, he has honed his skills in application security, cloud computing, and incident response.

speakers.name: Becca Lynch
speakers.bio: Becca Lynch is an offensive security researcher on the NVIDIA AI Red Team, where she works to secure AI models and model infrastructure. Her prior work includes applied machine learning for anomaly detection at Duo Security, and leveraging data science expertise to build robust and actionable processes for threat hunting and intelligence. Becca’s work has been presented at Black Hat, DEF CON AI Village, and CAMLIS.

speakers.name: Henry Wildermuth
speakers.bio: Henry Wildermuth is a software engineer at Cursor, where he works primarily on product security across application, infrastructure, and AI systems. As the company’s first security hire, he has helped establish foundational security practices and build systems designed to scale with the product. Previously, Henry was a security software engineer at SpaceX working on Starlink, where his work spanned embedded systems, backend services, cryptography, and global infrastructure supporting satellite internet at scale.

sessions.topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI

sessions.title: Securing AI Application: Red Teaming the Models and Applications
sessions.abstract: As organizations integrate AI into production systems, new and complex security challenges are emerging. In this panel discussion, we’ll explore where AI models are most vulnerable—data poisoning, evasion, and prompt-based exploitation—and how adversarial red teaming can help identify and mitigate these risks before attackers do. Learn how research–industry collaboration drives the development of scalable defenses and practical tools for securing machine learning pipelines. By the end of this session, you’ll have a high-level playbook for building resilient AI systems in an evolving threat landscape.

speakers.name: Daniel Rohrer
speakers.bio: Daniel Rohrer has held a variety of technical and leadership roles in his 24 years at NVIDIA. Daniel has taken his integrated knowledge of "everything NVIDIA" to hone security practices through the delivery of advanced technical solutions, reliable processes, and strategic investments to build trustworthy security solutions. He has a master’s degree in computer science from the University of North Carolina.

speakers.name: Nicole Carignan
speakers.bio: Nicole is senior vice president for security and AI strategy, field chief information security officer at Darktrace. She provides technical strategic guidance and expertise in cybersecurity, threat research, AI, and data science to customers, partners, and organizations within Darktrace. An expert in the secure and responsible application of AI in cybersecurity, Nicole engages in product innovation and advisement, thought leadership, research, and AI community engagement to ensure Darktrace delivers solutions that meet their customers' evolving needs. Her insights have been cited by global cybersecurity publications, and she's a frequent speaker at industry conferences.

speakers.name: Omar Santos
speakers.bio:
Omar focuses on AI security, cybersecurity research, incident response, and vulnerability disclosure at Cisco. He is the co-chair of the Coalition for Secure AI and a board member of the OASIS Open standards organization. Omar is also the chair of the OpenEoX and the Common Security Advisory Framework (CSAF) technical committee. His work led the creation of the CSAF ISO standard.
 
Omar is the author of over 25 books, 21 video courses, and over 50 academic research papers. Prior to Cisco, he served in the U.S. Marines, focusing on Command, Control, Communications, Computer, and Intelligence (C4I) systems.

speakers.name: Heather Adkins
speakers.bio: Heather Adkins is a 23-year Google veteran and a founding member of the Google Security Team. As VP, Security Engineering and head of Google’s Office of Cybersecurity Resilience she has built a global team responsible for maintaining the safety and security of Google’s networks, systems and applications. She has an extensive background in practical security, and has worked to build and secure some of the world’s largest infrastructure.  She is the co-author of Building Secure and Reliable Systems (O’Reilly, 2020), was deputy chair of CISA’s Cyber Safety Review Board, and has advised numerous organizations on adopting modern, defendable architectures.

sessions.topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI

sessions.title: How Virtual Twins are Shaping the Next Industrial Revolution
sessions.abstract:
AI-powered virtual twins, science-validated industry world models, and virtual companions are shaping the next frontier for industries.

Learn how Dassault Systèmes’ industry world models, agentic 3DEXPERIENCE platform, and virtual twin technologies—combined with NVIDIA AI infrastructure, open models, and libraries—are establishing new ways of working and transforming  biology, materials science, engineering,  and manufacturing.

speakers.name: Florence Hu-Aubigny
speakers.bio:
Florence Hu-Aubigny is the executive vice president of research & development at Dassault Systèmes. She is responsible for the global R&D strategy, driving the evolution of the 3DEXPERIENCE platform, and focusing on delivering next-generation virtual twin experiences. Her strategy centers on seamlessly integrating cutting-edge technologies like generative AI, while upholding the company’s foundation of solid scientific principles.

With over 30 years of experience at Dassault Systèmes, Florence played a pivotal role in scaling and democratizing 3D technology through the development of industry-leading cloud-native solutions for modeling, simulation, and collaboration.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: The State of the Art of Quantum Chemistry on GPUs: EXESS, Exascale, and Floating-Point Emulation on Blackwell
sessions.abstract: Quantum chemistry has long been the gold standard for molecular accuracy—and the bottleneck for scale. This session presents EXESS, a GPU-native quantum chemistry platform that establishes the state of the art of quantum chemistry on GPUs. Building on ACM Gordon Bell Prize–winning work, EXESS achieved record double-precision floating-point performance and scaled electronic-structure methods to exascale on tens of thousands of GPUs. These advances transform quantum-accurate biomolecular simulation from an academic endeavour into a platform capability. We highlight the scaling principles behind EXESS and show how floating-point emulation on NVIDIA Blackwell accelerates double-precision-critical workloads, enabling routine generation of quantum-accurate data for AI model training, accelerated drug and discovery, and automated, in-silico molecular design workflows.

speakers.name: Giuseppe M. J. Barca
speakers.bio: Giuseppe M. J. Barca is an expert in HPC, AI, and quantum chemistry applied to drug discovery. His team was awarded the 2024 ACM Gordon Bell Prize for enabling the first quantum-accurate biomolecular simulations at exascale. In 2025, he was awarded the WATOC Dirac Medal for pioneering GPU algorithms that accelerate computational chemistry. He is co-founder and head of research at QDX Technologies, and a professor at Monash and the ANU.

sessions.title: Building and Scaling AI Factories With Digital Twins and Robotics
sessions.abstract: Foxconn is collaborating with NVIDIA to build an AI factory in Texas for the production of AI servers. Foxconn will leverage NVIDIA Omniverse libraries to build a digital twin environment for simulation and real-time monitoring to rapidly scale up production lines, and will introduce humanoid robots and a high degree of automation for the first time. All these will first be simulated in NVIDIA's environment and optimized in a visual setting to help Foxconn create a safer, more efficient, and more flexible manufacturing environment. It's committed to building this factory into a benchmark plant that can serve as a model for others to follow.

speakers.name: Leo Guo
speakers.bio: Leo leads the FODT (Fii Omniverse Digital Twin) project and is general manager of the Fii Robotics business unit. He guides the team using Omniverse, Replicator, and Isaac Sim/Manipulator/Lab to design and train the arm behaviors of an industrial humanoid robot. His software background is from the Stanford Computer Science Master's program.

sessions.title: Build Physical Intelligence: Open Collaboration Across Robotics Ecosystems
sessions.abstract: This panel brings together leaders to reveal what it truly takes to build a robotics company today. We’ll discuss the integration of real-world data, advanced models, investment, and cloud solutions to accelerate robotics innovation, deployment, and impact on society. Gain insights into the challenges and opportunities of building intelligent, adaptable robots through shared infrastructure, and strategic partnerships, and how open collaboration can make a difference.

speakers.name: Markus Mueller
speakers.bio: Markus is a Senior Investment Manager at Hyundai CRADLE Berlin, leading the complete lifecycle of European investments: from strategy to investment execution, portfolio management, and exits. As a matchmaker between external startups and internal stakeholders, he drives strategic collaboration in mobility, robotics, energy. He previously worked at BMW Group in Tech Foresight & Scouting, and in Business Model Innovation. He holds a MSc in Materials Science (TU Munich), as well as a MBA (Collège des Ingénieurs, Paris).

speakers.name: Sandra Skaff
speakers.bio: Sandra Skaff is senior alliances and ecosystem manager at NVIDIA where she leads robotics ecosystem development and works with cross-functional teams to support partners and customers in developing their products using NVIDIA’s Isaac platform. Prior to this role she was global lead of academic research engagements including the NVAIL program, whereby she was responsible for setting the strategy and managing a portfolio of engagements with top research labs in the areas of AI, Robotics, and Data Science. She has 10 years of experience in computer vision and machine learning R&D at both large corporations and startups. Sandra received the PhD and MEng. degrees in Computer Vision from McGill University, Montreal, Canada. She has authored and co-authored 20 peer-reviewed conference and journal publications and has been granted 13 patents.

speakers.name: Steven Palma
speakers.bio: Steven's an established engineer with over six years of experience in ready-to-deliver, end-to-end robotics projects, with a record of blending technical and management expertise in an interdisciplinary and intercultural environments. His professional experience spans fields including open-source software and certified software, autonomy, perception, networking, machine learning, and electromechanics.

speakers.name: Nicolas Keller
speakers.bio: Nicolas Keller is Co-Founder & CEO of Poke & Wiggle, a startup tackling the data and deployment bottlenecks of robotic foundation models. Previously, he was part of the founding team at Franka Robotics, whose robots are the standard platform for AI robotics in academia and industry worldwide. After a stint as a VC, he joined Meshcapade to build the human layer of physical AI before founding Poke & Wiggle.

speakers.name: Evan Helda
speakers.bio: Evan Helda is the head of physical AI at Nebius, where he leads business development, product strategy, and go-to-market for physical AI and robotics. He is also the creator of Dream Machines, a newsletter and podcast exploring physical AI and the future of the human experience. Before joining Nebius, Evan founded and led AWS’ Spatial Computing business, where he drove go-to-market and product strategy for digital twins, simulation, and spatial data services— technologies that now form the backbone of many emerging physical AI workloads.

speakers.name: David Höller
speakers.bio: David Hoeller is CTO and co-founder of Flexion Robotics. He earned his PhD from ETH Zurich on representation learning for navigation and locomotion with quadrupedal robots. He worked at NVIDIA from 2017 to 2024, advancing from Research Scientist to Research Manager. He published extensively on learning-based control for legged robots and was a key contributor to Isaac Gym and related simulation tools. His research demonstrated that robots trained in massively parallel simulation can achieve agile navigation through demanding environments. At Flexion, he directs technical development while actively contributing to the company's control and manipulation advancements.

sessions.title: Build an Open Surgery Robot AI Platform With NVIDIA Frameworks
sessions.abstract: This session unveils a groundbreaking initiative to build an open, extensible AI platform specifically for surgery robots. This effort introduces a collaborative, open platform built on NVIDIA’s AI infrastructure, leveraging Isaac for Healthcare for photorealistic surgical simulation. It establishes the first unified framework tailored to surgery robotics, featuring modular robot control, multi-modal perception (vision and kinematics), and vision-language-action (VLA) pipelines—all designed for real-world deployment on accelerated computing platforms. Researchers worldwide can now generate high-fidelity synthetic datasets, train policies via imitation and reinforcement learning, and validate algorithms against shared benchmarks enabling reproducible, scalable research in open surgery automation for the first time.

speakers.name: Tao Li
speakers.bio: Tao Li, CTO of MicroPort MedBot (Group) Co., Ltd., has over 14 years of research experience in surgical robotics. He has led the development of multiple landmark systems including the world’s first remote surgical robot and MedBot’s first laparoscopic and orthopedic surgical robots. He has directed more than industrial key projects, participated in drafting international standards, and won multiple high-level science and technology awards. He has published over 10 papers, edited 1 monograph, and held more than 230 patents including over 60 international PCT patents.

speakers.name: Chao He
speakers.bio: Dr. He Chao is the founder, Executive Director, and CEO of Shanghai MicroPort Medical (Group) Co., Ltd. He serves as the Chinese expert on the IEC Surgical Robotics Standards Working Group and chief drafter of China’s first national standard for surgical robots. A pioneer in China’s surgical robotics field, he directs the Shanghai Minimally Invasive Surgical Robotics Engineering Center, has led 17 major projects, published 12 papers, holds 169 invention patents, and received a first-class provincial/ministerial technology award. He conducted research at Johns Hopkins University and is a recipient of the Shanghai Outstanding Young Talents and Rising Star programs.

sessions.title: The Generative AI Revolution: AI スーパーコンピュータ設計のパラダイムシフト
sessions.abstract:
生成AIは、基盤となる Transformer モデルから、複雑なマルチモーダルシステム、さらには自律的 Agentic AI へと急速な進化を遂げています。この変化に対応し、未来の AI を創出し続けるためには、AI スーパーコンピュータの設計を大きく変える必要がでてきています。本セッションでは、生成 AI 時代における、AI スーパーコンピューターのアーキテクチャの進化とそれを支える HW/SW の協調設計について説明します。

・アーキテクチャの進化: 高度化する生成 AI がもたらす爆発的な計算需要に対し、AI スーパーコンピュータのアーキテクチャはどのように進化すべきかを説明します。具体的には、従来の「学習中心」のパラダイムを超え、大規模な学習と推論のハイブリッドワークロードをネイティブにサポートする「生成 AI 時代の AI スーパーコンピュータ」が不可欠となっており、それを実現するアーキテクチャの進化について説明します。
