・相乗効果を生むプラットフォームの協調設計: 高度な生成 AI の開発には、AI創出のエンジンである NVIDIA Blackwell GPU、それらを繋ぐ神経網としての Spectrum-X の高速なネットワークファブリック、それらを制御する AI 開発のオペレーティングシステムとしての NVIDIA NeMo や NVIDIA TensorRT 等の先進ソフトウェア群の三位一体の協調設計が重要になります。それらの技術と設計を具体的に説明すると共に、何故緊密に協調設計された AI スーパーコンピュータが、単なる選択肢ではなく、生成AI時代を勝ち抜くための「必須要件」であるのかを説明します。

speakers.name: Takatoshi Kitano
speakers.bio: NEC入社後、中央研究所にて様々な大規模分散システムの研究開発に従事。その後、機械学習の研究テーマリーダーなどを経て、現在はNECのAIスーパーコンピューターの企画・設計・開発・運用を統括。

sessions.topic: Data Center / Cloud - Software-Defined Data Center

sessions.title: Best Practices of Multi-Modal and Vision Generation Training in M-Core
sessions.abstract: Learn our practical strategies for post-training multi-modal vision language models (VLMs) and diffusion models, leveraging M-Core to enhance GPU utilization and improve model performance. Explore our effective reinforcement learning workflow, which spans from training to resource scheduling, maximizing GPU resource efficiency.

speakers.name: Junyu Wu
speakers.bio: Junyu is a senior AI infrastructure engineer at Tencent WeChat, focusing on the training and deployment of generative models. He has have supported the development and implementation of various generative models in WeChat's business scenarios, including model scaling, reinforcement learning, and resource optimization. Junyu's goal is to drive innovative AI applications in real-world businesses, delivering smarter and more efficient services to users.

sessions.title: Optimize Mixture-Of-Experts for Low-Latency Inference Decode on Blackwell
sessions.abstract: This ninja-level tutorial will guide you through the techniques to build a low-latency inference kernel for Blackwell using CUDA and PTX. You will learn about techniques like programmatic dependent launch, prologue minimization, lightweight CGA parallelism, and operand swapping in the Blackwell Tensor Cores.

speakers.name: Allard Hendriksen
speakers.bio: Allard is a senior developer technology who specializes in the GPU memory subsystem.

speakers.name: Petrick Liu
speakers.bio: Petrick joined NVIDIA in 2020. He focuses on developing highly efficient Tensor Core-related operations library.

sessions.title: Best Practices for Scaling Inference
sessions.abstract: As organizations scale toward population-level AI adoption, running inference reliably, efficiently, and cost-effectively becomes essential. This session covers best practices for scaling inference across platform, model, and application layers, using real-world insights and NVIDIA technologies like DGXC-Lepton and NVCF. We’ll address challenges in GPU capacity, autoscaling, model optimization with NVIDIA S/W stack, concurrency, architecture, and observability. Learn how to use proven, field-tested strategies for scaling current AI workloads or launching new ones at scale.

speakers.name: Jalaj Thanaki
speakers.bio: Jalaj is a senior solutions architect at NVIDIA.

speakers.name: Anish Mukherjee
speakers.bio: Anish is a generative AI solutions architect at NVIDIA.

sessions.topic: Data Center / Cloud - Software-Defined Data Center

sessions.title: European Robotics Startup Showcase
sessions.abstract: Learn how European startups are leveraging NVIDIA platforms to advance robotics and automation across real-world industries. From teaching robotic hands dexterity with scalable world models and automating smart agriculture using edge AI to reinventing hospitality operations through simulation-based design and applying adaptive robotics to healthcare and rehabilitation—discover how these innovators integrate foundation models, teleoperation, and digital twins to unlock new possibilities for autonomous systems in manufacturing, agriculture, services, and beyond.

speakers.name: Josef Franko
speakers.bio: Josef Franko is the Founder and CEO of AI.Land and holds a PhD in Robotics Engineering. Founded in Germany in 2020, AI.Land develops and deploys humanoid upper-body robots for mixed vegetable cultivation, enabling field-to-fork market garden systems. During his PhD, he served on the board of the MASCOR Institute and deployed robotic systems in wind tunnels for multiple Formula One teams.

speakers.name: Snehal Deshmukh
speakers.bio: Snehal supports strategic initiatives for deep-tech startups at NVIDIA, driving ecosystem partnerships and fostering AI innovation to accelerate the adoption of cutting-edge technologies. With a background in Electronics and Computer Science Engineering, she brings a global, technically grounded perspective to working with startups, partners, and industry leaders.

speakers.name: Chang Liu
speakers.bio: Chang Liu is a visionary entrepreneur who merges cutting-edge academic research with the fast-paced world of high-tech startups. As the founder, CEO, and chief designer of Extend Robotics, he champions the next generation of intuitive human-robot interfaces and data-driven physical AI for dexterous robots. Chang guides a high-powered, cross-functional team at the forefront of disruptive innovation, blending immersive technology, robotics, artificial intelligence, and cloud infrastructure into transformative solutions. He has pitched on BBC Dragon’s Den stage and secured interest from Peter Jones, and been recognized by The Economist as a Young Innovator.

speakers.name: Elvis Nava
speakers.bio: Elvis Nava is the co-founder and CTO of mimic robotics, a Zurich-based startup working on scalable AI models for robotic manipulation, which raised more than $20M in funding from leading venture capital investors. Originally from Italy, he completed a MSc in Data Science at ETH Zurich and a PhD as an ETH AI Center Doctoral Fellow. During his career, he published at popular venues such as ICML, ICLR and TMLR.

speakers.name: Sandy Hefftz
speakers.bio:
Sandy Hefftz is a robotics and Physical AI leader with over 20 years of experience building and scaling autonomous systems, from space and aerial robotics to large-scale industrial automation.
As the Founder and CEO of Bellboy, Sandy leads a Physical AI company developing learning robots that operate within real-world buildings. Bellboy’s robots are currently deployed across Europe, performing inspection and operational tasks in hotels and commercial facilities. Her work focuses on embodied intelligence, enabling robots to learn from human demonstrations and operate reliably in complex environments alongside people.

speakers.name: Daniele Pucci
speakers.bio: Daniele Pucci is CEO of Generative Bionics, building humanoid robots for real-world industrial collaboration and embodied AI. Previously at the Italian Institute of Technology, he led research on Physical AI, whole-body control, physical interaction, and advanced humanoid platforms like iCub, ergoCub, and iRonCub, the only jet-powered flying humanoid robot. His work bridges robotics, sensing, and scalable autonomy to bring safe humanoids into production environments.

sessions.title: Distillation Recipes and Synthetic Data Fine-Tuning for Algorithmic Trading
sessions.abstract: Explore how model distillation and compression techniques are redefining the role of large language models in quantitative finance. In this expert talk, we’ll examine strategies for reducing cost, latency, and complexity while preserving accuracy for real-time alpha generation and risk prediction. You'll gain insights into the essential techniques behind efficient model design and discover how these methods drive scalable, high-performance deployment on NVIDIA’s accelerated computing platform.

speakers.name: Dhruv Desai
speakers.bio: Dhruv is a solutions architect at NVIDIA, focusing on capital markets. His background spans quantitative finance and applied AI research. Before NVIDIA, he worked as a Data Scientist at BlackRock, where he led research on pricing / trading / liquidity algorithms. He holds a master's degree in electrical engineering from the University of Pennsylvania and has authored multiple peer-reviewed publications.

speakers.name: Lavinia Ghita
speakers.bio: Lavinia is a Solutions Architect Manager at NVIDIA, leading the EMEA technical team for the Financial Services Industry (FSI). Her work focuses on large‑scale AI systems, with emphasis on distillation methods, domain-adaption techniques and model compression methods for efficient AI pipelines. Her research also covers foundation‑model architectures for time‑series analysis and the development of general methods for modelling structure and latent dynamics in temporal processes. Prior to NVIDIA, Lavinia worked on research and applications in this space at a quantitative hedge fund and in big tech. She holds advanced degrees in Mathematics from the École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL).

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Transfer Learning / Fine Tuning

sessions.title: Pioneering Autonomous Science: Toward a Unified Cross-Lab Workflow
sessions.abstract: This session brings together leading labs to showcase how they are using autonomous AI and high-performance computing to build scalable, reproducible data pipelines from edge to HPC. See real-world demonstrations of agentic AI automating complex, multi-facility workflows, optimizing resources, and accelerating scientific discovery with NVIDIA’s advanced frameworks. The featured labs will share insights on transparent resource management, rapid deployment, and collaborative experimentation that can be reproduced and scaled globally. Learn how these approaches unlock innovative science through seamless orchestration, portability, and reproducibility.

speakers.name: Stephen DeWitt
speakers.bio: Stephen DeWitt is a senior R&D staff member at ORNL, specializing in computational materials science and HPC. He develops scalable simulation software for additive manufacturing, such as Adamantine, and leads research on AI agents for autonomous experimentation. A key contributor to the Exascale Computing Project, he holds a Ph.D. in applied physics from the University of Michigan.

speakers.name: Bjoern Enders
speakers.bio: Bjoern Enders is a senior data science workflows architect at NERSC, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. As part of the Data Science Engagement Group he spearheads cross-facility initiatives to integrate high-performance computing with experimental facilities. Holding a Ph.D. in physics from TU Munich, he specializes in real-time data streaming and automated pipelines that connect instruments like electron microscopes directly to supercomputers for immediate analysis.

speakers.name: Adam Thompson
speakers.bio: Adam Thompson is a principal technical product manager for edge HPC applications and the creator of cuSignal, a GPU-accelerated library for building signal processing workflows. Adam's technical interests involve signal processing, HPC with Python, and applications of deep learning to radio frequency data. He holds a master’s degree in electrical and computer engineering from Georgia Tech and a bachelor’s degree from Clemson University.

speakers.name: Ian Foster
speakers.bio: Ian Foster is director of Argonne’s Data Science and Learning Division, a senior scientist and distinguished fellow at Argonne, and the Arthur Holly Compton Distinguished Service Professor at the University of Chicago. A pioneer in high-performance and distributed computing, he has published hundreds of papers and books, and his work laid foundations for modern cyber infrastructures. Foster holds top computing awards and fellowships.

speakers.name: Yuliana Zamora
speakers.bio: Yuliana Zamora is a senior developer relations manager driving the adoption of NVIDIA’s AI and accelerated computing across the Capital Markets ecosystem. She brings extensive experience from the high performance computing world, where she championed implementation strategies for critical workloads. She holds a Ph.D. and M.S. in computer science from the University of Chicago.

sessions.title: The Edge of the Edge: Redefining GPU-Enabled AI Sensor Processing
sessions.abstract: Physical AI and edge supercomputing are both concerned with the timely delivery and processing of physical measurements on GPU to build the future of autonomous decision-making across industries and domains—from scientific instruments to industrial robotics. During this talk, we will hear from leading sensor manufacturers who are building real-time AI solutions with NVIDIA and the Holoscan platform; they'll share their best practices and their vision, and highlight transformational work toward a future of AI-powered instruments.

speakers.name: Adam Thompson
speakers.bio: Adam Thompson is a principal technical product manager for edge HPC applications and the creator of cuSignal, a GPU-accelerated library for building signal processing workflows. Adam's technical interests involve signal processing, HPC with Python, and applications of deep learning to radio frequency data. He holds a master’s degree in electrical and computer engineering from Georgia Tech and a bachelor’s degree from Clemson University.

speakers.name: John Ammerman
speakers.bio: John Ammerman is a Distinguished Engineer and Product Architect in the Test and Measurement (T&M) division at Emerson (formerly National Instruments). As the lead architect for T&M Platform Hardware products, he drives the adoption of new technologies and capabilities across key platforms like PXI. John has over 20 years of experience designing T&M products, architectures, and technologies.

speakers.name: Felix Bachmair
speakers.bio: Felix Bachmair leads innovation and strategic development at DECTRIS. With a Ph.D. in experimental particle physics, he connects science, technology, and operations to help scientists make sense of rapidly growing detector data. He focuses on enabling coherent solutions and bridging stakeholders as new detector generations drive data volume and complexity ever higher.

speakers.name: Giovanni Campanella
speakers.bio: As the general manager of Industrial Automation & Robotics at Texas Instruments, Giovanni leads cross-discipline teams to solve design challenges in industrial automation, edge AI, and robotics. He champions safe, efficient robotic systems—from factory-floor automation to future-ready humanoids—by combining TI’s semiconductor strength with system-level vision.

speakers.name: Paul Golding
speakers.bio: Paul Golding is vice president of edge AI at Analog Devices, leading the company’s physical intelligence strategy—advancing AI that can understand and act within real-world sensing, signal, and robotic systems. He heads Lorenz Labs, ADI’s research program for domain foundation models and agentic computing, and drives the development of edge agents for next-generation autonomous machines.

sessions.title: Building Towards Self-Driving Codebases with Long-Running, Asynchronous Agents
sessions.abstract: Aman Sanger, co-founder and CTO at Cursor, will share how Cursor is building long-running coding agents that can autonomously execute more ambitious software tasks.

speakers.name: Aman Sanger
speakers.bio: Aman Sanger is a 25-year-old Indian-American tech entrepreneur and co-founder of Anysphere, the AI startup that developed the popular AI coding tool Cursor. Sanger and his three co-founders from MIT—Michael Truell, Sualeh Asif, and Arvid Lunnemark—founded Anysphere in 2022. Aman is also the CTO at Anysphere.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Developing Generative AI Models for Market Activity
sessions.abstract: Nasdaq's cutting-edge generative AI platform creates ultra-realistic synthetic market order books and order flow data using advanced deep learning architectures. By leveraging sequential generative models and transformer-based networks, it produces statistically accurate limit order book simulations that preserve the statistical properties and microstructure dynamics of real financial markets. The platform generates high-fidelity synthetic data indistinguishable from actual market conditions, while providing sophisticated vector embeddings for enhanced search and retrieval applications.

speakers.name: Douglas Hamilton
speakers.bio: Douglas Hamilton heads AI research and engineering at Nasdaq. Doug’s notable achievements at Nasdaq include establishing the first AI-powered strike listing system and launching the first AI-powered order type approved by the Securities and Exchange Commission. He has an M.S. in systems engineering from MIT and a B.A. in mathematics from the University of Illinois, Springfield.

speakers.name: Michael O'Rourke
speakers.bio:
Mike O’Rourke serves as SVP, Head of AI & Emerging Technology. He oversees the development of Nasdaq’s data platforms and our Artificial Intelligence program.
 
Mike’s team combines proprietary data with advanced analytics and machine learning to produce sophisticated solutions for Nasdaq’s global customers. These efforts have resulted in several technology breakthroughs and patents, including the recent SEC-approved product Dynamic Melo.  
 
He is also responsible for the data technology that powers Nasdaq’s U.S. and global market data feeds and critical market infrastructure, such as the UTP SIP.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation

sessions.title: Python All the Way Down: Speed-of-Light CUDA Without Leaving Python
sessions.abstract:
How would you write a Python library that uses CUDA today?

Most Python GPU libraries—PyTorch, CuPy, RAPIDS, and others—still build on CUDA C++ because many core CUDA building blocks have only ever existed in C++. That’s now changing. New Python-native CUDA libraries bring formerly C++-only capabilities directly into Python, enabling high-performance GPU functionality without bindings or C++ backends. This talk shows how to build modern GPU libraries with these tools, and what becomes possible when it’s Python all the way down.

To provide a real-world example of using these new tools to build accelerated Python libraries, we will present a case study of how the Awkward Array Python library used for high energy physics at CERN is replacing its custom CUDA C++ kernels with cuda.compute, delivering tremendous speedups while making the codebase vastly easier to evolve and maintain.

speakers.name: Ashwin Srinath
speakers.bio: Ashwin Srinath is a Senior Software Engineer at NVIDIA, where he works on the CUDA Core Compute Libraries (CCCL) for Python. Previously, he worked on the cuDF Python library, as part of RAPIDS team at NVIDIA.  He’s passionate about making the experience of using CUDA from Python delightful.

speakers.name: Ianna Osborne
speakers.bio: Ianna Osborne is an open-science advocate and research software engineer specializing in particle physics and high-performance computing. She builds scalable, open-source tools for scientific discovery, maintains the Awkward Array project, and leads international efforts that foster collaboration and sustainable research software.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries

sessions.title: Massive GPU Acceleration for Scalable Quantum Compilation and Graph Analytics
sessions.abstract: Subgraph isomorphism is a major bottleneck in graph analytics, scientific computing, and quantum-compilation pipelines, yet most solutions rely on backtracking that cannot exploit modern GPUs. Motivated by real quantum-compilation challenges, we present a GPU-native formulation that replaces serial search with parallel relational tabular operations built from reusable “motifs,” enabling efficient joins and filters using NVIDIA RAPIDS. Across diverse benchmarks, we achieve up to 600x speedups on a single NVIDIA H200 GPU. By combining Q-CTRL’s quantum performance management infrastructure software and NVIDIA’s GPU platforms, we demonstrate a data-centric method that simplifies high-performance graph analysis, accelerates large-scale applications, and opens a path toward emerging hybrid HPC–quantum architectures and future deployment on HPC–quantum interconnect systems like NVQLink.

speakers.name: Oded Green
speakers.bio: Oded works on a wide range of HPC problems. Previously, Oded was a senior solutions architect and a senior graph software engineer (part of the NVIDIA RAPIDS team). Oded received his Ph.D. in computational sciences and engineering at the Georgia Institute of Technology. Oded is currently an associate editor for the ACM Transaction on Parallel Computing.

speakers.name: Yulun Wang
speakers.bio: Yulun Wang is a staff scientist at Q-CTRL specializing in quantum circuit compilation, error-aware design, and scalable performance. He led the development of the quantum compiler within Q-CTRL’s performance management pipeline, delivering industry-leading gains in circuit quality, speed, and hardware efficiency. He earned his Ph.D. at Stony Brook University, focusing on quantum algorithms for dynamical simulations and engineering applications.

sessions.title: Beyond Guardrails: Best Practices Across the Industry for Trustworthy AI
sessions.abstract: Join Margaret Mitchell, Hugging Face chief ethics scientist, and Nikki Pope, head of AI and legal ethics at NVIDIA, in a conversation about how their respective companies are delivering trustworthy artificial intelligence products and tools.

speakers.name: Margaret Mitchell
speakers.bio: Margaret Mitchell is a computer science researcher and chief ethics scientist at AI startup Hugging Face. She has worked in the technology industry for 15 years, and has published over 100 papers on natural language generation, assistive technology, computer vision, and AI ethics. Her work has received numerous awards and has been implemented by multiple technology companies.

speakers.name: Nikki Pope
speakers.bio: Nikki Pope is an in-house counsel at NVIDIA who leads Trustworthy AI. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Nikki was the managing director of the High Tech Law Institute at Santa Clara University and an attorney at Cooley LLP for 10 years, and on the faculty at Santa Clara Law. Nikki has also held management positions in large companies and startups in financial services, entertainment, consumer products, and web-based services and applications.

sessions.title: Cutting-Edge Molecular Dynamics on the Latest Multi-Node NVLink Technology
sessions.abstract: Explore new techniques for scaling and optimizing GROMACS molecular dynamics on the latest NVIDIA Multi-node NVLink (MNNVL) systems, via the use of cuFFTMp and NVSHMEM, to minimize communication overhead and maximize performance. Together, we'll walk through practical, transferable steps you can apply to boost performance in your own HPC and AI workloads.

speakers.name: Mahesh Doijade
speakers.bio: Mahesh is a Compute DevTech Engineer at NVIDIA, specializing in accelerating machine learning and deep learning workloads on GPUs. His current focus areas include optimizing LLM inference and contributing to the GROMACS open-source molecular dynamics package, enhancing performance on multi-node, multi-GPU systems. Previously, he developed applications demonstrating best practices for the CUDA Toolkit.

speakers.name: Alan Gray
speakers.bio: Alan specializes in HPC and AI application optimization, including GPU-accelerated supercomputer scaling. Previously, he worked as an HPC consultant at EPCC (The University of Edinburgh). His career began in the area of theoretical particle physics, for which he received a Ph.D. from the University of Glasgow, followed by a postdoctoral fellowship at The Ohio State University.

sessions.title: Scalable Data Access for Next-Gen Algorithmic Trading
sessions.abstract:
Algorithmic trading is entering a new era defined by AI, massive data volumes, and unprecedented compute scale. Many leading firms now operate more like advanced AI labs than traditional trading desks, relying on large fleets of NVIDIA GPUs and consuming petabytes of real-time and historical market data. The main barrier is no longer the models, but accessing and processing the data fast enough to power them. 

This session traces how the industry has evolved over the past 20 years, where firms are finding real success with AI/ML, and what frontiers are emerging as compute and data converge. We’ll show how new approaches to data delivery, storage, and accelerated networking, together with solutions from Databento and NVIDIA, are opening the door to the next wave of innovation in finance.

speakers.name: Christina Qi
speakers.bio: Christina Qi is the CEO of Databento, a market data provider serving over 30,000 customers across finance, AI, technology, and other industries. She formerly founded Domeyard LP, a hedge fund focused on high frequency trading that traded up to $7.1 billion per day. Christina is a member of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology Board of Trustees and graduated from MIT in 2013.

sessions.title: Push the Performance Frontier of Computer Vision Models With NVFP4 Reduced Precision
sessions.abstract: Reduced-precision formats like NVFP4 are shaping the future of AI by enabling faster training and inference without compromising model accuracy. In this talk, we share best practices, insights, and lessons learned from training and deploying convolutional neural network (CNN) and transformer models using advanced reduced-precision techniques while maintaining full accuracy relative to high-precision baselines. We'll explore how reduced precision, combined with optimized CUDA kernels, delivers significant speedups for both training and inference—pushing the performance frontier of computer vision workloads on modern GPUs. This methodology can be broadly applied across industrial use cases requiring highly accurate, low-latency computer vision models, including manufacturing, medical imaging, autonomous vehicles, robotics, and more.

speakers.name: Leo Du
speakers.bio: Leo Du is a senior generative AI solutions architect at NVIDIA, with prior experience as an algorithm engineer at ASML. He has rich experience in the semiconductor industry. He helps partners design AI infrastructure, and architect and implement generative AI enterprise software and products. His expertise spans LLMs, domain-adaptive pre-training, post-training, reinforcement learning, and computer vision. Outside work, he enjoys cycling and hiking.

speakers.name: Pradeep Ramachandran
speakers.bio: Pradeep Ramachandran is the director and head of research at KLA’s Artificial Intelligence and Advanced Computing Lab (ACL) at IIT Madras Research Park. He holds a B.Tech. from IIT Madras and an M.S. and Ph.D. from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign. His work focuses on system-level optimization across algorithms, hardware, and software, spanning fault tolerance, chip design, video compression, and HPC. He enjoys cooking, travel, running, and spending time with his 6-year-old son.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: Global Governance and the Future of AI Safety Regulation
sessions.abstract: The pace of AI regulations is increasing, and the complexity of complying with different laws is challenging for global companies. For example, EU member countries are passing "companion" regulations for the Artificial Intelligence Act. Meanwhile, the U.S. federal government hasn't adopted any AI regs, but many states have, and the current administration's focus differs from that of the previous administration. At the same time, Congress is focused primarily on child safety. How can companies comply with laws in such a fragmented environment?

speakers.name: Iain Cunningham
speakers.bio: Iain is vice president and deputy counsel at NVIDIA. A recognized expert on intellectual property and cybersecurity, Iain worked at both Apple and Cooley Godward Kronish LLP before starting and selling legal technology startup Samwise.

speakers.name: Jennifer Barrera
speakers.bio: Jennifer Barrera is president and chief executive officer of the CalChamber. She took over leadership in October 2021, after serving as the executive vice president of policy for four years. As EVP, Jennifer oversaw the development and implementation of policy and strategy for four years. Prior to that, she led CalChamber advocacy on labor and employment and taxation from September 2010 through the end of 2017. From May 2003 until joining the CalChamber staff, Jennifer worked at a statewide law firm that specializes in labor/employment defense, now Carothers, DiSante & Freudenberger, LLP.

speakers.name: Minesh Tanna
speakers.bio: Minesh is an industry-recognised leader in AI law, having specialized in this area for almost 10 years. He is chair of both the Society for Computers & Law AI Group and the City of London Law Society AI Committee, and is a published author, lecturer, and speaker on AI. As the leader of Simmons & Simmons’ Tier 1 AI team, Minesh has been ranked in the AI "Hall of Fame" by Legal 500 UK for 2025 and 2026. He has been described as “a titan in the industry” and “brilliant and a leader in the field”.

sessions.title: Safety Design, Detection, and Responsibility in the Age of AI
sessions.abstract: Ensuring the safety of children and young people is a global effort. This panel brings together leaders from industry, law, and civil society engaged in protecting children and teens online to discuss what tools and techniques are available for companies interested in joining the fight.

speakers.name: Frances Waldmann
speakers.bio: Frances Waldmann advises global technology leaders on complex regulatory compliance and enforcement matters involving AI and digital platforms across the United States, U.K., and EU.  She helps clients navigate compliance with global frameworks such as the EU AI Act, Digital Services Act, U.K. Online Safety Act, and the evolving U.S. federal and state enforcement landscape. Her particular focus areas include content moderation, model transparency, and child-safety and child sexual abuse material prevention and reporting requirements.

speakers.name: Jessica Butler
speakers.bio: Jessica Butler is a senior product security engineer at NVIDIA, where she develops large-scale security tools that support safe AI and secure software releases. With 19 years of experience and deep expertise in Software Bills of Materials (SBOM) and Vulnerability Exploitability eXchange (VEX) automation, container image security, and AI model life-cycle assurance, she works across teams to improve security posture and developer workflows. She is committed to elevating child safety in AI through through engineering-informed, responsible design and detection practices.

speakers.name: Sean Litton
speakers.bio: Sean Litton is president and CEO of the Tech Coalition, a global alliance of tech companies working together to protect children from online sexual abuse and exploitation. Prior to his role with the Tech Coalition, Sean worked at the International Justice Mission for over 20 years, including as the organization’s global president. Sean and the Tech Coalition are dedicated to achieving tangible results so children can safely play, learn, and explore online.

speakers.name: Kerry Smith
speakers.bio:
Kerry joined the Internet Watch Foundation as Chief Executive Officer in August 2025. The IWF is an international not-for-profit organisation working closely with the global technology sector to disrupt, prevent, and remove images and videos showing the sexual abuse and exploitation of children from digital spaces. 

IWF operates Europe’s largest centre for assessing, grading, and securing the removal of child sexual abuse material online, the IWF provides trusted services, intelligence, and datasets used by technology companies, platforms, financial and other industries, and law enforcement worldwide. These capabilities support safety-by-design approaches that help protect victims and survivors, prevent the spread of criminal content, and strengthen the global investigation and disruption of abuse networks.

Prior to joining IWF Kerry was the  Chief Executive Officer at the Helen Bamber Foundation, who provide evidenced based therapy and holistic support for survivors of trafficking and torture. Previously, she has worked as Head of Girls’ Rights and Youth at Plan International, and was also a Senior Conflict and Fragile States Adviser at international NGO Save the Children.
 
A seasoned human rights lawyer with a Master’s Degree in International Human Rights Law, Ms Smith has also been a gender adviser to Amnesty International, and spent time as Head of Office at the International Committee of the Red Cross where she honed her diplomatic skills in Ethiopia and Colombia.

sessions.title: Accelerate Operational Excellence Through NVIDIA Resiliency Solutions
sessions.abstract: Explore the NVIDIA resiliency tools that keep AI systems reliable at scale. Learn how to match these solutions to your operational challenges to build a practical adoption roadmap.

speakers.name: Ava Arnaz
speakers.bio: Ava Arnaz is a senior solutions architect specializing in AI. With extensive experience spanning model development, MLOps, and enterprise-scale deployments across multiple industries, she's driven by solving the complex challenges that define the future of intelligent systems.

sessions.title: Quick Start to Accelerated Quantum Supercomputing
sessions.abstract: Get a hands-on introduction to NVIDIA CUDA-Q, the platform for seamlessly orchestrating both quantum and classical processors alongside each other in accelerated quantum supercomputers. Whether you are new to the field or an experienced developer, this session walks you through building a hybrid application to demonstrate the execution of quantum-classical workflows across NVIDIA GPUs and IQM’s quantum hardware.

speakers.name: Monica Van-Dieren
speakers.bio: Monica leads the CUDA-Q Academic initiative at NVIDIA. Previously, she created quantum training materials for IBM. She holds a Ph.D. in mathematical sciences from Carnegie Mellon University. Monica has over 20 years of academic experience at institutions like Stanford, the University of Michigan, and Robert Morris University. She has taught mathematics, launched an interdisciplinary honors program, organized STEM outreach activities, and conducted both theoretical research in logic and pedagogical research in educational technology. Monica is passionate about bridging the gap between classical and quantum computing, enabling practical solutions for complex real-world problems.

speakers.name: Mark Wolf
speakers.bio: Mark Wolf serves as a technical marketing engineer on NVIDIA's quantum computing team, where he focuses on bridging the gap between advanced research and user education. Mark works closely with researchers at NVIDIA and across the quantum ecosystem to develop CUDA-Q Academic content. Previously, Mark worked at Quantinuum, and holds a Ph.D. in physical chemistry from the Center for Computational Quantum Chemistry at the University of Georgia.

speakers.name: Danny Bulmash
speakers.bio: Danny Bulmash is the education lead for IQM Quantum Computers; his passion is helping everyone get excited and knowledgeable about quantum technology. He holds a Ph.D. in physics from Stanford University. Prior to joining IQM, he was an assistant professor in the United States, where his research focused on new quantum phases of matter.

sessions.title: Designing and Operating an Energy Efficient AI Factory
sessions.abstract: As AI factories become mission‑critical infrastructure, organizations must design, build, operate, and maintain electro‑intensive environments that are intelligent and scalable. This session shows how Schneider Electric and NVIDIA, along with open standards including SysML and OpenUSD enable the full AI Factory lifecycle through a unified approach powered by accelerated computing, high‑fidelity simulation, and agentic AI.

speakers.name: Natasha Nelson
speakers.bio:
Natasha is CTO of services and vice president of EcoStruxure Power for end users. She focuses on advancing the energy transition through sustainable, digital, and all-electric solutions. With her expertise in internet of things, AI, and connected systems, she collaborates with teams to develop innovative use cases and interoperability strategies for mission-critical and electro-intensive industries. This role enables our organization to create scalable, secure, and value-driven solutions powered by EcoStruxure platform. 

Natasha holds an M.B.A. in business administration from MIT. In the MIT Sloan Fellows Program, she specialized in digital platform business, innovation, and cybersecurity.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Scalable AI Localization for Live Media Using NVIDIA Holoscan for Media
sessions.abstract: Learn how leading broadcasters are using NVIDIA Holoscan for Media to deliver simultaneous multilingual versions of live content with precise lip-sync, localized graphics, and broadcast-grade latency—without writing code. We will explore how complete-frame localization pipelines can be operationalized across GPU-accelerated environments to expand audience reach, reduce human workload, and protect creative integrity at scale.

speakers.name: Tim Gunter
speakers.bio: Tim Gunter is the VP of Engineering at Monks, where he leads the integration of Computer Vision and AI innovation across the global digital landscape. With an organically grown engineering career spanning over two decades, Tim has evolved from building traditional web architectures to leading large-scale, mission-critical systems used by global brands. At Monks, he focuses on the intersection of emerging machine learning, real-time systems, and scalable product delivery—guiding multidisciplinary teams to turn bleeding-edge research into durable, production-ready platforms. Known for his pragmatic approach to AI, Tim specializes in translating complex technical capability into solutions that drive measurable business impact, while building engineering organizations designed to scale with speed, quality, and purpose.

speakers.name: Jeffrey Kember
speakers.bio: Jeff is a Senior Director of Product Manager for AI for Media2. He is responsible for leading product direction for Holoscan for Media2, AI for Media and Maxine. Before joining NVIDIA, Jeff led a team of Product Managers and was the Technical Director for Media and Entertainment in the Office of the CTO at Google Cloud. For more than two decades, Jeff worked in VFX for film and games concluding as a Computer Graphics Supervisor at Framestore. He has worked at a variety of companies including Pixar, Industrial Light + Magic, Sony Imageworks, Digital Domain, LucasArts, Blizzard/Activision, Tippett Animation and Animal Logic.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: Post-Training Nemotron With RL: Multi‑Environment Training for Reasoning and Tool-Using Agents
sessions.abstract:
How do we move beyond simple instruction-following to models that truly reason, plan, and use tools? The answer lies in the rigor of the post-training pipeline. In this session, we unveil the technology behind Nemotron’s reasoning and tool use capabilities. While we will cover the essentials of imitation learning and synthetic data generation, the spotlight will be on the frontier of reinforcement learning (RL).

We will demonstrate how multi-environment RL training serves as the catalyst for unlocking multi-step reasoning and robust tool use. By analyzing the interplay between reward modeling, reinforcement learning from human feedback, and environment-aware training, we will show how Nemotron achieves superior performance in complex agentic workflows. Join us to explore the specific techniques NVIDIA uses to align models not just for chat, but for cognitive action.

speakers.name: Oleksii Kuchaiev
speakers.bio: Oleksii leads an applied research team focused on AI model post-training, alignment, and reasoning. He joined NVIDIA as an applied researcher, initially contributing to machine translation and large language models, before becoming the first engineer and subsequently the manager of the NeMo project. Prior to NVIDIA, Oleksii was an applied scientist at Apple, where he worked on App Store recommendations, and earlier at Microsoft, contributing to the deep learning toolkit CNTK. Oleksii holds a Ph.D. in computer science from the University of California, Irvine (2010), and an M.S. in applied mathematics from the University of Kyiv, Ukraine (2007).

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Optimize Biologics Design Workflow With Auto-Tuned Models and Multi-Agent Systems
sessions.abstract:
Applying AI to antibody therapeutics often presents significant engineering challenges—particularly in effectively linking structure prediction, molecular generation, and property optimization into a cohesive pipeline. Learn how breakthrough AI frameworks can be leveraged to architect a zero-code antibody design and optimization workflow. We'll demonstrate how multi-agent capabilities enable intelligent interpretation of complex R&D requirements—from de novo generation to multi-objective optimization—without the need for manual coding.

We'll also share key learnings and identified bottlenecks while addressing the industry-wide “ADA prediction gap” through the construction of a high-fidelity immunogenicity simulation framework that models human immune responses, including MHC binding, APC presentation, and T-cell activation.

speakers.name: Hu Ge
speakers.bio:
Hu GE, Ph.D. is the founder and CEO of Wecomput Technology and a 20-year veteran of CADD/AIDD.
Hu's work includes dozens of innovative biologics projects, with over 10 molecules progressing to clinical trials. Notably, three biologics he designed secured successful overseas licensing. Previously, he led Computational Biology at Hengrui Pharmaceuticals and Molecular Informatics at Simcere Pharmaceutical.

speakers.name: Jiabo Li
speakers.bio: Jiabo Li has over 25 years of experience at the intersection of AI, chemistry, and biotechnology. He created ChatMol, an open-source molecular modeling system, and AlphaConf, a high-efficiency 3D conformation algorithm licensed by multiple biopharma firms. A former Humboldt Research Fellow, principal scientist at Schrödinger, and adjunct professor at Sun Yat-sen University, Jiabo has authored more than 65 peer-reviewed publications.

sessions.title: Discover Top In-Demand Learning Paths Across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa
sessions.abstract:
We invite you to join us as we explore NVIDIA's Deep Learning Institute Training Catalogue, uncovering practical use cases and real-world applications that extend beyond standard course descriptions.

In this session, our NVIDIA experts will guide you through some of our most popular and in-demand learning paths, demonstrating how they can deepen your AI knowledge, strengthen your skills, and support your career development.

speakers.name: Juan Jose Durillo Barrionuevo
speakers.bio: Juan is a principal instructor at NVIDIA for the Europe, Middle East, and Africa region. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Juan held positions at different universities and supercomputing centers as a researcher in the areas of artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and search-based optimization. Juan’s teaching experience includes courses on AI, GPU and accelerated computing, operating systems, and programming in modern C/C++.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Scaling Out and Across: Networking Innovations for Giga-Scale AI Systems
sessions.abstract:
As AI factories grow to hundreds of thousands, and soon millions, of interconnected GPUs, the network has become the defining architecture of large-scale AI. Achieving uncompromised performance requires fabrics that not only scale up and out within a single data center, but scale across multiple sites with predictable latency, lossless throughput, and end-to-end resilience.

In this session, NVIDIA unveils the full breadth of networking innovations that enable the construction of giga-scale AI infrastructure. Learn how advancements in networking, including ultra-high-radix switches, next-generation NVIDIA Quantum-X InfiniBand and Spectrum-X Ethernet platforms with co-packaged optics, and accelerator-optimized SuperNICs, deliver the performance required to train massive foundation models, deploy real-time inference pipelines, and interconnect federated AI factories globally.

speakers.name: Gilad Shainer
speakers.bio: Gilad Shainer focuses on high performance computing and artificial intelligence. He holds multiple patents in the field of high-speed networking. Gilad Shainer holds an M.S. and a B.S. in electrical engineering from the Technion Institute of Technology in Israel.

sessions.title: Unlock Fast, Cost-Effective Interactive Analytics on Massive Data Lakehouses
sessions.abstract: Running interactive SQL at scale is still far slower, and more expensive, than it should be. This session explores how GPU acceleration fundamentally changes that equation. We’ll dive into open-source community work speeding up the popular open data lakehouse engine Presto—work that required rethinking not just the core execution engine, but also the surrounding system components that drive performance at scale. We'll walk through benchmark results, lessons from real enterprise deployments, and the architectural details that actually matter in practice. You'll leave with concrete guidance for GPU-accelerating your own data processing workloads to achieve better performance at lower cost.

speakers.name: Greg Kimball
speakers.bio: Gregory Kimball is a software engineering manager at NVIDIA working on the RAPIDS team. Gregory leads development for libcudf, the CUDA/C++ library for columnar data processing that powers RAPIDS cuDF. Gregory holds a PhD in applied physics from the California Institute of Technology.

speakers.name: Zoltán Arnold Nagy
speakers.bio: Zoltán is the technical lead for GPU-accelerated data processing at IBM Research. He designs and builds complex, high-performance pipelines requiring deep understanding of the full stack, from storage and networking to schedulers, GPUs, and SQL engines. He previously designed storage systems and data centers, led DevOps teams, and advised Fortune 100 companies. Zoltán holds an M.Sc. in computer science and mathematics from Eötvös Loránd University.

sessions.title: AI-First Design: Build Smarter From Day Zero Through Every Funding Stage
sessions.abstract:
The decisions AI-first companies make on Day Zero will radically shift based on initial resources. Does a $5 million seed round change the fundamental AI strategy compared to a bootstrapped $0 start? Absolutely.
This session explores four distinct Day Zero scenarios, each representing a different starting capital: $0 (Bootstrapped), $500,000 (Pre-Seed), $5 million (Seed/Series A), and $50 million (Series B+). For each scenario, we'll highlight high-leverage AI-first tactics, illustrating how capital immediately dictates where to spend resources and how fast to build proprietary technology. You'll leave with a clear, resource-aligned playbook for your own AI-first launch.

speakers.name: Carlos Morais
speakers.bio: Carlos is a Developer Relations Manager at NVIDIA, where he is the head of AI Natives for France and Southern Europe, managing technical engagements across the region. His expertise lies in Agentic AI, having previously served as a Senior AI Solution Architect at IBM. In this role, he specialized in generative AI for highly regulated industries and trained over 200 consultants in agentic concepts, including Embeddings, Vector Search, PEFT, and RAG. Additionally, Carlos co-founded and served as the Chief Technology Officer at Nimest Tech, where he led the development of Virtual Humans powered by AI Agents for various AR/VR platforms. His academic background includes studying Telecommunications and Electronics Engineering at the Instituto Nacional de Telecomunicações (Inatel) and Business at the University of Toronto.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Climate Prediction and Smooth Navigation Use Cases
sessions.abstract:
This session will introduce two use cases of NVIDIA platform adoption—one in climate prediction, and the other in smooth navigation.

Climate Prediction: Accurate climate prediction relies on capturing small-scale processes, such as clouds and turbulence, which remain elusive in traditional models. This talk presents the CliMA solution: a physics-AI hybrid Earth System Model built in Julia to run natively on NVIDIA GPUs. Leveraging GPU acceleration, researchers generate large ensembles to drive a "calibrate, emulate, sample" workflow. 

Generalized Navigation: the CUDA-accelerated Delta-Corridor framework utilizes Delta-Corridor spatial modeling to achieve reliable and smooth generalized navigation with Isaac and Cosmos. By applying continuous safety corridors, it ensures reliable navigation for mobile robots, boats, and autonomous vehicles in dynamic environments.

speakers.name: Tapio Schneider
speakers.bio: Tapio Schneider is the Theodore Y. Wu Professor of Environmental Science and Engineering at Caltech and a principal scientist at Google. He leads the Climate Modeling Alliance (CliMA), a multi-institutional initiative developing a next-generation Earth System Model that fuses physical laws with AI to learn directly from global observations. His broader research explores the climate dynamics of Earth and other planets.

speakers.name: Wei-Chang Yeh
speakers.bio: Yeh is currently a chair professor in the Department of Industrial Engineering and Engineering Management at National Tsing Hua University in Taiwan. He received his M.S. and Ph.D. from the Department of Industrial Engineering at the University of Texas at Arlington. His research primarily focuses on algorithms, including exact solution methods and soft computing.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling

sessions.title: Self-Coding Agents: Architectures, Data Flywheels, and Autonomous Code Repair
sessions.abstract: AI agents are swiftly evolving toward true autonomy using self-coding, feedback-driven, and adaptive architectures. This session explores frameworks where agents self-improve via data flywheels—constantly refining their models through feedback and learning from every interaction. We'll spotlight data designer agents that not only process, but actively generate and curate training data, enabling ongoing evolution without routine human coding. Critical topics include self-repairing code, reinforcement-driven data loops, and orchestration of agent collectives for collaboration and scale. These advances are powering scalable, reliable, and collaborative autonomous systems for enterprise and research, ushering in agents that can continually adapt and co-create with humans in real time.

speakers.name: Bartley Richardson
speakers.bio: Bartley Richardson is chief technology officer of NVIDIA AI software and director of engineering for cybersecurity AI development and product engagement, including accelerated computing and generative AI. Previously, Bartley was a technical lead on multiple DARPA research projects. He holds a Ph.D. in computer science and engineering, with a focus on AI.

speakers.name: Kris Murphy
speakers.bio: Kris Murphy builds blueprints that provide NVIDIA partners and customers with best practices for developing applications that connect AI agents to data to drive greater efficiency and productivity. Prior to NVIDIA, she held software engineering and solutions architect roles at Red Hat and IBM.

speakers.name: Ali Golshan
speakers.bio: Ali Golshan is senior director of AI software at NVIDIA, driving partner-led go-to-market strategy for agentic AI products. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he was co-founder and CEO of Gretel (synthetic data for AI), co-founder and CTO of StackRox (Kubernetes security, acquired by Red Hat), and co-founder and CTO of Cyphort (security analytics, acquired by Juniper). He began his career in the U.S. intelligence community, designing secure data systems.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: From Data to Decisions: Enabling AI Agents With Business Knowledge
sessions.abstract: The next wave of business innovation is powered by AI developer platforms that grant agents deep access to your enterprise knowledge. In this session, discover practical strategies for leveraging these platforms to connect information from across business systems, automate workflows, and support smarter decision-making. Live demonstrations will highlight how you can use AI-powered tools to streamline processes, enhance compliance, and guide innovation in your own organization. You’ll leave with frameworks and real-world examples to help scale knowledge, efficiency, and impact—no matter your role or industry.

speakers.name: Roopa Prabhu
speakers.bio: Roopa Prabhu serves as the senior director of engineering for Applied AI Services and Infrastructure at NVIDIA. In her role, she oversees applications and services related to conversational AI, generative AI, and computer vision AI for various verticals, including customer service, industrial and physical AI, smart cities, and more, across both edge and cloud environments. Furthermore, she is responsible for leading projects related to edge and cloud infrastructure, in addition to security.

speakers.name: Rachel Allen
speakers.bio: Rachel is an engineering manager for cybersecurity AI at NVIDIA. Her team’s focus is the research and development of GPU-accelerated machine learning, deep learning, and GenAI applications to solve cybersecurity challenges. Prior to NVIDIA, Rachel was a lead data scientist at Booz Allen Hamilton, where she designed a variety of capabilities for advanced threat hunting and network defense. She holds a bachelor’s degree in cognitive science and a Ph.D. in neuroscience from the University of Virginia.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Building the Future of Manufacturing
sessions.abstract: The manufacturing industry is entering a new era, driven by AI, robotics, and simulation. Join distinguished leaders from the semiconductor and electronics, industrial and process manufacturing, and robotics industries as they share their pioneering use cases and how these technologies enable them to innovate, reimagine production systems, and transform the design and operation of advanced manufacturing and warehouse facilities.

speakers.name: Rev Lebaredian
speakers.bio: Rev Lebaredian is vice president of Omniverse and simulation technology at NVIDIA. Lebaredian has over 30 years of experience in the computer graphics industry, working in roles at Warner Brothers Digital and Disney Dream Quest Images before launching his own venture, Steamboat Software. He joined NVIDIA in 2002 and led the development of several real-time, multi-GPU scalable rendering, physics simulation, and AI platforms and technologies. Today, he leads the product, engineering, and research teams of NVIDIA Omniverse, a software platform and operating system enabling developers to build physical AI and robotic system simulation applications.

speakers.name: Seungyong Doh
speakers.bio: Seungyong Doh is chief information officer in charge of digital transformation of SK hynix, which he joined in December 2020. Prior to that, he worked for Samsung SDS as a vice president in charge of smart factory business. He has experience in building smart factories in various manufacturing fields such as semiconductors, smartphones, batteries, etc. Before that, he worked as a software engineer on the Manufacturing Execution System in the semiconductor division of Samsung Electronics from September 2003 through 2011.

speakers.name: Zhaopeng Chen
speakers.bio: Zhaopeng Chen is the founder and chief executive officer of Agile Robots. Under his leadership, Agile Robots has experienced rapid global growth by combining artificial intelligence and robotics to advance physical AI for smart manufacturing. Headquartered in Munich, Agile Robots employs more than 2,500 people from 60 countries and has installed over 20,000 robotic solutions worldwide. Zhaopeng studied engineering in China and earned his PhD at the German Aerospace Center (DLR).

speakers.name: Rainer Brehm
speakers.bio:
Rainer Brehm is the Chief Operations Officer (COO) for the automation business and Chief Technology Officer (CTO) at Siemens Digital Industries. In these roles, he leads the development of visionary technologies—including Industrial AI and software defined automation—and drives the strategic evolution of Siemens’ automation portfolio. His work focuses on making manufacturing more adaptive, sustainable, and autonomous, guided by his overarching ambition to automate automation.
Before taking on his current responsibilities, Rainer served as CEO of Siemens Factory Automation and previously held several leadership positions within the company, including Vice President of Automation Products and Systems for Digital Industries. His international experience includes serving in Brazil as Division Head for Process Industries & Drives and Power & Gas.
Born and educated in Germany, Rainer holds a degree in electrical engineering.

speakers.name: Marc Segura
speakers.bio: Marc Segura is President of ABB Robotics, appointed in January 2022. He drives the development and deployment of Autonomous Versatile Robotics (AVR™), accelerating Physical AI to transform the future of industry and manufacturing. Since joining ABB in 1998, he has held multiple leadership roles and oversaw ABB Robotics’ expansion into logistics, healthcare, retail, and food & beverage. Under his leadership, ABB Robotics has become the only company with a fully integrated, AI‑powered portfolio spanning robots, cobots, and autonomous mobile robots. ABB Robotics employs approximately 7,000 people worldwide, with manufacturing sites in the United States, China, and Sweden.

speakers.name: Roslyn Stuart
speakers.bio:
Roslyn Stuart serves as the vice president of synergy creation at Hitachi Digital, where she leads and accelerates the establishment of advanced digital solutions across the green energy and mobility sectors. In this role, Roslyn works closely with Hitachi Energy and Hitachi Rail, uniting the technological strengths of Hitachi Digital Services and GlobalLogic to co-create next-generation innovations that strengthen Hitachi’s global digital ecosystem. This ecosystems includes building external partnerships working very closely with NVIDIA, and has launched HMAX accelerated by NVIDIA.

Prior to joining Hitachi Digital, Roslyn successfully ran Hitachi Rail in Australia. Recognized for deep cross-industry expertise, Roslyn was invited by Hitachi Ltd. to help shape the One-Hitachi business strategy, fostering collaboration between Hitachi Energy, Hitachi Rail, and the broader Digital & Services organizations—including Hitachi Digital Services, Hitachi Vantara, and GlobalLogic—to accelerate digital transformation for sustainable, profitable growth.

With over 20 years of global experience in systems engineering and digital transformation, Roslyn has delivered industry-defining programs—including leading Rio Tinto’s AutoHaul, the world’s first fully autonomous heavy-haul rail system, which was recognized in the 2020 Guinness Book of Records as the world’s heaviest robot.

Earlier in Roslyn’s career, she developed advanced through-water communication systems for naval customers across Australia, the U.K., and the United States. Roslyn is recognized for authentic leadership and a commitment to uniting global teams to drive innovation and societal impact.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: 通过触觉 AI 解锁高自由度灵巧操作
sessions.abstract:
随着集成触觉感知的高自由度（High-DoF）灵巧手产品出现——例如 SharpaWave——机器人灵巧操作以及高质量数据采集迎来了新的发展机遇。
本次分享将介绍如何利用触觉信息来：1)训练模型，使机械手能够根据接触状态自适应调整行为，从而实现精细的操作动作，2)提升操作数据采集的效率与质量，3)缩小在接触丰富的任务中从仿真到现实（Sim-to-Real）的差距。演讲还将涵盖一系列实用方法与实现方案，包括：1) 在 NVIDIA Isaac Sim 中对触觉灵巧机械手进行建模与仿真，2)通过仿真中的强化学习生成训练数据，3)在真实硬件实验中进行模型训练与验证

speakers.name: 雪洲 朱
speakers.bio: 朱雪洲现任 Sharpa 研究副总裁，专注于触觉传感及相关算法的研究。在加入 Sharpa 之前，他在三维传感与半导体行业深耕十年。他毕业于清华大学工程物理系，获得学士和博士学位。

sessions.title: Architecting AI Systems That Power Business Growth in Sports
sessions.abstract: Learn how LALIGA is transforming its business through the most ambitious AI program in global sport, powered by Globant and its sports division, Sportian. This session explores how the league is using agentic AI to build connected intelligence across operations, competition management, content, marketing, sporting performance, broadcast, and fan engagement. In particular, we'll examine an NVIDIA-enabled use case that allows LALIGA to optimize match scheduling and drive tangible growth in broadcast audiences and stadium attendance. See how AI can be implemented in a secure, practical, and scalable way to accelerate business performance and strengthen a league’s global position.

speakers.name: Sepi Motamedi
speakers.bio: Sepi Motamedi leads marketing for live media and sports at NVIDIA, where she works with broadcasters, sports organizations, and technology partners to bring AI into every stage of live production. She champions real-time AI, accelerated computing, and cloud-native platforms to enable new levels of speed, scale, and creativity in live content. Through close collaboration with partners and media companies across the ecosystem, she advances the next generation of AI-driven production, distribution, and fan engagement.

speakers.name: Carolina Dolan Chandler
speakers.bio:
Carolina is the chief technology officer for media, entertainment, sports, and hospitality at Globant, where she helps some of the world’s most iconic brands reinvent how they connect with customers through AI-powered transformation. From creating next-generation fan engagement platforms to digitizing physical spaces, she brings together technology, creativity, and business strategy.

Since joining Globant in 2006, Carolina shaped the company’s evolution across delivery, marketing, and digital strategy in previous roles as technical director, global marketing director, and chief digital officer. She draws from extensive cross-industry experience spanning finance, retail, and entertainment.

speakers.name: Gonzalo Zarza
speakers.bio:
Gonzalo has a proven record as a researcher and contributor to the fields of fault tolerance and high performance computing. He also speaks at conferences and events about big data and AI.

Gonzalo currently holds the chief data officer position at Sportian, the joint venture between Globant and LALIGA. Previously, Gonzalo served as global head of data and AI in Globant, leading a global team of 1,100-plus highly skilled professionals with a strong emphasis on data engineering and strategy, across North America, Europe, Asia, and Latin America. He holds a Ph.D. in high performance computing awarded by the Universitat Autònoma de Barcelona.

speakers.name: Javier Gil Fernandez
speakers.bio:
Javier is a mechanical engineer who combines his passion for technology with dedication to the education sector. He's worked in various technology companies and served as an associate professor in several master's programs (University of Barcelona, LALIGA Business School, and EUDE Business School).

Since joining LALIGA in 2022, Javier has led LALIGA XTRA and other international projects, with a special focus on business growth and cross-disciplinary collaboration. As head of AI implementation and development, Javier leverages his innovative expertise to integrate AI into process redefinition, with a strategic focus on using AI as a transformative tool to reshape the sports industry.

sessions.title: China AI Networking Day - 迎接 10 亿瓦级 AI 工厂的时代
sessions.abstract: 当代理式 AI、生成式 AI 重塑产业边界，当数据中心成为 AI 工厂，海量算力、算存协同与能效优化成为新一代 10 亿瓦级（Gigawatt）数据中心的基石，一场定义未来的技术盛宴即将启幕！本场线上活动将围绕下一代 AI 工作负载对超大规模网络、无限互连能力和全数据中心综合能效来展开，从 NVIDIA、业界领先客户、合作伙伴以及科研学者的角度来探讨领先的 NVIDIA® NVLink™ ，NVIDIA® Quantum-X InfiniBand、NVIDIA® Spectrum-X™ 以太网、NVIDIA® BlueField® DPU/SuperNIC™ 、NVIDIA® ConnectX® SuperNIC™ 网络平台及其最佳实践，特别是专为大规模 AI 而设计的 NVIDIA® Spectrum-X/XGS 以太网络及其相应的软、硬件优化参考设计如何助力 AI 工厂实现数百万级 GPU 的无缝扩展，缩短部署时间，并优化整体性能和功耗。

speakers.name: Qingchun Song
speakers.bio: NVIDIA 网络亚太区高级总监，原 Mellanox 公司亚太区市场开发高级总监，首席架构师，技术总监。

speakers.name: 丽波 马
speakers.bio: 负责 NVIDIA 中国网络市场营销，包括网络产品和技术在各行业的推广工作。她在高科技行业拥有二十年以上的经验，长期专注于人工智能和数据中心网络领域。

speakers.name: Gaofeng Feng
speakers.bio: 负责 NVIDIA 公司在中国区的高性能网络技术市场方案，提供关键大客户的技术支持和方案选型，长期从事云计算、人工智能，高性能计算行业支持，在 计算系统架构，软件优化等有多年积累。

speakers.name: Jingxun Wang
speakers.bio: NVIDIA 网络解决方案架构师，对网络技术（尤其是 DPU 和 SuperNIC）有深入的了解，并且在与中国顶级云客户合作，在云端集成和部署 NVIDIA 领先的网络技术方面拥有丰富的经验。

speakers.name: 荣来 贾
speakers.bio: 本硕毕业于哈尔滨工业大学，2012 年加入腾讯，2016 年加入百度凤巢，2021 年加入快手负责推荐模型存储架构。

speakers.name: 永青 席
speakers.bio: 在网络领域发表多篇国际顶级会议论文（如 SIGCOMM和 NSDI 等），十余篇授权发明专利，目前是阿里智算集群网络架构设计和规划负责人，主导设计和落地阿里云 HPN 智算集群网络架构。

speakers.name: Yixin Zhu
speakers.bio: 北京大学心理与认知科学学院助理教授、博士生导师，博雅青年学者，兼任北京大学人工智能研究院院长助理、计算认知与常识推理研究中心执行主任。入选海外优青、北京市科技新星，UCLA 统计系博士，师从朱松纯教授。聚焦通用人工智能认知推理研究，融合高阶常识与原始感官信号构建交互式 AI，赋予机器抽象推理能力。相关成果获 Science 等顶刊头条报道，被国内外数十家主流媒体关注，在人机协作领域具国际影响力。

speakers.name: 晓平 卢
speakers.bio: 澳门科技大学计算机科学与工程学院教授，深耕大数据驱动的高性能计算与人工智能算法研究。2007 年入职该校，2018 年牵头筹办 “数据科学” 硕士课程。主持 / 参与多项澳门及国内外科研项目，发表 SCI 论文 50 余篇。曾赴芬兰、美国顶尖高校访学合作，在小行星建模、双体目标识别等领域取得突破性成果，相关论文发表于多个 Top 期刊。2023 年成为澳门首位 NVIDIA 校园大使，推动深度学习课程教学及 DPU 技术推广。

sessions.title: Accelerate GPU Scientific Computing With nvmath-python
sessions.abstract: In 2024, NVIDIA unveiled nvmath-python, a library designed to bridge the gap between Python scientific community and NVIDIA CUDA-X math libraries, and it’s now generally available. You’ll learn what makes nvmath-python a useful addition for GPU-accelerated computing with Python, along with how to use the library’s unique capabilities to reach extreme levels of performance to solve cutting-edge scientific problems previously unimaginable in Python.

speakers.name: Sergey Maydanov
speakers.bio: Sergey Maydanov is a senior software engineering manager at NVIDIA leading the nvmath-python team. He holds a master's degree in applied mathematics with a specialization in computer arithmetic, special functions, and random number generation.

speakers.name: Aart Bik
speakers.bio: Aart J.C. Bik received his master’s degree from Utrecht University and his PhD from Leiden University. He was a principal engineer at Intel, where he was the lead compiler architect of automatic vectorization. At Google he worked on the large-scale graph processing system Pregel, Google Glass, the Android optimizing compiler, and the MLIR Sparsifier. He is now a distinguished engineer at NVIDIA, researching sparsity in AI.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries

sessions.title: Autonomous AI Factories: A Technical Preview of Agent-Native Production
sessions.abstract:
For two decades, the "self-healing system" was just a theory. Today, it's an architectural reality. This session unveils the blueprint for the fully autonomous AI factory—a cognitive system where fleets of specialized agents replace static operations such as issues detection and troubleshooting to deliver true self-governance.

We'll move beyond the hype of simple chatbots to deconstruct a multi-agent architecture capable of complex reasoning, predictive failure analysis, and continuous self-improvement. But we won’t just tell you about it; we will show you.

This session features a high-stakes LIVE DEMO of an autonomous multi-agent swarm connected to a large-scale production system. Watch in real time as agents detect (and predict) anomalies and use advanced tools for troubleshooting as the first self-healing AI platform for autonomous AI factories.

speakers.name: JP Vasseur
speakers.bio: JP Vasseur is senior distinguished engineer and chief architect at NVIDIA, leading the design of autonomous, agentic data centers. A pioneer in networking and AI for over 30 years, he previously served as a Cisco Fellow and VP, deploying large-scale AI solutions. With a Ph.D. and over 750 patents, JP is a globally recognized inventor advancing the intersection of generative AI, networking, and neuroscience.

speakers.name: César Martinez Spessot
speakers.bio: Cesar is the Technical Head of AI Infrastructure at Microsoft Research, leading the end-to-end engineering of high-performance platforms that power next-generation AI. He holds 60+ patents across AI infrastructure, SRE, networking, and HPC. Cesar bridges cutting-edge research with industrial-grade production, architecting resilient AI Factory environments spanning massive GPU clusters, high-bandwidth, low-latency fabrics, and orchestration systems for large-scale distributed training. His work emphasizes reliability, unified observability, and deep optimization (from infrastructure efficiency to model quantization) along with automation and self-service to maximize researcher agility.

sessions.title: Learn how New AI Coding Agents and Tools Unlock GPU Performance for Everyone
sessions.abstract: Whether you're hand-tuning CUDA kernels for the latest GPUs or vibe-coding your way to the next great AI app, intelligent developer tools are here to help. This session will put you on the fast track to bug-free GPU-accelerated code by introducing the latest developer tool technologies from NVIDIA. We'll start with a brief overview of the free tools available to all developers, and then dive deep on the latest features that provide even more insights into application behavior and performance. These include Python, Tile, and AI framework profiling enhancements, new debugging and cloud-integrated workflows, and customized AI copilots to help along the way. No matter what level you're focused on in the software stack, you'll leave this session with new ideas and next steps to unlock the full potential of the latest GPU platforms.

speakers.name: Jackson Marusarz
speakers.bio: Jackson Marusarz is the product manager for Compute Developer Tools at NVIDIA. He focuses on how tools can be used to enable all developers to profile, debug, and optimize CUDA code easily and efficiently. Jackson holds a master’s degree in computer engineering from the University of Colorado, Boulder.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Profilers / Debuggers / Code Analysis

sessions.title: Advances in Decision Intelligence
sessions.abstract:
Explore the latest advancements in GPU-accelerated Mixed Integer Programming (MIP) solver, generating fast, high-quality feasible solutions, which is a critical capability for dynamic decision making. You’ll learn about the algorithms behind the cuOpt Linear Programming (LP) solver, which earned the top spot as an open source solver on the Mittelman LPfeas benchmark. 
We’ll explore how Nemo Agent Toolkit and cuOpt optimization are tackling some of today’s most complex real-world decision problems and share our latest work on a modular skill-based decision intelligence agent that enables users to generate optimized decisions and plans through natural language.

Additionally, you'll learn how Kinaxis achieved speedups of up to 14X on key supply chain planning use cases by integrating NVIDIA cuOpt Linear Programming (LP)​ on NVIDIA GPUs into the Maestro platform for scenario planning.

speakers.name: Alex Fender
speakers.bio: Alex Fender is the director of engineering and applied research for decision optimization at NVIDIA. His team developed NVIDIA cuOpt, the first open source platform for GPU-accelerated optimization and decision intelligence. Earlier, as a founding engineer of RAPIDS, Alex led graph analytics development scaling to trillions of edges across financial services, cybersecurity, and social networks. PhD in computer science and Stanford executive education.

speakers.name: Dane Henshall
speakers.bio: Dane Henshall leads the integration of GPU-accelerated optimization into the Maestro scenario planning engine. With over a decade of experience in high-performance algorithms, he works across the spectrum of core technologies that enable Kinaxis to solve the world's largest supply chain planning problems, from the parallel query engine of their in-memory database to the custom CUDA kernels that are integrated alongside NVIDIA cuOpt.

sessions.title: Measuring Brand, Fame, and Virality Using Spatio-Temporal Causal Graphs
sessions.abstract:
We introduce a GPU-accelerated system for decomposing engagement into Brand (persistent equity), Fame (spatial reach), and Virality (temporal dynamics). Standard analysis cannot separate these effects because they are mathematically entangled: observing any two determines the third. We address this by using spatio-temporal causal graphs that explicitly encode influence structure, with CUDA kernels for CSR propagation and distributed Shapley computation across GPU clusters.
Built on an Online Evolving Spike Neural Network distributed pipeline, the system uses variance-optimal stratified sampling for coalition evaluation and processes 100,000+ marketing time series at daily resolution via custom CUDA kernels and C++ simulation bridges. The framework enables teams to identify whether observed variation is driven by content quality, audience segmentation, or timing effects—separating forces that traditional attribution methods conflate. This turns engagement decomposition into actionable budget allocation grounded in large-scale causal inference.

speakers.name: George Mathew
speakers.bio: George Mathew is Head of AI Science at Alembic, where he works with the team on the development of causal inference, graph neural networks, and real-time AI systems on NVIDIA DGX H100 infrastructure. Alembic builds AI that connects marketing spend to revenue for Fortune 500 enterprises-replacing guesswork with causality. Alembic operates one of the world's fastest supercomputers for commercial AI inference. His personal area of interest is M-Statistics-a framework for exact inference when data is small or noisy. George holds multiple degrees and trained in statistics, analytics, and computer science at the Indian Statistical Institute, Carnegie Mellon, CUNY, and the Thayer School of Engineering at Dartmouth.

speakers.name: Tomás Puig
speakers.bio: Tomás Puig is the CEO and founder of Alembic, where he leads the creation of GPU-driven causal intelligence systems used by Fortune 500 companies. With a background in AI, large-scale data processing, and system modeling, he focuses on building technologies that reveal true cause-and-effect across complex enterprise environments.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: GTC 2026 Keynote
sessions.abstract: In this keynote, NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang looks ahead to the future of accelerated computing and AI, and how they will shape the next era of computing across every industry.

speakers.name: Jensen Huang
speakers.bio: Jensen Huang founded NVIDIA in 1993 and has served as president, CEO, and member of the board of directors since its inception. Since its founding, NVIDIA has pioneered accelerated computing. The company's invention of the GPU in 1999 sparked the growth of the PC gaming market, redefined computer graphics, and ignited the era of modern AI. NVIDIA is now driving the platform shift of accelerated computing and generative AI, transforming the world's largest industries and profoundly impacting society.

sessions.title: Real-Time Generative Video Re-Styling for Gaming in the Next-Generation Datacenter
sessions.abstract: Decart’s Lucy Restyle is a World Transformation Model that reimagines any video, game, or camera feed into a new digital world in real time. We will dive into how Decart and NVIDIA optimized Lucy Restyle inference for low-latency, cost-efficient cloud deployment on NVIDIA B40 GPUs through GeForce NOW (GFN). Finally, we’ll demonstrate how to harness Lucy Restyle to Lucy Restyle completely restyle titles via the ultimate modding platform: RTX Remix.

speakers.name: Bhavani Gopalakrishna Rao
speakers.bio: Bhavani Gopalakrishna Rao leads the architecture and development of ultra-low-latency streaming technology that powers the Graphics Delivery Network (GDN) - NVIDIA’s interactive AI and graphics streaming platform. Her team designs and delivers highly optimized end-to-end streaming pipelines to achieve imperceptibly low latency while preserving audio-visual fidelity in diverse network conditions. Bhavani and her team also actively collaborate with technical communities, standards bodies and partners around the globe to advance the state of the art in interactive, low-latency streaming for AI, graphics, and cloud-rendered workloads.

speakers.name: Ethan Petersen
speakers.bio: Ethan is a staff member at Decart with a focus on API and platform development. Previously, he was a staff software engineer for managed AI at Crusoe, and before that, the technical co-founder of a startup developing AI products for the United States Special Operations Command.

speakers.name: Alex Dunn
speakers.bio: Alex Dunn is Director of Engineering at NVIDIA Lightspeed Studios, where he leads teams working across real-time graphics, AI, and emerging technologies shaping the future of interactive experiences. A graduate of Abertay University’s Games Technology course, Alex began his career in graphics programming on console platforms before expanding into advanced rendering, machine learning, and next-generation technology development. At NVIDIA, his work focuses on pushing the boundaries of innovation to deliver breakthroughs in visual fidelity, intelligence, and player experience.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: Accelerate the Physical AI Era With Digital Twins and Real-Time Simulation
sessions.abstract: Join Rev Lebaredian, NVIDIA’s VP of Omniverse and simulation technology, as he shares how autonomous, OpenClaw-based agents, digital twins and real-time simulation—powered by OpenUSD and NVIDIA’s accelerated computing platform, libraries, and ecosystem—are accelerating breakthroughs across industries in the era of physical AI. Discover their impact on everything from product design and manufacturing to robotics, autonomous vehicles, smart cities, climate science, and space exploration.

speakers.name: Rev Lebaredian
speakers.bio: Rev Lebaredian is vice president of Omniverse and simulation technology at NVIDIA. Lebaredian has over 30 years of experience in the computer graphics industry, working in roles at Warner Brothers Digital and Disney Dream Quest Images before launching his own venture, Steamboat Software. He joined NVIDIA in 2002 and led the development of several real-time, multi-GPU scalable rendering, physics simulation, and AI platforms and technologies. Today, he leads the product, engineering, and research teams of NVIDIA Omniverse, a software platform and operating system enabling developers to build physical AI and robotic system simulation applications.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Healthcare Reimagined: Bridging Digital Intelligence and Physical Autonomy
sessions.abstract: Robotics and AI are redefining healthcare, accelerating diagnostics, surgery, patient care, and operational efficiency.
