We'll highlight how NVIDIA technologies drive this transformation, from physical AI systems that perform precision tasks and address workforce shortages to digital AI agents that interpret multimodal data, predict patient needs, and guide decision-making. We'll dive into the latest NVIDIA technology—spanning cutting-edge hardware, AI factories, and next-gen simulation—enabling both physical and digital robots. You’ll see how these technologies unify sensing, perception, planning, control, and generative reasoning into deployable devices. You'll also learn how medical technology developers are building on NVIDIA’s ecosystem of tools, reference workflows, and partner solutions to accelerate R&D, improve system intelligence, and bring autonomous capabilities to life.

speakers.name: Prerna Dogra
speakers.bio: Prerna Dogra is a senior manager for healthcare AI products at NVIDIA, where she leads the Clara Application Framework and the collaborative open-source initiative Project MONAI. She is responsible for productizing domain-specialized developer platforms, enabling researchers and data scientists to transform the healthcare imaging industry with technology for real-time analysis, artificial intelligence, and advanced visualization.

sessions.title: The Agentic Revolution for Specialty Care, Powered by AI for Better Lives and Bottom Lines
sessions.abstract: Agentic AI and generative AI are not just incremental upgrades—they represent a fundamental paradigm shift in healthcare, transforming myriad medical specialties in their own unique ways. From managing complex cancer pathways to scaling mental health support, autonomous AI agents are moving beyond simple data analysis to become proactive collaborators, reasoning, planning, and executing complex tasks with minimal human oversight. This transformation is making a demonstrable difference in saving patient lives and driving significant savings for hospitals and health systems. This session will bring together leaders in diverse specialty care—where the domain challenges are complex and distinct—to showcase real-world agentic AI implementations and quantified outcomes.

speakers.name: Brad Genereaux
speakers.bio: Brad Genereaux is the global lead for healthcare alliances at NVIDIA, focused on developer relations in digital health and medical imaging. With over 22 years in healthcare IT, he advocates for integrating AI into clinical practice at enterprise scale. Brad is also a leader in healthcare interoperability, contributing to standards like HL7, FHIR, DICOM, and IHE to help make healthcare a seamless experience for all.

speakers.name: Ricardo Rei
speakers.bio:
As the head of AI research at Sword Health, Ricardo Rei leads efforts to transform healthcare through artificial intelligence, with a specific focus on LLMs for mental health and multi-modal architectures for physical therapy.
Previously, he created the standard machine translation evaluation metric COMET during his Ph.D. at at Instituto Superior Técnico and Carnegie Mellon University. In the generative space, he was a core contributor to the multilingual EuroLLM and the lead researcher behind TowerLLM (70B), the model that secured first place in the WMT2024 translation competition.

speakers.name: Jaya Savkar
speakers.bio: Jaya Savkar is senior vice president of product at Maven Clinic, where she leads product strategy and development for the leading digital platform for women’s and family health. Her work focuses on advancing AI-enabled care, personalization at scale, and outcomes-driven experiences. She has spent her career building and scaling AI-powered consumer platforms globally. Previously, she led growth for Facebook Reels, Live, and Meta’s unified video platform, and served as VP of product at Shutterfly, helping scale the business from $400 million to $2 billion. Earlier, she launched Snapfish’s first major photo book platform, helping establish the category globally.

speakers.name: Mainul Mondal
speakers.bio:
Inspired by his mother’s health journey, Mainul Mondal founded Ellipsis Health to improve healthcare via empathy-driven agentic AI. In 2025, Ellipsis released Sage, an autonomous AI care manager. Sage meets patients where they are through adaptive communication, providing the empathetic support required for mental health and chronic care. Trained on 3M+ minutes of real clinical conversations, Sage manages workflows from assessments to care gap closure, reducing avoidable utilization and freeing care teams to focus on high-value interactions.

A Rockefeller Fellow and WEF member, Mainul is also an award-winning painter, merging technology and empathy to make quality care accessible to all.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Beyond the Workcell: Scaling Robotics Workflows Across the Factory Floor
sessions.abstract:
Scaling robot automation beyond isolated workcells remains a critical barrier for modern manufacturers, who struggle to coordinate diverse machines in complex, ever-changing production environments.

This session explores how leading robotics innovators—IdealWorks, Kuka, Universal Robots, and Vention—are breaking through these silos by integrating NVIDIA Omniverse libraries, open frameworks, and CUDA-accelerated tools. Discover how foundation AI models and simulation are accelerating robot development, streamlining digital twin integration, and enabling seamless coordination between heterogeneous robots across both high-mix/low-volume and scaled production settings.

speakers.name: Jimmy Nassif
speakers.bio: Jimmy Nassif is the chief technology officer and co-founder of idealworks, a BMW Group spinoff developing the logistics of the future through a scalable robotics ecosystem spanning cloud automation, mobile robots, and an intelligent orchestration platform. Prior to idealworks, he led the AI & Digitization Lab at BMW Group Logistics, where he pioneered internet of things, AI, and simulation-driven solutions for manufacturing and logistics. Jimmy is also the co-founder of Sordi.ai and author of a book on synthetic data’s role in shaping the industrial metaverse. His work focuses on safe, reliable, and customer-centric automation, leveraging robotics, digitization, and simulation.

speakers.name: Brian Klobucher
speakers.bio: Brian Klobucher leads Omniverse ecosystem at NVIDIA, focusing on supercharging partners with technologies for scalable, collaborative, and AI-enabled 3D digital worlds. Brian works closely with business leaders and technology developers to foster strong relationships and bring innovations to life.

speakers.name: Anders Billesø Beck
speakers.bio: Anders Billesø Beck is vice president, AI robotics products at Universal Robots, where he leads the global AI product strategy for the company’s collaborative robot platform with a focus on innovation, adaptability, and the AI ecosystem. He is widely recognized as a pioneer in flexible and collaborative automation, with more than 20 years of experience advancing product development, new applications, and smart manufacturing.

speakers.name: Etienne Lacroix
speakers.bio: Etienne is a product-driven business leader and entrepreneur building and transforming engineering-driven companies. At Vention, McKinsey, and GE, he developed new industrial technologies and supported manufacturers across sectors from aerospace to consumer electronics. He holds an M.B.A. from Harvard Business School and a mechanical engineering degree from École de Technologie Supérieure in Montreal.

speakers.name: Melonee Wise
speakers.bio: Melonee Wise is chief product officer for software and AI at KUKA, where she leads the company’s software, data, and AI strategy across industrial automation. Her work focuses on building the platforms and systems that turn advances in AI and robotics into scalable, reliable, and deployable automation across diverse markets and industries. Previously, Melonee co-founded Fetch Robotics, which was acquired by Zebra Technologies, and served as chief product officer at Agility Robotics, helping bring humanoid robots into real-world industrial deployment.

speakers.name: Justin Johnson
speakers.bio: Justin Johnson is a cofounder at World Labs working on large-scale models to advance spatial intelligence. Prior to that he was an Assistant Professor at the University of Michigan and a Research Scientist at Facebook AI Research. He completed his PhD at Stanford University, advised by Fei-Fei Li.

sessions.title: Practical Context Engineering: Eliminate Bugs With High-Signal AI Code Reviews
sessions.abstract:
AI-assisted coding has sped up code generation, but human-only code reviews are now the bottleneck. Too many pull requests (PRs), not enough reviewer bandwidth. This session shows how you can build the right context-engineering architecture that helps LLMs deliver high-signal AI code reviews. 

Catch logical or functional bugs, memory leaks, security vulnerabilities, code refactors, edge cases, and more. You'll see how bringing in the right context from external datasets (such as code graphs, PR history, issue tickets, MCP servers, and linters) yields better-quality reviews of PR diffs. You will leave with a practical playbook to pilot and scale AI code reviews that increase your release velocity with fewer bugs.

speakers.name: David Loker
speakers.bio: David Loker is VP of AI at CodeRabbit, leading agentic AI for code reviews and developer workflows. An entrepreneur and award-winning researcher, he has built large-scale ML systems since 2007. He’s published 12 papers at NeurIPS, ICML, and AAAI, and was an early generative AI pioneer. From startups to hands-on engineering, David pairs deep technical rigor with product vision to turn research into real value.

speakers.name: Harjot Gill
speakers.bio:
Harjot is CEO of CodeRabbit, the pioneer of AI code reviews. Previously, he co-founded and led FluxNinja, delivering rate/concurrency limiting, caching, and request prioritization for scalable Gen AI apps. Before that, he was senior director of technology at Nutanix, and co-founded Netsil, a network-centric observability startup acquired by Nutanix. 

Specialties: category creation, visionary products, early GTM, and enterprise consumerization.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: An Introduction to the Newton Physics Engine for Robotics
sessions.abstract: Robots learn faster and safer in simulation, but complex joints, balance, and contact-rich motions challenge current physics engines. Developers need high-fidelity simulation so virtual training transfers safely to real robots. This session with NVIDIA, Google DeepMind, Skild AI, and Disney Research introduces Newton, an open-source, GPU-accelerated physics engine built on NVIDIA Warp and OpenUSD to advance robot learning. Newton’s flexible design and multiple solvers enable realistic simulations—like walking through snow, or handling cups and fruit—and reliable deployment in the physical world. Explore Newton’s architecture, integration into robot-learning pipelines, and ways to develop new solvers for safer, more capable robots across diverse environments and tasks.

speakers.name: Miles Macklin
speakers.bio: Miles Macklin is a Director of Engineering at NVIDIA, leading the development of NVIDIA Warp, a high-performance Python framework for differentiable and GPU-accelerated simulation. He holds a Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of Copenhagen, with research focusing on physics-based animation and robotics. His recent focus is on applying Warp to enable accelerated computing for CAE/CFD, and Simulation AI.

speakers.name: Moritz Baecher
speakers.bio: Moritz Bächer is the associate lab director of Disney’s Zurich-based robotics team, where he leads a strategic program focusing on the development of novel model- and learning-based tools for the design and control of believable robotic characters. His core expertise is the optimal design and control of both soft and rigid systems, using a combination of differentiable simulation and reinforcement learning. Prior to joining Disney, Moritz received his Ph.D. from the Harvard School of Engineering and Applied Sciences and his master’s degree from ETH Zurich.

speakers.name: Erik Frey
speakers.bio: Erik Frey teaches robots to do useful things using simulation.  He is an author of MuJoCo, a popular open source physics simulator, and is also a founding member of the Newton open source simulator collaboration between Google DeepMind, NVIDIA, and Disney.  At Google DeepMind, Erik uses simulation to improve the capabilities of robotics foundation models such as Gemini Robotics, see https://deepmind.google/models/gemini-robotics/ for more information.

speakers.name: Deepak Pathak
speakers.bio: Deepak Pathak is CEO and co-founder of Skild AI and a professor at Carnegie Mellon University. He's known for pioneering contributions to AI including self-supervised learning, artificial curiosity, and adaptive robot learning. At Skild AI, he's developing an AI foundation model for robotics. Deepak has received several prestigious honors including MIT Technology Review's 35under35, Okawa, IIT Kanpur Young Alumnus Award, and multiple best paper awards.

sessions.title: Top-K Selection at the Speed of Light
sessions.abstract: Explore new techniques for top-k selection, a critical operation for accelerating scientific computing and mixture of experts that efficiently extracts the most relevant items from massive datasets. Dive into the design of our state-of-the-art GPU algorithm, cub::DeviceTopK, and learn how leveraging this primitive can accelerate high-throughput workloads by over 160x compared to previous methods.

speakers.name: Christina Zhang
speakers.bio: Christina (Jingrong) Zhang is a developer technology engineer. Her work focuses on optimizing parallel CUDA algorithms and accelerating deep learning workloads. Collaborating closely with colleagues, she has contributed to the design, implementation, and optimization of AIR Top K and Top P parallel algorithms, which are widely used in applications such as approximate nearest neighbor search and sampling operations in large language models. She is currently working on the routing component of mixture of experts models. She received her Ph.D. from the Institute of Computing Technology at the Chinese Academy of Sciences.

speakers.name: Elias Stehle
speakers.bio: Elias Stehle is a senior systems software engineer at NVIDIA specializing in fundamental CUDA/C++ algorithms. He is part of the CCCL team, maintaining the CUB and Thrust libraries.

speakers.name: Yue Weng
speakers.bio: Yue Weng is a developer technology engineer. His work focuses on analyzing and optimizing large language model inference. He has contributed to the implementation and optimization of speculative decoding in TensorRT-LLM, such as MTP, Eagle, and Medusa, as well as other runtime feature development for the framework. Currently, he is collaborating with colleagues on the optimization of Top K algorithms. Yue received his master’s degree from Sun Yat-sen University, where his research focused on GPU resource management.

sessions.title: Scaling Trustworthy Multi-Modal Video Intelligence: A Life Cycle-Aware Approach for Domain-Specific Real-World Platforms
sessions.abstract: This session will demonstrate how to move beyond basic spatial-temporal modeling and implement domain-specific video intelligence using techniques inspired by NVIDIA VSS to build robust, life cycle-aware platforms for real-world applications like brand safety and contextual advertising. Explore new solutions for accelerating large-scale video ingestion with modern multi-modal tooling like NVIDIA NeMo Curator, and discover strategies for achieving high-throughput inference through embedding-level optimization and evolving to next-generation, end-to-end validation frameworks. We'll present proven methodologies with customer success examples from enterprises like Samsung Electronics, LG Electronics, and Kenvue to help you deploy enterprise-grade, trustworthy AI systems that analyze video content at scale.

speakers.name: Jaeho Oh
speakers.bio: Jaeho Oh is the chief executive officer of PYLER, building trustworthy, safe, and scalable video understanding AI. During high school, he graduated from the Port Logistics System Decentralization project, and as a researcher at Sungkyunkwan University ISRC he focused on investigating multi-modal video captioning. He was named to the 2025 Forbes 30 Under 30 Asia list and the 2019 Talent Award of Korea.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: Build Next-Generation Foundation Models in Finance
sessions.abstract:
Financial transactions share key properties with language: temporal ordering, contextual dependencies, and patterns emerging from surrounding context. This session presents a framework for building transaction foundation models using masked prediction and next-item forecasting to learn rich representations of customer behavior without labeled data.

We'll cover our end-to-end approach—from tokenizing transactions into sequences to self-supervised objectives capturing spending rhythms, cross-merchant correlations, and anomaly patterns. Learn how this architecture drives improvements across fraud detection, credit risk, and behavior prediction while drastically reducing labeled data requirements.

speakers.name: Tadas Krisciunas
speakers.bio: Tadas Krisciunas is an accomplished data science leader with a strong foundation in credit risk modeling, holding an MMathPhil in Mathematics & Philosophy from the University of Oxford where he achieved a First in Part C and a Distinction in Prelims. Most recently leading Credit Data Science at Revolut from 2020 to 2025, he was the department's first data science hire and successfully scaled the function into five specialized teams while driving a three-year strategy and engaging in regulatory relationships with the ECB and FCA. His technical expertise was further honed as a Lead Data Scientist at Zopa, where he led IFRS9 PD model development and engineered deployment frameworks using Python and Kubernetes. Complementing his professional technical rigor, Tadas maintains a deep intellectual engagement with the humanities, having won a Gold Medal at the International Philosophy Olympiad and continuing as a regular reader on philosophical issues.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Pre-Trained / Foundation Models

sessions.title: 机器人仿真实战：从资产到真机部署的全流程讲解
sessions.abstract: 跨越 Sim2Real 鸿沟是通用机器人落地的核心挑战。本 Session 将分享一套基于 NVIDIA Omniverse 的端到端工程实践，展示如何利用高保真 USD 资产构建“训练-评估-优化”闭环。我们将结合工厂搬运与家庭精细操作的实战案例，探讨如何通过物理仿真解决数据稀缺与泛化性难题，实现从虚拟到现实的无缝迁移。

speakers.name: 钊 金
speakers.bio: 资深具身智能仿真工程师，拥有 5 年 Isaac Sim 开发经验。于 2025 年 6 月发布 ArtVIP 开源数据集，包括大量可活动关节物品以及 sim-ready 场景。

sessions.title: NVIDIA GPU 的 LLM 异步强化学习训练
sessions.abstract: 本次分享将介绍如何基于 NVIDIA Megatron-Core Trainer 与 vLLM/SGLang Rollouter 搭建一个高性能、可扩展的强化学习训练框架。该框架采用流式、全异步的架构设计，实现了样本生成与训练过程的完全解耦，大幅减轻了 rollout 阶段长尾样本带来的资源闲置问题，充分发挥 NVIDIA GPU 的并行算力。在此体系下，我们在 128 张 NVIDIA 加速卡上完成了 Qwen7B/Qwen-30B-A3B 等模型的强化学习实验，在效果持平的前提下，取得了 1.7 倍 ~ 2.7 倍的训练效率提升。

speakers.name: 驰 马
speakers.bio: 美团搜推平台部模型训练组负责人，负责搜推广模型、LLM 模型的训练框架建设与优化。

speakers.name: 正罡 侯
speakers.bio: 美团搜推平台部模型训练组核心成员，目前专注于 LLM 后训练的框架开发及优化工作

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: The Augmented Workforce: Build and Deploy AI Agents for Enterprise XR
sessions.abstract: The next generation of extended reality (XR) devices is set to revolutionize the hands-on workforce. In this session, learn how Ramblr, Convai, AWS and GridRaster built intelligent AI agents that deliver real-time guidance, boosting worker productivity and safety.

speakers.name: Greg Barbone
speakers.bio: Greg Barbone leads extended reality (XR) product and partner management at NVIDIA, with a mission of helping software developers and enterprise partners harness NVIDIA’s graphics and AI technologies to build cutting-edge XR solutions that deliver real value to end users. With a background in immersive media and spatial computing, Greg has worked across the XR ecosystem to bring innovative tools and platforms to market.

speakers.name: Purnendu Mukherjee
speakers.bio: Purnendu Mukherjee is the founder and CEO of Convai, the highest-rated platform for integrating conversational AI characters in simulations and virtual worlds. Before starting Convai, he was a senior deep learning engineer in NVIDIA's Conversational AI group. He focused on bringing state-of-the-art, deep learning-based speech and language models into production. Purnendu led projects that initiated major product lines and flagship AI models within NVIDIA. He is listed on several patents related to conversational AI and machine learning. Before starting at NVIDIA, Purnendu did his graduate studies at the University of Florida, with a focus on deep learning and natural language processing.

speakers.name: Bhaskar Banerjee
speakers.bio: Bhaskar is the co-founder and chief technology officer of GridRaster, leading its technological vision and engineering strategy focused on enterprise extended reality (XR) and spatial AI. With over 20 years of experience in R&D, spatial AI, XR, and wireless systems, he previously held key roles at Apple—working on immersive display technologies—and Broadcom—developing high-speed communication systems. Previously, Bhaskar was a faculty member at the University of Texas at Dallas, publishing extensively in high-speed communication systems. He holds a Ph.D. and M.S. in electrical engineering from Georgia Tech.

speakers.name: Roman Hasenbeck
speakers.bio: Roman is the co-founder and CEO of Ramblr.ai. He brings 15 years of experience in computer vision, augmented reality, and mixed reality—beginning at Metaio and continuing at Apple after the acquisition in 2015, where he worked on ARKit and Apple Vision Pro. In 2021, he co-founded Ramblr with the mission of bringing AI into the real world. Ramblr provides an end-to-end data platform for physical AI, delivering rich real-world context and enabling AI agents that can support humans throughout their day-to-day tasks.

speakers.name: Javier Figueras
speakers.bio: Javier Figueras leads Physical AI Technology Business Development and GTM strategy for Defense and Public Sector at Amazon Web Services (AWS). He specializes in enabling autonomous systems through spatial intelligence, simulation, and edge computing. With over 12 years of experience at the forefront of augmented reality and spatial computing, Javier pioneered enterprise XR adoption through deep collaborations with NVIDIA while at Magic Leap, and leading transformational initiatives across HoloLens and Azure AI at Microsoft. His deep expertise in AR/XR workflows and autonomous operations, combined with his strategic business acumen, enables him to humanize and translate complex Physical AI capabilities into mission-critical outcomes for aerospace, defense, energy, and manufacturing customers. He holds an MBA from Northwestern University's Kellogg School of Management.

sessions.topic: AR / VR - AR / VR Development Tools

sessions.title: From Prototype to Playable: Bringing Scalable ML Into "World of Tanks: HEAT"
sessions.abstract: This session explores how World of Tanks: HEAT plans to transition ML from research notebooks to live combat using NVIDIA Triton. We begin by defining the "strategic recommendation system" within a gaming context—covering data prep, model selection, and player-centric evaluation. From there, we dive into the infrastructure: why traditional in-process inference fails in high-intensity battles and how Triton standardizes deployment across CPU and GPU. Learn how we utilize ONNX, TensorRT, and dynamic batching to optimize high-frequency models and use Triton’s Model Analyzer to ensure peak performance before players hit the battlefield.

speakers.name: Dmitry Nozhnin
speakers.bio: Game product strategist Dmitry Nozhnin bridges data, monetization, and design to shape better games.

speakers.name: Dora Csillag
speakers.bio: Dora Csillag is a senior solutions architect at NVIDIA who helps customers building and deploying AI solutions, while focusing on Inference in the field of computer vision and generative AI. She was previously an applied researcher in video analytics, for road safety, border controls, and other industries.

speakers.name: Andrii Alieksieienko
speakers.bio: Andrii Alieksieienko is a Machine Learning Architect at Wargaming, specializing in designing and deploying scalable ML solutions for real-world interactive systems. He brings deep expertise in applied machine learning, MLOps, and bridging advanced AI capabilities with production game and product environments.

sessions.title: Build High-Performance Financial AI: Achieve Microsecond Latency and Scalable LLM Inference
sessions.abstract: Discover how financial institutions can accelerate real-time decision-making with high-performance AI systems built on NVIDIA’s Grace Hopper Superchip, H200 NVL, and RTX 6000 Blackwell GPUs. This session showcases breakthrough performance in time series forecasting, real-time inference, and retrieval-augmented generation for financial applications. Learn how these platforms are setting new standards in latency, throughput, and efficiency, enabling engineers to design scalable AI solutions that power trading, risk modeling, and market analytics at unprecedented speed and scale.

speakers.name: Martin Marciniszyn Mehringer
speakers.bio: Martin is a senior DevTech manager at NVIDIA. He holds a master’s degree in computer science from TU Munich and a Ph.D. in discrete mathematics at ETH Zurich. After graduating, Martin spent 15 years in the high-frequency trading industry, focusing on quantitative research and low-latency strategy development. At NVIDIA, he now focuses on optimizing deep neural network training and inference on NVIDIA GPUs for financial services applications.

speakers.name: Nikolai Markovskii
speakers.bio: Nikolay is a member of the DL Developer Technology team at NVIDIA, focusing on DL inference optimization. Nikolay has experience optimizing algorithms in HPC and holds a Ph.D. in computational physical chemistry from the University of Southern California.

sessions.title: Beauty Reinvented with L'Oréal: AI-Enabled Formulation Optimization
sessions.abstract: A beauty product has billions of possible formulations, rendering exhaustive lab testing impossible. L'Oréal is partnering with NVIDIA to accelerate and improve accuracy of in-silico formulation property prediction.

speakers.name: Guive Balooch
speakers.bio: Guive Balooch, global vice president for technology and open innovation at L'Oréal, has transformed the company into a technology leader for 18 years, overseeing the Augmented Beauty Department and Open Innovation. He's led innovations like AirLight Pro, a first-of-its-kind infrared hairdryer, and Colorsonic, an at-home hair coloring tool, and Cell BioPrint, a skincare diagnostic device, as well as strategic partnerships in biotech and longevity with global leaders like Timeline and Debut Bio. He holds degrees from the University of California Berkeley, UC San Francisco, and Stanford University.

speakers.name: Matthieu Cassier
speakers.bio: Matthieu Cassier, director of global transformation and dDigital at L'Oréal R&I, leverages the power of data, AI, tech, and 4,000 researchers to revolutionize the company's innovation model. With a background in chemical engineering and leadership experience across diverse categories and geographies—including Japan and the Southern Hemisphere—he champions a symbiotic relationship between technology and human ingenuity to drive sustainable performance.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science

sessions.title: Build Secure AI Infrastructure With DOCA
sessions.abstract: As AI factories continue to evolve, cybersecurity must protect infrastructure, applications, and emerging agentic workflows without consuming critical compute resources or impacting tokenomics. The NVIDIA DOCA software platform addresses these challenges with a suite of security microservices, frameworks, and APIs that advance protection without compromising performance. See how DOCA delivers accelerated, programmable runtime security for AI infrastructure. Powered by NVIDIA BlueField, DOCA enforces security at line rate without impacting computing performance, installing host-based software, or integrating directly into protected workloads. Learn how to apply granular network security policies at line rate, detect and block threats to AI workloads in real time, implement prompt-level defenses, and secure agentic workflows, the new frontier of AI security.

speakers.name: Ofir Arkin
speakers.bio: Ofir Arkin is a renowned information security expert with a career spanning academia, consulting, and executive roles. He's passionate about creating innovative products that address customer needs, and has introduced several industry-first technologies. Known for his dedication to mentoring, Ofir has authored numerous influential research papers and articles, and is a recognized speaker in the information security community.

speakers.name: Ariel Kit
speakers.bio: Ariel Kit manages the strategy and delivery of the NVIDIA DOCA software framework, bringing more than a decade of experience in product development in the fields of cybersecurity and embedded system-on-chip, backed up by over 12 years in R&D managerial roles. He holds a B.Sc. in communication systems engineering and an M.B.A.

sessions.title: AI in the Age of Regulation: Build Trust Through Compliance
sessions.abstract: As global AI regulation accelerates, understanding and aligning with evolving compliance standards has never been more critical. This session brings together AI and legal experts to unpack what the EU AI Act means for organizations developing and deploying AI systems today. The panel explores how companies identify and manage high-risk AI applications and support transparency, accountability, and audit readiness through robust documentation practices. Gain a comprehensive understanding of the EU AI Act framework and the Code of Conduct, as well as the enforcement mechanisms and penalties associated with noncompliance.

speakers.name: William Dunning
speakers.bio: William Dunning is AI regulatory lawyer at Simmons & Simmons LLP in London. He routinely advises AI developers and other major corporations on global AI regulatory compliance affairs and disputes. William is an expert in AI law and regulation, and is ranked by Legal 500 as a "Leading Associate" in Simmons & Simmons’ Tier 1 Artificial Intelligence practice. He has written extensively on legal issues relating to AI, including contributing chapters to "Artificial Intelligence - Law and Regulation" and "Artificial Intelligence in Finance - Challenges, Opportunities and Regulatory Developments" (both from Edward Elgar Publishing).

speakers.name: Barnaby Simkin
speakers.bio: Barnaby Simkin is the director of trustworthy AI at NVIDIA.

speakers.name: Jonathan Dotan
speakers.bio: EQTY Lab builds tools to reinvent trust in AI. As a serial founder, Jonathan has spent over 25 years navigating the intersection of technology and governance as a C-suite leader, investor, and researcher. He is also the founding director of The Starling Lab at Stanford University, where he leads pioneering applied research on hardware-accelerated cryptography. He teaches the first course on digital authenticity and provenance, and has co-written standards for Adobe and the Linux Foundation that are used across civil society and industry, including foundational models from OpenAI and Google.

speakers.name: Nikki Pope
speakers.bio: Nikki Pope is an in-house counsel at NVIDIA who leads Trustworthy AI. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Nikki was the managing director of the High Tech Law Institute at Santa Clara University and an attorney at Cooley LLP for 10 years, and on the faculty at Santa Clara Law. Nikki has also held management positions in large companies and startups in financial services, entertainment, consumer products, and web-based services and applications.

speakers.name: Antoine Gautier
speakers.bio: Antoine Gautier, co-founder and chief scientist at QuantPi, holds degrees in mathematics from EPFL and Saarland University. He's published in leading mathematical and computer science journals. He co-authored the DIN SPEC 92001-3 standard on AI explainability and actively contributes to both scientific and practical advancements in the field of trustworthy AI.

sessions.topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI

sessions.title: MLOps 101: Platforms and Processes for Building AI
sessions.abstract:
As AI adoption accelerates across every industry, teams are discovering that model quality alone is not quite enough to deliver real value. Scalable and reliable AI systems depend on robust MLOps that streamline the entire life cycle, from data to deployment. At the same time, the field is undergoing a major transformation driven by agentic AI, advanced reasoning, multi-modality, and new patterns for large-scale, disaggregated inference. 

This session introduces the foundational patterns of MLOps while also exploring how these new capabilities reshape AI design and operational strategy. We'll examine several use cases to break down the process and ideal platform for MLOps in this new era. Whether you're launching your first AI initiative or modernizing your infrastructure, this session provides the technical blueprint and best practices you need to operationalize AI at scale.

speakers.name: Michael Balint
speakers.bio: Michael is focused on cluster management, orchestration, and scheduling of NVIDIA DGX servers. Previously, Michael was a White House Presidential Innovation Fellow, where he brought his technical expertise to projects like then-Vice President Biden's Cancer Moonshot and Code.gov.

speakers.name: William Benton
speakers.bio: William works on data science product architecture at NVIDIA. He's passionate about making it easier for machine learning practitioners to benefit from advanced infrastructure and making it possible for organizations to manage ML systems. Will has also conducted research and development related to static program analysis, language runtimes, cluster configuration management, and music technology.

sessions.title: Accelerate Power Grid Interconnection for Load and Generation Using Agentic AI and Digital Twins
sessions.abstract: For AI and data center growth, time-to-power is most critical bottleneck. In the United States, average time for interconnection is between three to five years. This panel with Southwest Power Pool, GE Vernova, Southern California Edison, and ERCOT will discuss how they are using agentic AI and digital twins to better understand existing capacity, peak loads, and accelerating the interconnection process.

speakers.name: Ahsan Yousufzai
speakers.bio: Ahsan Yousufzai is globally responsible for thought leadership and building an ecosystem for energy transition by harnessing the power of AI and simulations, bringing over 20 years of experience in the development and adoption of digital technologies in the oil & gas and power sectors. Ahsan's career spans executive leadership roles at Siemens, GE, and Baker Hughes, where he led teams developing digital platforms and solutions.

speakers.name: Felek Abbas
speakers.bio: Felek Abbas is the senior vice president and chief technology and security officer at Southwest Power Pool, leading innovation and technology strategy in IT and operational systems. He’s responsible for cybersecurity, physical security, emergency management, business continuity, and managing facilities that support real-time electric grid operations.

speakers.name: Shinjini Menon
speakers.bio: Shinjini leads the development and execution of a comprehensive roadmap to transform and expand the electric grid to meet California’s and Southern California Edison’s decarbonization, electrification, and climate change mitigation goals. She also oversees transmission and distribution planning, asset management, and grid engineering functions at SCE. She's passionate about enhancing customer experience and environmental stewardship.

speakers.name: Vera Silva
speakers.bio: Vera Silva is the Chief Strategy and Technology Officer of GE Vernova's Electrification Systems, that includes Grid Solutions, Power Conversion and Storage Systems. In this role, she leads the innovation, technology and business strategy and commercial development. Prior to her role at GE Vernova, Vera Silva was R&D director and senior engineer at Electricité de France (EDF). Preceding EDF she was a research associate in Imperial College London and assistant professor at the Polytechnic Institute of Porto, Portugal. Vera has published three books and over 40 scientific papers and was recognized as "Engineer of the Year" in 2016 by the IEEE Power and Energy Society.

speakers.name: Venkat Tirupati
speakers.bio: Venkat Tirupati is Vice President and Chief Technology Officer at the Electric Reliability Council of Texas (ERCOT). He leads enterprise technology, data, and AI strategy, and oversees the Grid Research, Innovation, and Transformation (GRIT) initiative to address the increasing complexity of a rapidly transforming electric power grid. Prior to joining ERCOT in 2020, Venkat held leadership roles at the Lower Colorado River Authority (LCRA), the North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NERC), and Siemens Power Transmission and Distribution. Venkat holds an M.S. in Electrical Engineering (Power Systems) from Illinois Institute of Technology and an M.B.A. from MIT with a focus on data analytics and sustainability. He is a registered Professional Engineer in Texas and a Senior Member of IEEE.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Beyond the Basics: OpenUSD for Advanced Physical AI Simulation
sessions.abstract: Universal Scene Description (OpenUSD) is the foundational 3D data framework of NVIDIA’s physical AI technology stack, serving as a standard across industries for building digital twins and AI in 3D worlds. As the underlying technology for NVIDIA Omniverse libraries, Isaac Sim, and SimReady assets, OpenUSD streamlines development pipelines, enhances data compatibility across 3D tools, and establishes standardized processes for complex physical AI workflows. Join this session to learn about the latest developments and next major milestones of OpenUSD, and how OpenClaw can automate the continual assembly, validation, and synchronization of large-scale USD scenes and USD standards implementation across simulation and production environments.

speakers.name: Aaron Luk
speakers.bio: Aaron Luk leads product management for the Universal Scene Description (OpenUSD) ecosystem across NVIDIA Omniverse. Previously, Aaron co-developed and deployed OpenUSD into production pipelines at Pixar.

sessions.title: Harness Agentic AI and Reasoning Models to Accelerate Scientific Discovery
sessions.abstract: Learn how an AI co-scientist that combines powerful reasoning models with science simulations can expand the aperture for human scientists to achieve breakthroughs in domains such as fusion energy and targeted alpha therapy for cancer. This session delves into strategies for overcoming the challenge of data scarcity for training, for employing reinforcement learning leveraging physics simulations, AI surrogates, emulators, and building AI agents that can reiterate autonomously.

speakers.name: Radha Bahukutumbi
speakers.bio: Radha Bahukutumbi leads the Plasma Theory and Applications group at Los Alamos National Laboratory. Her work centers on high-power laser applications, including inertial confinement fusion, nuclear astrophysics, hydrodynamics, and laser–plasma interactions. She is a fellow of the American Physical Society and recipient of the Fusion Power Associates Leadership Award.

speakers.name: Ping Yang
speakers.bio: Ping Yang serves as the deputy director of the G.T. Seaborg Institute and a staff scientist in the Theoretical Division at Los Alamos National Laboratory. Her research focuses on elucidating the fundamental electronic structure and reactivity of actinide complexes and materials. She is particularly passionate about integrating AI/ML to accelerate molecular discovery and unlock the vast potential of chemical space.

speakers.name: Geetika Gupta
speakers.bio: At NVIDIA she works on defining products for applying GenAI/AI for Scientific Research, and Autonomous Discovery

sessions.title: Scale Sports Intelligence and Insight With Foundational Models
sessions.abstract:
Imagine that you could generate the world’s most in-depth dataset for sports using the same video you see on your TV screen. In this presentation we’ll show you how we combine computer vision and our own proprietary multimodal foundation model that takes away the need for in-venue hardware and processing and scales infinitely across the game of soccer and beyond; uncovering and explaining the language of sport.
  
We’ll show how GPU acceleration allows us to model entire matches as unified multi-agent sequences, and how this foundation model powers the next generation of soccer intelligence - from tactical reconstruction to semantic embeddings - within the Opta OS ecosystem. The session will detail the model design, engineering challenges, and key breakthroughs.

speakers.name: Patrick Lucey
speakers.bio: Patrick Lucey is the chief scientist at Stats Perform, where he leads AI innovation. With 25 years of experience in AI, he has held research roles at Disney Research, IBM, and Carnegie Mellon University, and authored more than 100 peer-reviewed papers. Patrick holds a B.Eng. in electrical engineering and a Ph.D. from Queensland University of Technology in Australia. His work has featured in The Economist and the BBC, and he delivered the ECML/PKDD 2024 keynote and spoke at the 2025’s UN AI for Good Summit.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: Workflow Revolution: How Doudna Drives HPC, AI, and Quantum Scientific Impact
sessions.abstract: This presentation will focus on how scientists can leverage the Doudna supercomputer, to be deployed in 2027 at the National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center (NERSC), to manage and execute complex workflows, particularly those involving data sourced from external scientific experiments. We'll explore the system’s architecture, including the integration of Rubin GPUs, and detail how Doudna’s advanced workflow-centric capabilities accelerate science. We will discuss the relevance of these workflows for cutting-edge fields such as AI, modeling and simulation, and quantum computing, and how these new workloads enhance throughput and support of novel scientific discoveries. Gain insight into what goes into designing an HPC system to support these diverse workflows.

speakers.name: Rollin Thomas
speakers.bio: Rollin Thomas is deputy director of the NERSC-10 Project and a senior computing engineer at NERSC. He is interested in new uses of high performance computing for science, especially interactive and time-sensitive supercomputing, new interfaces beyond the command line, and of course, complex workflows that enable new modes of scientific discovery.

sessions.title: Building Shopify’s Commerce Foundation Models
sessions.abstract: Modern commerce platforms must support millions of entrepreneurs while serving billions of buyers with personalized recommendations across massive product catalogs. To bridge this gap, we introduce Shopify’s Commerce Foundation Models: sequence autoregressive models built on the HSTU encoder to represent the journeys of merchants and buyers. Key innovations include generalizing to multiple tasks, large-scale contrastive learning, temporal encoding that fuses multi-scale time signals with commerce-specific features, and the scaling to petabytes volumes of data training and on-demand inference via CUDA kernels developed in collaboration with NVIDIA. While results are promising, challenges remain in handling non-stationary preferences and multi-objective optimization. We discuss our roadmap toward truly foundational commerce models that generalize across domains and market conditions.

speakers.name: Diego Ardila
speakers.bio: Diego Ardila is a senior staff machine learning engineer at Shopify. His interests include the areas of computer architecture, systems, and applied machine learning. He earned a D.Sc. in quantitative finance from ETH Zurich and an M.Sc. in computer science from Los Andes University, Colombia.

speakers.name: Yang Liu
speakers.bio: Yang Liu is a senior staff ML engineer at Shopify and a member of IEEE. His interests include artificial intelligence and multimedia.

speakers.name: Andrew Briand
speakers.bio: Andrew Briand is a senior developer technology engineer at NVIDIA, where he optimizes GPU kernels for RecSys deep learning workloads. He also accelerates end-to-end training and inference for RecSys and LLMs. He holds a master's degree in Computational Linguistics and a bachelor’s degree in Computer Science from the University of Washington.

sessions.title: From Isolation to Integration: Evolving Confidential Computing for a Scalable, Secure Future
sessions.abstract:
Generative AI is rapidly becoming the defining workload of modern computing, but securing these powerful systems without compromising performance is still a challenge for most enterprises. This session dives into how you can lock down your most valuable AI assets—models, data, and prompts—while continuing to push the limits on speed, scale and insights.

Google and NVIDIA will share how confidential computing on NVIDIA GPUs can protect your data and model using accelerated computing, so your information stays shielded even while in use.

speakers.name: Emily Sakata
speakers.bio: Emily Sakata is a senior product manager for data center GPUs at NVIDIA. Her work focuses on bringing security features on accelerated computing to deliver trustworthy solutions to market. She has held cybersecurity and security product management roles in software and industrial product companies.

speakers.name: Nelly Porter
speakers.bio: Nelly Porter is a director of product management, where she oversees the Trusted Cloud. Nelly has over 10 years of experience in platform and virtualization security, public key infrastructure, crypto, authentication and authorization, and has 25 patents and defensive publications. Nelly also worked at Microsoft in the virtualization and security space, HP Labs in clustering, and Scientix (Israel) as a firmware and kernel engineering lead.

sessions.title: Build a Software-Defined Multi-Tenant NVLinked Cluster
sessions.abstract:
AI workloads are scaling into full production for training and inference. Standing up these clusters for R&D is challenging on its own, but production brings complexity: stricter security requirements, operational rigor, and a broader user base demanding stability, flexibility, and mature features.

The solution is a single, enterprise-grade, software-defined NVL GPU cluster built to dynamically adapt to changing workloads, support rapid deployment, and simplify life-cycle management.

In this talk, we’ll examine considerations across the infrastructure, platform, and software layers. We’ll look at network automation, virtualization, and different orchestration platforms. We’ll focus with a concrete example based on our reference architecture, integrating Run:ai, Base Command Manager, vCluster, and Netris to create a secure, scalable, production-ready multi-tenant cluster.

speakers.name: Adam Tetelman
speakers.bio:
Adam leads the DGX Cloud Native Product Architecture team, which builds the systems and architectures that make DGX the best platform in the world for building and running AI applications.

Adam has degrees in computational robotics, computer and systems engineering, and cognitive science. He's led cross-team Kubernetes initiatives throughout NVIDIA and works on the cutting edge of LLMs, inference, and HPC. He's been doing DevOps and AI-related work for 15 years and has deployed several of the top 10 supercomputers. Previously at NVIDIA, he delivered NIMs at scale, built out the NVIDIA Deep Learning Institute, and continues to engage with numerous MLOps, AI, and cloud-native OSS communities.

speakers.name: Alex Saroyan
speakers.bio: Alex Saroyan is the CEO and co-founder of Netris, the leading network automation platform for AI cloud operators. With over 25 years of experience in large-scale data center and telecom networking, Alex brings deep expertise to AI infrastructure challenges. Before Netris, he held engineering leadership roles at Lycos, Orange (France Télécom) and Ucom, where he architected a global transit network and scaled carrier infrastructure nearly 100x. Alex co-founded Netris in 2018 to bring cloud-provider-grade network automation and multi-tenancy to every AI infrastructure operator.

speakers.name: Lukas Gentele
speakers.bio: Lukas Gentele is the CEO & Co-Founder of vCluster Labs, a venture-backed startup virtualizing Kubernetes to enable advanced tenancy models that increase utilization, reduce cost, and make Kubernetes more dynamic than ever. vCluster allows platform and infrastructure teams to create virtual Kubernetes clusters that are as scalable and isolated as traditional clusters, but far more lightweight and flexible. Trusted by companies like Nscale, Deloitte, Niantic, and Aussie Broadband, vCluster powers fully isolated tenant clusters across public clouds, private data centers, and even GPU-powered AI supercomputers.

sessions.topic: Data Center / Cloud - Software-Defined Data Center

sessions.title: How Simulation and AI are Giving Life to Digital Twins for Patient Care
sessions.abstract: Simulation has evolved from a niche research tool to a cornerstone of modern healthcare innovation. Early breakthroughs focused on GPUs to accelerate physical interactions for device design and testing. Today, the scope is much broader—incorporating physiological processes such as respiration, cardiac motion, and electrophysiology. These comprehensive simulations are already being used to conduct virtual clinical trials and train autonomous surgical robots. We're helping to build the future in which AI is used to design, accelerate, and interpret these simulations, enabling digital twins that guide the care of each and every patient. Throughout the day, as your smart watch records your activities or during surgical procedures when unexpected events occur, your AI-enabled digital twin will be there to detect, diagnose, and suggest the treatment that provides the best outcome for you.

speakers.name: Jim Peichel
speakers.bio: After nearly 25 years with the company—beginning as an intern and progressing through leadership roles in the Cardiovascular Portfolio—Jim is now serving as the Medtronic chief technology officer, advancing the products and processes of Medtronic in fulfillment of its mission: to alleviate pain, restore health, and extend life.

speakers.name: Mark Palmer
speakers.bio: Mark Palmer joined Ansys as its first field chief technology officer for healthcare. Previously, he led the global modeling and simulation strategy team at Medtronic. With over 25 years of experience in computational life sciences, he’s passionate about advancing virtual patient technologies and digital evidence standards to improve product quality, safety, and patient outcomes in healthcare.

sessions.title: From Concept to Production: Humanoid Robotics at Scale
sessions.abstract: Humanoid robots are evolving from prototypes to production-ready systems, powered by breakthroughs in reasoning AI, large-scale simulation, and real-time edge computing. But to get there, the industry must bridge the gap between digital intelligence and physical reality. Hear from luminaries at the world’s leading robotics companies on the future of physical AI, and what it will take to unlock truly capable humanoids.

speakers.name: Amit Goel
speakers.bio: Amit Goel is director of product management for autonomous machines at NVIDIA, where he leads the product development of NVIDIA Jetson and developer relations for the NVIDIA Isaac robotics platform. Amit holds an M.S. in electrical engineering from Arizona State University and an M.B.A. from the University of California at Berkeley.

speakers.name: Ashok Elluswamy
speakers.bio: Ashok Elluswamy is vice president of AI at Tesla. Ashok leads AI development for the Tesla self-driving software and Optimus, the humanoid robot. He was the first engineer hired onto Tesla’s AI team in 2014 and has been a key driver of the company’s autonomy strategy, including the shift to vision-only, end-to-end neural network–based driving systems.

speakers.name: Arnaud Robert
speakers.bio: Arnaud Robert is an international C-level executive with extensive experience in building and scaling businesses, launching global products, bringing innovation to market, and driving deep transformations—across six industries. Currently president for robotics at Hexagon, he was previously chief digital officer and Executive Committee member at Sanofi, CDO at Viking Cruises, and also worked at Disney, Nike, and Microsoft. Arnaud has a Ph.D. in computer science. He's received multiple industry awards and recognitions and is a regular keynote speaker.

speakers.name: Pras Velagapudi
speakers.bio: Pras joined Agility in 2023, with decades of experience in robotics planning and control, creating and deploying robots into home, industrial, and outdoor environments. He previously was a vice president of engineering and chief architect at Berkshire Grey, applying fleets of robots to logistics applications. In a landscape constantly disrupted by new technology, Pras stays focused on continually exploring what’s new.

speakers.name: Deepak Pathak
speakers.bio: Deepak Pathak is CEO and co-founder of Skild AI and a professor at Carnegie Mellon University. He's known for pioneering contributions to AI including self-supervised learning, artificial curiosity, and adaptive robot learning. At Skild AI, he's developing an AI foundation model for robotics. Deepak has received several prestigious honors including MIT Technology Review's 35under35, Okawa, IIT Kanpur Young Alumnus Award, and multiple best paper awards.

speakers.name: Chelsea Finn
speakers.bio: Chelsea Finn is an Assistant Professor in Computer Science and Electrical Engineering at Stanford University and a co-founder of Physical Intelligence (Pi). Her research interests lie in the capability of robots and other agents to develop broadly intelligent behavior through learning and interaction. To this end, her work has pioneered end-to-end deep learning methods for vision-based robotic manipulation, meta-learning algorithms for few-shot learning, and approaches for scaling robot learning to broad datasets. Her research has been recognized by awards such as the Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists and Engineers, the Sloan Fellowship, and the ACM doctoral dissertation award, and has been covered by various media outlets including the New York Times, Wired, and Bloomberg.

sessions.title: Harness AI to Accelerate Innovation in Healthcare Robotics
sessions.abstract: The next era in surgery will be defined by the convergence of advanced technology and human-centered care. In this session, Neda Cvijetic, global head of robotics and digital R&D at Johnson & Johnson MedTech, will highlight the role of AI, simulation, and accelerated computing in Johnson & Johnson’s mission to unlock the full potential of medical innovation for those who dedicate their lives to care.

speakers.name: Neda Cvijetic
speakers.bio: Neda Cvijetic is senior vice president, global head of robotics and digital R&D, MedTech, Johnson & Johnson.  Previously, Neda held several product and engineering leadership roles at NVIDIA, Tesla, Stellantis, and NEC Labs America, and served as an adjunct professor of electrical engineering at Columbia University in New York City. Neda holds a Ph.D. in electrical engineering from the University of Virginia.

sessions.title: Develop a Real-Time Digital Twin for Rapid Automotive Engineering Design
sessions.abstract: Learn how Rivian built a real-time digital twin for rapid engineering design through NVIDIA Omniverse, Apollo AI Physics models, and CUDA-X libraries.

speakers.name: Neil Ashton
speakers.bio: Neil is a distinguished engineer/product architect at NVIDIA, with a specific focus on industrial and computational engineering. Prior to NVIDIA, he was the WW tech lead for computer-aided engineering at AWS. He was previously a senior researcher within the Department of Engineering Science at the University of Oxford, and also worked in Formula 1 with the Lotus F1 team, Formula 1 Management, and the FIA.

speakers.name: Andy McAllister
speakers.bio: Andy McAllister has a master’s degree in aeronautical engineering from the University of Glasgow and currently serves as the senior manager of aerodynamics and environmental performance at Rivian, responsible for aerodynamics, aero-acoustics, soiling, water management, corrosion, and vehicle tightness. Before Rivian, Andy made significant contributions at Aston Martin over 10 years, most notably leading the aerodynamics development of the DBX. Andrew holds multiple U.S. patents, with others pending, in active aero devices and the application of neural networks to vehicle aerodynamics development.

sessions.title: Scaling Laws in Biology: Why Bigger Models Alone Aren’t Enough
sessions.abstract: Learn about how progress in Bio x AI, from understanding disease to designing potential medicines, will be powered by at-scale data generation and the three scaling laws of pre-training, post-training, and test-time scaling.

speakers.name: Emma Lundberg
speakers.bio:
Emma is internationally recognized for her research at the intersection of spatial proteomics, bioimaging, and artificial intelligence. She serves as associate professor of bioengineering and pathology at Stanford University and professor at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Sweden. She also directs the Cell Atlas of the Human Protein Atlas project, an ambitious global effort to map protein localization in human cells.

Her work has laid the foundation for building high-resolution virtual models of cells. These models enable simulations of biological systems that help researchers test hypotheses and study disease in silico, reducing reliance on traditional experimental approaches.

speakers.name: Ron Alfa
speakers.bio: Ron Alfa is co-founder and chief executive officer of Noetik, a next-generation precision oncology company using multi-modal foundation models to discover cancer therapeutics. Prior to founding Noetik, Ron was SVP, head of research, and acting chief science officer at Recursion. Ron holds an M.D. and Ph.D. from Stanford University School of Medicine, where he completed his doctoral work in neuroscience, and has an M.A. in history of medicine from University College London.

speakers.name: Philipp Lorenz
speakers.bio: Phil Lorenz graduated from the University of Oxford with a master's degree in biochemistry and a Ph.D. in bioinformatics. After teaching genetics at Oxford and working in the pharmaceutical industry in the AI in drug discovery space, Phil joined Basecamp Research as the first technical hire, and leads the company's genomics, AI, engineering, and product functions as their chief technology officer.

speakers.name: Daniel Burkhardt
speakers.bio: Daniel Burkhardt is a developer relations manager at NVIDIA, where he manages product strategy and partnerships with organizations building foundation models and AI agents for the life sciences. Previously, he led a machine-learning research group at Cellarity, an AI drug-discovery company. Daniel received his doctorate from Yale University by developing representation-learning algorithms for biomedical data.

speakers.name: Bo Wang
speakers.bio: Bo Wang is SVP and Head of Biomedical AI at Xaira Therapeutics, and Associate Professor at the University of Toronto. He earned his PhD in Computer Science from Stanford University and holds a CIFAR AI Chair at the Vector Institute. His work spans foundation models for genomics, single cells, and multimodal biology (e.g., scGPT, BioReason), advancing interpretable and scalable AI for biomedical discovery and therapeutics. He is a recipient of the Gairdner Early Career Investigator Award, a Canada Research Chair, and a member of the Royal Society of Canada.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Biology - Generative AI

sessions.title: LLM-Generated CUDA Kernels: Are We There Yet?
sessions.abstract: Dive deep into why current large language models struggle to generate performant CUDA kernels for accelerated systems, despite being capable of functional code. We'll explore the latest advancements in benchmarking, agentic frameworks, and industry efforts that are building the necessary infrastructure to close this performance gap and enable you to write "speed of light" AI-coded CUDA.

speakers.name: Mark Gabel
speakers.bio: Mark Gabel recently joined NVIDIA to work on AI development tools. He was a founding team member and chief scientist at Viv Labs, helping build AI systems that scaled to Samsung's virtual assistant used by millions worldwide. Most recently, he co-founded Solver, where he built autonomous AI agents that handle complex software development tasks from start to finish. Mark holds a Ph.D. from UC Davis, where he co-authored the first natural language model of source code—research later cited by OpenAI's Codex paper.

speakers.name: Mark Saroufim
speakers.bio: Mark Saroufim is a Software Engineer at Meta focused on performance where he helps maintain PyTorch. Mark is also the one of the co-founders of the GPU Mode community and is most recently interested in using AI to broadly improve the performance of ML Systems.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)

sessions.title: Running GenAI at Amazon Ads Scale: Lessons from Building and Operating LLM Inference Systems
sessions.abstract: Amazon Ads is using large language models (LLMs) to reinvent shopping and advertising experiences. This session shares how Amazon Ads built a LLM inference system with NVIDIA Dynamo on AWS EKS, shaped by workload-aware design patterns that apply broadly beyond Amazon. Learn how classical engineering disciplines evolve in the LLM inference domain and hear practical lessons from operating an always-on production system.

speakers.name: Muthu Muthukrishnan
speakers.bio: Muthu Muthukrishnan is the vice president of Sponsored Products and Brands and joined Amazon in 2018. He leads engineering, product, and science for Amazon Ads Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, and Brand Stores organizations serving millions of advertising customers and billions of shopping customers worldwide.

speakers.name: Runqing Yang
speakers.bio: Runqing oversees machine learning engineering efforts in the Sponsored Products and Brands organization at Amazon Ads, focusing on building user-friendly, scalable, high-performance, and always-on machine learning systems. He specializes on recommendation model training and inference, as well as LLM inference.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: MLOps 201: Master Operational Excellence With Agentic AI
sessions.abstract:
The next leap in enterprise AI is industrializing intelligence, where automation and human input combine to improve agility, decision quality, and process efficiency. This session shows how NVIDIA applies lean manufacturing principles and agentic AI to its own AI operations through three core MLOps platforms: the Data Factory for synthetic data generation and labeling, the Eval Factory for model benchmarking, and the NIM Factory for inference optimization. 

From these real-world examples, we distill a practical playbook for representing any process in AI-native terms, identifying high-impact automation opportunities, embedding continuous improvement, and building a culture of operational excellence. The result is an AI-first organization with a repeatable model for AI-enhanced operations, one that extends beyond MLOps to transform the entire enterprise and its business model.

speakers.name: Nik Spirin
speakers.bio: Nik is a hands-on technology leader and Ph.D. with over 15 years of experience applying AI/ML to drive business results as a startup founder, executive, consultant, and advisor. He's currently a director at NVIDIA working on generative AI and LLMOps Platform. In the past, he worked with Fortune 500 corporations and venture capital-funded startups on AI strategies, products, and platforms, and built high-performance AI organizations up from zero.

speakers.name: Jesse Oliver
speakers.bio: Jesse Oliver is a senior product manager at NVIDIA responsible for human-in-the-loop tools, services, and workflows as a part of the NeMo platform for generative AI. He’s focused on producing high-quality labels for foundation model training, model alignment, and human-in-the-loop evaluation for internal research teams and enterprise customers. Jesse has over a decade of experience working across engineering and product roles at NVIDIA, Apple, and Google.

speakers.name: Seph Mard
speakers.bio:
Seph Mard is a Sr. Product Manager at NVIDIA focused on building infrastructure for AI model evaluation and benchmarking. He leads initiatives such as NeMo Evaluator and EvalFactory, working to create scalable, reproducible systems that help researchers and developers evaluate frontier AI models and agentic systems more reliably.

Prior to NVIDIA, Seph worked on enterprise AI platforms at Weights & Biases and DataRobot. He holds a background in applied mathematics and behavioral economics and focuses on translating complex machine learning systems into practical, high-impact products.

sessions.title: Best Practices for Hashmap Optimization on NVIDIA Grace and Vera CPUs
sessions.abstract: Efficient hashmaps are among the most critical data structures in modern data analytics, genomics, and recommender systems. In this talk, we'll deep-dive into the amazing world of hashmaps for CPUs. We will discuss types, approaches, and properties of different CPU hashmap implementations with respect to our current data-center CPU platform NVIDIA Grace and the upcoming NVIDIA Vera platform. We'll tackle practical considerations for optimizing hashmap construction and lookups on NVIDIA CPUs, share lessons learned from integrating them into data analytical workloads, and describe theoretical performance limits, tuning knobs, and less well-known techniques to improve hashmap performance.

speakers.name: Matthias Langer
speakers.bio: Matthias Langer is a Senior AI & Technology Engineer at NVIDIA based in Melbourne. His work primarily focuses on workload distribution, algorithm parallelization, and performance optimization to help customers maximize the performance of their HPC applications on NVIDIA hardware. Matthias holds a Ph.D. in Artificial Intelligence from La Trobe University in Melbourne and has over 15 years of experience as a software engineer and machine learning researcher.

speakers.name: Yuzhong Wen
speakers.bio: Yuzhong Wen is a senior developer technology engineer at NVIDIA. Since joining the DevTech team in 2024, he primarily works on optimizing software for NVIDIA CPU platforms, including optimization operating system kernels, concurrent algorithms, database workloads, and video transcoding. He holds a master of science degree in computer science from Virginia Tech and has nine years of experience of performance-critical system software on ARM system-on-chips.

sessions.title: The AI-Native Digital Health Stack: A Developer's Guide to 2026
sessions.abstract: The digital health stack is undergoing a fundamental shift, moving from siloed data and manual processes to an AI-native, interoperable future. For developers, the components of 2026's most effective systems—powered by generative AI reasoning and advanced speech models—are already here. Join this digital health developer day event to get the essential know-how to reimagine your entire stack. We'll provide the overviews, strategies, and case studies you need to build what's next in healthcare.

speakers.name: Brad Genereaux
speakers.bio: Brad Genereaux is the global lead for healthcare alliances at NVIDIA, focused on developer relations in digital health and medical imaging. With over 22 years in healthcare IT, he advocates for integrating AI into clinical practice at enterprise scale. Brad is also a leader in healthcare interoperability, contributing to standards like HL7, FHIR, DICOM, and IHE to help make healthcare a seamless experience for all.

speakers.name: Raghav Mani
speakers.bio: Raghav Mani is the director of digital health, focused on building agentic AI products and platforms for providers, payers, and pharma. Prior to NVIDIA, Raghav worked at Epic, where he led different product and engineering teams including their deep learning team and their patient engagement platform, called MyChart. He holds a bachelor’s degree from Indian Institute of Technology in Madras and a master's degree from Texas A&M University.

speakers.name: Adi Margolin
speakers.bio: Adi Margolin is the product manager for Riva SDK and Speech NIM. With 16 years of product management experience, Adi has built high-impact speech technology solutions across enterprise software companies, developing expertise in bringing ASR and TTS innovations to market. Based in San Jose, Adi brings a unique perspective to speech AI development, having successfully navigated the transition from legacy systems to modern AI-driven platforms while addressing complex requirements of real-time media applications.

speakers.name: Arun Venkatesan
speakers.bio: Arun is a group product manager at NVIDIA and leads product management for generative AI Inference. Prior to this, Arun was an engineering lead at Samsung Research America and has broad experience building conversational AI applications, and other enterprise and consumer software. Arun graduated with an M.B.A. from the Yale School of Management and a master’s degree in electrical and computer engineering from the University of Maryland, College Park.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Introduction to Neural Rendering
sessions.abstract: Learn how neural rendering is transforming real-time graphics and simulation by embedding machine learning for massive performance gains and fidelity. This session introduces the core computational approaches and shows how the differentiable GPU programming layer essential to this work critically expands the high-performance compute ecosystem.

speakers.name: Shannon Woods
speakers.bio: Shannon Woods is the product lead for Slang at NVIDIA. She also chairs the Slang Working Group at Khronos, continuing her longtime involvement in standards bodies. In her previous roles, she led the ANGLE graphics translation project and helped ship foundational APIs like Vulkan and WebGPU to billions of users.

speakers.name: Alexey Bekin
speakers.bio: Alexey Bekin is a Senior Developer Technology Engineer at NVIDIA, where he specializes in productizing innovative rendering algorithms, neural graphics technologies, and developing SDKs. With extensive experience in the tech industry, Alexey's expertise encompasses a wide range of disciplines, including game development, computer-aided design (CAD), GPU programming, and high-performance computing (HPC).

sessions.topic: Content Creation / Rendering - Rendering Engines / Pipelines / Tools

sessions.title: MLOps 202: From Models to Production AI Systems at Scale
sessions.abstract: Modern production AI is more than a foundation model. The model monolith is evolving into the data center-scale distributed system made of modular microservices. This talk shows how NVIDIA brings the full stack together by applying extreme software and hardware co-design to maximize performance across infrastructure, orchestration, and application layers. We cover an AI project journey from zero to production. Starting with a simple cloud API, we progressively add complexity while introducing key NVIDIA technologies, including NVIDIA AI Enterprise, NIM, NeMo, Run.ai, Dynamo, and GPU infrastructure, to explain how each of them addresses real engineering and operational challenges. Get a clear guide for turning AI prototypes and isolated experiments into enterprise-grade systems, and understand the decisions, trade-offs, and architectural patterns behind modern production AI.

speakers.name: Erik Bohnhorst
speakers.bio: Erik Bohnhorst is a Director of Product Management at NVIDIA, specialized in building infrastructure software that powers modern AI. Erik has been at NVIDIA since 2014, and currently focuses on making AI workloads run effortlessly and efficiently on Kubernetes and virtualized platforms.

speakers.name: Nik Spirin
speakers.bio: Nik is a hands-on technology leader and Ph.D. with over 15 years of experience applying AI/ML to drive business results as a startup founder, executive, consultant, and advisor. He's currently a director at NVIDIA working on generative AI and LLMOps Platform. In the past, he worked with Fortune 500 corporations and venture capital-funded startups on AI strategies, products, and platforms, and built high-performance AI organizations up from zero.

speakers.name: Michael Balint
speakers.bio: Michael is focused on cluster management, orchestration, and scheduling of NVIDIA DGX servers. Previously, Michael was a White House Presidential Innovation Fellow, where he brought his technical expertise to projects like then-Vice President Biden's Cancer Moonshot and Code.gov.

sessions.title: Accelerate the Path to In-Silico Life Sciences and Drug Discovery
sessions.abstract: Representing life in silico and designing compounds and therapies that modulate that biology are among the most foundational goals of modern life sciences R&D. The community is at an inflection point: increasing volumes of sophisticated data and foundation modeling efforts require fundamental new technology to accelerate discovery. In this talk, learn about NVIDIA's efforts in addressing these challenges head-on, with new efforts in data generation, data processing, and biological and molecular foundation modeling, and research. We'll cover genomics, multi-omics, foundation model scaling, molecular discovery, and end-to-end AI-scientist workflows in this comprehensive product talk.

speakers.name: Anthony Costa
speakers.bio: Anthony leads the digital biology product and developer relations team at NVIDIA. After graduate and postdoctoral training in computational chemistry, Anthony joined the faculty at Mount Sinai Health System in New York. There, he served as founding director of AI Sinai and Sinai BioDesign. At NVIDIA, he leads product development and partnerships for NVIDIA platforms in drug discovery and life sciences.

sessions.title: AI on the Front Lines of Healthcare: Unlock Clinical, Operational, and Diagnostic Value
sessions.abstract: Learn from the digital health software leaders on the front lines of the agentic and ambient AI revolution. Panelists from a crosscut of the digital health ecosystem will share how they are deploying cutting-edge agents to solve critical healthcare challenges, such as transforming physician-patient conversations into structured notes, crafting diagnostic reports in real time, or interacting with caregivers to answer questions and guide them through receiving care.

speakers.name: Chelsea Sumner
speakers.bio:
Chelsea is a healthcare innovator and the North and Latin American and Africa healthcare AI startups lead at NVIDIA. With a background in the pharmaceutical industry and clinical settings, she manages relationships for over 1,500 healthcare startups. Her passion for healthcare and technology led to her current position, where she strives to democratize AI and advance digital health.
 
Chelsea is committed to uplifting underrepresented communities. She founded the nonprofit Dose of Melanin, Inc., connecting thousands of Black pharmacists and student pharmacists. Her multifaceted background, dedication to healthcare innovation, and efforts in community service make her an influential figure.

speakers.name: Brad Genereaux
speakers.bio: Brad Genereaux is the global lead for healthcare alliances at NVIDIA, focused on developer relations in digital health and medical imaging. With over 22 years in healthcare IT, he advocates for integrating AI into clinical practice at enterprise scale. Brad is also a leader in healthcare interoperability, contributing to standards like HL7, FHIR, DICOM, and IHE to help make healthcare a seamless experience for all.

speakers.name: Zachary Lipton
speakers.bio: Zachary Lipton is co-founder and CTO at Abridge, the leading platform for AI-based ambient listening technology in healthcare. Abridge’s industry-leading product listens to doctor-patient conversations and ingests reams of content from the electronic health record, leveraging its intelligent reasoning engine to generate high-quality drafts of after-visit notes and other artifacts. This automation frees up doctors to focus on their patients. He is also the Raj Reddy Associate Professor of Machine Learning at Carnegie Mellon University, where he directs the Approximately Correct Machine Intelligence (ACMI) lab.

speakers.name: Davide Tricarico
speakers.bio: Davide Tricarico is the chief technology officer at AITEM Solutions, leading the development of LAIKA, the AI copilot for veterinarians. He holds an M.Sc. in computer engineering and a Ph.D. in modeling and data science, with expertise in machine learning, deep learning, and computer vision. With over 15 years of experience in software and algorithm design, AI development, and project management, Davide has worked across a range of settings, from large corporations like General Motors and Dumarey Group to startups like AITEM.

speakers.name: Paul Lipton
speakers.bio: Paul Lipton is the lead product manager for Rad AI Reporting, where he focuses on deploying generative AI that delivers measurable efficiency gains in real clinical workflows. With over 15 years in healthcare and radiology informatics, he has led teams shipping production systems at scale, where reliability, explainability, clinical trust, and workflow efficiency are paramount. Previously, Paul was a solution manager at Agfa HealthCare, helping launch enterprise imaging platforms and viewers, including solutions recognized as best in KLAS. He brings a production-first perspective shaped by deploying AI in regulated, high-stakes healthcare environments.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Maximize Memory Bandwidth on Modern GPUs: Practical Techniques, Patterns, and Worked Examples
sessions.abstract: Modern GPU generations (since Volta) have increased memory bandwidth faster than streaming multiprocessor (SM) count, which makes the classic “add more threads” recipe insufficient for many memory-bound kernels.
