This tutorial gives hands-on, implementation-focused guidance to saturate memory bandwidth on contemporary NVIDIA GPUs using loop unrolling, vectorization, asynchronous pipelining (Tensor memory accelerator, or TMA), cache prefetching, and other techniques. Through step-by-step worked examples (e.g., stencils, SpMV), we show common pitfalls (peeling, misalignment, tiny kernels) and practical fixes, plus variants of TMA usage and a producer-consumer TMA pattern you can reuse.

speakers.name: Matthew Martineau
speakers.bio: Matthew Martineau has been a developer technology engineer since 2018, focusing primarily on the performance of future NVIDIA GPU architectures and the optimization of sparse iterative solvers. He holds a Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Bristol, U.K.

speakers.name: Benedikt Dorschner
speakers.bio: Benedikt Dorschner is a computational scientist and senior development technology engineer at NVIDIA, specializing in numerical methods and high‑performance scientific computing. He earned his Ph.D. in computational fluid dynamics from ETH Zurich and subsequently held positions as a postdoctoral scholar at Caltech and a senior research scientist at ETH.

speakers.name: Sam Mish
speakers.bio: Sam is a developer technology engineer at NVIDIA who earned a Ph.D. at UC Davis in civil engineering, specializing in finite element analysis, then went on to work at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory, writing software for differentiable physics simulations. After that, he joined NVIDIA to help accelerate HPC applications. In his spare time, Sam enjoys playing video games and Irish traditional music.

sessions.title: How Open World Models are Powering the Next Breakthroughs in Physical AI
sessions.abstract: Generating the photorealistic data and robust reasoning capabilities needed for machines to understand real-world complexity remains one of the biggest bottlenecks in physical AI. NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models are at the core of AI models and agents that can perceive, understand, and adapt to the real-world like never before. Learn how developers can use Cosmos open models and tools to accelerate training, customize pipelines, and deploy faster across robotics, autonomous vehicles, and vision AI agents. This session will also explore how the latest Cosmos updates advance physics-aware learning, unlock simulation-to-real capabilities, and enable long-running agents and frameworks like OpenClaw to orchestrate continuous synthetic data generation and reasoning workflows at scale.

speakers.name: Ming-Yu Liu
speakers.bio: Ming-Yu Liu is a vice president of research at NVIDIA and a fellow of IEEE. He leads the Deep Imagination Research group at NVIDIA, which focuses on deep generative models and their applications in content creation. NVIDIA Edify, NVIDIA Canvas [GauGAN], and NVIDIA Maxine [LivePortrait] are three products research from his group enabled.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: How to Build Planetary-Scale AI Infrastructure
sessions.abstract:
Gigawatt-scale AI facilities are pushing data center infrastructure beyond the limits of traditional design and operations workflows. Siloed approaches to buildings, power, cooling, and compute make it difficult to scale efficiently while meeting energy and sustainability requirements.
This panel explores how simulation-based co-design and physically accurate digital twins are being used to plan and operate large-scale AI infrastructure. Industry leaders will discuss how these methods help evaluate tradeoffs earlier, improve efficiency, and better align facility, power, thermal, and compute decisions.

speakers.name: Chris Dolan
speakers.bio:
As an innovation architect and business catalyst, Chris Dolan is the chief data center officer at Crusoe, where he designs and operates "AI factories of the future." He currently leads the development of the 1.2-gigawatt Stargate flagship in Texas and manages a pipeline exceeding 45 GW, including a massive 1.8 GW campus in Wyoming.

With over 25 years of experience, Dolan previously co-founded and served as CEO of 365 Main, leading it to a $725 million sale to Digital Realty Trust. He also directed $1.1 billion in developments for AboveNet, cementing his reputation for high-velocity, large-scale infrastructure delivery.

speakers.name: Scott Wallace
speakers.bio: Scott Wallace, director of data center engineering at NVIDIA, leads large-scale AI factory infrastructure reference designs, digital twins, and controls and monitoring strategy. A key focus for Scott and his team is to optimize the efficiency, reliability, and time to deploy AI factories at scale. Prior to NVIDIA, Scott held engineering and leadership roles at Facebook/Meta, and he has a background in mechanical engineering.

speakers.name: Catherine Kniker
speakers.bio: Catherine Kniker(CK) is PTC’s Chief Marketing and Sustainability Officer, leading PTC’s global marketing, and sustainability strategy. With over 20 years of executive experience successfully driving market growth, CK has accelerated PTC’s Digital transformation and built GTM programs that help customers achieve digital and sustainability goals.

speakers.name: Natasha Nelson
speakers.bio:
Natasha is CTO of services and vice president of EcoStruxure Power for end users. She focuses on advancing the energy transition through sustainable, digital, and all-electric solutions. With her expertise in internet of things, AI, and connected systems, she collaborates with teams to develop innovative use cases and interoperability strategies for mission-critical and electro-intensive industries. This role enables our organization to create scalable, secure, and value-driven solutions powered by EcoStruxure platform. 

Natasha holds an M.B.A. in business administration from MIT. In the MIT Sloan Fellows Program, she specialized in digital platform business, innovation, and cybersecurity.

speakers.name: Vivek Mishra
speakers.bio: Vivek serves as corporate vice president in systems design analysis at Cadence, where he's responsible for product engineering and customer engagement—focusing on systems design and analysis for automobiles, aerospace robotics, and data centers. Vivek and team focus on key customer engagements around the world, driving systems design and analysis as part of the intelligent system design strategy. Previously, Vivek was with Digital and Signoff Group, and before that with Magma. Before joining Magma, Vivek was into chip design, owning RTL to GDS product development in optical networking. Vivek attended Pune University, where he earned a bachelors in electronics engineering

sessions.title: Enable Enterprise AI Factories With Secure, High-Performance Networking
sessions.abstract: AI continues to reshape enterprise applications, boosting productivity, streamlining operations, and delivering better outcomes. To realize these gains, enterprises need infrastructure that can keep pace with modern AI. Traditional infrastructure has hit its limits, diverting compute and power from AI workloads and creating performance bottlenecks and security gaps at AI scale. Learn how Red Hat OpenShift, NVIDIA BlueField DPUs, and high-performance Ethernet fabrics provide optimal infrastructure for enterprise AI. DPU-based isolation separates infrastructure services from user workloads, reducing the attack surface and enforcing policy boundaries. We'll also cover how OpenShift automates provisioning, updates, monitoring, and troubleshooting for BlueField-enabled nodes to simplify adoption at enterprise scale.

speakers.name: Yael Shenhav
speakers.bio: Yaël Asseraf Shenhav leads NVIDIA's DPU and Ethernet NIC business and products. Previously, she managed product lines at Mellanox Technologies. Yaël also held various architecture and design positions at Mellanox and Intel over her 25 years in technology and management. She holds an engineering degree from Sup’Aero, with a major in telecommunications and space engineering.

speakers.name: Chris Wright
speakers.bio: Chris Wright is chief technology officer and senior vice president of global engineering at Red Hat, where he leads teams shaping the company’s technology strategy and driving innovation across its global ecosystem of customers, partners, and open source communities. With more than 25 years in software engineering, he brings deep expertise in Linux, security, virtualization, and distributed systems, and is a long-time open source advocate and Linux kernel contributor.

sessions.title: AI Agents, Robotics, and Digital Twins: The Full Stack of Self-Driving Labs and Biomanufacturing
sessions.abstract: The pharmaceutical industry’s quest for self-driving labs demands convergence of intelligent AI agents and precision robotics to create biology data factories and new medicine foundries. Multiply Labs provides the physical execution layer, using modular robotic clusters and digital twins to automate complex cell therapy workflows, reducing variability and setup time while capturing high-quality data. Thermo Fisher is delivering the lab of the future with industry-leading instruments, automation, and digital platforms that enable fully connected, AI-ready labs and end-to-end automated workflows. HighRes Biosolutions powers the labs of top TechBio and pharma through advanced automation, autonomous mobile robots, and agentic orchestration. This connected approach is the blueprint for truly self-driving bio and manufacturing labs.

speakers.name: Stacie Calad-Thomson
speakers.bio: Stacie, a former pharma and biotech leader, recently joined NVIDIA to further enable AI adoption across healthcare and the life sciences. She also serves as the vice chair of the Alliance for Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare. In her 13-plus years at GSK, she led R&D initiatives and co-founded the ATOM consortium. More recently, she held leadership roles at AI drug discovery startups Atomwise, where she was a VP of partnerships, and BioSymetrics, where she was the chief strategy officer. Stacie holds a B.S. in chemistry from the University of California, Berkeley and a Ph.D. in organic chemistry from UC Irvine.

speakers.name: Olga Ovchinnikova
speakers.bio: Olga Ovchinnikova is the director of product management for Thermo Fisher Digital Labs. She has expertise in multi-modal chemical imaging, nanoscale microscopy, and data-driven materials science, with a Ph.D. in chemical physics from the University of Tennessee, Knoxville and a track record of patents, team leadership, and industry collaboration.

speakers.name: Fred Parietti
speakers.bio: Fred Parietti is the co-founder and CEO of Multiply Labs, a robotics company transforming the manufacturing of cell, gene, and other advanced therapies through end-to-end manufacturing automation. Fred holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from MIT and has authored over 30 peer-reviewed robotics publications with more than 2,000 citations, along with over 15 patents and patent applications.

speakers.name: Ira Hoffman
speakers.bio: Ira Hoffman is CEO of HighRes Biosolutions, where he has spent 20 years pioneering lab automation and is leading AI and physical AI strategies transforming laboratory automation. HighRes' intelligent robotic platforms and orchestration software power drug discovery at leading pharma and biotech companies worldwide. His path from developing autonomous wheelchair robotics to lab automation brings unique insight into intelligent physical systems.

sessions.title: Reimagining Store Operations at Scale to Accelerate Retail ROI
sessions.abstract: SandStar, Lawson, and True Corp. will explain how to use both NVIDIA edge AI and cloud AI solutions to change thousands of retail stores' daily operations, and attain positive return on investment (ROI) within three to six months.

speakers.name: Yili Wu
speakers.bio: Yili is SandStar's founder and chief executive officer. SandStar rolls out AI solutions including AI vending, smart store, and unattended store across more than 20 countries serving over 25 Global Fortune 500 companies.

speakers.name: Takashi Tokita
speakers.bio: Takashi Tokita joined Lawson in 1993, and he accumulated deep insights into retail management. Takashi served as general manager of several key subsidiaries in different Lawson regions, and he led the launch of franchise operations, brand establishment, and expansion into new areas. Takashi is the pioneer in both Lawson and the retail industry to drive AI into daily operations.

speakers.name: Ekaraj Panjavinin
speakers.bio: Ekaraj has been chief digital officer of True Corp. since March 2023, as well as president of True Digital Group Co. since February 2023. He is also a member of Thailand Advisory Board of ASEAN Innovation Business Platform. Ekaraj is an experienced head of sales and business management with a demonstrated history of working in the computer hardware and software industry.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Reimagining the OR With Physical and Digital Robotics: Innovations, Challenges, and Opportunities
sessions.abstract: The integration of physical and digital robotics into operating rooms (ORs) is revolutionizing surgical care, driving precision, efficiency, and improved patient outcomes. Join us for an insightful panel discussion as we explore cutting-edge advancements in intelligent surgical robotics systems. We'll examine technological breakthroughs in digital robotic training through physics-based simulation, autonomous surgical assistance, and real-time edge AI deployment in the OR. We'll explore how these advancements empower surgeons with enhanced precision while reducing cognitive load. You'll hear unique perspectives on the future of the operating room and how physical and digital robotics enable unprecedented surgical excellence.

speakers.name: Robert Cohen
speakers.bio: Robert Cohen leads Stryker’s Orthopedics Group’s innovation strategy across digital, AI, surgical robotics, navigation, and healthcare data access. He holds 25 patents and has made significant contributions to musculoskeletal implant design, biomaterials, 3D printing, and surgical robotics. Robert serves as a thought leader and spokesperson at global industry events, speaking about the clinical benefits of digital, AI, and robotics. He also chairs the Digital Health Technology Board for AdvaMed, the U.S. MedTech association, and the Board of Trustees at New Jersey Institute of Technology. Robert is a fellow of both the National Academy of Inventors and the National Academy of Engineering.

speakers.name: David Niewolny
speakers.bio: David Niewolny is the global head of business development for healthcare/medical at NVIDIA, where he's focused on increasing the adoption of AI in healthcare and medical applications. David has 18 years of experience building and leading healthcare-focused businesses within leading technology companies such as AWS, Real Time Innovations, and NXP/Freescale.

speakers.name: Nadine Hachach-Haram
speakers.bio: Nadine Hachach-Haram is the founder and CEO of Proximie and a practicing National Health Service surgeon, lecturer, and clinical entrepreneur. She founded Proximie to improve access to safe surgery globally through digital connectivity, innovation, and education. Nadine has been awarded a British Empire Medal, selected for the Endeavor entrepreneur program, and served on the Royal College of Surgeons’ Commission on the Future of Surgery. She also continues to practice as a consultant plastic surgeon at Guy’s and St. Thomas’ NHS Foundation Trust in London.

speakers.name: Doug Harcombe
speakers.bio: Doug Harcombe serves as CEO of AdventHealth Central Florida’s South Market, overseeing AdventHealth Celebration, East Orlando, Kissimmee, and Lake Nona. He has dedicated over 34 years to AdventHealth, serving in a range of leadership roles. Doug holds a BBA from Washington Adventist University and an MBA from National Louis University. Doug has been married for 30 years and has two daughters. A supporter of Autism Awareness, he enjoys obstacle course races, golf, skiing, and sports.

speakers.name: Martin Roche
speakers.bio:
Martin Roche, MD is a surgeon-innovator who has spent his career turning frontier technologies into clinically proven surgical platforms. A world-renowned orthopaedic surgeon, inventor, and system architect, he operates at the intersection of surgery, robotics, and embodied artificial intelligence.
A principal surgeon-architect behind MAKO Surgical, Dr. Roche helped define robotic-assisted knee replacement. He later founded OrthoSensor, pioneering intelligent intraoperative sensing systems acquired by Stryker.
As Founder and Executive Chairman of peritasAi, Dr. Roche is building the orchestration layer for next-generation surgical care—re-architecting perioperative operations into agentic, intelligent environments that scale human expertise.

sessions.title: Building the Intelligent Control Plane for Medical Imaging
sessions.abstract: A shortage of scalable clinical expertise is becoming healthcare’s limiting factor, not access to imaging hardware. In this session, Taha Kass-Hout, Global Chief Science and Technology Officer at GE HealthCare, will share architecture patterns for an intelligent control plane where multimodal models run at the edge to interpret images plus workflow context and guide real-time decisions with human oversight. Using autonomous X-ray as an early proof point, you will learn how accelerated computing enables clinical-grade latency and deployment, and how simulation and digital twins support safe validation at scale. Roland Rott, President and CEO of Imaging at GE HealthCare, will highlight real-world examples and measurable outcomes from AI adoption across ultrasound, mammography, CT, and MRI, and how these results inform the path toward increasingly autonomous imaging workflows.

speakers.name: Taha Kass-Hout
speakers.bio: Taha Kass-Hout, M.D., M.S., is the global CTO at GE HealthCare, where he's driving advancements in AI-based medical imaging and diagnostics. His previous role at Amazon, as VP of machine learning and chief medical officer, saw him spearhead pivotal health tech projects. Before that, Taha was the first chief health informatics officer at the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, where he championed data transparency through initiatives like openFDA and precisionFDA.

speakers.name: Roland Rott
speakers.bio: Roland Rott is the President and CEO of GE HealthCare Imaging. Roland joined GEHC in 2011 and has held multiple leadership roles within Ultrasound, ultimately becoming president and CEO in 2021. In his tenure leading the Ultrasound segment he has presided over the acquisition of BK Medical, the company’s largest acquisition since 2017, the acquisition of AI healthcare leader Caption Health and the equity investment in at-home ultrasound start-up Pulsenmore and partnerships such as with Novo Nordisk and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. Before joining GE, Roland was Managing Director, EMEA and Executive Board Member of Exact Software, Netherlands.

sessions.title: How Snap Saves Millions with Accelerated Apache Spark
sessions.abstract: We'll detail our journey, achievements, and insights gained from implementing the Apache Spark RAPIDS accelerator across Snap's most substantial data processing pipelines. A significant cost saving of 76% to 90% has been realized in comparison to utilizing CPU-only jobs. We are currently collaborating with Google and NVIDIA to further deploy the RAPIDS accelerator to a broader range of our production workloads.

speakers.name: Liang Chen
speakers.bio: Liang is a staff software engineer at Snap, specializing in big data processing and distributed systems. He contributes to the design, development, and operation of critical enterprise platforms, including the A/B Experimentation and Agent Platforms. These highly available systems are engineered to manage and process data at a daily petabyte scale, directly supporting core business needs.

speakers.name: Prudhvi Vatala
speakers.bio: Prudhvi is a senior manager leading the Enterprise AI, Data, and Developer Productivity platforms at Snap Inc. His teams build and maintain complex, large-scale, high-availability systems processing petabyte-scale data, handling millions of requests per second—infrastructure designed to drive company-wide efficiency and enable critical business decisions.

sessions.title: From Grace to Vera: NVIDIA’s next data center CPU
sessions.abstract:
In the past two years, the NVIDIA Grace CPU has reshaped the foundation of modern data centers. Its successor, the NVIDIA Vera CPU, will become available soon. Vera marks an exciting evolution of CPU design. Based on a custom Arm CPU core, this new CPU provides unprecedented performance and efficiency. But what does this mean for system integrators and developers? In this talk, we will discuss how Vera improves upon Grace. We'll analyze the new features of the upcoming Vera CPU, and how you can use them to unleash Vera’s full potential. We will also revisit typical applications and usage scenarios, and detail how Vera’s architecture is particularly geared toward speeding up such workloads.

After the session, join us at our follow on "Connect with the Experts Session" to ask further questions with our team of experts - How to Run and Optimize Your Workloads on the NVIDIA Grace and Vera CPUs [CWES81771].

speakers.name: Matthias Langer
speakers.bio: Matthias Langer is a Senior AI & Technology Engineer at NVIDIA based in Melbourne. His work primarily focuses on workload distribution, algorithm parallelization, and performance optimization to help customers maximize the performance of their HPC applications on NVIDIA hardware. Matthias holds a Ph.D. in Artificial Intelligence from La Trobe University in Melbourne and has over 15 years of experience as a software engineer and machine learning researcher.

speakers.name: Lukas Alt
speakers.bio: Lukas Alt is a developer technology engineer who joined NVIDIA in 2024 and has been working on performance optimization of data center applications and libraries for NVIDIA’s data center CPU platforms, with a strong focus on compression and serialization frameworks. Lukas holds a master’s degree in computer science from RWTH Aachen University.

sessions.title: Revolutionizing Car Manufacturing: Inside AUDI's Fully Automated Factory
sessions.abstract: The session highlights AUDI’s strategy for integrating AI-driven robotics into both brownfield and greenfield plants, anchored by digital twins of its production facilities. These digital replicas allow AUDI to augment existing industrial robots with advanced AI capabilities, increasing line efficiency, adaptability, and overall competitiveness in the face of rapidly evolving manufacturing technologies. By operating a continuous “digital twin-to-real” loop, AUDI can monitor factory operations in near-real time, simulate changes before deployment, and rapidly adapt to new product variants or process adjustments. NVIDIA’s industrial platforms—such as Omniverse for factory-scale digital twins, Isaac for robotics AI workflows, and NVIDIA’s edge and data center AI systems—provide the compute, simulation, and optimization capabilities making this production model feasible and scalable.

speakers.name: Henning Loeser
speakers.bio: Henning Loeser is senior manager and head of Audi Future Production Lab at AUDI AG, steering the strategic innovation agenda for the smart factory and Audi’s 360Factory vision. Acting as a key interface between advanced research, digitalization, and large-scale manufacturing, he ensures that cutting-edge technologies are translated into robust, scalable production solutions that strengthen Audi’s global manufacturing competitiveness.

sessions.title: Accelerate Grid Modernization for AI Growth Using NVIDIA Omniverse
sessions.abstract: Building physically accurate digital twins for electrical grid infrastructure is becoming critical to be able simulate and predict outages during extreme weather events. We'll discuss Southern California Edison's journey of adopting Omniverse as a platform to bring all its ecosystem partners to build digital twins of the grid. Presenters will showcase how SoftServe built the solution, demonstrating Southern California Edison's vision, and in parallel helped Siemens Energy build scalable product integrating physics and AI-based models from substation.

speakers.name: Adnan Chaudhry
speakers.bio: Adnan Chaudhry is a seasoned professional in the energy sector, currently serving as the Senior Vice President of Digital Grid at Siemens Energy. With over 16 years of experience at Siemens, Adnan has held various leadership roles, including Vice President of Grid Automation Technology and Head of Control & Protection Engineering for Transmission Solutions. Adnan’s career began with a technical apprenticeship at RWE AG, where he gained experience in power transmission and distribution. Adnan holds a Diplom-Ingenieur degree in Electrical Engineering from Aachen University of Applied Sciences and a Master of Arts in Organizational and Human Resource Development from FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg.

speakers.name: Brenden Russell
speakers.bio: An experienced leader in the energy sector who's passionate about the adoption of digital technologies to accelerate the transition to a low-carbon, clean-energy economy, Brenden is focused on solving complex business challenges through the adoption of new smart grid technologies and creating business value through digital transformation.

speakers.name: Dennis Loktionov
speakers.bio:
Dennis Loktionov is Senior Director of Enterprise Solutions at SoftServe, where he focuses on NVIDIA technologies for enterprise clients and strategic projects. With extensive experience operating at the nexus of product management, engineering, and digital transformation, Dennis has a proven track record of commercializing advanced technology solutions in partnership with Fortune 100 companies and leading hardware manufacturers. He co-founded and led AI labs, built and scaled cross-functional teams, and driven large-scale transformation programs delivering billions in enterprise value. Dennis brings a practical, outcome-driven perspective to helping organizations adopt AI at scale.

Please let us know once updated here.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Diffusion Unlocked: Advanced Techniques for Training, Inference, and Deployment
sessions.abstract:
Diffusion models have exploded from research labs into the mainstream, powering everything from photorealistic image generation to creative co-pilots. But moving these models from a notebook to a scalable, low-latency, production-ready service is one of the greatest engineering challenges in modern AI. The "magic" of generation hides a complex stack of optimization and infrastructure hurdles.

We will bypass high-level concepts to debate the concrete architectural trade-offs required for low latency and high throughput, covering unique training challenges, up-and-coming model architectures, inference and runtime optimization, low-precision and NVFP4, and scaling topologies.

speakers.name: Ming-Yu Liu
speakers.bio: Ming-Yu Liu is a vice president of research at NVIDIA and a fellow of IEEE. He leads the Deep Imagination Research group at NVIDIA, which focuses on deep generative models and their applications in content creation. NVIDIA Edify, NVIDIA Canvas [GauGAN], and NVIDIA Maxine [LivePortrait] are three products research from his group enabled.

speakers.name: Stefano Ermon
speakers.bio: Stefano Ermon is an associate professor of computer science at Stanford University and a pioneer in generative AI. He co-founded Inception Labs, a company backed by NVIDIA that is building next-generation, diffusion-based large language models capable of significantly faster text generation than current standards. Stefano also co-founded Atlas AI, which utilizes satellite data and machine learning to monitor global economic development. A recipient of the prestigious IJCAI Computers and Thought Award, his research focuses on probabilistic modeling and computational sustainability, applying advanced AI techniques to solve complex real-world problems.

speakers.name: Anastasis Germanidis
speakers.bio: Anastasis Germanidis is a Greek AI researcher, software engineer, and artist based in New York City. He's the chief technology officer and co-founder of Runway. Previously, he was a machine learning researcher at IBM and a distributed systems back-end engineer.

speakers.name: Cynthia Lu
speakers.bio: Jingwan Lu is a Senior Director of Engineering at Adobe, where she leads an applied research organization focused on building the Adobe Firefly family of media generation models. Her team pioneered Adobe’s first large-scale foundational model in 2022. This technology—and its subsequent extensions— powers Photoshop’s Generative Fill and Generative Expand features, delivering intelligent inpainting and outpainting capabilities that were recognized by TIME as one of the Best Inventions of 2023 in the AI category. In 2024 and 2025, her team broadened Firefly into video, vector, design template, and enterprise use cases, launching the Firefly Video model, text2vector, text2template and model customization capabilities.

speakers.name: Robin Rombach
speakers.bio: Robin is co-founder and chief executive officer of Black Forest Labs, a frontier AI research lab dedicated to advancing generative models for images and videos. Before founding Black Forest Labs, he was a research director at Stability AI and pursued his PhD at LMU Munich. Robin's academic journey began with a master's degree in physics at the University of Heidelberg (2013-20). His research has centered around making generative models more efficient, contributing to the development of widely adopted models such as VQGAN, Latent Diffusion, Stable Diffusion, and Flux. He is a proponent of open-source models.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: Build Flexible and Secure Power Infrastructure for a Sustainable Future at Scale
sessions.abstract: To meet the growing demand for power of AI, we have to enable the entire ecosystem, from chip to grid. This lightning talk brings together how NVIDIA is working with its partners from power generation and transmission to distribution to build flexible and reliable infrastructure. This session will focus on use of AI for meeting energy demand, using digital technologies inside data centers to manage peak loads, and applying accelerated computing to simulate the grid and build physical infrastructure.

speakers.name: Varun Sivaram
speakers.bio: Varun is CEO and founder of Emerald AI, which transforms energy-intensive data centers into AI-powered grid allies. Varun was formerly at Orsted A/S, a $20 billion Fortune 500 Global energy firm, and served as CTO of India's largest clean energy company, ReNew Power. At the U.S. State Department, he created the First Movers Coalition. Varun is a senior fellow for energy at the Council on Foreign Relations; a senior advisor at the Boston Consulting Group, Aventurine Partners, and Everview Partners; and a board member of the Atlantic Council. A Rhodes and Truman Scholar, he holds a Ph.D. in condensed matter physics from Oxford University and undergrad degrees from Stanford University.

speakers.name: Josh Wong
speakers.bio: Josh is the Founder and CEO of ThinkLabs AI, a specialized AI company developing AI digital twins and agents for electrical grid planning and operations, to empower critical infrastructure towards energy autonomy and sustainability. ThinkLabs was named 2025 top 100 AI companies globally and across all industries and sectors by CB Insights. Josh brings over 20 years of experience in cleantech, including utility grid modernization, solar, storage, microgrids, hydrogen, and DERMS. Josh was formerly founder and CEO of Opus One Solutions, rated #1 Grid DERMS by Guidehouse, and General Manager of Grid Orchestration at GE Vernova.

speakers.name: Raj Vaswani
speakers.bio:
Raj Vaswani is a fellow and strategic advisor at Itron where he leads the technology and standards organization, coordinating unified architectures across Itron's diverse solution portfolio.
Raj joined Itron in January 2018 via its acquisition of Silver Spring Networks, where he was co-founder and chief technology officer. Raj has 25 years of innovation, product development and product management experience in the Internet, software and networking industries.
Raj received a Bachelor of Science degree in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from the University of California at Berkeley and a Master of Science in Computer Science from the University of Washington.

speakers.name: Shawntia McNeil
speakers.bio:
Shawntia McNeil is an accomplished business leader with over 20 years in the Energy industry, combining technical expertise in Electrical Engineering with an MBA in Finance. She has successfully led transformative projects across diverse global industries consistently delivering impactful business strategies that align technological advancements with Energy Solution portfolios.

At NVIDIA, Shawntia is the Global Ecosystem Partner Manager who leads strategic partnership engagements, bridging gaps to uncover synergies and drive impactful collaborations with key Energy Partners and NVIDIA stakeholder teams to develop innovative and actionable use cases that propel the Energy industry forward.

speakers.name: Gabe Mika
speakers.bio: Gabe Mika is a Managing Director at Accenture, leading the Transmission and Distribution AI & Data practice for U.S. Utilities. He brings deep experience applying computer vision, digital twins, and high‑resolution weather modeling to real‑world grid operations, informed by prior leadership roles inside large electric and gas utilities.

sessions.title: Quantum and HPC at Riken: The Sum is Greater Than Its Parts
sessions.abstract: Discover how we realize the vision in quantum-HPC integration that "the sum is greater than its parts" by integrating the Fugaku supercomputer and NVIDIA GB200 clusters with superconducting and ion-trap QPUs in our JHPC-Quantum project. Our tightly coupled software stack, including the low-latency RPC middleware, orchestrates heterogeneous resources, and when combined with our novel quantum-HPC hybrid algorithms, we achieve utility-scale results in realistic problems in areas such as advanced quantum chemistry and computational fluid dyamics using hybrid algorithms—not just those that are advantageous to quantum. With rapid advances in both quantum and HPC systems, these will likely lead to true quantum advantages in real applications in the very near future.

speakers.name: Satoshi Matsuoka
speakers.bio: Satoshi Matsuoka is director of Riken R-CCS, where he led the development of "Fugaku," the first supercomputer to simultaneously top all four global rankings. He currently co-leads the FugakuNEXT project, Riken’s AI for Science program (TRIP-AGIS), and the JHPC-Quantum hybrid computing initiative. Previously, he led the acclaimed TSUBAME series. Matsuoka is an ACM/ISC Fellow holding the IEEE Sidney Fernbach and Seymour Cray Awards, plus two ACM Gordon Bell Prizes. In 2022, he received the Medal with Purple Ribbon from the Emperor of Japan, and he's one of HPCWire’s 35 “HPC Legends.”

speakers.name: Mitsuhisa Sato
speakers.bio: Mitsuhisa Sato is a division director of Quantum HPC Hybrid Computing Platform Division in RIKEN Center for Computational Science (R-CCS) since 2023. He received the M.S. degree and the Ph.D. degree in information science from the University of Tokyo in 1984 and 1990. From 2014 to 2020, he was working as a team leader of architecture development team in FLAGSHIP 2020 project to develop Japanese flagship supercomputer, Fugaku. He was appointed to a deputy Director of R-CCS from 2018 to 202. Since 2023, he is a Professor of Juntendo University, and Professor Emeritus of University of Tsukuba.

sessions.title: Build Industrial-Scale Generative Recommenders with GPU-Optimized Retrieval and Training
sessions.abstract: This technical session distills lessons from multiple large-scale deployments—unifying recall and ranking with end-to-end generative recommenders, scaling ads/rec/search with generative retrieval, and pushing inference/training efficiency with NVIDIA-accelerated stacks—to show how to deliver measurable business lift with industrial-grade performance and cost efficiency.

speakers.name: Xuewu Jiao
speakers.bio: Now distinguished architect of Baidu, Xuewu graduated from the Institute of Computing Technology of the Chinese Academy of Sciences, and has been engaged in recommendation system work for more than 10 years. He leads the development of deep neural network model training framework AIBox/PaddleBox, graph model training framework PGLBox, generative model training framework Muque and inference engine Feihuo, etc.

speakers.name: Yongxiong Ren
speakers.bio: Yongxiong Ren received a Ph.D. in electrical engineering from the University of Southern California and is currently staff software engineer at Meta Platform, where he leads the AI infrastructure optimization for Facebook video recosys. Previously, he worked as principal engineer at Kuaishou Technology on heterogeneous computing for video recommendation and content understanding. He has more than 160 publications, including two book chapters, 15 U.S. patents, 78 journals, and 83 conference papers.

speakers.name: Fangyu Zhang
speakers.bio: Fangyu Zhang is a seasoned machine learning practitioner and software engineer based in Beijing, China. He serves as a core developer of Kuaishou's recommendation infrastructure, building machine learning infrastructure powering the recommender system for products like short videos, livestreaming, and ecommerce. He holds a master's degree from Tsinghua University.

sessions.title: Transform Content Delivery With Generative AI at the Edge
sessions.abstract:
As AI reshapes digital experiences, content delivery needs a major transformation. Traditional networks built for static caching can’t meet real-time, personalized expectations. Modern applications require generative AI inference at the edge, close to users and data, to meet strict performance, privacy, and efficiency needs. Learn how edge AI redefines how content is created, delivered, and monetized. 

By combining generative, agentic, and future physical AI with NVIDIA accelerated computing and software, you can synthesize content in real time and tailor experiences to users and markets. Learn how advanced edge compute and intelligent data systems reduce data movement, maintain sovereignty, and deliver adaptive, low-latency experiences across augmented/virtual reality, internet of things, and autonomous systems. Join us to see how Gen AI at the edge unlocks new value and innovation.

speakers.name: Kevin Deierling
speakers.bio: Combining both technical and business expertise, Kevin variously served as the chief officer of technology, architecture, and marketing at five startups; all achieved positive business outcomes. He's contributed to multiple technology standards and has over 25 patents in various technology areas. Kevin holds a B.A. in solid state physics from the University of California, Berkeley.

sessions.title: Reinventing Security for AI at Scale
sessions.abstract: As enterprises build AI factories, massive data flows, distributed compute, and real-time inferencing push infrastructure to new limits while introducing new security risks. An AI factory is a specialized data center built for intelligence at scale, yet traditional defenses struggle to secure dynamic AI pipelines and emergent model behavior without impacting performance. This session explores how a secure-by-design architecture powered by Palo Alto Networks AIRS and accelerated by NVIDIA BlueField delivers real-time, inline protection at the speed of AI. By embedding security controls at the infrastructure layer, organizations gain agentless visibility and enforcement across the AI life cycle. AIRS detects threats, blocks prompt-level attacks, and governs agent actions as systems scale. Together, these capabilities enable trustworthy, governed, high-throughput AI operations.

speakers.name: Ofir Arkin
speakers.bio: Ofir Arkin is a renowned information security expert with a career spanning academia, consulting, and executive roles. He's passionate about creating innovative products that address customer needs, and has introduced several industry-first technologies. Known for his dedication to mentoring, Ofir has authored numerous influential research papers and articles, and is a recognized speaker in the information security community.

speakers.name: Rich Campagna
speakers.bio: Rich Campagna is senior vice president for products and next-generation firewall at Palo Alto Networks, where he leads strategy for hardware, software, and cloud firewalls, 5G security, and PAN-OS. Rich joined Palo Alto with more than 20 years’ experience in technology marketing, sales, and product management, having previously led teams at Juniper, F5, Bitglass, and most recently, Zscaler. Rich holds a B.S. in electrical engineering from Penn State University and an M.B.A. from UCLA.

sessions.topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI

sessions.title: Hardware-Efficient and Lossless Video Generation at Scale: Native Sparse Attention and Mixed-Low-Bit Quantization
sessions.abstract: We present a unified, hardware-aware framework for scaling large video generation models—such as Diffusion Transformers (DiT)—without compromising visual fidelity. Learn how production-grade model compression techniques jointly eliminate the quadratic cost bottlenecks that limit training for long-context AIGC models. The session covers domain-specialized sparse attention for video sequences, robust low-precision training pipelines especially about low-bit attention mechanism. With industrial-scale results demonstrating 2x end-to-end acceleration from sparse attention and 60–70% savings via low-bit computation—all with near-zero accuracy loss—this talk provides a practical blueprint for building efficient, lossless, and deployable video generative models at scale.

speakers.name: Fei Li
speakers.bio: Fei focuses on research and implementation of low-precision computing (including attention mechanisms) in multi-modal model training.

speakers.name: Guangyun Han
speakers.bio: Guangyun is an accelerated computing specialist who joined NVIDIA in 2024. He focuses on kernel development, performance optimization, and inference acceleration support for key clients

speakers.name: Yiqiao Liao
speakers.bio: Tech Leader of the Kling Training Infrastructure Team. Ph.D. in Electronic Engineering from Tsinghua University, with 10 years of experience in AI infrastructure and AI algorithms. Deeply involved from the ground up in the end-to-end training of Kling Video Generation, Kolors Image Generation, and Klear Large Language Model. Leading the efforts on training cluster stability and MFU optimization for Kling, while actively exploring low-precision training workflows for multimodal models. Published 10+ papers in top-tier system and algorithm venues including DAC, KDD, SoCC, and ISCAS, with five as first author. Holds 30 patents across the US and China. Team's work has been featured in multiple invitedtalks at NVIDIA GTC, and contributed key features such as PP-aware Offload and Dynamic CP to the official Megatron-Core repository.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: Building, Measuring, and Using AI Scientists
sessions.abstract: Learn how AI scientists can automate generation and testing of hypotheses with experiments in a loop. See how to maintain long-running coherence required for their days runtime, and the nuances of benchmarking the process of discovery.  Explore their automated discoveries across domains like molecular biology, materials science, and chemistry.

speakers.name: Andrew White
speakers.bio: Andrew is co-founder of FutureHouse and Edison Scientific, where he has led pioneering research on applying AI and agents to science. Andrew is also a professor with published research in the topics of chemistry, explainable AI, statistical mechanics, and chemical engineering, and has received numerous awards, including junior investigator awards from the National Science Foundation and National Institutes of Health.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Biology - Generative AI

sessions.title: How We Scaled Kimi K2.5
sessions.abstract: Explore how we scaled Kimi K2.5 by pioneering the Muon optimizer to double token learning efficiency and maximize training throughput through Day 0 infrastructure co-design. Gain insights into AI-native training and linear attention architectures to help you unlock the potential of longer-running agents.

speakers.name: Zhilin Yang
speakers.bio: Zhilin Yang founded Kimi to seek the optimal solution for converting energy into intelligence. In 2025, Kimi empowered the open-source community with advanced agentic coding and reasoning capabilities via the trillion-parameter Kimi K2 and K2 Thinking. The Kimi app serves tens of millions of users monthly. Zhilin holds a B.S. from Tsinghua University and a Ph.D. in natural language processing from Carnegie Mellon University.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)

sessions.title: Quantum Computing at NERSC and Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
sessions.abstract: In this talk, we highlight how emerging quantum computing technologies are poised to impact the National Energy Research Scientific Computing Center’s scientific workload. We show how NVIDIA’s hardware and software stack already enable next-generation quantum device and application simulations, and explain how NERSC’s future quantum roadmap will build on these capabilities.

speakers.name: Katie Klymko
speakers.bio: Katie Klymko received her Ph.D. in theoretical chemistry from UC Berkeley in 2018. She completed a postdoctoral appointment at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory from 2018-21, working on quantum algorithms for molecular eigenvalue problems and thermodynamic properties. She joined NERSC as staff in 2021, where she works to bring quantum computing capabilities into NERSC’s future HPC workloads.

sessions.title: How Perplexity AI Scales Real-Time Search With Breakthrough Mixture-of-Experts Performance
sessions.abstract: Scaling mixture-of-experts (MoE) models in production while delivering high performance is challenging. This session explores how the NVIDIA Blackwell NVL72 makes MoE scaling practical and efficient with full stack innovation across compute, networking, and software. Hear from Perplexity AI on why they have adopted MoE models, detailing how they leverage Blackwell NVL72 to deliver real-time search with breakthrough performance.

speakers.name: Shruti Koparkar
speakers.bio: Shruti Koparkar is a senior manager, product marketing in the Accelerated Computing Group at NVIDIA, focusing on AI inference. She has cross-functional experience spanning engineering, product management, and product marketing—notably at Amazon Web Services and Arm. She holds a master’s degree in electrical and computer engineering from the University of Minnesota.

speakers.name: Kevin Hu
speakers.bio: Kevin Hu is an Inference Lead at Perplexity AI where he works on LLM inference. Prior to Perplexity AI, he was at NVIDIA on the NVIDIA TensorRT team. He holds a bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from UC Berkeley.

sessions.title: Agentic AI Using Open Source Models: Finetuning, Deployment and Chip Design Case Studies
sessions.abstract:
Open models have evolved from experimental alternatives to production-grade building blocks for agentic AI and enterprise applications. But deciding when to use open source models, and choosing and customizing them requires a clear framework. 

In this session, we'll walk through a practical decision framework for deploying open models at scale from picking the right model size for your agentic tasks to the software and practices for customizing, evaluating, and deploying them. We'll bring it to life with two case studies: how CodeRabbit runs open-source models in production, and how NVIDIA’s chip design team turned early failures into a repeatable flywheel: using a decision framework to pick use cases, then fine-tuning open-source models for the patterns that stuck. Whether you're building agentic AI, internal copilots, or cost-sensitive services, this talk will give you the framework and tools to ship with confidence.

speakers.name: Chris Alexiuk
speakers.bio: Chris Alexiuk is a Sr. Product Research Engineer at NVIDIA, working on creating technical assets that help developers use the incredible suite of AI tools available at NVIDIA. Chris comes from a machine learning and data science background, and he is obsessed with everything and anything about large language models.

speakers.name: David Loker
speakers.bio: David Loker is VP of AI at CodeRabbit, leading agentic AI for code reviews and developer workflows. An entrepreneur and award-winning researcher, he has built large-scale ML systems since 2007. He’s published 12 papers at NeurIPS, ICML, and AAAI, and was an early generative AI pioneer. From startups to hands-on engineering, David pairs deep technical rigor with product vision to turn research into real value.

speakers.name: Shraddha Sridhar
speakers.bio: Shraddha Sridhar leads Product Management for AI for Chip Design at NVIDIA, bringing agentic AI to thousands of hardware engineers to accelerate next-generation GPU development. With a background in analog circuit design and a track record of scaling AI products, she focuses on translating frontier AI into measurable impact in high-stakes engineering environments.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails と Amazon Bedrock による AI ガードレール構築と検知性能評価
sessions.abstract: 本講演では、NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails と Amazon Bedrock を用いて生成 AI アプリケーションにガードレールを実装し、有害コンテンツに対する検知性能をどのように評価・改善していくかを、実例とともに紹介します。言語間（英語・日本語）の違いや、検知率と誤検知率のバランスといった複数の観点から検知性能を比較し、安全性と利便性のバランスを意識した評価プロセスの具体例を共有します。

speakers.name: 松井 凌
speakers.bio: メーカー系SIerを経て、2023年に株式会社村田製作所へ入社。データサイエンティストとして画像データや時系列データを扱う社内プロジェクトに携わり、2024年からはRAG（Retrieval-Augmented Generation）やAIエージェントなどの生成AI技術を活用した社内アプリケーション開発に取り組んでいる。

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Voice-Native Agents: Rearchitecting Conversational AI with Open, Low-Latency Speech Models
sessions.abstract:
In this hands-on tutorial, two lead engineers walk through how to build real-time, voice-native conversational systems using integrated open-weight models and response generation. Learn when to open models to power low-latency, natural conversations in production.

You’ll get practical guidance on customizing Nemotron Speech for your domain, wiring in function calling, and adapting models quickly to new intents, accents, and acoustic environments. We also explore how to use tools like Data Designer to tame fragmented or franchise data sources and turn them into robust, queryable pipelines for conversational AI.

Through live coding and demos, you'll see how to stitch together end-to-end pipelines for high-value scenarios so you can develop voice agents with low latency and optimal efficiency at scale.

speakers.name: Oluwatobi Olabiyi
speakers.bio: Tobi is a senior engineering manager at NVIDIA, currently leading teams in delivering state-of-the-art multi-modal, multilingual conversational AI solutions for customers. Previously, Tobi was a senior manager of AI research at Capital One and a senior research scientist at Toyota Research Institute. He holds a Ph.D. in electrical engineering from Prairie View A&M University.

speakers.name: Rockford Yost
speakers.bio: Rockford “Rocky” Yost is an analytics and data engineering leader who builds enterprise-scale measurement strategies and high-performing teams. With deep expertise across the marketing analytics stack—from Adobe Analytics and Google Analytics to Python, SparkSQL, and modern data warehouses—Rocky unifies disparate data sources to drive measurable business impact. His track record spans agency innovation to enterprise execution: designing data strategies, rolling out analytics teams, and translating customer insights into competitive advantage.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation

sessions.title: Performance Optimization of cuTile Kernels
sessions.abstract: This tutorial guides you through the implementation of Mixture of Experts (MoE) using cuTile, focusing on how to express complex dataflows—including matrix products, gather and scatter of tokens, dynamic routing—directly in terms of multidimensional tiles. Explore how the cuTile compiler automates the most difficult aspects of GPU optimization, such as asynchronous memory movement, software pipelining, and Tensor Core utilization. By the end of the session, you'll understand how to move from a high-level algorithm description to a production-quality kernel that achieves near-peak performance while remaining readable and maintainable.

speakers.name: Rob Armstrong
speakers.bio: Rob Armstrong is a principal technical product manager for the CUDA toolkit. For over 20 years, he's focused on accelerating software with heterogeneous hardware platforms, and has particular interest in computer architecture and hardware/software interaction.

speakers.name: Vishal Mehta
speakers.bio: Vishal works as a senior developer technology engineer at NVIDIA, focusing on performance optimization for GPU applications. He's been working in the field of GPU computing for over 10 years. He is keen on teaching CUDA and GPU computing to users, and drives the content for the CUDA programming guide. His day-to-day activities involve collaborations with domain scientists and industry experts to improve their workloads on GPUs.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Evaluation-Driven Development: Best Practices for Building Reliable Agents
sessions.abstract:
Building agents is easy; reliably improving them is hard. As workflows shift to complex long-horizon tasks, traditional testing fails. How do you debug and improve non-deterministic behavior?

This session presents a framework for evaluation-driven development. We move beyond “vibe eval” to disciplined engineering covering ablations, unit tests, trajectory evaluation, and LLM-as-judge patterns. Using a case study of achieving No. 1 on a Deep Research leaderboard, we demonstrate how to build pipelines that catch hallucinations and regressions while preventing overfitting. You'll leave with a workflow for implementing evaluation methods that take agents from prototype to scalable deployment using a suite of tooling enabled by NVIDIA NeMo and open NVIDIA Nemotron models for advanced reasoning.

speakers.name: Adel El Hallak
speakers.bio: Adel El Hallak is vice president of Software Product Management for NVIDIA AI Enterprise, a suite of APIs, libraries, and runtimes that simplify the development, deployment, and scaling of agentic AI. He focuses on blueprints for operationalizing production-grade AI agentic systems through NVIDIA's partner ecosystem. Adel holds a Bachelor's in Computer Science from McGill University and an MBA from Warwick Business School.

speakers.name: David Austin
speakers.bio: David is a senior data scientist at NVIDIA, and a Kaggle Competitions Grandmaster.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Building Domain-Expert Agents: How to Optimize Txt2SQL and Tool-Calling with Open Models
sessions.abstract:
Enterprises are moving beyond prompt engineering toward repeatable, data-driven customization loops. This technical session explores how Aible and Yum! Brands transitioned NVIDIA Nemotron 3 from general-purpose models into specialized domain experts.
Aible demonstrates a Txt2SQL data flywheel designed for highly regulated environments, utilizing SME feedback and data augmentation to drive query accuracy and strict compliance. Yum! Brands details their approach to agentic tool-calling for high-volume ordering (e.g., Taco Bell), leveraging Small Language Models (SLMs) and SDG-style reasoning traces to improve tool-use disambiguation and execution latency.
Arijit Sengupta (CEO & Founder, Aible), Rocky Yost (Head of AI, Yum! Brands) and NVIDIA walk through these production data flywheels built with NVIDIA NeMo microservices. Learn how to design evaluation-led iteration cycles and determine when to use specialized SLMs versus full-scale model alignment for your own domain-specific use cases.

speakers.name: Santiago Pombo
speakers.bio: Santiago is a product manager for generative AI, agentic workflows, and retrieval-augmented generation at NVIDIA, working on NVIDIA NIM microservices that enable enterprises to build production-ready applications.

speakers.name: Arijit Sengupta
speakers.bio: Arijit Sengupta, Founder and CEO of Aible, the only Enterprise AI solution that guarantees business impact in just one month. Arijit is the former Founder and CEO of BeyondCore, a market-leading Automated Analytics solution that was acquired by Salesforce.com to become Einstein Discovery. Arijit co-created and co-instructed the first AI course in the MBA program of the Harvard Business School as an Executive Fellow. He also wrote a book entitled “AI is a Waste of Money” based on the results of a thousand AI projects he had led at that time. Arijit holds an MBA with Distinction from the Harvard Business School and Bachelor degrees with Distinction in Computer Science and Economics from Stanford University.

speakers.name: Rockford Yost
speakers.bio: Rockford “Rocky” Yost is an analytics and data engineering leader who builds enterprise-scale measurement strategies and high-performing teams. With deep expertise across the marketing analytics stack—from Adobe Analytics and Google Analytics to Python, SparkSQL, and modern data warehouses—Rocky unifies disparate data sources to drive measurable business impact. His track record spans agency innovation to enterprise execution: designing data strategies, rolling out analytics teams, and translating customer insights into competitive advantage.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: NVIDIA 101: From Chips to Software—How NVIDIA's AI Platform Enables the AI Revolution
sessions.abstract: Attend this introductory session for an overview of how NVIDIA’s AI platform sparked the agentic and physical AI revolution. We'll showcase real-world examples of how NVIDIA has disrupted some of the world’s major industries. An interactive Connect With the Experts session will follow the talk, where you can engage with NVIDIA’s hardware firsthand and ask any follow-up questions. Check out our Connect with the Experts session here.

speakers.name: Rambo Jacoby
speakers.bio: Rambo Jacoby is a principal engineer with a 24-year tenure at NVIDIA, and one of NVIDIA’s most dynamic and influential speakers. He currently contributes to NVIDIA’s Executive Briefing Center, where he's responsible for educating world leaders and Fortune 500 executives on NVIDIA’s AI platform and the transformative impact of graphics, digital twins, high-performance computing, and agentic and physical AI on the world’s major industries.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Parameterized CUDA Graph Launch in PyTorch: CUDA Graphs Without the Pain
sessions.abstract: Modern GPUs have gotten so fast that CPUs cannot launch new work to the GPUs fast enough, which reduces overall efficiency. CUDA Graphs can eliminate CPU overhead completely, but have traditionally been hard to use, so let’s break down the problems and fixes for them, focusing on PyTorch in particular.

speakers.name: Daniel Galvez
speakers.bio: Daniel Galvez is an AI developer technology engineer working on speech recognition and natural language processing inference and training. He has contributed to software like PyTorch, NeMo, Megatron, ESPNet, vLLM, and TRT-LLM. He is currently working on reducing CPU overheads in CUDA workloads.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Deep Learning Frameworks

sessions.title: Swallow LLM: Continual Pre-Training and RL for Sovereign AI
sessions.abstract: Dive deep into the engineering of Swallow, Japan’s sovereign AI, where we trained from Qwen3 and GPT-OSS using Megatron-LM with custom datasets like SwallowMath-v2 and SwallowCode-v2. Learn how our pipeline of continual pre-training, supervised fine-tuning (SFT), and reinforcement learning (RL) achieves high Japanese proficiency while preserving the original models’ reasoning capabilities, offering a scalable framework for sovereign AI development in other non-English languages.

speakers.name: Kazuki Fujii
speakers.bio: Kazuki Fujii is a graduate student at the Institute of Science Tokyo, leading the Swallow Project, an open bilingual LLM initiative for Japanese and English. His work focuses on large-scale pre-training, distributed training, FP8 mixed-precision with Megatron-LM and TransformerEngine, and improving the quality of code and math datasets for reasoning LLMs.

speakers.name: Rio Yokota
speakers.bio: Rio Yokota received his Ph.D. in engineering from the Graduate School of Science and Technology, Keio University in 2009. He joined the Global Scientific Information and Computing Center (GSIC) at Tokyo Institute of Technology as an associate professor in 2015, and has served as a professor since 2023.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Transforming the Automotive Industry With Industrial AI and Digital Twins
sessions.abstract:
The automotive industry is undergoing a major shift as AI, simulation, and digital twins redefine how vehicles are designed, engineered, manufactured, and validated. As automakers move to AI-enabled, data-driven development pipelines, they’re unlocking new levels of efficiency, quality, and innovation across the vehicle life cycle.

In this panel, leaders from NVIDIA, Hyundai, Lucid, and Schaeffler will explore how industrial AI, virtual development environments, large-scale digital twins, and advanced simulation are accelerating automotive innovation. They’ll share real examples of AI-powered design, automated engineering, factory optimization, and robotics training converging into a unified end-to-end pipeline, along with insights on the technologies and collaboration driving industry transformation.

speakers.name: Norm Marks
speakers.bio: Norm Marks drives NVIDIA’s global automotive industry enterprise go-to-market strategy. His focus is on establishing areas of application for the NVIDIA open, full-stack, and software-defined computing platform to transform the automotive and transportation industries.

speakers.name: Roberto Henkel
speakers.bio: Roberto Henkel is Senior Vice President Digitalization & Operations IT at Schaeffler, leading global IT and AI transformation across more than 100 production plants. An engineer and economist, he drives cloud-based operations, AI-enabled digital factories, OT security, and end-to-end IT for manufacturing, supply chain, and quality. Previously, he held leadership roles including General Manager of Schaeffler Digital Solutions and Vice President Precision Bearings. He holds degrees in Mechanical Engineering and General Management and is a professional mountaineer.

speakers.name: Jason Ryska
speakers.bio: Jason Ryska is Vice President of Global Manufacturing Engineering at Lucid, where he leads worldwide manufacturing engineering strategy. Based at Lucid’s manufacturing facility in Arizona, Jason is responsible for the architecture and deployment of advanced manufacturing systems, digital twins, industrial AI/ML, and data-driven process control across the company’s global manufacturing footprint. His work focuses on scaling highly automated, high-mix EV manufacturing while improving quality, throughput, and cost through closed-loop analytics, physics-informed models, and real-time factory intelligence.

speakers.name: Alpesh Patel
speakers.bio:
Mr. Alpesh Patel joined HMGICS as the head of the Technology Innovation Group in February 2023 and was appointed as the Chief Innovation Officer (CIO) ofHMGICS in January 2024. From March 2025, he also took on the role of head of the SDF Transformation Center at the Headquarters.​

Before joining HMGICS, Alpesh was recognized as a leading figure in Industry 4.0 in the Asia-Pacific region while serving as a senior leader at McKinsey & Company. He played a key role in the success of the firm’s Digital Capability Centers (DCCs), which he helped develop and expand across the region starting in 2016. These centers aimed at helping companies leverage advanced manufacturing technologies, showcasing use-cases supported by over 100 technology providers. Alpesh was instrumental in founding DCC Singapore in collaborationwith the Singapore Economic Development Board (EDB) and the Advanced Remanufacturing and Technology Center (ARTC), overseeing the network across Asia-Pacific, including India, Japan, China, Indonesia, and Singapore.​ Mr. Alpesh Patel has also accumulated extensive Industry 4.0 experience across a range of sectors, including automotive, motorsports, logistics, and public sector initiatives. He played a central role in driving the adoption of digital performance management among hundreds of SMEs in Singapore. Additionally, he assisted a Formula One team in improving engine performance and worked with automotive manufacturers to enhance
supply chain visibility, optimizing their operations with advanced digital solutions.​

speakers.name: Tracey Countryman
speakers.bio:
Tracey Countryman is a senior leader at Accenture focused on driving end-to-end transformation across manufacturing and supply chain. With a career spanning 30 years, of which 27 has been with Accenture, she helped build the original supply chain and manufacturing business of Accenture dating back to 2009 for North America.

Tracey’s career has transversed between leading deep functional teams, client account management, and industry leadership. She brings cross-discipline capabilities and deep domain expertise to help clients envision, plan, and deploy manufacturing, product engineering, and capital project transformation at scale—unlocking value through technology and human ingenuity.

Tracey has worked across a range of heavy asset intensive industries including Oil & Gas, Mining, Metals, Chemicals, Pharmaceuticals, Consumer Goods, Industrials, and Utilities, guiding organizations through all stages of their production, manufacturing and supply chain journey. She was previously the Industry X Global Manufacturing and Capital Projects Lead, industry X North American lead, Global Refining Industry lead, and North American Metals and Mining lead.

Her goal in every role is to deliver value-led outcomes underpinned by advanced engineering solutions, that enable high performing and sustainable operations with superior worker experiences.

A passionate advocate for breaking down silos, Tracey believes the full potential of purpose-led visions and sustainable growth will be realized when best practices and learnings are shared across industries, functions, and regions. Tracey truly understands the power of leveraging ecosystem partners to drive the next generation of manufacturing and supply chain solutions and believes that Accenture is in a prime position to deliver on the promise of Industry 4.0 and beyond.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: 複雑ドキュメント向けソブリンAI：日本の先端事例から学ぶ
sessions.abstract:
一般的な LLM では処理が難しい、製造業で用いられる技術文書などの複雑かつ機密性の高いドキュメントの読み取りに特化したソブリン AI / ドキュメント読解 LLM の開発について紹介します。また、ドメイン特化モデルのトレーニングにおいて直面した実際の課題と、それらを NVIDIA の先端技術を用いてどのように解決したかを解説します。
更に、信頼性・生産性・イノベーション向上のために、企業がこれらのソブリン AI をどのように導入しているかについて、先端事例を紹介します。

speakers.name: Kosuke Arima
speakers.bio: Kosuke co-founded Stockmark as its chief technology officer in 2016. Supported by the Japanese government through the official Generative AI development project (GENIAC), Stockmark developed a 100 billion-parameter LLM from scratch and released it as open source in May 2024. It's currently the largest open-source LLM in Japan. Kosuke received a master's degree in machine learning from the University of Tokyo in 2010.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Automotive Special Address: Advancing Level 4 Autonomy, the Path to Scalable, Safe AVs and Robotaxis
sessions.abstract: Autonomous vehicles (AVs) are emerging as the first mass-deployed physical AI agents, reshaping mobility and driving new demands in AI, safety, and scalable development. This special address explores the industry’s path to Level 4 autonomy and how NVIDIA is helping accelerate it. With the DRIVE Hyperion architecture, manufacturers (OEMs) and AV developers can build scalable, safety-certified autonomy solutions. NVIDIA will present its cloud-to-car platform—spanning training, simulation, and in-vehicle compute—to support developing and validating next-generation drive-policy models. The session will also touch on how agentic AI is poised to transform AVs end to end, from automating data-driven development and validation to enabling more intelligent, adaptive in-car experiences. Gain insights into L4 software architectures, the DRIVE Hyperion platform, and ecosystem collaboration across OEMs, AV developers, mobility providers, and robotaxi companies.

speakers.name: Xinzhou Wu
speakers.bio:
Xinzhou Wu is vice president of automotive at NVIDIA. He leads strategy, product planning, and engineering execution for the automotive business. He partners with auto manufacturers to enable autonomy in mass production cars around the world and to improve the user experience with both in-vehicle and in-cloud AI technologies from NVIDIA.

Previously, Xinzhou led XPENG Motor's Autonomous Driving Center for five years, with a team of more than 1,000 members across the U.S. and China. Before that, he led Qualcomm’s autonomous driving team.
 
Xinzhou received a B.S. from Tsinghua University, as well as M.S. and Ph.D. from University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign—all in electrical engineering.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Vision Language Models (VLMs)

sessions.title: CSO Insights: Driving AI-Optimized Energy Efficiency and Data Center Performance
sessions.abstract: Join Chief Sustainability Officers (CSOs) to learn how AI is transforming enterprise energy and infrastructure at scale. This session will include: insights on applying AI to optimize energy efficiency, grid-integrated operations, and enhance data center performance at scale, concrete examples from leading companies—including Amazon, PepsiCo and Equinix—demonstrating measurable impact on operations and supply chains, and executive perspective on translating sustainability and energy efficiency goals into bottom-line impact—exploring how AI efficiency translates into a win-win for operational and strategic advantage.

speakers.name: Kara Hurst
speakers.bio:
Kara leads Worldwide Sustainability at Amazon, executing The Climate Pledge, bringing sustainability to operations, science and innovation, products, social responsibility and supply chain management, circular economy, packaging and shopping, technology, transportation, and external engagement and policy setting. 

Previously, Kara was CEO of The Sustainability Consortium. Before that, she worked as vice president of BSR. Kara also co-founded of the Electronic Industry Citizenship Coalition and worked in Silicon Valley at OpenVoice. 
Kara holds a B.A. from Barnard College of Columbia University and an M.P.P. from the University of California, Berkeley.

speakers.name: Christopher Wellise
speakers.bio:
As the global VP of sustainability, Christopher sets the company's sustainability strategy and collaborates with other company leaders to interpret changes in the external sustainability environment and work out strategic consequences for Equinix. They communicate and cut through organizational complexity to enable Equinix to deliver on environmental, social, and governance commitments.

Previously, he led AWS’s global sustainability effort. Before that, he was the CSO at Hewlett Packard Enterprise, where he led the setting of the first science-based supply chain climate target and built a customer-facing sustainability team. He began his career working for the U.S. Department of Interior.

speakers.name: Tenika Versey Walker
speakers.bio: Tenika has over 15 years of expertise in AI and tech marketing. She's the global head of sustainable futures for the NVIDIA Inception program, as well as a visiting scholar at Stanford University in the Department of Geophysics' Stanford Doerr School of Sustainability, where she forges partnerships between Stanford researchers and the climate tech ecosystem. She's on the board of directors at the Seattle Aquarium, on the Advisory Board for Black Women in Venture Capital, and on the Industry Advisory Board for Duke University's Master of Engineering in Cybersecurity program. She earned her M.B.A. from Brown University and completed her undergraduate studies at the University of Washington.

speakers.name: Jim Andrew
speakers.bio:
As EVP and chief sustainability officer at PepsiCo, Jim leads global sustainability strategy. Since joining PepsiCo in 2016, he's been the architect of pep+ (PepsiCo Positive)—promoting regenerative agriculture, restorative practices, watershed health, resilient manufacturing, and sustainable packaging. Digitalizing and integrating AI into pep+ is a key focus.

Previously, Jim held other leadership positions at PepsiCo and led the Beyond the Bottle unit, which includes SodaStream. Before that, he served as EVP and chief strategy and innovation officer at Royal Philips in Amsterdam. 

Jim holds an M.B.A. from Harvard Business School and a B.S. in accountancy from the University of Illinois.

sessions.title: Turning Models Into Engines: The AI Factory Era
sessions.abstract: Step inside Mistral’s journey to industrial-scale AI as we share lessons learned and the principles guiding our approach in creating Mistral Compute. Built on four key pillars—open-source foundations, security by design, sustainability at scale, and digital sovereignty—we’ll share how our approach unlocks bare-metal GPU performance, why that matters in building trusted, European-hosted future-ready AI systems, and how we’re bringing collaboration to the next level by giving innovators access to the same infrastructure powering our models.

speakers.name: Gauthier Delerce
speakers.bio: After years of leading the delivery of large clusters for Atos, HPE, and NVIDIA, Gauthier joined the Mistral AI rocket to build an European NCP that meets the high-level services requested by MistralAI and its customers.

speakers.name: Jean-Olivier Gerphagnon
speakers.bio: Jean-Olivier (JOG), a principal software architect with a decade of experience as the architect of the Bull/Atos/Eviden HPC stack, joined Mistral to develop a state-of-the-aret management stack.

sessions.topic: Data Center / Cloud - Software-Defined Data Center

sessions.title: Big Compute Powering the Energy Value Chain
sessions.abstract: Herlinde Mannaerts-Drew, SVP oil and gas technology, will discuss how bp is working with NVIDIA to help enhance seismic imaging and reservoir modelling to enable faster exploration and reduce uncertainty.

speakers.name: Herlinde Mannaerts-Drew
speakers.bio:
Herlinde is the senior vice president of oil and gas technology at bp, leading a team of experts responsible for enhancing oil and gas delivery through technology solutions.
Herlinde brings over two decades of experience in subsurface transformation, geophysical operations and low-carbon technologies. She was previously the VP of subsurface transformation, leading strategic initiatives to enhance subsurface performance and supporting the growth of bp’s businesses, specifically in CCUS and geothermal. Previous roles at bp include manager of complex imaging, director of geophysics and global seismic delivery manager.
Herlinde holds a master’s degree in mining and engineering geology from Delft University of Technology.

sessions.title: Agentic AI for the Next Generation of Financial Advisors
sessions.abstract: In this session, we'll dive into designing an agentic system to support financial analysis and advisory. Inspired by NVIDIA AI-Q Research Assistant blueprint, and leveraging NVIDIA's multi-modal retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline, our system not only provides a tool that can take financial analysis—one of the core tasks in finance—to the next level by supporting internal experts, but also makes an important step toward building a sovereign agentic architecture.

speakers.name: Nicolas Moch
speakers.bio: Nicolas is a leader in digitalization, innovation, and IT/business alignment. He has extensive experience, both as a C-level executive and as consultant, driving and delivering digital transformation and adoption of new technologies. It includes introducing and rolling out (for example) cloud and AI, organizational design, and agile transformation, and a 10-year experience as group CIO for SEB as member of the executive committee, responsible for the overall digital agenda of the bank. He is currently responsible for SEBx, the exploratory organization of SEB. Nicolas sits on the bank’s venture capital investment committee and has an extensive board experience in technology and banking.

speakers.name: Anastasia Varava
speakers.bio: Anastasia Varava is currently head of research at SEBx, an innovation studio at SEB, where she's working with a team of AI experts toward bringing state-of-the-art AI to applications in finance. She is also a faculty member in machine learning and robotics at The Royal Institute of Technology, KTH, in Stockholm. Prior to joining SEB, Anastasia completed her Ph.D. in computer science at KTH, and then worked as a researcher in machine learning and robotics there.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Nemotron Unpacked: Build, Fine-Tune, and Deploy NVIDIA's Open Models
sessions.abstract: Hear from NVIDIA’s VP of Applied Deep Learning Research the story of NVIDIA Nemotron—an open-source ecosystem for the full AI life cycle. NVIDIA Nemotron is a collection of multi-modal and reasoning models, datasets, and training techniques designed to leverage NVIDIA’s experience in accelerated computing for each step of your AI journey.

speakers.name: Bryan Catanzaro
speakers.bio: Bryan is vice president of applied deep learning research at NVIDIA, where his team solves problems in fields ranging from video games to chip design. Bryan joined NVIDIA as a research scientist in 2011, when he drove the creation of CUDNN, now used by most AI researchers to train neural networks. He also worked at Baidu, where he built systems for training deep learning-based speech recognition on extremely large datasets, in both English and Chinese. Bryan earned his Ph.D. from the UC Berkeley, where he built the Copperhead language and compiler.
