He earned both his M.S. and B.S. from Brigham Young University, where he worked on computer arithmetic for field-programmable gate arrays.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: From Code to Capital: Empowering Women Tech Founders
sessions.abstract: This session explores proven strategies, real-world challenges, and ecosystem shifts shaping the journey from building breakthrough technologies to securing critical funding for women tech founders. Despite receiving only a fraction of global venture capital, women-led startups are achieving remarkable growth and impact—particularly in deep tech and AI sectors. Join a dynamic conversation with investors, entrepreneurs, and advocates to uncover pathways for women in tech to access capital, expand networks, and drive innovation, ensuring talent and ambition translate into tangible business success.

speakers.name: Jessica Driscoll
speakers.bio: Jessica Driscoll leads the NVIDIA Inception program across the U.K. and Ireland, accelerating innovation by connecting AI startups with NVIDIA’s experts, resources, and cutting-edge technology. Deeply embedded in the U.K.’s innovation ecosystem, she previously served as director of immersive technology at Digital Catapult. There, she shaped the region’s physical AI strategy and led high-impact national acceleration initiatives helping early-stage companies secure investment and scale effectively.

speakers.name: Moojan Asghari
speakers.bio:
Born in Iran and based in Paris, Moojan Asghari is a serial entrepreneur, technologist, activist, and a strong advocate for diversity and inclusion. In 2017, Moojan founded the global nonprofit Women in AI, an organization across more than 150 countries with over 20,000 members, to encourage women to take part in the field of AI and embrace its opportunities.

She is the co-founder of Thousand Faces, a funding platform to enable female founders to raise capital and build impact-driven companies, focused on the U.N.'s sustainable development goals. She has served on several  advisory boards promoting gender diversity and inclusion.

speakers.name: Carrie Baptist
speakers.bio: Carrie is chief strategy officer of Conception X, Europe's top Ph.D. deeptech accelerator. Conception X works with Ph.D. researchers from more than 80 universities across the U.K. and Europe, building deeptech startups now valued at more than $1 billion.

speakers.name: Sara Landfors
speakers.bio: Sara Landfors is the Co-Founder and CEO of Normain, an AI platform that enables business teams to turn chaotic files into structured insights, pioneering Extractional AI. With an Engineering degree from Stanford, she previously built data science solutions at BCG X and helped grow Validio, a data observability startup that raised $15M. Under her leadership, Normain—valued at €4M within just six months of being founded—has attracted top investors and been featured in Sifted, and local media as one of the top Nordic AI startups to watch. Sara herself is a recognized thought leader in AI and entrepreneurship, known for bridging deep tech and business impact.

speakers.name: Zoe Qin
speakers.bio:
Zoe is an investor at Dawn Capital, Europe’s leading B2B investor, where she covers early-stage SaaS and fintech opportunities. Her investments include stellar startups Flatpay, Doubleword and Runware.  
  
She also co-founded AI Engine, the innovation ecosystem for technical talent and forward-thinking organisations. Through curated events, including hackathons, AI Engine has opened up access to the tech industry across diverse demographics.
 
Zoe joined Dawn from Goldman Sachs’ trading floor as a quant strategist where she found her passion for technology investment. She holds a Physics degree from the University of Cambridge.

sessions.title: Vibe Decoding Quantum Error Correction With CUDA-Q
sessions.abstract: Any fault-tolerant quantum computer must be tightly integrated with classical co-processors that decode quantum error correction measurements in real time. These decoders must operate with extreme speed, high accuracy, and broad versatility. In this talk, I will present our recently proposed Vibe Decoding strategy, in which an ensemble of diverse decoders is run in parallel to achieve performance beyond current state-of-the-art approaches. I'll also discuss its practical realization using CUDA-Q.

speakers.name: Joschka Roffe
speakers.bio: Joschka Roffe is an EPSRC Quantum Technologies Fellow at the University of Edinburgh, where he leads a team on fault-tolerant quantum computing architecture. His research focuses on the practical implementation of quantum error correction across a range of qubit hardware modalities, including questions concerning the integration and optimization of classical co-processing systems.

sessions.title: The Road to Intelligent Mobility: How In-Vehicle GenAI Is Redefining the AI-Defined Vehicle
sessions.abstract: The automotive industry is rapidly pivoting toward a future where intelligence inside the vehicle defines brand differentiation, customer experience, and long-term value. Breakthroughs in GenAI, cloud–edge orchestration, and accelerated computing are enabling automakers to deliver safer, smarter, and more intuitive experiences—turning every vehicle into a continuously improving digital product. In this panel we will share how leaders across different companies in the ecosystem - AI infrastructure, cloud, Tier-1 platforms, and next-generation EV OEMs - are shaping the in-vehicle GenAI landscape. The session offers a view of where the industry is headed and what it will take to bring truly intelligent, AI-powered vehicles to market at global scale.

speakers.name: Sri Subramanian
speakers.bio: Sri Subramanian is responsible for NVIDIA’s global automotive Gen AI business, including go-to-market strategy, business development, and customer and partner relations. He's focused on Gen AI opportunities with automotive customers everywhere, from in-car to manufacturing to enterprise, and establishing AI foundries using NVIDIA assets such as NeMo and NIMs. Sri has 25 years of experience spanning the automotive, mobile, and internet-of-things industries, leveraging cloud and embedded technologies. Prior to NVIDIA, he held product and engineering leadership roles at Amazon, Qualcomm, NXP, and Motorola, among others. Sri holds a master's degree in electrical engineering from Georgia Tech and an M.B.A. from the University of Texas, Austin.

speakers.name: Thomas Evans
speakers.bio: Dr. Thomas Evans serves as the CAIO Head of Artificial Intelligence for Lucid Motors. Within this role he is responsible for leading AI product development and enterprise digital transformation. Prior to Lucid, Evans held positions of Chief AI Transformation Officer and CTO of Robotics & Advanced Automation at Honeywell, and Research Director supporting NASA's autonomous robotics programs.

speakers.name: Raj Paul
speakers.bio:
Raj Paul serves as Microsoft’s Mobility Industry Leader for the Americas region. He has responsibility for Industry Sales at mobility customers and partners involved in the movement of people and goods. He and his team engage with vehicle manufacturers, their suppliers, mobility service providers, travel, transportation, and logistics providers.  Additionally, he plays a global role as leader of the Automotive vertical, where he crafts the overarching go-to-market strategy and perspectives.
Raj is a passionate advocate of the connected world, with more than 20 years of experience in the Automotive industry. He has been involved in the evolution of vehicle connectivity, creating in-vehicle innovations and infrastructure to connect owners and Vehicle OEMs with their vehicles and with each other. With his work in Telematics and Infotainment at various OEMs, which include 15 years at GM OnStar, he has helped create new Automotive experiences, new ways of connecting with customers and platforms that fuel Intelligence.

speakers.name: Auston Payyappilly
speakers.bio:
Auston Payyappilly is an accomplished technology leader with extensive experience across product management, systems & software architecture, and advanced vehicle computing for Cockpit & ADAS vehicle computers. With a background spanning software engineering, technical leadership, and innovation strategy, he has contributed to the development of next-generation automotive technologies and long-term product roadmaps. His career began with entrepreneurial pursuits before transitioning into the automotive sector, where he has guided teams, shaped complex products, and built strong industry partnerships.
In 2025, Auston received the prestigious Automotive News Rising Star Award, recognizing top U.S. automotive executives who are young and poised to become future industry leaders. Known for his industry leadership, forward-thinking and collaborative approach, he continues to champion innovation and collaborate with global stakeholders for the future of mobility.

speakers.name: Bryan Goodman
speakers.bio: Bryan Goodman is Executive Director of Artificial Intelligence at Ford Motor Company, where he leads the enterprise AI strategy, oversees Ford’s AI ethics policy as chair of the AI Technology and Ethics Council, and directs teams delivering scalable, high‑impact AI solutions across the business. He is responsible for Ford’s AI and machine learning platform and for providing generative AI capabilities company‑wide, including multimodal systems for audio, image, video, and 3D. Goodman drives incorporation of AI into products and services spanning engineering and design, marketing and forecasting, customer assistance (web, contact centers, apps, in‑vehicle), and manufacturing.

speakers.name: Jewel Li
speakers.bio: Jewel Li is Chief Operating Officer and a founding team member at Tensor, a Silicon Valley AI company building physical AI and agentic products for individual consumers. Tensor’s flagship Robocar is the world’s first personal Robocar and AI agentic vehicle that is fully autonomous, automotive-grade, and designed for private ownership. Before Tensor, Jewel got her Ph.D. in artificial intelligence and worked on the IBM Watson labs, and later served at the IBM Research’s T.J. Watson Research Center, where she was a member of the Watson Core Algorithms team delivering the core AI that powered IBM Watson’s DeepQA systems.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Puma’s Smart Stores: Agents and Digital Humans Redefine Customer Interaction
sessions.abstract: In this session, we explore how agents and digital humans are transforming customer in store experiences by improving the connection with customers, employees and store operations. From answering questions in multiple languages to providing product education and recommendations, and seamless integrating with key systems, PUMA is redefining the way retailers work and the way customers shop.

speakers.name: Ivan Dashkov
speakers.bio: Ivan Dashkov leads Emerging Marketing Tech at PUMA, driving the brand’s generative AI, synthetic media, and digital product initiatives. He built PUMA AI Creator, the fan-driven jersey co-design platform used by global football clubs, and oversees the company’s AI Digital Humans program. Previously at the National Basketball Association, he helped architect its social strategy and creator ecosystem. He partners with NVIDIA, Google, Meta, and other leading tech companies on next-generation AI applications.

speakers.name: Jia Li
speakers.bio: Jia is the co-founder, president, and chief AI officer of LiveX AI, which builds human-like AI agents. She has been elected as an IEEE Fellow for Leadership in Large Scale AI. She was the founding global head of R&D at Google Cloud AI, overseeing the full stack of AI products. She is the co-founder and chairperson of HealthUnity Corp and she served briefly at Accenture as a part-time chief AI fellow. Jia has served as a mentor and professor-in-residence at StartX, advising founders and companies from Stanford University. She received her Ph.D. in computer science from Stanford.

speakers.name: Kapil Dabi
speakers.bio: Kapil Dabi is the Americas market lead, retail and consumer, at Google, where he drives global retail strategy, industry solutions, and key partnerships. A highly experienced executive, Kapil is a recognized thought leader and frequent speaker at industry events, known for his expertise in leveraging AI, cloud, and data to drive digital transformation and optimize business operations for Fortune 500 retailers. He has a strong track record of delivering significant top-line and bottom-line impact, and previously served as chief digital officer at Signet Jewelers.

speakers.name: Andrew Sun
speakers.bio: Andrew is Senior Director, Retail Business Development at NVIDIA. He defines the strategy and drives go-to-market for NVIDIA’s software through partnership with cloud service providers and global system integrators.  Prior to NVIDIA, Andrew worked at Google, Walmart, HP, and Intel, where he led companies through major evolutionary shifts in the market by leveraging technology and partnerships.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation

sessions.title: Unified Architectures and Evaluation Frameworks for Financial AI Agents
sessions.abstract: This talk presents a DeepResearch-powered long-context framework for financial multi-modal conversational models, two specialized financial benchmarks (BizFinBench and MME-Finance), the any-to-any omni architecture for real-time cross-modal agentic interactions, and a general evaluation engine for standardized AI performance evaluation across key capabilities.

speakers.name: Wenchen Li
speakers.bio: Richard Li works as a product director at Tonghuashun. Richard graduated from Columbia University and is currently based in the United States.

speakers.name: Adam Wang
speakers.bio: Adam Wang has had extensive work experience in various leadership roles in the finance industry. Adam is currently the chief executive officer of Ainvest, a position they have held since March 2022. They served as the managing director of the same company from August 2021 to March 2022.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Leverage Physical AI to Simulate and Orchestrate Robotic Fleets for Retail Fulfillment Centers
sessions.abstract: Dive into the technology behind the new era of physical AI that's redefining Europe's supply chain and facility automation. Otto Group presents the “robotic coordination layer,” which connects robot fleets in real warehouses to a digital twin, enabling the deployment of the latest technology for intelligent automation.  Discover how Otto Groups, NVIDIA, and Reply collaborated to not only launch the latest AI and robotics systems in the field of logistics across Europe, but also create a scalable blueprint for the future of logistics.

speakers.name: Martin Umland
speakers.bio: With his background in engineering, Martin's career evolved from intralogistics engineering into shaping organizations and strategy. Leading European Logistics for the fashion brand bonprix provided invaluable holistic management experience. Now Martin is VP Supply Chain Management and focuses on strategically advancing the logistics activities of the Otto Group.

speakers.name: Stefan Borsutzky
speakers.bio: Stefan Borsutzky is Co-CEO of Otto Group one.O GmbH, the Otto Group’s technology and consulting subsidiary, where he co-leads cross‑group initiatives at the intersection of technology and logistics. Working closely with Otto Group Logistics, he drives AI-enabled operations to further enhance efficiency and customer experience. Previously, he held leadership roles at the Boston Consulting Group and within the Otto Group, including CEO of Otto Group Solution Provider (OSP) GmbH. He holds a doctorate and is based in Hamburg.

sessions.title: Build a High-Performance Research Cluster
sessions.abstract: Designing a modern research cluster is more than racking servers. In this session, we walk through the end-to-end design of a high-performance research cluster using B200 HGX and Spectrum-X, covering NVLink/NVSwitch topologies, intra- and inter-host multi-GPU communication, and how to validate performance with real workloads. We’ll share hard-won lessons from deployment, including observability patterns, common misconfigurations, troubleshooting techniques, and practical tips to keep your cluster both fast and reliable.

speakers.name: Chris Turpin
speakers.bio: Chris Turpin designs and operates high-performance research clusters for HPC workloads. He has led the architecture and deployment of multi-GPU systems based on NVIDIA HGX platforms and Spectrum-X networking, with a focus on observability, performance, and operational simplicity at scale. Chris has lengthy experience in data center network design and build, and works closely with researchers to translate benchmarks into real workload outcomes. He brings hands-on expertise in topology design, automation, and troubleshooting across the full fabric stack.

speakers.name: Jerome Vienne
speakers.bio: Jerome Vienne is an HPC performance engineer with extensive expertise in evaluating and optimizing high performance computing clusters to support demanding computational workloads. Since 2018, he has applied his technical skills within the finance industry to deliver robust, scalable performance solutions. Previously, he served as a research associate at the Texas Advanced Computing Center, working on scientific computing infrastructure and high-end research workloads. With a strong foundation in both academic research and financial-industry operations, Jerome combines analytical rigor with practical experience to ensure computing systems perform reliably and efficiently.

sessions.title: Accelerate Geospatial Workflows for Planetary Insight
sessions.abstract: Learn how the integration of NVIDIA's accelerated computing and Planet Labs' cloud-scale imagery platform is driving a revolutionary shift in geospatial workflows at scale and in real time. This integration of Earth observation innovation pioneered by Planet, NVIDIA’s platform, and AI unlocks the full potential of petabytes of Earth observation data, providing detailed, actionable insights that address some of the world’s most pressing environmental and humanitarian challenges.

speakers.name: May Casterline
speakers.bio: May Casterline is a director of solutions architecture at NVIDIA, focusing on deep learning and AI applications. She holds a Ph.D. and B.S. in imaging science from Rochester Institute of Technology, with a focus on remote sensing. In industry, she has acted as a product owner, technical lead, lead developer, and image scientist on both research initiatives and development projects.

speakers.name: Kiruthika Devaraj
speakers.bio: Kiruthika Devaraj is a vice president of engineering at Planet, where she leads the spacecraft team that designs  and builds Planet’s satellites. She has worked on Planet’s radio communication systems and advanced payloads, including real-time insights using next-gen GPUs and real-time connectivity onboard satellites. Prior to Planet, Kiruthika was a research scientist at Stanford University and the National Radio Astronomy Observatory. She has an M.S. and Ph.D. in electrical engineering from Georgia Tech.

sessions.title: Powering Agentic Commerce with Open-Source AI: Easy to Build, Train, and Deploy
sessions.abstract: PayPal is leveraging NVIDIA’s open-source AI models to accelerate the evolution of agentic commerce—a new era where intelligent digital agents autonomously handle commerce tasks across industries. By integrating NVIDIA’s Nemotron and reasoning AI frameworks, PayPal enables businesses to automate customer engagement, personalize financial recommendations, and optimize payment flows in real time.

speakers.name: Farhad Farahani
speakers.bio:
Farhad leads the AI team for agentic commerce for PayPal. This includes full stack agentic tool calling, memory, LLM context management, and fine-tuning work using supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning.

Farhad also leads consumer personalization for PayPal and Venmo—personalizing apps, ranking, and the recommendations for shopping feed, PayPal ads, Venmo feed, search, next-best action, and smart wallet.

speakers.name: Ali Sahami
speakers.bio: Ali Sahami is Director of Machine Learning and AI at PayPal, where he leads AI/ML and personalization for Agentic Commerce and Ads. A seasoned AI leader with over a decade of experience, he specializes in building innovative, scalable AI/ML solutions that drive measurable impact across enterprise ecosystems.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Think You Know AI? 25 Startups Prove You Wrong
sessions.abstract: AI isn’t just about chatbots and automation—startups are finding unexpected, creative ways to apply it to the world's hardest problems in ways you might not imagine. In this session, we’ll spotlight 25 innovative use cases that reveal the breadth and depth of what’s possible when bold founders with big visions meet cutting-edge technology. Get ready to expand your view of AI’s potential and discover opportunities you didn’t see coming.

speakers.name: Ashutosh Joshi
speakers.bio: Ashutosh Joshi is an entrepreneur, technology leader, and startup mentor. He is an Inception partner manager at NVIDIA, working with AI developer tools startups in the NVIDIA Inception program. He helps founders build and scale their products on NVIDIA platforms. Ashutosh has spent his career building and leading technology teams—from founding startups to leading platforms and data science groups at VMware and Grubhub. In addition to his operating roles, he has been active in the YCombinator and Techstars communities as an alum, mentor, and past investor, supporting founders at every stage, from minimum viable product through growth and scale.

speakers.name: Chris Milroy
speakers.bio: Chris leads teams in NVIDIA's Developer Programs that use NVIDIA tech to accelerate business and serve developers worldwide. His teams' creations include the AI-powered nvidia.com search engine, Deep Learning Institute platform, recommender systems for developer.nvidia.com, and a dozen others. Previously at NVIDIA, he was a solution architect and data scientist for the Public Sector team, creating prototypes ranging from computer vision on embedded systems to high-performance data analytics on clusters. Previously, he was director of AI for Alion Science and Technology and ran AI labs for government applications. He holds degrees in economics and philosophy from the University of Chicago.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling

sessions.title: From Concept to Capability: Scaling AI Surrogates Across JLR
sessions.abstract: As Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) continues its digital transformation journey, AI surrogates are unlocking new frontiers in computer-aided engineering (CAE). This session explores how the Aerodynamics team pioneered AI as an essential engineering “pilot,” proving its value in one of the most complex and high-impact CAE domains. Through robust data workflows and scalable deployment strategies, this approach has enabled faster, cleaner, and more reliable delivery of AI-driven insights. Discover how this foundation is now being expanded across broader CAE disciplines, transforming modeling efficiency, data integrity, and decision-making throughout the organization.

speakers.name: Christopher Johnston
speakers.bio:
Chris leads the development of intelligent, cross-domain digital twins that blend AI with traditional physics simulation and modeling. He replaces fragmented, file‑based processes with scalable, traceable, and automated simulation pipelines, bringing software practices such as versioning and continuous integration and continuous delivery/deployment to integrate and automate engineering, and enabling surrogate modeling for faster, data‑driven decisions.

As a systems and visual thinker, Chris partners across disciplines to create a continuum from computer-assisted design and engineering through to vehicle experience, embedding machine learning to support holistic, evidence-based engineering.

sessions.title: Game Agent Models - Picking & perfecting the right AI models for your gaming applications
sessions.abstract: As developer approaches to in-game agentic AI become more advanced, standard off-the-shelf models often fall short. Game developers frequently seek expertise in developing and customizing the most suitable models for their specific applications, as well as optimizing these models for peak performance. In this session, our experts will demonstrate how we develop and evaluate language models with Agentic AI capabilities for gaming platforms. They will guide you on utilizing NVIDIA tools to assess, train, and fine-tune AI models specifically designed for your gaming applications.

speakers.name: Ameya Sunil Mahabaleshwarkar
speakers.bio: Ameya Sunil Mahabaleshwarkar is a senior deep learning scientist at NVIDIA working on pre-training and post-training of language models for edge devices. He previously received his M.S. from the Language Technologies Institute in Carnegie Mellon University's School of Computer Science.

speakers.name: Vinay Raman
speakers.bio: Vinay Raman is a senior deep learning scientist at NVIDIA. He spearheads projects spanning edge-ready LLMs for agentic and gaming applications, text-based and multi-modal retrieval-augmented generation, and synthetic data generation, along with building generative AI solutions for a variety of enterprise use cases in different domains. He has an academic background in applied math with a Ph.D. from MIT in computational physics.

speakers.name: Jani Joki
speakers.bio: Jani Joki leads NVIDIA’s benchmarking and technical marketing teams for client computing. With more than 20 years of experience, he has helped create and define industry-standard benchmarks used to evaluate computer performance across a broad range of applications.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models

sessions.title: The AI behind Teammates, Ubisoft's Experimental Interactive Characters
sessions.abstract: Ubisoft recently unveiled Teammates, its first playable AI-based R&D project. This experience redefines NPC interaction by allowing players to lead a proactive, intelligent squad via real-time voice commands. In this session, the developers behind Teammates will detail their process for selecting and tuning AI models to maximize player immersion. They will also provide a technical deep dive into their deployment strategy for high-performance, real-time inference.

speakers.name: Maxime Sazadaly
speakers.bio: Maxime is a machine learning engineer specialized in natural language processing, with experience spanning from applied research to scaling ML systems. You can usually find him diving into ArXiv or open-source projects in search of ideas. He is now the technical lead of the machine learning engineering team working on AI Teammates.

speakers.name: Joel Gregoire
speakers.bio: Joel is a veteran developer at Ubisoft with 25 years of experience spanning game engines, online systems, and technical leadership. Specializing in AI for the past two years, he now leads the team developing the infrastructure and SDK powering AI Teammates.

sessions.title: Next-Generation Intelligent Surgical Robots
sessions.abstract: Explore how NVIDIA technologies are transforming the design and scalability of intelligent surgical robots, enabling software-defined, AI-powered systems that improve outcomes across the healthcare ecosystem. Learn how NVIDIA Holoscan and agentic frameworks are shaping the future of smart operating rooms and hospitals.

speakers.name: Mahdi Azizian
speakers.bio: Mahdi Azizian is a senior director of engineering at NVIDIA, focusing on development of the Holoscan Platform, Holoscan for Medical, ISAAC for Healthcare, and MONAI. Mahdi holds a Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from Western University in Canada. Prior to joining NVIDIA in 2021, he spent almost a decade at Intuitive, bringing image guidance and intelligence to surgical robots. Mahdi’s technical interests involve intelligent medical devices, medical imaging, medical robotics, and real-time systems.

sessions.title: AI Transformation in Healthcare: Helping Surgeons Help Patients
sessions.abstract: The next wave of surgical innovation lies in the intelligence behind robotic systems. Advances in accelerated computing—enabling performance gains on the order of thousands—are transforming surgical data into a powerful feedback loop for continuous improvement. This session will share how a massive leap in computational power—up to 10,000 times greater than before—is enabling continuous learning in the operating room. Real-time analytics, AI-generated technique insights, and digital twin simulations are reshaping how surgeons learn, collaborate, and refine their practice. Combined with remote connectivity and scalable compute, these technologies mark a shift toward smarter, more adaptive operating rooms and improved patient outcomes.

speakers.name: Tony Jarc
speakers.bio: Anthony Jarc, vice president of digital and AI/ML at Intuitive, focuses on digital and AI innovation in surgical robotics. With over a decade of experience since joining the company in 2011, Anthony has been instrumental in driving the development of cutting-edge AI-enabled solutions to elevate surgical performance and patient care. His expertise has significantly contributed to the field of surgical data science, where he has collaborated with top surgeons to extract and apply valuable insights from the da Vinci surgical platform's data. Anthony's academic background includes a B.S. in mechanical engineering from Cornell University and a Ph.D. in biomedical engineering from Northwestern University.

speakers.name: Douglas Stoddard
speakers.bio: Dr. Doug Stoddard is the director of surgery and robotics at the CHRISTUS Health system and serves as medical director for Bariatric and Robotic Surgery. He brings extensive experience spanning both elective and acute care surgery, with a current clinical focus on bariatrics, foregut, and general surgery, as well as acute care surgery. A veteran of the U.S. military, Doug completed 12 years of active duty service with deployments to Kuwait, Iraq, and Afghanistan, where he practiced as a trauma and emergency general surgeon. He has performed over 1,000 robotic procedures using the da Vinci surgical system and is a strong advocate for the integration of advanced robotics and digital technologies to improve surgical outcomes and patient care.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Real-time Voice Transformation with GPU Accelerated AI
sessions.abstract: Modulate’s VoiceWear enables real-time voice transformation, demanding high-fidelity audio and near-zero latency. This session details how Modulate and NVIDIA migrated critical VoiceWear workloads from CPU to GPU, significantly reducing latency within their streaming pipeline. We’ll explore the process of identifying bottlenecks, the impact of precision and framework selection on performance, and how targeted CUDA and inference-stack optimizations delivered measurable gains. Attendees will gain practical strategies for accelerating sequential, real-time ML workloads for production.

speakers.name: James Maki
speakers.bio: James Maki is a senior solutions architect on the NVIDIA Media & Entertainment SA team focused on the intersection of agentic and generative AI with video games. James has a master's degree in data analytics from Penn State.

speakers.name: Carter Huffman
speakers.bio: Carter Huffman is the CTO and co-founder of Modulate, the frontier AI company turning real-time voice conversations into actionable intelligence that prevents fraud, protects communities, and improves customer experiences. A physicist and machine learning expert trained at MIT and with past experience at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, Carter leads the development of Modulate’s purpose-built Voice Intelligence Engine, which analyzes emotion, behavior, and intent in voice—not just words—to reveal insights traditional AI misses.

sessions.title: Using Agentic AI to Transform Science: From Molecules to Materials
sessions.abstract: AI is accelerating breakthroughs across scientific domains, transforming complex data into actionable insights for discovery and design. This session explores how agentic AI systems are being engineered to drive innovation—from decoding biological mechanisms for therapeutic science to modeling quantum matter for next-generation materials. Two visionary thought leaders will share how their research teams are applying robust, domain-specific AI frameworks to reshape the future of their fields.

speakers.name: Eun-Ah Kim
speakers.bio: Eun-Ah Kim is the Hans Bethe Professor of Physics at Cornell University. A pioneer at the intersection of quantum many-body physics and artificial intelligence, she has recently expanded her research to guide the quantum simulation of topological states and accelerate material discovery using AI. Her contributions have been recognized with prestigious honors, including a Radcliffe Fellowship, two Simons Fellowships for Theoretical Physics, and election as a Fellow of the American Physical Society. She received her Ph.D. from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and completed postdoctoral research at Stanford University before joining the Cornell faculty in 2008.

speakers.name: Marinka Zitnik
speakers.bio: Marinka Zitnik is an associate professor of biomedical informatics at Harvard Medical School, associate faculty at the Kempner Institute for the Study of Natural and Artificial Intelligence at Harvard University, associate member at the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard, and affiliated faculty at the Harvard Data Science Initiative. She is a computer scientist investigating foundations of AI that contribute to the scientific understanding of medicine and therapeutic design, enabling AI systems that can learn, reason, and innovate on their own.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Biology - Generative AI

sessions.title: Build Fault-Tolerant Distributed AI Training at Scale
sessions.abstract: Learn how to build resilient distributed training pipelines using NVIDIA's Resiliency Extension (NVRx) on clouds like AWS so your long-running jobs automatically restart from fast, asynchronous checkpoints. We'll dive deep into practical integration patterns with PyTorch-based frameworks and show how you can simplify operations while confidently scaling to multi-node, multi-GPU clusters.

speakers.name: Aravind Neelakantan
speakers.bio: Aravind Neelakantan is a specialist solutions architect at AWS, focusing on generative AI training and inference solutions. His expertise spans artificial intelligence and high-performance computing, with research interests in performance analysis and optimization of AI workloads on heterogeneous architectures. Prior to AWS, Aravind developed reference architectures for large-scale AI clusters and specialized in AI inference performance optimization using hardware accelerators. He holds a Ph.D. from the University of Florida, specializing in heterogeneous HPC architectures.

speakers.name: Shreya Gupta
speakers.bio: Shreya is an AI architect building resiliency products to make large-scale AI training fault-tolerant, including open-source tools like NVIDIA Resiliency Extension. Previously, she fine-tuned LLM software for enterprises. Shreya holds a master’s degree in symbolic systems from Stanford University, where she conducted research at the Stanford Vision and Learning Lab on robot learning and vision neuroscience. Her experience spans model training, natural language processing, and product development. She's co-authored papers in EACL and CoRL and received the Google Women Techmaker Scholar award. She loves mentoring, technical writing, and supporting innovation through hackathons and roundtables.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models

sessions.title: The AI-XR Co-Scientist Lab: How Stanford - Princeton's LabOS is Accelerating Advanced Science With XR
sessions.abstract: What if we could dramatically shorten the path from discovery to lifesaving therapy? Discover how Stanford Medicine’s LabOS, an AI extended reality (XR) operating system, makes this a reality. Learn how LabOS drives CRISPR GPT, a groundbreaking AI agent to design complex experiments and deliver step-by-step instructions through augmented reality glasses. LabOS unites computational reasoning with physical experimentation, built on NVIDIA Cosmos-powered XR/AI system. By connecting multi-model AI agents, smart glasses, and robots, LabOS allows AI to see what scientists see, understand experimental context, and assist in real-time execution. From immunotherapy to stem cell engineering, LabOS turns any lab into an intelligent environment where human and machine discovery evolve together—not just accelerating research, but delivering personalized, accessible therapies at unprecedented scale.

speakers.name: Le Cong
speakers.bio: Le Cong is a professor in the Pathology and Genetics Departments at Stanford University and a Donald and Delia Baxter Scholar. His lab develops AI co-scientists to accelerate scientific discovery, pioneering platforms such as CRISPR-GPT, RNAGenesis, and LabOS—the first AI-XR system that enables human–AI collaboration in real-world experiments through agentic AI, smart glasses, and robotic perception, paving the way toward a self-driving laboratory to accelerate science and medicine.

speakers.name: Mengdi Wang
speakers.bio: Mengdi Wang is co-director of Princeton AI for Accelerated Invention, and a professor of the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering and the Center for Statistics and Machine Learning at Princeton University. She is also affiliated with the Department of Computer Science, Omenn-Darling Bioengineering Institute, and Princeton Language+Intelligence. She was a visiting research scientist at Google DeepMind, IAS, and Simons Institute on Theoretical Computer Science. Her research focuses on machine learning, reinforcement learning, generative AI, large language models, and AI for science.

sessions.title: Robotic Automation in Neurosurgery Navigation With Isaac and Holoscan
sessions.abstract: Join XRlabs and Olympus for a session exploring how technologies such as NVIDIA Isaac for Healthcare are shaping innovation in surgical environments. The discussion will include an overview of Olympus' 3D 4K Exoscope technology, as well as XRlabs' simulation and model training expertise, highlighting how AI-powered workflows can help bridge the gap between digital and physical environments. You'll see how simulation, digital twins, and robotics are being used to enable safer procedures, faster training, and smarter decision-making in the operating room. From modeling complex anatomy to validating robotic tasks in virtual environments before potential deployment in patient care, this session will explore emerging approaches that may influence the future of medical device development and precision surgery.

speakers.name: Ali Haddad
speakers.bio: Ali Haddad is a practicing neurosurgeon and the founder of XRlabs, a company redefining surgical simulation and digital-twin technologies. With deep expertise in robotics and real-time 3D workflows, he has built XRlabs into a go-to partner for medical device companies advancing next-generation imaging, training, and robotic systems. Ali’s mission is to modernize how clinicians and engineers design, test, and master complex procedures—using simulation-first pipelines that improve safety, accelerate development, and transform the operating room.

speakers.name: Ernst Jonscher
speakers.bio: Ernst Jonscher is the senior business development manager for video microscopy at Olympus Europa, driving growth and innovation for the ORBEYE exoscope across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. He leads strategic initiatives and partnerships with robotics and AI startups to advance surgical technology. With a Ph.D. in Biology from Universität Hamburg and research published in top journals, Ernst combines scientific depth with commercial expertise. His unique perspective on disruptive technologies and strategic leadership makes him a compelling voice in shaping the future of healthcare.

sessions.title: Build the Future of AI Infrastructure With NVIDIA DOCA
sessions.abstract: AI-driven workloads are redefining data center design, challenging infrastructure developers to deliver higher performance, stronger security, and greater networking, storage, and compute efficiency. This session covers how the latest capabilities and announcements in the NVIDIA DOCA software platform power the next generation of AI clouds and factories. It introduces the latest DOCA advancements that power massive-scale AI factories and cloud infrastructure. Gain insight into the DOCA software stack, APIs, microservices, and developer tools that enable accelerated, programmable infrastructure. Learn how NVIDIA DOCA’s unified capabilities deliver breakthrough networking performance while maintaining backward compatibility, and explore how to apply new capabilities and best practices to transform AI networking, storage, and security architectures.

speakers.name: Yuval Degani
speakers.bio: Yuval leads a software engineering organization developing the BlueField ecosystem: DOCA SDK, DPU-based services in the areas of SDN, cybersecurity, virtualization and emulation, storage, 5G, HPC/AI/ML, and DPU kernel drivers and performance engineering. Previously, Yuval led big data platform engineering at LinkedIn, scaling the world's largest Spark and Hadoop clusters. Before that, Yuval was a developer, architect, and later a team leader at Mellanox Technologies, working in low-level kernel development for cutting-edge high-performance network devices and big data and ML framework acceleration. Yuval holds a B.Sc. in computer science from the Technion Institute of Technology, Israel.

speakers.name: Ariel Kit
speakers.bio: Ariel Kit manages the strategy and delivery of the NVIDIA DOCA software framework, bringing more than a decade of experience in product development in the fields of cybersecurity and embedded system-on-chip, backed up by over 12 years in R&D managerial roles. He holds a B.Sc. in communication systems engineering and an M.B.A.

sessions.title: How AI is Changing the Scientific Method in Biology and Beyond
sessions.abstract: Understand where AI fits into the scientific method: from hypothesis generation to experiment design, in-silico experimentation, and  programmable labs to data interpretation.

speakers.name: John Marioni
speakers.bio: John Marioni is a senior vice president and head of computation at Genentech Research and Early Development (gRED) in South San Francisco. Previously, he was the head of research at the European Bioinformatics Institute and held an appointment at the Wellcome Sanger Institute and the Cancer Research UK Cambridge Institute in Cambridge University. He is a computational biologist known for his research on statistical and computational methods for the analysis of genomics data, in particular single-cell biology and evolutionary genomics. He continues to co-chair the Human Cell Atlas Analysis Working Group.

speakers.name: Rory Kelleher
speakers.bio: Rory Kelleher is the senior director and global head of business development at NVIDIA. He’s responsible for defining the go-to-market strategy, key partnerships, ecosystem engagement, and solution development to bring accelerated computing and AI into life sciences, drug discovery, and genomics markets. He collaborates with industry leaders to establish partnerships that aim to improve productivity through AI.

speakers.name: Andrew Beam
speakers.bio: Andy Beam is an expert in machine learning for biology and medicine. As chief technology officer at Lila, he’s focused on architecting an environment that enables the development of scientific superintelligence. Before joining Lila Sciences, he was an associate professor in the Department of Epidemiology at the Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health. He earned his Ph.D. in bioinformatics, focusing on Bayesian neural networks.

speakers.name: Marinka Zitnik
speakers.bio: Marinka Zitnik is an associate professor of biomedical informatics at Harvard Medical School, associate faculty at the Kempner Institute for the Study of Natural and Artificial Intelligence at Harvard University, associate member at the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard, and affiliated faculty at the Harvard Data Science Initiative. She is a computer scientist investigating foundations of AI that contribute to the scientific understanding of medicine and therapeutic design, enabling AI systems that can learn, reason, and innovate on their own.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Biology - Generative AI

sessions.title: How NASCAR Uses GPU-Accelerated Simulation for Safer, More Exciting Racing
sessions.abstract: Go under the hood of NASCAR’s rigorous engineering workflow, where computational fluid dynamics (CFD) now accounts for 80% of all aerodynamic development. We'll explain how simulation drives critical decisions regarding driver safety, thermal management, and the racing package itself—specifically through "liftoff safety" studies and multi-car traffic modeling. This is made possible by NASCAR’s transition from CPU-based CFD to the CUDA-X accelerated Siemens Simcenter STAR-CCM+ package, in partnership with TotalSim and the Ohio Supercomputer Center. Leveraging the latest NVIDIA GB200 GPUs, this workflow delivers a 10x improvement in turnaround time, enabling larger, higher-fidelity multi-physics models.

speakers.name: Neil Ashton
speakers.bio: Neil is a distinguished engineer/product architect at NVIDIA, with a specific focus on industrial and computational engineering. Prior to NVIDIA, he was the WW tech lead for computer-aided engineering at AWS. He was previously a senior researcher within the Department of Engineering Science at the University of Oxford, and also worked in Formula 1 with the Lotus F1 team, Formula 1 Management, and the FIA.

speakers.name: Eric Jacuzzi
speakers.bio: Eric Jacuzzi serves as vice president of vehicle performance at NASCAR’s Research & Development Center in Concord, North Carolina. An 11-year NASCAR veteran, he previously worked as a computational aerodynamicist at Corvid Technologies and Richard Childress Racing. His career highlights include leading the aerodynamics for the 2023 Garage 56 Le Mans entry and receiving the Dino Toso Racecar Aerodynamicist of the Year award. A 2024 Charlotte Business Journal 40 Under 40 honoree, he recently began advising the USA Luge team. Eric holds a Ph.D. from North Carolina State University, an M.S.E. from the University of Michigan, and a B.Sc. from Kettering University.

sessions.title: Scaling AI Agents Globally Across Brands, Use Cases, and Restaurants
sessions.abstract: Scaling and deploying AI into restaurant operations requires unique technology and partners. Learn how Yum! Brands, the world’s largest restaurant company, is using NVIDIA software to scale intelligence into their restaurant technology, expanding use cases, applications, and partners.

speakers.name: Cameron Davies
speakers.bio:
Cameron Davies is the chief data officer for Yum! Brands, the world’s largest restaurant company, hosting such iconic brands as KFC, Taco Bell, Pizza Hut, and The Habit Burger. He leads the corporate data and analytics strategy and champions the development and adoption of advanced data capabilities. Previously, Cameron spent seven years as a senior VP at NBCUniversal and over 18 years at the Walt Disney Co., where he led the Corporate Center of Excellence for AI and Machine Learning. 

Cameron has an M.B.A. and master of accountancy from the University of West Florida and a B.S. in business administration/accounting from Pensacola Christian College. He lives in Charlotte, North Carolina.

speakers.name: Andrew Sun
speakers.bio: Andrew is Senior Director, Retail Business Development at NVIDIA. He defines the strategy and drives go-to-market for NVIDIA’s software through partnership with cloud service providers and global system integrators.  Prior to NVIDIA, Andrew worked at Google, Walmart, HP, and Intel, where he led companies through major evolutionary shifts in the market by leveraging technology and partnerships.

sessions.title: Agentic AI in Financial Services: Model Use Cases
sessions.abstract: Hear from a panel of leading financial services innovators on how they leverage agentic AI for production use cases. Panelists will also discuss what it takes to get to production with agentic AI in the highly regulated financial services industry, and how they effectively measure return on investment (ROI) for new use cases.

speakers.name: Bobby Grubert
speakers.bio:
Bobby Grubert is the head of AI and digital innovation for RBC Capital Markets, where he leads the firm’s end-to-end AI and digital innovation strategy to deliver transformative client solutions, drive scale, and accelerate adoption across the firm to enable the capital markets business to compete in a digital world. He is responsible for embedding AI-driven technologies and capabilities as core drivers of client value and revenue growth with tangible commercial and measurable outcomes.

Prior to this position, Bobby was instrumental in driving the electronification of RBC’s Global Markets franchise, 
fostering innovation and prioritizing AI for commercial growth. He also played a key role in implementing Aiden, an award-winning algorithmic trading platform, in partnership with RBC Borealis. Bobby began his career at RBC as an energy trader in 1996 and has since held multiple senior roles across the organization.

Bobby is a member of the RBC Capital Markets Operating Committee and founding member of the RBC U.S. Capital Markets Diversity Leadership Council. He lectures M.B.A.s and executives at MIT Sloan School of Management and serves as an executive board member and chairman emeritus at Ronald McDonald House New York.

speakers.name: Jennifer St. John-Foster
speakers.bio:
Jennifer is the sales leader for North America Financial Services at NVIDIA. Her team works with the top banking and asset management firms to drive growth and innovation by leveraging NVIDIA’s full stack AI platform to transform their business. 
 
In her seven years at NVIDIA, Jennifer has worked with top financial firms to harness the power of AI and GPU-accelerated compute in the cloud and on-premises data centers. She aligns NVIDIA’s groundbreaking AI software and innovations to empower financial services customers to drive new capabilities in areas such as trading, asset management, and customer engagement by unlocking the potential of Gen AI through AI factories and GPU-accelerated HPC workloads.

speakers.name: Chintan Mehta
speakers.bio:
Chintan Mehta serves as Wells Fargo’s chief information officer and head of digital technology and innovation, and is a member of the Technology Executive Leadership Team. He and his organization play a critical role in advancing the company’s transformation and elevating the customer experience and capabilities across 
customer segments.

Chintan leads the development of safe, secure, resilient, and highly responsive platforms and capabilities—including the Wells Fargo Mobile App, Fargo, Vantage, and critical foundations for AI and blockchain. He also oversees Wells Fargo’s academia and industry research partnerships, including collaboration with Stanford University’s Human‑Centered AI group on next‑generation AI, and with MIT on quantum computing. 

Previously, Chintan was group vice president and chief technology officer at Walgreens, with earlier leadership roles at American Express and Barclays Bank PLC (London). He studied computer science and applied mathematics at the University of Hertfordshire (U.K.), and business management at Stanford University. Chintan is based in the San Francisco Bay Area.

speakers.name: Sarthak Pattanaik
speakers.bio:
Sarthak Pattanaik is an engineering leader at BNY, where he serves as chief data and artificial intelligence officer, Corporate Trust & Loans, Depository Receipts Engineering, Clearance, and Treasury Payments.

He is responsible for the strategy, build-out, adoption, and scaling of AI capabilities in the bank. In addition, he leads the engineering teams that are responsible for the Artificial Intelligence Hub, Corporate Trust & Loans, Depository Receipts Engineering, Clearance, and Treasury Payments. 

Sarthak is a trusted and innovative leader with over 20 years of experience with leading teams that are responsible for the build-out of scalable mission-critical platforms and innovative solutions that help clients. Prior to joining BNY, he spent 13 years at Nomura Securities in New York, where he served as the global head of market risk and counterparty risk technology, responsible for value-at-risk, stress testing, economic capital, and regulatory capital models. Before this, he was a quantitative analyst responsible for pricing 
structured credit and securitized products.

Sarthak holds three patents related to the use of blockchain technology. He is certified as a chartered financial analyst and financial risk manager. He earned a B.Tech. from the Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur and an M.B.A. from the Indian Institute of Management Lucknow.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Supercharge LLM Inference With Dynamo
sessions.abstract: NVIDIA Dynamo is a distributed inference serving framework built to run large language models efficiently, reliably, and at massive scale. It enables AI providers to reduce cost-per-token, meet strict service-level objectives, and scale deployments across large GPU clusters. In this interactive session, you’ll learn how Dynamo delivers high-performance distributed inference across frameworks such as SGLang, TensorRT-LLM, and vLLM. We’ll cover how to compose KV-aware routing, disaggregated serving, and KV offloading to achieve the most optimal throughput and latency given available GPU resources. Additionally, we will detail how to configure Dynamo deployment for Kubernetes (K8s) with topology-aware hierarchical gang scheduling, enable auto-scaling to adapt to real-time changes in traffic, and ensure reliability with fine-grained LLM fault tolerance.

speakers.name: Harry Kim
speakers.bio: Harry Kim is a principal product manager at NVIDIA, enabling performant and scalable AI/ML inference with Triton. He has experience working on recommendation systems at Meta, AI infrastructure at Intel AI, and ads ranking and recommendation at Google. He holds a Ph.D. in statistics from the University of California, Berkeley.

speakers.name: Kyle Kranen
speakers.bio: Kyle Kranen is a team lead and manager in the Deep Learning Algorithms Organization at NVIDIA. His charter centers on blending engineering and research in order to accelerate LLM inference at the data center scale.

speakers.name: David Lu
speakers.bio: Xianglong  is an NVIDIA senior solutions architect with a B.Eng. from Huazhong University of Science and Technology and an M.S. in engineering from Arizona State University. Xianglong  specializes in NVIDIA inference software solutions, including Dynamo, Triton, and TensorRT-LLM, with deep experience in designing and optimizing large-scale AI inference systems.

sessions.title: AI Digital Twin of the Climate System
sessions.abstract: Scientists use computer simulations to understand how different parts of the Earth’s system interact, or to explore how they respond to external forcing. These simulations have been carried out using numerical models (global climate models, or GCMs). Recently, modern machine-learning-based emulators have been used to reproduce the same dynamics using data. We will present the first generation of AI digital twin of the climate system, using data from state-of-the-art GCMs. These AI emulators can simulate the climate system 100x faster than typical GCMs with one GPU. We will discuss how we build, evaluate, and leverage these emulators to make discoveries about the climate system. For example, it offers strategies to improve simulations of extreme weather such as hurricanes, and forcing them with past ocean states reveals how and why hurricane counts have varied over the past 1,000 years.

speakers.name: Elizabeth Barnes
speakers.bio: Elizabeth (Libby) Barnes is the incoming Dalton Family Chair in Environmental Data Science & Sustainability, and a professor of computing and data sciences and of Earth and environment at Boston University. Her research focuses on understanding Earth system variability, predictability, and change across time and space, with an emphasis on developing and implementing AI tools in a way that mimics scientific human reasoning to improve intrinsic interpretability. Her overarching goal is to responsibly harness AI to anticipate human-Earth system futures in support of a thriving society in the decades ahead.

speakers.name: Laure Zanna
speakers.bio: Zanna is the Joseph B. Keller and Herbert B. Keller Professor of Applied Mathematics at NYU, Courant Institute School of Mathematics, Computing and Data Science. She combines theory, numerical simulations, statistics, and machine learning (ML) to tackle a wide range of problems in climate and fluid dynamics, including ocean turbulence, multiscale modeling, ocean heat and carbon uptake, and sea level. Since 2020, she is leading M²LInES, an international collaboration sponsored by Schmidt Sciences to transform climate modeling with AI, and since 2026, the Simons Center for Computational Geophysical Flows at NYU. She advised 40 graduate students and postdocs, many of whom are now holding faculty positions, research positions in labs, or in industry.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling

sessions.title: Activate Always-On AI Defense Across Local and Enterprise Agents
sessions.abstract:
AI is rapidly transforming the cybersecurity landscape, with security operations centers (SOCs) facing growing AI-powered threats that overwhelm traditional tools and workflows. AI-driven defense is therefore no longer optional—it is a necessity. Organizations are not only re-architecting their SOCs but also their infrastructure, with the transition to AI factories. 


This session explores how organizations can move from experimentation to secure deployment of AI agents using an agentic security platform that streamlines analyst workflows and orchestrates adaptive defenses across dynamic environments. Learn how CrowdStrike’s agentic cybersecurity platform integrates with NVIDIA’s AI stack to secure AI across the enterprise, building a resilient foundation for AI-driven cyber defense at enterprise scale.

speakers.name: Mike Petronaci
speakers.bio: Mike Petronaci is responsible for CrowdStrike’s technology vision and proactive security strategy, leading innovation across AI, threat intelligence, and IT. Prior to CrowdStrike, he led product and engineering teams at high-growth software-as-a-service, cloud, and cybersecurity companies including Akamai and HubSpot. Most recently, he served as the vice president of products for SentinelOne, where he led teams responsible for delivering innovative customer-led products.

speakers.name: Bartley Richardson
speakers.bio: Bartley Richardson is chief technology officer of NVIDIA AI software and director of engineering for cybersecurity AI development and product engagement, including accelerated computing and generative AI. Previously, Bartley was a technical lead on multiple DARPA research projects. He holds a Ph.D. in computer science and engineering, with a focus on AI.

sessions.topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI

sessions.title: Accelerated HPC+AI Workflow Enables Live-Steering of Vera C. Rubin Observatory and X-ray Free Electron Laser
sessions.abstract:
The landscape of experimental science is being reshaped by the demand for real-time insight at the petabyte scale. With the Vera C. Rubin Observatory’s Legacy Survey of Space and Time (LSST) Camera and the Linac Coherent Light Source II (LCLS II) at SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory, data are arriving at unprecedented speed and volume.
 
NVIDIA-accelerated computing, coupled with GPU-accelerated Python libraries—CuPy, cuPyNumeric—are making possible what was previously out of reach. Now, by running these same scientific analysis pipelines on NVIDIA DGX Grace Hopper and Blackwell, Spark, and RTX PRO, researchers are gaining powerful new advantages for both performance and collaboration. Many months of data analysis compresses to a few hours through cleverly solved equations using distributed computation on GPUs.

speakers.name: Quynh Nguyen
speakers.bio: Quynh Nguyen is a high performance computing and AI alliance manager at NVIDIA, partnering with national labs, academia, and industry to advance large-scale computing and AI for science. Previously a physicist focusing on X-ray and fusion energy sciences at SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory, she brings deep expertise in HPC workflows, AI-accelerated research, and community engagement to help organizations adopt next-generation computing technologies.

speakers.name: Nate Lust
speakers.bio: Nate is an astronomical software developer at Princeton University, as a part of the Legacy Survey of Space and Time to enable the Vera C. Rubin Observatory. His work focuses on Nate received his PhD in Astrophysics from University of Central Florida.

sessions.title: Developing AI Physics Models for Spacecraft Reentry
sessions.abstract: Learn how Blue Origin has been fine-tuning and using the NVIDIA AI physics models for their hypersonic spacecraft re-entry challenges. You’ll hear about how they use simulation within their research and design processes; how they see the role of AI within their overall workflows and work done to fine-tune and optimize AI Physics models for their applications.

speakers.name: Neil Ashton
speakers.bio: Neil is a distinguished engineer/product architect at NVIDIA, with a specific focus on industrial and computational engineering. Prior to NVIDIA, he was the WW tech lead for computer-aided engineering at AWS. He was previously a senior researcher within the Department of Engineering Science at the University of Oxford, and also worked in Formula 1 with the Lotus F1 team, Formula 1 Management, and the FIA.

speakers.name: David Halaas
speakers.bio: David Halaas has nearly 20 years of experience in aerodynamic modeling, simulation, and AI/ML integration. Since 2015, David has led critical initiatives at Blue Origin, including creating custom simulation architectures that achieved 10x speedups in Monte Carlo analysis and developing hardware-in-the-loop test beds for launch operations. He also built geospatial modeling tools to support lunar landing analysis. Currently, David focuses on accelerating aerospace development through ML-based surrogate models using DoMINO and GPU-acceleration techniques. He holds B.S. and M.S. degrees in aeronautical and astronautical engineering from the University of Washington.

sessions.title: The Era of GPU Data Processing: From SQL to Search and Back Again
sessions.abstract: This session delivers a technical state of the union on GPU-accelerated data processing across SQL/DataFrames, vector search, ML, and decision optimization. Learn how GPU-native engines enable interactive analytics on massive lakehouse-scale datasets, real-time semantic and vector search over billions of embeddings, and makes the hardest ML and decision science workloads tractable, cost-efficient, and energy-efficient. The talk highlights the implications for high-impact scientific and enterprise computing, then looks ahead to what’s in flight for 2026 and beyond, outlining concrete architectural patterns and practical guidance for building the next generation of GPU-accelerated data platforms and using them in your day-to-day work.

speakers.name: Todd Mostak
speakers.bio: Todd Mostak is a software engineering leader at NVIDIA in the RAPIDS team, where he focuses on GPU-accelerated data processing. Previously, Todd was founder and CEO of HEAVY.AI (originally MapD), where he helped pioneer the use of GPUs for data analytics.

speakers.name: Joshua Patterson
speakers.bio: Josh Patterson is vice president of solutions architecture—data processing at NVIDIA, where he helps customers and partners build GPU-accelerated data platforms, query engines, and applications. Previously, he was CEO of Voltron Data, focused on high-performance query engines. He has a long history in large-scale machine learning, distributed systems, and open-source data infrastructure.

sessions.title: Accelerating Quantum Chemistry on GPUs—Latest Advances
sessions.abstract: Gaussian-basis quantum chemistry on NVIDIA GPUs is primed to change the world. Join this session to learn about new technologies and offerings from NVIDIA that help accelerate this paradigm shift.

speakers.name: Robert Parrish
speakers.bio: Rob Parrish is a quantum chemist whose favorite topic is finding accelerations for Gaussian-basis electronic stucture on NVIDIA GPUs. Rob has a Ph.D. in quantum chemistry from Georgia Tech, where he was a senior author of the CPU-based PSI4 project. Subsequently, he was a postdoc and associate staff scientist as Stanford / SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory, and spent six years at the startup QC Ware, working on quantum chemistry software on both quantum processing units (for the future) and GPUs (for right now).

speakers.name: Anders Blom
speakers.bio: Anders Blom is the Technical Product Manager for Synopsys’ atomistic modeling platform QuantumATK. He has worked with atomistic modeling for over 20 years. After obtaining his PhD in quantum physics from Lund University in Sweden in 2003, he was one of the founding members of QuantumWise, which was acquired by Synopsys in 2017. Based in California, Anders’ role is in the interface between customer and product, to advise on how to use atomic-scale simulations as effectively as possible, and to ensure the tool delivers true value to the users.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries

sessions.title: Don’t Leave Tensors on the Table: Programming and Optimizing Tensor Cores
sessions.abstract: Explore practical techniques for maximizing Tensor Core performance in your CUDA kernels. We’ll discuss CUTLASS-based coding patterns to unlock efficient pipeline utilization and streamlined data management. Together, we’ll use Nsight Compute to analyze, optimize, and fine-tune your workflows for optimal Tensor Core performance.

speakers.name: Magnus Strengert
speakers.bio: Magnus Strengert is the engineering manager of the Nsight Compute team at NVIDIA. Magnus obtained his Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Stuttgart, Germany in 2010. He joined NVIDIA in 2008.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Profilers / Debuggers / Code Analysis

sessions.title: Accelerate AI Inference Using DOCA for Storage
sessions.abstract: Real-time AI inference at scale requires high-performance GPUs combined with efficient data movement, preprocessing, and data access from edge to cloud. Developers often struggle to eliminate bottlenecks that slow down inference pipelines and waste valuable compute cycles. The NVIDIA DOCA software platform enables high-performance AI inference processing by accelerating data movement and preprocessing tasks using RDMA, NIXL, and GPUNetIO. DOCA-powered storage solutions like the NVIDIA AI Data Platform design and S3-compatible storage enable seamless, high-performance data access for AI workloads. This session demonstrates how to design inference services that deliver responsive and reliable results for latency-sensitive applications in both AI factory and edge environments. It also shows how to apply DOCA-accelerated networking and storage for high-performance data access.

speakers.name: Oren Duer
speakers.bio: Oren Duer is a software architect specialized in storage solutions using DPUs, focusing on enhancing performance and security by leveraging DPUs to offload, accelerate, and isolate data center and storage systems workloads.

speakers.name: Omri Kahalon
speakers.bio: Omri Kahalon is a senior manager in NVIDIA Network Software Architecture. Omri has extensive experience in developing and integrating accelerated networking technologies used in hyperscale cloud. His focus is on software-hardware co-design and large-scale distributed systems. Omri holds a bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering from the Technion - Israel Institute of Technology and a master’s degree in Electrical Engineering from Tel Aviv University.

speakers.name: Ariel Kit
speakers.bio: Ariel Kit manages the strategy and delivery of the NVIDIA DOCA software framework, bringing more than a decade of experience in product development in the fields of cybersecurity and embedded system-on-chip, backed up by over 12 years in R&D managerial roles. He holds a B.Sc. in communication systems engineering and an M.B.A.

sessions.title: Strategic Capital and the Evolution of the AI Ecosystem
sessions.abstract: This session examines strategic capital deployment in AI and infrastructure. Panelists will explore trends in investment, collaboration models, and the evolving business landscape that supports scalable AI growth.

speakers.name: Will Brilliant
speakers.bio:
Will Brilliant serves as global head of digital infrastructure and is a member of GIP’s Investment Committee and Operating Committee. Based in New York, Will oversees GIP’s digital infrastructure investments, including data centers and telecommunications infrastructure. Before joining GIP in 2007, he was an investment banker at Lehman Brothers. 

Will currently serves on the board of directors of CyrusOne and Hess Infrastructure Partners. He also chairs the Board of Directors of Make-a-Wish Metro New York and is a member of the UCLA Social Sciences Dean’s Advisory Board. Mr. Brilliant holds a B.A. in economics from UCLA and an M.B.A. from the Wharton School of the University of Pennsylvania.

speakers.name: Vishal Bhagwati
speakers.bio: Vishal leads growth strategy through acquisitions, strategic investments, and partnerships globally. Prior to NVIDIA, Vishal was at Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), responsible for acquisitions, strategic investments, divestitures, and integration. Previously, Vishal led Oracle's global mergers and acquisitions. He was also co-founder and CEO of Emadison and vice president at Credit Suisse and Wasserstein Perella. Vishal holds an M.B.A. from the Wharton School at the University of Pennsylvania, M.S. and Eng. degrees in electrical engineering and computer science from MIT with a minor in finance from the MIT Sloan School of Management; and a B.S. from the University of California, Berkeley.

speakers.name: Anjney Midha
speakers.bio:
Anjney Midha is a general partner at Andreessen Horowitz, where he invests in AI, infrastructure, and open-source technology. He serves on the boards of Black Forest Labs, Luma AI, Mistral AI, and Sesame AI.

A former founder and CEO, Anjney joined the firm in 2023. Immediately prior, Anjney was vice president of platform ecosystem at Discord, where he oversaw all developer products for the company’s 200 million users. In this role, Anjney set up Discord’s first dedicated developer platform organization, launched its partnership with Midjourney, and helped it scale to one of the world’s fastest-growing generative AI businesses.

speakers.name: Mira Murati
speakers.bio: Mira Murati is the founder and CEO of Thinking Machines Lab, an AI company focused on building accessible, customizable systems that collaborate closely with human experts. Prior to starting Thinking Machines, she served as CTO at OpenAI, where she oversaw research, product, and safety teams and led development of high-profile systems including ChatGPT, DALL·E, and GPT4. Earlier in her career, she held senior engineering and product roles at Tesla, contributing to Model X and early Autopilot efforts, and at Leap Motion, and she is widely recognized as one of the most influential technical leaders in modern AI.

speakers.name: Brian Venturo
speakers.bio: Brian Venturo is the Chief Strategy Officer and co-founder at CoreWeave. Brian works closely with CoreWeave’s clients, engineers, and partners to ensure teams have access to the hardware and technology they need to run efficiently on CoreWeave Cloud.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: The Creative OS: AI Built With Human Imagination at the Center
sessions.abstract: Explore how Canva engineered AI systems with foundational models that understand creative context; preserve artistic control; and amplify, rather than replace, imagination. We'll share technical approaches for building AI platforms and products where AI becomes an invisible enabler, letting designers focus on their work, from editable designs to video, from web apps to interactive experiences.

speakers.name: Stef Corazza
speakers.bio:
Stef leads Canva's AI Research division. His background spans creative efforts in film, animation, and 3D interactive art, complemented by deep expertise in computer graphics, biomechanics, and immersive media.

Previously, Stef was head of studio at Roblox, where he created the industry's first AI assistant for game development. Before Roblox, he served as vice president at Adobe, leading the augmented reality team following Adobe's acquisition of Mixamo, a company he founded.

Stef holds a Ph.D. from the University of Padova and completed a postdoc at Stanford in computer vision and biomechanics. He's also an angel investor and startup advisor who creates music and large-scale artwork.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: From Research to Production: How Alpamayo Accelerates Autonomous Vehicle Development
sessions.abstract: Discover the latest advancements in end-to-end autonomous driving as the industry accelerates toward scalable, Level 4 AI-driven systems. This session explores how NVIDIA’s Alpamayo—particularly reasoning vision-language-action models (VLA)—enable vehicles to interpret, reason about, and navigate complex real-world scenarios with greater humanlike understanding. We’ll highlight how this approach is strengthened by a continuous, self-improving loop powered by NVIDIA Cosmos and agentic AI, and safeguarded by NVIDIA Halos, NVIDIA’s comprehensive full-stack safety system for physical AI. Finally, we’ll discuss how NVIDIA is building an open ecosystem to empower partners in developing next-generation, robotaxi-ready autonomous vehicles.

speakers.name: Marco Pavone
speakers.bio: Marco Pavone is mainly interested in developing methodologies for the analysis, design, and control of autonomous systems, with an emphasis on self-driving cars, autonomous aerospace vehicles, and future mobility systems. Marco is also an associate professor of aeronautics and astronautics at Stanford University, where he's the director of the Center for Automotive Research.

speakers.name: Patrick Liu
speakers.bio: Patrick Langechuan Liu leads machine learning development for NVIDIA’s end-to-end autonomous driving system, Alpamayo, focusing on production deployment of vision-language-action models for real-world driving. Previously, Patrick was Head of AI at Xpeng Motors, where he led algorithm development for autonomous driving systems. Patrick began his career as a physicist, conducting research on large-scale simulations of high-energy particle interactions with materials.

sessions.title: Powering the AI Era in Genomics With Advanced Next-Generation Sequencing Technologies
sessions.abstract: Learn how low-cost and high throughput next-generation sequencing from Ultima, combined with NVIDIA’s accelerated computing technologies, is shaping the future of genomics. Discover how these innovations enable research at an unprecedented scale across academic data generation, drug discovery, and diagnostics. We will discuss how NVIDIA’s accelerated computing technologies power Ultima’s roadmap and latest technology advancements, opening new applications areas like virtual cell modeling, population scale genomics, and cancer detection.

speakers.name: Gilad Almogy
speakers.bio:
Gilad Almogy founded Ultima Genomics in 2016 with a mission to continuously reduce the cost of sequencing in order to enable the era of data-driven healthcare, transform biology, and improve human health. Before founding Ultima, Gilad had applied deep technology, engineering, and leadership expertise to push technological boundaries and drive transformation across multiple industries. 

Gilad earned a Ph.D. in applied physics from the California Institute of Technology and a B.Sc. in mathematics and physics from the Hebrew University in Jerusalem’s Talpiot program. He is the inventor of more than 80 U.S. patents.

sessions.title: Real-Time Science and Engineering With AI Physics and Kit-CAE
sessions.abstract: Physical engineering is moving from static reports and siloed workflows to real‑time, interactive insight. This session shows how AI physics meets simulation‑driven engineering by putting NVIDIA’s GPU‑accelerated libraries and NVIDIA Omniverse open libraries first. We’ll cover accessing common computer-aided engineering (CAE) formats via OpenUSD technologies in kit-cae; high‑fidelity field visualization with RTX and IndeX; Warp‑accelerated CAE/visualization algorithms; physics NeMo surrogates made from solver-generated ground truth; and application streaming for instant, hardware‑agnostic review and collaboration. We’ll highlight current industry use cases and advancements in the area of digital twins for science and engineering. You’ll leave today with an adoption path and a clear view of how AI physics and Omniverse libraries can accelerate your organization.

speakers.name: Jeff Larkin
speakers.bio: Jeff is a principal high performance computing application architect in NVIDIA's HPC Software team, where he leads a team responsible for HPC programming models and standards. He's passionate about the advancement and adoption of parallel programming models for high performance computing. Jeff is also the chair of the OpenACC technical committee and has worked in both the OpenACC and OpenMP standards bodies.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Building the Architecture of Advertising Intelligence: How GPU-Accelerated AI Agents are Reshaping Digital Advertising
sessions.abstract: Digital advertising now rivals high-frequency trading in both speed and complexity: tens of millions of auctions occur every second, each demanding a decision in under a millisecond. AI is transforming the industry as agents autonomously plan, negotiate, and execute media transactions in real time.
