This session details how PubMatic re-engineered its global infrastructure using NVIDIA-accelerated computing to power some of the world’s first agentic advertising transactions, offering a blueprint for how GPU-driven intelligence is reshaping not just programmatic advertising, but real-time digital markets of every kind.

speakers.name: Rajeev Goel
speakers.bio: Rajeev Goel co-founded PubMatic in 2006 based on the belief that publishers are underserved from a technology and services perspective, and set out to build a software company focused on publishers. Under his leadership, the company has grown and matured into a publicly traded enterprise, with end-to-end products serving publishers, buyers, data providers, and commerce media networks, with more than 1,000 employees and 18 offices around the world. PubMatic’s trajectory has been fueled by a commitment to strong company culture, rapid innovation, and owned and operated technology infrastructure.

speakers.name: Vasu Cherlopalle
speakers.bio: Vasu Cherlopalle is an engineering leader with 20-plus years of experience building large-scale, real-time data platforms. They specialize in leading high-performing data engineering teams, translating complex business needs into scalable technical solutions, and delivering reliable systems that power data-driven decision-making.

sessions.title: Speed Meets Safety: Accelerating Automotive Crash Simulations With AI Physics
sessions.abstract: Traditional automotive crashworthiness assessment relies on computationally expensive finite element simulations, creating a bottleneck in automotive design. This work with General Motors introduces machine learning surrogate models, utilizing MeshGraphNet and Transolver architectures within the NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo framework. Evaluated on a Body-in-White dataset of 150 simulations, the study compares time-conditional and stability-enhanced autoregressive modeling strategies. The models successfully predict spatiotemporal deformation trends using undeformed geometry and variable thickness inputs. Although full finite element accuracy remains out of reach, the approach achieves massive computational speedups. These results demonstrate the feasibility of ML-driven dynamics for rapid, early-stage crashworthiness optimization.

speakers.name: Sudeep Chavare
speakers.bio: to be added

speakers.name: Srinivas Tadepalli
speakers.bio: Srinivas Tadepalli is Director of WW Go-To-Market Strategy for Industrial Engineering at NVIDIA, where he is responsible for working with our customers and ecosystem partners across Automotive, Aerospace, and Industrial verticals. Prior to NVIDIA, he has held leadership roles at AWS and Dassault Systemes within Product Management and Go-To-Market functions. He holds a Ph.D. in Bioengineering and Computational Mechanics and an Executive MBA.

sessions.title: Shaping the Next Era of Autonomous Driving Through Safety-Centered AI
sessions.abstract:
Achieving Level 4 (L4) autonomy at global scale requires both technical mastery and a verifiable safety case to earn public trust. This panel brings together engineering, mobility, and regulatory leaders from across the autonomous vehicle ecosystem to examine the breakthroughs enabling trustworthy autonomous vehicle deployment. 


We’ll explore the core challenges: integrating large foundation models, using reasoning vision-language-action models to handle edge cases, generating billions of validation miles using physically accurate simulation, and using unified safety frameworks like NVIDIA Halos to achieve certification and regulatory compliance. You’ll learn how teams are translating abstract safety mandates into verifiable software and hardware constraints to deliver truly trustworthy autonomy.

speakers.name: Ali Kani
speakers.bio: Ali Kani is the vice president of automotive at NVIDIA, overseeing NVIDIA DRIVE’s end-to-end platform for autonomous vehicle development and production. The NVIDIA automotive group is engaged with hundreds of car and truck makers, robotaxi companies, tier 1 suppliers, high-definition mapping companies, sensor manufacturers, and startup companies—all building on NVIDIA DRIVE and its high-performance AI capabilities.

speakers.name: Raquel Urtasun
speakers.bio: Raquel Urtasun is the founder and CEO of Waabi, an AI company building the next generation of self-driving technology. Raquel is also a full professor in the Department of Computer Science at the University of Toronto and a co-founder of the Vector Institute for AI. Raquel earned her Ph.D. from the computer science department at Ecole Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL) in 2006, and did her postdoc at MIT and the University of California, Berkeley.

speakers.name: Ashok Elluswamy
speakers.bio: Ashok Elluswamy is vice president of AI at Tesla. Ashok leads AI development for the Tesla self-driving software and Optimus, the humanoid robot. He was the first engineer hired onto Tesla’s AI team in 2014 and has been a key driver of the company’s autonomy strategy, including the shift to vision-only, end-to-end neural network–based driving systems.

speakers.name: Noah Zych
speakers.bio: Noah is passionate about reimagining the way the world moves for the better, and has strived throughout his career to conscientiously develop and deploy state-of-the-art technology in pursuit of improved mobility. He joined Uber in early 2015, and in his current role as Global General Manager for Autonomous Mobility & Delivery he holds responsibility for the successful commercialization of autonomous vehicles on the Uber Platform. In this position, Noah leads efforts to establish and implement partnerships with trustworthy technology developers and fleet operators around the world who share Uber’s vision of safe, sustainable, and affordable autonomous transportation services.

speakers.name: Laura Major
speakers.bio:
Laura was appointed President and CEO of Motional in June 2025 following a successful tenure as the company's Chief Technology Officer since 2020.

Laura began her career as a Cognitive Engineer for Draper Laboratory, where she combined her psychology and engineering skills to design decision-making support devices for U.S. astronauts and soldiers. After 12 years with the company, she served as Division Leader for the Information and Cognition Division. During this time Laura was recognized by the Society of Women Engineers as an emerging leader. 

Laura also spent time at Aria Insights, Inc. (formerly known as CyPhy Works), a US-based drone manufacturer that specialized in developing highly advanced drones. She served as VP of engineering, and then Chief Technology Officer, working on the development of autonomous aerial vehicles.

Laura is a graduate of Georgia Institute of Technology and Massachusetts Institute of Technology. She is an accomplished writer and co-author of the book What To Expect When You're Expecting Robots: The Future of Human-Robot Collaboration.

speakers.name: Miguel Acosta
speakers.bio: Miguel Acosta was appointed Chief of the DMV’s Autonomous Vehicles Branch in August 2018. He has 26 years of experience in the public sector, with over a decade in executive leadership. His previous executive assignments at the DMV include Branch Chief in the department’s Special Interest License Plate Program, International Registration Plan and Motor Carrier Permit programs; Assistant Deputy Director of Field Operations; and Chief of the Justice and Government Liaison Branch. Miguel earned a Bachelor of Arts in Political Science from the University of California, Davis, as well as a Master of Arts in Public Policy and Public Administration from the California State University, Sacramento.

sessions.title: Accelerate Cloud Platforms for the Next Era of AI
sessions.abstract: AI advancement depends on cloud infrastructure to deliver massive compute scale, high-performance, high resilience, and robust security. Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is addressing these challenges through an innovative architectural approach featuring the Oracle Acceleron fabric integrated with NVIDIA BlueField technology. This session explores how OCI’s accelerated networking, intelligent isolation mechanisms, and high-speed data movement capabilities collectively form a robust and secure platform for giga-scale. Speakers will share details of the Oracle Acceleron architecture and show how it enables the world’s most demanding AI workloads. Gain a deeper understanding of the infrastructure patterns shaping modern AI platforms and how organizations can leverage them as they accelerate their AI journeys, securely and efficiently.

speakers.name: Pradeep Vincent
speakers.bio: Pradeep Vincent is the chief technical architect and senior vice president at Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). A founding member of OCI, Pradeep has been with Oracle for over 10 years, leading a team of architects and software engineers to build OCI. With more than 24 years of experience in building high-scale cloud services at Oracle and Amazon Web Services, Pradeep has an extensive understanding of cloud infrastructure, compute, storage, and networking.

sessions.title: Open Source AI Shaping the Next Era of Intelligent Digital Workers
sessions.abstract: Open source AI is redefining how we build and scale intelligence. In this session, we explore how AI factories unlock a new generation of digital workers who learn continuously, collaborate with humans, and drive breakthroughs across every industry.

speakers.name: Kari Briski
speakers.bio: Kari Briski is the vice president of generative AI software for enterprise at NVIDIA, where she oversees strategy, roadmaps, and requirements for AI software and services. Kari has over 25 years of experience in software development and 20 years as a leader in technology product management, working at IBM before joining NVIDIA in 2016. She holds a bachelor’s degree in computer engineering from the University of Pittsburgh.

speakers.name: Adel El Hallak
speakers.bio: Adel El Hallak is vice president of Software Product Management for NVIDIA AI Enterprise, a suite of APIs, libraries, and runtimes that simplify the development, deployment, and scaling of agentic AI. He focuses on blueprints for operationalizing production-grade AI agentic systems through NVIDIA's partner ecosystem. Adel holds a Bachelor's in Computer Science from McGill University and an MBA from Warwick Business School.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: An AI-Driven Autonomous Lab of the Future for Chemistry and Materials Science
sessions.abstract: Explore how agentic AI and automated laboratories are transforming chemistry and materials discovery by seamlessly integrating AI-driven atomistic simulations with experimental workflows. Learn from industry leaders how they're accelerating the path from computational prediction to lab validation, enabling faster discovery cycles and breakthrough material innovations that were previously out of reach.

speakers.name: Justin Smith
speakers.bio:
Justin obtained a B.S. in math and Ph.D. in chemistry from the University of Florida, then was a postdoc at Los Alamos National Laboratory, where he was promoted to staff scientist. He was a ML scientist in drug design at Relay Therapeutics.

Justin developed the ANI machine learning interatomic potential (MLIP) and pioneered the development of general-use machine learning interatomic potentials for small molecule drug design. He later contributed to the HIP-NN and AIMNet MLIPs, and active learning methodologies for training MLIPs for materials science and reactive chemistry applications. Now he helps the ML chemistry and materials science community fully utilize NVIDIA hardware.

speakers.name: Rafael Gómez-Bombarelli
speakers.bio: Rafael received his B.S., M.S., and Ph.D. in chemistry from the University of Salamanca in Spain, followed by postdoctoral work at Heriot-Watt University in Scotland. As a postdoc at Harvard University, he worked on high-throughput virtual screening for organic light-emitting diode (OLED) and battery electrolytes. He entered industry in 2016 as a senior researcher at Japanese technology company Kyulux, applying Harvard-licensed technology to build commercial OLED products. He joined the MIT Department of Materials Science and Engineering faculty in 2018. Rafael co-founded Calculario, a materials discovery company that uses quantum chemistry and machine learning to target advanced materials.

speakers.name: Kristin Persson
speakers.bio: Kristin A. Persson is the Daniel M. Tellep Distinguished Professor at UC Berkeley and Senior Faculty Scientist at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. She is the Director and founder of the Materials Project (materialsproject.org), a world-leading resource for materials data, design, and the training of machine learning algorithms. Her recognitions include the Cyril Stanley Smith Award, membership in the Royal Swedish Academy of Science and the US National Academy of Engineering, and her designation as an Office of Science Distinguished Scientist Fellow in 2024. She holds several patents in the clean energy space and has co-authored more than 300 peer-reviewed publications.

speakers.name: Joseph Krause
speakers.bio: Joseph F. Krause is the co-founder and CEO of Radical AI, which combines AI and robotics to accelerate the design, development and discovery of novel materials. A US Army National Guard veteran and materials scientist, Joseph worked neuromorphic semiconductor research at the Army DEVCOM Research Lab and led deep tech investments as an investor at AlleyCorp before founding Radical AI.

speakers.name: James Gin
speakers.bio: James Gin-Pollock is the CTO and co-founder of Orbital, which develops hardware technologies from the atoms up using AI and is best known for the Orb series of machine learning interatomic potentials. He is a serial technical founder who previously co-founded Datasine, which Shutterstock acquired. James holds degrees in physics and machine learning from Oxford University and University College London.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science

sessions.title: The State of Open Source AI
sessions.abstract: Open source AI is evolving at record speed, reshaping how we train, refine, and deploy models across industries. Join leading builders as we explore the current state of open models and tools, the breakthroughs driving their momentum, and the new capabilities needed as AI becomes more specialized, multi-modal, and agent-driven.

speakers.name: Jonathan Cohen
speakers.bio: Jonathan Cohen is vice president of applied research at NVIDIA, where he leads development of platforms for large language models and speech AI. He also oversees development of genomics and drug discovery software. Previously, he spent three years at Apple as a director of engineering. Earlier in his career, Jonathan specialized in computer graphics as a software developer in the visual effects industry, winning a 2007 Scientific and Technical Academy Award.

speakers.name: Vartika Singh
speakers.bio: Vartika leads the strategic technical partner engagements at NVIDIA.

speakers.name: Ion Stoica
speakers.bio: Ion Stoica is a Professor in the EECS Department and holds the Xu Bao Chancellor Chair at the University of California, Berkeley. He is the Director of the Sky Computing Lab and the Executive Chairman of Databricks and Anyscale. His current research focuses on AI systems and cloud computing, and his work includes numerous open-source projects such as vLLM, SGLang, Chatbot Arena, SkyPilot, Ray, and Apache Spark. He is a Member of the National Academy of Engineering, an Honorary Member of the Romanian Academy, and an ACM Fellow. He has also co-founded several companies, including LMArena (2025), Anyscale (2019), Databricks (2013), and Conviva (2006).

speakers.name: Jeff Boudier
speakers.bio: Jeff Boudier builds products at Hugging Face, the No. 1 platform for AI builders. Previously, Jeff was a co-founder of Stupeflix, acquired by GoPro, where he served as director of product management, product marketing, business development, and corporate development.

speakers.name: Ranjay Krishna
speakers.bio: Ranjay Krishna is an Assistant Professor at the Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering. He co-directs the RAIVN lab at UW and directs the PRIOR team at the Allen Institute. His research lies at the intersection of computer vision, natural language processing, robotics, and human computer interaction. This research has received best paper honorable mentions at CVPR'25 and CSCW'23, outstanding paper at NeurIPS'21 and ACL'21, and dozens of orals at CVPR, ACL, CSCW, NeurIPS, UIST, and ECCV, and has been reported by Science, Forbes, the Wall Street Journal, and PBS NOVA. He is also recognized as one of MIT Technology Review's 35 under 35 Asia Pacific '25. His research has been supported by Google, Apple, Ai2, Amazon, Cisco, Toyota Motor Inc, Toyota Research Institute, NSF, ONR, and Yahoo. He holds a bachelor's degree in Electrical & Computer Engineering and in Computer Science from Cornell University, a master's degree in Computer Science from Stanford University and a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Stanford University.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Pre-Trained / Foundation Models

sessions.title: Accelerated Building Blocks for Next-Generation AI+HPC Workloads
sessions.abstract: Embracing next-generation technology like AI-infused scientific workflows involves blending familiar building blocks with innovation, timeliness, and purpose. Whether it's models pre-trained on petabyte-scale data for common forecasting tasks, or real-time digital twins, developers need building blocks to enable their vision. In this talk, we walk through developer software used to enable AI and HPC innovations, including recent enhancements that maximize the use of NVIDIA hardware and improve the developer experience.

speakers.name: Heidi Poxon
speakers.bio: Heidi Poxon is director of product for HPC software at NVIDIA. She comes most recently from AWS, where she combined 25 years of supercomputing experience with cloud capabilities to expand the technology choices available to HPC workloads. Prior to AWS, Heidi led the technical and strategic direction at HPE for the HPE Cray Programming Environment, and led engineering teams at Cray, SGI, and at Unlimited Scale (a short-lived startup). Heidi’s passion is to make things run fast with software that maximizes hardware potential, so scientists and engineers can deliver exciting breakthroughs.

sessions.title: Build Gigascale AI Factories With Next-Generation Rack-Scale Systems
sessions.abstract: The next generation of AI models is driving unprecedented compute consumption, exposing the limitations of traditional system architectures. Enterprises are now evolving data center designs to support new rack-scale architectures for modern inference, as well as large-scale training optimized for these rapidly escalating demands. Join this session to learn how NVIDIA is systemizing the new NVIDIA Vera Rubin architecture to help customers rapidly deploy gigascale AI factories.

speakers.name: Charlie Boyle
speakers.bio: As vice president and general manager of DGX Systems at NVIDIA, Charlie Boyle leads the company's effort to drive worldwide adoption of its AI supercomputing systems and bring the world of AI to enterprise customers. Charlie brings a wealth of knowledge in the IT and service provider industry, having run marketing, engineering, and data center operations for some of the world's largest telcos and service providers.

speakers.name: Brian Knapp
speakers.bio: Brian leads AI and Data for Global Technology at Walmart. His team’s mission is to enable decentralized AI development, across multiple tech and non-tech personas, to power exceptional customer and associate experiences. They provide builders with a suite of fast, safe, and cost-effective AI services and manage the enterprise data powering the models. Before joining Walmart, Brian spent 21 years at Amazon in senior leadership roles spanning software engineering and data science, operations, product management, and retail P&L management. He has led large technical and non-technical organizations, automated complex business processes at scale, and owned end-to-end financial performance.  Brian holds a BS in Chemistry from Purdue University and grew up in Joplin, Missouri.

sessions.title: Superior Tokenomics for AI Factories: Building Cost-Efficient Inference at Scale
sessions.abstract:
The next wave of enterprise innovation hinges on successfully deploying large language models with predictable cost and performance. This session demystifies the token economy and highlights why the critical, yet often overlooked, network and security infrastructure layer, together with the accelerated Intelligent AI Proxy, has become essential for Gen AI success. Learn how this infrastructure layer enables two fundamental shifts:

•	Intelligent Traffic Management to Optimize Tokens, the Currency of AI Factories: How you can increase output token throughput by more than 50% and drive significantly higher return on investment from their AI factories. 
•	Disaggregated Inference, a Critical Architectural Shift for Token Efficiency: How you can significantly improve GPU utilization and enable a far more scalable enterprise AI factories, delivering up to 3X increase in inference performance.

speakers.name: Kunal Anand
speakers.bio:
Kunal leads the F5 product organization as chief product officer. Responsible for product vision, strategy, and execution, he ensures development of breakthrough solutions that solve critical challenges and create exceptional experiences for customers. In his previous role as chief technology and AI officer, Kunal charted the company’s technology and AI strategy and vision.

Prior to F5, Kunal held the dual role of CTO and chief information security officer at Imperva. Kunal has over 15 years of experience in AI and machine learning, ranging from model training, employing AI-driven algorithms to enhance products, and designing and implementing AI architectures.

speakers.name: Kevin Deierling
speakers.bio: Combining both technical and business expertise, Kevin variously served as the chief officer of technology, architecture, and marketing at five startups; all achieved positive business outcomes. He's contributed to multiple technology standards and has over 25 patents in various technology areas. Kevin holds a B.A. in solid state physics from the University of California, Berkeley.

sessions.title: From Cluster to Factory: Scale AI Infrastructure Operations With Software-Defined Intelligence
sessions.abstract: Be among the first to hear how software-defined intelligence is aiding enterprises to scale AI operations. Hear directly from an AI innovator as they reveal key learnings from deploying NVIDIA DGX, sharing firsthand insights into architecting and deploying high-performance AI infrastructure and orchestrating complex inference and training workflows. In this session, we’ll also discuss the critical role of software for NVIDIA Rubin and how to simplify complex liquid-cooled deployments at scale, boost site reliability engineering (SRE) productivity, and enable enhanced AI-powered anomaly detection and remediation across the entire AI factory.

speakers.name: Jon Klinginsmith
speakers.bio: Jon Klinginsmith is Executive Director of Cloud Engineering and High Performance Computing at Eli Lilly and Company. In his current role at Lilly, Jon oversees strategy and operations for both cloud computing and high performance computing, ensuring Lilly’s cloud and HPC capabilities remain world-class to support the company’s mission to unite caring with discovery to create medicines that make life better for people globally. Jon received his Ph.D. and M.S. in Computer Science from Indiana University. He obtained his B.A. in both Computer Science and Mathematics from DePauw University.

speakers.name: Bijal Shah
speakers.bio: Bijal Shah is the Director of Product Management for Data Center Enterprise Products at NVIDIA, where she spearheads the DGX x86 business. With a strategic focus on delivering hardware and software products to scale AI infrastructure, she ensures that NVIDIA's DGX offerings continue to define the standard for modern enterprise computing. She holds an M.S. in Electrical Engineering from USC and an MBA from UC Berkeley’s Haas School of Business.

sessions.topic: Data Center / Cloud - Software-Defined Data Center

sessions.title: Developing AI 2035: The Next Decade of Intelligence
sessions.abstract: As AI evolves into the engine of global innovation, its future will define how we work, create, and solve humanity’s greatest challenges. This luminary panel brings together AI leaders to chart the decade ahead—where breakthroughs in large-scale compute, foundation models, and intelligent systems converge to expand human potential. Building on a decade of NVIDIA DGX innovation, panelists will explore how next-generation AI balances power and sustainability, fosters discovery, and advances responsible progress for all.

speakers.name: Charlie Boyle
speakers.bio: As vice president and general manager of DGX Systems at NVIDIA, Charlie Boyle leads the company's effort to drive worldwide adoption of its AI supercomputing systems and bring the world of AI to enterprise customers. Charlie brings a wealth of knowledge in the IT and service provider industry, having run marketing, engineering, and data center operations for some of the world's largest telcos and service providers.

speakers.name: Beth Meinert
speakers.bio:
Beth is senior vice president and general manager of MITRE Public Sector, overseeing strategy and partnerships across the organization’s five federally funded R&D centers supporting civilian agencies, including the Federal Aviation Administration, Health and Human Services, U.S. Treasury, Department of Homeland Security, and National Institute of Standards and Technology.   

Since joining MITRE in 1988 after four years in the U.S. Army, Beth has held leadership roles across MITRE's defense and civilian work programs and serves as the officer champion for MITRE Veterans. She holds a B.S. in electrical engineering from Gannon University and executive credentials from Harvard and Wharton.

speakers.name: Nako Sung
speakers.bio: Nako is a distinguished executive and AI technology leader with a record of developing and commercializing pioneering AI and gaming technologies. He currently leads Naver’s hyperscale AI initiatives as executive vice president and head of hyperscale AI at Naver Cloud, overseeing the technology, research, and deployment of HyperCLOVA X across core services and major B2B solutions. He previously drove Naver CLOVA’s AI platform, sovereign AI strategy, and award-winning AI services, and earlier pioneered multi-agent reinforcement learning and advanced game engine development at NCSOFT. His contributions have garnered multiple national awards and leadership roles in engineering and informatics.

speakers.name: Ashiq Khan
speakers.bio: Ashiq Khan is in the Telecom and Cloud industry for over 20 years. He is the Head of Unified Cloud & Platform Division in SoftBank, responsible for a true HCI deployment of IT, Telecom, Enterprise, MEC and AI/ML infrastructure combined together. He started his career in NTT Docomo in 2004, where he led the R&D of NFV, 5G mobile core, network slicing, and the consequent ETSI NFV and 3GPP standardization. He worked in the open source community as a founding member of OPNFV. He then led the commercialization of the world's first fully virtualized E2E mobile network in Rakuten Mobile. He received his PhD in Computer Sciences from the University of Tsukuba in 2015. In his recent work, he led the build and deployment of 10K+ NVIDIA GPU-based AI infrastructure by SoftBank, which is the largest in Japan.

speakers.name: Anders Ynnerman
speakers.bio:
Anders Ynnerman is a recognised leader in scientific visualisation and high-performance computing, and a co-founder of Sferical. He is also Professor of Scientific Visualisation at Linköping University in Sweden, where he leads research focused on turning complex data into intuitive visual insight.

Over the past two decades, Anders has worked at the intersection of advanced graphics, large-scale data processing, and scientific discovery. His work has helped organisations across research, healthcare, industry and government better understand complex datasets through immersive and interactive visualisation techniques. He is widely known for pioneering approaches that combine GPU acceleration, high-performance computing and advanced rendering to make large and multidimensional data accessible to decision-makers.

Through Sferical, Anders is helping bring these capabilities to a broader range of organizations, enabling real-time exploration and analysis of complex scientific and industrial data using modern GPU-accelerated infrastructure.

sessions.title: Foundation Models Revolutionize Molecular Dynamics Simulations for Chemistry and Materials Science
sessions.abstract: Molecular dynamics simulation, using the explicit equations of quantum mechanics (e.g., density functional theory) is the workhorse of computational materials science and chemistry. Until recently, more than a third of academic supercomputer time was spent on this task. We can now speed up this task by a factor of a million or more using machine learning, without substantial loss of accuracy. This amount of speedup leads to a qualitative change in what is possible to simulate, and therefore to understand, in a wide range of application fields, all the way from catalysis and battery materials to glasses and alloys, and even drug discovery. While some, by now classic, rules of thumb continue to deliver (e.g., more data yield better accuracy), the problem may be special in some ways. We find that encoding the known laws of physics, such as symmetry and locality, help generalization.

speakers.name: Gabor Csanyi
speakers.bio: Gabor Csanyi is a professor of molecular modeling at the University of Cambridge. He pioneered the use of machine learning for molecular dynamics and, together with his group, the creator of the MACE force field architecture. He is a Fellow of the Royal Society of London for Improving Natural Knowledge.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Computational Chemistry / Materials Science

sessions.title: Integrating AI and Quantum Computing to Accelerate the Future of Supercomputing
sessions.abstract: The path to fault-tolerant quantum computing is an AI and supercomputing challenge. This talk outlines a vision to accelerate scientific discovery for everyone, everywhere, through the complete convergence of AI with quantum hardware and systems. We will present the blueprint for a collaborative, open quantum ecosystem that transcends boundaries. By leveraging open standards, CUDA-Q, and NVQLink, we are creating a unified, hybrid infrastructure where academia, labs, startups, and industry can collaborate to drive the future of supercomputing

speakers.name: Sam Stanwyck
speakers.bio: Sam Stanwyck is group product manager for quantum computing at NVIDIA, where he is responsible for strategy, roadmaps, and requirements for the company's quantum computing products. Prior to NVIDIA he worked as an engineering manager at Rigetti Computing and a senior quantum engineer at Keysight Technologies. Sam holds a Ph.D. in applied physics from Stanford University.

sessions.title: Provide the Best Care for Patients Using Foundation Models and Agents
sessions.abstract:
The next round of innovation in healthcare is being defined by the fusion of foundation models and intelligent agents, creating systems that learn, reason, and act alongside clinicians to deliver truly personalized medicine. In this session, we explore how AI is transforming the very fabric of healthcare delivery by embedding reasoning systems directly into the flow of care.
 
Agentic AI systems, built on these models, interpret, anticipate, and act—surfacing insights at the right moment, summarizing encounters, and coordinating care across disciplines. The result: care that is faster, safer, and more human.

This talk will show how Epic is using the NVIDIA AI platform in making this future real—combining accelerated computing and specialized medical AI models and agents to enable intelligent, closed-loop healthcare systems.

speakers.name: Raghav Mani
speakers.bio: Raghav Mani is the director of digital health, focused on building agentic AI products and platforms for providers, payers, and pharma. Prior to NVIDIA, Raghav worked at Epic, where he led different product and engineering teams including their deep learning team and their patient engagement platform, called MyChart. He holds a bachelor’s degree from Indian Institute of Technology in Madras and a master's degree from Texas A&M University.

speakers.name: Phil Lindemann
speakers.bio: Will be provided later

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: How Open Models, Agents, and Physical AI Are Fueling a Flywheel of Healthcare Innovation
sessions.abstract: Healthcare is entering a once-in-a-generation leap as AI, accelerated computing, and robotics converge to turn data into decisions and accelerate breakthrough discovery. At GTC 2026, NVIDIA will showcase how open foundation models, agentic AI, and physical AI are creating entirely new categories of applications across care delivery, drug discovery, and laboratory operations. AI for science is closing the loop between digital dry labs and autonomous wet labs, catalyzing biology’s transformer moment. Healthcare robotics that combine digital and physical agents are closing care gaps around the world at historic speed. Together with industry leaders, NVIDIA is building the AI-powered platforms that are enabling this next generation of medicine.

speakers.name: Kimberly Powell
speakers.bio: Kimberly is responsible for the company’s worldwide healthcare business, including hardware and software platforms for accelerated computing, AI, and visualization that power the ecosystem of medical imaging, life sciences, drug discovery, and healthcare analytics. Kimberly received a B.S. in electrical engineering from Northeastern University in Massachusetts.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Biology - Generative AI

sessions.title: Achieving Gigascale AI: Insights From Infrastructure Visionaries
sessions.abstract: For AI developers and infrastructure pioneers, achieving competitive advantage now demands more than raw compute—it requires unprecedented efficiency, scalability, and automation. This session brings together infrastructure visionaries advancing gigascale AI across leading organizations. They will share real-world architectures, optimization strategies, and operational best practices that enable faster training, smarter orchestration, and greater system autonomy at every layer of the AI stack.

speakers.name: Chris Kawalek
speakers.bio: Chris has over 20 years of experience in product management and product marketing for enterprise technologies. Prior to joining NVIDIA, Chris held senior product roles at Oracle, Sun Microsystems, and ForgeRock, and was a founder of Caststream. Chris works on product marketing for the NVIDIA DGX platform.

speakers.name: Chris Lesniewski
speakers.bio: Chris Lesniewski is the associate vice president of enterprise infrastructure services at Eli Lilly and Co. In this transformative role, he oversees the company’s enterprise network, data centers, high performance computing and cloud innovations. His journey at Lilly began in 1996 as an information analyst in manufacturing IT after earning his B.S. in computer information systems from Indiana University. Chris’ expertise extends across various leadership roles within tech infrastructure, global services IT, human resources, and Lean Six Sigma. His accomplishments include implementing an industry-leading NVIDIA DGX B300 supercomputer, delivering integrated automation of IT operations, and driving Lilly’s enterprise cloud strategy.

speakers.name: Kenji Motohashi
speakers.bio: With 15 years of experience across public clouds (AWS, Google Cloud) and private platforms (VMware, OpenStack), Kenji blends deep technical expertise with a strong track record as a cloud evangelist. His full‑stack background—from infrastructure to enterprise application frameworks—gives him a comprehensive view of digital transformation. As Head of NTT DATA’s Global AI Office, he led the company’s worldwide AI strategy and drove adoption of advanced AI capabilities. Now he leads new AI business development as Chief Strategy Officer of NTT DATA AIVista. Kenji also focuses on harnessing cloud and GPU‑powered innovation to accelerate enterprise AI at a global scale.

speakers.name: Leonie Neuhäuser
speakers.bio:
Leonie Neuhäuser is the Research Team Lead for the Foundation Model Task Adaptation Team at DeepL. Her team is responsible for DeepL’s post-training stack for large multi-modal models, focusing on developing algorithms and systems that align pre-trained models with specific tasks and performance goals through techniques like reinforcement learning. 

Previously, she led the Analytics & AI team at Amprion, one of Germany’s four transmission system operators, developing AI capabilities for electricity grids. Leonie holds a PhD in Computer Science from RWTH Aachen University, where she worked on models for group effects in networks and the evaluation of fairness within algorithmic decision-making systems.

sessions.title: AI Research Breakthroughs from NVIDIA Research (Hosted by Karoly of Two Minute Papers)
sessions.abstract: Don't miss this exciting live panel discussion, hosted by Károly Zsolnai-Fehér (better known as the creator of Two Minute Papers), featuring top researchers from NVIDIA Research. They'll dive into the latest breakthroughs in AI, spotlight the most promising emerging technical trends, and candidly explore the biggest open challenges facing the field today. Join us for an interactive, high-energy conversation packed with cutting-edge insights!

speakers.name: Sanja Fidler
speakers.bio: Sanja Fidler is an associate professor at the Department of Computer Science, University of Toronto, Ontario, which she joined in 2014. Sanja is also the vice president of AI research at NVIDIA. Previously, she was a research assistant professor at TTI-Chicago, a philanthropically endowed academic institute located on the campus of the University of Chicago, Illinois.

speakers.name: Yejin Choi
speakers.bio: Yejin Choi is a distinguished scientist of Language and Cognition Research at NVIDIA. Her current research focuses on large language models, large reasoning models, and alternative architectures. She is a MacArthur Fellow (class of 2022), named among Time100 Most Influential People in AI in 2023, and a co-recipient of 2 Test-of-Time awards (ACL 2021 and CVPR 2021) and 8 Best and Outstanding Paper Awards at ACL, EMNLP, NAACL, ICML, NeurIPS, and AAAI. She was a main stage speaker at TED 2023, and a keynote speaker for conferences across several disciplines including MLSys, CVPR, VLDB, ACL, ICLR, WebConf, and AAAI. She is currently serving as a General Chair for the inaugural Conference on Language Modeling (CoLM).

speakers.name: Karoly Zsolnai-Fehér
speakers.bio: Károly is a research scientist and the creator of Two Minute Papers, a video series for making advanced research papers accessible and fun for everyone.

speakers.name: Yashraj Narang
speakers.bio: Yashraj “Yash” Narang is a research manager in NVIDIA’s robotics research lab. His work lies at the intersection of robotics, AI, and simulation, with recent projects in sim-to-real transfer, deep reinforcement learning, and neural physics models. Prior to NVIDIA, he completed a Ph.D. in materials science and mechanical engineering from Harvard University and a master’s in mechanical engineering from MIT.

speakers.name: Marco Pavone
speakers.bio: Marco Pavone is mainly interested in developing methodologies for the analysis, design, and control of autonomous systems, with an emphasis on self-driving cars, autonomous aerospace vehicles, and future mobility systems. Marco is also an associate professor of aeronautics and astronautics at Stanford University, where he's the director of the Center for Automotive Research.

sessions.title: All the Precision, None of the Transistors
sessions.abstract: We challenge the notion that modern AI-era GPUs are unsuitable for high-precision computations, specifically double-precision (FP64), the cornerstone of scientific computing since 1985. While scalar and vector FP64 throughput in GPUs have continued to improve over generations, Tensor Core throughput for FP64 has not grown at the same rate as reduced-precision operations used by AI. This is alleviated with mixed-precision linear algebra algorithms and Ozaki schemes that utilize reduced-precision Tensor Cores delivering significant performance and efficiency gains while guaranteeing native FP64 accuracy. We will showcase recent advancements in these algorithms, how to use them, and demonstrate their effectiveness in real-world applications. We will also preview an experimental analysis tool designed to improve developer productivity in reasoning about precision choices.

speakers.name: Harun Bayraktar
speakers.bio: Harun has been leading the Math Libraries organization at NVIDIA since 2017. Prior to joining NVIDIA, his career included developing HPC linear algebra software for an independent software vendor and simulation-based research and technology development for advanced composites materials in aerospace. Harun holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from the University of California, Berkeley.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries

sessions.title: Reinventing Clinical Decision Support With Agentic AI and a Data Flywheel
sessions.abstract: Clinical decision support is evolving from static, rule-based systems to adaptive, agentic AI that can reason, learn, and collaborate with clinicians. This talk explores how OpenEvidence is building an agentic system that combines real-world clinical data and clinical literature to deliver context-aware insights at the point of care. It'll also discuss the critical role of a continuously learning data flywheel—where every interaction enhances reasoning, accuracy, and safety—and how this dynamic feedback loop can redefine trust and performance in medical AI systems.

speakers.name: Daniel Nadler
speakers.bio:
Daniel is the founder of OpenEvidence, a Sequoia-backed AI company valued at $3.5 billion used by over a quarter of doctors in the United States. In 2025, Daniel was named to the TIME100 list of the 100 Most Influential People in global health.

While still a Ph.D. student at Harvard, Daniel founded Kensho Technologies, an AI company that became the most valuable AI company of the 2010s when it was acquired by S&P Global for $700 million in 2018. 

Outside of technology, Daniel is an award-winning poet and visual artist; his debut collection of poetry, "Lacunae," was published in 2016 and named by NPR as one of the Best Books of the Year.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Leverage DL to Enable Blood-Based Multi-Cancer Detection and Empower Health Systems to Reach More Patients
sessions.abstract: Multi-modal liquid biopsy tests with paired clinical data have the potential to unlock deep learning (DL) models to improve population-level multi-cancer detection to reach the 120 million people in the United States alone who are eligible for cancer screening. Such tests produce data for millions of molecules, such as cell-free DNA (and billions of bases) per patient, before incorporating EMR, imaging, and other data types. Learn more about the unique challenges and opportunities that Freenome and others are collaborating on to validate, launch, and improve ML/DL models for multi-cancer, and ultimately multi-disease, detection.

speakers.name: Riley Ennis
speakers.bio:
As the company’s co-founder and CPO, Riley heads the clinical and commercial development of Freenome’s noninvasive tests for early cancer detection and treatment selection, helping realize their vision of a cancer-free world.

A Thiel Fellow, Riley developed a cancer vaccine and founded his first biotech company, Immudicon, while still in high school. While earning his degree in molecular biology at Dartmouth, Riley co-authored several publications and worked with Novartis. In addition to his work as a scientist, Riley worked as an analyst at Morgan Stanley and an associate at Bridgewater, where he observed the positive impact of a strong organizational culture firsthand.

sessions.title: Physical AI for Healthcare Robotics—Part 1: Simulation-First Design and Accelerated Development
sessions.abstract: Join Part 1 of a two-part curated session on how simulation-first design is changing the way healthcare robots are built. You’ll see how teams are using NVIDIA Physical AI—spanning world foundation models, physics-based simulation, synthetic data, and real-time sensor-to-action deployment—to move faster with less risk. This session focuses on how simulation and digital twins are being used in practice to speed up development, test decisions earlier, and optimize systems long before they reach the operating room

speakers.name: Mostafa Toloui
speakers.bio: Mostafa leads product management for NVIDIA Healthcare Robotics, overseeing the NVIDIA Holoscan and Isaac for Healthcare platforms. His passion is to enable medtech companies to deliver intelligent, patient-centric solutions and disrupt the way diseases are prevented, diagnosed, and treated. Before NVIDIA, Mostafa worked in the medtech industry in various capacities from strategy manager to scientist, bringing products (software and hardware) from concept to launch. Mostafa holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering and an MBA from the University of Minnesota.

speakers.name: Yossi Bar
speakers.bio:
Yossi is the CEO and Founder of LEM Surgical and a seasoned leader in the area of surgical robotics (ex-R&D Director at Mazor Robotics; former COO/VP R&D at Radiaction medical).
He comes from a mechanical-engineering/Med-Tech R&D background and pursued further Philosophy and Management studies at Tel-Aviv and Reichman Universities.

speakers.name: Filippo Filicori
speakers.bio: Dr. Filicori is a robotic surgeon and System Chief of Surgical Innovation at Northwell Health, New York’s largest provider. He leads the Intra-operative Performance Analytics Laboratory in collaboration with Columbia Engineering, applying machine learning to outcomes research, computer vision, performance analytics, and simulation in robotic surgery. He is an Associate Professor of Surgery at Northwell School of Medicine. Dr. Filicori graduated cum laude from the University of Bologna, completed his Surgery Residency at Weill Cornell Medicine, and a Minimally Invasive Surgery Fellowship at Providence Portland Medical Center. He chairs the AI committee at SAGES.

speakers.name: Axel Krieger
speakers.bio: Axel Krieger is an Associate Professor and Carol Croft Linde Faculty Scholar at Johns Hopkins where he leads a team of students and engineers researching smart and autonomous robotic systems for surgery and interventions. He's a recipient of the NSF CAREER award and an inventor of over 30 patents and patent applications. Axel was an assistant professor in mechanical engineering at the University of Maryland and assistant research professor at Children’s National. He has several years of experience in private industry at Sentinelle Medical and Hologic. Axel completed his undergraduate and master’s degrees at the University of Karlsruhe in Germany and his doctorate at Johns Hopkins University.

speakers.name: David Noonan
speakers.bio: David Noonan, PhD, chief technology officer at Moon Surgical, which is building the Maestro System. Billed as the first AI-powered collaborative robot for soft-tissue laparoscopic surgery, Maestro earned a CE mark in 2023 and FDA clearance in March 2025. Before joining Moon in 2020, Noonan spent five years in engineering roles at Auris Health and over three years at Philips Research North America working on R&D for minimally invasive interventions. He has a  B.Eng in Mechatronics Engineering from Dublin City University, an M.Sc in Mechanical Engineering Research from King's College London, and a Ph.D. from Imperial College London. He is a named inventor on over 50 granted US patents.

sessions.title: Physical AI for Healthcare Robotics—Part 2: Closing the Sim-to-Real Gap and Real-World Deployment
sessions.abstract: Join Part 2 of this two-part curated session on what it takes to bring simulation-trained robotics into the real world. This session looks at how teams are translating behaviors developed in simulation into reliable clinical performance. We’ll cover practical approaches to closing the sim-to-real gap, along with the real challenges of deploying safe, robust, low-latency sensor-to-action systems in live healthcare environments.

speakers.name: Tao Li
speakers.bio: Tao Li, CTO of MicroPort MedBot (Group) Co., Ltd., has over 14 years of research experience in surgical robotics. He has led the development of multiple landmark systems including the world’s first remote surgical robot and MedBot’s first laparoscopic and orthopedic surgical robots. He has directed more than industrial key projects, participated in drafting international standards, and won multiple high-level science and technology awards. He has published over 10 papers, edited 1 monograph, and held more than 230 patents including over 60 international PCT patents.

speakers.name: Jay Carlson
speakers.bio: Jay leads product for Holoscan and Holoscan Sensor Bridge at NVIDIA, driving real-time, ultra-low-latency, software-defined data pipelines for healthcare, robotics, industrial, and scientific applications. He previously led hardware and software efforts in surgical robotics and has deep expertise in embedded systems and high-performance sensing.

speakers.name: Patrick Thornycroft
speakers.bio: Patrick Thornycroft is Chief Engineer at CMR Surgical, where he leads the technical development of the Versius surgical robotics platform. Since joining CMR in 2017, he has held senior engineering and strategy roles across research, product development and special programmes, shaping the company’s R&D strategy and guiding complex engineering programmes from concept through implementation. His work on bringing real-time AI and system-level intelligence to surgical robotics is driven by CMR Surgical’s mission to bring the benefits of minimal access surgery to everyone who needs it.

speakers.name: Pooria Kashani
speakers.bio: Pooria Kashani, PhD is Chief Product Officer at Horizon Surgical Systems, where he leads product strategy and execution for next-generation robotic platforms in ophthalmic surgery. He brings 20+ years of experience in eye care, digital health, product development. Previously, he was Head of R&D Digital Solutions at Alcon, where he founded three global centers of excellence in Software as a Medical Device, Product Security, and Data Science And AI. Dr. Kashani is an inventor on 20+ patents and holds a PhD/MS from UCLA. His current focus lies at the intersection of AI, robotics, real-time imaging, and surgical autonomy, translating advanced computation into safe, scalable clinical impact.

sessions.title: 利用 NVIDIA Cosmos VSS 构建智慧交通（ITS）违章检测系统
sessions.abstract: 与传统的计算机视觉模型相比，基于 VLM 的 Cosmos VSS 服务在视频分析领域的意图识别相关场景中表现出色。在本次演示中，我们将深入探讨一个实际的商业项目，以介绍 VSS 在实际应用中的使用方式，以及如何利用它来构建能够检测交通违规行为的服务。本次演示将带您了解我们在部署 Cosmos VSS 时所面临的挑战、资源需求，以及我们如何利用真实数据集对其进行微调以满足客户特定的业务目标。

speakers.name: Frank Chen
speakers.bio:
超过 20 年的全球顶级 IT 企业产品及相关软件、硬件开发，项目管理经验。
具备丰富的从云计算、服务器系统到嵌入式设备、嵌入式系统及移动应用的端到端的系统设计、开发和交付经验。 
曾服务于多家国内外知名企业
- 图灵新智算 产品副总裁
- 原联想基础设施服务方案集团 产品总监
- 原微软亚太研发集团 资深产品经理
- 原 AMD 北京技术开发中心 高级开发经理

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: Pushing the Boundaries of CAE and EDA With NVIDIA cuDSS, the CUDA Direct Sparse Solver
sessions.abstract: NVIDIA CUDA Direct Sparse Solver (cuDSS) is increasingly being adopted in large-scale simulations for engineering and design, such as those pertinent to electronic engineering automation (EDA) and computer-aided engineering (CAE). In this session, we will cover recent advances, including most recent performance number, highlights of key features, and new APIs in cuDSS that enable users across various workflows to leverage GPU acceleration for sparse solvers.
sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries

sessions.title: How We Scaled RL for Agentic Coding: Lessons from Training SWE-1.6
sessions.abstract: Sharing lessons from the training of SWE-1.6.  Preview which beat top open-source models on SWE-Bench Pro. We will cover RL environments for agentic coding, a stable algorithm for async RL, how we made our training 6x faster with NVFP4 & more, and how we approach GPU allocation for async RL.

speakers.name: Silas Alberti
speakers.bio: Silas Alberti is a mathematician, AI researcher, and founding team member at Cognition, the company behind the autonomous software engineer Devin. He previously pursued a Ph.D. in AI at Stanford, working with Stefano Ermon and Chris Ré, and has held research and engineering roles at Google DeepMind and Jane Street. Silas co-created ChatBCG and PIGEON, early demos of practical AI agents.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Architecting Scalable Pipelines for Foundation Model Experiments
sessions.abstract: Financial applications demand high precision and ultra-low latency. Learn firsthand from engineering leaders at Capital One how they're building distributed data pipelines to curate high-quality datasets that enable laser-focused foundation models for financial applications.

speakers.name: Roman Yokunda Enzmann
speakers.bio: Roman studied economics in his undergraduate and graduate studies. He specialized in machine learning and dynamic optimization, graduating with his master’s thesis on the topic of explainable AI. He has a background in data science and risk modeling within the financial services industry. Roman concentrates on accelerated data science using RAPIDS, with a focus on fraud detection.

speakers.name: Brian Nguyen
speakers.bio: Brian Nguyen is a software engineer and a dedicated Kubernetes enthusiast, with a career spanning engineering and consulting roles across both startup environments and large corporations. He currently leads as a senior engineering manager at Capital One, where he directs the compute and distributed system infrastructure for the Capital One machine learning platform. Additionally, Brian lectures at a local university and resides with his family in Florida.

speakers.name: Nick Resnick
speakers.bio: Nick Resnick is an AI engineer and full-stack machine learning practitioner. He currently serves as a lead AI engineer at Capital One, where he specializes in building and accelerating AI infrastructure using GPUs. Nick has worked as an applied researcher and engineer in the domains of predictive customer analytics, entity matching, and encoder models, holding several patents for his innovations in these fields. Outside of his technical work, Nick occasionally attempts stand-up comedy.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models

sessions.title: NVIDIA NeMo を活用した半導体領域知識を組み込む小規模 LLM のファインチューニング
sessions.abstract: 本セッションでは、半導体製造装置の立ち上げ・保守作業などの支援を目的としたLLMのチューニング方法をご紹介します。NVIDIAが公開しているオープンソースの合成ペルソナデータセットであるNemotron-Personas-USA, Nemotron-Personas-Japanと合成データ生成ツールであるNVIDIA NeMo Data Designerを活用した独自データセットの構築から、NVIDIA NeMoを用いたモデルチューニングまでのプロセスを紹介し、製造業におけるAI活用の可能性を探求します。

speakers.name: Kenya Nishiguchi
speakers.bio: 北海道大学で工学博士号を取得後、住友電気工業に入社。化合物半導体トランジスタの設計に従事し、ニューラルネットワークを組み込んだRF等価回路モデルを開発。2023年に東京エレクトロンへ転籍し、生成AIを活用した社内アプリの開発や、自社データを用いたAIのファインチューニング研究に従事。近年は、Kubernetesクラスターの構築にも取り組んでいる。

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Building an AI Factory to Power the Next Era of eCommerce
sessions.abstract: Coupang is evolving from a digital retailer into an AI-native commerce platform by building an AI Factory in partnership with NVIDIA. In this session, we’ll examine how large-scale GPU infrastructure enables end-to-end AI lifecycle management — from data pipelines and model training to real-time inference across billions of interactions. We’ll discuss the system architecture, performance optimizations, and deployment strategies required to scale foundation models across search, recommendation, and merchant services.

speakers.name: Ashish Suryavanshi
speakers.bio: Ashish Suryavanshi is a vice president of engineering at Coupang and is the founder/tech owner of CIC. During his time at Coupang, he has defined, built and managed Coupang's technology infrastructure and cloud strategy to build platforms that are scalable, reliable, efficient, and highly productive for our engineers. In his current role as CIC tech leader, Ashish is on a mission to provide an AI platform/infrastructure to CIC customers at the highest scale, performance, and reliability to maximize availability at lowest cost.

speakers.name: Krishna Chaitanya Kota
speakers.bio: Krishna Chaitanya Kota is a Senior Director of Back‑end Engineering at Coupang and a key technology leader within CIC. Over his tenure at Coupang, he has architected, scaled, and managed critical backend systems that power some of the company’s most high‑performance, high‑reliability services. Krishna has been instrumental in driving engineering excellence across data, services, and platform layers ensuring that Coupang’s infrastructure remains resilient and efficient. In his current role as an engineering leader within CIC, Krishna is focused on building the next generation of AI platform capabilities. He is on a mission to deliver scalable, high‑performance AI infrastructure that enables CIC customers to develop and deploy AI solutions with maximum reliability, velocity, and cost‑efficiency. His work brings together deep backend engineering expertise with modern AI systems to create robust, enterprise‑grade foundations for AI at global scale.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: 基于 verl 进行大模型强化学习的最佳实践
sessions.abstract: verl 是目前最受欢迎的大模型（LLM）强化学习框架。在本次分享中，我们将深入探讨 verl 的架构设计，介绍我们在提升推理和训练性能方面所做的工作，以及如何进行端到端的 Agentic RL（智能体强化学习）训练。

speakers.name: 巫 锡斌
speakers.bio: verl 项目 maintainer，专注于构建大规模 LLM 强化学习框架。

speakers.name: Yan Bai
speakers.bio: Yan joined NVIDIA in 2024, focusing on LLM training optimization and reinforcement learning optimization.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: From Challenge to Competitive Advantage: How VC Portfolio Companies Leverage NVIDIA to Win Markets
sessions.abstract: AI venture funding reached $100.8 billion in 2024—37% of all venture capital (VC) investment. Yet, most promising AI startups don’t fail due to weak markets or teams, but from challenges bridging research and production: scaling infrastructure, solving domain-specific problems, and building defensible technical moats. The focus is now on “doing AI right” for real impact. Top VCs now treat NVIDIA partnership as essential portfolio infrastructure—not just for compute, but as a strategic accelerator that helps founders solve hard problems, lead technically, and speed up R&D. This session shares how startups used NVIDIA’s ecosystem to achieve breakthrough results.

speakers.name: Miroslav Gasparek
speakers.bio:
Miroslav is the chief executive officer and co-founder of Sensible Biotechnologies, a company pioneering the world’s first integrated, AI-enabled, cell-based platform for mRNA design, manufacturing, and scale-up. Under Miro’s leadership, the company has raised over $13 million from leading life science investors, including Recode Ventures, BlueYard Capital, Y Combinator, and prominent industry executives.

Miro earned an M.Eng. in biomedical engineering from Imperial College London and is finalizing his D.Phil. in engineering science at the University of Oxford. He previously conducted research in leading labs at Imperial, Caltech, and Stanford University.

speakers.name: Arijit Sengupta
speakers.bio: Arijit Sengupta, Founder and CEO of Aible, the only Enterprise AI solution that guarantees business impact in just one month. Arijit is the former Founder and CEO of BeyondCore, a market-leading Automated Analytics solution that was acquired by Salesforce.com to become Einstein Discovery. Arijit co-created and co-instructed the first AI course in the MBA program of the Harvard Business School as an Executive Fellow. He also wrote a book entitled “AI is a Waste of Money” based on the results of a thousand AI projects he had led at that time. Arijit holds an MBA with Distinction from the Harvard Business School and Bachelor degrees with Distinction in Computer Science and Economics from Stanford University.

speakers.name: Alyss Noland
speakers.bio: Alyss has diverse experience in sales engineering, developer advocacy, and product marketing with companies such as GitHub, Box, Atlassian, BigCommerce, and Honeycomb. She is a co-author of the 2021 State of the Octoverse report, alongside Nicole Forsgren and Eirini Kalliamvakou, and previously launched GitHub Codespaces and GitHub Copilot's technology preview. As a staunch developer advocate and startup ecosystem enabler, Alyss grew NVIDIA DGX Cloud's Innovation Lab from its inception.

speakers.name: David Katz
speakers.bio: David brings his breadth of experience to support Radical portfolio companies in their go-to-market efforts and to source and evaluate new deals. David started his career as a lawyer, but switched paths early and went on to found/scale/sell a number of businesses, including an online insurance platform for financial institutions, InsLogic, followed by a marketplace for freelancers, Portfolios.com. David then spent several years building and scaling new technologies and ventures in the AdTech space, with a focus on data platforms and ML. Most recently, David was an executive at Benecaid, where he created Honeybee, a customer acquisition platform for insurance companies.

speakers.name: Joseph Nelson
speakers.bio: Joseph Nelson is the co-founder and CEO of Roboflow. He attended The London School of Economics and Political Science. Previously, Joseph co-founded and sold a natural language processing startup, Represently, that helped the U.S. Congress more efficiently respond to constituents (like "Zendesk for Congress"). He also ran a data science consultancy that built ML prototypes. He's taught over 2,000 hours of data science curriculum at General Assembly and The George Washington University, and interned at Facebook.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: AI Coding for the GPU: Build a Coding Agent to Help GPU Developers Write Speed-of-Light Code
sessions.abstract: Discover the evolution of AI-assisted development for accelerated computing as we move beyond general-purpose coding assistants to specialized agents designed for high-performance CUDA optimization. While frontier models have improved at syntax correctness, they often lack the domain-specific context required to utilize the latest hardware features and achieve "speed of light" performance. This session details NVIDIA’s "CUDA Intelligence" ecosystem—a transition from simple retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines to advanced agentic workflows using the model context protocol (MCP) and agentic orchestration. We will demonstrate how we are democratizing state-of-the-art code transformation by integrating cloud profiling, Nsight tools, and expert-level optimization skills into a constellation of AI services that can run locally or in the cloud.

speakers.name: Matt Frazier
speakers.bio: Matt Frazier empowers the next generation of experts in accelerated computing by creating coding, profiling, and development assistants and positive feedback loops for developer expertise and productivity. He's built AI solutions for over 20 years, from foundational work in computer vision to most recently productizing video intelligence and code healing products at Google.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: How AI Is Changing Everyday Work, Insights from NVentures Portfolio Companies
sessions.abstract: This NVentures-hosted panel explores how AI is redefining the way we work across industries, from healthcare to manufacturing. Hear from innovative portfolio leaders who are applying AI to streamline operations, enhance decision-making, and unlock new levels of human productivity.

speakers.name: Fabian Hedin
speakers.bio: Fabian Hedin is an accomplished entrepreneur and technology leader with a diverse background in founding and leading innovative companies. As Co-Founder of Lovable, Founder and CEO of Tentium, and Founder and CTO of TenFAST, Fabian has demonstrated a strong ability to drive business growth and technological advancement. Experience as Frontend Lead at Depict and Fullstack Engineer at CurbFood further showcases Fabian's comprehensive skill set in software development and team leadership within the tech industry.

speakers.name: Pete Florence
speakers.bio: Pete Florence is co-founder and chief executive officer of Generalist AI. Prior to starting Generalist in March 2024, Pete spent four-and-a-half years at Google Brain/DeepMind, and did his Ph.D. at MIT, advised by Russ Tedrake. The focal points of Pete’s work in robotics and AI have been in bringing large multi-modal models to robotics, and in general-purpose dexterous manipulation.

speakers.name: Shiv Rao
speakers.bio: Shiv Rao is the founder and CEO of Abridge, a generative AI platform that relieves clinicians of clerical work such as clinical documentation, enabling them to focus more fully on patient care. Abridge integrates seamlessly within electronic health record clinician workflows—across care settings, specialties, and spoken languages. In 2025, Abridge was named to the Forbes AI 50 and the Fast Company Most Innovative Companies lists.

speakers.name: Anna-Katrina Shedletsky
speakers.bio: Anna-Katrina Shedletsky is the CEO and co-founder of Instrumental, which she founded to save the 20 cents of every dollar spent in manufacturing that is wasted. Instrumental (www.instrumental.com) builds a manufacturing AI and data platform that accelerates development and improves yield in production. Prior to Instrumental, Anna-Katrina was an Apple product design engineer for six years, leading system product design for the Apple Watch Series 1 and several iPods. Anna has two degrees in mechanical engineering from Stanford University and sits on the National Leadership Council for the Society for Science.

speakers.name: Maithreyi "Maithra" Raghu
speakers.bio: Maithra Raghu is the founder and chief executive officer of Samaya AI, an expert intelligence platform for financial services that lets users build personalized AI agents for complex financial tasks. Backed by NEA, Eric Schmidt, Jeff Dean, and Yann LeCun, Samaya is transforming how finance professionals use AI. Named to the TIME100 AI 2025 list, Maithra is recognized as a leading figure in AI. Previously, she spent seven years as a research scientist at Google Brain, specializing in neural networks for expert knowledge work.

speakers.name: Matan Grinberg
speakers.bio: Matan Grinberg is a founder and chief executive officer of Factory, an AI company building an autonomous AI platform to optimize the software development life cycle. Prior to Factory, Matan was a theoretical physicist at UC Berkeley and Princeton University, working on string theory.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Optimize Performance of Vision AI Models on the Edge
sessions.abstract: As vision AI continues to expand into real-world applications—from multi-modal systems like vision language models (VLMs) to depth-aware robotics using stereo vision—optimizing model performance on edge devices has become mission-critical. This talk dives into practical strategies for deploying high-performance vision models on constrained hardware. We'll explore techniques for customizing large-scale VLMs while maintaining multi-modal alignment, and optimizing depth estimation models for real-time stereo processing with limited compute and power budgets. You'll leave with a clear understanding of how to balance accuracy, latency, and resource usage when bringing advanced vision capabilities to the edge.

speakers.name: Louise Huang
speakers.bio: Louise is a product manager on the NVIDIA Metropolis team. She leads efforts in computer vision and vision-language models and their fine-tuning. Over the years, she has built a strong technical foundation in computer vision, large-scale data processing, and creative design software. Louise holds her degree in computer science from University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: Shaping the Future of Semiconductor Manufacturing With AI
sessions.abstract: Join Samsung experts to explore how AI and digital twins are reshaping semiconductor manufacturing—from electronic design automation (EDA) and computational lithography to the design and operation of advanced fabrication plants. Learn how Samsung builds solutions with NVIDIA CUDA-X libraries, including cuLitho, to achieve up to 20x speedups in key manufacturing processes. Explore how they leverage NVIDIA Omniverse libraries to build large-scale industrial digital twins to conduct closed loop simulations and predict and optimize fab scheduling, maintenance, and yield. Discover how AI agents, built on NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models, are driving chip, process, and system-wide optimization and innovation. Learn how Samsung’s AI Factory, powered by over 50,000 NVIDIA GPUs, makes all of this possible.

speakers.name: Yong Ho Song
speakers.bio: Yong Ho Song serves as head of the AI Center in the Device Solutions Division at Samsung Electronics, driving AI-powered innovation across all semiconductor products and business areas. Previously, he led Memory Solution Product & Development, creating solutions for AI and data center applications. Previously, he was a professor in the department of Convergence Electronics Engineering, head of the ITRC center at Hanyang University, and director of the National Intelligent Semiconductor Development Project for Future Growth Engines in South Korea. He brings 35 years experience. Yong Ho earned his Ph.D. in electrical engineering from the University of Southern California and holds M.S. and B.S. degrees from Seoul National University.

sessions.title: Build Trust and Discovery Through Open‑Source AI in Research
sessions.abstract: Open-source AI is essential for trust, learning, and discovery in education and research, but only if we can inspect, reproduce, and improve how models are made. In this session, Professor Hanna Hajishirzi will present Olmo 3: a fully open language and “thinking” model, plus complete model flows—the full life cycle of checkpoints, data, and dependencies—enabling customization and scientific inspection beyond final weights. Professor Percy Liang will introduce Marin, an open lab for frontier AI where experiments happen in public, with community review and contributions via GitHub, advancing reproducibility, benchmarking, and data-efficient training. Together, these approaches outline a path to verifiable, improvable AI systems that broaden access and accelerate open scientific progress.

speakers.name: Hanna Hajishirzi
speakers.bio: Hanna Hajishirzi is the Torode Family Associate Professor in the Allen School at the University of Washington, where she leads the H2Lab, and a senior director of natural language processing at AI2. She received her PhD in computer science from University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, and spent a year as postdoctoral associate at Disney Research and Carnegie Mellon University.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)

sessions.title: Visual AI Agents for Real-Time Video Understanding
sessions.abstract:
The next generation of visual AI agents starts with NVIDIA Blueprint for video search and summarization (VSS), which enables the creation of interactive analytical agents that reason through massive amounts of video, assess physical processes, and provide decision-making insights to streamline operations. The blueprint combines powerful vision language models (VLMs), advanced search capabilities with large language models, and Cosmos Reason VLM into a full suite of software for building,

We've made it easier to customize and deploy at the edge, on-premises, or in the cloud. Be the first to hear about the new features of VSS to accelerate your AI agent development.

speakers.name: Roopa Prabhu
speakers.bio: Roopa Prabhu serves as the senior director of engineering for Applied AI Services and Infrastructure at NVIDIA. In her role, she oversees applications and services related to conversational AI, generative AI, and computer vision AI for various verticals, including customer service, industrial and physical AI, smart cities, and more, across both edge and cloud environments. Furthermore, she is responsible for leading projects related to edge and cloud infrastructure, in addition to security.

speakers.name: Adam Ryason
speakers.bio: Adam Ryason is a Product Manager on the Metropolis team and leading the AI Blueprint for Video Search and Summarization. An experienced startup founder and researcher, his background consists of vision-based AI, digital twins, and human-computer interaction. He holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: Extend Robot Perception With Industrial Safety Agents
sessions.abstract:
Autonomous forklifts and mobile robots are transforming loading docks and warehouse aisles, but relying only on onboard sensing leaves them vulnerable to blind spots, occlusions, and conservative safety behaviors that increase cost. KION will show how they use NVIDIA Halos for outside-in safety on NVIDIA IGX, combining infrastructure cameras, low-latency detections, and safety logic to supervise multiple robots with virtual fences, tripwires, and dynamic zones. 

Learn how outside-in safety architecture boosts throughput, reduces false stops, and enables closer human–robot collaboration for use cases such as autonomous forklift trailer loading and autonomous mobile robot (AMR) zoning with Metropolis VSS and Cosmos Reason.

speakers.name: Johannes Hinckeldeyn
speakers.bio: Johannes Hinckeldeyn is director of advanced core technologies at KIONAG, where he spearheads R&D and product development in intralogistics, collaborating with industry and science. His extensive experience includes optimizing and simulating processes, technical logistics, and new product development. Notably, as COO of EL-Cell GmbH, he managed manufacturing and commercialization of electrochemical test equipment for battery research. Johannes holds a Ph.D. in operations management for product design and engineering, alongside degrees in production engineering and physical technologies, showcasing his deep expertise in technical innovation and practical application.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Sensor Fusion

sessions.title: GPU Access to Unbounded Dataset Sizes Breaks Open Document Ingestion and Search
sessions.abstract: Vector databases with 1 trillion vectors cannot fit in memory, and VecDB ingestion and search requires the massive concurrency, memory bandwidth, and latency tolerance of GPUs. Come see new programming models, new storage server reference designs, new SSD offerings, and new customer-oriented scaled results.

speakers.name: CJ Newburn
speakers.bio: Chris J. Newburn, who goes by CJ, is a distinguished engineer who drives HPC strategy and the software product roadmap in NVIDIA Compute Software, with a special focus on data center architecture and security, storage and network IO, systems, and programming models for scale. He's a community builder with a passion for extending the core capabilities of hardware and software platforms from HPC into AI, data science, and visualization.

speakers.name: Vikram Sharma Mailthody
speakers.bio: Vikram is a founding member of the Systems and Storage research group at NVIDIA Research, with a special focus on addressing fundamental systems-level challenges in emerging data-center workloads and scalable GPU memory and storage systems designs

speakers.name: Corey Nolet
speakers.bio: Corey is a distinguished engineer at NVIDIA, leading engineering for unstructured data processing acceleration. Prior to NVIDIA, Corey spent over a decade building massive-scale exploratory data science & real-time analytics platforms for big-data and HPC environments in the defense industry. Corey holds Bs. & Ms. degrees in Computer Science. He is also a Ph.D candidate in the same discipline, focused on the acceleration of algorithms at the intersection of graph and machine learning. Corey has a passion for using data to make better sense of the world.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Smart Agents, Safer Systems: Engineering AI for Trust and Transparency
sessions.abstract:
As autonomous AI agents expand enterprise automation, they introduce a growing attack surface that traditional security controls were not designed to address. This session shows why visibility and policy enforcement in the AI factory are critical for trust, and how GPU-accelerated cyber models act as intelligent guardrails against threats such as prompt injection, model exfiltration, and data leakage.
 
Through examples and an architectural walkthrough, we demonstrate a full-stack solution using NVIDIA NIM-based inference, NVIDIA Zero Trust Networking, the NVIDIA Morpheus SDK, DPU-accelerated firewalls, and F5 AI Guardrails. Learn how to offload security functions into an isolated trust domain to ensure zero-trust enforcement without compromising workload performance, and leave with an actionable blueprint for SecOps and AI teams to turn AI risk into a competitive differentiator.

speakers.name: Kunal Anand
speakers.bio:
Kunal leads the F5 product organization as chief product officer. Responsible for product vision, strategy, and execution, he ensures development of breakthrough solutions that solve critical challenges and create exceptional experiences for customers. In his previous role as chief technology and AI officer, Kunal charted the company’s technology and AI strategy and vision.

Prior to F5, Kunal held the dual role of CTO and chief information security officer at Imperva. Kunal has over 15 years of experience in AI and machine learning, ranging from model training, employing AI-driven algorithms to enhance products, and designing and implementing AI architectures.

speakers.name: Ash Bhalgat
