speakers.bio: Ash is responsible for driving revenue and market share growth of NVIDIA's data center and AI products (DPU and GPU) into cloud, telecom, and cybersecurity markets through the developer ecosystem. He has more than 20 years' experience building and marketing solutions for enterprise, service provider, and cloud, including at Cisco, Polycom, and Mellanox.

sessions.topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI

sessions.title: Edge Computing 101: Introduction to Smart Edge and Autonomous Robots
sessions.abstract: Discover the transformative potential of NVIDIA’s edge AI platform in shaping the future of smart edge and autonomous robotics. This session will provide an overview of the platform’s capabilities across embedded, industrial, and enterprise edge environments, with a focus on how general intelligence at the edge enables machines to perceive, reason, and make real-time decisions, directly where data is generated. Learn about the best practices and software frameworks that power AI-driven robots and intelligent vision systems—from advanced multi-modal sensor processing to low-latency, on-device inference—unlocking greater autonomy without reliance on the cloud. Explore how these innovations are accelerating the rise of generative physical AI systems and reshaping industries including healthcare, retail, robotics, transportation, manufacturing, and beyond.

speakers.name: Chen Su
speakers.bio: Chen Su is passionate about collaborating with cross-functional teams and growing businesses fueled by the internet of things, AI, and edge computing. Bringing clarity and creativity to cutting-edge technology and tools, he drives dynamic messaging and engagements to help global developers and partners achieve faster time-to-value and outcome.

sessions.title: The Future of Industrial Autonomy
sessions.abstract: This panel brings together industry leaders from Honeywell Industrial, KION Group, Caterpillar, Medtronic, Hitachi Rail and GXO to explore how advanced AI and edge computing are reshaping the future of industrial and healthcare systems. Together, they will discuss the major trends accelerating edge intelligence—from autonomous operations and multi-modal sensor fusion to real-time decision-making and digital twins—and share high-impact AI use cases driving measurable business growth and societal value. The conversation will highlight challenges in deploying scalable, safety-critical edge AI and reveal how these global innovators are architecting their next-generation solutions using NVIDIA’s accelerated computing and end-to-end edge platform.

speakers.name: Rodolphe Katra
speakers.bio: Rodolphe is the chief global AI officer at Medtronic, leading the AI Center of Excellence (COE) and driving innovation, efficiency, and best practices. He has more than 25 years of experience spanning startups, consulting, and companies of all sizes. At Medtronic, he established the Cardiac Rhythm Management AI COE and was lead architect for the AccuRhythm LINQ II AI-powered cardiac monitor. A strong advocate for the responsible and ethical use of AI, Rodolphe leads teams advancing personalized and precise healthcare through AI, ML, and predictive analytics. He holds a Ph.D. in biomedical engineering, an M.B.A., is a Medtronic Technical Fellow, and is inventor on more than 150 patents.

speakers.name: Deepu Talla
speakers.bio: Deepu Talla is Vice President of Robotics and Edge Computing, where he's responsible for deploying AI technology in devices such as factory robots, commercial drones, and video analytics. Previously, he was responsible for NVIDIA’s mobile business unit. Prior to joining NVIDIA in 2013, he worked at Texas Instruments for more than 10 years in various executive management and technical leadership roles. Deepu holds a bachelor of engineering degree in electronics and communication engineering from Andhra University in India, a master of science degree in electrical engineering from Villanova University in Pennsylvania, and a Ph.D. in computer engineering from the University of Texas at Austin.

speakers.name: Suresh Venkatarayalu
speakers.bio:
Suresh Venkatarayalu is Senior Vice President, Chief Technology
Officer (CTO), and President of Honeywell Connected Enterprise.
His principal focus is helping customers transform and automate
their operations at scale. Suresh drives Honeywell’s core
innovation engines, leading the company’s global technology
strategy, connected solutions, and software business.
As CTO, Suresh sets Honeywell’s enterprise-wide research and
development agenda, advancing breakthrough technologies,
shaping the company’s AI and autonomy strategy, and defining the
roadmap for its automation portfolio and path to industrial
autonomy. As President of Honeywell Connected Enterprise, he
oversees the full software lifecycle, including product
management and engineering, the company’s labs and operations, and Honeywell Forge, which
serves as a key enabler of the company’s vision—helping the industrial world transition from
automation to autonomy.
Additionally, Suresh leads Honeywell’s Software Transformation Initiative—driving
standardization, operational excellence, and recurring revenue growth—and spearheads
ecosystem partnerships across AI platforms, cloud hyperscalers, edge computing, and industrial
technologies to co-create advanced automation solutions for real-world environments.
Suresh began his career at India’s Department of Defense Research and Development. He joined
Honeywell in 1995, and has held senior engineering, technology, and business leadership roles
across the organization. He also served as President of Honeywell Technology Solutions, where
he scaled global engineering operations across emerging markets and helped globalize
Honeywell’s R&D footprint.
A strong advocate for talent development and inclusive leadership, Suresh mentors emerging
Honeywell leaders worldwide. He holds an Executive MBA from the Indian Institute of
Management and a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science from Bharathidasan University,
India.

speakers.name: Mariella Guerricchio
speakers.bio: Mariella holds a master’s degree in electronic engineering and microelectronics from the University of Pisa and has a strong background in innovation and digital transformation. She joins us from Accenture Italy, where she was the Managing Director of the Industry X Engineering Services division. Before that she has been involved in delivering research & development and engineering solutions at Altran Group (now part of Capgemini) and ST Microelectronics.

speakers.name: Brandon Hootman
speakers.bio:
Brandon Hootman is Vice President of AI Platforms & Technologies at Caterpillar. In this role, he leads Caterpillar’s physical AI platform for machine and jobsite autonomy. This platform brings together complex jobsite data; simulation; and AI development, training, and inference at scale to accelerate AI-based autonomy in construction and mining. Brandon also leads Caterpillar’s AI Accelerator, a cross-functional organization focused on transforming the enterprise with AI and ensuring adoption results in value realization. 
 Brandon has 25+ years of experience in software and data engineering and applied ML/AI, and his functional experience spans multiple business areas, including Cat dealers, supply chain, manufacturing, sales, and digital.  His tenure at Caterpillar has included international assignments in France and Belgium. Brandon is a graduate of Roosevelt University. He serves on MIT’s Center for Information Systems Research (CISR), MIT’s Data Board, and MIT’s Computer Science & AI Labs. He also represents Caterpillar on executive product councils for key data and AI technology partners.

speakers.name: Rob Smith
speakers.bio:
Rob Smith is CEO of KION GROUP AG since January 2022. Before joining KION, Rob
Smith was President & CEO of the publicly listed Finnish group Konecranes, a leading
international manufacturer of industrial cranes and container port automation. From
2013 to 2019, he was Senior Vice President & General Manager Europe, Africa and
Middle East of the global agricultural machinery company AGCO Corporation. Prior to
that, he held management positions in automotive supply and capital goods companies
in the U.S., France and Germany. Smith holds a BSE in Systems Engineering from
Princeton University, an MBA in International Operations & Finance from the University
of Texas at Austin – Red McCombs School of Business. He earned his DiplomKaufmann and Dr. rer. pol. degrees from the WHU – Otto Beisheim School of
Management in Koblenz, Germany and wrote his dissertation on Global Supply Chain
Performance and Risk Optimisation.

speakers.name: Patrick Kelleher
speakers.bio:
Patrick Kelleher is Chief Executive Officer of GXO, the world’s largest pure-play contract
logistics provider, serving the world’s leading brands in 27 markets.
Mr. Kelleher has more than three decades of global supply chain experience. Prior to
GXO, he served as CEO, North America at DHL Supply Chain, leading the highest
performing region within Deutsche Post DHL Group’s €18 billion contract logistics
division.
Prior to this role, Mr. Kelleher served as DHL Supply Chain’s global chief development
officer and CEO, Americas for Williams Lea Tag, when it operated under DHL’s
ownership. He has a distinguished track record of operational excellence and was at
the forefront of DHL’s deployment of advanced robotics.

sessions.title: Drive Optimal Tokens per Watt on any AI Infrastructure Using Benchmarking Recipes
sessions.abstract: Join our session to learn how benchmarking recipes can evaluate performance and inform infrastructure tuning to optimize tokens/watt. Learn about the intersection of performance and total cost of ownership (TCO) through NVIDIA's performance tools, and understand the NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud initiative.

speakers.name: Emily Potyraj
speakers.bio: Emily Potyraj is the product manager for DGX Cloud performance and benchmarking at NVIDIA. Her work focuses on developing tools that enable the characterization and optimization of AI workloads.

speakers.name: Paul Edwards
speakers.bio:
Paul Edwards is a Principal Technical Program Manager at Microsoft, where he leads customer-facing engineering for some of Azure's largest AI infrastructure customers. He specialises in the design, deployment, validation, and operational support of multi-thousand GPU clusters, spanning both Kubernetes and classical scheduler environments, used for large-scale AI training and inference.

With over 20 years of experience in high-performance computing, Paul's career spans the full arc of HPC: from developing DDT, an MPI debugger used on supercomputers worldwide, to optimising HPC applications for performance and scalability (including contributions to OpenFOAM adopted by the community), to his current work enabling AI workloads at scale on Azure. He builds custom tooling and automation to accelerate GPU cluster readiness, diagnose complex performance issues, and close the feedback loop between customer deployments and Azure product teams.

Paul holds an MSc (Distinction) in Advanced Computer Science from the University of Manchester and a BSc in Computer Science from the University of Warwick. He is based in Warwick, UK.

sessions.title: 洞察 2026 中国 AI 市场 — AI 智能体和物理 AI 浪潮下的创业风口
sessions.abstract: NVIDIA 将与创投联盟中的优秀投资人代表及典型被投企业代表共同探讨 2026 年中国 AI 市场的前景，分析市场的潜在爆发点、新兴创业企业以及新机遇。此外，他们还将就大模型、生成式 AI、AI 智能体、物理 AI 等热门行业的投资趋势，以及投资人和投资机构如何选择被投企业等话题进行深入探讨。

speakers.name: Jack Pei
speakers.bio:
裴晟，NVIDIA 初创加速计划经理，主要负责 NVIDIA 初创加速计划创投联盟以及中西部地区的会员管理，通过构建健康的 AI 生态合作体系，加速中国创业公司的发展。
裴晟先生拥有超过 18 年的工作经历，主要专注于市场和创新发展，并曾有多年的创业经历。
在加入 NVIDIA 之前曾任职百度智能云、百度业务运营部高级经理，IBM 区域拓展市场经理。
毕业于清华大学自动化系，拥有清华大学硕士和学士学位。

speakers.name: 张 煜
speakers.bio: 张煜博士现任清智资本及清智孵化器创始合伙人。2020 年起，张煜博士任清华大学智能产业研究院院长助理及战略发展与合作部主任，并成立了清智资本和清智孵化器。短短两年时间，清智孵化器已经成为中国最顶尖的人工智能孵化器之一，清智资本也成为中国最有影响力的早期创投之一，在人工智能领域不断投出具有行业突破性的人工智能创新公司。

speakers.name: Haofei Liu
speakers.bio: 刘昊飞，中国国际科技促进会母基金分会联席会长，盛景嘉成创投创始合伙人，盛景网联的早期投资人，清华大学全球私募股权研究院行业导师。 刘昊飞先生 2005 年进入创业投资领域，拥有 20 年投资行业经验。刘昊飞先生 2013 年加入盛景集团，主管投资板块，并于 2014 年开创盛景嘉成母基金业务，多次获得中国证券报“金牛奖”股权投资精英奖。

speakers.name: 吴 杰茜
speakers.bio: FilmAction 一站式 AI电影制作平台创始人。好耶科技（北京）有限公司 CEO。拥有北京大学光华管理学院工商管理硕士（MBA）学位，曾在麻省理工、中央戏剧学院进修。多年项目管理、流程自动化、企业技术转型的经验。曾于 Deutsche Bahn 主导全司系统升级及流程自动化。在影视领域亦拥有多年丰富实战经验，深度参与《功夫》、《北京遇上西雅图》等近 20 个好莱坞及国内一线影视项目。

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: A Playbook – Operating Cloud AI Factories at Scale
sessions.abstract: Discover how to build and operate AI factories that scale with your ambitions. In this session, NVIDIA shares hard-earned lessons from working alongside Cloud Partners and ecosystem ISVs to power massive AI infrastructure around the world. You’ll gain a practical framework—built on open, composable infrastructure software—for managing the full lifecycle of large AI systems, from reliability and performance to multi-tenant orchestration. Walk away ready to accelerate your time-to-production and deliver sustainable scale for your own AI operations.

speakers.name: Vishal Ganeriwala
speakers.bio: Vishal Ganeriwala is the product marketing and go-to-market lead for NVIDIA DGX Cloud. Prior to NVIDIA, Vishal was a vice president of product marketing at Citrix.

speakers.name: Warren Barkley
speakers.bio: Warren Barkley is vice president of product management of DGX Cloud at NVIDIA. Prior to NVIDIA, Warren led core AI/ML platform teams in Google Cloud. Before Google, he held executive roles managing AI and machine learning product groups at Amazon Web Services and at Microsoft, where he helped drive several major cloud and communications products.

sessions.title: Deep Dive on Gigawatt AI Factories
sessions.abstract:
As at-scale AI workloads for training and inference make use of gigawatt-scale multi-GPU systems, massive data transfer, and memory access capabilities of Vera Rubin NVL72 systems in AI factories, we will show how these large systems get architected and deployed in the data center, leading to a robust reference architecture embraced globally for its speed of deployment and performance.

This session will showcase internal deployments and the tools and software stack developed and leveraged on these platforms. You'll also have the opportunity to have deep dive views on how multi-node NVLink, system interconnects, and storage, as well as the latest liquid-cooled designs, are shaping the future of AI infrastructure.

speakers.name: Julie Bernauer
speakers.bio: Julie Bernauer leads a team of systems, systems performance, and deep learning engineers for building next-generation scale-out and hyperscale platforms. Her team built the Selene and Eos supercomputers, the basis of the Superpod and at-scale GPU performance designs. She joined NVIDIA in 2015 from academia.

speakers.name: Adam DeConinck
speakers.bio: Adam DeConinck is a director on the NVIDIA Applied Systems Engineering team, where he supports a team of system architects who design next-generation AI supercomputers. His past experience includes building high-performance compute and storage systems at scale at Los Alamos National Laboratory and Facebook, as well as for NVIDIA’s HPC and AI customers.

sessions.title: How NVIDIA Tapped Its Unstructured Data for AI Insights
sessions.abstract: Enterprises sit on a treasure trove of knowledge, the biproduct of years of digital operations, locked away in silos. In this session, NVIDIA IT, along with ecosystem partners, will walk through the architectural patterns, governance models, and “vendor lab” environment NVIDIA created to unlock the value of this data for AI developers and agents.

speakers.name: Rama Akkiraju
speakers.bio: Rama Akkiraju, vice president of enterprise AI and automation at NVIDIA, is a former IBM Fellow, master inventor, and CTO of IBM Watson AI Ops. She co-authored more than 100 papers, holds over 50 patents, and received multiple awards, including Forbes's "Top 20 Women in AI Research" (2017), the University of California Berkeley's Athena Award (2020), and AI Industry Leader of the Year 2022, awarded by the Women Leaders in Data & AI organization.

speakers.name: Steve Kearns
speakers.bio: Steve Kearns is the GM of Search Solutions, bringing 20+ years of experience, including information extraction/retrieval, search technologies, analytics, machine learning, and artificial intelligence.

sessions.title: The Builder’s Toolkit: Scaling Enterprise AI Factories
sessions.abstract: This session explores the architectural foundations required to build and scale enterprise AI factories for LLMs, agentic AI, physical AI, and HPC workloads. We’ll outline the full-stack infrastructure and software requirements of an AI factory and demonstrate how NVIDIA’s NV-Certified systems, enterprise reference architectures, validated designs, and exemplar AI factories distill our learnings from real-world deployments into repeatable patterns that help system partners accelerate implementation with consistency, performance, and efficiency.

speakers.name: Peter Lillian
speakers.bio: Peter Lillian is a director of product management in the NVIDIA Enterprise Platforms and Solutions organization. Leveraging his 20 years of data center solutions experience, he has a passion for combining hardware, software, and infrastructure to create compelling, customer-driven solutions. Past experience with Acceleration Technologies, HPC, Linux, High Performance Storage, Networking, and numerous product management roles has provided him with a strong foundation to establish creative ways to solve customer challenges.

speakers.name: Bert Condensa
speakers.bio: Bert Condensa leads NVIDIA’s Enterprise AI Factory Segment Sales - a global team focused on accelerating AI adoption through NVIDIA’s full-stack infrastructure solutions. His team partners with strategic enterprises across key verticals to deliver transformative business outcomes through NVIDIA’s partner network.    With over 25+ years of experience in enterprise technology sales and executive leadership roles. his background spans cloud infrastructure, enterprise systems, and go-to-market strategy across both startups and global organizations, providing a strong foundation for building scalable, customer-driven solutions.  He is known for his strategic vision, collaborative leadership, and deep expertise in data center infrastructures, cloud computing, and technology distribution channels.

sessions.title: Enterprise AI at Speed: Simplifying AI Factory Deployments at Scale
sessions.abstract: Learn how NVIDIA Enterprise AI Factory powers a versatile, full-stack ecosystem, where specialist platforms provide the backbone for running inference and agents in production. We’ll share patterns for deploying AI at scale—when to build vs. buy, how to work with software partners, and how to keep efforts tied to real ROI instead of science projects.

speakers.name: Venky Veeraraghavan
speakers.bio:
As Chief Product Officer at DataRobot, Venky Veeraraghavan drives the definition and delivery
of the DataRobot Agent Workforce Platform. Venky has thirty years of experience focusing on
big data and AI as a product and engineering leader at top technology companies (Microsoft)
and early-stage startups (Trilogy).

speakers.name: Meghana Puvvadi
speakers.bio: Meghana is director of engineering to build AI products and platforms.

sessions.title: Enhance Data Center Efficiency and Throughput by Co-Scheduling on NVIDIA CPU+GPU Systems
sessions.abstract: Coherent NVIDIA CPU-GPU systems provide a versatile platform to support a wide spectrum of workloads. However not all jobs require all capabilities at scale. Co-scheduling CPU-only, partially accelerated, and fully GPU-accelerated jobs on NVIDIA CPU-GPU nodes can boost throughput and energy efficiency, removing the need for multiple dedicated clusters. We discuss isolation mechanisms including CPU affinity via cgroups, cache and memory bandwidth partitioning through ARM’s MPAM extensions, and InfiniBand QoS for network traffic separation. Learn to identify co-schedule candidates and assess scheduling policy design options and incentives for user adoption. We'll present workload studies aligned with NERSC’s upcoming Doudna system, demonstrating projected improvements in utilization and efficiency while highlighting open challenges in enabling fine-grained resource sharing.

speakers.name: Brian Austin
speakers.bio: Brian Austin is a senior computing engineer at NERSC. His research explores the intersection of scientific computing algorithms and HPC system architecture. Brian holds a Ph.D. in chemistry from the University of California, Berkeley.

speakers.name: Mathias Wagner
speakers.bio: Mathias Wagner is a member of the European developer technology team at NVIDIA, where he works on high performance computing and scientific applications. Before joining NVIDIA, he worked as a postdoc in high-energy physics in Europe and the United States, focusing on lattice quantum chromodynamics simulations using GPUs. Mathias holds a Ph.D. in theoretical physics from Darmstadt University of Technology.

sessions.title: Inside NVIDIA DGX AI Factory: Accelerating Networking for AI Across Cloud, Core, and Edge
sessions.abstract: AI infrastructure must deliver predictable, high‑throughput pipelines for large‑scale training and inference while enforcing strict security, data sovereignty, and multi‑tenant isolation across cloud and on‑premises environments. These demands can overwhelm traditional host‑based networking and security, reducing utilization and fragmenting policies. This session will bring together NVIDIA technologists building NVIDIA’s own DGX Cloud platform powered by NVIDIA Networking and show how they are extending the learnings to sovereign DGX AI factory solutions. Understand design patterns such as zero‑trust for hardened multi‑tenant clusters, accelerated Kubernetes networking, intelligent data platforms, and security services that enable consistent, high‑performance AI factories everywhere.

speakers.name: Alexander Petrovskiy
speakers.bio: Alexander Petrovskiy is a senior product manager at NVIDIA, driving DPU and DOCA product strategy to accelerate and orchestrate networking for cloud and AI. With over a decade of expertise in networking, software-defined networking, and data center technologies, he has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions across cloud, telecom, and enterprise sectors. Alex holds an M.Sc. in computer engineering from the Moscow Technical University of Communications and Informatics.

speakers.name: Arts Yang
speakers.bio:
Arts is a principal hardware architect for Datacenter Systems Engineering and product architect for DGX products.
She is the main author of DGX SuperPOD reference architectures and is one of the original architects for the NVIDIA Cloud Partner reference design program.

sessions.title: Accelerated AI Networking with DOCA
sessions.abstract: As AI models scale, data center networking must provide ultra-low latency, high throughput, and programmable data paths so training and inference workloads remain efficient. With the NVIDIA DOCA software platform, developers can move critical data-path functions onto NVIDIA BlueField data processing units (DPUs) using DOCA libraries and microservices to offload, accelerate, and isolate cloud infrastructure while enhancing observability. We’ll cover how DOCA enables centralized orchestration of BlueField‑accelerated services, dynamic traffic steering, and fine‑grained network and security controls that work together to deliver secure, high‑performance fabrics with predictable performance and efficient resource utilization for multi‑tenant AI environments at scale.

speakers.name: Alexander Petrovskiy
speakers.bio: Alexander Petrovskiy is a senior product manager at NVIDIA, driving DPU and DOCA product strategy to accelerate and orchestrate networking for cloud and AI. With over a decade of expertise in networking, software-defined networking, and data center technologies, he has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions across cloud, telecom, and enterprise sectors. Alex holds an M.Sc. in computer engineering from the Moscow Technical University of Communications and Informatics.

sessions.title: 基于具身智能加速即时物流机器人研发：从大语言动作模型训练到数字孪生验证
sessions.abstract:
基于 PI0.5 架构与英伟达 GPU 集群的大语言动作模型训练：在 Isaac Lab 平台上，依托 PI0.5 架构训练灵巧手操作及换电夹爪模型，在仿真环境中实现 90% 的抓取成功率；融合 100 余个真实场景运动捕捉数据集与 Isaac GR00T 拟态技术，完成 10 倍数据增广，有效缩小仿真到现实的落地差距。
面向快速原型开发的物理数字孪生技术：在物理原型制作前，于 Isaac Sim 平台完成无人机机器人对接及换电站的设计验证；以 0.1 毫米的精度仿真 500 余种对接场景（如对位偏差、振动干扰等）；硬件落地前，基于数字孪生实现 75% 的首次对接成功率，将现场测试故障率降低 30%。
实际场景性能提升：通过数字孪生验证替代 80% 的物理测试，研发周期缩短 50%；经 200 次数字孪生迭代优化后，货物转运成功率达 92%。

speakers.name: HAOTIAN WU
speakers.bio:
吴昊天博士现任美团高级总监、无人机硬件研发负责人，同时担任美团硬件委员会常务委员。他获评北京市杰出创新创业人才、国家引进海外高端人才，拥有普渡大学博士学位，并完成斯坦福大学高管课程学习。
吴博士深耕智能无人系统、机器人技术及高性能边缘控制计算平台领域多年。此前，他曾任职英特尔，担任机器人与智能边缘控制高级产品总监、无人系统首席系统架构师，主导智能边缘控制技术及无人工厂解决方案的研发与产业化落地。加入美团后，他牵头设计了基于机器人技术与具身智能的物流装备体系，为美团低空自主配送网络筑牢技术根基。
吴博士曾斩获美国能源部研发类奖项 6 项（含一等奖、二等奖、三等奖）；职业生涯中，主导落地的商业化产品累计创造超 10 亿美元价值；拥有 30 项专利发明，发表技术论文 20 余篇，并多次荣获科技成果类奖项。

sessions.title: CUDA: New Features and Beyond
sessions.abstract: The CUDA platform is the foundation of the GPU computing ecosystem. Every application and framework that uses the GPU does so through CUDA's libraries, compilers, runtimes and language—which means CUDA is growing as fast as its ecosystem is evolving. Presented by one of the architects of CUDA, at this engineering-focused talk you will learn about all that's new and what's coming next for both CUDA and GPU computing as a whole.

speakers.name: Stephen Jones
speakers.bio: Stephen is one of the architects of CUDA, working on defining the language, the platform, and the hardware that it runs on to span the needs of parallel programming, from high performance computing to artificial intelligence. Previously, he led the Simulation & Analytics group at SpaceX, working on large-scale simulation of rocket engines. Stephen has been a part of CUDA since 2008.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: 从 Isaac Gym 迁移到 Isaac Lab，及未来技术演进
sessions.abstract: 本次分享将结合云深处在实际项目中的训练和部署经验，为大家带来从之前的 Isaac Gym 框架，向模块化程度更高、仿真内容更丰富的 Isaac Lab 框架，迁移强化学习工作流的技巧和注意点。内容涵盖 API 适配、环境搭建与行业应用级部署等核心环节，同时会详解迁移流程简化的关键策略、仿真到现实迁移的性能优化，以及拓展至 Lite3/M20/X30/DR02 系列机器人等工业级应用场景的关键策略。

speakers.name: Percy Peng
speakers.bio: 彭博的主要研究方向为机器人运动控制和规划决策。他长期致力于基于模仿学习与强化学习技术，攻克人形机器人与四足机器人领域的关键技术难题。

sessions.title: Bringing Physical AI to Smart Spaces
sessions.abstract: Creating truly scalable AI solutions for cities is a massive challenge, one that requires many AI models and platforms (and tons of data) working in harmony to make it possible. Announced at GTC Paris, NVIDIA Blueprint for smart city AI leverages 3-computer to activate physical AI and apply to cities for a unified, real-time intelligence solution for creating a smart city. Learn about the latest workflow updates. Linker Vision will share how they've been able to apply the blueprint with U.S. cities, from training models with synthetic data and NVIDIA Cosmos to deploying video analytics AI agents using NVIDIA Metropolis Blueprint for Video Search and Summarization (VSS) that leverages the fleet of camera sensors deployed in cities—an end-to-end approach to processing tens of thousands of camera streams for proactive automation of city operations, safety, and planning.

speakers.name: Ray (Yi-Ting) Wang
speakers.bio: Leveraging his Ph.D. in Clinical Medicine, Ray brings a unique interdisciplinary perspective to Linker Vision, bridging domain expertise with AI innovation. He participated in the core development of the VLM-enabled VisionAI Platform and defines solutions across Healthcare, Public Health, and Smart City sectors. In support of Linker Vision’s global deployment of Video Search and Summarization (VSS) empowered by Synthetic Data (SDG), Ray specializes in market alignment and deploying Post-Trained VLMs for complex scenarios.

speakers.name: Carlos Garcia-Sierra
speakers.bio: Carlos is a product manager at NVIDIA, focusing on making AI accessible to all. Carlos manages Metropolis AI Workflows, enabling new uses across a diverse set of markets. He's held multiple roles at NVIDIA, including strategic partnerships for the Metropolis business unit, business development for the Automotive BU, and technical marketing. In his free time, Carlos enjoys hiking with his family, mountain biking, reading, and growing organic food.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: A New Frontier for Vision AI with Expert Reasoning Agents
sessions.abstract:
This session is designed for companies already deploying vision AI applications with NVIDIA Metropolis (DeepStream, TAO). It will outline the next steps – how to integrate Vision Language Models (VLMs) into existing pipelines to unlock new reasoning and contextual understanding capabilities. Milestone Systems, a leader in video management software, will share how they have fine-tuned NVIDIA Cosmos Reason to create a specialized and highly accurate VLM using 150,000 hours of curated video.

Attendees will learn how they can use Milestone's VLM directly within their own applications and walk through their experience integrating VLMs into their video management system, XProtect. Through these use cases, we’ll examine how the next generation of VLM technology can transform video analytics into reasoning-driven applications.

speakers.name: Edward Mauser
speakers.bio: Edward Mauser is director and product lead for Project Hafnia at Milestone Systems, where he specializes in emerging technologies and AI-driven video analytics platforms. With expertise spanning user experience design, data analytics, and business strategy, Edward leads cross-functional teams in developing responsible AI solutions. He recently presented Project Hafnia at the Cities Today Institute Leadership Forum, and is recognized for his work in creating scalable, ethical AI deployment frameworks for urban environments.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Vision Language Models (VLMs)

sessions.title: Transforming Traffic Data into Actionable Insights for Safer Roads
sessions.abstract:
The California Department of Transportation (Caltrans) is responsible for 50,000 miles of roadways across California. Years in the making, Caltrans has worked with companies like Accenture, Deloitte, Google Cloud, and Microsoft, and independent software vendors such as Centific, to deliver a custom solution to solve two major concerns for California citizens: roadway efficiency, and the safety of vulnerable road users. 

We'll dive deep into the specific capabilities of NVIDIA solutions including NVIDIA Blueprint for video search and summarization, generative AI vision language models, Cosmos world foundations, and analytics ingestion to solve these statewide challenges.

speakers.name: Marcie Kahbody
speakers.bio: Marcie Kahbody is the deputy secretary of technology, agency information officer for the California State Transportation Agency, as well as the chief information officer for Caltrans. She is responsible for setting policies and coordinating information technology activities for Caltrans and agency-wide. Marcie has held IT positions within the State of California for over 32 years.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: Automating Vision and Reporting for Utility Inspections
sessions.abstract:
Pacific Gas and Electric (PG&E), one of the largest utility companies in the United States serving millions across Northern and Central California, is facing the same urgent grid modernization challenge as the broader energy sector—and is meeting it with NVIDIA’s physical AI stack. In this session, Levatas and PG&E will show how Cosmos Reason VLM, Metropolis, Blueprint for Video Search and Summarization (VSS), and synthetic data generation are being leveraged to transform reactive grid maintenance into predictive, automated asset inspection at national scale. 

Learn how PG&E is beginning to deliver rapid, high-accuracy assessments like thermal anomaly detection, segmentation-based corrosion detection, and analog gauge reading at 99% accuracy, while accelerating post-storm recovery and reducing downtime through automated, geo-tagged, time-stamped asset condition reporting.

speakers.name: Chris Nielsen
speakers.bio: Chris Nielsen, the founder and CEO of Levatas, is a trailblazer in the field of AI and robotics. Under his leadership, Levatas has become a leading company in developing and implementing AI-driven solutions and advanced robotic systems. Chris’ expertise and vision have propelled the company to the forefront of technological innovation, making him an ideal speaker to discuss the future of AI and machine learning.

speakers.name: Kellen Kirk
speakers.bio: Kellen Kirk, currently the senior manager for Aviation Operations at PG&E, is a former U.S. Navy aircraft mechanic and commercially rated airplane pilot, and helped ramp up PG&E’s drone inspection program starting in 2019.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: From Data to Meaning: Vision-Language Models Shaping the Cities of Tomorrow
sessions.abstract:
In this session, we’ll explore how K2K is applying data-compliant Vision-Language Models (VLMs) integrated with NVIDIA Cosmos Reason and Metropolis Blueprint for Video Search and Summarization (VSS) to turn passive footage into proactive decision-making.
We’ll walk through how VLM-driven understanding enables cities to automate operational processes, anticipate incidents, and optimize resource allocation through granular semantic insight rather than traditional detection alone. We’ll also share how generated data, synthetic data and simulation pipelines are being used to scale robust urban AI without exposing personal identity data.
The result: AI agents that don’t just “see,” but interpret, reason, and support real-time city operations in a trustworthy way.

speakers.name: Giuditta Zardoni
speakers.bio: Giuditta Zardoni, executive manager at K2K, combines expertise in economics and management with a specialization in sustainability and circular bioeconomy. Specializing in neural network architectures and video generative AI, she has a strategic role in coordinating and leveraging cutting-edge video AI technologies.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: Decoding Quantum Errors With AI
sessions.abstract: Learn the basics of quantum error correction (QEC) and why decoding is a primary bottleneck for quantum computing. Using hands-on exercises, learn about key decoder metrics like accuracy, scalability, throughput, and latency and their implications for quantum computation. Lastly, implement your own AI decoder workflow using the CUDA-Q QEC library. We assume that you're familiar with the fundamentals of quantum computing (gates, circuits, measurement, etc.), but do not necessarily have experience with QEC.

speakers.name: Monica Van-Dieren
speakers.bio: Monica leads the CUDA-Q Academic initiative at NVIDIA. Previously, she created quantum training materials for IBM. She holds a Ph.D. in mathematical sciences from Carnegie Mellon University. Monica has over 20 years of academic experience at institutions like Stanford, the University of Michigan, and Robert Morris University. She has taught mathematics, launched an interdisciplinary honors program, organized STEM outreach activities, and conducted both theoretical research in logic and pedagogical research in educational technology. Monica is passionate about bridging the gap between classical and quantum computing, enabling practical solutions for complex real-world problems.

speakers.name: Mark Wolf
speakers.bio: Mark Wolf serves as a technical marketing engineer on NVIDIA's quantum computing team, where he focuses on bridging the gap between advanced research and user education. Mark works closely with researchers at NVIDIA and across the quantum ecosystem to develop CUDA-Q Academic content. Previously, Mark worked at Quantinuum, and holds a Ph.D. in physical chemistry from the Center for Computational Quantum Chemistry at the University of Georgia.

speakers.name: Justin Lietz
speakers.bio: Justin Lietz is a senior quantum computing software architect on the NVIDIA HPC and Quantum Computing team. He is focused on quantum error correction and integrating quantum computers with classical HPC systems. Prior to NVIDIA, Justin was on the research staff at the National Center for Computational Sciences at Oak Ridge National Laboratory. Justin received his Ph.D. in computational nuclear physics in 2019 from Michigan State University.

sessions.title: Achieving 8x Lower Cost Analytics with GPU-Accelerated DuckDB
sessions.abstract: DuckDB has become the analytical engine of choice everywhere—from notebooks and embedded applications to production data workflows. At the same time GPUs have rapidly evolved into powerful and cost-efficient engines for general-purpose parallel compute. Sirius brings these two trends together by enabling GPU-native execution for DuckDB—without requiring users to change how they write queries. In this session, we’ll explore how Sirius offloads DuckDB workloads to GPUs, accelerating analytics by up to 8x at the same hardware rental cost. Learn how this new architecture combines DuckDB’s simplicity with the power of GPU compute, unlocking faster, more cost-efficient interactive analytics while preserving the elegance of a single-node engine.

speakers.name: Bobbi Yogatama
speakers.bio: Bobbi Yogatama works on GPU-accelerated data processing at NVIDIA, where he is a core contributor to Sirius. Bobbi also holds a Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Wisconsin–Madison, where he worked on GPU databases with Prof. Xiangyao Yu.

speakers.name: Xiangyao Yu
speakers.bio: Xiangyao Yu's research interests include cloud-native databases, new hardware for databases, and core database techniques in both transaction and analytical processing. Before joining UW-Madison, he finished his postdoc and Ph.D. at MIT. Xiangyao received the NSF CAREER Award and an MIT Sloan Research Fellowship.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries

sessions.title: Transforming Industrial Ops: Real-Time Forecasting and Anomaly Detection With Foundational Time-Series Models at Scale
sessions.abstract:
Bringing AI into the physical world is a massive challenge. Industrial environments are complex and noisy, yet businesses demand accurate forecasts from thousands of assets.

Join NVIDIA and Cognite to see how we solve this. We’ll take a deep dive into the NVIDIA NV-Tesseract family of models and Cognite Data Fusion, focusing on how to deploy foundational time series models in the industrial world to identify process deviations, and potential reliability issues. You'll learn:

•	How to integrate NV-Tesseract with Cognite’s Industrial Knowledge Graph to scale across thousands of sensors.
•	Real-world case studies of customers applying these capabilities in production.
•	Benchmarks showing how GPU-accelerated analysis delivers faster insights.
•	Discover how to reduce costs, prevent disruptions, and free your teams from manual firefighting to focus on high-value innovation.

speakers.name: Peet Cremer
speakers.bio: Peet Cremer is a principal AI architect at Cognite and a Ph.D. physicist with over a decade of experience bridging theoretical modeling and scalable AI. Co-creator of eight patents in industrial engineering, he specializes in deploying mission-critical systems—ranging from edge-based sensor fusion to large-scale industrial knowledge graphs and information retrieval.

speakers.name: Geir K. Engdahl
speakers.bio: Geir Engdahl is Cognite's Chief Technology Officer and co-founder who is responsible for driving Cognite's continued leadership in the industrial AI space. In 2011, Engdahl founded Snapsale, a machine learning classifieds startup. He served as CEO and CTO until Schibsted acquired the company in 2017. Before that, he spent three years working as a senior software engineer for Google in Canada. Engdahl has a master's degree in computational science from the University of Oslo.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Shatter the Memory Wall: Composable Building Blocks for Massive Scale Analytics
sessions.abstract: As GPU-accelerated analytics scale to terabytes and beyond, memory management and observability become critical infrastructure. We introduce a composable, engine-agnostic approach to shattering GPU memory limits and understanding query-level resource consumption. We'll deep-dive into cuCascade, a library for memory reservation and topology discovery that prevents out-of-memory failures by gracefully spilling data between GPU, host, and disk memory tiers. We'll also introduce a semantic telemetry layer for always-on profiling, enabling developers to visualize query plans and resource throughput across GPUs in real time. We demonstrate both tools working together inside Sirius, NVIDIA's GPU-native analytics engine, showing real telemetry output and memory tier management on live workloads. Learn how these composable building blocks help engine developers identify bandwidth bottlenecks, optimize memory utilization, and push toward speed-of-light analytics performance.

speakers.name: Felipe Aramburu
speakers.bio: Felipe has been working on accelerated SQL engines and CUDA computational primitives for over a decade. At NVIDIA, he works on accelerating hardware-accelerated execution engines with a focus on larger-than-memory problems, distributed computing, and working on components that can be leveraged by other libraries and engine builders.

speakers.name: Rodrigo Aramburu
speakers.bio: Rodrigo Aramburu leads developer relations for accelerated data processing at NVIDIA, helping developers adopt and innovate with GPU-accelerated analytics. Previously, he co-founded BlazingSQL and Voltron Data, contributing to the RAPIDS ecosystem and advancing Arrow-native data systems.

sessions.title: Charting a Course for the Next Decade of Gaming With AI
sessions.abstract: AI is fundamentally transforming real-time applications, reshaping both content creation and player experiences. Join John Spitzer, VP of developer technology and performance at NVIDIA, for an industry panel featuring AI leaders from Creative Assembly, Decart, RIOT Games and Krafton. These panelists will explore the current state of the art—from AI digital humans to generating entire worlds instantaneously—and offer their vision for the future of AI-powered gaming.

speakers.name: John Spitzer
speakers.bio: As vice president of developer and performance technology at NVIDIA, John directs the group that helps game developers make the most out of GeForce GPUs. Prior to his current role, John led development of NVIDIA's gaming platform, GeForce Experience, and in 2003 founded NVIDIA's office in Moscow, which now has over 300 employees. He holds bachelor's and master's degrees in computer science from Rice University.

speakers.name: Duygu Cakmak
speakers.bio: Duygu Cakmak is the R&D director at Creative Assembly, where she leads the studio’s research and AI strategy. She actively contributes to the wider game AI community in such ways as co-organizing Europe's largest AI and games developer conference in London, advising the GDC AI Summit, and chairing the IGGI industry board. She also collaborates closely with academia and research organizations to advance innovation in AI and games.

speakers.name: Kangwook Lee
speakers.bio: Kangwook oversees KRAFTON AI's entire AI R&D efforts, including AI for next-generation video games and game development. Previously, he was an associate professor in the departments of Electrical and Computer Engineering and Computer Sciences (by courtesy) at the University of Wisconsin-Madison. He received his Ph.D. in 2016 from UC Berkeley. He is the recipient of the NSF CAREER Award, Amazon Research Award, the KSEA Young Investigator Grant Award, and multiple best papers awards. His professional service includes serving as area chair for NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, and COLM, on the Program Committee for MLSys, and as an action editor for Transactions on Machine Learning Research.

speakers.name: Dean Leitersdorf
speakers.bio:
Dean grew up between Israel, Switzerland, and Silicon Valley. He completed his Ph.D. at the Technion at age 23 while serving in Unit 8200, then completed his postdoc at NUS Singapore. In 2023, Dean won the ACM PODC Dissertation Award for the best Ph.D. in distributed computing worldwide. Additional awards include three best student paper awards at PODC and the Israel Defense Prize from the IDF.

Dean currently serves as CEO of Decart, a full-stack AI research lab he founded in 2023 with Moshe Shalev. Decart aims to become the leading consumer AI company by helping users transform their imagination into visual reality, blending interactive, generative AI experiences into everyday life.

speakers.name: Trotter Cashion
speakers.bio: Trotter Cashion is the Head of AI Product at Riot Games, where he leads the studio's global AI strategy. A Riot veteran of 15+ years, Trotter has touched nearly every game in the company’s history, including a recent tenure leading the VALORANT core technology team. He holds a bachelor’s degree from Emory University and an MBA from the Kellogg School of Management.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - 3D Model Generation

sessions.title: Interactive Digital Twin for Fusion With Integrated Sensors and Agentic AI
sessions.abstract: The session will describe the development of the digital twin for the DIII-D Experimental Fusion Reactor. The talk will describe the development of the AI models and live sensor data integrated with the CAD data to develop an interactive digital twin.

speakers.name: Raffi Nazikian
speakers.bio: Raffi M. Nazikian is a physicist known for his contributions to nuclear fusion research and plasma physics. He has been associated with the Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory and has conducted significant work at the DIII-D National Fusion Facility.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: 多模态大模型训练的性能优化 Performance optimization for training Multimodal Large Language Models
sessions.abstract:
与大语言模型相比，多模态大模型（MLLM）能够处理文本、图像和音频等多种类型的数据，更接近人类对世界的感知方式。然而，不同模态之间的数据和模型结构存在差异，这使得传统的模型训练方式难以高效地训练多模态大模型。DistTrain 通过解耦编码器、主干网络和生成器的训练并行策略，实现了更高的训练效率。NVIDIA 与 Stepfun 合作，在开源框架 Megatron-LM 上实现了 DistTrain 方案，使社区能够更轻松且高效地训练多模态模型。
Multimodal large language models (LLMs) can process and generate different types of data, such as text, images, and audio.
However, combining these modalities creates challenges for model training due to differences in their data and model structures.
DistTrain introduced optimizations that separate the training parallelism of different model components (encoders, backbone, and generators) and has achieved better training efficiency.
NVIDIA and Stepfun have collaborated to implement the DistTrain solution on the open-source framework Megatron-LM, making it easier for the community to train their own multimodal models with optimized efficiency.

speakers.name: 亦博 朱
speakers.bio: 朱亦博博士是阶跃星辰（StepFun）的联合创始人兼首席技术官，负责领导AI基础设施方向的工作。他的过往工作主要集中在AI系统、高性能网络和分布式系统领域，包括DistServe（最早的PD分离推理方案之一）、BytePS（高性能参数服务器）、DCQCN（经典的RoCE拥塞控制协议）等。在加入阶跃星辰之前，朱亦博博士曾在谷歌、字节跳动和微软担任技术总监、经理和研究员等职位。他拥有加州大学圣芭芭拉分校的博士学位，以及清华大学的学士学位。

speakers.name: Shifang Xu
speakers.bio: 许士芳博士是英伟达（NVIDIA）的一名高级工程师。她毕业于浙江大学，在深度学习算法和框架工程实践方面拥有多年的经验。目前，她专注于提升多模态大模型训练的训练效率和可扩展性。

sessions.title: オールフォトニクスネットワークとNVIDIA DynamoによるAIエージェント向けプラットフォームの最適化手法
sessions.abstract: 参加者は、Dynamo と LMCache、NIXLを用いて 1,000 km 超にわたり KV キャッシュを共有し、分散データセンター間で大規模言語モデル（LLM）推論をスケールさせる方法を学びます。複数サイトの GPU クラスタや LLM サービスを運用する参加者にとって、本セッションは、分散データセンター間のサイロを解消しつつ、低遅延かつ省電力なサイト間推論を実現する実践的なアーキテクチャを提示する点で重要です。

speakers.name: Kota Tsuyuzaki
speakers.bio: 2010年 日本電信電話株式会社に入社、分散オブジェクトストレージOpenStack Swiftの研究開発に従事し、コアデベロッパーとしてOSS開発を主導。2018年より、AI計算基盤の開発プロジェクトに参画し、構築、運用をリード。2023年NTTドコモビジネス株式会社(旧NTTコミュニケーションズ株式会社)に入社し、ハイブリッドクラウド、データマネジメントプロジェクト、及び、LLM をはじめとした生成 AI 関連の技術検証に携わる。

sessions.title: Augmenting Industrial Operations with Factory Playback Intelligence
sessions.abstract:
Manufacturers have more operational data than ever. When something goes wrong on the line, data alone still only tell us what happened, not why. In this session, Tulip shares how Factory Playback, powered by NVIDIA Blueprint for video search and summarization, creates a new standard for operational truth by fusing synchronized multi-camera video with real-time manufacturing data.

Learn how Tulip has deployed solutions with Terex, a global manufacturer of lifting and material processing equipment. We’ll cover Factory Playback solutions for creating a searchable "time machine" across production and warehouse operations and associated ROI. By synchronizing operational events with multi-camera video, data, and workflows, teams gain verifiable visual context to enforce standard operating procedures (SOPs), accelerate root-cause analysis, and automate responses to process deviations.

speakers.name: Kenneth McIntosh
speakers.bio: Ken McIntosh is senior director of digital manufacturing for Terex. He is passionate about driving digital transformation in manufacturing by uniting strategy, technology, and innovation to accelerate growth. Since joining Terex in 2019, he has leveraged more than 20 years of IT experience across industrial, medical device, and aerospace sectors to deliver scalable, data-driven solutions.

speakers.name: Rony Kubat
speakers.bio: Rony Kubat is the co-founder, CIO, and CISO of Tulip Interfaces. He was the first employee at Bluefin (acquired by Twitter). He is a playwright, is a member of the Junkyard Wars team and the Geeks (third place, U.S. season three), and previously served as a science and technology advisor for Hollywood film productions. Rony is a Ph.D. graduate from MIT with a research focus on applied machine learning.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: ファインチューニングによる金融領域に特化したVertical LLMの実現
sessions.abstract: 楽天では、かねてより多様な領域でのAIの実装・活用を進めてきました。近年は、オープンかつ高性能なLLM開発・公開だけでなく、Vertical AI開発にも注力しており、特定の分野に特化したVertical LLMのファインチューニングに取り組んでいます。今回は、金融機関に不可欠な機密性・安全性を確保しつつ、従来モデルでは対応が難しかった金融関連タスクを実現するためのモデル開発における、設計思想や学習データ選定、開発プロセスについて紹介します。

speakers.name: 柴田 祥
speakers.bio: 外資系保険会社でアプリケーションエンジニアとして従事。第二次AIブーム期に注目されたPrologを組み込んだ保険契約管理システムの開発を担当し、AI技術の実装経験を積む。その後、Cheif Strategy Officer配下でDXプロジェクト・ポートフォリオ構築・管理をリードし、技術活用の戦略的視点を強化。資産運用業界向けFinTechスタートアップに転職後、プロダクト戦略と開発責任者として証券会社・銀行・運用会社向けに革新的なSaaSプロダクトを提供、金融DXを推進。2020年より楽天に参画し、楽天グループ内の金融各社におけるAI・データ活用を牽引。モデル開発から業務適用まで、金融領域でのAI実装をリード。

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)

sessions.title: 次世代GPU基盤の実力とサービス価値 NVIDIA HGX B300とNVIDIA Spectrum-Xがもたらす変化
sessions.abstract:
本セッションでは、GMO GPUクラウドに新しく導入したHGX B300とSpectrum-Xを組み合わせた次世代GPU基盤について、どれだけ性能が変わったのかをベンチマークを通じて分かりやすくお伝えします。
また、こうした性能向上が、学習時間の短縮やコストの抑制、安定したGPUの提供といった形で、実際にサービスをご利用いただく皆さまにどんなメリットを生むのか、という点にも触れたいと思います。
技術的な進化とサービス価値の両面から、次世代GPUクラウドの可能性を共有します。

speakers.name: 多田 善樹
speakers.bio: 2022年にGMOインターネットグループ株式会社へ新卒入社。個人向けの画像推論基盤の開発に携わった後、2023年4月よりGPUクラウドサービスの開発に従事。現在では大規模GPUインフラの設計・構築・運用を担当。

sessions.title: The Case for Block-Based Programming With cuTile and Triton for Image Processing Workloads Used in Semi Manufacturing
sessions.abstract:
Deploying image processing workloads on NVIDIA GPUs at near speed-of-light performance has required hand-tuning kernels using CUDA C++, given the unique nature of the loads used in semiconductor manufacturing. However, maintaining and debugging CUDA C++ code is challenging, often requiring significant re-tuning when adopting new GPU architectures. Tile-based programming, enabled by domain-specific languages (DSLs) such as NVIDIA cuTile and OpenAI Triton, offers a more portable and maintainable alternative while achieving performance close to hand-tuned CUDA C++.

We'll evaluate tile-based programming for image processing workloads used in semiconductor manufacturing, comparing development effort and performance when porting operations from CUDA C++ to cuTile and Triton. We share insights from implementing GPU-accelerated algorithms, debugging, and optimizing performance in these DSLs.

speakers.name: Pradeep Ramachandran
speakers.bio: Pradeep Ramachandran is the director and head of research at KLA’s Artificial Intelligence and Advanced Computing Lab (ACL) at IIT Madras Research Park. He holds a B.Tech. from IIT Madras and an M.S. and Ph.D. from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign. His work focuses on system-level optimization across algorithms, hardware, and software, spanning fault tolerance, chip design, video compression, and HPC. He enjoys cooking, travel, running, and spending time with his 6-year-old son.

sessions.title: VLM・VSSによる現場の安全性向上と作業効率向上の実現
sessions.abstract: 本セッションでは、現場の安全性確保と業務効率向上を同時に実現するため、視覚言語モデル(VLM)・要約技術（VSS）を活用した最新のアプローチを探求します。ウェアラブルデバイスを用いて、危険な動作や手順ミスのリアルタイム検知から技能継承に至るまで、現場の課題に対する具体的な技術手法を深く掘り下げます。

speakers.name: Tatsunori Ohara
speakers.bio: サービスロボットのロボットビジョン解析技術からスタートし、画像処理・AIを活用したビル内監視ソリューション開発に従事。現在はその技術応用として、現場作業における安全性向上と作業効率化を実現するAIソリューション開発に従事。

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: NVIDIA Cosmosを活用した自動運転向けデータセット基盤の実装事例
sessions.abstract:
本講演にご参加いただくことで、最先端の自動運転AI開発に必須となる、NVIDIA Cosmosを活用した高性能データセット基盤の具体的な構築ノウハウと実装事例を習得できます。 今日の自動運転AIの性能向上のボトルネックとなっている、大規模データの効率的な管理手法や、AIのロバスト性を左右する収集困難なエッジケースデータの生成・補完技術を深く理解し、皆様のプロジェクトにおける開発コストの削減とAIの実用性の向上に直結する知見を得ることが重要です。 具体的には、Cosmosの機能とAutolabeling基盤を連携させることで、実世界での収集が難しいロングテールデータを効率的に補完しつつ、ラベル付きのエッジケースデータが同時に得られる手法を解説します。
1. Cosmos Reason：高精度なデータ検索とフィルタリング
2. Cosmos Transfer：多様な仮想シーンの生成
3. Cosmos Predict：未知のシナリオの補完と拡張
これらの技術を組み合わせることで、より現実的で高性能な自動運転AIを実現するためのデータセット基盤構築の実践的な知識を提供します。

speakers.name: 梅田 弾
speakers.bio: 2015年早稲田大学基幹理工研究科情報理工専攻博士後期課程修了。早稲田大学の助手として並列コンパイラの研究に携わり、その後、電気メーカー研究所でビッグデータ可視化の研究、自動車メーカーで自動運転AIの研究開発に従事。2023年より株式会社ティアフォーにて、Low Power AIを目指したAI実装技術やActive Learningを通じたスケール可能な大規模データセットに関する研究開発に従事。早稲田大学 招聘研究員を兼務。

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: Europe’s AI Launchpad: Unlock Startup Growth Through Sovereign AI Infrastructure
sessions.abstract: This session will explore Europe’s dynamic AI compute landscape and guide founders through leveraging both publicly funded AI factories—designed for open research—and private NCP/AI factories focused on commercial activity, using the concept of a unified digital continuum for strategic growth. Discover actionable pathways to access sovereign AI infrastructure, including a comprehensive overview of services from major players and how startups can engage with high-speed, secure NVIDIA-powered systems through tailored programs and partnerships developed for European innovators. We'll also highlight how new regional AI growth zones and an expanding network of AI factories offer localized access to advanced technology, talent, and venture funding—enabling startups to build competitive advantages rooted in European digital sovereignty and regional collaboration.

speakers.name: Cedric Auliac
speakers.bio: Cédric Auliac is a senior researcher and AI program director in France, serving as AI program manager and coordinator of AI Factory France (AI2F) at GENCI. Before that, he worked at CEA‑List, where he led research in ML, neural networks, and data-driven modeling—authoring several academic publications in those domains. At AI2F, Cédric coordinates access to high performance computing resources and supports the development of AI-driven innovation projects in industry, research, and public sectors. He's recognized in the French tech and science community for bridging advanced AI research and practical applications, advocating for responsible, transparent, and impactful uses of AI.

speakers.name: Pierre-Antoine Beaudoin
speakers.bio: Pierre-Antoine is a finance and marketing major applying new technologies and innovation. He's held several positions in marketing and PR with large multinational companies such as Lenovo, Intel, and now NVIDIA, first as embedded AI ecosystem manager for Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, and since 2018 as inception manager for South Europe and BeNeLux. Today he focuses on creating the synergies between startups, universities, research centers, and the business operations world by helping startups grow and scale their solutions from software to hardware to overall architecture. Passionate about innovation and technologies, he’s a big Star Wars fan, a longtime skier, and a father of one.

speakers.name: Sadaf Alam
speakers.bio: Sadaf drives Bristol’s frontier supercomputing capabilities as CTO at the Bristol Centre for Supercomputing, U.K. Since joining the University of Bristol in 2022, she has led transformation of research computing and data services, co-founding Isambard-AI, national federated supercomputing resources, and sustainable digital research infrastructure strategies. Previously CTO at CSCS (Switzerland), she was chief architect for multiple generations of the Piz Daint systems and the MeteoSwiss operational forecasting platforms. With a Ph.D. from Edinburgh University and experience at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Sadaf blends technical expertise with leadership in sovereign AI, HPC, and quantum.

speakers.name: Yann Lechelle
speakers.bio: Yann Lechelle is an entrepreneur & Executive President at Probabl.ai, an INRIA spinoﬀ whose mission is to distribute Open Source data science and machine learning technology globally around scikit-learn and its more than 2 billion downloads. Previously, he was CEO at Scaleway, a leading European cloud provider. Over the past decades, he has founded, grown and exited a total of 6 tech startups, including Snips.ai, a privacy-by-design voice processing deep-tech acquired by Nasdaq-listed Sonos. Yann holds an MBA from INSEAD.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: AI Factories in Europe: Building the Foundations for Scalable Intelligence
sessions.abstract:
As Europe accelerates its AI ambitions, the concept of “AI factories” has become central to scaling innovation and production. This panel examines the three critical components required for successful AI factories: a reliable and sustainable energy supply, advanced data center infrastructure, and strong customer use cases that demonstrate measurable business value.

Experts will discuss how Europe can integrate these elements to establish efficient, secure, and sovereign AI production systems. The conversation will highlight pathways to strengthen Europe’s competitiveness in the global AI landscape while aligning with sustainability goals and economic resilience.

speakers.name: Carlo Ruiz
speakers.bio: Carlo is passionate about the transformative potential of emerging and disruptive technologies, and has dedicated his career to shaping the future of AI computing. With over a decade of experience in the industry, Carlo has honed his skills and expertise to foster innovation and create lasting impact.

speakers.name: Julia Koch
speakers.bio: As chief development officer and chief customer officer, Julia Koch is responsible for the strategic direction and execution of software development, guiding her teams in delivering innovative and customer-centric banking solutions for the German Savings Banks Group, which serves over 50 million people in Germany. Her core objective is to lead her teams in shaping the future of modern banking. She’s committed to harnessing AI and other innovative technologies to enhance customer experience.

speakers.name: Imran Shafi
speakers.bio: Imran Shafi heads up the Global Affairs function at Nscale. He was formerly Director of AI Policy for the UK Government, Private Secretary to the Prime Minister at 10 Downing Street and at McKinsey & Company.

speakers.name: Eric Leandri
speakers.bio:
Eric Leandri is the CEO of Aleria and Tawasal, leading the development of sovereign AI and large scale digital infrastructure for governments and regulated enterprises.
He is the co founder and former CEO of Qwant, and brings leadership experience in building and governing digital platforms at national and institutional scale.

speakers.name: Zhaopeng Chen
speakers.bio: Zhaopeng Chen is the founder and chief executive officer of Agile Robots. Under his leadership, Agile Robots has experienced rapid global growth by combining artificial intelligence and robotics to advance physical AI for smart manufacturing. Headquartered in Munich, Agile Robots employs more than 2,500 people from 60 countries and has installed over 20,000 robotic solutions worldwide. Zhaopeng studied engineering in China and earned his PhD at the German Aerospace Center (DLR).

speakers.name: Peter Lorenz
speakers.bio:
Peter Lorenz serves on the Board of Management of T-Systems International GmbH and has headed the digital area as Senior Vice President Digital Solutions since 2019. He leads over 10,000 specialists worldwide in executing large-scale digital transformation initiatives. He is known for enabling large and medium-sized organizations to modernize platforms and implement scalable digital solutions. 
With decades of leadership experience in enterprise IT, he bridges strategy and execution in complex international environments. Driven by deep tech and innovation expertise, he continuously advances technology driven business growth. His latest project, the development of Germany’s first AI factory with Nvidia, enables T Systems to build a sovereign AI infrastructure for Germany and accelerate enterprise AI transformation, delivering large scale platforms and regulated solutions for industrial, public, and healthcare sectors through strategic partnerships and mission critical deployments.
His background includes senior roles at T-Systems, SAP, and Siemens Nixdorf, driving large-scale systems integration and innovation initiatives.

sessions.title: AI 重塑影视产业：打造基于 NVIDIA 全栈的 AI 算力池，生成式 AI工作流与 OpenUSD 资产平台
sessions.abstract:
上海电影集团成立于76年前，是中国电影产业的重要先驱者。2023年，我们迈出了关键一步——投入建设AI算力体系。
我们围绕上海电影集团昊浦智慧产业社区，构建并规模化落地一套面向影视内容生产、产业协同与生态共创的 NVIDIA 全栈智能基础设施体系。构建面向 CG、AIGC、虚拟拍摄与大模型服务的统一GPU算力池，实现跨企业、跨团队的资源池化、服务化与精细化调度；基于 NVIDIA Omniverse 与 OpenUSD 打造影视行业级数字资产平台，将场景、角色与内容资产从“项目文件”升级为“可复用、可流转、可进化的产业级生产资料”；依托 NVIDIA AI Enterprise 与 NVIDIA NIM，将AI能力深度嵌入创作、制作、交付与运营全流程，形成可复制、可审计、可规模化推广的影视AI生产体系。

speakers.name: Yue Xi
speakers.bio: 郗岳现任上影昊浦智慧产业园执行副总裁。他在中国大型影视制作基地与电影产业园区的规划、建设与数字化运营方面拥有十余年经验，曾参与青岛东方影都等国家级影视制作基地的体系化建设，对中国电影产业从传统制作模式向工业化、数字化与平台化转型发挥了重要推动作用。自2023年加入上海电影集团以来，郗岳负责上影昊浦智慧产业社区的建设和运营，并依托昊浦支撑上影集团数智赋能战略，构建面向未来影视工业的新一代生产与产业协同体系。助力上影在智能化影视制作与产业生态构建方面确立行业引领地位。

sessions.title: Accelerate AI Through Open-Source Inference
sessions.abstract: The open AI ecosystem is thriving—powered by a new wave of high-performance inference frameworks and community-driven model development. In this session, we hear from ecosystem leaders as they discuss the critical role of open models in progressing AI innovation. We’ll dive deep into the collaborative infrastructure enabling state-of-the-art generative systems—from tokenizer to transformer to GPU kernel. Whether you’re building your own language model, optimizing inference pipelines, or contributing to open AI research, this is your chance to learn about optimization breakthroughs, interoperability standards, and the future of deploying open models at scale.

speakers.name: Anu Srivastava
speakers.bio: Anu Srivastava is a senior technical marketing manager who focuses on NVIDIA’s lighthouse AI model collaborations. She works with key partners and foundations to enable NVIDIA accelerated platform support for the open-source developer ecosystem. Prior to NVIDIA, she worked at Google for over a decade in various engineering and management roles. Anu holds a degree in computer science from the University of Texas at Austin.

speakers.name: Ofir Bibi
speakers.bio: Ofir's research is in machine learning, statistical signal processing, computational photography, and content generation. He leads research teams in building systems for estimating and predicting various signals from weather, electricity consumption, and financial markets to photos, videos, and auditory signals. Currently, as director of research at Lightricks, Ofir's focused on ways to create consistent results from neural networks in resource-constrained environments.

speakers.name: Tim Dockhorn
speakers.bio: Tim Dockhorn is a co-founder of Black Forest Labs.

speakers.name: Jeff Boudier
speakers.bio: Jeff Boudier builds products at Hugging Face, the No. 1 platform for AI builders. Previously, Jeff was a co-founder of Stupeflix, acquired by GoPro, where he served as director of product management, product marketing, business development, and corporate development.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Inside the NVIDIA AI Platform and Ecosystem
sessions.abstract: A deeper dive into key announcements from the keynote with a focus on enabling technologies, predicted effects, and opportunities across industries.

speakers.name: Ian Buck
speakers.bio: Ian is the vice president and general manager of NVIDIA's Accelerated Computing Data Center business and the inventor of CUDA, which is the established standard for GPU computing worldwide. CUDA powers the deep learning AI revolution used by Google, Facebook, Amazon, Alibaba, IBM, and others.

sessions.title: Neural Physics for AI-Powered Differentiable PDE-Solvers and Digital Twins
sessions.abstract: AI has revolutionized surrogate models in the field of computational physics. However, in this session we take a look at how AI tools can benefit physics-based modeling. We will introduce the neural physics approach, which writes multi-grid solvers as convolutional neural networks whose weights are set from numerical theory, rather than by training. We give demonstrations that indicate the breadth of application possible from complex environmental problems to industrial problems; demonstrate the scalability of the approach; describe the potential for optimization tasks; and discuss future possibilities of combining physics-based models (untrained neural networks from neural physics) with surrogate models (trained neural networks).

speakers.name: Christopher Pain
speakers.bio: Prof. Christopher Pain leads the Applied Modeling and Computation Group (AMCG) at ICL. AMCG is Imperial’s largest research group, comprising about 60 scientists. He directs the data assimilation lab in the Data Science Institute at ICL and is co-director of the Centre for AI-Physics Modeling at Imperial-X. Has interests in numerical methods, AI, environmental and engineering fluids (e.g., multi-phase flows, urban flows). He developed the first large eddy simulation air pollution models, the first 3D tetrahedral-based mesh optimization method and conservative mesh-to-mesh interpolation method; he was the original developer of the FETCH transient criticality model for nuclear systems.

sessions.title: 从视觉还原到物理交互： 物理可驱动3D 资产生成技术 Beyond Visuals: A Physics-Informed Generative Method for Articulated 3D Assets
sessions.abstract:
本演讲将介绍一种将多模态输入转化为物理可驱动资产的生成式方法。我们将详细阐述如何利用 3D 大模型，从输入图像或自然语言，得到具有完整关节结构的3D资产，涵盖自动化关节估计、部件生成以及物理属性推断等技术。
This session introduces a generative method that transforms multimodal inputs into physically drivable assets. We will detail how we leverage 3D large models to transform images or natural language descriptions into fully articulated assets, featuring automated semantic joint estimation, part-aware mesh generation, and precise physical property inference.

speakers.name: Dongping Li
speakers.bio: Dongping Li is a researcher at Bytedance's AIG3D team. His research focuses on 3D reconstruction, computer vision, and 3D AI-generated content. Prior to joining Bytedance in 2025, he was the R&D Director at Faceunity from 2017-25, where he led various innovative projects. Dongping holds a Ph.D. in computer science (2017) and a B.S. in mathematics (2011), both from Zhejiang University.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - 3D Model Generation

sessions.title: 基于 DeepGEMM × FlashMLA × DeepEP 对 DeepSeek 模型在 Blackwell 的全栈深度优化
sessions.abstract: 我们持续优化并维护新一代的 DeepGEMM × FlashMLA × DeepEP 技术栈，为 Blackwell 平台上的训练和推理提供强大支持。本次报告将重点介绍围绕 DeepSeek 模型在 FlashMLA 和 DeepGEMM 上所做的一系列优化工作，其中包括提升训练与推理核函数性能的 MLA 形状相关流水线优化、针对稀疏注意力内核的优化、面向特殊 batch 分布的优化，以及在特定场景和组件上的 GEMM 相关改进，例如 MoE GEMM、反向传播和小 batch 推理等；同时，我们还将在 DeepEP 中介绍 FP8/FP4、节点内通信支持及其优化。

speakers.name: Ray Wang (HW-Comp DevTech-CN05)
speakers.bio: 王辉，来自 NVIDIA 加速计算专家团队，于2022年加入 NVIDIA。专注于 GPU 上的 kernel 优化，主要包括 GEMM kernel、Embedding cache kernel 以及 ANN kernel 的优化。

speakers.name: Zeyu WANG
speakers.bio: 王泽宇，来自 NVIDIA 加速计算专家团队，于 2024 年加入 NVIDIA，他专注于 GPU 上的 kernel 优化及推理框架优化，主要包括传统、稀疏和线性 Attention Kernel 优化。

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Deep Learning Frameworks

sessions.title: 面向灵巧操作的高效强化学习框架
sessions.abstract: 当前的具身基础模型，仍然难以在真实世界任务中同时满足接近 100% 的成功率与严格的执行周期的要求。强化学习被认为是弥合这一差距的关键技术路径，然而在灵巧操作场景中，强化学习仍面临奖励稀疏、样本效率低以及真实世界试错成本高等核心挑战。我们提出一个高效强化学习框架，它建立在一个大一统的具身基础模型之上，不仅实现了视觉和语言的理解，在同一架构中同时也建模图像生成、动作生成以及价值生成。这样的一个统一模型范式带来了多重好处，它结合了VLA和世界模型的能力，通过语言、2D视觉、3D结构以及本体状态的时间空间思维链大幅提升了模型跨时间、跨模态的联合推理，从而整体提升了策略的泛化能力；同时也为强化学习提供了过程级的稠密信息，这些信息不仅仅包含动作的，同时也包含了视觉和语言的隐空间，显著提升了灵巧操作在真实世界中的强化学习效率。这个学习框架已经在NVIDIA Thor上实现了端侧的部署，展示了其在具身基础模型规模化落地与量产应用中的潜力。

speakers.name: 鹏 贾
speakers.bio: 贾鹏，毕业于厦门大学和马里兰大学，曾在 IBM、NVIDIA 和理想汽车任职，负责高性能计算、自动驾驶算法研发、具身智能预研等工作。目前任至简动力 CEO，专注于高上限具身智能模型的研发及高可靠机器人本体的制造与产品化。

sessions.title: Qwen3-Omni：驱动下一代智能座舱的全模态交互引擎
sessions.abstract: Qwen3-Omni 作为业界首个原生端到端全模态大模型，正重新定义智能座舱的人机交互范式。其核心突破在于将“被动响应”升级为“主动感知-理解-服务”闭环，实现真正拟人化、always online 的座舱智能体。

speakers.name: 露露 胡
speakers.bio: 目前在阿里云负责智能终端相关的算法场景，关注智能座舱、机器人、AI Phone等。

sessions.title: Agentic Systems for End-to-End Surgical Workflows
sessions.abstract:
In this presentation, we'll introduce the Surgical AI Copilot, an agentic assistant developed by NVIDIA and Orsi Academy to support robotic surgery in the operating room. The system combines MCP-based agentic control with NVIDIA Holoscan, enabling low-latency video processing, streaming, and real-time interaction with surgical workflows. This tight integration operated on a IGX optimized for healthcare application allows the Copilot to operate alongside robotic systems with the responsiveness and determinism required in safety-critical environments, while remaining invisible when not needed.
Through the lens of robotic surgery, this session highlights how agentic AI and fluent human–machine interaction can transform the operating room into a more intuitive, adaptive environment. One where AI augments surgical expertise without introducing friction, complexity, or distraction.

speakers.name: Pieter De Backer
speakers.bio: Pieter De Backer leads the Surgical AI group at Orsi Academy, one of the world's largest robotic surgery training centers, located in Belgium. His team is building OROS, a realtime intra-operative agentic framework to objectively assess and improve surgical team performance. Pieter is both a robotic surgeon and an engineer who obtained a Ph.D. in medical sciences and biomedical engineering on the topic of AI for robotic surgery.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Speech Generation

sessions.title: Powering Research: Real Academic Use Cases With NVIDIA Accelerated Computing and AI
sessions.abstract:
Effective use of AI, accelerated computing, and advanced infrastructure is essential to drive research innovation and scientific breakthroughs. Researchers need to focus on sustainability, funding approaches, and strategic collaborations to maximize the impact of their work and fully leverage NVIDIA technologies.
  
This panel will provide successful research use cases of accelerated computing, showcasing how to better advocate and utilize research infrastructure available at academic institutions.

speakers.name: Tomasz Bednarz
speakers.bio: Tomasz's team at NVIDIA builds active collaborations with top researchers and premier research institutions that do compelling, computationally intense work to solve some of the world’s most challenging scientific problems.

speakers.name: Mirco Musolesi
speakers.bio: Mirco Musolesi is Professor of Computer Science at the Department of Computer Science at University College London, where he leads the Machine Intelligence Lab. He is also Professor of Computer Science at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at the University of Bologna, Italy. Previously, he held research and teaching positions at Dartmouth, Cambridge, St Andrews, and Birmingham. He has broad research interests spanning several traditional and emerging areas of Computer Science and beyond.

speakers.name: Tomasz Trzciński
speakers.bio: Tomasz Trzciński (D.Sc., WUT '20; Ph.D., EPFL '14; M.Sc., UPC/PoliTo '10) is a full professor at Warsaw University of Technology, where he leads a Computer Vision Lab. He frequently serves as a reviewer and area chair in major computer science conferences (CVPR, ICCV, ECCV, NeurIPS, ICML) and journals (TPAMI, IJCV, CVIU). He is a senior member of IEEE, fellow of ELLIS Society and director of ELLIS Unit Warsaw, member of the ALICE Collaboration at CERN, and an expert of National Science Centre and Foundation for Polish Science. Tomasz is also a chief scientist at Tooploox and a co-founder of Comixify, a technology startup focused on using machine learning algorithms for video editing.

speakers.name: Andy Cooper
speakers.bio: Andy is a Royal Society Research Professor (2023) and the Academic Director of the Materials Innovation Factory (MIF) in Liverpool. He is also the Director of the £10 M Leverhulme Centre for Functional Materials Design. In 2007, he was the founding Director of the Centre for Materials Discovery—the forerunner of the MIF—which was one of the first academic facilities worldwide for materials research using automation and robotics. He was elected to the Royal Society in 2015. He is Co-Director of the EPSRC AI for Chemistry (AIchemy) Hub. He is also the Editor in Chief for Chemical Science.

speakers.name: Oya Celiktutan
speakers.bio:
Oya Celiktutan is a reader in AI and robotics at the Centre for Robotics Research in the Department of Engineering, and leads the Social AI & Robotics Laboratory. Oya’s research focuses on multi-modal machine learning to develop autonomous agents, such as robots and virtual agents, capable of seamlessly interacting with humans. This encompasses tackling challenges in multi-modal perception, understanding and forecasting human behavior, and advancing the navigation, manipulation, and social awareness skills of these agents. Her work has been supported by EPSRC, The Royal Society, and the EU Horizon, as well as through industrial collaborations. She received the EPSRC New Investigator Award in 2020. Her team’s research has been recognized with several awards, including the Best Paper Award at IEEE Ro-Man 2022, NVIDIA CCS Best Student Paper Award Runner Up at IEEE FG 2021, First Place Award and Honorable Mention Award at ICCV UDIVA Challenge 2021.

Oya received a B.Sc. in electronics engineering from Uludag University, and an M.Sc. and Ph.D. in electrical and electronics engineering from Bogazici University, Turkiye. During her doctoral studies, Oya was a visiting researcher at the National Institute of Applied Sciences of Lyon, France. After completing her Ph.D., she moved to the United Kingdom and worked on several projects as a postdoctoral researcher at Queen Mary University London, the University of Cambridge, and Imperial College London, respectively.

sessions.title: 利用 NVIDIA Dynamo Router 的 LongCat-Flash 高效推理案例研究：面向智能体时代的模型-系统协同设计
sessions.abstract:
聚焦 LongCat-Flash 560B MoE 模型的系统级协同设计实践，展示如何实现面向 Agent 场景的极低延时与高性价比推理。在多层次并行调度与动态算子融合的支撑下，LongCat-Flash 在单用户 100 TPS 的高并发场景中，以每百万输出 token 仅 0.7 美元 的成本，达成吞吐与成本的最优平衡。
该架构通过结构相关的算子编排策略，充分调度 NVLink 高速互联、NIC 网络接口卡、GPU 计算单元及内存带宽 等异构资源，构建高吞吐、低延时的推理系统。依托 NVIDIA Dynamo router 等关键组件，系统在生产环境中实现高可用性与动态容错能力，稳态维持推理服务的 SLA 一致性，为智能体时代的大规模推理服务提供可落地的通用范式。

speakers.name: yulei qian
speakers.bio: 美团研发工程师。在 LLM、多模态、搜索、推荐、广告等场景的大规模机器学习方向有比较深的经验。主导 LongCat-Flash 的 Efficient 设计和部署落地

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: AI 与工业的融合：探索工业具身智能新路径
sessions.abstract: 我们开发了与英伟达AI生态深度融合的工业具身智能控制平台。该平台基于云边端架构，并采用虚拟化和容器化技术，使得工业控制不再局限于特定硬件和操作系统，进而可根据 AI 算力需求部署到不同的硬件设施上，加速推动了 NVIDIA AI 生态与工业的融合。基于该平台，我们开发了一套工业具身智能机器人拆码垛工作站，并已批量交付给客户，以此验证了上述技术方案的可行性和可落地性。

speakers.name: Xianglin Mao
speakers.bio: 思摩尔创始人，15 年工业控制器产品和市场销售经验

speakers.name: 晓辉 张
speakers.bio: 中国科学院博士，20 年工业控制产品研发和产业化落地经验

sessions.title: Inside Resilient AI Factories: How Predictive Analytics and IST are Driving Next-Level Data Center Reliability
sessions.abstract: AI factories are transforming AI workloads, but maintaining uptime in these GPU-powered environments is a critical challenge—where even a single fault can disrupt weeks of progress and cost millions. Learn how NVIDIA’s advanced fault detection, predictive analytics, and innovative In-System Test (IST) facilitate rapid diagnostics, enabling organizations to quickly identify and resolve issues. By minimizing downtime and preventing costly interruptions, these technologies drive a significant reduction in total cost of ownership and deliver improved return on investment. This session will highlight how automated recovery and hierarchical diagnostics empower organizations to deploy and scale AI infrastructure with confidence and efficiency.

speakers.name: Mahmut Yilmaz
speakers.bio: Mahmut Yilmaz is a director at NVIDIA, leading the In-System Test architecture and design team. With a Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from Duke University, he brings 20 years of industry experience in design-for-test. His work focuses on product quality, data center reliability, and uncovering advanced process node defects in complex silicon. Mahmut holds numerous patents in test architecture and design and has published many peer-reviewed papers in top journals and conferences.

speakers.name: Shantanu Sarangi
speakers.bio: Shantanu manages the DFT Architecture organization, defining the design-for-test (DFT) strategy across all NVIDIA products, from GPUs to system-on-chips. Drawing on 20 years of industry experience and an M.S. in electrical and computer engineering, he focuses on maximizing system resiliency and ensuring world-class outgoing quality. His team drives crucial initiatives, including NVIDIA system life-cycle management, DFX analytics, next-generation test access architectures, and high-reliability in-system testing for demanding applications in data centers and autonomous vehicles. Shantanu holds multiple patents and is a published author, presenting his innovative research at major conferences.

sessions.title: Scaling Immersive Experiences: From Design Collaboration to Gaming With Apple Vision Pro and NVIDIA CloudXR
sessions.abstract: Learn how to stream the world's most demanding RTX-powered applications directly to Apple Vision Pro with NVIDIA CloudXR. This technical session outlines how NVIDIA RTX and Apple Vision Pro enable 4K fidelity for high-end gaming, industrial design reviews, immersive training simulations, and more. From game developers who want to stream rich interactive experiences to enterprise teams who work with ultra-realistic digital twins, dive into the new and improved ways CloudXR with visionOS unlocks unparalleled potential.
