speakers.name: Teng Wei
speakers.bio:
Teng Wei is a principal software engineer at NVIDIA specializing in spatial streaming for extended reality (XR). He works with customers and partners to provide XR solutions and leads development for the NVIDIA CloudXR SDK, a platform accelerated by GPUs that delivers high-fidelity XR experiences to lightweight devices. 

Previously, Teng served as a staff software engineer at Google. He architected Google Distributed Cloud and designed systems for Daydream that focused on low-latency streaming. Teng holds a Ph.D. from the University of Wisconsin Madison and possesses an experienced research background in wireless networking and mobile computing.

speakers.name: Matt Stern
speakers.bio: Matt leads strategy and partnerships for enterprise and interactive experiences within the Vision Products Group at Apple. Formerly, he was co-founder of Mira, bringing immersive technology to everything from industrial manufacturing to theme park rides. As a lifelong spatial computing enthusiast, Matt has always advocated for focusing on the value creation, be it within the home or the workplace.

sessions.title: From Signal to Strategy: Unlock Alpha With AI-Powered Research and Trading
sessions.abstract:
In today's real-time markets, every millisecond and every signal matters. Financial firms are overwhelmed by the scale and variety of data, from ultra-low-latency market feeds to an explosion of unstructured and alternative content such as news, filings, research, and transcripts. Traditional research and trading stacks struggle to turn these data into production-grade strategies fast enough and with the accuracy needed to generate sustainable alpha.

By fusing precise quantitative time-series data with unstructured intelligence and running it on NVIDIA GPUs, the bank can build domain-accurate models, reduce hallucinations through time-aware retrieval and vector search, and safely deploy agentic AI that supports quants, researchers, and traders in real time; shortening the path from research to live deployment, and improving productivity through research copilots and execution agents.

speakers.name: Ashok Reddy
speakers.bio: Ashok Reddy, CEO of KX, is a leader in high-performance AI software, including time-series, vector databases, and temporal LLMs with 20-plus years of experience driving growth for Fortune 500 and PE-backed tech companies, including IBM, CA Technologies, Broadcom, and Digital.AI. He is recognized as a visionary and innovative leader, and for creating new market categories.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: 高速公路数字化转型——基于云边协同的 VLM 大模型行业长尾场景落地
sessions.abstract: 在本次演讲中，我们主要探讨高速公路数字化转型的商业背景及技术挑战。基于我们对智能交通领域的深入积累，我们采用了云边协同的架构，在控制成本的同时，显著提升了系统性能。通过紧耦合的软-硬件协同设计，我们在特殊交通场景下提升了系统的视觉感知与视频理解能力。此外，我们还针对特定交通场景，对 VLM 模型进行了 finetune，使其更好地融入现有系统，从而提高了系统的准确性与稳定性。

speakers.name: 明旭 刘
speakers.bio: 刘明旭，万集科技算法工程师，具有5年研发经验。其主要研究领域包括自动驾驶规划与控制，以及基于视觉的技术在交通事件检测中的应用。

speakers.name: 永强 邓
speakers.bio: 邓永强，万集科技北京研究院院长，正高级工程师，曾带领团队开发了多项公司关键产品，在激光雷达、智能交通等领域拥有 20 余年研发经验。

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: 基于 NVIDIA Jetson Thor 的机器人视觉解决方案
sessions.abstract: 本演讲课程主要围绕 NVIDIA Jetson Thor 为核心，重点分享基于 Jetson Thor 的机器人视觉解决方案。并分析和讨论行业主流功能应用，并深入讨论全新的机器人视觉解决方案，行业应用和未来展望。

speakers.name: Bin Li
speakers.bio: 李斌（Nick Lee），在硬件半导体行业拥有十多年行业经验，曾任职于多家头部芯片半导体公司。主要专注视觉，机器人感知领域的应用和产业化落地。

sessions.title: NVIDIAソフトウェアとの統合により進化する富士通の自律型AIプラットフォーム
sessions.abstract:
本講演では、富士通の自律型AIプラットフォーム「Kozuchi AI プラットフォーム」に対し、NVIDIAの多様なソフトウェア（NVIDIA NeMo、NVIDIA NIM、NVIDIA Blueprints、NVIDIA Metropolis など）をどのようにマッピング・統合することで、プラットフォーム全体の機能性や拡張性、そして適用可能なユースケースのカバー範囲がどのように進化しているのかを、全体アーキテクチャと具体的な取り組みを交えて解説します。AIプラットフォームが単体技術の集合から、学習・推論・AIエージェント構築・運用までを一体で支える基盤へと進化する中、ソフトウェアスタック全体を俯瞰した設計思想と統合アプローチを理解することは、AIを実業務で活用する上で極めて重要だからです。

Kozuchi AI プラットフォームでは、富士通独自のTakaneやマルチAIエージェント技術、セキュリティ機能を中核に据えつつ、NVIDIA NeMo によるモデル学習・カスタマイズ、NIM による推論およびデプロイ環境の標準化、Blueprint を活用したエージェント構築と最適化を組み合わせることで、信頼性と運用性を兼ね備えた自律型AI基盤の実現を目指しています。この構成は性能向上にとどまらず、AIの再利用性、スケーラビリティ、ガバナンスといった実装・運用上の課題に対応するためにも不可欠です。

特に本講演では、NVIDIA AI Blueprint for video search and summarization（VSS）の取り組みに焦点を当て、映像データを起点とした自律型AIエージェントの実装と進化を紹介します。製造現場や物流、公共空間などで生成される膨大な映像データを、VSS blueprint と Metropolis を連携させて検索・要約・理解し、業務知識やルールと結び付けて判断や提案へとつなげるアプローチは、現場AIの実用化を大きく前進させるためです。

speakers.name: Kohta Nakashima
speakers.bio: 2002年に富士通研究所入社。HPCや大規模並列処理を専門とし、「富岳」など最先端計算技術の実用化を推進。現在はコンピューティング研究所所長として、AIや量子とHPC技術の融合による次世代基盤の研究開発を統括。

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: NVIDIA サービスを活用し、LLM の精度向上からフィジカル AI への応用について
sessions.abstract:
生成系 AI およびロボティクス領域では、モデル性能の向上と同時に、学習データの品質・安全性・効率性がこれまで以上に重要な競争軸となっています。
本セッションでは、NVIDIA NeMo Curator のフィルター機能を用いた高品質データの選別による学習効率化、Nemotron-Personas-Japan を活用した安全性を重視した合成データの作成、さらに NVIDIA NeMo Curator による動画データの効率的な分割・抽出を紹介します。
また、NVIDIA Cosmos を用いたロボット領域でのデータ生成を通じて、シミュレーションデータを活用した実践的な学習手法を紹介します。
LLM、VLM、Physical AI を紹介し、モデルの精度向上における、効率的な実務的アプローチを共有します。

speakers.name: Ryo Takashina
speakers.bio:
Since university, I have been interested in AI and machine learning, and natural language processing.
After graduation, I worked at AI startups and in fintech, in image-centric machine-learning models.
I also worked as a freelancer, developing AI applications using natural language and image-processing models in various industries.
Using my experience in machine-learning models and development, with a focus on data quality, I joined APTO in 2024.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: 利用 PhysicsNeMo 加速机器人中的柔性触觉传感器仿真
sessions.abstract: 我们将展示探索如何利用图神经网络 (GNN) 和 NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo 将触觉传感器建模为具有实时、高保真物理特性的可变形体，从而加速柔性仿真的计算。

speakers.name: Juana Du
speakers.bio: 负责开发并推广面向机器人行业的解决方案，帮助客户充分利用 NVIDIA 加速计算技术，重点关注物理仿真、合成数据生成和机器人学习领域。

speakers.name: Lia Liang
speakers.bio: 负责 NVIDIA 机器人领域软硬件产品的落地解决方案搭建与推进，例如 Isaac 平台全流程应用、机器人学习开发关键工作流设计，以及 Jetson、数据中心 GPU 等核心硬件的行业适配，助力中国客户高效落地 NVIDIA 机器人技术，加速业务创新。

sessions.title: DiT 视频模型推理加速新方案：兼顾精度与效率的平衡之道
sessions.abstract: 我们将深入探讨三种面向 DiT 视频模型的全新解决方案，解决 training-free 加速技术中存在的精度损失问题。随着高效、高质量的视频生成在降本增效和提升用户体验方面变得愈发迫切，本次分享希望能提供一些具有参考价值的思路与经验。

speakers.name: 康 何
speakers.bio: 将介绍 training-free 的 DiT 推理加速方案，其分享将重点聚焦于 ScalingCache 以及端到端的混合低比特（BF16、FP8、nvFP4 混合）推理工作流

speakers.name: 瑞亮 周
speakers.bio: 将介绍 FP8 融合量化、通信以及定制 GroupGemm 的技术细节，并分享 ScalingAttention 的设计，阐释稀疏注意力中精度—稀疏度（密度）曲线的价值

sessions.title: インタラクティブCFD最前線：Omniverseで体験するデジタルツインと都市シミュレーション
sessions.abstract:
本講演では、CFD（数値流体力学）シミュレーションの新たな可能性として、Ansys FluentとNVIDIA Omniverseを統合し、リアルタイムで流体解析結果を高精度かつ直感的に可視化する実践的手法を紹介します。従来のCFD解析は膨大な計算時間と静的な結果表示に制約されていましたが、GPUアクセラレーションとOmniverseの仮想環境を活用することで、エンジニアや研究者は没入型の3D空間でシミュレーションを対話的に操作し、流れ場の変化を瞬時に確認できます。これにより、設計検討や意思決定のスピードが飛躍的に向上し、複雑な流体現象をより深く理解することが可能になります。
さらに、物理ベースのCFD解析とデジタルツイン基盤を組み合わせることで、製品開発や都市計画におけるシナリオ検証が容易になり、複雑な流体挙動を直感的に共有できるため、協調設計やチーム間コミュニケーションの質が向上します。教育分野では、物理の専門知識を持たない学生が視覚化やインタラクティブな学習を通じて計算工学に親しむための効果的な導入戦略を提案し、コンピュータサイエンス教育における新しい学習モデルを提示します。
加えて、東京工科大学のDGX B200スーパーコンピュータ「青嵐」を用いたGPU加速CFDによる東京都区内大規模流体解析の事例を紹介します。都市スケールのシミュレーションでは、膨大なメッシュデータ、境界条件の設定、並列計算の最適化など、技術的課題が山積しています。本講演では、これらの課題を克服するための最新手法と、国土交通省が主導するプロジェクト、PLATEAUのデータを使った都市環境における風況解析を現実応用へ拡張する際の将来展望についても考察します。

speakers.name: Soichiro Ikuno
speakers.bio:
筑波大学大学院工学研究科博士後期課程修了（1999年3月）
東京工科大学工学部（1999年4月〜2003年3月）
東京工科大学コンピュータサイエンス学部（2003年4月〜現在）
東京工科大学デジタルツインセンター長（2023年〜現在）

speakers.name: 藤井 明
speakers.bio: アンシス社（2025年よりシノプシス社のS&A部門）のプリンシパルアプリケーションエンジニアとして、製造業分野におけるデジタルツインとAIに関するソリューション提供を担当している。物理シミュレーションにおいて10年以上の経験を持つとともにシミュレーションプロセスの自動化・最適化に取り組み、近年はデジタルツインやAIといった最新の技術分野を設計・製造分野に取り込む活動に注力している。

speakers.name: Takashi Umezawa

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: 基于 NVIDIA 全栈技术打造代理式 AI 与物理 AI 的未来基石
sessions.abstract: 从帮助团队快速搭建和优化多智能体工作流的 NVIDIA NeMo Agent Toolkit，到围绕 Data Flywheel 与 NVIDIA Agentic Blueprint 构建可自我提升的代理式 AI 系统，再到面向物理 AI 的 NVIDIA Isaac GR00T 基础模型、辅助驾驶以及 NVIDIA Cosmos 世界模型等平台能力。NVIDIA 技术专家将系统讲解如何借助 NVIDIA 完整技术栈，更高效地设计、训练与部署新一代代理式 AI 与 物理 AI 应用。同时，3 家 NVIDIA 初创加速计划会员企业将通过生动的案例展示如何借助这些技术突破业务瓶颈，推动行业变革与发展。

speakers.name: Meiran Peng
speakers.bio: 彭美然，NVIDIA 开发者计划资深解决方案工程师，硕士毕业于清华大学生物医学工程专业。专注于为专攻大型语言模型、检索增强生成（RAG）和 NVIDIA Holoscan SDK 的 NVIDIA 初创加速计划会员企业提供技术指导，在基于 RAG 的应用开发和产品化方面拥有丰富经验。

speakers.name: 李 泞伶
speakers.bio: Dify 全球社区运营。长期专注于全球 AI 开发者社区与技术生态建设，将技术能力转化为可被理解、参与和持续共建的品牌与社区体系。在 NVIDIA 产品技术与创业生态的助力下，持续连接开发者、创业团队与产业伙伴，将 AI 能力转化为可被理解、参与和持续共建的生态体系与技术影响力。

speakers.name: 卓 羽
speakers.bio: 卓羽是枢途科技的创始人兼首席执行官。在他的领导下，枢途科技秉承以视频为数据驱动，让更多机器人落地干活的愿景；领先开发出了将互联网视频转化为具身智能多模态训练数据的算法，大幅降低了具身智能数采难度，提高具身训练效率，让海量互联网视频及真实工作场景视频训练物理人工智能成为可能。卓羽毕业于上海交通大学，曾就职于头部产业投资基金及双币风险投资基金，曾获得胡润 U30、中关村 U30、大湾区优秀人才等称号。

speakers.name: 任 冬淳
speakers.bio: 任冬淳博士，汤元科技联合创始人兼 CEO。他拥有模式识别博士学位及十余年自动驾驶研发管理经验。曾任美团自动驾驶算法总监、七鑫易维首席科学家及某智驾独角兽 CTO，主导落地数亿元商业项目。他学术成果丰硕，发表论文 30 余篇，获专利 50 余项；并率队在 CVPR、ICRA、NeurIPS 等国际顶级赛事的轨迹预测项目中多次斩获全球冠军。

sessions.title: AIBOX + AIOS：构建生成式 AI 在智能汽车的端侧落地新范式
sessions.abstract:
随着生成式 AI 的爆发，汽车正成为继手机之后的下一个核心载体。然而，当前车载系统仍面临端侧算力不足、架构碎片化、高延迟及隐私安全等严峻挑战。
本次演讲将深度解析 AIBox + AIOS 的全栈技术方案。该方案在不破坏既有座舱架构的前提下，通过即插即用的 AI 硬件底座与 AI 原生操作系统，结合 TS-EdgeLLM-Server 推理架构，实现了多模态模型（如 Qwen2.5-VL）的端侧高效部署。通过端云协同与多智能体（Multi-Agent）协作，我们将分享如何优化量化算法与 Eagle3 调优经验，将车辆从“被动响应”升级为“主动感知”的智能体，助力 OEM 构建低延迟、高可靠且可持续演进的车内 AI 体验。

speakers.name: 东超 徐
speakers.bio: 徐东超现任中科创达智能汽车事业群副总裁、畅行智驾首席执行官，负责中科创达全球智能汽车战略规划，以及 畅行智驾的整体运营与管理。有近 20 年的汽车行业从业经验，在智能汽车技术、全球业务拓展及企业管理等领域具备深厚的实践经验与行业洞察。

speakers.name: 晟 卢
speakers.bio: 2020 年加入中科创达，是创达 AIOS 智能中心推理部署方向技术骨干，也是创达 TS-EdgeLLM-Server 的主要开发者。

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: How HBM4 Unlocks Efficient LLM Serving at Scale (Presented by SK hynix)
sessions.abstract: Explore how next-generation high-bandwidth memory (HBM4) improves large language model serving by addressing two critical inference constraints: memory bandwidth and capacity. Through detailed profiling and controlled scaling studies across modern LLMs and reasoning- and agent-based workloads, we show how HBM4’s substantially higher bandwidth and increased capacity translate into lower latency and higher throughput in LLM serving systems, and we project the performance gains production AI systems can expect from GPU platforms equipped with HBM4.

speakers.name: Donguk Moon
speakers.bio: Donguk Moon is director of the AI Platform Software team in Memory Systems Research at SK hynix. He leads research and engineering efforts focused on improving AI and data analytics systems through advanced memory and storage technologies, including HBM, LPDDR, and CXL. He holds a master’s degree in electrical engineering from Stanford University, a master’s degree in data science from the University of California, Berkeley, and an M.B.A. from the University of Oxford.

sessions.title: MindVLA-o1：开启全能范式——下一代统一视觉-语言-动作自动驾驶大模型探索
sessions.abstract: 随着⾃动驾驶进⼊端到端基础模型时代，系统挑战体现为对推理效率、⻓时稳定性与复杂场景泛化能⼒的整体建模与权衡。本⽂介绍理想汽⻋下⼀代⾃动驾驶基础模型 MindVLA-o1：基于统⼀ VisionLanguage-Action 的 Omni 架构，在单⼀ Transformer 中联合建模感知、推理与控制。模型通过离散Action Tokens 与 MoE 实现⾼效、稳定的精准控制；采⽤快慢双系统推理，在实时性与复杂场景推理间⾃适应切换；并结合基于模型的强化学习闭环与软硬件协同设计，实现可量产、拟⼈化的端到端⾃动驾驶。

speakers.name: 锟 詹
speakers.bio:
詹锟现任理想汽车自动驾驶事业部 VLA（Vision-Language-Action）模型高级总监，负责面向自动驾驶与通用物理智能的多模态大模型体系建设与落地，包括世界模型、感知-认知-规划一体化学习以及云端仿真-强化学习闭环等方向，带领团队构建了数据、算力、算法训练闭环平台，推动自动驾驶从规则工程向模型驱动与自进化系统转型。

在加入理想汽车之前，詹锟长期深耕自动驾驶与 AI 领域，在百度 Apollo L4 业务担任预测算法负责人，拥有丰富的一线研发与团队管理经验，主导或参与多项行业前沿项目，并在顶级学术与产业会议上持续输出技术成果；关注从技术到产品的长期演进路径，致力于探索 VLA、世界模型与 embodied AI 在真实复杂环境中的规模化应用。

sessions.title: 基于扩散模型的具身智能：从统一理论到 GPU 加速的机器人策略 Diffusion-Driven Embodied AI: From Unified Theory to GPU-Accelerated Robot Policies
sessions.abstract:
本报告将系统阐述基于可控扩散模型的新一代机器人学习框架。首先，我们提出一种用于全新的可控扩散模型的统一理论框架，揭示其如何为机器人决策提供一种兼具表达力与可控性的数学新范式。随后，将展示一种突破性的、原生运行于 GPU 的 on-policy 扩散强化学习算法，在效率上树立新的标杆。最后，我们将通过真实的机器人实验展示其在精细操作与运动控制等核心任务上达到的业界领先性能。
This session will systematically elaborate on a new generation robot learning framework based on controllable diffusion models. First, we propose a unified theoretical framework for a novel controllable diffusion model, revealing how it provides a new mathematical paradigm for robot decision-making that combines expressiveness and controllability. Subsequently, a groundbreaking on-policy diffusion-based reinforcement learning algorithm, natively running on GPUs, will be presented, setting a new benchmark in efficiency. Finally, we will demonstrate its industry-leading performance in core tasks such as dexterous manipulation and motion control through real-world robot experiments.

speakers.name: 野 石
speakers.bio: 石野博士是上海科技大学信息科学与技术学院的助理教授，研究员，博导，YesAI 实验室负责人, 近年来发表顶会顶刊 70 余篇（NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, CVPR, ICCV, TNNLS, TSG等）。近期研究聚焦在具身智能、生成模型、优化与控制等领域。石野博士曾入选上海市海外领军人才计划，上海市扬帆计划，主持国家自然科学基金，曾获得国家优秀留学生奖，ICLR 2025 生成式理论研讨会杰出论文奖, IEEE ICCSCE 2016 最佳论文奖。担任 NeurIPS 和 ICLR 等会议领域主席。

speakers.name: Yu Huang
speakers.bio: 黄煜，NVIDIA 解决方案架构师，毕业于北京邮电大学，在深度学习算法和工程实践方面有多年经验，熟悉数据中心架构和解决方案。主要负责在 GPU 加速的软硬件开发、深度学习的训练、优化和部署等方面，为 Smart Space/NCP/OEM 合作伙伴提供技术支持。

sessions.title: 十载相伴，NVIDIA 赋能创业公司在 AI 时代加速前行
sessions.abstract: NVIDIA 初创加速计划深耕中国十年，陪伴上千家创业企业从技术探索走向商业落地。2025 年，我们与生态伙伴持续携手，为中国创业公司提供了一系列产品、技术、市场、资本与业务对接的全链路支持，同时迎来了会员规模突破 3000 家的重要里程碑。本视频将回顾 2025 年该项目如何赋能中国创业者加速成长，并展望 2026 年在生态协同与创业扶持上的新机遇。同时，3 家 NVIDIA 初创加速计划会员企业也将分享他们如何借助 NVIDIA 的创新技术快速实现业务跃迁，为行业与社会创造更大价值。

speakers.name: Ming Lou
speakers.bio: 娄明，NVIDIA 初创生态中国区总负责人，带领团队管理 NVIDIA 初创加速计划 (NVIDIA Inception) 中国区业务，构建创业生态环境，招募应用或计划应用深度学习，机器学习，数据科学以及 CUDA, DOCA 等 NVIDIA 相关技术, 已经使用或计划使用 GPU，DPU 作为运算基础的创业公司成为 NVIDIA 初创加速计划成员，为初创加速计划会员提供产品，技术，市场，融资，客户推荐等一系列支持，加速创业公司的发展。 在加入 NVIDIA 之前曾任职英特尔医疗和生命科学部创新业务经理，柯惠医疗中国研发中心产品经理，通用电气医疗集团项目经理以及埃森哲分析师。 毕业于清华大学自动化系，拥有清华大学控制科学与工程硕士学位和自动化学士学位。

speakers.name: 冯 雷
speakers.bio: 冯雷，毕业于北京语言大学对外汉语专业。亲历移动互联网发展，多年古典产品经理经验，曾就职于三星、兰亭集势、Boss 直聘和多家创业公司。2022 年投身 AI 赛道，加入 MiniMax 成为海螺 AI 的产品负责人。2024 年创办 MarsWave 公司，担任 CEO。

speakers.name: 胡 叶凡
speakers.bio: 胡叶凡，贝湾生物创始人兼 CEO，计算免疫学专家。其研究成果多次发表于 Cell Host & Microbe、Nature Communications 等顶尖期刊。他致力于 AI 免疫编程技术开发，通过计算模型模拟个体化免疫互作，以前所未有的速度驱动个体化免疫疗法的研发。他带领团队利用免疫编程工具箱计算攻克生物计算瓶颈，旨在通过精准医疗解决全球健康挑战，让创新药物惠及每一生命个体。

speakers.name: 雨培 吴
speakers.bio: 吴雨培，阿丘科技 CTO 兼首席 AI 科学家，毕业于清华大学计算机系 AI Lab，专注深度学习、强化学习、多模态学习研究，以学术分全系第一毕业，并获得 Siebel Scholars 称号。

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging

sessions.title: Revolutionize Automated Parcel Handling With Humanoid Robots
sessions.abstract: This session unpacks how Koerber is building a humanoid “robot brain” for parcel logistics using NVIDIA Omniverse, Isaac Sim, and Jetson across simulation and real-world deployments. We’ll walk through how Unitree class humanoid hardware is trained in Omniverse scenes with integrated physics and flow simulation, how Gr00t and Cosmos are evaluated as foundation models for control, and how the same policies are deployed on Jetson-powered lines for adaptive, vision-driven and safety-aware parcel handling.

speakers.name: António Conde
speakers.bio: António is the COO of Körber Supply Chain, leading global operations and strategic transformation in the logistics sector. With over 25 years’ experience in industrial management, he has driven successful turnarounds, international expansion, and innovation across Europe and beyond. António holds a mechanical engineering degree from the University of Coimbra and a postgraduate diploma in business administration from Porto Business School. He's been recognized for his leadership in business growth and establishing global engineering excellence centers.

sessions.title: 基于 NVIDIA HPC 打造端到端生成式推荐 OneRec 训练：面向工业级推荐大模型的“超算引擎”
sessions.abstract: 我们基于 NVIDIA GPU，针对快手内部的生成式推荐模型 OneRec 做了较为深入的软硬结合优化，高效利用 TensorCore 算力、HBM、高速 RDMA 网络在单机算力/多机互联两个维度加速生成式推荐训练，助力 OneRec 在全场景的部署上线。

speakers.name: 晋 欧阳
speakers.bio: 快手 AIPlatform 训练负责人，负责快手搜推广模型训练、多模态大模型训练

speakers.name: Bin Chai
speakers.bio: NVIDIA Devtech 加速计算专家，从事企业用户 GPU 加速计算支持工作，目前主要负责 HPC 程序优化和 kernel 开发。

sessions.title: Elevating Business Jet Customer Experience With an Enhanced Rendering Workflow
sessions.abstract: We'll walk through how we built our graphics pipeline in Omniverse, an upgrade that slashed rendering times by nearly a factor of 100 and cut our production cycles in half. This improvement now lets us deliver photorealistic videos alongside traditional renderings, significantly enhancing what we can offer our customers. We’ll delve into our customer workflow and the custom tooling we’ve developed around Omniverse to maximize efficiency.

speakers.name: Axel Cocat
speakers.bio: After earning his master's degree in computer science, Axel joined Dassault Aviation's business jet division, where he led the development of in-house software for creating 3D visuals of aircraft cabins. Driven by a passion for software engineering and a solid technical background, he's been dedicated to building robust, user-friendly tools that streamline business processes and boost efficiency.

speakers.name: Jean-Charles Fedini
speakers.bio: After seven years as a software engineer in the fields of science and aeronautics, Jean-Charles has led a team of computer graphics engineers for about five years in the business aviation sector at Dassault Aviation. His background in software engineering, combined with his graphic design sensibility and leadership skills, has enabled Jean-Charles to drive innovative projects and deliver high-quality visual solutions.

sessions.topic: Content Creation / Rendering - Rendering Engines / Pipelines / Tools

sessions.title: 基于 NVIDIA Hopper 架构的 LLM 长文本推理场景性能优化实践和探索
sessions.abstract: 在腾讯混元大模型支持的各项服务中，长文本请求消耗的 GPU 计算资源占比高达 60%。随着深度搜索（DeepSearch）与智能体（Agent）技术的兴起，长文本处理已成为大模型推理服务的主要成本负担与优化核心方向。为此，我们针对长文本场景落地实施了稀疏注意力机制、并行计算策略及键值缓存（KV Cache）优化等一系列技术方案，不仅取得了显著的性能提升，还有效降低了线上长文本推理的部署成本。

speakers.name: Jeson Xiang
speakers.bio: Jeson Xiang is an AI inference architect at Tencent, specializing in GPU inference acceleration technology. He has extensive experience in performance optimization and high-performance heterogeneous computing. Currently, he mainly works on Tencent AngelHCF-LLM, a framework designed for accelerating inference in Hunyuan LLM models.

sessions.title: From Vision to Reality: Building AI Factories
sessions.abstract: Discover how to turn the vision of AI into a tangible reality within the industry. This session explores the organization, design, and deployment of “AI factories,” environments that integrate data, algorithms, and processes to accelerate innovation. We will share our experience creating real use cases on the NVIDIA platform, collaborating with strategic partners, and driving operational efficiency—a complete journey through digital transformation that turns AI into a true value engine for the organization.

speakers.name: Raquel Hernansanz
speakers.bio: Raquel Hernansanz, an industrial engineer working since 2008 in the group, is currently the excellence and digital supply chain director at HORSE POWERTRAIN SOLUTIONS, a technology and innovation company that researches future mobility solutions that are carbon-neutral, sustainable, and inclusive. Raquel leads key projects, including digital governance and the implementation of new technologies, and works with different teams ensuring strategic alignment and project integration, always guaranteeing quality and efficiency in processes.

speakers.name: Andres Benitez Pozo
speakers.bio: Andrés Benítez Pozo is a Manager in Deloitte Spain’s AI & Data practice, where he works with industrial and manufacturing clients to design and deploy AI-driven transformation programs. He supports organizations in translating AI strategy into scalable solutions, leveraging NVIDIA platforms to enable digital twins, computer vision, and AI factory architectures that deliver measurable operational impact. Previously, he worked as a data scientist at Amazon Alexa AI, with a background in natural language processing that informs his work on Physical AI and robotics.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: Spain's First Physical AI Lab: Empowering the Northern Spanish Industrial Ecosystem
sessions.abstract: We will share our experience in building the first physical AI research lab in northern Spain, dedicated to empowering the local industrial ecosystem. This lab serves as a collaborative space where researchers can design, prototype, and validate cutting-edge technologies in robotics and automation. By providing access to advanced physical AI tools such as NVIDIA Omniverse and Isaac platforms, we enable rapid development and testing before deployment in real-world scenarios. Local industries can actively engage with these innovations, gaining hands-on experience and exploring practical applications that drive competitiveness and technological adoption. We'll show how collaboration between academic research and industrial implementation can foster a dynamic environment for innovation and growth.

speakers.name: Jorge Posada
speakers.bio: Jorge Posada has been associate director from Vicomtech (Spain) since 2001. He holds a Ph.D. in engineering from the Technische Universität Darmstadt (Germany) in computer graphics applications for manufacturing. After working for some years at the Fraunhofer Institute for Computer Graphics in Darmstadt, he helped to create and establish the Applied Research Centre Vicomtech. He is also president of the Board of GraphicsVision.ai, an international network based in Germany for the cooperation in applied research in computer graphics, multi-modal-multimedia technologies, and visual interactive digital media technologies, with leading institutions in different locations worldwide.

speakers.name: Jairo Sánchez
speakers.bio: Jairo Roberto Sánchez Tapia holds a PhD in Computer Sciences from the University of Navarra. He began his research career at CEIT in simulation and augmented reality algorithms, defending his thesis on real-time monocular SLAM in 2010. Currently, he leads the Cognitive Vision group at Vicomtech, focusing on computer vision for quality control and robotics for advanced manufacturing. He also teaches “Virtual and Augmented Reality: Multimodal 3D Interaction” in the Master’s in Computational Engineering and Systems at UPV/EHU. In 2017, he earned a Master’s in Advanced Manufacturing Engineering from UNED.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: 智能融合：下一代端到端架构演进
sessions.abstract: 当前自动驾驶方案已经从两段式演进到了一段式端到端方案，如何更进一步激活智能提升自动驾驶能力，如何拥抱大模型带来的行业变革，真正让自动驾驶能力超越 L2 还欠缺哪些关键要素，本次分享提供一些思路以供抛砖引玉。

speakers.name: 沐 杨
speakers.bio: 杨沐先生毕业于清华姚班，清华计算机科学硕士学位，曾获国际信息编程奥林匹克比赛（IOI）金牌。2011年本科毕业期间，杨沐与姚班同学联合创办人工智能公司旷视科技，是中国最早一批AI创业者的代表。杨沐曾主导多个人工智能开源项目，从0到1搭建算法工程体系并支持多个AI云计算平台的设计实施和工程开发，极大推动了人工智能算法在企业级硬件和移动终端的落地。 在千里智驾，杨沐负责整体技术战略制定与研发体系建设，主导完成多团队技术整合，构建统一技术骨干与数据驱动研发范式，系统整合算法、算力与数据资源。在技术架构上，他推动全轨迹端到端模型架构升级，引入世界模型、强化学习与大模型基座能力，打造覆盖数据系统、仿真闭环与模型迭代的完整技术体系，持续提升系统“含模量”，推动自动驾驶能力向更高上限演进。

sessions.title: Venture Capitalist Reverse Pitch: EMEA
sessions.abstract:
Finding the right investor for your startup can be tricky. Venture capital (VC) firms offer more than just funding. They provide mentorship, technical guidance, access to valuable partners and customers, and a range of other benefits. Making the right connections can help your business reach its potential.

Join us to hear three visionary VCs pitch their firms and present their thesis. This event is for founders and business leaders looking to connect with some of the most impactful VCs in the technology industry. You'll also get a chance to connect with a VC of your choice for a deeper individual discussion.

speakers.name: Troy Estes
speakers.bio: Troy Estes is head of VCs for Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, focused on connecting innovative startups to the resources they need to build, scale, and succeed.

speakers.name: Rebecka Löthman Rydå
speakers.bio: Rebecka Löthman Rydå is a general partner with Norrsken Evolve, investing in early stage companies in resilience and sustainability. Rebecka has spent over 10 years in venture at leading firms. She has extensive experience in investing, boards, and exits, having worked with dozens of companies over the years. Rebecka has an operator background, having held both CFO and CEO positions. When she is not working, she loves sci-fi books and long-distance running.

speakers.name: Miriam Shtilman-Lavsovski
speakers.bio: Miriam Shtilman is a venture capital partner, public speaker, and deep tech strategist operating at the intersection of technology, investment, and policy. As a partner at Tal Ventures, she screens more than 300 startups each year and supports a portfolio of more than 50 companies, guiding founders through multi-stage funding, scaling strategies, and global expansion. She is the co-founder of DeepTech Nation, Israel’s first nationwide deep tech innovation community; Israel’s representative at the Quantum Community Network (QCN), part of the European Quantum Flagship initiative; and a member of the World Economic Forum Quantum Economy Network.

speakers.name: Philipp Moehring
speakers.bio:
Philipp is a Berlin-based technology investor. His investment focus is on the earliest stages of ambitious and technically challenging startups. That’s why he co-founded the Tiny Supercomputer Investment Co. in 2017, which backs lots of amazing founders across Europe.

Philipp cares about the stuff that’s going to be cool in five years, and has fun debating versions of the future. That’s how he got to back some very successful founders early in their journey, and helped others navigate the ups and downs of being a founder.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Beyond the Model: Driving Cutting-Edge Drug Discovery
sessions.abstract: How can we accelerate breakthrough drug discovery beyond model performance? This panel unites leaders from Apheris, Latent Labs, and Owkin to explore how AI-driven platforms, multi-modal data integration, and large-scale simulation are reshaping how we design and develop protein therapeutics. Together, we’ll dive into lessons learned from cutting-edge deployments, share emerging best practices, and examine what’s next for collaborative AI ecosystems driving the next era of precision medicine.

speakers.name: David Ruau
speakers.bio: David Ruau is head of business development and strategic alliances for Europe, the Middle East, and Africa at NVIDIA, where he leads strategic scientific partnerships in healthcare and life sciences across the region. He previously served as CSO at a healthcare AI company, vice president of global data assets and decision science at Bayer, and head of scientific computing at AstraZeneca, and has a computational biology background with 30 peer‑reviewed publications.

speakers.name: Simon Kohl
speakers.bio:
Dr. Simon Kohl is the founder and CEO of Latent Labs, a frontier AI company turning drug design from an empirical science into a true engineering discipline. Latent Labs builds generative models that automate therapeutic design, with a mission to transform every pharma and biotech company into an AI-native organization—compressing discovery timelines from years to weeks and multiplying portfolio productivity.
Simon previously founded and co-led DeepMind's protein design team and was a senior research scientist on the Nobel Prize-winning AlphaFold2 project. Under his leadership, Latent Labs has raised $75M from Radical Ventures and Sofinnova Partners and developed Latent-X and Latent-X2—generative models that design lab-validated antibody and peptide candidates with hit rates and binding affinities matching or exceeding traditional methods that screen millions of compounds.
Simon is building toward a future where drug discovery runs autonomously, with AI systems designing, testing, and iterating on candidates at unprecedented speed.

speakers.name: Eric Durand
speakers.bio: Experienced leader with over 15 years in building AI-driven solutions in the biotech industry. I currently serve as Chief R&D Officer at Owkin, where I lead the research and technology teams in developing frontier biology reasoning models and our agentic AI solutions for drug discovery. Previously, I was Director of Data Science at Novartis, heading the Oncology Data Science team in Basel, and a Senior Computational Biologist at 23andMe. I hold a PhD in Applied ML and completed my postdoctoral research at UC Berkeley. I am dedicated to bridging the gap between advanced ML and biological discovery to transform patient outcomes and accelerate therapeutic innovation.

speakers.name: Robin Röhm
speakers.bio: Robin Röhm is the CEO and Co-Founder of Apheris, where he focuses on building enterprise AI applications and federated data networks for drug discovery. With a background in Medicine, Philosophy, and Mathematics, he brings a multidisciplinary perspective to the scientific and computational challenges of working with distributed, IP-sensitive data. His work centers on enabling pharma companies to run, fine-tune, and benchmark drug discovery models securely within their own environments and to collaborate through governed data networks without sharing raw data. Before founding Apheris, Robin built life sciences data start-ups working with sensitive genomic and patient data.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Biology - Generative AI

sessions.title: From Physical Vision Systems to OpenUSD: Building Digital Twins for Virtual Commissioning
sessions.abstract: As conventional development of machine vision solutions is time-consuming and costly, and AI-driven automation accelerates, the ability to virtually commission vision systems before physical deployment is a game-changer. Basler has developed a comprehensive workflow for virtual commissioning by creating digital twins of machine vision systems built on NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD. This approach helps companies configure, simulate, and optimize vision solutions in a virtual environment—cutting prototyping costs, reducing deployment risks, and speeding up AI-driven automation. This talk will explore how Basler transformed traditional camera hardware into interoperable, scalable virtual models that enable sensor realistic simulation of vision systems and synthetic data generation for AI training.

speakers.name: Justus Basler
speakers.bio: Justus Basler leads the technical development of Basler’s Vision System Simulation project. He works across the full virtual commissioning workflow for vision systems, from virtual setup and synthetic data generation to AI optimization, benchmarking, and deployment. His background is in electrical engineering and computer vision, with a focus on simulation-driven development of robust vision solutions.

speakers.name: Kamil Lelowicz
speakers.bio: Kamil Lelowicz is a machine learning engineer and technical lead with a Ph.D. in sensor modeling. He specializes in designing and optimizing AI-based computer vision solutions for real-time and edge deployment, focusing on accuracy and efficiency. Kamil works at the intersection of research and production to deliver scalable, high-impact products.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Model Builders at the Frontier of EMEA’s Open and Sovereign AI Movement
sessions.abstract: Europe’s drive toward sovereign AI is redefining the way open, transparent, and multilingual large models are built at scale. This panel explores the technical foundations that drive this effort. Speakers will discuss best practices in data quality, domain adaptation, and culturally aligned evaluation for low-resource languages, showing how sovereign and open models can meet European requirements while delivering state-of-the-art performance. The discussion will also highlight how NVIDIA Nemotron enables European, Middle Eastern, and African (EMEA) developers to build and fine-tune competitive large models, accelerating innovation while reducing dependence on proprietary platforms. In addition, the panel will touch on how European developers and companies are preparing these models for real-world inference and deployment across diverse applications.

speakers.name: Meriem Bendris
speakers.bio: Meriem is a deep learning solutions architect manager, leading a team that helps European, Middle Eastern, and African partners build neural networks for speech, natural language processing, and computer vision. Her areas of expertise include conversational AI and multi-modality. Meriem holds a Ph.D. in signal and image processing from Telecom ParisTech, where she focused on machine learning applied to speech and computer vision.

speakers.name: Shaltiel Shmidman
speakers.bio: Shaltiel is a software developer with over a decade of experience specializing in deep learning and training large language models, particularly for Hebrew. Over the past five years, he has led the training and release of many state-of-the-art models for Hebrew natural language processing. Shaltiel has published research and built products serving hundreds of thousands of users monthly.

speakers.name: Pontus Stenetorp
speakers.bio: Pontus Stenetorp is a Professor of Natural Language Processing and Deputy Director for the Centre for Artificial Intelligence at University College London (UCL). In addition, they are a Specially Appointed Professor at the Research and Development Center for Large Language Models (LLMC) at the National Institute of Informatics (NII). They are the founder and leader of the UK-LLM project, which publishes freely available large language models (LLMs) for the languages of the British Isles.

speakers.name: Imanol Schlag
speakers.bio: Dr. Imanol Schlag is an AI Research Scientist at the ETH AI Center, co-leading Apertus, the Swiss AI Initiative's LLM effort. He completed his PhD at USI/IDSIA under Jürgen Schmidhuber (2023), with prior degrees from the University of St Andrews and FHNW. His research focuses on open-source LLMs, neural architecture innovations (particularly fast weight programmers like the DeltaNet), and responsible AI development. He has interned at Meta FAIR, Google Research, and Microsoft Research, and currently teaches Large-Scale AI Engineering at ETH Zürich.

speakers.name: Andres Desantes
speakers.bio: Andrés Desantes de Mergelina is a Spanish tech leader with an international mindset. He studied engineering — and later added a business degree to the mix — with the dream of building his own Iron Man, or at least a device that could amplify his physical and mental abilities. After twelve years delivering enterprise technology across France, the U.S., and Canada — holding executive responsibilities in large organisations — he built a pragmatic edge: results only count when they hold under real constraints: governance, security, scale, and cost. In 2023, he took the helm of 1MillionBot as CEO, focused on turning applied AI into a dependable operational capability.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Pre-Trained / Foundation Models

sessions.title: 用基座模型重塑智能边界
sessions.abstract:
在城市NOA走向大规模量产的当下，整个智能驾驶行业正面临两大共性挑战：一是如何更有效地应对复杂的长尾场景，二是如何以可控成本实现规模化能力泛化与交付。而基座模型（Foundation Model）的出现，为解决这两大痛点提供了新的范式可能。
作为最早实践“视觉-语言-动作”（VLA）模型的人工智能企业之一，元戎启行正通过构建统一架构的 Foundation Model，打造具备更强认知推理能力的辅助驾驶解决方案。目前，元戎启行已实现超过 20 万辆具备城市 NOA 功能的辅助驾驶系统量产交付，并通过真实道路数据形成持续优化的研发飞轮，计划于 2026 年达成百万级辅助驾驶交付规模。
本次演讲将系统分享元戎启行如何以 Foundation Model 为智能辅助驾驶系统的“研发引擎”，结合 NVIDIA 平台协同，构建从训练、验证及部署的研发闭环，为下一代辅助驾驶系统打造智能进化的“底座能力”。

speakers.name: 通易 曹
speakers.bio:
元戎启行首席技术官，负责公司前沿技术及量产产品研发。在 NeurIPS、COLT、CVPR 等顶级会议发表多篇论文。
2024 年曹通易带领团队研发推出业内首套搭载导航地图、应用端到端模型的辅助驾驶平台DeepRoute IO，搭载该方案的数款辅助驾驶汽车已投入消费者市场，累计量产交付超 20 万台。2025 年他带领团队研发的 VLA 模型也已经量产，该模型集成思维链能力，打造具备“防御性驾驶”意识的 AI 司机。目前他正带领团队研发基座模型（Foundation model），重塑自动驾驶的底层逻辑。

sessions.title: AI Performance and Inference Economics for Engineers
sessions.abstract: This technical deep dive analyzes the cost drivers for AI systems, focusing on the interdependent relationship between total cost of ownership (TCO) and performance. We will show how the influence of extreme co-design, spanning silicon, software, and the rack-scale system, unlocks new efficiency levers. The session will detail how these integrated optimizations fundamentally improve operational metrics like cost per token and token per watt in modern deployments.

speakers.name: Eduardo Alvarez
speakers.bio: Eduardo Alvarez is a senior technical lead at NVIDIA, where he focuses on AI inference at scale, performance optimization, workload economic analysis, and application enablement. He has a deep background in AI systems engineering, workload optimization, and accelerated computing—focused on translating innovations into real-world applications. Before NVIDIA, Eduardo held engineering roles at various semiconductor and energy tech companies.

speakers.name: Farshad Ghodsian
speakers.bio: Farshad Ghodsian is a senior technical marketing engineer at NVIDIA, where he focuses on AI training and inference at scale, performance optimization insights, new model releases, and AI engineering enablement. He brings a wealth of experience at the intersection of AI infrastructure, distributed training, GPU-accelerated computing, and cloud-native MLOps—translating cutting-edge research into practical insights for developers, enterprise teams, and business leaders. Prior to NVIDIA, Farshad held technical roles at leading semiconductor and consulting companies, where he helped build and manage large-scale generative AI and MLOps platforms for top technology customers.

sessions.title: Accelerating Innovation With AI Studio and Foundry: Cases Driving Customer Success (Presented by World Wide Technology)
sessions.abstract: Discover how organizations are accelerating their AI journeys with WWT’s AI Studio and Foundry—leveraging NVIDIA NIM, NVIDIA NeMo, and NVIDIA AI Blueprints to move quickly from ideas to validated prototypes and scalable, production-ready solutions. We will share real-world examples of how this approach is delivering meaningful business outcomes in industries such as global service providers and energy production.

speakers.name: Nathan McKie
speakers.bio: After overseeing services related to software and automation engineering for over 25 years, Nathan now has the honor and pleasure of being a senior-level AI advisor. He helps customers understand how best to use AI technologies to accomplish their business goals, whether it involves leveraging hardware, software, or data. Nathan is thrilled to use his lifetime of experience building amazing solutions for customers to help others make good decisions on their AI journey.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Immersive Robotics Teleop 101: Standardize Large-Scale Data Collection and Processing Pipeline
sessions.abstract: Learn to build scalable data pipelines for training robot policies using Teleop Core, an open-source framework that unifies simulation and real-world teleoperation. We will demonstrate how to standardize integrations with human interface devices such as PICO headsets and controller to accelerate the creation of very large robotics datasets.

speakers.name: Tiffany Chen
speakers.bio: Tiffany Chen is a distinguished software engineer and manager at NVIDIA, leading AI and robotics for spatial computing. Her team advances robot teleoperation for physical AI, enabling high-fidelity imitation learning and optimized human-machine interaction. Tiffany also initiated NVIDIA’s XR-AI efforts, bridging foundation models with spatial devices via agentic workflows to transform industrial productivity. Previously, she led multi-modal perception and map-robust perception at Waymo, advancing autonomous vehicle safety. She holds a Ph.D. from MIT's Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory, specializing in machine learning systems.

speakers.name: Ke Jing
speakers.bio: Ke Jing is the lead product manager at PICO, working on spatial computing developer products and ecosystem. His recent work on the open-source XRoboToolkit has been adopted by leading robotics companies and research teams and nominated for the best paper award at IEEE/SICE SII 2026. Before PICO, he worked as a software engineer at Unity on the XR Interaction Toolkit. He also worked as a tech lead at Genies on the Avatar SDK and apps. He studied extended reality and human-computer interfaces and received his Ph.D. in informatics from the University of California, Irvine, in 2021. He won the first prize of the RoboMaster Robotics Competition in 2015 and 2016.

sessions.title: Deep Dive: Streaming Immersive Robotics Teleoperations
sessions.abstract: Join NVIDIA expert Jiwen Cai for a technical deep dive on architecting scalable teleoperation pipelines that bridge simulation and reality. Learn how to leverage NVIDIA CloudXR to stream high-fidelity, low-latency visuals to wireless headsets, unlocking operator comfort and precise dexterity for industrial robotics deployments and data collection at scale.

speakers.name: Maarten Witteveen
speakers.bio: Maarten is a co-founder and the chief technology officer at MANUS, which builds high-precision data gloves that capture the full spectrum of human hand motion with millimeter-level accuracy. Their gloves power natural teleoperation for robots, train embodied AI, and open new ways to interact in virtual/extended reality, entertainment, and research. Maarten loves helping creators, scientists, and engineers turn hand movements into real-world or digital action, making interactions more intuitive, precise, and human. MANUS is proud to set the benchmark for hand tracking and manipulation across industries worldwide.

speakers.name: Jiwen Cai
speakers.bio: Jiwen Cai is a distinguished engineer at NVIDIA, where he leads the development of Isaac Teleoperation and Data Collection pipelines. He’s a hands-on software and product leader with a track record of building complex, high-performance systems across both startups and large-scale tech organizations. Before NVIDIA, Jiwen spearheaded the web-based OpenUSD platform at Cloud Zeta, drove remote rendering and multimedia development at Magic Leap, and led AR Assistant, Daydream OS, and virtual/augmented reality graphics initiatives at Google. Based in San Francisco, Jiwen brings deep technical expertise and a passion for turning ambitious ideas into scalable, real-world products.

speakers.name: Jay Yang
speakers.bio: Jay Yang is a founding engineer and chief architect at Lightwheel, focusing on large-scale robotics simulation, reinforcement learning training, and embodied AI evaluation infrastructure. His work spans Isaac Lab–based benchmarking frameworks, reinforcement learning training and evaluation pipelines, egocentric data collection and annotation systems, and sim-to-real workflows for humanoid robot manipulation. He works closely with industry partners to build reproducible, industry-grade platforms for embodied AI development.

sessions.title: Telecom Special Address: The AI Grid—Intelligently Connecting AI Infrastructure
sessions.abstract: The unsatiable demand for AI computing is creating the need for many new kinds of AI infrastructure providers. As AI inference traffic scales, compute must move beyond a few centralized data centers to a vast, distributed AI grid. Telcos, with their nationwide networks and dense footprint of data centers, are uniquely positioned to transform existing infrastructure into this scaled AI grid. This talk explores how telcos can build an AI grid to power new AI services and applications, gives a first look at NVIDIA’s reference architecture, highlights edge AI applications that run best on this grid, and shares announcements from partners already on this journey. It also offers a forward-looking view of how innovations in AI-RAN, autonomous networks, and 6G come together as workloads on the AI grid—creating a once-in-a-decade opportunity for telcos to redefine their role in the AI era.

speakers.name: Ronnie Vasishta
speakers.bio: Ronnie Vasishta is responsible for NVIDA’s telecom business and strategy. He joined NVIDIA in October 2020 from Intel, where he served as vice president and general manager of the Network and Configurable Logic Division, Data Products Group. Ronnie formerly served as CEO of privately held eASIC, which focused on the networking and storage markets and was sold to Intel in 2018. Previously, he was vice president of technical marketing at LSI Logic.

sessions.title: Liquid Cooling: How to Achieve 100% Heat Capture on 500kW+ Racks (Presented by CoolIT Systems)
sessions.abstract: As rack power densities surge beyond 100 kilowatts (kW), achieving 100% heat capture becomes increasingly essential for thermal management and operational efficiency. Current coldplate designs focus on high-thermal design power components such as CPUs and GPUs, but future architectures demand complete heat removal across all components. Even at 90% heat capture, a 500kW rack leaves 50kW for air cooling, which is a stretch for even the best air-cooled implementations. Enabling 100% heat capture will be critical for supporting next-generation AI infrastructure. Join us to learn how integrated cooling loops and advanced coldplate technologies enable complete heat removal across the entire server.

speakers.name: Andrew Buckrell
speakers.bio: Andrew Buckrell holds a Ph.D. in heat transfer and numerical methods, with deep expertise in modeling and thermal sciences. He has previously spent over a decade in the automotive industry, contributing multiple patents across the areas of cooling, energy efficiency, and system design. Andrew now applies his knowledge of heat transfer and thermal management to the advancement of technology in the data center industry at CoolIT Systems.

sessions.title: The AI-Augmented Surgeon: Inside the Operating Room of the Future
sessions.abstract: Learn how Medivis is revolutionizing surgical care with the University of Pittsburgh Medical Center (UPMC) and MAIA, their groundbreaking extended reality (XR) and AI platform. Discover how MAIA gives surgeons real-time, conversational access to vast medical data through an advanced retrieval-augmented generation system. We'll also showcase its powerful image recognition capabilities powered by NVIDIA Nemo Agent Toolkit and Cosmos, delivering the next generation of surgical precision and care.

speakers.name: Edward Andrews
speakers.bio: Edward Andrews, M.D., is a neurosurgeon. He practices at UPMC Mercy and is also affiliated with UPMC Presbyterian and UPMC Shadyside. Edward completed his fellowship at UPMC, residency at the University of Pittsburgh School of Medicine, and medical education at Thomas Jefferson University Hospital. His research focuses on the integration of augmented reality, artificial intelligence, and computational imaging to enhance neurosurgical precision and patient outcomes. His work has advanced surgical navigation and intraoperative visualization, leading to novel techniques that improve accuracy and efficiency in complex procedures.

speakers.name: Osamah Choudhry
speakers.bio: Osamah Choudhry is a neurosurgeon and co-founder/CEO of Medivis. His research and entrepreneurship interests have focused on the use of augmented reality to enable the future of surgical navigation. He's an assistant professor of neurosurgery at New York University Langone Medical Center. He completed his medical degree at Rutgers University in New Jersey, and then completed a residency in neurosurgery at New York University.

sessions.title: AI Grid Explained: From Secure AI Factories to Distributed Intelligence
sessions.abstract: As AI adoption accelerates, isolated AI factories must scale across to a connected, distributed and secure AI grid that can run diverse workloads —from edge AI applications and telco network functions to internal AI services, digital twins, and RAN—while delivering the right performance and efficiency. This session defines what an AI grid is, why it matters, and how to build it.  The speakers will walk through its reference architecture, from the compute substrate and high-performance networking to an intelligent, workload-aware control plane and orchestration that spans data centers and sites of all sizes. They will show the types of workloads that are ideal for running on an AI grid and share concrete steps telcos can take to implement a secure AI grid, where to start that journey, and how to unlock new revenue and monetization opportunities today.

speakers.name: Sree Sankar
speakers.bio: Sree is a visionary AI product and business executive and global head at NVIDIA, where she leads the strategy and vision for AI-GRID, the company’s distributed inference platform. With two decades of experience and eight years of AI/ML product leadership, she has delivered transformative impact across computer vision, natural language processing, recommendation systems, and search in ecommerce, retail-tech, software as a service, and advertising. Previously, as SVP at Grabango, she helped disrupt retail with computer-vision-powered checkout-free tech, scaling enterprise adoption, securing multiple 100-store expansion deals, and cutting operational costs through Gen AI-driven automation.

speakers.name: Masum Mir
speakers.bio: Masum Mir is senior vice president and general manager of product management for Cisco’s Service Provider Cable and Mobility business, responsible for strategy, product, and technical marketing. His current focus areas are building 5G, network automation, SP cloud and orchestration solutions, technology, and product line. He brings more than 20 years of experience working in the networking and telecommunication industry.

speakers.name: Shawn Hakl
speakers.bio: Shawn Hakl is the Senior Vice President of AT&T Business Product responsible for the portfolio servicing Enterprise, Public Sector, Partner and Small Business Segments, which generates over $30 billion in annual revenue. A seasoned leader in cloud, AI and communications, Shawn’s unique knowledge of software, security and infrastructure are helping enterprises, government and small businesses harness the power of technology to successfully execute on their digital transformation. In his current role, Shawn is responsible for wireline, 5G/wireless, voice/UC, security/SASE, data center, AIOps and Network as a Service product lines. Before joining AT&T, Shawn led the Microsoft Azure for Operators strategy, integrating Microsoft AI and Cloud assets with acquisitions like Affirmed Networks and Metaswitch. His efforts established Azure as the preferred platform for 5G networks and AI-driven operations across operators, enterprises, and governments. Initially an incubation project, success with key clients propelled Azure for Operators into Azure’s core product families for edge computing and AI. Prior to Microsoft, he served as Senior Vice President of Business Product at Verizon, managing a $29 billion portfolio that included 5G, Mobile Edge Compute, SaaS, Security, Internet of Things, Cloud Collaboration and Professional services. His experience at Verizon also spans leadership roles in operations, IT, and sales engineering. Shawn serves on the board of directors of LevelBlue and Mplfy. He is a Syntropy Executive Advisor and a Versa Technical Advisor. Shawn was named as a Top 25 Telecom Movers and Shakers in 2018 and in 2021 by Fierce Telecom. Shawn began his career at Accenture and holds a bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering from the University of Calgary. Shawn is a former national team athlete on the Canadian luge team. He and his wife Leslie reside in Dallas, TX.

sessions.title: AI Grid in Action: Killer Applications for Distributed Inferencing That Telcos Can Monetize
sessions.abstract: This lightning session showcases three edge-native AI applications that only reach their full potential when deployed on a distributed AI grid powered by telecom networks and NVIDIA’s AI infrastructure. Through back-to-back talks, each presenter will showcase their application, the real-world challenge it solves, and the importance of network-proximate inference in enabling hyper-personalized, real-time use cases at scale—all delivered in a cost-efficient and compliant manner. See these applications in motion, hear how NVIDIA’s software stack helps to build and optimize them, and learn how telcos can unlock new revenue streams and return on investment by hosting and scaling these services on the AI grid.

speakers.name: Sree Sankar
speakers.bio: Sree is a visionary AI product and business executive and global head at NVIDIA, where she leads the strategy and vision for AI-GRID, the company’s distributed inference platform. With two decades of experience and eight years of AI/ML product leadership, she has delivered transformative impact across computer vision, natural language processing, recommendation systems, and search in ecommerce, retail-tech, software as a service, and advertising. Previously, as SVP at Grabango, she helped disrupt retail with computer-vision-powered checkout-free tech, scaling enterprise adoption, securing multiple 100-store expansion deals, and cutting operational costs through Gen AI-driven automation.

speakers.name: Kfir Aberman
speakers.bio: Kfir Aberman is a founding member at Decart AI, where he leads the San Francisco office and drives research-to-product efforts in real-time generative video. Previously, Kfir was a principal research scientist at Snap, leading the company’s Personalized Generative AI initiative, and before that, a researcher at Google. Kfir holds a Ph.D. in computer science from Tel Aviv University, and has published over 50 papers at top-tier venues with over 10,000 citations. He also serves as an area chair and committee member at leading conferences including SIGGRAPH, CVPR, and ICCV.

speakers.name: Willy Kuo
speakers.bio: Willy drives the company’s technical vision, focusing on AI and digital twin solutions for smart cities. With expertise in AI, machine learning, and system architecture, he's been instrumental in building scalable solutions to address urban challenges. Willy's leadership ensures Linker stays at the forefront of innovation, making significant contributions to smart city transformation.

speakers.name: Sharon Zhang
speakers.bio: Sharon Zhang serves as chief AI and technology officer of personal AI, where she pioneered the development of small language models that deliver programmability, precision, and privacy at scale. Sharon brings over 15 years of experience in AI and natural language processing, having built innovations across both large technology companies and startups. Sharon led development teams at major companies including Nuance Communications, Kaiser Permanente, LinkedIn, and Glint (acquired by LinkedIn). Sharon holds degrees from MIT in biology and electrical and computer science engineering. She lives in San Francisco.

sessions.title: AI Grid Tokenomics: Powering Distributed AI Inference at Scale
sessions.abstract: As AI usage explodes, optimizing economics and performance of inference is becoming just as critical as training the models themselves. A distributed AI grid makes tokenomics radically more efficient—improving cost per token, time-to-first-token, and token throughput while delivering deterministic performance for the applications that need it most, including highly interactive and latency-sensitive experiences. In this session, two companies advancing AI grids will share which applications they prioritize for distributed versus centralized deployments and how key workload key performance indicators (KPIs) drive those choices. See concrete examples, benchmark results, and economic models that illustrate how NVIDIA-powered distributed infrastructure can enable “least-cost, best-performance” serving at scale—and what that unlocks for the next wave of AI services and business models.

speakers.name: Nader Foroughi
speakers.bio:
Nader is a Distinguished Engineer at Comcast, where he is responsible for artificial intelligence, advanced compute and automation. Prior to joining Comcast, Nader was the Chief Technology Officer of Americas at Technetix, responsible for technology strategy and AI. His career also includes significant contributions at Shaw Communications, where he was responsible for access architecture and technology in conjunction with data sciences, making key advancements in proactive network maintenance, profile management application, and DOCSIS 4.0 development. Nader has a background in mathematics, engineering and systems architecture, with
numerous papers published spanning from DOCSIS 4.0 to applications of AI and quantum computing in telecommunications.

speakers.name: Chris Penrose
speakers.bio: As the global head of business development for telco at NVIDIA, Chris works across the telecoms ecosystem to drive the growth of NVIDIA’s hardware and software solutions. Coordinating across NVIDIA and partners, Chris helps to create telco-specific offerings that leverage AI/ML, generative AI, 5G, edge/cloud computing, and advanced networking. Previously, as the COO of FogHorn, Chris led the go-to-market efforts across business development, technical sales, strategic partnerships, third-party distribution, marketing, advertising, and public relations globally. He holds a B.S. in marketing and an M.B.A. from Indiana University. His personal passions are family, skiing, dining out, and travel.

speakers.name: Andy Champagne
speakers.bio: Andy Champagne is senior vice president in Akamai’s CTO organization, driving the company’s strategy for distributed cloud and low-latency AI at the edge. A 25-year veteran of Akamai, he helped build the foundational architecture of its global content delivery network and now spearheads efforts to bring compute and intelligence as close to users as physics allows. His work spans platform engineering, large-scale optimization, and early-stage venture partnerships shaping the next wave of cloud and AI innovation.

sessions.title: AI Grid for Robots: Applications and Infrastructure for Physical AI Powered by Telcos
sessions.abstract:
Physical AI is moving robots from labs into streets, offices, factories, and public spaces—where they must see, think, and act in milliseconds. Traditional cloud-centric architectures can’t meet the latency, reliability, and resiliency demands of these real-world uses, making a distributed AI grid essential. 

Two leading telecom operators will showcase physical AI use cases, demonstrating how AI-enhanced radio networks and distributed computing let robots offload heavy perception and decision workloads while maintaining real-time responsiveness and safety. The session will outline key architectural building blocks of an AI-native network, explore its evolution toward 6G, and explain why telecom AI grids are poised to become the default fabric for the next wave of physical AI.

speakers.name: Ryuji Wakikawa
speakers.bio: Ryuji Wakikawa serves as the vice president and head of the Research Institute of Advanced Technology at SoftBank Corp. He is also a project professor at the Global Research Institute at Keio University, Japan, where he received his Ph.D. in 2004. He also serves on the Board of Directors of the O-RAN Alliance and on the Governing Board of LF Networking. Currently, he leads SoftBank's research institute, where he focuses on the development of advanced technologies, architecture design, and shaping future visions. Ryuji's work spans areas such as AI-RAN, 5G Advanced, 6G, HAPS, non-terrestrial networks, LLM/transformer, and quantum technologies.

speakers.name: Grant Ries
speakers.bio: Grant Ries joined T-Mobile in 2023 as chief data and AI officer, where he leads a team dedicated to transforming customer experiences through advanced technologies. Previously, he was executive vice president of B2B and Emerging Markets at LiveRamp. He also co-founded two startups—BlueKai (acquired by Oracle) and Pacific Data Partners (acquired by LiveRamp)—and held leadership roles at companies like Oracle, YuMe, and Audience.

sessions.title: Autonomous Networks: The New Brain of Telco Operations
sessions.abstract:
Automation is not the same as autonomy. Automated networks follow predefined rules; autonomous networks understand context, analyze data, reason, act, and learn continuously. Autonomous networks are the next frontier for telecoms, and progress is accelerating rapidly. 

This panel brings together leading telecom operators and solution providers to show how agentic AI and large telco models are transforming operations. Speakers will share how they unify data across operations and business support systems and network domains and deploy AI agents that self-configure, self-optimize, and self-heal networks while improving performance and customer experience. Hear real-world examples of capabilities already in production, the NVIDIA software and infrastructure behind them, and what the next 12 months of innovation look like as operators advance toward higher levels of autonomy.

speakers.name: Lilach Ilan
speakers.bio: With 17 years of experience in the telecom industry, Lilac is a visionary leader who collaborates with leading telcos across the globe to revolutionize their operations through advanced AI technologies, specifically generative AI. Having served in various roles at AT&T, Lilac has a proven record of turning new technologies into successful businesses. Lilac holds bachelor's and master's of science degrees in economics and marketing from The Hebrew University.

speakers.name: Amol Phadke
speakers.bio: Amol Phadke is a senior executive business leader, providing technology and business leadership in all areas of AI, cloud, software networks and IT, big tech, and telecommunications industry, specializing in strategy definition, driving execution of large-scale engineering and innovation development/delivery units, and leading global multi-discipline teams. Amol is currently CTO at Tech Mahindra, supporting the CEO on various strategic initiatives across the company including elevating the communications Industry vertical globally. Prior to Tech Mahindra, Amol served as EVP and group CTO at Telenor Group, and a member of Telenor’s Group Executive Management Team.

speakers.name: Tunc Yorulmaz
speakers.bio: Tunc Yorulmaz is a seasoned executive with over 25 years of leadership experience in the telecom and high-tech industries, spanning roles across vendors, operators, and integrators. As senior managing director at Accenture, he heads the Global Networks practice, where he drives large-scale transformations and oversees complex sales engagements for corporate clients. His career includes senior positions at Ericsson, Turk Telekom, and Itochu, where he successfully led sales teams, developed business strategies and championed innovation.

speakers.name: Séainín McCoy
speakers.bio: With 13+ years’ experience in telecommunications, Séainín leads BT’s Network Data & AI engineering and strategy, delivering practical innovations in autonomous networks, AI-native operations, and generative AI. Her team transforms network telemetry into actionable insights, driving operational excellence and customer experience improvements at national scale. Previously, Séainín headed technical solutions for data enablement in TV and broadband, where she modernized hybrid data platforms, streamlined supplier ecosystems, and built high-performing teams. Séainín holds advanced degrees in data analytics and ML and software engineering, as well as a B.A. from Queen’s University Belfast.

speakers.name: Rajeev Koodli
speakers.bio: Dr. Rajeev Koodli is a distinguished global technology leader with over two decades of pioneering work in mobile communications, Internetworking, distributed cloud computing and AI. He is currently a Principal Fellow at SoftBank Corp., and SVP at SB Telecom America, a subsidiary of SoftBank Corporation. With his vast expertise in leadership, strategy, architecture, product development, research and innovation, Dr. Koodli has influenced the telecommunications and technology sectors through his transformative contributions to hyperscale cloud infrastructure, AI, 4G/5G systems and IP networking. His innovations have had significant impact on global mobile networking standards, and his technical leadership has contributed to reshaping the industry. His most recent work includes founding and launching the Large Telecom Models (LTM) and Transformer-based Radio Signal Processing initiatives at SoftBank. He started the Sunnyvale, California site and leads the AI Infrastructure Software (“Infrinia”) team and the project.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Accelerating Industrial Engineering: From Product Design to Manufacturing in the AI Supercomputing Era
sessions.abstract: Accelerated computing and AI supercomputing are redefining industrial engineering, spanning computational engineering (CAE) to semiconductor design and manufacturing. Together, these fields drive how the world conceives, simulates, and builds everything from advanced machinery to next-generation chips increasingly in the digital world before physical production begins. In this address, NVIDIA’s Tim Costa, general manager for Industrial and Computational Engineering, will show how CUDA-accelerated platforms, AI physics, DGX and cloud-scale AI infrastructure, and NVIDIA Omniverse are reshaping workflows across computer-aided engineering and semiconductor design and manufacturing. He will demonstrate how customers use GPU-accelerated solvers, AI physics models, and agentic AI to cut turnaround, broaden design exploration, and boost yield and reliability, revealing a unified accelerated-computing blueprint spanning classical, AI, and emerging quantum acceleration.

speakers.name: Timothy Costa
speakers.bio:
Dr. Timothy Costa is the General Manager for Industrial and Computational Engineering at NVIDIA, leading strategy and innovation across these sectors. He holds a Ph.D. in Mathematics focused on numerical modeling of fluids and semiconductors.
At NVIDIA, Tim has directed key initiatives in HPC, Quantum, and CUDA-X, including building the NVIDIA Quantum platform and launching the HPC SDK. He now drives the use of accelerated computing and AI to tackle complex challenges in industrial and semiconductor applications.

sessions.title: Scaling the AI Life Cycle: Next-Gen Training and Inference With Crusoe Cloud (Presented by Crusoe)
sessions.abstract:
Scaling complex models requires infrastructure that evolves with your code. From prototyping to deployment, learn how to optimize your NVIDIA infrastructure for training and inference workloads at scale. Join Erwan Menard, SVP of Product, for a technical look into Crusoe Cloud’s high-performance, energy-first platform and Dhruv Batra, Co-founder and Chief Scientist at Yutori, to hear how they are leveraging Crusoe’s Managed Inference service to re-imagine how people interact on the web

In this session, we will deep-dive into Crusoe’s latest capabilities, optimized for the newest NVIDIA GPU architectures. You will learn how Crusoe Cloud will help you navigate the technical demands of massive-scale model training, efficient fine-tuning, and low-latency / high throughput inference. Dhruv will join Erwan to share how Yutori brought their own model to Crusoe’s Managed Inference Service to run agents at scale and they will discuss how to optimize multi-architecture inference for the agentic era. Whether you are an ML engineer optimizing compute resources, a leader scaling production workloads, or a developer delivering the next AI-powered agent or application, this session will show you how to unlock the full potential of NVIDIA compute on Crusoe Cloud.

speakers.name: Erwan Menard
speakers.bio: Erwan leads the innovation and scaling of Crusoe Cloud to advance sustainable AI infrastructure solutions. Before joining Crusoe, he was the director of product management for cloud AI at Google Cloud, where he led outbound product management for the Vertex AI portfolio. His team was pivotal in enabling customers to build advanced AI and Gen AI-powered experiences, and in shaping Google Cloud's AI partnerships strategy. Before that, Erwan was the CEO of Elastifile, a software-defined storage company that was acquired by Google. He also played a key role in building Google Cloud Solution Engineering, developing programs for migration, application modernization, and data center transformation.

speakers.name: Dhruv Batra
speakers.bio:
Dhruv Batra is a co-founder and the Chief Scientist of Yutori, and an adjunct professor at Georgia Tech.

Previously, he was a Senior Director at Meta leading FAIR Embodied AI, and an Associate Professor in the School of Interactive Computing at Georgia Tech.

He works on understanding and advancing the limits of artificial intelligence (AI). More specifically, his research lies at the intersection of machine learning and computer vision, with forays into robotics and natural language processing.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models

sessions.title: Sovereign AI for Telcos: Offtake Stories and Monetization Models From Around the World
sessions.abstract: Sovereign AI is rapidly becoming a new growth engine for telcos as they build national AI clouds and monetize them in very different ways. This panel brings together leading operators from across the world to share how they are turning NVIDIA-powered sovereign AI infrastructure into real revenue and real impact for their nations. Panelists will share their customer stories and discuss a variety of business models. They will also highlight how innovation hubs are enabling local developers, startups, and enterprises to build country-specific AI applications. The session will close with concrete lessons learned, demand generation, and a call to action for telcos and app builders looking to participate in the sovereign AI opportunity.

speakers.name: Joao Kluck Gomes
speakers.bio:
Joao is Senior Director of Business Development for AI Factories at NVIDIA, where he leads global initiatives helping telecom operators evolve into providers of sovereign AI infrastructure, platforms and services. With more than two decades of experience,  Joao has led product management, business development and technical sales across Juniper Networks, Nokia and Siemens in AI, cloud and edge spaces.

He specializes in creating new solution businesses end to end — from concept and ecosystem development to go-to-market design and large-scale sales execution with strategic partners and operators worldwide. Joao is passionate about the transformational impact of technology, and his proposed innovations include a granted U.S. patent (US 9,439,106) in the areas of mobile user experience and QoS. Joao holds a B.Eng. degree and an Executive MBA from the University of São Paulo.

speakers.name: Chris Madan
speakers.bio: Chris leads TELUS Digital, with accountability for the digital client experience and commercial outcomes. He's best known for delivering large-scale transformational change and his ability to drive business impact in a competitive market. As a successful visible minority leader, Chris is committed to inclusion and has chaired a number of diversity groups with organizations he has worked for. Chris holds a number of professional designations in accounting and marketing, serves as a member of a nonprofit board and audit committee in the medical field, and holds an M.B.A. from the Richard Ivey School of Business.

speakers.name: Vikram Sinha
speakers.bio: Vikram Sinha is a seasoned global telecom executive who has worked in various markets in Asia and Africa and has a strong record of driving top-line growth, increasing profitability, and building teams across diverse industries and geographies. He spent nearly a decade with Bharti Airtel in India and Africa, serving as COO of Bharti Airtel Congo Brazzaville and managing director of Bharti Airtel Seychelles.  He then moved to the Ooredoo Group where was CEO, director, and COO of Indosat Ooredoo. Vikram is passionate that technology benefits every Indonesian, helping bridge the digital divide, unlock opportunities, and create meaningful impact.

speakers.name: Ahmed El Beheiry
speakers.bio:
Ahmed is an entrepreneur, technology executive & an AI expert. He joined Cassava Technologies Group in May 2002 as Sr. Advisor for the Group President & CEO for AI and is now the Group COO & CEO Cassava AI. Ahmed was the Managing Director & CEO for “Telecom Egypt” between 2017-2019. He started his career as a design engineer for mobile networks at Alcatel between 1995-1998, Ahmed then joined “MobiNil” (currently Orange Egypt) as the Network 
Development Manager in 1998. In 2002 he joined the company executive committee as Networks and Roaming Director till 2007.

In 2007, Ahmed started his entrepreneurship career founding “Telecomax Group for Investment and Development”, a group of companies providing end-to-end engineering services in the fields of telecommunication and Information Technology services in Africa and the Middle East, he served as the group Chairman & CEO till 2016. In 2013, Ahmed co-founded “IDEA; Investment for Development in Emerging Africa” focusing on human necessities technologies as “water out of thin air” and “solar power”, he served as the company Managing Director till 2016. Ahmed also founded “WAKANDAI ventures” in 2019, a technology savvy investment firm focusing on investing in AI startups in Africa & Middle East.

Ahmed is a Harvard Business School Alum since 2020 after completing HBS General Management Program, he also concluded HBS programs on Data & AI. Ahmed holds a M.Sc. in Mobile Communication from University of Alexandria since 1997, he also holds MBA from the University of Rennes since 2005. Ahmed has several research contributing in the field of Mobile Communication that was published in the Telecom World Conference in California in 1997 and in the International Institute for Communication and Information Technology (IEEE) Magazine May 2001.

speakers.name: Tony Ha
speakers.bio:
Professional Experience
• Head of AI DC Biz. Development, SK Telecom (24.12 ~ Current)
  - Responsible for leading AI infrastructure business at SKT
• Member, Board of Directors, Penguin Solutions (25.01 ~ Current)
• Member, Board of Directors, Rebellions (24.12 ~ Current)
• Chief Development Officer, SK Telecom (22.04 ~ 24.12)
• CEO, SAPEON Inc. / Korea (24.06 ~ 11)
• Executive of Innovation Suite, SK Telecom (21.01 ~ 22.03)
• Head of Management Planning Group, SK Telecom (20.01 ~ 12)
• Head of Global Alliance Group, SK Telecom (19.01 ~ 12)

Education
• Indiana University - Kelley School of Business (MBA)
• University of Seoul, Bachelors Degree in Economics

sessions.title: A Deep Dive Into Two Telco-Built Sovereign AI Clouds
sessions.abstract: More than 20 telcos worldwide have already built sovereign AI infrastructure powered by NVIDIA, with many more underway. These telco-operated national AI clouds are becoming strategic assets, combining local control and regulatory compliance with high-performance AI capabilities to serve priorities ranging from secure government workloads and public services to industrial clouds for sectors like manufacturing. This session brings together two leading operators who will dive deep into their vision and journey for sovereign AI in their markets: how they designed and built their infrastructure, what they are optimized for, how adoption is ramping, and where their roadmaps are headed. Gain a front-row view into how telcos are turning sovereign AI into real platforms for innovation, growth, and national resilience.

speakers.name: Bill Chang
speakers.bio: Bill Chang is CEO of Singtel’s Digital InfraCo and CEO/executive director of Nxera, Singtel’s regional data center business. Previously, Bill was CEO of Group Enterprise since 2012. Bill joined Singtel in 2005 as executive vice president of corporate business, and subsequently became managing director, business group.

speakers.name: Peter Lorenz
speakers.bio:
Peter Lorenz serves on the Board of Management of T-Systems International GmbH and has headed the digital area as Senior Vice President Digital Solutions since 2019. He leads over 10,000 specialists worldwide in executing large-scale digital transformation initiatives. He is known for enabling large and medium-sized organizations to modernize platforms and implement scalable digital solutions. 
With decades of leadership experience in enterprise IT, he bridges strategy and execution in complex international environments. Driven by deep tech and innovation expertise, he continuously advances technology driven business growth. His latest project, the development of Germany’s first AI factory with Nvidia, enables T Systems to build a sovereign AI infrastructure for Germany and accelerate enterprise AI transformation, delivering large scale platforms and regulated solutions for industrial, public, and healthcare sectors through strategic partnerships and mission critical deployments.
His background includes senior roles at T-Systems, SAP, and Siemens Nixdorf, driving large-scale systems integration and innovation initiatives.

sessions.title: Wireless Network Digital Twins: Use Cases for 5G and 6G
sessions.abstract: Wireless network digital twins are a critical building block for AI‑native networks, especially as next‑generation 6G systems will be born first in simulation. NVIDIA Aerial Omniverse Digital Twin (AODT) creates realistic, physics‑based replicas of wireless networks that reflect radio conditions, topology, and traffic patterns, allowing operators and partners to model, test, and optimize 5G and 6G safely before touching the live network. In this session, NVIDIA and partners will show how AODT supports workflows from network planning and optimization to validating new AI‑for‑RAN algorithms and agentic AI scenarios, where agents explore actions in the twin first. Speakers will share real use cases and how integrating AODT helps them create and run more intelligent networks today, and why digital twins are becoming the default way to build AI‑native wireless networks of the future.

speakers.name: Satoshi Konishi
speakers.bio: Satoshi joined KDD (former name of KDDI) in 1993 after receiving his M.Eng. from the University of Electro-Communications in Japan. He was involved in R&D for wireless communication systems including low-Earth-orbit satellites, mesh-type fixed wireless access, and mobile communications. He led development of LTE base stations and LTE commercialization from 2008-12. He also led the performance evaluation technical group for IMT-Advanced systems in ITU-R, and chaired the technical evaluation group in 3GPP2. Satoshi held titles including general manager, EVP, head of Advanced Technology Laboratories, and head of mobile access technology before being appointed president and CEO in April 2025.

speakers.name: Samir Kumar
speakers.bio:
Samir Kumar has been leading the Services unit for Nokia Mobile Networks since the establishment of the new Mobile Networks business group in 2021. In addition, he heads the Services unit for the Americas market, allowing him to co-create directly with top-tier customers to develop a comprehensive service portfolio across the entire network lifecycle.
Samir pioneered the concept of Global Delivery Centers (GDCs) in the telecom industry and spearheaded their establishment for Nokia in India, Portugal, and Romania. These GDCs provide highly automated remote services to Nokia customers globally.
