He has also been driving the development of a digital service portfolio, while creating best-in-class automated tools for the design, deployment, optimization, and operation of networks for both Communication Service Providers and enterprises.

Samir joined Nokia in 1997 and has held various leadership roles across Sales, Pre-Sales, Product Management, Learning Solutions, and Services. Before taking on his global role, he led the Services division in India and the Middle East and Africa region, where he successfully delivered major rollout and Managed Services projects for key customers, including Bharti, Vodafone, Idea, STC, Vodacom, and Orange. He also played a key role as part of the core team in previous mergers and acquisitions with Siemens, Motorola, Alcatel, and Lucent.

Samir holds a bachelor’s degree in Electronics and Electrical Communication from Thapar University, India. Currently based in Dallas, USA, he has previously lived and worked in India, the Philippines, Taiwan, the UAE, and Finland, gaining valuable experience in leading multicultural teams across diverse environments.

speakers.name: CC Chong
speakers.bio: CC Chong is the senior director and head of Aerial product management at NVIDIA. Before joining NVIDIA, she was most recently senior director and GM of the wireless and access business unit in the Intel Programmable Solutions Group. CC received her Ph.D., in electronics and electrical engineering from the University of Edinburgh in Scotland and her bachelor's in electronics and electrical engineering from the University of Manchester. She was a recipient of the Ten Outstanding Young Malaysian Awards under the category “Scientific and Technological Development” in 2006.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Building America’s AI-Native 6G: Architecture, Timelines, and Ecosystem Innovations
sessions.abstract: AI‑native 6G is a strategic opportunity for the United States to lead the next era of wireless infrastructure, not just adopt it. Building on the AI-WIN initiative and the first all‑American AI‑native wireless stack announced in 2025, this session focuses on moving from concepts and demos to deployable systems. Speakers from government, industry, and the broader ecosystem will explain why AI‑native 6G matters for U.S. competitiveness and security, how policy and public‑private partnerships accelerate progress, and why an implementation‑first approach must advance with standards. The session will cover deployment timelines, key milestones, progress on AI-RAN for 5G and 6G using the NVIDIA AI Aerial platform, and early lessons from AI‑native stack trials, including pre‑6G applications and innovations toward commercial‑grade AI‑native 6G networks.

speakers.name: Thomas W. Rondeau
speakers.bio: Dr. Tom Rondeau is the Principal Director for the FutureG Office for the US Department of War, serving in the Office of the Undersecretary of War for Research and Engineering (OUSW(R&E). In this role, Dr. Rondeau is responsible for the research, funding, and execution of programs to advance warfighting capabilities using future-generation wireless technologies. Before assuming his role as Principal Director of the FutureG Office, Dr. Rondeau spent more than six years as a Defense Advanced Research Project Agency (DARPA) program manager, where he led efforts that challenged and advanced studies in a variety of warfighting domains, earning him the Distinguished Public Service Medal. Prior to joining DARPA, Dr. Rondeau led the GNU Radio project, consulted on wireless communications problem sets and worked as a visiting researcher with the University of Pennsylvania and as an Adjunct with the IDA Center for Communications Research in Princeton, NJ. Dr. Rondeau holds a Ph.D. in electrical engineering from Virginia Tech, where his dissertation won the Council of Graduate Schools’ 2007 Outstanding Dissertation Award in math, science, and engineering

speakers.name: Ankur Kapoor
speakers.bio: Ankur Kapoor is the Chief Network Officer at T-Mobile US, leading the company’s end-to-end network strategy and innovation agenda. With over 26 years of global experience in the wireless industry, he is responsible for driving the evolution of T-Mobile’s mobile core network, radio access network (RAN), AI and automation initiatives, device strategy, emerging technologies, and network labs. Ankur spearheads the transformation of T-Mobile’s network through cloud-native architectures, AI-driven automation, and next-generation device integration, ensuring world-class performance, reliability, and customer experience. Under his leadership, T-Mobile is advancing the use of AI in RAN optimization and accelerating innovation through its network labs ecosystem, fostering rapid testing and deployment of innovative technologies. He leads T-Mobile’s forward-looking network strategy, driving industry collaboration and research on 6G and future connectivity frameworks that will define the next decade of mobile innovation. His vision positions T-Mobile at the forefront of the global wireless landscape—pioneering breakthroughs that shape the future of how people and businesses connect. Ankur also serves as a technology liaison with government affairs and regulatory bodies, advocating for progressive U.S. wireless policies and strengthening the nation’s leadership in advanced communications. Ankur holds an honors degree in Electronics and Telecommunications, graduating with distinction.

speakers.name: Soma Velayutham
speakers.bio: Soma Velayutham is the vice president for AI and telecoms at NVIDIA, where he is responsible for developing and evangelizing AI into telecoms and wireless communications. He is a successful serial intrapreneur and product leader with more than 25 years in the software and high-tech industries. He has incubated and launched multiple software products globally for large corporations. He has 11 patents and drives the full product life-cycle experience, from R&D to market.

speakers.name: Bill Vass
speakers.bio: William (Bill) Vass is chief technology officer at Booz Allen, where he sets the firm’s technical vision and strategy, driving the development of advanced and dual-use technologies across AI, space systems, quantum, robotics, and simulation. He brings more than 40 years of experience spanning government and industry, including serving as vice president of engineering at Amazon Web Services, where he led the development of over 60 core AWS services, as well as senior executive roles across the U.S. Department of Defense, Fortune 100 companies, and high-growth startups.

sessions.title: How Sony PlayStation Built a Multi-Tenant GPU Cluster to Accelerate R&D
sessions.abstract: This talk outlines how we design and operate a global GPU research cluster that supports AI and visual computing work for our console platform and game development studios. We use NVIDIA GPUs to train advanced models that run on PlayStation hardware to improve rendering, gameplay systems, and overall player experience. Running shared GPU infrastructure at scale brings challenges: securing data center space, long procurement cycles, multi-tenant Kubernetes management, and noisy-neighbor pressures as research needs shift. We will share lessons in cluster design, planning, security, and operations that help studios innovate and push the console experience forward.

speakers.name: Erick Flores
speakers.bio: Erick Flores is a director of visual computing and AI infrastructure at Sony Interactive Entertainment, where he leads global efforts in machine learning research, advanced rendering, and high-performance compute systems. He oversees the design and operation of large-scale GPU platforms that support R&D teams worldwide and drives initiatives in video quality, real-time graphics, and next-generation AI workflows. His work focuses on building the tools, data, and compute environments needed to accelerate innovation across the company.

speakers.name: Richard Martin
speakers.bio: Richard Martin is a Staff Machine Learning Engineer at Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) within the Game Development and Future Technology group, on the Visual Computing Group (VCG) team. He serves as ML System Architect and technical lead for SIE’s Deep Learning Research System (DLRS), versions 2 and 3 (DLRS 2 and DLRS 3). From 2022–2026 he has focused on turning advanced GPU hardware, high performance storage, and modern data practices into a reliable, reproducible ML platform.

sessions.title: Engineering Real-World LLM Inference: Bridging Open-Source and Production Systems
sessions.abstract: In production, LLM inference performance is shaped by model architecture, hardware, workload patterns, and service-level agreement constraints. This session presents a practical workflow for profiling inference stacks, identifying real bottlenecks, and evaluating optimizations across kernels, scheduling, and system design, showing how to leverage fast-moving open-source innovations while applying our own enhancements to achieve production-grade performance and stability, and how to feed lessons from production back into upstream development.

speakers.name: Yineng Zhang
speakers.bio: Yineng Zhang is a senior director at Together AI and SGLang Core Maintainer. He has initiated and led the end-to-end DeepSeek V3/R1 effort on SGLang—from Day 0 support and performance optimization to large-scale EP deployment and GB200 NVL72 integration—driving roadmap, coordination, and execution across community collaborations that pushed the frontier of open-source inference engines.

sessions.title: 从云端到本地，重塑座舱 AI
sessions.abstract: 从本地化模型开发与工程化部署、推理，展开讨论 AI 座舱助手应用的发展

speakers.name: 婧文 陈
speakers.bio: Jingwen has cross-industry expertise covering mobile device R&D, automotive/internet product management, strategic operations, and end-to-end solution leadership.

sessions.title: Accelerating Small Molecule Drug Discovery for Hard-to-Target Proteins
sessions.abstract: Learn about the practicalities and challenges of developing a flow-matching sequence-to-ensemble model for intrinsically disordered proteins, and an evaluation framework for ensemble prediction. You’ll see how augmented training data, experimental biophysical constraints, and dedicated benchmarks enable robust conformational ensembles where conventional predictors fail, and how these ensembles plug into GPU-accelerated protein structure generation workflows for hard-to-target oncology, central nervous system, and immunology programs.

speakers.name: Carlo Fisicaro
speakers.bio: Carlo was born in Verbania, Italy, in 1992. He earned a bachelor’s degree in physics from the University of Turin in 2015, then went on to complete an M.Sc. in physics of complex systems there in 2017. During his master’s studies, he collaborated with the ISI Data Science Institute in Turin to develop an AI and quantum computing‑based tool for natural language processing applications. In 2018, he joined Moviri in Milan as a data scientist. By 2020 he had advanced to senior data scientist and led the company’s modeling and AI activities. He's served as chief technology officer at Peptone since 2021.

speakers.name: Michele Invernizzi
speakers.bio:
After obtaining a bachelor’s and master’s in physics at the University of Milan, Michele moved to Switzerland, where he earned a Ph.D. in computational physics at ETH Zurich under the supervision of Prof. Michele Parrinello. His main focus was developing novel enhanced sampling methods to extend the scope of molecular dynamics simulations.

Michele later moved to Berlin for a postdoc in AI for the sciences in the group of Prof. Frank Noé. There, he studied generative models based on deep artificial neural networks, such as normalizing flows and diffusion models. His main interest remains atomistic simulations of physical systems, from condensed matter to biomolecules.

sessions.title: Optimize KV Caches for LLM Inference: Dynamo KVBM, FlexKV,  LMCache
sessions.abstract: This tutorial provides a practitioner-led deep dive into why KV-cache design is a first-order performance lever for production LLM inference, directly impacting throughput, tail latency, and GPU utilization. Drawing on real production experience, the session walks through KV-cache design principles and implementation approaches used in FlexKV, LMCache, and Dynamo KVBM. The discussion highlights practical lessons from deploying KV caching with modern attention patterns such as MQA, GQA, DS3.2, and speculative decoding. You'll leave with actionable recipes, code patterns, and profiling techniques to integrate KV caching into existing inference engines and scale beyond a single node.

speakers.name: Fan YE
speakers.bio: Ye Fan is director of accelerated computing at Tencent Cloud (TACO), where he leads heterogeneous computing and AI infrastructure. A pioneer in the field, Fan founded and drove the development of the TACO series of acceleration components, which serve as the core AI engine powering key internal and external AI services. TACO-X stands out as the industry’s first self-developed inference framework capable of supporting full-modality models—including LLM, VLM, and DIT—within a unified system. The TACO portfolio also encompasses TACO-LLM, FlexKV, TACO-DIT, and TACO-Train. Earlier in his career, Fan was a founding developer of NVIDIA TensorRT and later initiated the PAI-Blade project.

speakers.name: Junchen Jiang
speakers.bio: Junchen Jiang is a co-founder and CEO of TensorMesh, faculty lead of LMCache Lab, and an associate professor of computer science at the University of Chicago. He's worked on large-scale systems and their intersections with ML, and particularly, KV cache in LLM inference. He received his Ph.D. from Carnegie Mellon University in 2017 and B.S. from Tsinghua University (Yao Class) in 2011. He has received CMU Computer Science Doctoral Dissertation Award (2017), two Google Faculty Research Awards on ML Systems (2018, 2026), and an NSF CAREER Award, as well as best paper and best paper runner-up awards. For more information: https://people.cs.uchicago.edu/~junchenj/

speakers.name: Ziqi Fan
speakers.bio: Ziqi Fan is a core developer of Dynamo KVBM. Before joining NVIDIA, he worked at Uber, LinkedIn, Samsung, and Novumind, gaining broad experience across diverse ML deployment scenarios including training, recommendation systems, and large-scale serving. Ziqi holds a Ph.D. from the University of Minnesota.

speakers.name: Vikram Sharma Mailthody
speakers.bio: Vikram is a founding member of the Systems and Storage research group at NVIDIA Research, with a special focus on addressing fundamental systems-level challenges in emerging data-center workloads and scalable GPU memory and storage systems designs

sessions.title: Build an AI-Ready Data Center: Practical Insights From YTL, Wiwynn, and NVIDIA (Presented by Wiwynn Corp.)
sessions.abstract: This session reveals how YTL partnered with Wiwynn and NVIDIA to build an AI-ready data center. We’ll walk through the NVIS deployment methodology—covering site qualification, high-density GPU rack integration, and power/cooling validation under real-world stress. You’ll also learn how to implement end-to-end observability and manage liquid-cooling risks. Finally, we share benchmarking results using MLPerf and AI workloads to confirm the environment meets AI-ready standards.

speakers.name: Ted Pang
speakers.bio: Ted has led Wiwynn's software engineering team since 2017. He's responsible for strategy, architecture, and software development, covering data center management, testing, and AI. With over 30 years in the IT industry—spanning server, storage, and cloud computing—Ted brings extensive experience. He earned his graduate degree from the University of Florida.

sessions.title: Scaling Toward Quantum-GPU Supercomputing
sessions.abstract: Join Andrew Dzurak as he explores how integrating GPU supercomputing with quantum processors, enabled by NVIDIA’s NVQLink, is driving the development of high-performance, scalable hybrid quantum-classical systems. Andrew will share Diraq’s work with NVIDIA and outline a practical path toward building the next generation of quantum computing architectures.

speakers.name: Andrew Dzurak
speakers.bio:
Andrew is a global leader in nanoelectronics and quantum computing technologies. He is CEO and founder of Diraq, a company that is redefining scalable quantum computing by enabling millions of qubits on a single chip—far beyond the hundreds available today—to unlock practical, commercial quantum applications.  

Over his career, Andrew has played a pivotal role in fostering enduring scientific and strategic collaborations between the United States and Australia, and has raised over $150 million in venture capital and research funding. This funding has supported the work carried out by Diraq’s founding team over the past 25 years to develop its current IP portfolio in 12 patent families.

sessions.title: AI Factory in Action:  Achieving Agile Automation With Gen AI Agents and Digital Twins (Presented by ASUS)
sessions.abstract:
As manufacturing shifts from mass production to high-mix, low-volume demands, flexibility becomes key. Traditional automation faces a critical bottleneck: rigidity. We'll explore the paradigm shift toward the "AI factory," a facility where intelligence is manufactured alongside products. ASUS will present a full-stack architecture with our solution partners that integrates agentic AI—powered by generative AI and vision language models—with digital twins built on NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and OpenUSD.

Discover how to build a high-performance, closed-loop system where AI agents perceive changes, reason through complex tasks, and validate strategies within a high-fidelity virtual environment using NVIDIA Isaac Sim before physical execution. By leveraging NVIDIA Omniverse for digital twins, manufacturers can simulate edge cases and optimize robotic workflows in a risk-free virtual space.

speakers.name: Joseph Lu
speakers.bio: Joseph Lu specializes in storage product and solution development, leading teams across solution product management, storage product management, and marketing. Joseph works closely with ecosystem partners to integrate storage software with ASUS hardware platforms, delivering end-to-end storage solutions that address enterprise and AI-driven workloads.

speakers.name: Jesse Chen
speakers.bio: Jesse Chen, CEO of Spingence Technology, brings extensive expertise in machine automation, instrument testing, and applied AI, including machine learning and deep learning. With years of frontline experience serving manufacturing customers—most notably as a district sales engineer at National Instruments, he possesses a deep understanding of customer pain points, production environments, and industry workflows. Jesse combines strong technical foundations with rich industry know-how to translate advanced technologies into practical, high-impact solutions, driving innovation while building long-term, trusted customer partnerships.

sessions.title: Building the Enterprise AI Factory:  A Unified Stack With ASUS, IBM, and NVIDIA (Presented by ASUS)
sessions.abstract:
Most enterprise AI initiatives fail to scale—not because of models, but due to fragmented infrastructure, data bottlenecks, and weak governance. GPUs alone are not an AI factory.

In this session, ASUS, NVIDIA, and IBM present a validated, converged AI factory architecture that transforms raw data into trusted intelligence for agentic AI and retrieval-augmented generation at scale. This end-to-end solution combines NVIDIA Certified servers, NVIDIA AI Enterprise and NIMs, and IBM intelligent storage to accelerate inferencing while ensuring security and governance.

Learn how Red Hat OpenShift, NVIDIA GPU Operator, NVIDIA NIM Operator, and IBM Content Aware Storage enable containerized AI deployments from the data center to the edge—supported by validated blueprints and centralized management for faster, more reliable AI production.

speakers.name: Alber Wu
speakers.bio: Alber Wu leads the Solution PM, Software R&D, and Solution Marketing teams at ASUS. His division focuses on delivering integrated solutions that seamlessly combine server and storage hardware with customized software, offering comprehensive, end-to-end solutions to meet diverse enterprise requirements.​

speakers.name: Vincent Hsu
speakers.bio: Vincent (Yu-Cheng) Hsu is an IBM Fellow, Chief Technology Officer, and Vice President of IBM Storage, with more than 30 years of leadership in storage systems architecture, innovation, and strategy. He drives IBM Storage’s technical vision across hybrid cloud, AI, and cybersecurity, leading research, architecture, and solution development. A Master Inventor with 106 granted patents, Vincent was named an IBM Fellow in 2015 for his transformative contributions to storage innovation. He represents IBM on the Ceph Community Governance Board Vincent holds an MS in Computer Engineering and an MBA from the University of Arizona.

sessions.title: Accelerate Hybrid Quantum-Classical Research at Scale With NVIDIA CUDA-Q (Presented by Foxconn)
sessions.abstract: This talk explains why Hon Hai Research Institute’s integrated strategy for advancing quantum science across the full stack requires an integrated strategy, developing both better hardware and applications algorithms together. We'll outline Hon Hai Research Institute’s approach to this—including Foxconn’s ion-trap roadmap—from first-generation blade-type traps to the establishment of Taiwan’s first industry-based ion-trap quantum computing laboratory and the creation of multi-layer and chip-based architectures. We'll also highlight advancements from Hon Hai Research Institute in software and theory for areas including quantum error correction, efficient tomography and noise benchmarking, quantum machine learning, and quantum networking.

speakers.name: Min Hsiu Hsieh
speakers.bio: Since January 2021, Min-Hsiu Hsieh has served as the director of the Hon Hai Quantum Computing Research Center in Taiwan. Previously, he was an associate professor at the University of Technology Sydney in Australia and a member of the UTS Centre for Quantum Software and Information from 2014-20. From 2010-12, he was a postdoc at the University of Cambridge in the U.K. Before that, he worked as a research scientist with the ERATO-SORST Quantum Computation and Information Project in Japan, while also being affiliated with the Department of Computer Science at the University of Tokyo. From 2014-18, he held an Australian Research Council Future Fellowship.

sessions.title: Supercharging Responsible AI Innovation: Compute, Synthetic Data, and Regulatory-Informed Design
sessions.abstract:
As AI adoption accelerates across financial services, firms face a dual challenge: moving fast enough to stay competitive while meeting growing expectations for safety, resilience, and governance.

The Financial Conduct Authority’s Supercharged Sandbox addresses this by enabling firms to develop and test advanced AI use cases using high-quality synthetic data, secure compute, and structured regulatory engagement.

Drawing on insights from the first cohort, this session shares how firms experienced the sandbox and how it reshaped their approach to AI development and assurance. Firms tested complex models against realistic scenarios, explored cases difficult to observe in live data, and integrated governance earlier in the development life cycle. The session highlights practical lessons and design patterns relevant beyond financial services.

speakers.name: Colin Payne
speakers.bio: Colin Payne is head of innovation at the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA), where he leads the FCA’s innovation support and engagement across programs including the Innovation Lab, Regulatory Sandbox, and the FCA’s wider innovation initiatives around AI and emerging technologies.

speakers.name: Savannah Price
speakers.bio: Savannah is the Founder and CEO of Serene, a UK fintech using behavioural intelligence to help financial institutions identify risk earlier, reduce harm, and enable safer growth. Serene works with banks and lenders across credit risk, vulnerability, and scam prevention, and was one of the firms selected for the FCA's inaugural Supercharged Sandbox. Recognised as FinTech London’s Rising Star 2025, Savannah frequently speaks on financial inclusion, consumer protection, and outcomes-led financial services. She is passionate about enabling earlier, more human support through better use of data and decisioning.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Empower US Manufacturing Through Wistron's Smart Manufacturing (Presented by Wistron Corp.)
sessions.abstract: Join Wistron technology leaders to explore how the company’s digital twin platform, built on NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and OpenUSD, is accelerating factory deployment, from Hsinchu to Dallas. Discover how these factory-scale digital twins are enabling many use cases; including streamlined factory design, layout validation, and manufacturing process simulation in the early planning. Get a deep dive into how these digital twins are enabling virtual commissioning and helping identify integration issues among robots, conveyors, and automation systems before physical installation. Learn about the real business outcomes being generated across Wistron’s operations, from faster iteration cycles to fewer deployment risks and eliminated rework during ramp-up.

speakers.name: John Lu
speakers.bio: Wistron Plant manager John Lu oversees the NVIDIA product line and has 20 years of manufacturing expertise. Before that, he served as global IE Division director, focusing on long-term efficiency improvements. He is passionate about utilizing cutting-edge technology to enhance production lines. After encountering Omniverse, he integrated it into the production management, striving to achieve digital twin capabilities and operational excellence.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: The 2026 Infrastructure, Operations, and Cloud Services Agenda: Scale Agentic AI With NVIDIA Nemotron 3 (Presented by Vultr)

speakers.name: Kevin Cochrane
speakers.bio: Kevin is a 25-plus-year pioneer in the digital marketing and digital experience space. Kevin pioneered the creation of the web content management market in 1996, open-source enterprise content management in 2005, and later digital experience platforms in 2016. Kevin is now building Vultr's global brand presence as a leader in the independent cloud platform market and composable infrastructure for organizations worldwide.

sessions.title: Industrial Takeoff: Reinventing Operations with Physical AI at Scale (Presented by Accenture)
sessions.abstract:
Industrial operations are entering a decisive new phase. As manufacturers face rising pressures on resilience, sustainability, productivity, and geopolitical exposure, emerging technologies—Physical AI, Robotics, and next generation digital twins—are converging to redefine how factories and supply chains operate.

Discover how Accenture and Airbus are reinventing operations—from foundational technologies to transformative business applications. With NVIDIA Omniverse and Isaac Sim, Accenture can enable high-fidelity simulation, synthetic data generation, and scalable AI training across interoperable industrial digital twin environments. With this foundation for Physical AI, the industry is ready for takeoff.

speakers.name: Christian Souche
speakers.bio: Christian leads robotics activities at Accenture Innovation, working at the intersection of human, business, and technology trends to imagine and launch the next generation of robots in industrial and non-industrial domains (such as retail, consumer packaged goods, banking, utilities, and smart cities). Christian specifically fosters innovation, sustainability, inclusion, and positive impact for society through robotics and creativity.

speakers.name: Pierre-Jean Rivière
speakers.bio: Pierre-Jean Rivière is a key leader in Airbus's Digital Design Manufacturing Services (DDMS) program, driving digital twin solutions. Bringing 15 years of entrepreneurial experience—including founding a company focused on improving passenger experience in complex environments like airports—his work at Airbus involves deploying IoT and Mixed Reality to boost operations and collaboration. He currently defines the common strategy and trajectory for federating Airbus Digital Twins (Product, Industrial, and Service) across divisions, ensuring timely decisions and optimization throughout the product lifecycle.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: vLLM in 2026: Architectural Challenges and Performance Optimizations
sessions.abstract: As LLMs grow in size, context length, and architectural complexity, vLLM must evolve to meet new performance and scalability challenges. This talk presents key improvements in vLLM's core architecture, including a GPU-first design for zero CPU overheads and an architecture for cluster-scale serving deployment. It also highlights major optimizations in KV cache management and GPU kernels. Gain a detailed technical view of how vLLM is advancing to deliver next-level performance on NVIDIA GPUs at scale.

speakers.name: Woosuk Kwon
speakers.bio: Woosuk Kwon is CTO at Inferact and co-creator and co-lead of vLLM, the most popular open-source LLM inference engine. He completed his Ph.D. at UC Berkeley and has been recognized with open-source awards from a16z and Sequoia.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)

sessions.title: Desk-to-Scale: Engineering Sovereign Financial Agents With Open Tools and Edge AI (Presented by HP)
sessions.abstract: Financial institutions face a dilemma: build complex AI agents using the latest innovation, but without risking sensitive data leaving their secure perimeter. We will demonstrate how to bridge this gap by combining high-performance edge AI with a frictionless developer workflow leveraging open-source tooling. We explore how teams from Red Hat and Columbia University's Secure FinAI Lab utilized the HP ZGX Nano and the ZGX Toolkit to transform a bare-metal AI station into a fully governed "AI factory" in under 30 minutes—automating the setup and supporting the rapid development of innovative solutions to real-world financial problems. See how the ZGX Toolkit enables a seamless "desk-to-scale" workflow, where an Anti-Money Laundering (AML) detective agent achieves an 80% success in identifying financial crime topologies, is built, tested, and audited locally before being deployed to the cloud.

speakers.name: Andrea Schiliro'
speakers.bio: Andrea Schilirò leads solution product marketing for HP’s AI Solutions portfolio, HP ZGX, HP Z Boost, and HP Z Centralized Compute. With over 10 years in advanced engineering, he drives adoption through strategic messaging and enablement, blending technical marketing, product management, and AI integration across edge computing and high‑performance workflows to accelerate enterprise transformation.

speakers.name: Vincent Caldeira
speakers.bio: Vincent Caldeira serves as the chief technology officer for Red Hat in the Asia-Pacific region (APAC), where he is responsible for both technology strategy and emerging technology engineering. He is also charged with forging high-impact partnerships with key strategic customers, and ultimately helping to shape the company's technological vision.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - 3D Model Generation

sessions.title: Five Bottlenecks, Five Fixes: How We Avoid Leaving Training Performance on the Table
sessions.abstract: GPU clusters are fast, until they're not. We'll walk through five performance pitfalls from real researcher code, the fixes that made them disappear, and how to use technologies like CUDA streams, CUDA graphs, and custom kernels along the way.

speakers.name: Sylvain Gugger
speakers.bio: Sylvain Gugger is a machine learning engineer at Jane Street, mainly working on performance on GPUs for training and inference. He started his journey in machine learning in 2017, first at fast.ai to democratize deep learning, then at Hugging Face, where he was a core maintainer of the Transformers library and created the Accelerate library.

speakers.name: Corwin de Zahr
speakers.bio: Corwin de Zahr is a computer scientist, performance engineer, hacker, and ML Engineer at Jane Street. After several years working on high performance trading systems, Corwin built Jane Street’s first GPU workload orchestration system, then the inference platform, and now spends his time back on optimizing ML training. Outside of work, Corwin and his team organize PlaidCTF and have won DEFCON CTF 6 times.

sessions.title: From AI Factories to the Edge: Architecting Distributed Inference to Unlock Enterprise ROI (Presented by Akamai)
sessions.abstract: AI inference and agents are forcing infrastructure to evolve. As interaction shifts from human to machine-centric, unlocking latency, cost, and performance advantages will be critical. AI Factories excel at foundational model training and large-scale concurrent GPU workloads. However, production inference—especially multi-modal workloads and agentic workflows requiring low-latency responses, multi-step reasoning, and real-time tool calls—demands a different approach. This session demonstrates how distributed inference architectures optimize GPU utilization for responsiveness to maximize ROI by keeping training centralized while deploying fine-tuning, post-training, and production inference on high-performance global networks.

speakers.name: Shawn Michels
speakers.bio: Shawn Michels, VP of Product Management at Akamai is responsible for driving the strategy and execution for the company’s cloud computing product portfolio. Shawn leads a global team charged with delivering a portfolio of computing solutions that enable developers to build next generation, distributed edge native applications. Shawn, a graduate of the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign holds a degree in Quantitative Economics, has been with Akamai for 11 years and has spent 21 years in the product management field.

sessions.title: How to Build Your Custom AI Advantage (Presented by Mistral AI)
sessions.abstract: Enterprises need AI that builds on their unique expertise and strengthens their competitive edge. In this session, Tim Lacroix (co-founder and CTO, Mistral AI), Kari Briski (vice president of Gen AI, NVIDIA), and Carlos Pereira (chief architect, Cisco Customer Experience) reveal how Mistral AI’s open-source leadership—paired with a full-stack AI approach—delivers unmatched customization, efficiency, and control. We’ll reveal new models, products and platforms from Mistral AI that help to unlock the ROI of AI in the enterprise.

speakers.name: Timothee Lacroix
speakers.bio:
As chief technical officer, Timothée Lacroix leads Mistral AI’s engineering teams and drives the development of the company’s cutting-edge AI solutions.

Timothée graduated from ENS rue d’Ulm in computer science in 2015 and holds a master's degree in mathematics vision learning from Paris Saclay. He began his career as an engineer at Facebook AI Research in 2015 in New York, where he completed his thesis between 2016 and 2019, in collaboration with École des Ponts, on Tensor factorization for recommender systems. He continued his career at Meta, working with Guillaume Lample until he co-founded Mistral AI.

speakers.name: Carlos Pereira
speakers.bio:
Carlos Pereira is a Fellow and the Chief Architect of Cisco’s Customer Experience (CX) organization.

Carlos’ main focus are industry transitions, technology innovations and cross-domain related architectures, development, integrations, strategy and alignment with customers' business initiatives, vectors of growth and value realization through technology adoption and consumption.

Carlos' expertise touches a wide range of technical and business fields, such as business applications, scalable platforms, infrastructure, observability, security, multi cloud, predictive analytics, AI as well as consumption models.

For over two decades Carlos has been helping and engaged with Cisco customers and partners on technology strategy, design, architectures, implementation and experience improvement.

speakers.name: Jonathan Cohen
speakers.bio: Jonathan Cohen is vice president of applied research at NVIDIA, where he leads development of platforms for large language models and speech AI. He also oversees development of genomics and drug discovery software. Previously, he spent three years at Apple as a director of engineering. Earlier in his career, Jonathan specialized in computer graphics as a software developer in the visual effects industry, winning a 2007 Scientific and Technical Academy Award.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Architect for AI Breakthroughs: Co-Design Memory + Storage for What’s Now and Next (Presented by Samsung Semiconductor)
sessions.abstract: As AI workloads evolve toward complex agentic reasoning and large-scale physical intelligence, the integration between compute and the memory-storage hierarchy has become vital. This session explores collaborative architectural approaches co-designed to support the transition to the NVIDIA Rubin platform powering AI factories, alongside platforms powering AI at the edge and physical AI. We will discuss the technical integration of HBM4, HBM4E, and SOCAMM2 within accelerated CPU-GPU clusters to optimize system-level throughput and thermal efficiency, while also examining the role of high-performance PCIe Gen6 storage in emerging inference context management frameworks. Join us to explore how these synchronized memory and storage layers provide the robust performance and reliability required for the current state and next frontier of AI.

speakers.name: Eyal Pnini
speakers.bio: Eyal Pnini is a seasoned business leader with over 20 years of experience in marketing, sales, and operations in the semiconductor industry. As senior director and head of system-on-chip and automotive marketing at Samsung Semiconductor Inc., Eyal drives product marketing and strategic initiatives for advanced memory and storage solutions. Previously, Eyal held leadership roles at Western Digital and SanDisk, managing multi-billion-dollar portfolios and driving revenue growth. With expertise in market strategy, business development, and customer engagement, he offers deep insights into the semiconductor industry.

speakers.name: Pankaj Kalra
speakers.bio: Pankaj Kalra leads the definition and delivery of a broad portfolio of NAND-based storage products at Samsung Semiconductor, Inc. These solutions are designed to accelerate enterprise digital transformation and power scalable AI infrastructure, addressing the growing demands of today’s data-intensive environments. In his role as Director of Product Planning, Pankaj leads Samsung’s industry ecosystem strategy in solid-state storage, shaping partnerships and long-term collaboration. He drives strategic initiatives and deep customer collaboration to ensure product roadmaps are closely aligned with evolving market and technology needs. Pankaj brings nearly 16 years of experience in the storage industry and serves on the Board of the SNIA SCSI Trade Association (STA) Community. He holds a B.Tech. from IIT-BHU, India, and a Ph.D. from the University of California, Berkeley.

sessions.title: Transform Your Organization With End-to-End AIOps
sessions.abstract: The difference between a stalled AI pilot and enterprise-wide AI agent adoption is often your AIOps platform. NVIDIA Enterprise AI Factory ecosystem partners will teach you how an end-to-end AIOps stack unifies observability, keeps efforts focused, and turns noisy logs into reliable performance. Learn best practices from NVIDIA's IT team and key partners on how to build and deploy your own AI factory.

speakers.name: Meghana Puvvadi
speakers.bio: Meghana is director of engineering to build AI products and platforms.

speakers.name: Jed Dougherty
speakers.bio: Jed Dougherty is SVP of AI & Platform at Dataiku, where he helps companies shape the future of their enterprise AI platforms. He leads the strategy for building and scaling end-to-end AI ecosystems across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud, and for deep integration with partners such as Snowflake, Databricks, and NVIDIA. With a background in data science and field engineering leadership, Jed works with executives and builders to turn AI ambition into secure, governed, production-grade impact. A frequent industry speaker and media contributor, he is known for pragmatic leadership on operationalizing responsible AI at enterprise scale.

speakers.name: Lukas Biewald
speakers.bio: Lukas Biewald is the CEO and co-founder of Weights & Biases, the AI developer platform acquired by CoreWeave. He also co-founded Figure Eight (formerly CrowdFlower), a pioneer in the ML data-labeling space. Figure Eight was acquired by Appen (APX) in 2019. Lukas has dedicated his career optimizing ML workflows, teaching ML practitioners, making machine learning more accessible to all, and occasionally tinkering with robots.

sessions.title: Performance Without Compromise: How Memory Innovation is Central to AI System Effectiveness (Presented by Micron)
sessions.abstract: Artificial intelligence is driving unprecedented computational demands that rely on massive data access at low latency. Learn how advanced semiconductor technologies—high-bandwidth memory, low-power DRAM, and next-generation storage—have become critical to meeting these computing challenges in AI systems. We will spotlight how Micron’s innovations reduce power consumption in large-scale AI training and inference systems while delivering the bandwidth to advance next-generation generative AI.

speakers.name: Girish Cherussery
speakers.bio: Girish is VP and general manager of AI solutions for Micron Cloud Memory, leading strategy for advanced memory technologies. With more than 20 years of experience at Micron, Girish has been instrumental in shaping the company's high-bandwidth memory roadmap. His leadership has positioned Micron as a market leader, as his technical depth and business insight have made him a recognized voice on balancing power and performance. Girish holds a certificate of business excellence from UC Berkeley Haas. He earned a bachelor's degree in electrical and electronics engineering from the University of Madras and a master’s in electrical and computer engineering from the University of Nevada, Las Vegas.

sessions.title: Accelerating Enterprise AI with NVIDIA: How SAP Builds, Scales, and Runs Agentic Workflows (Presented by SAP)
sessions.abstract: In this session, SAP shares how it partners with NVIDIA to accelerate enterprise AI from model development to production-grade agentic workflows. This session shows how SAP combines NVIDIA AI with deep business context from SAP applications to move AI beyond prototypes into real-world impact. Learn how developers build and adapt domain-specific models, operationalize them using SAP GenAI hub and NVIDIA NIM, and apply them through SAP Joule for Developers to tackle complex challenges like code explanation and cloud migration. The session closes with a forward-looking view on agentic and physical AI, where live business context connects digital intelligence to real-world execution.

speakers.name: Mayank Shrivastava
speakers.bio:
Mayank Shrivastava is Vice President of Foundation Models and Generative AI in SAP’s Office of the CEO, where he leads the Generative AI Foundations organization. He is responsible for strategic disruptive initiatives including the SAP RPT-1 foundation model for enterprise tabular data, the SAP-ABAP-1 LLM for developer acceleration, the SAP Business Knowledge Graph for semantic data access, and Joule-powered orchestration across SAP’s product suite. These initiatives are central to embedding generative AI into SAP’s core offerings and enabling customers to transform how they build, run, and extend enterprise applications. 

Prior to SAP, Mayank spent a decade at Microsoft, most recently as Principal Director of AI, where he led global teams driving the infusion of AI across Dynamics 365 and the Power Platform, including the development of Copilot for enterprise applications. 
 
Mayank received both Bachelor’s and Master’s degrees in Computer Science from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Kharagpur. He has built a career at the intersection of AI research and enterprise productization, managing large-scale research collaborations, shaping multi-million-euro investments, and driving measurable impact for customers. He is recognized for building high-performing cross-disciplinary teams, aligning executive stakeholders, and delivering innovations that shape the future of enterprise software.

speakers.name: Ted Way
speakers.bio: Ted Way, PhD, is a VP and Chief Product Officer of SAP Business AI Product Engineering, partnering with SAP lines-of-business product teams to ship AI that makes the world run better. Previously he was at Microsoft for 15 years as a principal product manager lead, shipping some of the world's first generative AI Copilot capabilities. He received BS degrees in electrical and computer engineering, MS degrees in electrical and biomedical engineering, and a PhD in biomedical engineering from the University of Michigan – Ann Arbor. His PhD dissertation was on "spell check for radiologists," a computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) system that uses AI to predict lung cancer malignancy on chest CT scans.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Solving the Trust Dilemma by Ensuring Model and Data Privacy On Premises with Confidential AI
sessions.abstract:
Regulated industries like healthcare and finance require running AI models locally for privacy and compliance, as moving up to 70% of sensitive enterprise data to the public cloud is costly and impractical. This necessitates bringing AI to the data.

This creates a Trust Dilemma: Enterprises need strong guarantees that sensitive data remains private and inaccessible to external parties, while model providers need assurance that their proprietary models are protected when deployed outside their direct control. This dilemma often restricts enterprises to open-source models, with advanced proprietary models only available as SaaS.

NVIDIA Confidential Computing resolves this by using trusted execution environments to protect both sensitive data and model intellectual property. We will showcase a reference architecture that securely deploys proprietary models into enterprise infrastructure without exposing models or data to operators or vendors. This approach allows AI providers to meet customers where their data resides, enabling enterprises to safely apply advanced AI to their most sensitive data.

speakers.name: Erik Bohnhorst
speakers.bio: Erik Bohnhorst is a Director of Product Management at NVIDIA, specialized in building infrastructure software that powers modern AI. Erik has been at NVIDIA since 2014, and currently focuses on making AI workloads run effortlessly and efficiently on Kubernetes and virtualized platforms.

speakers.name: Zvonko Kaiser
speakers.bio: Zvonko Kaiser is a Principal Systems Software Engineer at NVIDIA, specializing in cloud-native confidential computing and GPU security infrastructure. He serves on the Kata Containers Architecture Committee, the Confidential Containers Technical Steering Committee, and chairs the Kubernetes WG AI Integration. Zvonko has driven both Kata Containers and Confidential Containers at NVIDIA for several years, serving as the primary architect behind both projects. His work centers on hardening the cloud-native stack, from silicon to service, under his principle that in multi-tenant environments, "If it's shared, it's vulnerable".

speakers.name: Shaheen Lavie-Rouse
speakers.bio: Shaheen Lavie-Rouse is a Forward Deployed Engineer at ElevenLabs, where he helps teams take conversational AI agents from prototype to production. He works with customers ranging from early-stage AI startups to large media brands, with a focus on in-app voice assistants, customer support, and interviewing use cases. Previously, he worked across data-intensive systems at Lyft as both Senior Product Manager and Senior Data Scientist. Shaheen is also a cellist and founded Hire Notes, a live music booking platform and AI agent that has helped musicians earn over $8M.

sessions.title: Industrial intelligence at gigawatt scale (Presented by Schneider Electric)
sessions.abstract: The convergence of AI and advanced simulation technologies is transforming how industries design, build, and operate both products and facilities. This session explores how NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and OpenUSD-based digital twins are enabling Schneider Electric to drive a new era of AI-driven simulations and smart buildings across all sectors—from manufacturing and energy to data centers and AI factories. Learn how these technologies create interoperable, scalable, and virtual environments that accelerate innovation, optimize workflows, and unlock unprecedented collaboration. Discover how AI factories and intelligent infrastructure can leverage real-time simulation to reduce costs, improve efficiency, and drive continuous improvement. Join us to understand why open standards and immersive simulation ecosystems are the foundation for the future of AI.

speakers.name: Caspar Herzberg
speakers.bio: Caspar Herzberg has been chief executive officer of AVEVA since March 2023 and is a member of the Schneider Electric Executive Committee. Before becoming CEO, he served as AVEVA’s chief operating officer and chief revenue officer. He previously led Schneider Electric’s business in more than 80 countries as president, Middle East and Africa, and has spent more than 25 years in the technology industry, including senior leadership positions at Cisco and Accenture. He is also an experienced military officer, having achieved the rank of major (reserve) in the German Federal Armed Forces.

sessions.title: The 1 MW Rack and Going Beyond the Sidecar: Optimizing 800 VDC for Power Distribution (Presented by Schneider Electric)
sessions.abstract: As data centers and high performance computing environments scale to unprecedented levels, traditional power distribution architectures face critical limitations. This session explores the next frontier in energy delivery: the 1 megawatt (MW) rack and the evolution beyond sidecar designs through advanced low-voltage direct current (LVDC) strategies. We will examine how 800 VDC optimizes efficiency, reduces losses, and supports the growing demands of AI workloads and hyperscale infrastructure. Gain insights into design considerations, safety standards, and integration approaches that enable sustainable, high-density power distribution for future-ready facilities. Join us to understand why 800 VDC is not just an alternative—it’s the foundation for powering the next generation of compute.

speakers.name: Jim Simonelli
speakers.bio: Jim Simonelli, senior vice president and chief technology officer of the €6 billion Secure Power Division at Schneider Electric, leads R&D deployment, common platforms development, and forward-looking activities. With prior experience in leadership positions at Schneider Electric and a venture-backed startup, he brings extensive expertise in technology and innovation.

sessions.title: Improving AIDC Operational Efficiency with AI Hardware Diagnostic Innovations (Presented by Aivres)
sessions.abstract: This session will dive into the current difficulties in addressing common data center conundrums, with a specific focus on the intricate world of AI hardware interaction and failure analysis.  With a comprehensive overview of existing strategies for effective troubleshooting and a detailed case study of real-world data center diagnostic scenarios, we will analyze the limitations of current methodologies. Furthermore, this talk will explore forward-looking perspectives beyond present-day solutions and outline the desired features and functionalities that next-gen AI hardware could incorporate to transform and simplify the diagnostic landscape. Gain actionable insights into current best practices and contribute to the advancement of shaping the future design of resilient data center hardware.

speakers.name: James Zou
speakers.bio: James Zou is an R&D fellow at Aivres Systems Inc., specializing in data center server solutions. James’ work has a global reach, providing reliable, performance-optimized, and cost-effective solutions to major data centers worldwide. His research spans server architecture, testing, diagnostics, and manufacturing. With a Ph.D. from Auburn University, James previously led the company’s R&D department, overseeing the development of hundreds of server products and leading the expert team in resolving challenging issues across design, firmware, testing, and manufacturing.

sessions.title: Innovating Liquid Cooling Solutions for New-Generation AI Data Centers (Presented by Aivres)
sessions.abstract: With drastically increasing power and thermal density in new-generation AI chips and servers, AI data centers face enormous challenges in effectively and efficiently cooling their AI computing infrastructure at all levels—node, rack, pod, and data center. In this session, we will share Aivres energy-efficient liquid-cooled AI server products and solutions based on NVIDIA GPUs that fully meet the needs and technical challenges of new-generation AI data centers. Building on the primary liquid-cooling loop design, we extend the discussion to the secondary cooling loops, coolant distribution units, power supply, and liquid-cooled server rack designs. Finally, we will showcase our reference solutions and deployments for new-generation liquid-cooling AI data centers.

speakers.name: Jie Hu
speakers.bio: Jie Hu, a distinguished R&D principal engineer at Aivres Systems, specializes in server platform architecture and AI data center solutions. Jie’s academic journey includes a Ph.D. in computer science and Eengineering from Pennsylvania State University. Currently, Jie is leading Aivres' liquid-cooling server projects, demonstrating his expertise and leadership. Before his current role, Jie held key positions at Intel Corp., including server processor architect, data center server power management technologist, and server platform firmware architect.

sessions.title: Sovereign World Models: Scaling AI Memory, Data Gravity, and Governance (Presented by DDN)
sessions.abstract: World models demand long-horizon reasoning that rapidly outgrows GPU memory, while sovereign AI requires strict control over where data and models live. This session shows how a data intelligence architecture designed with native multi-tenancy, KV Cache, and hybrid cloud capabilities can extend the AI pipeline beyond the GPU to deliver ultra-low-latency, high-concurrency access patterns and policy-based governance to scale world models under sovereign constraints. You'll leave with practical design patterns for building NVIDIA-accelerated AI factories that maintain performance without sacrificing sovereignty or cost-per-token.

speakers.name: Victor Ghadban
speakers.bio: Victor Ghadban is an AI Field CTO at DDN, where he works with global enterprises to design and scale infrastructure for the next generation of AI factories. His work focuses on GPUs, high performance storage, and data architecture that power modern AI factories, large model training, and real time inference. Victor has spent his career helping organizations move advanced analytics and AI from research into production, with leadership roles across cloud, HPC, and machine learning platforms at AWS and HPE. He advises enterprises, research institutions, and AI native companies on how to design full AI workflows that fully utilize GPU infrastructure and support the growing demands of Enterprise AI.

speakers.name: Shilpa Kolhatkar
speakers.bio: Shilpa Kolhatkar is head of global business development for AI Nations at NVIDIA, where she drives the adoption and growth of NVIDIA’s AI platforms and solutions across the public sector. She cultivates partnerships and a rich ecosystem to take NVIDIA solutions to market. In her previous role, Shilpa drove business development for Cisco’s internet of things solutions. She holds an M.B.A. and a bachelor's degree in computer science from the University of California.

sessions.title: 800 VDC and Modular Data Center Solutions: Powering Next-Generation AI Infrastructure (Presented by Delta Electronics)
sessions.abstract: This session explores the convergence of 800 Volts Direct Current (VDC) power distribution and modular data center solutions as essential enablers for high-density AI and HPC workloads. Learn how 800 VDC architectures reduce power conversion losses, improve efficiency, and support scalable operations, while modular designs accelerate deployment and provide flexible power and cooling strategies.

speakers.name: Ralf Pieper
speakers.bio: Ralf Pieper is the R&D director for server, networking, and telecom applications. He's worked for Delta Energy Systems (Germany) since 1998, starting as an electrical design engineer for switched-mode power supplies until he took over R&D management in 2008. Ralf studied electrical engineering at the University of Paderborn, Germany, and after earning his master's degree he worked for two years at the university.

speakers.name: Jason Agee
speakers.bio: Jason leads enterprise-scale power and cooling strategies for next-generation artificial intelligence data center platforms. He specializes in grid-to-chip electrical architecture, 800 volt high-voltage direct current systems, liquid cooling integration, and modular artificial intelligence factory deployments across black, gray, and white space environments. He focuses on capital-aligned engineering roadmaps that reduce execution risk and enable scalable, multi-megawatt artificial intelligence infrastructure expansion.

sessions.title: Develop Physical AI Applications and Build Data Factories With the Robotics Ecosystem (Presented by Foxconn)
sessions.abstract: In this session, we will share how Foxconn leverages its own advantages in the era of Physical AI, together with Project Genesis, a group-level centralized AI platform initiative, to cooperate with global ecological partners such as NVIDIA, BCG, ABB, Intrinsic, Siemens, etc., to build “AI Factory” and “Data Factory,” and dive into the application of various automation and robots in production lines that integrate the concept of Physical AI technology to jointly promote the transformation of Foxconn manufacturing into “AI intensive.”

speakers.name: Dr. Shi Zhe
speakers.bio:
Zhe Shi is the chief digital officer of Foxconn Technology Group and head of the Smart Manufacturing Platform, as well as CEO of Maxnerva. He focuses on building the group's "Lighthouse Factory" clusters and deploying internet-of-things and digital supply chain technologies.

By driving breakthroughs in core technologies, Zhe Shi accelerated the group's transition from labor-intensive to technology-intensive operations. He's spearheaded the creation of eight World Economic Forum Lighthouse Factories and 92 corporate Lighthouse Factories, establishing a benchmark for intelligent manufacturing transformation. Zhe Shi holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from the University of Cincinnati.

sessions.title: Physical AI at Scale: Turn Real-World Intelligence Into Enterprise Advantage (Presented by Tata Consultancy Services)
sessions.abstract:
Physical AI moves enterprise intelligence into real-world systems that sense, reason, and act. Scaling from pilots to enterprise operations remains challenging. In this session, TCS and a global enterprise discuss how Physical AI is operationalized using NVIDIA accelerated computing to deliver measurable outcomes across physical operations.

The session walks through a representative use case integrating Physical AI assets—combining real-time sensing, simulation, and decisioning using NVIDIA Omniverse for digital twin simulation, Isaac Sim for robotics and physical system modeling, NVIDIA Metropolis VSS Blueprint for video analytics AI agents, and NVIDIA NIM for optimized inference. Speakers share production learnings on integrating physical data streams and sustaining performance and reliability at scale.

Attendees leave with a grounded view of how to scale Physical AI in production and turn real-world intelligence into enterprise advantage.

speakers.name: Naresh Mehta
speakers.bio: Naresh Mehta, CTO for manufacturing globally, defines the strategies for transformation; OM, AI, and cloud; design thinking and customer experience (CX); and digital solutions across the Gen AI, data and analytics, automation, internet of things, and emerging tech lines. With 20 years of experience, Naresh is a member of the Forbes Technology Council, CX Advisory Board of Seton Hall University, Stillman School of Business, and MIT Sloan-certified advanced design thinking expert.

speakers.name: Sreenivasa Chakravarti
speakers.bio: Sreenivasa Chakravarti is a Vice President at IOT & Digital Engineering Business at Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) responsible for Services, Sales and Operations. He has over 30 years of cross functional experience, cutting across P&L Management, Sales, Consulting & Advisory Services & Manufacturing Industry Functions. As a P&L Owner and Thought Leader, he is responsible to help enterprises realize their vision of the ‘AI-powered Connected Digital Enterprise’ and adopt the tenets of the 21st Century Value Chains.

sessions.title: Turn Your Data Estate Into AI Fuel (Presented by NetApp)
sessions.abstract:
Enterprises sit on massive data estates, often 100 exabytes or more of unstructured information. Yet 95% of AI pilots fail to reach production. The problem isn't a lack of data. It's that the data aren't AI-ready.

In this session, discover how NetApp and NVIDIA are transforming enterprise data infrastructure into an AI-ready data platform that fuels AI factories. Learn how NetApp AI Data Engine, designed on NVIDIA AI Data Platform, refines raw enterprise data into AI-ready intelligence. See how NetApp AFX and validated AIPod configurations deliver the performance foundation your AI factory demands. The result is agentic AI that can truly understand and unlock the value hidden across your business.

speakers.name: Tore Sundelin
speakers.bio:
Tore Sundelin is a seasoned product leader with over two decades of experience driving innovation at some of the world’s most influential technology companies, including Microsoft, Google, and now NetApp. With a proven track record in developing AI-powered solutions, Tore has been at the forefront of transformative product innovation. Among his notable achievements is the conceptualization, development, and successful launch of the first machine-learning stack and AI-driven, per-user personalized features in Microsoft Office—redefining productivity experiences for millions of users globally.
Tore’s expertise spans identifying strategic business opportunities, defining product roadmaps, designing user-centric solutions, building high-performing teams, and fostering key alliances to create robust product ecosystems. His leadership has consistently delivered impactful, scalable products that have shaped the tech landscape and reached hundreds of millions of users. At NetApp, Tore is leveraging his deep product expertise to shape the company’s AI vision, design forward thinking solutions, and drive the development of its AI product roadmap—empowering customers to unlock the full potential of their data in the AI era.

sessions.title: 基于多模态端到端世界模型的智驾体验
sessions.abstract: 卓驭作为国际一流的智能驾驶供应商及服务商，以“为世界提供安全、轻松的移动智能”为使命，提供行业领先的智能驾驶系统及自研核心零部件，为多种价位、多种动力类型的乘用车提供灵活多样的智能驾驶解决方案，推动智能驾驶功能全面普及，提升消费者的移动出行体验。演讲内容将为听众介绍卓驭多模态端到端世界模型的最新技术进展、相关量产成果以及未来规划。

speakers.name: Xiaozhi Chen
speakers.bio: AI Chief Technology Officer Chen Xiaozhi is responsible for the research and development of perception and AI technologies, as well as the product development of intelligent driving systems.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: Accelerate Open Science: Incorporating CUDA Into the SciPy Ecosystem
sessions.abstract:
As GPUs become central to scientific Python workloads, SciPy-ecosystem projects—including NumPy, SciPy, and scikit-learn—face the challenge of adopting CUDA without sacrificing usability, portability, or community values. This panel brings together open-source maintainers and ecosystem partners to discuss how tools such as CuPy, Numba, and CUDA Python are being used to accelerate core numerical routines while preserving Pythonic APIs.

Panelists will share lessons from real-world integrations, including managing build and packaging complexity, handling optional GPU dependencies, and supporting diverse hardware environments. The discussion highlights how collaboration across open-source communities, hardware vendors, and scientific users enables sustainable GPU acceleration aligned with the long-term goals of the SciPy ecosystem.

speakers.name: Leo Fang
speakers.bio: Leo Fang is a computational scientist and a theoretical physicist by training. He has extensive experience in computational projects involving Python, CUDA, and C/C++. His expertise encompasses code optimization, GPU programming, software engineering, Monte Carlo simulation, partial differential equation solving, numerical integration, and data analysis.

speakers.name: Travis Oliphant
speakers.bio: Travis Oliphant is a creator and core contributor to foundational Python projects including NumPy, SciPy, Numba, Conda, and Jupyter. Travis has founded and led multiple open-source–driven companies, including Anaconda, Quansight, OpenTeams, FairOSS, and OSS Risk Guard, and serves as a strategic advisor to organizations across the data science ecosystem, including Zyphra, bodo.ai, Saturn Cloud, and NumFOCUS.

speakers.name: Gaël Varoquaux
speakers.bio:
Gaël Varoquaux is a researcher, entrepreneur, and open-source leader, and a co-founder of scikit-learn and :probabl. A long-time contributor to the scientific Python ecosystem, he is known for his work on machine learning, reproducible science, and high-performance data analysis in Python.

Gaël combines academic research with open-source development, focusing on building robust, interpretable, and accessible machine-learning tools. Through scikit-learn and :probabl., he has helped shape how practitioners and researchers apply machine learning responsibly and at scale, while advancing sustainable models for open-source software.

speakers.name: Ianna Osborne
speakers.bio: Ianna Osborne is an open-science advocate and research software engineer specializing in particle physics and high-performance computing. She builds scalable, open-source tools for scientific discovery, maintains the Awkward Array project, and leads international efforts that foster collaboration and sustainable research software.

speakers.name: Katrina Riehl
speakers.bio: Katrina is an experienced data science and software development manager with a history of building data mining, machine learning, visualization, and artificial intelligence technologies. She's a strong engineering professional skilled in Python, NumPy, SciPy, Scikit-learn, R, C++, C, Java, Spark, and data science.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Build Trust at Scale: Governing AI Across Your Enterprise (Presented by ServiceNow)
sessions.abstract: Effective AI deployment requires more than powerful models—it demands end-to-end governance that spans your entire AI ecosystem. In this session, discover how ServiceNow and NVIDIA collaborate across the full AI stack, from model development and agent deployment to comprehensive governance frameworks. Learn how ServiceNow's AI governance capabilities provide the critical connective tissue connecting your AI investments, enabling you to leverage AI effectively, efficiently, and responsibly. We'll explore industry-specific implementations in financial services, healthcare, technology, and beyond, demonstrating how tailored governance approaches address unique regulatory requirements while accelerating innovation. Built on NVIDIA infrastructure and AI software, this solution ensures your AI initiatives deliver business value while maintaining trust, compliance, and control.

speakers.name: Dorit Zilbershot
speakers.bio: Dorit is a seasoned professional in the tech industry, known for her expertise in product development and management. With a strong background in computer science, she's been instrumental in driving forward technological solutions, with a special focus on AI. Throughout her career, Dorit has focused on innovating and improving product offerings, while also mentoring young professionals. Recently, Dorit played a strategic role in integrating Gen AI into ServiceNow's products, supporting ServiceNow's mission to put AI to work. Most recently, Dorit and her growing team have been driving ServiceNow's first AI agent offerings.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Optimizing $1 Trillion in Payments: From Contextual Bandits to Foundational Models
sessions.abstract: Making split-second decisions that balance fraud, cost, and conversion is central to global payments. This session explores how machine learning techniques—such as reinforcement learning, contextual bandits, and transformers—can be applied to optimize decision-making at scale. Gain insights into deploying low-latency models in production environments, as well as research approaches for moving from manual feature engineering to self-supervised representation learning.

speakers.name: Dhruv Ghulati
speakers.bio:
Dhruv is a founder turned AI product manager, building applied AI systems at global scale. He's currently on the Product team at Adyen.

Previously, at Uber AI, Dhruv launched Gen AI assistants on Uber Eats and worked on multi-modal AI, probabilistic forecasting systems, foundational models for document understanding and receipt transcription, Gen AI-based image enhancements, and more. These products touched millions of earners and users. He also led the AI/ML fraud squad at identity verification company Onfido, launching computer vision fraud models that cut false acceptance and rejection rates by over 90%.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Customer-Centric Innovation in Automotive
sessions.abstract: This session showcases how Porsche Engineering fosters innovation in the context of automotive engineering. Learn how NVIDIA technology helps push the frontiers of the automotive V-Model in terms of innovation depth and time-to-market. Learn about methods and components used throughout the vehicle development and validation process to ultimately enhance the customer experience in the car.

speakers.name: Benedict Kistner
speakers.bio: A technology and product leader with a background in aerospace and industrial engineering, Benedict currently works for Porsche Engineering in AI and future business fields. Previously, he worked as a product and engineering manager for battery analytics, internet of things, and data services, driving software, hardware, and analytics platforms in safety-critical environments.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Scaling Trusted AI: A Hybrid Playbook for Impact in FinServ (Presented by AHEAD)
sessions.abstract: Financial services leader Intercontinental Exchange and AHEAD share how the Aurora platform is scaling enterprise AI across cloud and on-premises environments. The session highlights how to apply a proven hybrid AI playbook combining NVIDIA accelerated platforms, AI software, standardized pipelines, proprietary data, and governance frameworks to move AI from experimentation to production. Learn how this approach is delivering measurable impact across Intercontinental Exchange while improving performance, cost control, operational efficiency, and trust.

speakers.name: Ryan New
speakers.bio: A technology executive and operational leader with 25 years of experience driving digital transformation, infrastructure modernization, and enterprise-wide performance optimization, Ryan is currently serving as chief information officer, leading AI strategy implementation, platform engineering initiatives, and strategic technology partnerships that deliver measurable business value.

speakers.name: Josh Perkins
speakers.bio: Josh Perkins is a passionate technologist with over 15 years of IT leadership experience. Josh has excelled in leadership roles across customer, technology, and consulting organizations. He serves as vice president for emerging technologies at AHEAD, leading the development of AI, edge computing, automation, and operational technology. Previously, Josh served as CTO at technology startup Link Solutions Group (acquired by AHEAD).

sessions.title: Authorship Reimagined: AI, Art, and the Future of Creative Expression
sessions.abstract: A new era of storytelling is unfolding, where artists and intelligent systems co-create, challenge tradition, and push the boundaries of expression. This panel brings together leaders at the intersection of art, education, and technology, including the creative visionaries shaping the next frontier of cinema, design, and digital experience.  From generative animation tools built for Hollywood to AI frameworks informing visual storytelling, these panelists are not asking whether AI belongs in the creative process but how to wield it with intention, imagination, and integrity. Together, they will explore what authorship means when machines become part of the medium, how education is evolving to prepare tomorrow's creators, and why protecting creative agency matters more than ever in an AI-powered world.  This is a conversation about design and disruption, culture and code, where the future of art is not automated but amplified.

speakers.name: Ia Robinson
speakers.bio:
Ia Robinson is a Gen AI producer and member of Black Women in AI. She’s now the course producer at Curious Refuge, which is the world’s largest AI film school, and volunteers with grassroots Gen AI community groups such as Machine Cinema and AI & The Culture. Previously, at BET Media Group, Ia developed new programming for linear and streaming platforms. At Food Network, she transformed original concepts into hit franchises like "Cutthroat Kitchen" and "Holiday Wars," and expanded the Chopped brand with
Chopped Junior and Chopped Sweets. Her career began in live production at both MTV and ABC's Good Morning America. Ia graduated from University of Pennsylvania and lives in New York City.

speakers.name: Louis Stewart
speakers.bio: Louis Stewart is currently the head of strategic initiatives. He's responsible for working with minority-serving institutions, government entities, industry partners, and a variety of affinity groups to build relationships that enable an increase in tech preparedness, grow the overall developer base, and foster a more inclusive AI community.

speakers.name: Jason Harvey
speakers.bio: Jason Harvey leads BET+, the premier streaming service for Black content, where he’s built a tech-forward foundation using AI and machine learning to enhance user experience and content discovery. A seasoned executive with global experience at Google, Microsoft, and Ford, Jason is also an author, mentor, and advocate for diversity in tech and media.

speakers.name: Paul Trillo
speakers.bio: Paul Trillo is an Emmy award winning director and video artist known for his innovative approach to blending conceptual storytelling with cutting-edge technology. His work spans a wide range of genres and formats, consistently pushing the limits of visual creativity. Whether he's crafting intricate in-camera illusions, building the world’s first mobile bullet-time rig with smartphones, choreographing drone-based aerial smoke shows, directing on the massive canvas of the Sphere, or pioneering generative AI visuals at the Louvre, Trillo’s work is marked by a relentless curiosity and technical inventiveness. This diverse body of work has garnered him 21 Vimeo Staff Picks, international film festival recognition, and features in a wide range of media outlets, including The New York Times, The New Yorker, Forbes, Rolling Stone, Vice, The Atlantic, The LA Times, GQ, and more. Paul remains committed to continuous innovation with his studio Asteria Films, a technology led production company ushering in an ethical approach AI to the film industry.

sessions.title: 工业级具身智能，正在从概念走向现实
sessions.abstract: 依托合成数据驱动的具身 VLA 大模型，Galbot G1 在通用性与泛化能力上实现关键突破，真正打通了通用机器人从感知、决策到执行的闭环，并在复杂、非结构化的真实世界环境中验证了其可靠能力。打造高质量仿真数据是我们的核心优势。通过与 NVIDIA Isaac Sim 的深度合作，我们构建了一套可规模化、具备确定性的训练与验证路径，使机器人能够系统性地掌握全身协同控制、灵巧操作等高阶技能。与此同时，随着 NVIDIA Jetson Thor 的性能释放， Galbot G1 Premium 的自主工作能力得到进一步提升，在严苛的工业环境中显著提升了实时规划能力与整体执行性能，推动具身智能迈向更高水平的工业应用。

speakers.name: 鹤 王
speakers.bio: 王鹤博士任北京大学助理教授，他创立了北大具身感知与交互实验室；他获评《麻省理工科技评论》“35 岁以下科技创新 35 人”(TR 35 中国区），2024 年北京大学-中国光谷科技成果转化奖，英特尔中国学术英才计划荣誉学者等称号。他的论文获得 ICCV2023 最佳论文候选，ICRA2023 最佳操纵论文候选，2022 年世界人工智能大会青年优秀论文 (WAICY-OP) 奖，Eurographics2019 最佳论文提名奖等，并担任 CVPR 和 ICCV 的领域主席。王鹤博士毕业于斯坦福大学，师从美国三院院士 Leonidas.J Guibas 教授。并获得清华大学学士学位。

sessions.title: Build, Optimize, Run: The Developer's Guide to Local Gen AI on NVIDIA RTX AI PCs
sessions.abstract:
The AI PC ecosystem is exploding. Developers are now running local, high-performance AI workloads. This technical session dives into how NVIDIA accelerates the top open-source software stack—from the different quantization techniques, training recipes, as well as inference frameworks and tools like Ollama, ComfyUI and custom pipelines. 

Crucially, we will move beyond simple inference to address the architecture of reliable, local agentic workflows. Join us to learn about the technical intricacies and considerations to develop robust, local AI workflows on NVIDIA RTX.

speakers.name: Parth Sareen
speakers.bio: Parth Sareen is a software engineer at Ollama. His work focuses on AI agents, model capabilities, tool calling, structured outputs, and systems engineering. Parth previously worked on distributed systems at Tesla and Autodesk, and built on-device ML pipelines in C++ for Siri at Apple.

speakers.name: Annamalai Chockalingam
speakers.bio: Annamalai Chockalingam is a product manager on the GeForce RTX AI PC team, focused on the developer segment.

sessions.title: Building the AI Stack of Tomorrow: IBM and NVIDIA’s GPU-Accelerated Full-Stack (Presented by IBM)
sessions.abstract:
Accelerating AI at enterprise scale demands a unified platform that seamlessly connects data, software, and infrastructure. This session examines how IBM and NVIDIA bring together GPU-proximate storage, open AI and data services, and accelerated computing to deliver production-ready AI at scale. Learn how IBM Fusion and watsonx integrate with NVIDIA’s platform to eliminate data bottlenecks, streamline pipelines, and enable secure, scalable deployments.
