We’ll also highlight how IBM Storage powered NVIDIA’s Velox project—advancing a new era of GPU-aware, data-intelligent infrastructure.

Also hear from Liqiang Wang, BioHPC Director at UT Southwestern, on how the medical center is deploying next-generation AI infrastructure with IBM Fusion and NVIDIA’s AI Data Platform to train larger models, process complex datasets, and power advanced research - from accelerated drug discovery to AI-driven clinical training and improved researcher productivity.

speakers.name: Ritika Gunnar
speakers.bio: Ritika Gunnar leads IBM’s Data and AI business, driving strategy and execution across the portfolio. She brings deep product and field experience, helping enterprises translate AI innovation into real-world outcomes.

speakers.name: Liqiang Wang
speakers.bio: Liqiang Wang is the BioHPC Director at UT Southwestern, where he designed and architected the BioHPC Cloud - the institution’s first cloud-based HPC resource built for long-term scalability of storage and compute. He oversees the daily operations and management of the HPC team and works closely with the BioHPC user advisory council and steering committee to ensure the platform continues to meet evolving research needs.

speakers.name: Sam Werner
speakers.bio: Sam Werner is General Manager of IBM Storage, with over two decades of leadership experience across product management, strategy, and development. He drives innovation in enterprise storage, hybrid cloud, and AI infrastructure, aligning technical advancements with evolving customer needs. An IBM Master Inventor with more than 30 patents, Sam is widely recognized for his expertise in resilient, high-performance storage systems.

sessions.title: Pioneering NVIDIA DGX GB300 Liquid-Cooled Deployment in EMEA (Presented by PNY)
sessions.abstract: Join us to explore how innovative collaboration drives real-world impact. We’ll dive deep into how our joint work with WWT, VERTIV, and NVIDIA is redefining performance, efficiency, and scalability through one of the first NVIDIA DGX GB300 liquid-cooled deployments in Europe, the Middle East, or Africa (EMEA). You’ll gain valuable insights into how this partnership turned complex challenges into breakthrough results, improving AI infrastructure and accelerating outcomes. Through a customer testimonial journey, we’ll highlight practical lessons, measurable benefits, and how you can apply these insights to your own AI and data center strategies.

speakers.name: Scott O'Mara
speakers.bio: Scott O’Mara is a senior vice president at World Wide Technology, where he leads international growth and strategic partnerships. He brings extensive experience in technology leadership, business development, and global client engagement, with a strong focus on delivering impactful, innovative solutions.

speakers.name: simon horton
speakers.bio: Simon is the DGX sales lead for Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, and an AI and data center sales specialist for Northern Europe (U.K., Ireland, and Nordics). He has been with PNY for 15 years and brings over 25 years of experience in the GPU and high performance computing industry. Known for his deep technical expertise, market insight, and trusted relationships, Simon consistently helps customers align advanced computing solutions with real business outcomes.

speakers.name: Anders Ynnerman
speakers.bio:
Anders Ynnerman is a recognised leader in scientific visualisation and high-performance computing, and a co-founder of Sferical. He is also Professor of Scientific Visualisation at Linköping University in Sweden, where he leads research focused on turning complex data into intuitive visual insight.

Over the past two decades, Anders has worked at the intersection of advanced graphics, large-scale data processing, and scientific discovery. His work has helped organisations across research, healthcare, industry and government better understand complex datasets through immersive and interactive visualisation techniques. He is widely known for pioneering approaches that combine GPU acceleration, high-performance computing and advanced rendering to make large and multidimensional data accessible to decision-makers.

Through Sferical, Anders is helping bring these capabilities to a broader range of organizations, enabling real-time exploration and analysis of complex scientific and industrial data using modern GPU-accelerated infrastructure.

speakers.name: Martin Olsen
speakers.bio: Martin Olsen serves as Global Vice President of Edge and Integrated Solutions at Vertiv, where he leads the company's efforts to deploy integrated physical infrastructure, deployment services, and remote management at the network edge. Originally from Denmark, he joined Vertiv in 2017 and has held several leadership positions in engineering, strategy, and commercial planning. With over 20 years of experience with global critical infrastructure providers in the data center industry.

sessions.title: 端到端模型部署：从 L2 到 L4 — Thor 如何加速 L4 技术栈与 Robotaxi 规模化落地
sessions.abstract: 本场分享将探讨文远知行（WeRide）如何结合 NVIDIA DRIVE Thor 与通用仿真平台 WeRide GENESIS，加速端到端（E2E）模型从 L2 到 L4 的部署与迭代，并推动城市 Robotaxi 的规模化落地。通过“数据中心训练 + Thor 车端推理”的云到车全栈架构，文远知行提升了 AI 模型的跨域泛化能力与实时推理效率；同时借助 GENESIS 的 Smart Agent、视频生成（Video Generation）、风格迁移（Style Transfer） 等能力，快速生成多视角、多世界变化的传感器数据与高价值长尾场景，用于训练与评测，使端到端闭环迭代显著提速，支撑 Robotaxi 的量产与稳定运营。

speakers.name: Hua Zhong
speakers.bio:
钟华是文远知行 WeRide 的资深副总裁。文远知行 WeRide 是全球领先的 L4 级自动驾驶科技公司。钟华负责带领文远知行中国的技术团队，进行无人驾驶全栈技术的研发和应用。
加入文远知行之前，钟华曾任职谷歌及西门子研究院，是微软亚洲研究院的最早期成员之一，负责计算机视觉、图形和机器学习算法的研发。
钟华是卡内基梅隆大学计算机系博士，毕业于由美国两院院士金出武雄教授建立的全球顶尖的机器人学院，共获得 9 项美国专利。

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Vision Language Models (VLMs)

sessions.title: The Blueprint for a Modern Resource-Responsible AI Factory
sessions.abstract:
Hudson River Trading (HRT) is at the forefront of automated trading, processing vast amounts of data at lightning speeds. But their most impressive metric might be their efficiency. In this session, HRT engineers reveal how they are leveraging NVIDIA infrastructure to build the ultimate "AI factory."

Discover how to break the trade-off between speed and sustainability. We will cover the end-to-end journey of deploying AI-driven trading models that are faster, smarter, and greener. Learn how innovative data center design, renewable power strategies, and NVIDIA-optimized workloads combine to create a trading infrastructure that is resilient, autonomous, and environmentally conscious.

speakers.name: Gerard Bernabeu Altayo
speakers.bio: Gerard Bernabeu Altayo is compute systems lead at Hudson River Trading (HRT), a quantitative trading firm at the forefront of technical innovation in global financial markets. Gerard leads HRT’s R&D Systems Team, which advances the firm’s high-performance computing, applied AI infrastructure, and systems automation to support quantitative research, development, and trading across HRT’s global platform.

sessions.title: Architecting Trust: Design Secure and Sovereign AI Systems (Presented by HPE)
sessions.abstract:
As AI adoption accelerates, organizations face a growing tension between data gravity, regulatory constraints, and the need to scale AI quickly. Data sovereignty requirements, air-gapped environments, and operational security expectations increasingly shape how—and where—AI systems can be built and deployed.

This session explores how to build secure, scalable AI platforms in highly regulated environments. Drawing on real-world implementations from regulated industries such as government and finance, we’ll examine how organizations are aligning AI performance with strict data residency and sovereignty requirements. You'll leave with a practical framework for designing AI environments that minimize unnecessary data movement, support rapid experimentation, and maintain strong control over sensitive data across private and hybrid infrastructures.

speakers.name: Thierry Pienaar
speakers.bio:
Thierry is the worldwide CTO for HPE’s HPC and AI sales division, responsible for strategic leadership across the segment's go-to-market approach, technology portfolio, and partner ecosystems, focused on delivering solutions to customers. Thierry is broadly recognized as an innovative thought leader, subject matter expert, and strategic partner to our customers. 

Prior to HPE, Thierry held executive positions at Nokia, Cable & Wireless, and Persetel-Q-Data, and served as an advisor to the George Washington University’s Digital Marketing Council. Thierry holds an M.B.A. from Henley Management College U.K. and a NHDP in securities practice, and key industry certifications.

speakers.name: Alan Ni
speakers.bio: Alan Ni is the Senior Director of Cross Portfolio solutions at Hewlett Packard Enterprise with over 20 years of technology and financial expertise with mobile computing, technology infrastructure and networking. He and his team are responsible for sovereign and AI solutions marketing across HPE. Alan joined HPE from Goldman Sachs where he was Vice President of Technology Infrastructure for the firm’s mobile app development initiatives and worldwide mobility infrastructure. He holds a bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering from Cornell University.

speakers.name: Kaushik Shirhatti
speakers.bio:
Kaushik Shirhatti serves as the Vice President of Global AI Networking at NVIDIA. where he leads strategic initiatives to evangelize, activate, and operationalize AI factories while managing the networking business at NVIDIA.

Kaushik champions the practical application of AI technologies to solve complex networking challenges, helping organizations build robust AI factories that deliver measurable business value.

speakers.name: Sadeepa Wijesekara
speakers.bio:
Sadeepa Wijesekara is Vice President of Engineering at Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), where he leads the AI software organization for the Private Cloud AI portfolio. He is responsible for building enterprise-grade AI platforms that enable secure, scalable deployment of generative and machine learning workloads in private cloud environments.

With more than 25 years of experience, Sadeepa has led global teams across engineering, product, data, security, and IT. Prior to HPE, he served as CTO of The Princeton Review, where he drove enterprise AI transformation and launched GenAI-powered learning applications. He has also held senior leadership roles at TaskRabbit, Intuit, Advantage Solutions, and JPMorgan Chase.

He holds an MS in Systems Engineering from the University of Pennsylvania and a BS in Computer Engineering from the University of Bridgeport.

sessions.title: The NVIDIA Solutions for Workstation/Data Center/Embedded and Edge Computing (Presented by PNY)
sessions.abstract:
NVIDIA has been at the forefront of innovation, consistently delivering top-notch solutions for various sectors. The NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Workstation, Data Center, Embedded, and Edge Computing solutions are no exception. These solutions leverage the power of NVIDIA GPUs to enhance performance, accelerate workflows, and provide scalable solutions that can adapt to a wide range of applications.

Whether you're working on complex simulations, AI-driven data analysis, or developing cutting-edge embedded systems, NVIDIA has a solution tailored to your needs. The NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Workstation solutions are perfect for those in the creative and design industries, offering unparalleled graphics performance and real-time ray tracing capabilities.

speakers.name: WILLY ORTIZ
speakers.bio: Willy Ortiz is an experienced field sales engineer manager with a history in the consumer electronics industry. Skilled in marketing management, sales, partner management, storage, and customer relations management, Willy's a strong operations professional specializing in NVIDIA GPU technology with 20-plus years of experience.

sessions.title: From Idle to Optimized: Managing GPUs, Kubernetes, and Automated Compute at Scale (Presented by HPE)
sessions.abstract:
As AI workloads grow in size and complexity, infrastructure teams are under pressure to deliver high GPU utilization, predictable performance, and strong operational controls across a mix of Kubernetes and bare-metal environments. Limited visibility into GPU behavior can quickly lead to inefficiency, cost overruns, and governance gaps.

This session examines practical approaches to observing, provisioning, and optimizing GPU-accelerated infrastructure for AI workloads. We’ll discuss strategies for end-to-end GPU monitoring, automation patterns for Kubernetes and bare-metal environments, and techniques for aligning performance optimization with policy and governance requirements. The focus is on repeatable, scalable practices that infrastructure and platform teams can apply in real-world AI environments.

speakers.name: Abhishek Agarwal
speakers.bio: Abhishek Agarwal is a Product Manager at HPE, where he leads the product strategy and development for HPE Private Cloud AI, an enterprise GenAI platform built on GPU-accelerated infrastructure, Kubernetes orchestration, and automated workflows that help customers move from pilots to production. Previously, he built AI platform and MLOps capabilities for Amazon SageMaker at AWS, partnering closely with engineering teams to standardize ML delivery at scale. He is the author of Product Mastery: A Masterclass in Product Management, an Amazon best-seller, and holds an MBA from Yale and a Bachelor's from IIT Kharagpur. In this session, he’ll share practical approaches to improving GPU utilization and operational efficiency for AI workloads.

sessions.title: Large-Scale JAX Training With Pipeline Parallelism
sessions.abstract: We'll give an overview of the first practical system for enabling pipeline parallelism (PP) for JAX on NVIDIA GPUs. JaxPP accepts simple annotations on the training step functions, and then will introduce Multiple Programs operating on Multiple Data (MPMD). This system is a compiler that works on top of JAX and its XLA compiler. We will give an introduction to pipeline parallelism and how it works on training. We'll also describe our experience of using JaxPP effectively on Cohere's training models.

speakers.name: Vinod Grover
speakers.bio: Vinod led the team that created and developed the CUDA C++ language and compiler, helping make GPU computing faster and easier across many fields. He is now applying  language and compiler ideas to accelerate deep-learning models, leading a small group focused on performance and developer productivity. He also continues to advance GPU architectures and the CUDA programming model. Previously, he held engineering, research, and management roles at Sun Microsystems and Microsoft. Vinod holds a bachelor’s degree in physics from IIT Delhi and a master’s in computer science from Syracuse University.

speakers.name: Nikolas Gritsch
speakers.bio: Nikolas is a Member of Technical Staff at Cohere, where he specializes in advancing large-scale machine learning systems. His work focuses on the architecture, stability, and efficiency of Mixture of Experts (MoE) models, with a particular emphasis on scalable training techniques. He contributes to Cohere's internal training framework through techniques like Jax-based pipeline parallelism.

speakers.name: Anxhelo Xhebraj
speakers.bio: Anxhelo Xhebraj is a Software Engineer at NVIDIA specializing in systems and DSLs for Machine Learning. His work focuses on designing expressive and composable primitives that simplify and accelerate parallel computations. He is a core developer of JaxPP, a library that enables safely running MPMD (Multiple Program, Multiple Data) computations within the JAX/XLA ecosystem

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Training AI Models

sessions.title: Build and Deploy AI Models and Agents for Media & Entertainment
sessions.abstract:
As artificial intelligence evolves from passive tools to active collaborators, a new paradigm is emerging: agentic AI. These autonomous, goal-driven systems—known as AI agents—are designed not just to answer questions, but to plan, act, and adapt in pursuit of creative objectives. In this session, we’ll demystify what AI agents are, how they work, and why they matter for artists, designers, storytellers, and technologists.

From intelligent production assistants that automate repetitive tasks to co-creative agents, this talk explores real-world use cases across film, animation, gaming, and immersive media. We’ll also look under the hood at the frameworks used to build these agents and discuss how to keep humans in the loop—preserving creative intent and artistic control.

speakers.name: Rick Champagne
speakers.bio: Rick Champagne leads global media & entertainment industry management for NVIDIA. He works closely with customers and industry partners in the film, TV, game development, advertising, and broadcast industries to promote the advancement and adoption of new technologies. With a long history of managing products such as the Autodesk 3ds Max and Maya Entertainment Creation Suites, Softimage, Mudbox, and Painter, Rick has been in the service of artists and technologists for over 20 years. He maintains close ties to industry as a member of the Television Academy, Visual Effects Society, and HPA, and serves as vice chair of the Advance Imaging Society. Rick holds an M.B.A.

speakers.name: Ashlee Martino-Tarr
speakers.bio: Ashlee is a 3D workflow specialist based in California. She has a passion for designing and creating creatures and specializes in 3D materials, texturing, and lighting to accomplish her designs. Recently, she became a main driver in NVIDIA's initiatives with generative AI, through third-party models or NVIDIA Picasso.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: Artist to Audience: The Developer Platform for Real-Time AI in Media
sessions.abstract: In this session, we’ll show how real-time AI is becoming the new fabric of modern media—connecting artist workflows to studio pipelines to distribution at global scale. You’ll learn the reference architecture behind an AI-native media stack: accelerated compute, low-latency networking, and developer-ready SDKs and APIs that let teams build, integrate, and ship AI into real production systems. We’ll walk through practical examples across creation, live production, enhancement, understanding, and delivery—focusing on what it takes to move from demos to deployed workflows, with measurable gains in speed, quality, and cost.

speakers.name: Richard Kerris
speakers.bio: Richard is the vice president and general manager of media & entertainment at NVIDIA, where he's responsible for industry strategy, business development, product management, developer relations, and marketing. At Alias/Wavefront, Apple, Lucasfilm, and other brands, Richard drove product development and marketing. At Alias/Wavefront, he was director of Maya technologies and led the teams that brought the groundbreaking 3D software to manufacturers and new platforms. At Apple, he was senior director of developer relations and managed the ProApps teams for Final Cut Pro, Logic, and Aperture. At Lucasfilm, he was CTO. He's a member of the Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: Simulating Reality Inside General World Models
sessions.abstract: This session explores how world models are emerging as a critical next step in advancing AI, enabling applications across physical AI, entertainment, and beyond. We'll go inside Runway's GWM-1: its architecture, capabilities, and why we believe video diffusion-based world models are the most direct path to general-purpose simulation. We'll also discuss the research and infrastructure challenges involved in deploying world models that run in real time.

speakers.name: Anastasis Germanidis
speakers.bio: Anastasis Germanidis is a Greek AI researcher, software engineer, and artist based in New York City. He's the chief technology officer and co-founder of Runway. Previously, he was a machine learning researcher at IBM and a distributed systems back-end engineer.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: Data Analytics and Streaming Media
sessions.abstract: In this "Lightning Talks" session, industry experts will explore the evolving world of data analytics and streaming, covering AI-driven innovations in sponsorship analytics, data processing needs of streaming services, and real-time audience insights. Speakers will discuss how advanced data tools are driving end-to-end content automation and improving attribution accuracy, helping businesses optimize strategies and increase engagement. Gain actionable insights on the role of AI in refining analytics and enhancing operational efficiency in the streaming industry.

speakers.name: Bianca Pryor
speakers.bio:
Bianca is the director of data science for Media & Entertainment (M&E), NVIDIA. Bianca’s team views themselves as a new startup within NVIDIA’s M&E vertical. Their mission is to partner with streaming platforms and help solve big data challenges using NVIDIA AI, machine learning, deep learning, and computer vision products and solutions.

Bianca spent the last five years at BET Media Group, leading insights and multi-platform analytics. Before that, she spent many years in the insights and analytics industry building client relationships, leading teams from the front, driving impact, and contributing thought leadership. She's also a member of the Television Academy and Black Women in AI.

speakers.name: Jason Harvey
speakers.bio: Jason Harvey leads BET+, the premier streaming service for Black content, where he’s built a tech-forward foundation using AI and machine learning to enhance user experience and content discovery. A seasoned executive with global experience at Google, Microsoft, and Ford, Jason is also an author, mentor, and advocate for diversity in tech and media.

speakers.name: Joao Sobreira
speakers.bio: João is the founder of Advolve and an experienced executive in the gaming, innovation, startups, and technology industries. Recently, Advolve was acquired by iFood/Prosus. João currently serves as general manager of Advolve and ads new business director at iFood, leading the integration and development of new advertising and growth initiatives. In the past, he was involved with brands and businesses such as Tipspace, Final Level Co., Rensga Esports, and Orbi Gaming, in addition to founding the largest gaming advertising agency in Latin America, which was acquired by Go4It. João has launched four companies since age 16, when he sold his first startup.

speakers.name: Raghav Gupta
speakers.bio:
Raghav Gupta is a senior engineering leader with deep expertise in building and scaling enterprise-grade AI, computer vision, and analytics platforms. As Vice President of Engineering at Relo Metrics, he leads the engineering organization and has been instrumental in establishing and growing core teams—including building the company’s Computer Vision team from the ground up—to deliver AI-powered products at global scale.
With over a decade of experience across software engineering, big data, and applied AI, Raghav has held engineering leadership roles at Relo Metrics and GumGum, where he drove the development of highly scalable, production-ready systems and fostered high-performing, collaborative teams. His career began in hands-on systems and software engineering, grounding his leadership in strong technical fluency and execution.
At Relo Metrics, Raghav focuses on applying computer vision, multimodal AI, and large-scale analytics to solve real-world problems in sports and media intelligence—transforming how brands, teams, and media companies measure sponsorship impact. He is passionate about mentoring engineers, advancing engineering best practices, and leveraging modern GPU-accelerated architectures to turn cutting-edge research into resilient, customer-facing platforms.
Raghav holds advanced degrees in computer science and consistently brings a mindset of innovation and scale to every initiative he leads.

sessions.title: Take AV2.0 to the Next Level With End-to-End Embodied AI
sessions.abstract: Discover how Wayve is evolving embodied AI foundation models from frontier research into production-ready driving intelligence that can scale across vehicle platforms, markets, and real-world conditions. Learn how AV2.0 techniques in end-to-end learning, fleet-scale training, and mapless operation enable safe, adaptable autonomy for automotive applications and robotaxi services.

speakers.name: Alex Kendall
speakers.bio: Alex Kendall founded Wayve in 2017 to reimagine autonomous mobility through embodied AI. Building on his award-winning research in computer vision, he pioneered a new end-to-end AI approach to self-driving. As CEO, he leads the company’s strategy, and under his leadership, Wayve has become one of the most exciting companies in the autonomous vehicle industry.

sessions.title: Deploy Lambda’s Bare Metal Instances with NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 & GB300 NVL72 (Presented by Lambda)
sessions.abstract: We explore how Lambda’s bare-metal cloud unlocks the full performance of NVIDIA rack-scale systems by removing overhead and delivering the hardware as designed. You’ll learn how direct access to GPUs, CPUs, NVIDIA NVLink, and high-bandwidth fabrics increases efficiency, reduces time-to-train, and enables scaling for frontier AI workloads.

speakers.name: Maxx Garrison
speakers.bio: Maxx Garrison leads product efforts for Lambda’s AI/ML cloud infrastructure. He has a background in electrical engineering, hardware development, and product management, including ASIC design, edge computing, and GPU infrastructure.

speakers.name: Doug Pan
speakers.bio: Doug is currently focused on accelerating the development of Lambda's AI Cloud. His background includes scaling AWS EC2 infrastructure and leading hardware engineering for AI-based self checkout at Mashgin.

sessions.title: Always-On Innovation: How Nasdaq Deploys AI in Mission-Critical Market
sessions.abstract: In this session, we'll explore how an exchange moving hundreds of billions of dollars in trades each day evaluates and governs AI under strict latency, reliability, and transparency requirements. We'll discuss where AI can augment the market stack, and where deterministic behavior must remain non-negotiable.

speakers.name: Ioana Boier
speakers.bio: Ioana Boier is the global head of capital markets strategy at NVIDIA, where she defines and leads the strategy to support institutional trading firms and ecosystem partners in building AI factories for scalable alpha generation. She holds a Ph.D. in computer science from Purdue University and has deep experience spanning quantitative research and AI systems, with senior roles at Alphadyne Asset Management, Citadel LLC, BNP Paribas, and IBM T.J. Watson Research.

speakers.name: Tal Cohen
speakers.bio:
Tal Cohen is president of Nasdaq, leading Nasdaq's Market Services and Financial Technology divisions. In this capacity, he is responsible for Nasdaq’s North American and European Markets, as well as the company’s portfolio of marketplace technology, surveillance, risk management, and regulatory reporting solutions.

Prior to joining Nasdaq in April 2016 as the senior vice president of North American equities, Tal was the chief executive officer of Chi‐X Global Holdings, LLC from 2010-16. From 2008 to 2010, he served as CEO of Chi‐X Americas.

sessions.title: Holistic Model Co-Design: Close the Loop With Software and Hardware for Inference Efficiency
sessions.abstract:
The economics of inference are changing faster than ever, with the cost of serving frontier models growing at an unprecedented rate. To continue advancing intelligence per unit cost, we must look beyond isolated optimizations and adopt a holistic, cross-layer design philosophy spanning silicon, software, and model architecture.
 
This session will explore the opportunities of hardware/software/algorithm co-design, highlighting how aligning model innovations with NVIDIA hardware features can unlock orders-of-magnitude efficiency gains. By educating the community on these co-design principles, we can accelerate the development of the next generation of frontier models and deepen our strategic advantage through tighter integration between hardware and intelligence.

speakers.name: Ritchie Zhao
speakers.bio: Ritchie is a Senior Deep Learning and AI Engineer at NVIDIA, driving algorithm/systems co-design to improve inference efficiency of generative AI models. He has worked on a range of topics including efficient attention, speculative decoding, and inference-time scaling. Prior to Nvidia, Ritchie worked at Microsoft and contributed the Microscaling (MX) datatype specification. Ritchie holds a PhD in electrical and computer engineering from Cornell University.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Advancing AI and HPC Competency in Higher Education Through Faculty Instructional Enablement
sessions.abstract: NVIDIA’s developer training programs support faculty teaching students and researchers through downloadable teaching kits, a cloud-based GPU-accelerated platform, and technical certification exams. Instructional content spans foundational topics—deep learning, accelerated computing, and data science—and advanced areas including agentic AI, natural language processing, and generative AI. Online courses and instructor-led workshops with project-based assessments help faculty align coursework with certifications and support students in earning recognized qualifications. We highlight ECPI integrating GPU-accelerated AI into student projects, and Lebanese American University using hands-on, faculty-led workshops to advance applied learning and prepare students for real-world AI applications and industry pathways.

speakers.name: Paul Nussbaum
speakers.bio: Paul Alton Nussbaum, Ph.D. is a senior IEEE member, a Kaggle notebook expert, and a patented inventor in the areas of pattern analysis and machine intelligence. As a product manager for several multinational companies, including Cisco and Ericsson, Paul learned to explain complex solutions in ways that non-engineers could understand and use.

speakers.name: Joe Bungo
speakers.bio: Joe Bungo is the Deep Learning Institute (DLI) program manager at NVIDIA, where he enables universities to use deep learning and GPU-accelerated computing technologies, including curriculum and teaching material development, DLI University Ambassador/Instructor certification, facilitation of academic ecosystems, and hands-on workshops. Previously, he managed university programs at Arm and worked as an applications engineer. Joe received his degree in computer science from the University of Texas at Austin.

sessions.title: High Performance AI-RAN Made Simple; Python to CUDA Signal Processing at the Speed of Light
sessions.abstract:
The Aerial Framework has been designed from the ground up to meet the needs of 3GPP radio access networks (RANs)—signal processing workloads with microsecond latency requirements. It is a platform that unites research, test beds, and production deployments to solve development challenges for real-time applications.  

The session will give a hands-on tutorial to design algorithms in JAX/Torch and instantly deploy to real-time GPU pipelines with inline networking.  

GitHub: https://github.com/NVIDIA/aerial-framework

sessions.title: 2025 Gordon Bell Winner: Forecasting Tsunamis in Real Time With Digital Twins
sessions.abstract: The Cascadia subduction zone is capable of unleashing a magnitude 9 earthquake with tsunamis as high as 30 meters, endangering millions in the Pacific Northwest. While it has been quiet since 1700, scientists place a 37% probability of an 8.2+ earthquake in the next 50 years. In response, the 2025 Gordon Bell Prize was awarded to UT-Austin, UC San Diego, and Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory for the world’s first digital twin for real-time, full-physics-based tsunami early warning. Leveraging exascale-class HPC systems, it delivers unprecedented accuracy and speed, achieving a ten-billion-fold performance improvement over existing state-of-the-art methods. This enables rapid, data-driven hazard assessment and actionable insights during critical windows. The work not only redefines the boundaries of real-time tsunami forecasting, but also sets a new standard for disaster resilience.

speakers.name: Omar Ghattas
speakers.bio: Omar Ghattas is a professor of mechanical engineering at The University of Texas at Austin who holds the Cockrell Chair in Engineering. He is also principal faculty and director of the OPTIMUS Center in the Oden Institute. His research focuses on theory and algorithms for large-scale Bayesian inversion and stochastic optimal control/design for complex engineered and natural systems. He is a three-time winner of the ACM Gordon Bell Prize, a recipient of the SIAM Geosciences Career Prize and the SIAM Babuska Prize, and a fellow of SIAM and USACM. He received a B.S. in engineering and M.S. and Ph.D. (computational mechanics) degrees from Duke University.

speakers.name: Stefan Henneking
speakers.bio:
In his work as a research associate at the Oden Institute for Computational Engineering and Sciences,
Stefan develops computational methods to tackle large-scale problems in science and engineering. His principal
interests lie in high performance computing (parallel algorithms and architecture) and the analysis and
implementation of numerical methods for solving linear and nonlinear systems of partial differential equations.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling

sessions.title: High-Performance Classical Control for Scaling Quantum Computers
sessions.abstract: Large-scale, useful quantum computers will require the full machinery of fault tolerance, bringing a formidable challenge for classical control electronics. In this talk, we'll illustrate the importance of hybrid quantum-classical systems by outlining PsiQuantum's single-photon architecture for quantum computing, how it implements quantum error correction, and initial work with NVIDIA to accelerate this with GPU supercomputing.

speakers.name: Terry Rudolph
speakers.bio: Terry Rudolph is a co-founder and the chief architect at PsiQuantum who invented the fusion-based quantum computing approach that PsiQuantum’s quantum computer is based on. Terry did his Ph.D. in theoretical quantum optics and held positions at the University of Toronto and Bell Labs before moving to Imperial College in 2003, where he became a professor of quantum physics in 2011. From 2004-15 he wrote four academic papers founding a theoretical approach to photonic quantum computing that PsiQuantum’s current architecture has further developed. He also founded, and then co-directed for seven years, the U.K.’s first Centre for Doctoral Training, which graduated over 130 Ph.D. students in the field of quantum information. His research receives more than 1,000 citations per year.

sessions.title: AI Literacy at Scale: K-to-Career Access That Delivers Real Student Outcomes
sessions.abstract:
AI literacy has moved from a "nice-to-have" to a readiness requirement. The open question is execution: how schools build access that scales, supports educators, and reaches students across different learning paths, geographies, and resources.

This panel brings together education leaders and practitioners shaping that execution from four angles: classroom and school implementation, learning science and workforce-relevant skill-building, platform-level delivery at scale, and institutional systems that connect education to long-term opportunity.

Together, the panel will share practical approaches to expand AI literacy and access without turning schools into pilot sites. You'll leave with a clear view of what to teach, how to equip educators, how to deploy tools responsibly, and how to build pathways that connect early literacy to real-world readiness over time.

speakers.name: Paul Turnbull
speakers.bio:
Paul transitioned from superintendent in the California public schools to become the ninth president of Mid-Pacific Institute in 2013. Mid-Pacific is a college-prep preschool-grade 12 school in Honolulu, Hawaii, and the only PS–12 school in the United States offering an immersive technology program that features robotics, game design, computer programming, and 3D laser technology and virtual reality for preserving historic treasures.

Paul is also the recipient of the CyArk Summit Award for Education, in recognition for his work in support of digitally preserving world heritage sites, and was twice recognized by EdTech Digest as a Top 100 EdTech Influencer in the United States.

speakers.name: Muffie Waterman
speakers.bio: Muffie Waterman leads NVIDIA’s global EBC demo experience, showcasing how technology can help customers and partners solve their toughest challenges. She joined NVIDIA from Cisco Networking Academy, a global skills training program reaching 4.5 million learners annually. Muffie holds a Ph.D. in learning sciences from Stanford University and has worked in R&D, nonprofits, startups, academia, and corporations. She is a former university professor and the author of "Wired to Listen: What Kids Learn from What We Say."

speakers.name: Ryan Trattner
speakers.bio: Ryan Trattner is a co-founder and CTO of StudyFetch, an AI learning platform that has grown to millions of users and earned billions of social media views since its launch in 2023. He works on engineering, product development, and enterprise partnerships, building tools that turn course materials into personalized study experiences. Ryan believes AI can fundamentally change how students learn, making quality education accessible to anyone, regardless of background or path. His focus is on bringing individualized learning to students, teachers, educational institutions, and corporate training at scale.

speakers.name: Anthony Holloman
speakers.bio: As the 11th commissioner of the SIAC, Anthony Holloman is a powerhouse of strategic leadership and institutional transformation. A visionary executive, he has spearheaded fundraising initiatives exceeding $150 million, driving critical advances in student scholarships and infrastructure including state-of-the-art stadium modernizations. With a career spanning leadership roles at institutions such as Fort Valley State, Jackson State, and Tuskegee, Anthony bridges athletic excellence and fiscal innovation. An All-American scholarship athlete and 2024 Hall of Fame inductee, he understands the grit required for high performance.

speakers.name: Kollin Napier
speakers.bio: Dr. Kollin Napier is the Director of the Mississippi Artificial Intelligence Network (MAIN), the nation’s first statewide artificial intelligence (AI) initiative, where he leads Mississippi’s coordinated strategy for AI literacy, workforce development, and applied AI adoption across higher education, K–12 education, state and local government, and business and industry. Based at Mississippi Gulf Coast Community College, he works closely with the Governor’s Office, state agencies, legislators, school districts, colleges and universities, and industry partners to position Mississippi for long-term economic competitiveness in an AI-driven economy.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Advancing to AI's Next Frontier: Insights From Jeff Dean and Bill Dally
sessions.abstract: In this 60-minute wide-ranging discussion, NVIDIA Chief Scientist and GPU architect Bill Dally engages in a focused dialogue with Google's Chief Scientist Jeff Dean, co-instigator of TPUs, overall Gemini co-tech lead, and pioneer in large-scale ML systems. The conversation explores the critical intersections of hardware innovation, systems scaling, and algorithmic advancement needed to propel AI into the 2026–2030 era of agentic systems, ultra-low-latency reasoning, and energy-efficient scaling.

speakers.name: Jeff Dean
speakers.bio:
Jeff Dean (ai.google/research/people/jeff) joined Google in 1999 and is currently Google's chief scientist, focusing on AI advances for Google DeepMind and Google Research. His areas of focus include machine learning and AI, and applications of AI to problems that help billions of people in societally beneficial ways. He has co-designed/implemented many generations of Google's crawling, indexing, and query serving systems, and co-designed/implemented major pieces of Google's initial advertising and AdSense for Content systems. He is also a co-designer and co-implementor of Google's distributed computing infrastructure, including the MapReduce, BigTable, and Spanner systems, protocol buffers, the open-source TensorFlow system for machine learning, and a variety of internal and external libraries and developer tools.

Jeff received a Ph.D. in computer science from the University of Washington in 1996, working with Craig Chambers on whole-program optimization techniques for object-oriented languages. He received a B.S. in computer science and economics from the University of Minnesota in 1990. He is a member of the National Academy of Engineering and of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences, a Fellow of the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM), a Fellow of the American Association for the Advancement of Sciences, and a winner of the 2012 ACM Prize in Computing.

speakers.name: Bill Dally
speakers.bio: Bill Dally joined NVIDIA in 2009 as chief scientist after spending 12 years at Stanford University, where he chaired the computer science department. Bill and his Stanford team developed the system architecture, network architecture, signaling, routing, and synchronization technology that's found in most large parallel computers today.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)

sessions.title: Insights From NVIDIA Research
sessions.abstract: The talk will give some highlights from NVIDIA Research for the past year. Detailed topics will be disclosed closer to the event.

speakers.name: Bill Dally
speakers.bio: Bill Dally joined NVIDIA in 2009 as chief scientist after spending 12 years at Stanford University, where he chaired the computer science department. Bill and his Stanford team developed the system architecture, network architecture, signaling, routing, and synchronization technology that's found in most large parallel computers today.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - 3D Model Generation

sessions.title: AI-Driven Digital Twins Optimize Facility Simulation and Autonomous Driving (Presented by Delta Electronics)
sessions.abstract: Delta has unlocked the potential of NVIDIA Omniverse with scenarios beyond data centers through building automation, autonomous driving, and advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS). Learn how you can maximize efficiency across all built environments.

speakers.name: Gary Harvey
speakers.bio: Gary Harvey is the director of business development and operations for Delta Electronics Americas, BSABD division. With over 15 years of experience in business development, operations, and a strong background in lighting and smart building technologies, Gary is a key driver of growth and innovation in the industry. He plays a pivotal role in advancing Delta's newly launched Delta Intelligent Building Technologies, focusing on the integration of cutting-edge smart building solutions to enhance efficiency, sustainability, and user experience.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Generative AI in Media & Entertainment
sessions.abstract: This fast-paced lightning session features leading voices in generative AI and autonomous agents, exploring breakthroughs in creative generation, tool-using systems, and practical, real-world deployments. Each speaker will offer a distinct perspective on how these technologies are redefining creativity, productivity, and the future of intelligent software. Expect rapid insights, vivid demonstrations, and forward-looking ideas that reveal where generative and agentic AI are heading—and what they make possible today.

speakers.name: Rick Champagne
speakers.bio: Rick Champagne leads Global Media & Entertainment Industry Management for NVIDIA. In this role, Rick works closely with customers and industry partners in the film, TV, game development, advertising, and broadcast industries to promote the advancement and adoption of new technologies. With a long history of managing products such as the Autodesk 3ds Max and Maya Entertainment Creation Suites, Softimage, Mudbox, and Painter, Rick has been in the service of artists and technologists for over two decades. Rick maintains close ties to industry as a member of the Television Academy, Visual Effects Society, and Hollywood Professional Association, and serves as vice chair of the Advance Imaging Society. Rick holds an M.B.A.

speakers.name: Kanami Oyama
speakers.bio: Kanami Oyama is an AI engineer at Hakuhodo Technologies, an AdTech company based in Tokyo. Since joining in 2024, Kanami has contributed to the development of deep learning models for large-scale evaluation of advertisement visuals, supporting data-driven creative production. Her work includes building end-to-end video analysis pipelines that integrate visual, temporal, and textual features, as well as conducting large-scale experiments to improve model accuracy, interpretability, and robustness. More recently, she has been working on agentic AI systems for automating and enhancing the creation of advertising creatives. She holds a B.A. in physics from the University of Cambridge.

speakers.name: Naama Manova-Twito
speakers.bio: Naama Manova-Twito is a co-founder and CEO of MarkeTeam.ai, building the first AI-native integrated marketing environment, where high-autonomy agents run end-to-end marketing as a fully functional AI marketing team. She brings 25-plus years of executive leadership across startups, small- and medium-sized businesses, public companies, and enterprise organizations—scaling global brands, leading strategic growth, and driving capital raises, IPOs, and mergers and acquisitions.

speakers.name: Alex Mashrabov
speakers.bio: Alex Mashrabov is the CEO and co-founder of Higgsfield AI, a fast-scaling generative video startup going viral right now. With over a decade of experience in video AI, he recently raised $130 million at a $1.3 billion valuation.

speakers.name: Tian Pei
speakers.bio:
Tian is the Director of Strategic Partnerships for LTX, the creative tech company behind the leading open-source generative AI model LTX and LTX Studio. With a career spanning partnerships, finance, and P&L ownership across tech, gaming, and media & entertainment, Tian has helped scale organizations at companies like Roblox, Unity, and Adobe.

Tian brings a builder’s mindset and a passion for empowering creatives. She thrives at the intersection of creativity, technology, and human connection, and loves partnering with like-minded people to bring ambitious ideas to life.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: Turning Telecom Networks Into Sovereign AI-Ready Platforms (Presented by Nscale)
sessions.abstract: As AI and data center infrastructure become powerful engines of economic growth, the next competitive edge is for telecom operators to plug sovereign AI directly into their national networks. In this session, you’ll get an inside look at how Nscale is partnering with telecom service providers in the U.K. to deploy full-stack NVIDIA-powered AI Infrastructure on existing telecom sites across the country, bringing AI closer to users.

speakers.name: Arno van Huyssteen
speakers.bio: Arno Van Huyssteen is a senior technology and business leader focused on the convergence of telecommunications and artificial intelligence. He leads Telco and AI Service Provider strategy at Nscale, working with operators and partners to extend the value of existing assets—power, data centres, network infrastructure and operational trust—into scalable AI platforms and services. Arno brings deep experience across AI infrastructure, cloud platforms and go-to-market execution, with a strong emphasis on practical deployment, commercial sustainability and long-term industry transformation.

sessions.title: Building Local, Sovereign AI in Practice (Presented by Nscale)
sessions.abstract: Europe has built the foundation for having domestic AI compute positioned within the region, with projects across the U.K., Norway, Portugal, Iceland, and beyond, all using NVIDIA-accelerated infrastructure to keep compute, data, and value on European terms. In this 40-minute session, you’ll get a fast, practical view of how public–private partnerships are structuring these deployments—what’s working, what’s hard, and how to apply the same playbook in your own country or organization.

speakers.name: Philippe Sachs
speakers.bio: Philippe's 25 years of experience span banking where he focused on sovereigns, most recently setting up Standard Chartered Bank's Global Public Sector Business, as CEO of an educational company with over 500 employees in the U.K., and a blockchain and deep tech venture capitalist, where he led the early investment rounds in Nscale.

sessions.topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI

sessions.title: Beyond the Black Box: Interpretability of LLMs in Finance
sessions.abstract: Financial institutions are deploying LLMs for several use cases including trading, advisory, and consumer-facing applications—but opaque model behavior limits trust, especially in high-stakes situations. This session shows how mechanistic interpretability can reveal internal circuits and activations that drive predictions, making LLMs auditable and controllable. We’ll cover theory and practical applications across trading strategies, sentiment analysis, and hallucination reduction, linking interpretability not only to compliance and risk governance, but also to actionable trading signals.

speakers.name: ARIYE SHATER
speakers.bio:
Ariye is an established senior leader in quantitative analytics and model development. He's very hands-on in adopting new technologies: AI/ML, cloud, big data, and distributed compute graphs. Ariye is managing a global team of full-stack quants, supporting all aspects of risk, AI, and treasury modeling. 

Previously, Ariye was a credit derivatives quant, with 10 years of experience supporting flow and structured credit businesses. Ariye started his career at Lehman Brothers as a credit quant analyst, focusing on initial rollout of early CDX indices, developing core derivative pricing and risk management systems, and eventually supporting structured credit trading as a desk quant strategist.

speakers.name: Hariom Tatsat
speakers.bio: Hariom Tatsat currently works as a vice president in the Quantitative AI team at Barclays in New York. Hariom has extensive experience as a quant in the areas of financial instrument pricing and algorithmic trading in several global investment banks and financial organizations. He completed his M.S. at UC Berkeley and his B.E. at IIT (India). He has co-authored a book called "Machine Learning and Data Science Blueprints for Finance." He is a frequent speaker at global conferences on the topics related to the latest trends in AI-driven financial models and is a recognized thought leader in the community.

sessions.topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI

sessions.title: How AI Factories Can Shift-left to Accelerate Infrastructure and Ecosystems Readiness
sessions.abstract: As AI infrastructure scales, the traditional “build-then-test” model has become a critical bottleneck. Join NVIDIA and CoreWeave to explore how a shift-left methodology, powered by digital twins, is transforming the AI factory lifecycle. CoreWeave will share how NVIDIA Air decouples software automation from hardware lead times, enabling validation of complex systems well before physical infrastructure arrives. In parallel, NVIDIA uses these digital twin environments to empower a global ecosystem of software partners to develop and optimize next-generation platforms without requiring access to physical hardware. This session demonstrates how a simulation-first approach ensures the entire stack, from cloud automation to ISV solutions, is production-ready on day one, dramatically reducing time-to-market and capital risk.

speakers.name: Amit Katz
speakers.bio: Amit is vice president for Ethernet switch at NVIDIA. He's served as senior director of worldwide Ethernet switch sales since 2014, and previously worked in various product management roles at Mellanox, beginning in 2011. Prior to that, Amit held product management positions at Voltaire and at RAD Data Communications. He graduated from the Academic College of Tel Aviv-Yaffo with a B.A. in computer science and an M.B.A. from Bar Ilan University.

speakers.name: Harshdeep Banwait
speakers.bio: Harshdeep Banwait is the Director of Product at CoreWeave, where he leads the strategy for next-generation AI infrastructure. He focuses on the intersection of distributed systems and cloud product strategy, ensuring that CoreWeave’s high-performance platforms stay ahead of the rapidly evolving needs of the AI ecosystem. Harshdeep works at the front lines of the industry, partnering with top-tier AI labs to scale the infrastructure that makes the next generation of Foundation Models possible.

sessions.title: Secure Multi-Tenant AI: Sovereign Neo-Cloud Infrastructure With Fractional GPU Sharing
sessions.abstract: This session examines how DCAI, a sovereign neo-cloud operator, supports secure multi-tenant access to high-performance AI compute on the Gefion supercomputer. We will show how organizations can share infrastructure while maintaining isolated environments for data, workloads, and networks. The talk covers fractional GPU allocation for efficient resource utilization, pre-configured AI workspaces, and new automation developed with NVIDIA Run:ai that speeds deployment of secure environments. Learn practical approaches for enabling multiple teams to develop and scale AI workloads on shared infrastructure without compromising security or sovereignty.

speakers.name: Ali Syed
speakers.bio: Ali Syed is senior vice president of infrastructure at the Danish Centre for AI Innovation (DCAI), where he leads the design and operation of sovereign, secure AI and HPC platforms, including the Gefion supercomputer. He has more than 20 years of experience building large-scale cloud and high performance computing infrastructures, with deep expertise in secure multi-tenant environments, sensitive data platforms, and AI-ready infrastructure. Prior to DCAI, Ali held senior roles at the Danish National Genome Center, Nordic e-Infrastructure Collaboration, and Computerome, where he helped shape national and cross-border platforms for genomics, life sciences, and AI at scale.

speakers.name: Sam Heywood
speakers.bio: Sam Heywood is a global product marketing leader with deep expertise in AI infrastructure, cybersecurity, and data platforms. Currently Director of Product Marketing at NVIDIA, he previously led marketing teams at Run:ai, Venafi, and Cloudera, driving product growth and industry partnerships. He works closely with engineering, product, and enterprise leaders to bring complex infrastructure technologies to market and drive their adoption in production environments.

sessions.title: Reinforcement Learning at Scale: Engineering the Next Generation of Intelligence
sessions.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) is entering a new phase—one defined as much by systems engineering as by algorithmic innovation. In this panel, we convene industry leading experts to discuss current challenges in scaling RL along with emerging RL paradigms, which together can unlock scientific discovery, multi-modal reasoning, collaborative agents, and continual learning—the vanguard of more adaptive and intelligent systems.

speakers.name: Linden Li
speakers.bio: Linden Li is a co-founder and chief architect at Applied Compute, where his work combines scalable AI systems engineering with practical reinforcement learning deployment for real-world applications an AI. He has B.S. and M.S. degrees in computer science from Stanford University, and has previously worked as a researcher and engineer at OpenAI, where he contributed to machine learning systems and infrastructure for reinforcement learning and high-compute training workflows.

speakers.name: Yuchen He
speakers.bio: Yuchen He is a highly experienced AI researcher and engineer focused on reasoning, agent reinforcement learning, and post-training of large language models. She has degrees from UT Austin and a Ph.D. from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign. She previously worked at Meta, OpenAI, and xAI.

speakers.name: William Fedus
speakers.bio: William (Liam) Fedus is the co-founder of Periodic Labs, a San Francisco Bay Area startup building a scientific superintelligence to accelerate discovery across the sciences. Before launching Periodic Labs, Liam served as vice president of post-training research at OpenAI, where he led the group responsible for refining large language models into safe, aligned, and globally deployed products and was a co-creator of ChatGPT. Earlier, Liam was a research scientist at Google Brain, where he co-authored the influential Switch Transformer paper.

speakers.name: Vartika Singh
speakers.bio: Vartika leads the strategic technical partner engagements at NVIDIA.

speakers.name: Jerry Tworek
speakers.bio: Jerry Tworek is an AI research leader known for work in deep reinforcement learning, robotics, and language-model reasoning. As a research lead and later VP of Research at OpenAI, he led work scaling reinforcement learning on LLMs, where capability continues to improve with additional RL compute - an approach that underpins the o-series and reasoning models in the GPT-5 family. Over the years, Jerry has driven a lot of progress in LLM-assisted coding, from models behind Github Copilot, to recent codex coding agents, as well as applying reinforcement learning to robotics. Recently, Jerry has left OpenAI to start a new kind of company focused on fundamental ML research and work automation.

sessions.title: The Earth System at 1 km Resolution: Breaking Frontiers in Climate Science
sessions.abstract: We present the first-ever global simulation of the full Earth system at an unprecedented 1 km resolution—a milestone recognized with the 2025 Gordon Bell Prize in Climate Modeling. Led by researchers from the Max Planck Institute for Meteorology and the German Climate Computing Center, this simulation captures the dynamic flow of energy, water, and carbon across the atmosphere, ocean, and land, unlocking new frontiers in climate science. The team harnessed the full computational power of JUPITER (the world’s largest GH200 Superchip installation) and ALPS systems, building on a long-standing collaboration with NVIDIA to advance the ICON codebase. The heterogeneous architecture, balancing Earth system components on both Grace CPUs and Hopper GPUs, enabled innovative optimization and acceleration techniques, reducing the code complexity by half while increasing performance and portability.

speakers.name: Daniel Klocke
speakers.bio: Daniel Klocke holds a Ph.D. in meteorology with extensive experience with numerical models for weather forecasting and climate research. His work focuses on high-resolution simulations and is pioneering global coupled storm and ocean-eddy resolving simulations. At the Max Planck Institute for Meteorology, he leads the Computational Infrastructure and Model Development team, and is responsible for the coordination of the ICON developments at MPI-M.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling

sessions.title: From Complexity to Confidence: Core-to-Edge Secure AI Factories with Consistent Operations (Presented by Cisco Systems)
sessions.abstract: While AI practitioners push rapid innovation, IT teams struggle with fragmented guidance and operational overhead when deploying secure, high-performance AI infrastructure across data center and edge to meet training and inference needs. Discover how prescriptive, validated reference designs such as Cisco Secure AI Factory with NVIDIA simplifies deployment and operations of trusted AI infrastructure with the same capabilities and operating model across data center and edge. Get actionable patterns to accelerate deployment and focus on innovation.

speakers.name: Kevin Wollenweber
speakers.bio:
Kevin is senior vice president and general manager of the Cisco Networking Data Center and Internet Infrastructure organization, where his portfolio transforms how customers build and manage their network architectures. He has worked with the world's largest service provider, enterprise, and web companies for the last three decades and has been with Cisco since 1997. He leads Cisco's Data Center and Provider Connectivity portfolio, focusing on how the industry transforms and evolves Cisco's products for the world's largest data centers, provider infrastructure, and cloud. 

Kevin has a B.S. and M.S. in computer engineering from Santa Clara University and a routing and switching CCIE.

sessions.title: Transforming Bottlenecks into Breakthroughs: Scaling Secure AI Networks for AI Factories (Presented by Cisco Systems)
sessions.abstract: AI networking is at a turning point as performance and security demands surpass traditional infrastructure limits. Cisco and NVIDIA tackle bandwidth, latency, and security challenges head-on with innovations in silicon, systems, software, and optics. Scale fabric architectures to maximize AI workloads, ensuring efficient expansion and secure data in motion. Explore validated architectures that simplify deployment, optimize GPU use, and future-proof AI infrastructure. Chart your path to breakthroughs.

speakers.name: Will Eatherton
speakers.bio: Will leads the Cisco team responsible for data center, web, and service provider solutions. He joined in 2000 and grew his expertise in routing systems architecture. In 2013 Will co-founded Skyport Systems, a cloud tech pioneer focused on cloud-managed, secure virtualized systems. With the Skyport acquisition in 2018, Will returned to lead the Cisco Engineering team. He's an active researcher and co-author of many packet processing patents.

sessions.title: 从物理近似到物理真实：基于真实物理参数的 Physical AI 合成数据基础设施
sessions.abstract: 随着 Physical AI（包括具身智能与机器人）的快速发展，行业对高质量合成数据的需求正从“视觉真实”转向“物理真实”。然而，传统仿真流程往往依赖经验参数、人工调试与近似建模，导致仿真与真实世界之间存在难以量化的物理差距，限制了物理 AI 的规模化训练与可靠部署。本次分享中，Lightwheel 将介绍其提出的物理真实仿真的方法论与工程体系：通过完全自动化的 Real-to-Sim 物理工厂，将真实物体的接触、摩擦、柔顺与结构特性进行物理测量，并直接转化为可用于仿真的 SimReady 资产；同时结合自研物理求解器，基于 NVIDIA Isaac Lab 构建物理仿真框架，确保仿真器在数值层面能够稳定、准确地复现真实物理属性。基于这一基础设施，Lightwheel 实现了面向复杂操作任务与工业场景的自动化合成数据生成流程，显著提升物理真实的高质量合成数据的生产效率和规模。本次演讲将从基础设施角度，探讨如何通过“物理真实”的仿真体系，支撑下一代 Physical AI 的规模化数据生产。

speakers.name: Jianwei Zhang
speakers.bio: Jianwei is the vice president of solutions at Lightwheel, with many years of experience in simulation and engineering across autonomous driving and embodied AI.

sessions.title: Benchmarking the Path to Useful Quantum Computers
sessions.abstract: Learn where quantum computing technologies are today, where they’re going, and how to track progress toward the goal of (someday) outperforming any supercomputer at solving useful problems. We’ll discuss how we assess and improve the performance of quantum computing hardware, the expanding role for HPC/GPUs/AI in this quest, and when it will (and won’t!) be useful to integrate HPC and GPUs with fault-tolerant quantum computers.

speakers.name: Robin Blume-Kohout
speakers.bio: Robin Blume-Kohout is the founder and codirector of Sandia's Quantum Performance Lab. A world-recognized expert in quantum computer benchmarking and characterization, he also serves on the editorial review board for the journal PRX Quantum.

sessions.title: Build Custom Large-Scale Generative AI Models
sessions.abstract: Learn how to design, train, and deploy large-scale custom generative AI models. This session will step you through data preparation, fine-tuning, and infrastructure management, and will highlight best practices for scaling large models efficiently. You’ll also see real-world examples with Adobe Firefly Foundry and NVIDIA NeMo integration that demonstrate how teams are applying these techniques to production-grade AI systems.

speakers.name: Ely Greenfield
speakers.bio: Ely Greenfield is chief technology officer and senior vice president of Adobe’s Creative Products Group, Digital Media at Adobe. He oversees Creative Cloud—including flagship apps such as Photoshop, Premiere Pro, and Illustrator—as well as Adobe’s generative AI initiatives, including Adobe Firefly.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: Real Cloud Infrastructure for Real AI Workloads: Training and Inference at Production Scale (Presented by CoreWeave)
sessions.abstract: As dense frontier and mixture-of-experts models move toward multi-trillion parameters, AI pioneers need real cloud infrastructure ready for real-world AI workloads spanning from training trillion-parameter models to long-context inference and reinforcement learning. In this session, we will provide a deep dive on how we optimized every layer of our full stack architecture from infrastructure and orchestration to observability to run large-scale training and inference workloads efficiently to power all AI workloads, especially agentic AI. We will examine the architectural breakthroughs, and new innovations that enable AI pioneers to engineer rack-scale systems with ultra-low latency and reliability for their training and inference workloads.

speakers.name: Chen Goldberg
speakers.bio: Chen Goldberg has more than 25 years of expertise leading global engineering teams, product R&D initiatives, and high-profile customer engagements with Fortune 500 enterprises. She is senior vice president of engineering at CoreWeave, joining the executive team to lead and grow engineering functions, as well as accelerate CoreWeave’s world-class product development and offerings.

speakers.name: Corey Sanders
speakers.bio: Corey is senior vice president at CoreWeave, where he leads strategic initiatives focused on scaling innovation, advancing market leadership, and partnering with customers to deliver purpose-built AI infrastructure. He plays a key role in driving sustainable growth across the rapidly evolving AI ecosystem, including public-private partnerships supporting AI innovation in New Jersey. Previously, Corey held senior leadership roles at Microsoft, including corporate VP for Microsoft Cloud for Industry and leader of Commercial Solution Areas, spanning engineering, product, and go-to-market strategy. He began his career as a software developer on Windows after graduating from Princeton University.

speakers.name: Sebastian Yunge
speakers.bio: Sebastian Felipe Yunge Del Pozo is a Senior Staff Software Engineer at Mercado Libre, where he designs the distributed systems and search infrastructure supporting the company’s e-commerce and fintech operations. With over a decade of experience in systems architecture, he specializes in solving complex data retrieval and backend challenges at scale. Currently, Sebastian focuses on operationalizing high-performance distributed workloads, including the specialized infrastructure required to run production-scale AI. His work ensures that search and AI-driven features remain efficient and reliable across diverse applications. Throughout his career, he has remained committed to building the resilient, scalable systems necessary to handle high-volume data and drive practical technological innovation.

sessions.title: Deploy Reliable Agents at Scale With Reinforcement Learning and High-Performance Inference (Presented by CoreWeave)
sessions.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) is essential for developing reliable and autonomous agents that learn from experience, delivering enhanced accuracy and cost efficiency. In this deep dive session, learn how to use the technical architecture of CoreWeave’s RL infrastructure and inference solutions, highlighting how to define model weights, use Weights & Biases with serverless RL and the deployment of high performance inference stacks. We will cover use cases to show how to leverage these tools on NVIDIA GPUs to build a unified stack that scales seamlessly, optimizes performance, and accelerates your time to production.

speakers.name: Kyle Corbitt
speakers.bio: Kyle Corbitt is director of engineering at CoreWeave, where he leads the OpenPipe team, driving reinforcement learning–based product development and integrating OpenPipe’s platform and customers into the CoreWeave stack. Previously, Kyle was founder and CEO of OpenPipe and held engineering and leadership roles at Y Combinator and Google. He brings deep experience building developer platforms, scalable products, and high-impact teams.

speakers.name: Aaron Batilo
speakers.bio: Aaron Batilo is a principal engineer, AI/ML at CoreWeave, where he designs and operates large-scale machine learning training and inference infrastructure. He brings deep experience from Microsoft AI, Inflection AI, and Cohere, building GPU supercomputer-scale Kubernetes platforms and multi-cloud inference systems. Earlier in his career, Aaron led cloud and platform engineering efforts across healthcare, media, and enterprise software, modernizing complex, high-scale production environments.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: 端到端用于自动驾驶的强化认知（Reinforced Cognitive）框架
sessions.abstract: 在小米汽车（Xiaomi EV），我们提出了一套全新的强化认知（Reinforced Cognitive）端到端自动驾驶框架：以“理解—预测—决策”一体化为目标，将世界模型（World Model）与强化学习（Reinforcement Learning, RL）深度融合，在同一端到端网络中同时完成驾驶环境理解与轨迹/动作规划，从而在多样化真实道路场景中显著提升行驶安全性与舒适性。具体而言，该框架以多模态感知输入（环视图像/BEV 特征、车身状态、导航意图与车道拓扑等）为统一上下文，先由“认知表征”模块形成可用于决策的紧凑隐空间；随后引入可学习的世界模型在数字空间内进行多主体交互与时序演化建模，输出未来占用/可行驶区域、关键交通参与者的多模态意图分布、风险与不确定性，并支持“想象式滚动（imagination rollout）”在若干步预测内生成可解释的候选未来；在此基础上，策略/价值网络通过强化学习进行闭环优化，以“安全—合规—效率—舒适”为核心奖励体系，结合离线RL与安全约束 RL/风险敏感 RL 实现稳定可控的策略改进；最终帮助模型不仅“会开”，还能“知道为什么这么开、哪里风险更高”，在复杂场景中获得更平顺、更安心且更可靠的驾驶行为。

speakers.name: 光 陈
speakers.bio: 陈光，现任小米辅助驾驶（HAD）算法与功能负责人，主导小米汽车辅助驾驶算法的研发与落地，带队发布了小米HAD千万clips端到端智驾软件、基于世界模型和强化学习的HAD增强版智驾软件等。学术上带领团队获得ICCV 2025自动驾驶方向数据合成挑战赛世界冠军，发表EMNLP、NIPS、ICLR等多篇论文。他博士毕业于美国密苏里大学，加入小米前先后担任百度美国 Apollo 感知系统负责人、一汽集团智驾总架构师等职务。作为创始成员参与Apollo多版本软件发布并在行业内产生广泛影响力，也交付了行业领先的NOA功能软件、L4 Robotaxi系统。多次获得计算机视觉和智驾国际比赛冠军，并在顶级学术会议发表多篇论文。

sessions.title: Build a GPU-Accelerated Database Engine With CUDA
sessions.abstract: Join us for a deep dive into how data-intensive workloads can be accelerated using GPUs. This session explores the inner workings of a GPU-accelerated query pipeline that offers excellent performance by leveraging custom kernels and NVIDIA libraries like Thurst and nvCOMP. Learn how data transfer becomes the primary bottleneck, and how faster interconnects like NVLink and GPU-accelerated decompression help mitigate the issue.

speakers.name: Harishankar G
speakers.bio: Harishankar is spearheading efforts in training and optimizing foundational models for Zoho Corp. With a focus on machine learning, GPU acceleration, and CUDA kernels, he drives advancements in high performance computing for AI.

speakers.name: Jalakandeshwaran A
speakers.bio: Jalakandeshwaran is leading the GPU acceleration efforts at Zoho Corp. He is the architect and lead of Zoho's GPU-accelerated database engine built in-house.

sessions.title: Manufacturing Intelligence: Agentic AI to Unlock the Next Level of Performance
sessions.abstract:
Hear about an intelligent, self-learning, and adaptive system where manufacturing expertise is accelerated, orchestrated, and augmented in real time through a unified, virtual platform (digital twin). The platform is accessible to operators, engineers, maintenance teams, and managers via natural interfaces and smart tools.
All knowledge, experience, and best practices related to production—world-class manufacturing—are transformed into structured digital models, continuously enriched by data and made available through AI agents acting as “digital mentors.”

speakers.name: Paolo Avagliano
speakers.bio: Paolo is passionate about software-defined approaches to physical industries, fostering innovation in complex, evolving environments and experienced in business strategy, planning, and operational control, with a focus on digital transformation. He currently serves as a senior advisor at Accenture; previously, he held CEO and COO roles at Bright Machines, driving strategic vision and building full-stack technology teams across hardware, software, and AI/ML. Paolo is also the former head of digital at Comau North America. Core competencies include business strategy, operational execution, digital technologies, and AI/ML.

speakers.name: Pierluigi Astorino
speakers.bio: Driving global operations and building purpose-driven teams to achieve excellence and sustainable growth, Pierluigi currently serves as chief operating officer at Ariston Group, with responsibility for optimizing performance and leading strategic initiatives across international markets. He brings over 15 years of experience in the automotive industry, specializing in operational efficiency, supply chain management, and transformative leadership. Pierluigi is committed to fostering innovation and collaboration to deliver measurable impact and long-term value.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - 3D Model Generation

sessions.title: Megatron Core: Customer Stories on the Open-Source Training Framework for Ultra Speed at Scale
sessions.abstract: Join this session as Megatron core customers Reflection, Periodic Labs, XHS Rednote, and ByteDance describe their engineering use cases and best practices for pre-training their workloads. You will also hear about their contributions into this OSS project, features they leveraged for NVIDIA's latest hardware systems, and tuning for best performance at scale.

speakers.name: Santosh Bhavani
speakers.bio: Santosh Bhavani is a product manager at NVIDIA focusing on deep learning frameworks and Transformer Engine. Santosh has a BS in computer science and an MS from Carnegie Mellon.

speakers.name: Costa Huang
speakers.bio: Costa Huang works on using reinforcement learning to train AI scientists at Periodic Labs. He is interested in asynchronous reinforcement learning training techniques and large-scale distributed training infrastructures for large language models.

speakers.name: Wang Zhang
speakers.bio: Wang Zhang is a machine learning architecture engineer at ByteDance Seed, where he focuses on building scalable infrastructure for reinforcement learning. He is the co-initiator of the veRL project, specifically prototyping the system's single-controller architecture. Wang Zhang holds an M.Eng. from Carnegie Mellon University.

speakers.name: Aakanksha Chowdhery
speakers.bio: Aakanksha currently serves as a research leader at Reflection AI focused on pretraining and as an Adjunct Professor of Computer Science at Stanford. Previously, Aakanksha served as the lead for scaling and stability of pretraining for Gemini models at Google Deepmind.

speakers.name: Hongbin Liu
speakers.bio: Hongbin joined NVIDIA in 2020 to focus on large-scale distributed training. He also has experience on CUDA parallel computing and HPC.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Deep Learning Frameworks

sessions.title: Architect the AI Factory for the NVIDIA Rubin Era: Scaling is Key (Presented by GIGABYTE)
sessions.abstract:
As we enter the era of the NVIDIA Rubin architecture, the global demand for trillion-parameter models and agentic AI is redefining the modern data center. Infrastructure is no longer just about individual servers; it is about building the "AI factory"—a seamless, high-density environment capable of processing massive workloads with unprecedented efficiency. 

In this session, GIGABYTE (Giga Computing) will reveal how we are bridging the gap between cutting-edge silicon and a successful industrial-scale deployment with RIKEN QHPC project in running NVIDIA Blackwell products. We will explore the architectural evolution of our latest server lineup, specifically engineered to harness the power of NVIDIA Vera CPUs and Rubin GPUs.

speakers.name: Leon Chang
speakers.bio: Leon Chang is a product and market lead at Giga Computing (GIGABYTE), specializing in high-density AI infrastructure and advanced cooling technologies. Over six years of experience in the U.S., India, and Australia/New Zealand markets, Leon has participated some of the industry's most significant projects, including 3,000-node AI clusters for NCP, and servers for top-tier ecommerce giants and pioneering immersion cooling projects. A strategic link between R&D and end users, Leon is dedicated to building the foundational hardware that drives the global AI revolution.

sessions.title: Transform Enterprise AI and Connectivity From Cloud to Edge (Presented by QCT)
sessions.abstract: Explore how QCT collaborates with ecosystem partners to deliver optimized AI systems as application-ready solutions. Built with scalable GPU-accelerated infrastructure, the QCT AI POD powered by NVIDIA provides a pre-validated AI platform that simplifies AI cluster deployment and management, enabling enterprises to efficiently run AI workloads while accelerating time-to-market. To address the telecom industry's focus on AI-native networks, QCT is also introducing a new 2U AI-RAN platform based on NVIDIA ARC-Pro reference architecture. This converged infrastructure supports radio access network (RAN) and AI workloads simultaneously, enables AI-powered performance and spectral efficiency improvements, and supports new edge AI use cases, such as real-time industrial automation.

speakers.name: Daphne Tsai
speakers.bio: Daphne Tsai is a solution manager at QCT (Quanta Cloud Technology), specializing in AI and cloud infrastructure solutions. Dedicated to transforming enterprise AI, she collaborates with ecosystem partners to enable cluster-level infrastructure. Her role focuses on streamlining the AI development life cycle and simplifying system management through innovative solutions that boost overall enterprise efficiency.

speakers.name: Pei-Wen Wang
speakers.bio: Pei-Wen is a solution manager at QCT (Quanta Cloud Technology), focusing on partner collaboration and AI-driven applications. She works at the intersection of physical AI, AI software, and server infrastructure, translating real-world AI use cases into scalable, production-ready deployments. By closely working with ecosystem partners, Pei-Wen enables seamless AI–server integration that maximizes performance and reliability across diverse industry scenarios.

sessions.title: Accelerate Physical AI: An Integrated Solution for Rapid Development (Presented by QCT)
sessions.abstract:
Explore how to accelerate physical AI deployments using the QCT Application-Ready Solution concept. By deep-diving into the QCT Dev Kit architecture, a toolkit that integrates high-performance QCT GPU servers with NVIDIA Cosmos, NVIDIA Isaac GR00T, and NVIDIA Omniverse, customers can deliver end-to-end physical AI solutions faster than ever.  

 
Learn how QCT works with TM Robot to overcome humanoid era challenges and achieve industrial workload validation.
