Through the TM Robot "Xplore I" collaboration, the companies manage to improve humanoid performance via specialized GUI designs and enable five key features (system, skills, datasets, models, and USD file management).

speakers.name: Kevin Wu
speakers.bio: Kevin Wu is an engineer and a key member of the Techman Humanoid Innovation and Knowledge Center (THINK Center) at Techman Robot. He received an M.S. from the Program on AI Robotics, National Cheng Kung University, Taiwan. He focuses on advancing embodied intelligence by integrating physical AI and agentic reasoning, and is dedicated to the industrial application and real-world deployment of humanoid robots.

speakers.name: Pei-Wen Wang
speakers.bio: Pei-Wen is a solution manager at QCT (Quanta Cloud Technology), focusing on partner collaboration and AI-driven applications. She works at the intersection of physical AI, AI software, and server infrastructure, translating real-world AI use cases into scalable, production-ready deployments. By closely working with ecosystem partners, Pei-Wen enables seamless AI–server integration that maximizes performance and reliability across diverse industry scenarios.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation

sessions.title: Close the Sim-to-Real Gap: Next-Gen Techniques for Synthetic Data Mastery (Presented by World Wide Technology)
sessions.abstract: Stop relying on limited real-world datasets. Join us to explore proven techniques to leverage NVIDIA Omniverse Replicator and NVIDIA Cosmos to create synthetic imagery for building robust computer vision models that recognize rare objects and challenging conditions. We will dive deep into synthetic imagery best practices, the NVIDIA hardware and software stacks that accelerate the process, and the critical decision points that maximize your synthetic data return on investment and accelerate your path to production-ready physical AI.

speakers.name: Paige Reiter
speakers.bio:
Paige Reiter is a technical leader focused on advancing computer vision and synthetic imagery to solve real-world business challenges. She works at the intersection of AI, simulation, and digital twins, helping organizations accelerate model development by using synthetic data, physics-based simulation, and visual AI pipelines.

With hands-on experience leveraging NVIDIA technologies to generate, label, and validate high-fidelity visual data, Paige specializes in translating complex AI concepts into practical, production-ready solutions. Her work centers on enabling teams to move faster from experimentation to deployment, by reducing data constraints and improving model performance at scale.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Image / Video Detection & Recognition

sessions.title: NVIDIA 加速辅助驾驶 2.0 时代 AI 定义汽车的大规模应用
sessions.abstract: 本次演讲将介绍 NVIDIA 的辅助驾驶核心模块，从云端训练到车端推理的全栈解决方案，包括 NVIDIA Cosmos Reason（视频理解和 VLA 骨干网络）、Cosmos World Model（全局场景仿真）、 NuRec 神经重建和闭环仿真、ACCV-Lab 辅助驾驶训练工具包以及面向 NVIDIA DRIVE Thor 的 VLA 模型部署及展望

speakers.name: 浩楠 邵
speakers.bio: 中国汽车高级解决方案架构师。负责国内汽车行业客户，包括数据中心软硬件相关支持、辅助驾驶技术栈支持、中国汽车客户 Cosmos 相关解决方案。

speakers.name: 文静 朱
speakers.bio: GPU 计算专家组经理，主要负责数据中心中自动驾驶以及具身智能任务的加速。

speakers.name: 涛 尹
speakers.bio: 尹涛，软件经理，主要从事嵌入式 GPU 平台的模型性能优化，曾支持多个 Orin 和 Thor 项目算法量产落地。

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Video Analytics

sessions.title: Vision to AI-Driven Software Engineering
sessions.abstract: This session explores how bold product vision can be translated into real, production-grade software using AI-driven engineering practices. We’ll walk through the evolving role of AI across the software life cycle—from ideation and system design to coding, testing, deployment, and continuous improvement.

speakers.name: Eiso Kant
speakers.bio: Eiso is an engineer who's been building early-stage companies in the AI and developer tools space for over a decade. Prior to founding poolside, Eiso was the founder and CEO of Athenian, a data-enabled engineering platform. Before that, he built source{d}—the world’s first company dedicated to applying AI to code and software—where he lived firsthand through the challenges poolside faces, which seeded the thinking for poolside’s early technical and applied research decisions. As CEO of his previous three companies, Eiso raised funds from prominent European and U.S. investors, many of whom have followed him across companies. Eiso also sits on the board of a private GPU cloud provider.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Maximize LLM Throughput and Improve GPU Efficiency
sessions.abstract: This session focuses on practical and system-level techniques to increase large language model throughput while reducing GPU cost and waste. It covers both training and inference optimization, with an emphasis on real-world bottlenecks such as memory bandwidth, kernel inefficiencies, batching limits, and underutilized GPUs.

speakers.name: Dwarak Talupuru
speakers.bio: Dwarak is a Staff Research Engineer at Cohere and leads the training performance engineering and hardware-aligned architecture design team. Main responsibilities include contributing to Cohere's internal training framework based on JAX to utilize thousands of GPUs, and research fused low-level CUDA kernels and optimization techniques at scale.

speakers.name: Bharat Venkitesh
speakers.bio: Bharat Venkitesh is a Staff Research Engineer on Cohere's Foundations team, where he leads inference performance and efficiency initiatives for large-language models. His work spans quantization, speculative decoding, pruning, and kernel-level optimizations, and he collaborates closely with pre-training, reinforcement learning, and hardware-aligned architecture design teams to ensure models are inference-aware from the ground up. Bharat holds a master's degree from the University of Waterloo and a bachelor's degree from the Indian Institute of Technology Hyderabad.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Liquid-Cooled AI for Next-Generation Platforms (Presented by Supermicro)
sessions.abstract: As AI factories scale to support larger models and higher cluster densities, liquid cooling has become essential. This session, presented by Supermicro and NVIDIA, explores how next-generation AI factories are built using liquid-cooled NVIDIA HGX systems and NVL72 rack-scale platforms, enabling dense, high-performance GPU clusters for large-scale AI training. Gain insight into Supermicro's deployment of these platforms as validated, rack-scale solutions, rather than standalone servers. We'll cover key design and deployment considerations, including direct-to-chip cooling and rack-level liquid integration, and show how liquid-cooled total solutions accelerate time-to-online while improving performance and energy efficiency for modern AI factories.

speakers.name: Alok Srivastava
speakers.bio: Alok  Srivastava is responsible for helping Supermicro customers along their journey to AI/ML and HPC solutions. He's an interface with customer and SMCI leadership, driving collaboration among the other core account groups like Sales, FAE, Support, Solutions Architecture, and Professional Services, product/engineering teams, and customer teams, a key part of supporting the customer. He also translates strategic initiatives in the account plan into executable actions. Alok has more than two decades of industry experience.

speakers.name: Steven Huang
speakers.bio: Steven Huang joined Supermicro in 2019 and focuses on liquid-cooled AI infrastructure and DCBBS system architecture. He works across platform definition and customer-aligned solution design, as well as deployment and operations, to deliver scalable, production-ready data center solutions, with an emphasis on enabling high-density, rack-scale AI platforms.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: Distributed AI Computing From AI Factories to the Edge (Presented by Supermicro)
sessions.abstract: Enterprises are moving from AI experimentation to production, where return on investment (ROI) matters as much as performance. This session, presented by Supermicro and NVIDIA, explores how AI factories, edge AI, and on-premises deployments work together to deliver scalable, cost-effective AI solutions. We'll highlight how systems powered by GPUs such as the NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition support inference, visualization, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) workloads across data centers and edge environments. We'll also examine how RAG pipelines benefit from tightly integrated GPUs, networking, and storage to deliver low-latency, context-aware results—helping enterprises improve data control, reduce operational costs, and maximize ROI from AI investments.

speakers.name: Steve Stein
speakers.bio: Steve is a product marketing manager with a decade of experience selling AI and HPC systems, as well as developing next-generation space technologies for the U.S. Air Force. He collaborates with global system partners to assist enterprise customers in deploying AI infrastructure at scale, ensuring high performance, faster time-to-market, and reduced costs. Steve is passionate about generative AI and its transformative potential across industries.

speakers.name: Mory Lin
speakers.bio: Mory is a global leader of engineering and solution architecture teams in Supermicro Headquarter in Silicon Valley, driving worldwide business and partnership in AI inference and edge computing for the internet of things, retail, telco, smart place, intelligent industry 4.0, and healthcare IT segments.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Accelerate Industrial Intelligence: Practice Across Design and Production (Presented by GIGABYTE)
sessions.abstract:
Deploying autonomous robots and smart manufacturing systems requires a computing architecture that extends far beyond the edge. This session explores how to build a converged "AI factory" infrastructure that prioritizes the low-latency demands of advanced robotics and intelligent production, while simultaneously supporting the massive compute requirements of upstream physical AI workflows.​

Learn architectural strategies to manage these mixed workloads on a single platform, ensuring real-time responsiveness for machines without sacrificing simulation performance. Finally, we will demonstrate how the GAIFA (GIGABYTE AI Factory Accelerator) ecosystem integrates specialized partners to power this end-to-end industrial autonomy.

speakers.name: Weiru Li
speakers.bio: Weiru Li is a technology leader specializing in AI infrastructure, MLOps, and cloud computing. He serves as deputy director at the Software and Solutions Center of Giga Computing, driving AI and HPC solution strategies, and as COO of Myelintek, an AI/MLOps software company. With deep expertise in AI platforms, GPU-centric infrastructure, and cloud-native systems, he focuses on turning advanced AI technologies into scalable, production-ready solutions.

speakers.name: Huei-Ru Tseng
speakers.bio: Dr. Huei-Ru Tseng obtained her Ph.D. in Computer Science from National Chiao Tung University, Taiwan, in 2010. Since then, she has worked at the Division for Unmanned Vehicle Network & Sensing System of the Information and Communications Research Laboratories (ICL) at the Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI), where she currently serves as a Department Manager. Her research interests include service robots, autonomous sensing systems, and V2X communication protocols.

sessions.title: Transform Data for Industrial Mobility Automation
sessions.abstract: Discover how Oxa transforms both real-world data (real-to-real) and data from SIM (sim-to-real) leveraging NVIDIA Cosmos for industrial domains such as ports, airports, or solar farms. We'll showcase results, both qualitative and quantitative, from training downstream tasks such as perception on this transformed data.

speakers.name: Horia Porav
speakers.bio: Horia leads Oxa's Foundry team, which develops the in-house data generation and development toolchain that powers Oxa's machine learning capabilities.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Image Generation

sessions.title: Scaling the AI Factory With Full-Stack, Digitally Orchestrated Infrastructure (Presented by Vertiv)
sessions.abstract: As AI factories scale from megawatts to gigawatts, building around repeatable building blocks, including prefabricated modules for power, cooling, heat rejection, overhead infrastructure, and services into a single, factory-assembled system designed to simplify execution and compress schedules by reducing on-site labor intensity and complexity, is paramount. This session introduces the AI Factory playbook, physics-driven design, and digital-first strategy, as an innovative migration from static Building Information Modeling (BIM) to a dynamic digital twin environment, powered by Universal Scene Description (OpenUSD) and NVIDIA Omniverse libraries. This full-stack infrastructure evolves from disaggregated parts to a unified systems that addresses the friction of traditional design and construction by fundamentally changing the physics of deployment.

speakers.name: Scott Armul
speakers.bio: Scott is responsible for driving the company’s global technology and portfolio strategy, leading engineering research and development, and overseeing the business units that comprise Vertiv’s comprehensive portfolio, including thermal management, power management, IT systems, infrastructure solutions, and global services. Scott is focused on delivering innovative solutions that address the evolving needs of customers in today’s increasingly dynamic digital economy. Scott began his career with Emerson Network Power (now Vertiv) in 2009, and holds an M.B.A. from The Ohio State University Fisher College of Business and a B.S. in mechanical engineering from Washington University in St. Louis.

sessions.title: The Ecosystem Engine: Co-Creating the AI Factory (Presented by Vertiv)
sessions.abstract:
This session explores how deep, pre-validated collaboration is the only path to de-risking the AI factory. We will discuss how digital twin-driven design allows the ecosystem to visualize and solve thermal and power challenges before physical deployment, enabling a "design once, deploy everywhere" approach. We will examine how this co-creation strategy supports the data center as a unit of compute, ensuring that power, thermal, and IT systems operate as a single, synchronized organism, rather than disjointed components.
 
Anchored in the Vertiv Frontiers 2026 trends, we’ll show how ecosystem-driven integration becomes mission-critical as architectures evolve. We’ll also cover how digital twins—using physically accurate simulation—enable teams to validate designs, failure scenarios, and performance before steel is cut, reducing schedule and operational risk.

speakers.name: Martin Olsen
speakers.bio: Martin Olsen serves as Global Vice President of Edge and Integrated Solutions at Vertiv, where he leads the company's efforts to deploy integrated physical infrastructure, deployment services, and remote management at the network edge. Originally from Denmark, he joined Vertiv in 2017 and has held several leadership positions in engineering, strategy, and commercial planning. With over 20 years of experience with global critical infrastructure providers in the data center industry.

sessions.title: Solving the “Three Computer” Problem: Building the Cloud Infrastructure for Physical AI (Presented by Nebius)
sessions.abstract: Physical AI represents a fundamental shift in computing, demanding the seamless integration of three distinct workloads—high-fidelity simulation, large-scale model training, and low-latency real-world inference—which NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang calls the "Three Computer" problem. Join this panel featuring leaders from Nebius and pioneering robotics and simulation companies as they dissect what it takes to bring this architecture to life. Moving beyond theory, the discussion will focus on the concrete infrastructure, orchestration layers (including NVIDIA OSMO and Cosmos), and pipeline strategies required to close the loop between virtual worlds and physical reality.

speakers.name: Bill Vass
speakers.bio: William (Bill) Vass is chief technology officer at Booz Allen, where he sets the firm’s technical vision and strategy, driving the development of advanced and dual-use technologies across AI, space systems, quantum, robotics, and simulation. He brings more than 40 years of experience spanning government and industry, including serving as vice president of engineering at Amazon Web Services, where he led the development of over 60 core AWS services, as well as senior executive roles across the U.S. Department of Defense, Fortune 100 companies, and high-growth startups.

speakers.name: Evan Helda
speakers.bio: Evan Helda is the head of physical AI at Nebius, where he leads business development, product strategy, and go-to-market for physical AI and robotics. He is also the creator of Dream Machines, a newsletter and podcast exploring physical AI and the future of the human experience. Before joining Nebius, Evan founded and led AWS’ Spatial Computing business, where he drove go-to-market and product strategy for digital twins, simulation, and spatial data services— technologies that now form the backbone of many emerging physical AI workloads.

speakers.name: Lerrel Pinto
speakers.bio: Lerrel Pinto is the CEO of Assured Robot Intelligence (ARI). His work focuses broadly on robot learning and decision-making, with an emphasis on large-scale learning (both data and models), representation learning for sensory data, developing algorithms to model actions and behavior, reinforcement learning for adapting to new scenarios, and building open-sourced affordable robots. This work has received best paper awards at ICRA and RSS. Lerrel has also been recognized with major honors including the Samsung AI Researcher of the Year Award, Sloan and Packard Fellowships, an NSF CAREER Award, and recognition as a TR35 Innovator Under 35.

speakers.name: Steve Xie
speakers.bio: Steve is founder and CEO of Lightwheel, a company leading simulation infrastructure for embodied AI and a pioneer in generative AI-powered simulation for robotics. He holds a B.S. from Peking University and a Ph.D. from Columbia University. Steve has led simulation efforts at NVIDIA and Cruise, where he built end-to-end synthetic data pipelines that set industry benchmarks for realism, scalability, and sim-to-real transfer.

speakers.name: Jon Quick
speakers.bio: Jon Quick is the CEO of Launchpad.build, where he leads the development of robotics with embedded intelligence to make manufacturing flexible, fast to deploy, and economically compelling. Prior to Launchpad, Jon served as CEO of two technology firms and as a venture partner at a $4.5 billion venture capital fund, where he spent nearly a decade investing in and scaling frontier technology companies. He began his career as a consultant at Bain & Co. Jon is a member of Canada’s influential C100 technology network and a contributor to the World Economic Forum.

sessions.title: Scale Inference Using Open Models: How Nebius Token Factory Delivers Control and Efficiency (Presented by Nebius)
sessions.abstract: AI is moving from experimentation to global production, and inference efficiency and quality are the defining competitive edge. This session dives deep into the engineering behind high-performance, open-model inference, covering the system decisions, runtime techniques, and architectural patterns that unlock predictable, real-world performance at scale. Learn how Nebius Token Factory and NVIDIA push beyond benchmark-driven results to deliver structural, compounding gains in latency, throughput, and cost.

speakers.name: Rodolphe Cambier
speakers.bio: Rodolphe Cambier is the Lead Data Scientist for AI Automation at Revolut, where he drives the development of intelligent, scalable automation systems that enhance operational efficiency and decision-making across the organization. With deep expertise in machine learning, applied AI, and production-grade data systems, he focuses on translating advanced models into high-impact business outcomes within fast-growing, regulated financial environments.

speakers.name: Roman Chernin
speakers.bio: Roman Chernin is co-founder and chief business officer of Nebius, a full-stack AI company. He leads product direction and business strategy, including for Token Factory, Nebius’ enterprise-grade inference platform for deploying and scaling open-source models. Roman previously spent more than a decade at Yandex, where he led search, and later maps & navigation.

speakers.name: Omri Bruchim
speakers.bio: Omri Bruchim is the Engineering Group Lead of the AI Platform Group at monday.com, where he leads the development of trustworthy, production-ready AI systems that power the Monday's next-generation as AI work platform. With nearly 20 years of experience in software engineering and leadership, including a GM role at Wix and founding a real-time data platform, he holds degrees from Ben-Gurion University and an MBA from Tel Aviv University.

sessions.title: TACC Horizon System in the Converged World of HPC and AI
sessions.abstract: Learn about the role of the Horizon HPC-AI Supercomputer for the scientific community, and how Horizon will be a step-change technology system with all of the NVIDIA hardware and software platforms.

speakers.name: Dan Stanzione
speakers.bio: Dan Stanzione, associate vice president at The University of Texas at Austin since 2018 and executive director of TACC since 2014, is a recognized leader in HPC. He serves on the National AI Research Resource Task Force, formed by the National Science Foundation and the White House. He's the principal investigator (PI) for an NSF grant to deploy Frontera, the fastest supercomputer at any U.S. university, and the PI of TACC's Stampede2 and Wrangler supercomputers. Previously, he was co-PI of the CyVerse cyber-infrastructure. Dan was also a co-PI for TACC's Ranger and Lonestar supercomputers at UT Austin. Dan received his bachelor's and master's degrees and his Ph.D. from Clemson University.

sessions.title: Build Faster, Smarter AI Agents: Techniques for Scalable Reasoning and Real-Time Efficiency
sessions.abstract: As AI agents evolve from simple task executors to adaptive systems capable of reasoning and decision-making, efficient orchestration of compute and model execution becomes essential. In this session, two NVIDIA engineers will walk through the latest frameworks and techniques for accelerating AI agents—from optimizing memory and compute pipelines to managing multi-stage reasoning workflows. Learn how to design high-performance agent architectures that can dynamically plan, retrieve, and act in real time while scaling seamlessly across GPUs.

speakers.name: Michael Demoret
speakers.bio: Michael Demoret is the lead engineer and architect for NVIDIA Morpheus, an application framework that allows developers to build high-performance pipelines integrated with AI capabilities for the cybersecurity domain. Michael has been with NVIDIA for over seven years and focused on building production-ready inferencing pipelines for large-scale, multi-model AI deployments across multiple sectors. Prior to NVIDIA, Michael spent seven years as a software developer, producing commercial off-the-shelf software for the aerospace industry. His past projects include collision avoidance for the International Space Station and building the first GPU-accelerated ground system at NASA.

speakers.name: Kyle Kranen
speakers.bio: Kyle Kranen is a team lead and manager in the Deep Learning Algorithms Organization at NVIDIA. His charter centers on blending engineering and research in order to accelerate LLM inference at the data center scale.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: The 50-State Plan: Public-Private Models for AI Infrastructure and University Transformation
sessions.abstract:
As AI becomes the definitive engine of economic and academic growth, the race is on to democratize access to high performance computing across the United States. This panel explores "The 50-State Plan," a strategic roadmap for building robust, statewide AI infrastructure through innovative public-private partnerships. The session brings together visionary tech-industry pioneers, leaders from the National Science Foundation (NSF), and executives from top-tier research universities to discuss the shift from isolated pockets of innovation to unified strategies for university transformation and mission success in the age of AI.

Join us to learn how your organization can participate in this nationwide effort to bridge the digital divide and accelerate the next era of discovery.

speakers.name: Jack Wells
speakers.bio:
Jack Wells engages thought leaders across the computing ecosystem, focusing on aligning NVIDIA platform research requirements. Jack joined NVIDIA in 2021 following 23 years at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, where he was director of science for the team that delivered three world-leading supercomputers at the Oak Ridge Leadership Computing Facility, a U.S. Department of Energy national user facility. He's authored or co-authored more than 100 scientific papers and edited two books. 

Jack serves as president of OpenACC, a consortium dedicated to the research community’s advancement by expanding accelerated and parallel computing capabilities. Previously, he served as chair of the Division of Computational Physics of the American Physical Society and as science adviser to U.S. Senator Lamar Alexander. Jack has a Ph.D. in physics from Vanderbilt University.

speakers.name: Chris Malachowsky
speakers.bio: Chris Malachowsky founded NVIDIA in 1993 and has more than 40 years of industry experience. He’s a member of the executive staff and a senior technology executive for NVIDIA. He’s been instrumental in managing, defining, and driving the company’s core technologies as it has grown to the global leader in accelerated computing. At NVIDIA, he’s led numerous functions, including IT, operations, and product engineering.

speakers.name: Deborah Crawford
speakers.bio: Deborah Crawford is the vice chancellor for research, innovation, and economic development at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Since joining UT in 2020, she has led the university’s research, innovation, and economic development initiatives, elevating its national and global impact. She previously held leadership roles at George Mason University, the International Computer Science Institute, Drexel University, and the National Science Foundation. She holds a Ph.D. in information systems engineering from the University of Bradford and a B.S. (honors) in electronic and electrical engineering from the University of Glasgow.

speakers.name: Brian Stone
speakers.bio: Brian W. Stone is performing the duties of the director for the U.S. National Science Foundation, guiding policy and institutional priorities and serving as the agency’s principal representative to the White House, Congress, federal agencies, and domestic and international partners. NSF funds bold research and innovation to advance the progress of science and engineering, national health, and help secure the national defense through investments in areas such as AI, quantum information science, and biotechnology. NSF is one of the largest non-defense federal funders of AI research.

speakers.name: Theresa Mayer
speakers.bio:
Dr. Theresa Mayer is the vice president for research at Carnegie Mellon University, supporting the research, creativity, and entrepreneurship that drives its mission and working with partners across the public and private sectors to bring the benefits of this work to all of society. 
Prior to Carnegie Mellon University, Dr. Mayer served in executive positions at Purdue University and Virginia Tech, where she played leading roles in enhancing cutting-edge research, building strategic partnerships, and stimulating economic development. Previously, she was a distinguished professor of electrical engineering at Penn State University, where she served as the associate dean for research in engineering and the site director for the NSF National Nanotechnology Infrastructure Network.
Dr. Mayer has been an active advocate for the critical role that science, technology, and innovation play in driving economic prosperity and security both nationally and globally through her testimonies before the U.S. House of Representatives Science, Space, and Technology Committee and the House Armed Services Committee, and her service on boards such as the Engineering Research Visioning Alliance, National Defense Industry Association, and others.
She earned her B.S in electrical engineering from Virginia Tech, and M.S. and Ph.D. in electrical engineering from Purdue University.

sessions.title: Unlocking Data at Scale for AI Factories (Presented by DDN)
sessions.abstract: In next-generation AI infrastructure, the data layer determines how efficiently GPUs run, how much energy and cooling are consumed, and how fast retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines and multi-modal workloads can operate. This session reveals why storage and data architecture built from the NVIDIA AI Data Platform reference design—including NVIDIA AI Enterprise—are the key drivers for AI factory success. Learn the core design patterns and pitfalls that decide whether turnkey, large-scale AI runs at full speed or stalls.

speakers.name: Jacob Liberman
speakers.bio: Jacob Liberman is a manager in the Enterprise Product group. His team builds NVIDIA AI blueprints, agentic AI platforms, and open-source connectors to leading generative AI programming framworks. Previously, he held product management and engineering roles at Red Hat, AMD, and Dell.

speakers.name: James Coomer
speakers.bio: James steers the technology roadmap and analytics for AI and HPC solutions at DDN. His expertise centers on advancing whole-solution performance for diverse AI and HPC industries, from life sciences through finance. With a foundational Ph.D. in theoretical physics, James has over a decade of experience at leading tech companies like Sun Microsystems and Dell.

sessions.title: Scale Foundation Models With Google Cloud AI Hypercomputer (Presented by Google Cloud)
sessions.abstract:
As AI models evolve from billions to trillions of parameters, the infrastructure requirements have shifted from simple compute to complex, vertically integrated systems. In this session, Google Cloud product leaders will unveil the latest advancements in the AI hypercomputer architecture, designed for the NVIDIA Blackwell era.

We will dive deep into the performance benchmarks and architectural breakthroughs of our newest GPU instances, including A4X VMs (powered by NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 GPU), A4X Max (powered by NVIDIA GB300 NVL72) and G4 VMs (powered by NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server edition).

Learn how Google Cloud leverages its third-generation liquid cooling, Jupiter network fabric, storage, and Titanium ML adapters to eliminate bottlenecks, reduce costs, improve GPU utilization, and provide the most scalable environment for the next wave of generative and physical AI.

speakers.name: Pramod Ramarao
speakers.bio: Pramod Ramarao is a product manager at Google Cloud, helping to build GPU systems infrastructure for giant-scale training and serving workloads. Prior to joining Google, he worked for several years at NVIDIA—as the product lead for CUDA and then as part of the HGX product team to build and launch NVLink scale-out systems for CSPs/hyperscalers. Pramod started his career as a compiler engineer at IBM.

speakers.name: Miguel Holguin
speakers.bio: Miguel Holguin is a Google Cloud Product Manager based in Austin, Texas. With over three years at Google, he specializes in AI infrastructure, having led the market launches of NVIDIA-powered G4 and A3 Ultra VMs to provide customers with high-performance compute solutions.

sessions.title: Blueprint for AI Scale: How Industry Architects Success With NVIDIA GPUs on Google Cloud (Presented by Google Cloud)
sessions.abstract:
The gap between a successful "proof of concept" and a production-grade AI system is often defined by infrastructure choices. In this panel, technical leaders from leading companies pull back the curtain on how they built their AI stacks using NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs (A4X Max, A4X, G4) on Google Cloud’s AI Hypercomputer.

Our panelists will engage in a technical post mortem of their deployment journeys. We will explore:

•	The Infrastructure: A look at how Google Cloud customers configured their architecture to manage massive GPU clusters
•	The Use Cases: Deep dives into high-impact implementations, from training sovereign LLMs and high-fidelity digital twins to deploying real-time agentic workflows
•	The Results: Key outcomes and metrics on both technical performance and business impact

speakers.name: Jason Monden
speakers.bio:
Jason Monden is a Group Product Manager at Google Cloud, specializing in AI Infrastructure. With over two decades of experience in the technology sector, Jason has held senior leadership and technical roles at several influential enterprise organizations, including Oracle, EMC, and DXC Technology.

A seasoned entrepreneur and technologist, Jason’s background includes founding and leading cloud startups. Throughout his career, he has focused on cloud computing, IT modernization, and scaling infrastructure. Based in Denver, Colorado, he continues to work at the intersection of cloud technology, enterprise software, and the evolving landscape of AI infrastructure.

speakers.name: Perry Nightingale
speakers.bio:
Perry Nightingale is the SVP for Creative AI, working in the office of the CTO managing groundbreaking research and furthering WPP’s market-leading combination of creativity, AI and advanced data science. Perry is a passionate software engineer, currently specialising in generative AI, robotics and computational creativity, but is also a leading figure in the traditional communication industry - sitting on innovation juries for Cannes and D&AD, identified by Creativity magazine in the US as one of the pioneers in the new industry genre of Creative Technologists and listed in AdAges's annual 50 most influential people in media. He works with WPP creative agencies around the world on groundbreaking new campaigns and advises major clients on digital disruption, robotics and brand-led innovation, winning many awards for his work, as well as lecturing regularly on topics such as ethics & AI for the Said Business School at Oxford University.

Outside of work Perry champions diversity in the media & technology industries and co-founded the WPP Creative Technology Apprenticeship bringing a wide range of junior talent into the organisation.

speakers.name: Sony Mohapatra
speakers.bio:
I am a Director in AI/ML Engineering at General Motors, where I lead the AV ML Infrastructure organization powering large-scale autonomous vehicle simulations and machine learning training. My team builds and operates the compute platforms and distributed systems that enable rapid experimentation, model development, and performance validation across GM’s autonomous stack.

I hold a Master’s degree in Computer Science from The University of Texas at Dallas and began my career in California at Informatica, later contributing to engineering teams at LinkedIn, Apple, and Cruise. Across my career, I have focused on scaling high-performance infrastructure, optimizing GPU utilization, and building resilient platforms that support cutting-edge ML workloads.

A passionate advocate for women in technology, I mentor through Women in Autonomy and Plato and actively invest in developing the next generation of engineering leaders. I lives in California with my husband and our 12-year-old son.

speakers.name: Shane Brauner
speakers.bio: Shane Brauner is the EVP, CIO of Schrödinger. For 15+ years at Schrödinger, Shane has built and scaled the infrastructure, data systems, and security capabilities that enable researchers to run advanced molecular simulations. Before becoming CIO, he held a series of leadership roles across the organization, shaping everything from operations to large-scale GPU computing. Earlier in his career, he led global grid computing support at Pfizer and worked across academia, startups, and enterprise environments. Shane also serves as chair of NewYorkCIO.

sessions.title: Fraud Prevention in the Age of Agentic Commerce
sessions.abstract: Join a Stripe engineering lead for an inside look at how Stripe’s AI‑powered fraud prevention solution, Radar, is built with NVIDIA hardware and software to detect and block fraudulent transactions at massive scale, including emerging risks and new fraud vectors from agentic commerce to free trial abuse prevention. Trained on patterns derived from more than $1.4 trillion in annual payment volume, Radar uses advanced ML techniques to surface high‑confidence signals that reduced fraud rates for its users by 17% year-over-year.

sessions.title: The Physics of Long-Context Inference: Breaking the Memory Wall With NVIDIA Dynamo (Presented by VAST Data)
sessions.abstract:
Long-context AI conversations create a "memory wall" where GPU high-bandwidth memory becomes a bottleneck for scaling inference during agentic AI. This session shares a practical study of how NVIDIA Dynamo’s KV Block Manager (KVBM) accelerates large scale inference.

We'll examine Dynamo’s four-tiered architecture (G1–G4) that places data in the right tier at the right time to balance higher concurrency, throughput, and cost. We’ll show how VAST Data as the G3 tier achieves maximum memory and storage efficiency to eliminate the memory wall for long-running agents. Finally we’ll dive into the new NVIDIA Inference Context Management Storage platform, which provides a new class of AI-native storage infrastructure designed for gigascale inference. 

You'll leave with blueprints for architecting secure, persistent memory for the next generation of agentic AI.

speakers.name: Alon Horev
speakers.bio: Alon is co-founder and CTO at VAST. Alon has led multiple engineering tracks and driven success with strategic customer engagements.

speakers.name: Anat Heilper
speakers.bio: Anat Heilper is a seasoned technology leader with over 20 years of industry experience and a decade focused on AI accelerator architecture. As director of AI architecture at VAST Data, she leads the strategy and execution of large-scale AI model training and inference pipelines, bridging cutting-edge research with enterprise applications. Anat has a proven record of innovation, holding multiple AI optimization patents, and is skilled in hardware/software integration, LLM analysis, and scaling AI systems across diverse platforms.

sessions.title: Engineering Medical-Grade Platforms for Real-Time AI
sessions.abstract: Join us to learn how to turn NVIDIA IGX and Holoscan into a reusable, medical-grade platform for real-time AI. We'll walk through the complete stack, from system architecture and safety concepts to Holoscan graph design and containerized SaMD deployment, with a focus on the decisions, trade-offs, and patterns you can apply to your own devices. The session emphasizes how to meet strict latency and reliability constraints in a pass-through video pipeline, how to design a common regulated platform that can host multiple AI applications, and how to enable external teams to build and ship models on top of that platform.

speakers.name: Pietro Salvagnini
speakers.bio: Pietro Salvagnini is the director for science, AI, and data at Cosmo MedTech AI, where he leads the teams that develop artificial intelligence solutions for medical devices, covering the full pipeline from data curation and labeling to model design and real-time implementation. He holds a Ph.D. in pattern analysis and computer vision and has extensive experience in designing and deploying software for clinical and real-time AI applications.

speakers.name: Nhan Ngo Dinh
speakers.bio: Nhan Ngo Dinh is the president of Cosmo MedTech AI, a division of the Cosmo Pharmaceutical Group. He has nearly 25 years of experience in leading operations for developing and deploying healthcare products and medical devices in multiple specialties.

sessions.topic: Computer Vision / Video Analytics - Medical Imaging

sessions.title: AI Success Stories: Insights From the Frontlines (Presented by Dell Technologies)
sessions.abstract: How will you unlock the true value of AI? That’s the critical question organizations are addressing today. In this session, Honeywell and Southern California Edison will join us on stage to share the AI impact they’re delivering, their challenges and outcomes, and how they turned concepts into production-ready solutions.

speakers.name: Varun Chhabra
speakers.bio:
Varun Chhabra is Senior Vice President of Infrastructure (ISG) Product Marketing at Dell. In his role, Varun oversees product marketing for areas such as Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions, Enterprise Storage, Compute/Servers, HCI, Data Protection and Networking, as well as solutions in the Multicloud, Cyber Security, Edge, and Telecom spaces. He has been with Dell since 2015, and has previously led portfolio marketing, cloud product marketing, and storage product marketing teams at the company.
Prior to joining Dell, Varun spent time at Microsoft, as well as at Oracle. Varun has an MBA from the University of Michigan, and a BS in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from the University of California, Berkeley.

speakers.name: Kenny Ma
speakers.bio:
Kenny Ma is an AI and Automation Advisor at Southern California Edison, where he architects on-premises AI platforms for grid utility operations. He leads the development of the organization’s AI Factory, enabling secure and scalable deployment of advanced AI capabilities while ensuring data sovereignty, regulatory compliance, and operational resilience. His work focuses on bringing AI from concept to production in mission-critical environments, supporting grid reliability, cybersecurity, predictive maintenance, and enterprise decision-making.

Kenny’s experience spans AI engineering, automation, and large-scale data systems, grounded in a multidisciplinary foundation that bridges scientific rigor with enterprise technology. He holds a Master’s degree in Bioengineering from UC Riverside and a Bachelor’s degree in Integrative Biology from UC Berkeley. Throughout his career, he has focused on building high-throughput, production-grade systems that connect systems-level thinking with software engineering and operational strategy in highly regulated industries.

speakers.name: Mehul Patel
speakers.bio:
As the Vice President of Forge and Artificial Intelligence at Honeywell International Inc., Mehul oversees AI, data analytics, and product and software connectivity initiatives across the company.
Before assuming his current position, he served as the Vice President of Advanced Automation, where he led various automation projects and initiatives throughout Honeywell.
Earlier in his career, he held the roles of Chief Technology Officer and Vice President of Engineering for Honeywell Safety and Productivity Solutions. In this capacity, he managed the technology organizations within the Productivity Solutions and Services, Personal Protective Equipment, Sensing and Safety Technologies, and Honeywell Intelligrated business units, guiding all research and development as well as new product development efforts.
Over the past 12 years at Honeywell, Mehul has taken on various positions, including Chief Technical Officer and Vice President of Engineering for several Strategic Business Groups, such as Honeywell Connected Enterprise and Honeywell Homes & Buildings Technologies. He has also led Honeywell Technology Solutions (HTS) in India, which focuses on cross-business technology development and engineering.
Mehul is recognized as a thought leader and inventor, having presented technical papers at numerous global conferences hosted by Honeywell, Danaher, Symbol Technologies, and SPIE. He holds over one hundred and twenty U.S. and international patents.

sessions.title: Accelerating the Path to Production: The Evolution of Enterprise Storage to Deliver AI-Ready Data
sessions.abstract: Roughly 70% of AI projects fail to transition to production due to challenges related to data compliance and security. To address this challenge, enterprise storage systems are evolving from data delivery to knowledge and intelligence delivery systems. Join us to learn how NVIDIA is collaborating with leading partners from the industry on architectures that deliver AI-ready data to accelerate the path from proof of concept to production.

speakers.name: Jacob Liberman
speakers.bio: Jacob Liberman is a manager in the Enterprise Product group. His team builds NVIDIA AI blueprints, agentic AI platforms, and open-source connectors to leading generative AI programming framworks. Previously, he held product management and engineering roles at Red Hat, AMD, and Dell.

speakers.name: Jason Hardy
speakers.bio: Jason Hardy is vice president of storage technology at NVIDIA, where he drives storage architecture and ecosystem alignment for accelerated computing and enterprise AI. With more than 20 years of experience spanning enterprise data infrastructure and AI platforms, he has led the development and scaling of high-performance storage systems as well as AI solutions supporting analytics, enterprise, and industrial workloads. Jason works across engineering, product, and industry partners to advance storage architectures designed for scale, performance, and reliability in modern AI environments. Previously, he served as vice president and CTO at Hitachi Vantara, where he led global technology strategy, platform innovation, and AI initiatives across the portfolio.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Accelerate Enterprise AI Adoption: Secure Your Agentic Foundations from Day 1 (Presented by CrowdStrike)
sessions.abstract:
Enterprise AI adoption is accelerating through AI agents embedded in applications and workflows. While these agents drive innovation, they present unique security challenges as organizations move to production. Traditional security approaches weren't built for agent architectures, creating vulnerabilities in agent logic, prompts, model interactions, and infrastructure.

Learn how CrowdStrike is collaborating with NVIDIA to drive secure enterprise AI adoption across the entire AI life cycle, from governing agent behavior to protecting models, and delivering built-in protection for AI factories. By integrating security into agent design and runtime, organizations can confidently scale AI systems while managing risk.

speakers.name: Mike Petronaci
speakers.bio: Mike Petronaci is responsible for CrowdStrike’s technology vision and proactive security strategy, leading innovation across AI, threat intelligence, and IT. Prior to CrowdStrike, he led product and engineering teams at high-growth software-as-a-service, cloud, and cybersecurity companies including Akamai and HubSpot. Most recently, he served as the vice president of products for SentinelOne, where he led teams responsible for delivering innovative customer-led products.

sessions.topic: Trustworthy AI / Cybersecurity - Security for AI

sessions.title: Driving Factory Autonomy With Digital Twins: Hyundai’s Virtual Commissioning Journey
sessions.abstract:
Hyundai Motor Group is advancing factory autonomy by leveraging NVIDIA Omniverse to build scalable, high-fidelity digital twins across its manufacturing operations.

In this session, we share Hyundai’s real-world journey of implementing Omniverse-powered factory digital twins, with a focus on virtual commissioning as a critical enabler for reducing deployment risk, accelerating system integration, and improving factory startup efficiency. Gain insights into how Hyundai virtualizes diverse manufacturing workloads—ranging from robotics and automation systems to production line logic—within a shared, collaborative digital environment. We will also highlight how cross-functional collaboration between engineering, IT, and operations teams is transformed through a unified digital twin platform, enabling faster decision-making and continuous innovation.

speakers.name: Jong-ho Shin
speakers.bio:
Shin Jong-ho is a Senior Manager at the E-FOREST Center within Hyundai Motor Company’s Manufacturing Solution Division, where he leads the deployment of digital twin–based smart factory initiatives.

He began his professional career in 2011 in the mobile and arcade game development industry. Since joining Hyundai Motor Company in 2015, he has spent the past decade developing core manufacturing technologies, including drive motor production processes.

Leveraging his background in game engine technologies, he currently spearheads NVIDIA Omniverse–based digital twin projects across key manufacturing sites, including the Ulsan Engine Plant and the Gwangmyeong EVO Plant, driving innovation in next-generation smart manufacturing.

speakers.name: Banseok Kim
speakers.bio: Kim Banseok is a senior manager at Hyundai Motor Co.’s Electrification Manufacturing Technology Center, where he leads initiatives focused on smart factory transformation for powertrain and electrification plants, as well as the strategic adoption of digital twin technologies. He joined Hyundai in 2014 as an engine production technology engineer, beginning his career by managing engine machining lines. He later spent six years overseeing manufacturing execution systems and quality systems across Hyundai and Kia manufacturing sites worldwide, contributing significantly to the advancement of manufacturing efficiency and quality performance.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Industrial Digitalization / Digital Twin

sessions.title: Standardize Gen AI Service Evaluation: An API-Centric Benchmarking Approach
sessions.abstract: Evaluating Gen AI services across heterogeneous environments presents significant visibility gaps for developers, as traditional hardware-centric benchmarks often fail to reflect production-level API performance and accuracy. This session introduces MLPerf Endpoints, an open-source framework by MLCommons designed to address these challenges through transparent, reproducible, and vendor-neutral benchmarking. By shifting the evaluative focus to the API layer, this methodology enables developers to conduct iterative, on-demand measurements that mirror real-world user experiences and infrastructure optimizations. You'll analyze early cross-provider results and explore visualization tools designed to drive data-driven decisions in AI procurement and system design.

speakers.name: David Kanter
speakers.bio: As the co-founder of MLCommons, David Kanter led the MLPerf benchmarks and other initiatives. He founded a microprocessor and compiler startup, was an early employee at Aster Data Systems, and has consulted for industry leaders such as Intel, NVIDIA, KLA, Applied Materials, Qualcomm, and Microsoft, among others. David holds a B.S. in mathematics with a specialization in computer science, and a B.A. in economics from the University of Chicago.

sessions.title: Australia’s Radical Shift in AI Infrastructure: Project Southgate (Presented by Firmus Technologies)
sessions.abstract:
This session introduces Project Southgate, a sovereign network of AI factories across Australia that rethinks the fundamentals of how intelligence is produced. Each site in the Southgate network is purpose-built for high-density GPU systems, using direct-to-chip liquid cooling and modular architecture to reduce energy and water use while maintaining throughput at scale. With a flagship AI Factory campus in the state of Tasmania, powered by firmed renewables.

Through a technical lens, we’ll explore how infrastructure design choices—from thermal systems to site integration with the grid—can shape the next decade of AI development. Join this session to learn how Australia’s green AI factory network is redefining what AI infrastructure can be—and why energy-aligned, token-efficient design is the shift the industry needs.

speakers.name: Daniel Kearney
speakers.bio: Daniel Kearney is the chief technology officer at Firmus in Singapore, overseeing design and development of next-gen AI infrastructure. He began his cloud computing career at AWS in 2018, later leading Enterprise Solutions Architecture in Singapore. Prior to AWS, he was a senior research scientist at ABB specializing in thermal management. Daniel holds a B.E., Ph.D., and M.B.A., and is a fellow of the the American Society of Mechanical Engineers and chartered engineer with Engineers Ireland.

sessions.title: 如何迈过具身智能的 ChatGPT 时刻
sessions.abstract: 介绍宇树科技近期在具身智能方面的关键技术进展，分享当前观察到的具身智能在任务泛化、数据效率与强化学习规模效应方面面临的核心瓶颈，并针对性地提出解决上述问题的一些思考。

speakers.name: 兴兴 王
speakers.bio:
王兴兴，宇树科技（Unitree）创始人/CEO/CTO。在硕士研究生期间，全球范围内，个人创先研发了使用低成本外转子无刷电机驱动的高性能四足机器人 XDog，开创了全球低成本高性能足式机器人的技术先河。
随后 2016 年 8 月创立了宇树科技（Unitree），在全球率先公开零售高性能四足机器人，四足机器人及人形机器人全球销量历年领先，显著推动了全球高性能四足机器人和人形机器人的商业化进程。累计提交国内外专利申请 200 余项。获得《时代》周刊“全球 100 位最具影响力 AI 人物”。

sessions.title: LLM 如何赋能量化投资
sessions.abstract:
LLM 作为 AI 技术的核心分支，正在重塑量化投资范式。LLM 具有超乎寻常的语义理解与推理能力，可以作为强大的工具模型嵌入量化投资流程。
从 LLM 的输出随机性出发，我们可以依赖 LLM 进行因子挖掘，并基于 NVIDIA NIM 和 TensorRT-LLM 加速挖掘；
从 LLM 的语义理解能力出发，我们基于 GPU 本地部署LLM解读海量文本、提取情感信息，构建或优化量化选股策略；
从 LLM 的多模态思维链出发，我们可利用 LLM 对K线图像进行交互式技术分析。LLM 正在引发一场量化投资技术更迭的革命......

speakers.name: 康 何
speakers.bio: 北京大学心理学博士、学士。2018年入职华泰证券。主要从事量化策略和金融产品研究。《中国量化投资白皮书》联合发起人。

speakers.name: 洋 沈
speakers.bio: 2023年加入华泰证券，主要从事量化策略与大模型研究。

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Large Language Models (LLMs)

sessions.title: PAI 集成 NVIDIA Physical AI：驱动数据合成、模型训练测评一体化 PAI Integrates NVIDIA Physical AI: Power E2E SDG, Model Training & Evaluation
sessions.abstract: 自 2025 年，人工智能平台 PAI 融合 NVIDIA Physical AI 领域的多项技术，服务中国及全球的具身智能、自动驾驶及 AGI 大模型团队。此分享将详细介绍 PAI 的 AI 云服务能力和 NVIDIA Physical AI 如何紧密协同，依托 PAI 的高性能计算资源、分布式计算服务以及开箱即用的开发平台，解决数据合成、模型训练、模型测评的实际问题。 同时还将隆重推出 2026 年 PAI & NVIDIA Physical AI 带来的最新升级能力。Since 2025, PAI (Platform for AI) has integrated advanced NVIDIA Physical AI technologies to empower Embodied AI, Autonomous Driving, and AGI teams worldwide. This session provides a technical deep-dive into the seamless synergy between PAI’s cloud-native AI infrastructure and NVIDIA Physical AI, strategically leveraging PAI’s advantages in high-performance computing resource, distributed computing service, and easy-to-use development platform to resolve critical bottlenecks in Synthetic Data Generation (SDG), model training, and performance benchmarking. Furthermore, we will unveil the 2026 roadmap and the latest full-stack feature upgrades.

speakers.name: Boyuan Huang
speakers.bio: Boyuan Huang is the product director of the Big Data and AI platform at Alibaba Cloud. He joined Alibaba Group in 2014, taking charge of engineering in Alimama (online advertisement) and the Commercial Search team. Beginning in 2018, he worked as the product owner of Platform for AI (PAI), DataWorks, and the Search platform in Alibaba Cloud. Before joining Alibaba, he was in Microsoft's Search Technology Center, where he worked as a senior developer lead in search ads and display ads, beginning in 2007.

sessions.title: 面向大规模 Agent RL 的高并发沙箱系统构建与实践
sessions.abstract: 随着 Agent 从对话式演进为能够与真实世界交互的自主智能体，Agent 强化学习（AgentRL）逐渐成为核心训练范式。与传统 RL 或监督学习不同，AgentRL 对基础设施提出了前更高要求和挑战，包括：超大规模的环境并发、环境的快速启动与销毁、强隔离安全要求，以及 GPU&CPU 的高效利用。 本次分享将聚焦于支撑大规模 AgentRL 的基础设施设计与工程实践，我们将从系统工程视角，拆解 AgentRL 背后的关键 Infra 挑战，并分享可复用的架构模式与设计原则。 内容将覆盖 AgentRL 任务编排、基于NVIDIA Dynamo的模型部署、环境生命周期管理、执行隔离机制以及多层次加速技术，重点介绍如何将环境启动延迟从分钟级压缩到毫秒级，如何支撑数十万级并发 Agent 沙箱环境运行，以及如何提高资源利用。

speakers.name: 超凡 王
speakers.bio: 关注云原生、Agent Infra等领域。负责腾讯云Agent Sandbox产品研发，重点关注沙箱安全、性能、成本等优化方向。同时负责TKE大规模K8s 集群治理及etcd 集群治理、性能优化、安全保障、资源利用率提升等工作，保障了全网数万K8s集群和数千万核心计算资源的稳定运行，支撑了腾讯全面云原生上云战略，服务了众多内外部客户，在云原生和Agent Infra领域有丰富的实践。

speakers.name: 志宇 李
speakers.bio: 关注云原生、AI infra和多模态数据领域。负责腾讯云 Agentic RL 平台建设，重点关注大规模模型、镜像分发加速。同时负责TKE大规模节点管理、在离线混部、资源利用率提升等工作，在云原生、容器运行时、性能等领域有丰富经验。

sessions.title: ROLL：集成 NVIDIA Megatron、高效易用的大规模 LLM 强化学习框架 An Efficient and User-Friendly Large-Scale RL Framework Integrating Megatron
sessions.abstract: ROLL 是专为 LLM 设计的高性能分布式强化学习库，旨在兼顾大规模集群处理千亿参数 LLM 的线性扩展、系统容错和高利用率。它深度集成 Megatron-LM 实现强化学习工作流与分布式训练原语无缝互操作，结合 Megatron-Core、SGLang 及 vLLM 构建端到端训推加速链路。ROLL 在偏好对齐、复杂推理及多轮智能体交互等场景显著优化了大规模集群性能。此外，ROLL 实现了千亿规模 MoE 训练在数千卡 GPU 节点的拓展，支持长达两周的生产级无中断运行，展现了卓越的可扩展性和容错能力。ROLL is a high-performance RL library designed for LLMs to balance scalability, fault tolerance, and high utilization for trillion-parameter models on large-scale clusters. It integrates Megatron-LM to achieve seamless interoperability between RL and distributed training primitives, it leverages M-Core, SGLang, vLLM for an E2E training and inference pipeline. In scenarios including preference alignment, complex reasoning, multi-turn agent interactions, ROLL significantly optimizes cluster utilization and throughput. Notably, ROLL scales trillion-parameter MoE training to thousand GPU nodes with production-grade, uninterrupted operation over two weeks.

speakers.name: Siran Yang
speakers.bio:
目前是平台技术智能引擎事业部大模型训练团队负责人。自2017年开始在阿里从事深度学习训练系统的开发，工作包括图学习系统 Euler、稀疏模型训练系统 XDL、高性能稀疏模型训练、大语言模型训练系统等。
Currently serving as the Head of the Large Model Training Team at the Platform Technology Intelligent Engine Division, Siran joined Alibaba in 2017, focusing on the development of deep learning training systems. His core expertise and projects include Euler (a graph learning system), XDL (a sparse model training system), high-performance sparse training, and large language model training systems.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Ozaki Scheme: Addressing the Accuracy Challenge in the Era of Low-Precision Accelerators
sessions.abstract:
This talk explores the Ozaki Scheme, including its two variants, as a practical approach for recovering high accuracy from low-precision computations in numerical linear algebra.
Participation is important as it sheds light on how we can effectively respond to the accuracy challenges posed by next-generation accelerator architectures.

speakers.name: Katsuhisa Ozaki
speakers.bio: Katsuhisa Ozaki is a Professor in the Department of Mathematical Sciences at Shibaura Institute of Technology, Japan. He received his Ph.D. in Engineering from Waseda University in 2007. His research interests include floating-point arithmetic, numerical linear algebra, and verified numerical computation, particularly fast and reliable algorithms for matrix multiplication and its applications to numerical linear algebra.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries

sessions.title: Revolutionize Warehouse and Shipping Operations Through AI Optimization (Presented by EY)
sessions.abstract: Discover how to transform warehouse and shipping operations in collaboration with EY using NVIDIA’s advanced AI-driven optimization technologies. This session will explore how to reboot operational designs toward achieving measurable improvements in efficiency, cost, cashflow, and safety through an innovative solution powered by NVIDIA CuOpt. Get insights into the strategic objectives and scalable deployment model, and how this approach drives rapid innovation across global logistics operations.

speakers.name: Anand Ponnuswamy
speakers.bio: Anand is an established supply chain leader distinguished by the Dayton Business Journal for the leadership and community involvement. He has strong expertise in various verticals including consumer packaged goods, telecommunications, manufacturing, and automotive. He has successfully designed and implemented tail-end spend management programs through aggregator and managed service provider programs for various customers.

sessions.title: Enable Seamless Human-Humanoid Collaboration in a Shared Operational Environment (Presented by EY)
sessions.abstract: Learn how Fincantieri, one of the world’s largest shipbuilders, designed and implemented humanoid robots engineered to perform complex tasks in dangerous shipyard environments characterized by high variability and stringent safety requirements. Leveraging NVIDIA’s simulation and AI orchestration solutions, EY helped Fincantieri accelerate workflow automation and reduce manual interventions. Dive into Fincantieri’s strategic vision and understand the challenges for integrating humanoids into an industrial environment.

speakers.name: Giuseppe Perrone
speakers.bio: Giuseppe leads EY AI & Data and the Innovation Consulting practice in Italy. He focuses on business transformation capabilities through emerging technologies, as well as driving the EY engineering solution initiatives.

speakers.name: Paolo Cerioli
speakers.bio:
Paolo is responsible for defining the short- and long-term innovation strategy and plan, guiding and managing the project portfolio, as well as building and developing innovation ecosystems to shape and accelerate the path of innovation and digital transformation, leveraging synergies between open innovation, infrastructure development, technological applications, and new technologies, including artificial intelligence.

Prior to joining Fincantieri, Paolo was Head of Open Innovation & Ecosystems Development for Eni Group, furthermore he spent more than ten years in The Boston Consulting Group (BCG).

Paolo graduated with a Master in Mechanical Engineering and he holds an MBA.

sessions.title: Driving National Growth Through Sovereign AI Investment
sessions.abstract:
Around the world, nations are accelerating their efforts to build AI capabilities that drive long‑term economic competitiveness. In this fireside chat, Marcus Wallenberg—a global business leader with deep experience in innovative models—joins NVIDIA to explore how countries can design, fund, and operate AI infrastructure as a strategic national asset.

Together, the conversation will unpack how sovereign AI platforms function as economic backbones, the policy frameworks that unlock large‑scale private capital, and the practical playbooks leaders can use to establish durable AI ecosystems. Drawing on real‑world examples across finance, industry, and national innovation systems, the session will provide actionable guidance for governments, investors, and organizations seeking to accelerate AI adoption and national productivity.

speakers.name: David Hogan
speakers.bio: David is a vice president and senior AI business leader driving the AI, deep learning, and generative AI revolution, with significant international experience and particular expertise in establishing partnerships and nonstandard business models. David also advises national governments and industry on the adoption of artificial intelligence.

speakers.name: Marcus Wallenberg
speakers.bio: Marcus Wallenberg is chair of the Board of Directors of Skandinaviska Enskilda Banken (SEB), a North European financial group for 400,000 corporate customers and 4 million private customers. In Sweden and the Baltic, SEB offers universal financial services, and in Denmark, Finland, Norway, Germany, and U.K. the bank focuses on corporate and investment banking. Wallenberg also chairs the board of Saab AB, Wallenberg Investments AB, FAM AB, Patricia Industries, and The Royal Swedish Academy of Engineering Sciences. He also serves on the boards of Investor AB, AstraZeneca Plc, EQT AB, and the Knut and Alice Wallenberg Foundation.

sessions.title: Magic Attention: A Composable Framework for Exploring Warp-Specialized Attention on Blackwell
sessions.abstract: Optimizing attention on NVIDIA Blackwell demands more than a monolithic kernel to handle diverse models, training, inference, and RL workloads. We introduce Magic Attention, a high-performance, composable framework built on CUTLASS/CuTe that navigates the vast scheduling design space of the FlashAttention algorithm on the NVIDIA Blackwell architecture. We discuss four different forward attention kernel schedules, leveraging two Tensor Memory (TMEM) partitionings for deep pipeline overlap, and convolution-inspired runtime tensor packing. We will also discuss the use of softmax emulation and correction skipping.

speakers.name: Manish Gupta
speakers.bio: Manish Gupta is a member of technical staff at Magic, specializing in AI training, inference, and GPU performance. With a background including NVIDIA, Google, and Meta, he has been a key early contributor to critical AI infrastructure like CUTLASS and MLIR-based performant GPU kernels. Manish holds a Ph.D. from UC San Diego and is an IIT Roorkee alumnus.

sessions.title: Realizing the Promise of Quantum Computation
sessions.abstract: Quantum computers are fundamentally different than conventional computers. They promise to address certain problems that are practically prohibitive, and even impossible, to solve using today’s supercomputers. The challenge is building one that is large enough to be useful. In this talk, we will provide an overview of contemporary quantum computing at an intuitive level, including the technology, the promise, the hype, the importance of high-performance classical computing in this endeavor, and the challenges ahead associated with realizing useful quantum computers at scale.

speakers.name: William Oliver
speakers.bio: William D. Oliver is the Henry Ellis Warren (1894) Professor of EECS and professor of physics at MIT. He serves as director of the Center for Quantum Engineering, associate director of the Research Laboratory of Electronics, and principal investigator with the Engineering Quantum Systems Group. Will’s research interests include the materials growth, fabrication, design, and measurement of superconducting qubits, as well as the development of cryogenic packaging and control electronics. Will received his Ph.D. in electrical engineering (EE) from Stanford; M.S. in electrical engineering and computer science (EECS) from MIT; and B.S. in EE and B.A. in Japanese from U. Rochester (NY).

sessions.title: Unlock Sovereign AI With Confidential RAG and Secure Inference for Regulated Data
sessions.abstract:
In this session, we showcase a real-world national deployment that enables confidential retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and AI inference on protected data—without exposing those data to cloud operators, infrastructure administrators, model providers, or third-party vendors.

Learn how NVIDIA Confidential Computing, along with the combined technology from OPAQUE’s Confidential AI Platform and the TII, the applied research arm of the Advanced Technology Research Council (ATRC), establishes hardware-backed trusted execution environments that deliver cryptographic guarantees of data confidentiality, integrity, and verifiable governance across the entire AI life cycle. This architecture enables real-time AI inference and analytics directly on encrypted data, unlocking high-value AI use cases while preserving zero-trust security boundaries.

speakers.name: Aaron Fulkerson
speakers.bio: Aaron Fulkerson has built and scaled category-defining enterprise platforms around a simple principle: trust unlocks growth. As founder of MindTouch and later at ServiceNow—where he led the creation and growth of ServiceNow Impact and was part of the leadership team scaling Customer Workflows into a multi-billion-dollar business—his work has centered on turning trust into measurable enterprise outcomes. Today as CEO of OPAQUE, he applies that discipline to the defining constraint of the AI era: moving AI from experimentation to production in environments where risk, governance, and scrutiny are non-negotiable, so organizations can capture AI’s upside without compromising their data, reputation, or future.

sessions.title: Physical AI in Enterprises: What’s Real, What Scales, What’s Next (Presented by Deloitte)
sessions.abstract:
Top leaders from Siemens and Volkswagen come together to explore how physical AI, Siemens’ industrial digitalization portfolio, and digital twins built on OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse libraries are redefining the future of automated virtual commissioning. This executive conversation highlights how physics-accurate digital twins, real-time interoperability, and closed-loop automation are enabling faster engineering cycles, higher production quality, and more resilient manufacturing operations. 

The panel will examine how combining Siemens’ end-to-end automation and life-cycle platforms with real-time simulation, powered by NVIDIA libraries and accelerated AI infrastructure, creates a unified environment where design, controls, and operations continuously learn from each other. Together, Siemens and Volkswagen will share strategic insights on how these technologies are accelerating the shift from simulation to scalable, AI-driven production systems across global manufacturing networks.

speakers.name: Nitin Mittal
speakers.bio: Nitin Mittal is a principal at Deloitte and leads Deloitte’s global AI program. He advises organizations on AI applications and the implications of emerging technologies on the strategy and competitive positioning of businesses. At Deloitte, Mittal is responsible for shaping the AI market and creating new business opportunities by harnessing emerging technologies. He is also leading Deloitte’s own effort to be a global AI-fueled organization and transform how they deliver professional services.

speakers.name: Jochen Fichtner
speakers.bio:
Jochen Fichtner is chief information officer at Volkswagen de México, leading enterprise technology strategy, and delivery for a major automotive manufacturing organization. He previously served as CIO at Audi México and was recognized at the CIO100 Awards (2019) for aligning IT with business objectives and as AI IT Leaders Mexico (2024) . His experience includes building plant IT capabilities from the ground up, covering infrastructure, IT services and processes, governance, application development, and organizational culture. As Creator for innovative solutions he drives the digital transformation realizing benefits for the entire company.

Across roles at Volkswagen Group brands, he has led IT for production, logistics, and engineering in highly operational and strategic environments.

speakers.name: Mark Hindsbo
speakers.bio: Mark Hindsbo joined Siemens in 2025 as Head of Operations Software. He is leading a team to build an integrated and modular industrial operations software suite that allows customers to design, engineer, and operate their factories, data centers, or plants – powered by agentic AI and digital twins. His extensive career includes leadership roles at Ansys, Parallels, and Microsoft, alongside experiences at The Boston Consulting Group, Novo Nordisk, and CERN. Mark is also an Adjunct Professor at Carnegie Mellon University. Born and educated in Denmark, he holds an M.Sc. in Applied Physics & Mathematics from the Technical University of Denmark.

sessions.title: AI-Powered Huge Ensembles for Portfolio Climate Risk Management
sessions.abstract: Discover how large‑scale AI‑accelerated ensemble forecasting is reshaping the way we assess physical climate risk in financial portfolios. This session dives into practical, data‑driven methods for quantifying asset‑level climate exposure—bridging advanced climate modeling with real‑world financial and energy use cases. You’ll learn how massive ensembles help uncover cross‑dependencies among temperature, wind, and solar patterns that drive volatility in energy commodities, grid stability, and portfolio performance under extreme conditions.

speakers.name: Duncan Anderson
speakers.bio:
Duncan Anderson is a research director at S&P Global Energy, leading a team specializing in probabilistic simulation architectures for climate, weather, energy systems, and agricultural commodities. From a background in power market and locational marginal pricing forecasting, his expertise extended to probabilistic weather simulation and integrating weather and climate uncertainty into agricultural yield forecasts and asset risk. 
Duncan holds a B.S. in applied economics from Cornell University and an M.S. in industrial engineering and operations research from the University of Texas Austin. His research focused on probabilistic simulation of physical infrastructure systems.

speakers.name: Georg Ertl
speakers.bio: Georg Ertl works as a solutions architect on the NVIDIA Earth-2 project. He holds degrees in meteorology from the University of Hamburg and in industrial engineering from the Karlsruhe Institute of Technology. He's worked for a commodity analytics business at the intersection of weather, data science, and its applications to energy markets. Georg enjoys helping clients make use of the powerful suite of Earth-2 tools.

speakers.name: Danielle Montagne
speakers.bio: Danielle Montagne is a data and climate scientist specializing in physical climate risk modeling and probabilistic portfolio risk analytics at S&P Global Energy. Her work focuses on developing climate-driven hazard models and translating them into scalable risk analytics, leveraging high-performance and accelerated computing outputs. She holds a Ph.D. in sociology from Duke University, bringing an interdisciplinary perspective to climate science, data, and risk assessment.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Climate / Weather / Ocean Modeling

sessions.title: Teach AI to Code in Every Language With NVIDIA NeMo
sessions.abstract:
Large language models for code are transforming how developers create, maintain, and understand software—but building these models from the ground up requires the right tools and know-how. In this talk, we’ll explore how to leverage the NVIDIA NeMo framework and NVIDIA’s accelerated computing infrastructure to train state-of-the-art (SOTA) coding LLMs capable of handling multiple programming languages.

Gain practical insights into dataset preparation, multilingual training challenges, and effective strategies for balancing diverse code sources. We’ll cover workflow design for large-scale distributed training, discussing optimization techniques and best practices for scaling across GPU clusters. The session will also highlight real-world applications of multilingual coding LLMs—from powering intelligent developer assistants to managing complex enterprise codebases.

speakers.name: Miguel Martinez
speakers.bio: Miguel Martínez is a senior applied deep learning researcher at NVIDIA, where he focuses on training large language models as part of the ADLR team. He specializes in generative AI and machine learning, with deep expertise in GPU-accelerated, end-to-end data science workflows. Previously, he mentored students in Udacity’s Artificial Intelligence Nanodegree program, helping practitioners build practical skills in modern AI.

speakers.name: Meriem Bendris
speakers.bio: Meriem is a deep learning solutions architect manager, leading a team that helps European, Middle Eastern, and African partners build neural networks for speech, natural language processing, and computer vision. Her areas of expertise include conversational AI and multi-modality. Meriem holds a Ph.D. in signal and image processing from Telecom ParisTech, where she focused on machine learning applied to speech and computer vision.

speakers.name: Oleg Sudakov
speakers.bio: Oleg Sudakov concentrates on LLM training and conversational AI. Previously, he worked as a machine learning engineer and data scientist at Apple, Huawei, and Rakuten. He has a strong background in natural language processing and speech processing. Oleg is based in Germany.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: 基于多模态数据湖的新一代人工智能应用：NVIDIA 工具链落地实践的深度洞察
sessions.abstract: 多模态数据湖已成为新一代人工智能开发的核心基石，能够助力企业高效处理各类数据类型，为生成式人工智能流水线提供数据支撑。本次分享将深入讲解构建可扩展、GPU 加速型多模态数据湖的实操策略 —— 重点聚焦性能提升与工作流的无缝集成两大核心。我们将探讨 NVIDIA 工具链（包括 Nemo Curator、PyNvVideoCodec、CVCUDA）如何简化企业级数据处理与数据治理流程，同时解析 GPU 解决方案如何与基于 Ray Data 和 Daft 的工作流协同融合，降低从 CPU 到 GPU 的迁移难度。此外，我们还将分享真实的行业落地案例，拆解自动驾驶、机器人、AI 生成视频（AIGV） / VLM等不同业务场景的个性化需求，并重点解读面向定制化流水线设计的消融实验结果。

speakers.name: 彦辉 王
speakers.bio: 王彦辉先生现任火山引擎产品总监，拥有逾 10 年数据分析领域产品从业经验，主导火山引擎多模态数据湖产品体系从 0 到 1 的搭建工作。其核心工作为基于企业级数据基础设施，打造适配人工智能时代的多模态数据处理解决方案，覆盖模型微调、自动驾驶、游戏开发等多元业务场景，相关方案已服务各行业众多头部客户。

sessions.title: Inside Pixar’s RenderMan: XPU Technology and Storytelling on Toy Story 5 (Presented by Dell)
sessions.abstract: As one of the world’s most influential animation studios, Pixar has long pushed the boundaries of storytelling through technology. RenderMan has powered Pixar’s films since before Toy Story, evolving with artists, stories and hardware. That evolution continues with Toy Story 5 and the rollout of RenderMan XPU, Pixar’s hybrid CPU+GPU designed for modern production workflows. This session explores how XPU transforms look development, lighting, and final-frame rendering for artists—treating rendering not just as compute, but as an active part of storytelling through faster iteration, visual exploration, and immediate feedback. The talk also highlights Pixar’s Machine Learning Denoiser—used on every Pixar film since Toy Story 4—as a predictable, reliable artist-facing ML tool that accelerates rendering without sacrificing creative control. The session includes live demos of RenderMan XPU running on Dell Precision workstations with NVIDIA GPUs, showing how local GPU power supports performance and creative flow. Attendees will also see the Toy Story 5 trailer rendered entirely with XPU. And don’t miss the end‑of‑session giveaway—one attendee will walk away with a Dell Precision mobile workstation. Must be in the room to win.

speakers.name: Logan Lawler
speakers.bio: Logan Lawler has worked in various roles at Dell for 16 years, including sales, marketing, merchandising, services, and ecommerce. Before joining Dell, Logan grew up in Missouri and graduated from the University of Missouri (MIZ!). Logan previously owned and invested in several business ventures as an entrepreneur between 2013-19.  Logan lives in Round Rock with his wife Ally, daughter Calloway, and labradoodle Truman.

speakers.name: Dylan Sisson
speakers.bio:
Dylan Sisson is a digital artist and VFX veteran who helps make advanced 3D tools feel intuitive and usable for artists. For more than 30 years he’s worked in animation and visual effects, focusing on demystifying how modern rendering works and showing how to apply the technology creatively in real production.
Before joining Pixar in 1999, Dylan worked in comics, games, and independent animation, including directing an award-winning short film. At Pixar, he’s been closely involved with RenderMan on every feature film since Monsters, Inc., working at the intersection of art, technology, and education to support artists and studios around the world.
Outside the studio, Dylan experiments with stylized rendering, VR sculpting, and other evolving workflows. He also created Pixar’s RenderMan Walking Teapot, which has since become an industry icon.
He lives in San Francisco and still doodles monsters whenever he can.

sessions.title: Vertically Integrated AI Cloud: Accelerating AI Development (Presented by IREN)
sessions.abstract:
