As AI workloads scale, infrastructure complexity increases — across power, cooling, networking, and compute. For customers deploying large GPU clusters, the question is no longer just about access to hardware, but about how reliably and predictably that infrastructure can be delivered and operated.
This session examines the role of vertical integration in building AI cloud environments at scale. IREN develops its own sites, secures grid interconnections, engineers high-voltage substations, designs and constructs data centers, and deploys and operates its own GPU clusters — all within a single operating platform.

By controlling the full stack — from power infrastructure through to compute — the platform reduces dependency on third parties, shortens deployment timelines, and provides tighter alignment between facility design and cluster architecture.

speakers.name: Kent Draper
speakers.bio: Kent has 19+ years’ experience in investment banking and financing in infrastructure, power & renewables. Kent was most recently Vice President at First Solar, leading ~$8bn+ of investments and financings in the US and Asia Pacific.

sessions.title: SeamlessFlow：NVIDIA GPU 集群上基于 Megatron-Core 的 Trainer-Agent 隔离与标签调度无空泡强化学习框架
sessions.abstract: 探索基于 Megatron-Core 构建的 SeamlessFlow 可扩展 RL框架，了解如何通过 Trainer-Agent 隔离和标签驱动调度在 NVIDIA GPU 集群上消除流水线气泡。我们将展示如何利用时空复用技术最大化资源利用率，实现相比 VERL 提升 100% 的吞吐量并将训练时间缩短 62%。

speakers.name: Yinghan Cui
speakers.bio: 崔英翰是快手科技的资深 AI Infra专家，深度参与了快手大语言模型的Reinforcement Learning 和Post-training，以及Infrastructure搭建工作。作为 KAT Coder Pro 和 KAT Coder Air 等系列模型 RL基础设施的核心贡献者，他助力 KAT 在 2025 年 12 月的Artificial Analysis 评测中取得全球第九名的成绩。他是SeamlessFlow 的核心架构师与主要开发者，该系统作为支撑 KAT的高性能底层架构，成功实现了工业级 AI 的训练解耦与资源调度优化。他拥有加州大学洛杉矶分校（UCLA）电气与计算机工程硕士学位及上海交通大学学士学位。

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: 规模化架构：大语言模型持续学习的探索与实践 Architectures of Scale: Navigate the Continual Learning in Large Language Models
sessions.abstract: 当前的机器学习模型，尤其是大语言模型（LLMs），其知识在很大程度上被 “封存” 在两个地方：一是在预训练阶段习得的静态信息，二是由输入窗口提供的瞬时上下文。一旦训练终止或上下文消失，这些知识便会固定下来。最直接的解决方案似乎是 “持续学习”—— 利用新数据不断更新模型参数。然而，这种方法往往会带来巨大的计算开销，并引发灾难性遗忘问题。本次分享将从架构的角度，对大语言模型中的持续学习进行全面的介绍。Current machine learning models, especially LLMs, have their knowledge largely "sealed" in two places: the static information they learned during the pre-training phase, and the transient context provided by the input window. Once training stops or the context disappears, the knowledge becomes fixed. The most straightforward solution seems to be "continual learning"—continuously updating model parameters with new data. But this approach often leads to huge computation and catastrophic forgetting. This session will give a comprehensive introduction to continual learning in LLMs from the perspective of architecture.

speakers.name: xue chao
speakers.bio:
薛超是京东科技副总裁，目前在京东担任系统与基础模型部负责人，也是京东言犀大模型（JoyAI-LLM）的执行负责人。他曾任职于 IBM 研究院长达十年，担任 IBM 中国研究院首席研究员，同时也是IBM 中国研究院神经网络搜索领域的全球联合负责人。
薛超主导并参与了国内外多家大型企业云计算平台上各类大数据服务与自动化机器学习服务的研发与落地工作，近期的研究方向聚焦于大模型训练与推理系统。他在国际顶级学术会议及期刊上发表多篇高水平论文，并拥有30 余项已授权美国专利。

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Transforming Retail Commerce and Supply Chain Operations With AI
sessions.abstract:
Retail and consumer packaged goods (CPG) companies are uniquely positioned to use AI to drive revenue growth and margin expansion. Leading retailers around the world are partnering with NVIDIA to deploy AI at enterprise scale. NVIDIA’s VP and General Manager of Retail and CPG, Azita Martin, will share how retailers can build an end-to-end, CEO-sponsored AI strategy and infrastructure that avoids siloed initiatives and delivers measurable return on investment.

Discover how agentic AI and physical AI are transforming supply chains and store operations through simulation, robotics, and computer vision, while Agentic Commerce, AI Blueprints, and data science libraries unlock new opportunities in ecommerce and retail media. Learn best practices for deploying AI at scale with interoperability across any cloud and with full ownership of models trained on proprietary retail data.

speakers.name: Azita Martin
speakers.bio: Azita leads NVIDIA's AI initiatives for the retail and consumer package goods (CPG) industries, advising retail and CPG executives on their AI strategy and best practices of leveraging AI, physical AI, and agentic AI to become a highly agile and AI-first company. Azita’s team develops NVIDIA's retail/CPG solution stack, leveraging NVIDIA’s Accelerated computing platform, and builds the ecosystem of software partners for intelligent supply chain, intelligent stores, and ecommerce.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Building Liquid-Cooled AI Cloud Gigafactories (Presented by Lenovo)
sessions.abstract: AI cloud providers are under pressure to deploy next‑generation AI infrastructure faster, more efficiently, and at multi‑gigawatt scale. In this session, Lenovo introduces the AI Cloud Gigafactory program, co-developed with NVIDIA to industrialize hyperscale AI cloud buildouts. See how this framework, with Lenovo's global manufacturing, integrated solutions and services, enables rapid, energy-efficient deployment of NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra (GB300) systems and provides a seamless path to the next-generation Vera Rubin architecture. Leveraging real-world AI cloud deployments with NScale, this session demonstrates how Lenovo’s Neptune liquid cooling has evolved from niche HPC roots into a scalable foundation for multi-gigawatt AI clouds, utilizing global manufacturing and end-to-end services to accelerate time-to-first-token and de-risk the industry’s shift to liquid-cooled infrastructure.

speakers.name: Binoy Unnikrishnan
speakers.bio:
Binoy is a strategic global sales executive who's been building sales organizations across semiconductor, cloud, and client markets for over 20 years. As vice president of worldwide cloud sales, he leads Lenovo's global cloud sales organization and go-to-market strategy, driving growth and deepening engagement with hyperscale and neo‑cloud customers worldwide.

Binoy holds an M.B.A. and M.S. in electrical and computer engineering from the University of Washington and a B.S. in electronics engineering from the University of Bombay. With his experience, deep technical expertise, and customer‑first mindset, he's key to accelerating Lenovo’s AI cloud business and expanding its global footprint.

speakers.name: Sagar Rawal
speakers.bio: Former Head of Infrastructure at Microsoft, Sagar brings a wealth of leadership experience to his role as Nscale’s Chief Infrastructure Officer. Prior to Microsoft, he led compute product management at OCI, where he focused on delivering flexible, scalable, and highly performant infrastructure. He previously held senior roles at NVIDIA and AWS.

speakers.name: Greg Lewis
speakers.bio:
Greg Lewis serves as Global Senior Director of AI Factory Partner Development at NVIDIA, where he leads strategic collaboration with global OEM, GSI, and storage partners to advance the integration of NVIDIA’s AI Factory platforms, GPU architectures, and AI infrastructure into enterprise product portfolios. He also oversees the Equinix Private AI Data Center program for NVIDIA, aligning technical engagement and partner strategy to accelerate enterprise AI adoption worldwide.
Throughout his career, Greg has built extensive expertise in business‑to‑business sales, strategic alliances, and partner ecosystem development. At NVIDIA, he is responsible for defining reference architectures and validation frameworks that streamline the deployment of AI‑optimized servers and infrastructure, enabling partners to deliver high‑performance, enterprise‑grade AI solutions.
Greg’s leadership emphasizes technical excellence, cross‑functional collaboration, and customer‑centric innovation. His work continues to strengthen NVIDIA’s position at the forefront of global AI infrastructure, empowering organizations to harness the full potential of advanced computing and AI technologies.

speakers.name: Vinod Kamath
speakers.bio:
Dr. Vinod Kamath leads the platform and systems design architecture for efficient cooling of systems and data centers at Lenovo.

With over 60 patents in systems cooling technologies and data centers he has pioneered cooling innovation since 1991 in x86 and accelerated computing systems. These innovations are at the core of Lenovo’s Neptune cooling designs deployed broadly in the HPC data centers globally.

He has authored a book on “Energy Efficient Computing and Data Centers” in 2019 and has over 15 published papers. He leads the innovations at Lenovo on systems cooling and pioneered efficient warm water-based AI and HPC cooling solutions targeted at 45 deg C inlet water operational in solutions for more than a decade.

sessions.title: Reinvent Omnichannel Retail With Physical AI: Connect Stores From Edge to Cloud
sessions.abstract: Retail is no longer a choice between online and in-store. Success now depends on physical and digital experiences operating as one. Instacart is reinventing retail with AI by connecting stores, shelves, customers, and digital experiences, from edge to cloud. This session explores how Instacart applies AI across the retail stack to build real-time understanding of physical stores—one of grocery's most valuable and least digitized assets. Instacart offers enterprise solutions designed to democratize AI for grocers of all sizes, including Kroger and Sprouts. Powered by NVIDIA Jetson at the edge and NVIDIA Dynamo-Triton in the cloud, signals are processed on GPUs to continuously build and update a real-time digital model of in-store reality and inventory.

speakers.name: Azita Martin
speakers.bio: Azita leads NVIDIA's AI initiatives for the retail and consumer package goods (CPG) industries, advising retail and CPG executives on their AI strategy and best practices of leveraging AI, physical AI, and agentic AI to become a highly agile and AI-first company. Azita’s team develops NVIDIA's retail/CPG solution stack, leveraging NVIDIA’s Accelerated computing platform, and builds the ecosystem of software partners for intelligent supply chain, intelligent stores, and ecommerce.

speakers.name: David McIntosh
speakers.bio: As the chief connected stores officer, David leads Instacart’s in-store offerings including Caper, an AI-powered smart-cart, enabling retailers to deliver seamless omnichannel retail experiences online and in-store. Before joining Instacart in 2021, David co-founded and was the CEO of Tenor, a GIF and expression search engine integrated into WhatsApp, Discord, and iMessage, which Google acquired in 2018. At Google, David grew Tenor to over 1 billion users and more than 1 billion queries per day. David was previously named Forbes 30 under 30 in consumer technology. He attended the University of California at Berkeley and studied electrical engineering and computer sciences.

sessions.title: Keeping Pace with F1: How AI Technology Drives Performance at Speed (Presented by Lenovo)
sessions.abstract: Few environments have greater demand for rapid, relevant, and reliable operations than Formula One. In this session, you’ll get privileged visibility into how AI-ready hardware is powering high-performance workloads across the uniquely distributed, time-critical operations of Formula One racing. The session will also cover best practices for optimizing inferencing across enterprise environments, based on Lenovo AI inferencing infrastructure, workstations, and services and real-world AI use case deployments with NVIDIA.

speakers.name: Chris Roberts
speakers.bio:
Chris has been with F1 for over 20 years, during which he has influenced the sport's technological and business landscapes globally. As the director of IT, he plans and leads complex projects that transform digital workplaces into streamlined operations within robust infrastructures. Notably, he spearheaded F1's transition to cloud-based technologies.

Recently, Chris has collaborated with technical partners to enhance fan experiences. He has also led large, multidisciplinary teams. His expertise in enterprise architecture has addressed disruptive forces, promoting sustainability and minimizing operational risks, particularly in cybersecurity and protecting sensitive data and systems.

speakers.name: Art Hu
speakers.bio:
As Lenovo’s Global CIO, Art leads the enterprise-wide IT organization that provides information services, manages critical operational systems, and drives technology-enabled transformation for Lenovo. With his vision of IT as a strategic partner to the business, he has delivered capabilities that enabled Lenovo to grow from a PC-led company into a global technology solutions leader.

The transformation includes building a global technology platform, powering Lenovo's fast-growing businesses, including eCommerce and gaming, as well as the new “as a Service” business models that offer customers increased choice and outcome-based delivery.

In addition to his role as Global CIO, Art was appointed as Chief Technology and Delivery Officer (CTDO) of SSG in February 2023. As CTDO, Art leads a new organization that brings together the global IT, research and development, services support, and delivery functions to advance how Lenovo brings its innovative solutions to customers worldwide.

In 2023, Art was named one of the CIOs to watch on the Forbes CIO Next List and has since guided his team to multiple CIO 100 Awards, which celebrate organizations and their IT teams for driving innovation, transformation, and business value through technology. His visionary leadership and strategic excellence have also earned him the CIO of the Year (AI Engineering) – Global 2025 award from C-Suite Insider, as well as the Outstanding CIO Award for 2024 at the Enterprise Intelligent Transformation Summit in Beijing.

Prior to Lenovo, Art was with McKinsey & Company, focused on strategy and technology management. He holds a Bachelor of Science and a Master of Science degree in computer science from Stanford University.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Under the Hood of Building and Scaling AI-Native Applications (Presented by Together AI)
sessions.abstract: Building AI-native applications is fundamentally different from traditional software—and scaling them demands infrastructure designed for models, not just compute. Decagon builds AI agents for enterprise customer support across voice, chat, and email, backed by a research org that trains and fine-tunes specialized models to execute complex workflows through Agent Operating Procedures. As Decagon scaled, running autonomous agents reliably and economically became the defining challenge—especially for real-time voice, where silence reads as failure and latency is instantly perceptible. In this session, learn how Together AI, the AI-native cloud, helped Decagon turn a multi-model agent stack into a production system that meets sub-second latency targets and stays reliable through traffic surges.

speakers.name: Alan Yiu
speakers.bio:
Alan Yiu is the vice president for product at Decagon—a company dedicated to reimagining digital customer experience and to democratize AI agent building. With Decagon, every business will have an AI concierge experience that’s always-on, multi-channel, and personalized.

Alan has also led product teams at Meta, Glean, and Airtable.

speakers.name: Charles Zedlewski
speakers.bio: Charles Zedlewski is the chief product officer at Together AI, the AI-native cloud. He helps exceptional technologists build companies and products that realize their vision. As an operating executive, Charles has partnered with founders early on to build and scale two young, low/no-revenue open-source platform companies into $5 billion category leaders: Cloudera and Temporal. As an advisor or investor, he’s helped other company founders build and scale category leaders, including Airbyte, Arive, H2O.ai, Hugging Face, LanceDB, Metronome, Streamsets, and more in the making.

sessions.title: Hard-Won Lessons From Production Inference at Scale (Presented by Together AI)
sessions.abstract: Inference is where AI systems face real-world constraints: latency users can feel, costs that scale with every request, and reliability requirements that leave no room for failure. In this session, hear lessons in delivering and continuously optimizing inference in production on Together AI, the AI-native cloud—powering Cursor’s Composer 2.0 background coding magenta at massive scale. Drawing on Together’s research-driven inference platform and Cursor’s experience operating AI products under real developer traffic, the discussion explores how foundational systems work translates into real-world improvements in speed, cost, and reliability—serving trillions of tokens per day across multi-data center deployments and running production inference on NVIDIA HGX B200 and NVIDIA GB200 NVL72, even under unpredictable demand.

speakers.name: Yuchen Wu
speakers.bio: Yuchen Wu is an engineer at Cursor.

speakers.name: Ce Zhang
speakers.bio: Ce is the chief technology officer of Together, the AI-native cloud. Ce is also the Neubauer Associate Professor of Data Science at the Department of Computer Science of the University of Chicago. The goal of his research is to democratize machine learning.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: 基于 Megatron-Core 的动态自适应并行训练方案
sessions.abstract: 本讲座将介绍基于 Megatron-Core 的动态自适应并行训练方案，该方案通过运行时自适应技术解决长尾序列训练中计算与内存的严重失配问题。其直接应对当前大规模视频模型训练中极端样本带来的效率瓶颈，已在数千 GPU 集群上验证能显著提升训练速度与资源利用率，是推动下一代多模态基础模型高效训练的关键技术。

speakers.name: 国昊 吴
speakers.bio: 专注于分布式训练框架的性能分析和优化，毕业于清华大学。目前主要从事开发和优化视频生成基座模型的训练效率。

speakers.name: Kunlun Li
speakers.bio: Kunlun focuses on performance optimization for CUDA programming and LLM training. He is currently working on development and optimization in Megatron-Core and Transformer-Engine.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: 基于 NVIDIA AI Enterprise 全栈赋能：“AI Factory” 重塑智造新标杆
sessions.abstract: 金盘科技携手 NVIDIA，针对传统制造业面临的效率瓶颈、数据孤岛及安全管理被动等痛点，构建了软硬一体的“AI 工厂”。依托 NVIDIA AI Enterprise 算力底座，融合 NIM 推理微服务、NeMo Agent Toolkit 智能体编排、VSS 视频分析及 Omniverse/Isaac 数字孪生平台，企业成功落地了多项关键场景。在工厂智能体分析方面，利用 VSS 视觉大模型技术，将工程现场非结构化的监控视频实时转化为结构化数据，主动识别 PPE 穿戴合规性与设备异常，实现从“事后回溯”到“事前预警”的跨越，将使项目安全水平，运营效率大幅提升。此外，通过标书生成智能体、职能数字员工、供应链智能控价等应用，标书制作周期从周级压缩至小时级并辅助中标金额超 10 亿元，效率大幅提升，数字化解决方案对外累计输出斩获超 9 亿元订单，成功打造了制造业从“人力驱动”向“数据与硅基劳动力驱动”转型的行业标杆。

speakers.name: 青 杨
speakers.bio: 杨青在人工智能领域的工作，聚焦于将 AI 技术与传统制造深度融合。他拥有近 30 年制造业一线生产、管理与数字化转型的完整履历：自 1997 年加入金盘电气集团，现任人工智能科技研究院副院长。这一从基层技术到全面管理，再到集团战略引领的复合型经历，使其对制造全流程痛点有深刻理解，为 AI 技术的精准应用奠定了坚实基础。他主导在工厂中应用机器视觉进行 AI 质检，提升检测效率；利用AI算法优化生产排程与能耗管理；并积极探索构建数字孪生系统和工艺优化大模型，推动工厂从数字化向智能化跃迁，为制造业转型升级注入新动能。

speakers.name: 龙 王
speakers.bio: 王龙毕业于清华大学，在中国、德国和美国的跨国公司拥有超过 20 年的创新产品研发、管理和运营经验。他曾作为创始人和联合创始人创办过几家企业，并于 2021 年初创立了矩阵起源。矩阵起源的使命是通过比以往更简单、更容易、更快的数据智能平台，帮助企业真正实施和执行数据驱动的运营和创新战略。为实现这一目标，矩阵起源聚集了具有强大创新能力和大量行业最佳实践的世界级团队，并构建了一个开源项目——MatrixOne。 在矩阵起源之前。王龙于 2017 年加入腾讯云担任副总裁，负责人工智能和大数据产品和服务的研发。在此之前，他在硅谷负责 VMware 的 vRealize Automation 产品管理和猎豹移动的内容推荐产品 。

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Building the Future of AI Infrastructure: The HUMAIN-AWS AI Factory Partnership (Presented by AWS)
sessions.abstract: Discover how HUMAIN and Amazon Web Services are pioneering sovereign AI infrastructure at unprecedented scale through their groundbreaking AWS AI Factory partnership. This session showcases a multi-gigawatt AI Zone engineered from inception to serve accelerating local and global demand for AI compute using NVIDIA GB300 GPUs.

speakers.name: Pranav Chachra
speakers.bio:
Pranav Chachra leads a product management team within Amazon EC2 at Amazon Web Services (AWS) in Seattle, focused on AWS AI Factories, AWS Local Zones, and advanced compute and AI infrastructure initiatives. Prior to AWS, Pranav served as a Group Product Manager at Lybrate, a startup recognized as one of the most innovative companies in healthcare by Fast Company, where he led multiple product teams. He started his career as a consultant at ZS Associates, where he co-founded the India APAC consulting group.

Pranav holds an MBA from Columbia Business School, where he earned Dean's Honors and leadership fellowships, as well as a gold medal in Electrical Engineering from the National Institute of Technology, India.

speakers.name: Dr. Yaser Al-Onaizan
speakers.bio:
Dr. Yaser Al-Onaizan is the Deputy CEO & President of AI Products at HUMAIN.
Previously, he served as CEO of the National Center for AI (NCAI), the innovation, research, and development arm of the Saudi
Data and AI Authority (SDAIA). At NCAI, he led a team of over 600 scientists and engineers, advancing Saudi Arabia’s national
Arabic LLM capabilities, including the launch of ALLaM in collaboration with IBM Watson and Microsoft Azure.
Prior to returning to Saudi Arabia, Dr. Al-Onaizan spent five years at Amazon, where he led key AI initiatives focused on speech and
language services. He headed the AI Languages Lab, managing over 100 scientists and engineers across multiple regions, and
contributed to establishing AWS AI’s presence in New York City.
Earlier in his career, he spent 15 years at IBM Research and the Watson Group, where he led work in neural machine translation,
speech translation, multilingual information extraction, and medical coding. He directed several multi-year DARPA projects in
collaboration with leading universities worldwide, including Stanford, MIT, CMU, and Cambridge.
Dr. Al-Onaizan has been recognized with multiple international awards, has published over 60 peer-reviewed papers, and holds
more than 20 patents. He is a senior member of several professional organizations, has served as program chair for international
conferences, and was Editor-in-Chief for ACM Transactions as well as a board member of AMTA.
He holds an M.S. and Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of Southern California’s Information Sciences Institute, and an
MBA from Columbia University

sessions.title: Crack the Code: Enable AI Assistants for Massive C++ Codebases
sessions.abstract: Most AI code assistants struggle with large, complex codebases because they lack reliable project-wide context, leading to shallow or incorrect suggestions. This talk presents a technical approach to improving AI code generation by combining NVIDIA NeMo Retriever NIM with the Model Context Protocol (MCP). We show how long-context embedding models from NeMo Retriever, paired with AST-based chunking that preserves logical code boundaries, enable higher-quality retrieval across massive C++ projects. By exposing this retriever as an MCP server, AI assistants can dynamically pull in relevant project-specific context, such as engine APIs, architectural patterns, and recent changes, at generation time. The result is more accurate, context-aware AI code generation that aligns with a project’s structure and conventions.

speakers.name: James Maki
speakers.bio: James Maki is a senior solutions architect on the NVIDIA Media & Entertainment SA team focused on the intersection of agentic and generative AI with video games. James has a master's degree in data analytics from Penn State.

speakers.name: Sanda Ho
speakers.bio: Sanda Ho is a Development Director at EA, focused on enabling Generative AI technologies across the software development lifecycle for game developers. With a 20-year career at EA, Sanda began in Quality Assurance, contributing first to NBA Live 06, and has since taken on a wide range of roles across the organization. A significant chapter of this journey was leading Game Preservation efforts at EA, providing a deep, end-to-end understanding of how games are built, maintained, and sustained over time. This broad perspective now informs Sanda’s work at the intersection of game development and generative AI, shaping practical approaches to the future of how games are made.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: 以 WAM 为核心构建全域 AI 2.0，AI + 车技术实践分享
sessions.abstract: 汽车行业的发展正迎来一个大变革时代，处在从“功能叠加”迈向“体验融合”的关键节点，这其中的核心是“AI + 车”；我们将分享吉利是如何探索，将 AI 融入智能汽车的架构、动力、底盘、座舱、辅助驾驶以及生产制造、企业发展等全链路，以及我们最新提出全域 AI 2.0 技术架构和 WAM 模型的实践应用。

speakers.name: 传海 李
speakers.bio: 李传海，男，2004 年加入吉利汽车，先后负责多款吉利战略车型开发设计及项目管理工作。当前担任吉利汽车集团副总裁、吉利汽车研究院院长职务，主要负责吉利汽车整车研发及技术规划工作。

sessions.title: Use Reinforcement Learning to Turn AI Agents Into Continuous Learners
sessions.abstract:
Static prompt engineering breaks under real-world complexity. This session shows how Reinforcement Learning (RL) enables agents to make better decisions, use tools reliably, and adapt in production.

This talk distills lessons from Atlassian on how it built Rovo, its enterprise AI solution for search, AI chat, and agents. The team explains how it moved from static prompt engineering to policy training via RL thereby improving intent interpretation, retrieval quality and how they use NVIDIA NeMo RL to evolve query rewriting from brittle rules to adaptive learning.

The session then expands to platform RL, where Google Cloud Vertex Training Cluster showcases how its integration of NVIDIA NeMo libraries has helped customers build best-in-class models. The team will deep dive on Vertex’s managed GPU infrastructure and how NeMo RL and other techniques can be used to build high performance agents."

speakers.name: Kang Li
speakers.bio: Kang Li is Atlassian's head of engineering for Rovo & AI Fundamentals, leading the team responsible for enterprise search, in-product AI capabilities, and intelligent agents to boost team collaboration and speed in products like Jira and Confluence. With over 15 years in large-scale search and AI, his experience includes building search, recommendation, and autonomous driving systems, holding multiple patents. He specializes in transforming advanced machine learning into practical "future of work" capabilities. Kang combines deep expertise in search, information retrieval, computer vision, and AI/ML with strategic communication to deliver reliable, high-impact AI features at scale for Atlassian.

speakers.name: Robert Van Dusen
speakers.bio: Robert Van Dusen is a Senior Product Manager at Google. He supports the Vertex AI Training team, which helps customers build state of the art deep learning models via managed infrastructure.

sessions.title: 基于全局 KV Cache 存储系统的高效 LLM 推理加速方案 Efficient LLM Inference Acceleration via a Global KV Cache Storage System
sessions.abstract:
KV Cache 通过“以存储换计算”的方式显著提升了大语言模型（LLM）的推理效率。然而，受限于高带宽内存（HBM）容量有限且成本高昂，将 KV Cache 扩展至外部高性能存储系统已成为关键优化方向。
本次 Session 聚焦我们与 NVIDIA 联合开展的系统级协同优化工作，在满足服务等级目标（SLO）的前提下，针对 LLM 推理中动态变化的工作负载，提出一套端到端的全局 KV Cache 解决方案。该方案涵盖：推理引擎与远程存储的深度集成优化，全局 KV Cache 元数据的统一管理与配置策略，面向 KV Cache 访问模式定制的远程存储架构设计。在典型 LLM 推理场景中，该方案实现了显著性能与成本收益：Cache 命中率提升 39%，P99 延迟降低 78%，单位 token 的计算与存储综合成本降至原来的 24%（即降低 76%）。

speakers.name: Shunkang Zhang
speakers.bio: 张顺康，NVIDIA GPU 计算专家团队（DevTech）工程师，专注于大模型训练和推理系统优化。

speakers.name: Wei Zhang
speakers.bio: 张为是阿里云智能集团的资深技术总监，致力于为大规模智能体应用（Agentic AI）打造高性能的数据技术设施。他拥有加州大学圣塔芭芭拉分校（UCSB）计算机博士学位，曾是 Pure Storage 的早期核心工程师，并作为创始成员参与了 Meta AI Infra团队的组建。目前，他正在主导阿里云 Tair KVCache 技术的研发，这是一种专为提升自主智能体响应速度而设计的高性能数据存储核心技术。他的工作聚焦于弥合海量数据系统与实时推理，确保新一代AI模型应用在实现极致效率的同时，将延迟降到最低。Wei Zhang is a Distinguished Engineer and Senior Director at Alibaba Cloud, where he leads high-performance data platforms optimized for large-scale Agentic AI. Previously, he was an early engineer at Pure Storage and a founding member of Meta’s AI infrastructure team. With a Ph.D. from UC Santa Barbara, Wei currently spearheads the development of Tair KVCache, a specialized memory-centric technology designed to enhance the responsiveness of autonomous agents. His work bridges the gap between massive data systems and real-time inference, ensuring that next-generation model-driven applications scale with maximum efficiency and minimal latency.

sessions.title: Building Sovereign AI Systems Under Real-World Constraints (Presented by Radiant)
sessions.abstract: This session examines sovereign AI as a systems engineering challenge that emerges from the interaction of architecture, control planes, operations, and life-cycle ownership. Drawing on real-world experience designing large-scale AI infrastructure under persistent supply and operational constraints, the talk focuses on the long-term consequences of foundational design decisions and how they shape autonomy, resilience, and adaptability over the life of an AI system.

speakers.name: Christopher Galloway
speakers.bio:
Chris is the chief technology officer at Radiant, leading the company's technical vision and the team building the Radiant platform. Prior to Radiant, he was a pivotal part of Ori's engineering leadership.
 
Before Ori, Chris was senior solution design engineer and manager at Broadcom, following its acquisition of Symantec. During his nearly 10 years at Symantec, he held several key infrastructure and engineering roles. There, he re-platformed a large-scale production email security service to Amazon Web Services.

Chris began his career at MessageLabs (later part of Symantec), where he implemented secure SMTP TLS arrangements and managed infrastructure for large-scale email encryption.

sessions.title: Transform Enterprise and Edge Infra with the Latest Blackwell Platform
sessions.abstract: Join us for a deep dive into the newest addition to NVIDIA's Blackwell data center portfolio, a compact, energy-efficient, multi-workload accelerator designed for mainstream data center and edge platforms. Discover how the technologies behind this new platform, backed by a broad ecosystem of infrastructure and independent software vendor partners, paired with NVIDIA enterprise software solutions for AI, data science, scientific computing, collaboration, and 3D design, deliver breakthrough performance across a wide range of applications.

speakers.name: Abhishek Verma
speakers.bio: Abhishek Verma is a principal product manager for data center GPUs at NVIDIA. His work focuses on bringing accelerated computing for AI, deep learning, HPC, and graphics to customers and partners around the world, with deployments spanning across cloud, on-premises, and edge locations.

speakers.name: Anthony Larijani
speakers.bio: Anthony Larijani is senior product marketing manager in NVIDIA’s data center enterprise platforms team, focused on NVIDIA’s portfolio of accelerated computing, networking, and software platforms. He is a marketing and sales professional with over 10 years of experience in data center infrastructure and cloud platform technologies. Larijani holds a bachelor’s degree from West Virginia University and an M.B.A. from Carnegie Mellon University.

sessions.title: Best Practices in Building and Packaging CUDA Python Libraries
sessions.abstract:
Packaging CUDA‑enabled Python code can seem daunting. With many build backends, Python wheels, conda packages, and CUDA compatibility rules in the mix, even experienced developers hesitate. The key is separating Python packaging complexity from CUDA‑specific challenges.

This session starts with a deep dive in build backends and packaging formats like wheels and conda packages, then moves into compiled extensions and how tools like pip, uv, and conda ensure broad compatibility. We’ll explore CUDA‑accelerated packages from pure‑Python wrappers to custom CUDA C/C++ kernels, emphasizing practical decisions and best practices for reliable installs.

speakers.name: Jonathan Dekhtiar
speakers.bio: Jonathan Dekhtiar has been contributing to many deep learning libraries, compilers, and frameworks since 2019. At NVIDIA, he played a key role in designing Python software and libraries release mechanisms and solving packaging challenges for GPU-accelerated Python code. As a founding member of the WheelNext initiative, Jonathan has dedicated significant energy to advance this open-source work with the community, and is actively working on proofs of concept, demos, and on the writing of PEPs.

speakers.name: Vyas Ramasubramani
speakers.bio: Vyas leads the development of Python tabular data processing in RAPIDS, including libraries like cudf-polars and cudf.pandas. Vyas has extensive experience across the CUDA development stack in Python, C, and C++, as well as with packaging CUDA-accelerated libraries.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Redefining Scalable AI Performance: OCI Supercomputing in the Cloud (Presented by Oracle)
sessions.abstract: Organizations building or scaling frontier AI models can’t be constrained by infrastructure. In this session, discover how Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) connects hundreds of thousands of NVIDIA GPUs. Oracle’s AI superclusters deliver up to 16 zettaFLOPS of peak performance, giving you the scale, low-latency GPU-to-GPU communication, and price-performance required for the largest AI training and inference workloads. In this session you will learn how OCI’s Acceleron ultra-low-latency RoCE networking and NVIDIA’s next-generation GPU instances dramatically increase LLM throughput, accelerate multimodal and scientific workloads, and improve cluster utilization, so you can train faster, deploy sooner, and operate more efficiently. Walk away with a clear understanding of how to move from experimentation to industrialized AI at global scale, with the performance, reliability, and sovereignty controls enterprise AI demands.

speakers.name: Jagwinder Brar
speakers.bio: Jag Brar is a vice president and distinguished engineer at Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). He has over 25 years of experience in the field including key technical roles at Oracle, Amazon, Arista Networks, AT&T, Telus, Electric Lightwave, and Terabeam Networks. Jag has been with OCI for almost 10 years, where he has played critical roles in physical networking, virtual networking, compute, database, and data center engineering. He is an inventor or co-inventor in over 100 patent applications.

speakers.name: Taylor Newill
speakers.bio:
Taylor Newill is a technology executive currently serving as Vice President at Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), where he focuses on high-performance computing (HPC) and artificial intelligence infrastructure. Based in Nashville, Tennessee, he has spent nearly a decade at Oracle building and scaling their cloud capabilities from the ground up. Taylor is a recognized expert in HPC, GPUs, and cluster networking. His career milestones at Oracle include:	

•	Director/senior director of HPC: Led the development of OCI’s HPC product, specifically focusing on bare metal servers and low-latency networks for demanding workloads.
•	Motorsports Engineering: Served as senior director of motorsports engineering, where he managed the technical partnership between Oracle and the Oracle Red Bull Racing Formula One team.

sessions.title: Accelerate Oracle AI Database Embedding and Vector Index Creation With NVIDIA (Presented by Oracle)
sessions.abstract: As enterprise vector data grows, keeping vector indexes up to date becomes increasingly important for similarity search and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). In partnership with NVIDIA, Oracle AI Database uses GPUs to accelerate vector workflows by speeding up large-scale embedding generation and vector index creation. With Oracle AI Database, users can run vector similarity search directly in SQL, as well as via APIs and SDKs. Join us for a walkthrough of using Oracle AI Database capabilities with NVIDIA GPUs, learn how they can reduce time-to-value for vector workloads, and see how Deutsche Bank is employing them.

speakers.name: Weiwei Gong
speakers.bio: Weiwei Gong is senior director of development at Oracle. With a passion for hardware-software co-design, Weiwei leads a team that builds the performance-critical features of database engines designed for analysis, distributed processing, and AI workloads. Her expertise is reflected in her numerous publications and more than 10 patents spanning various aspects of high-performance databases. She earned her master’s degree from Renmin University of China and her PhD from UMass Boston, both in computer science.

speakers.name: Tirthankar Lahiri
speakers.bio: Tirthankar Lahiri is Senior Vice President of Mission-Critical Data and AI Engines for Oracle Database. He is responsible for the Data Engine for Oracle Database, including areas like AI vector search, indexing, in-memory columnar , data compression, time travel, etc. He also manages the Oracle TimesTen In-Memory Database and the Oracle NoSQLDB product teams. Tirthankar has 30 years of experience in the database industry, and has worked on a variety of areas such as performance, scalability, manageability, caching, in-memory architectures, and developer-focused functionality. He has 73 issued and 24 pending patents, a B.Tech in Computer Science from the Indian Institute of Technology and an MS in Electrical Engineering from Stanford University.

speakers.name: Corey Nolet
speakers.bio: Corey is a distinguished engineer at NVIDIA, leading engineering for unstructured data processing acceleration. Prior to NVIDIA, Corey spent over a decade building massive-scale exploratory data science & real-time analytics platforms for big-data and HPC environments in the defense industry. Corey holds Bs. & Ms. degrees in Computer Science. He is also a Ph.D candidate in the same discipline, focused on the acceleration of algorithms at the intersection of graph and machine learning. Corey has a passion for using data to make better sense of the world.

speakers.name: Harsh Gupta
speakers.bio: Harsh Gupta , Vice president, Head of Oracle cloud and database Engineering in Deutsch Bank. A technologist (Oracle certified Master) and leader working in Oracle technology space for 23 years. Successfully transformed 8000+ Database to Oracle cloud for Deutsch Bank. Adopting AI in infra and other projects.

sessions.title: Enterprise AI Platforms and AI Agents to Business Value: How Enterprise AI Leaders are Scaling AI
sessions.abstract: The era of agentic AI is upon us. Generative AI and agentic architectures are no longer novelties—they’re rapidly becoming core infrastructure in leading enterprises. In this panel, moderated by NVIDIA’s vice president of enterprise AI, leading chief AI officers, CIOs, and AI platform leaders from NVIDIA, customers will share candid perspectives on building, scaling, and governing AI platforms and agentic systems in real-world settings. They will discuss how to move beyond pilot projects to large-scale adoption, manage infrastructure and inference pipelines, integrate agents into business workflows, and maintain trust, transparency, and resilience. Join us to hear from those who are navigating the challenges of enterprise AI transformation at scale.

speakers.name: Rama Akkiraju
speakers.bio: Rama Akkiraju, vice president of enterprise AI and automation at NVIDIA, is a former IBM Fellow, master inventor, and CTO of IBM Watson AI Ops. She co-authored more than 100 papers, holds over 50 patents, and received multiple awards, including Forbes's "Top 20 Women in AI Research" (2017), the University of California Berkeley's Athena Award (2020), and AI Industry Leader of the Year 2022, awarded by the Women Leaders in Data & AI organization.

speakers.name: Deepak Agarwal
speakers.bio:
Deepak Agarwal is LinkedIn’s chief AI officer, focused on empowering the global workforce, delivering economic opportunity, and ensuring LinkedIn remains at the forefront of building with AI in a responsible and compliant way. Previously, Deepak was the chief AI officer and vice president of consumer and trust engineering at Pinterest, where he delivered transformative AI innovations that redefined the member experience. Before that, he spent eight years as LinkedIn's VP of AI, leading key initiatives to infuse AI into the fabric of the company culture and closely integrate the technology into all facets of how the platform was built. 

Deepak is also on the advisory board for VentureBeat Transform, and has written a book ("Statistical Methods for Recommender Systems") and published extensively at top-tier conferences. He has a Ph.D. in statistics from the University of Connecticut.

speakers.name: Ed Chi
speakers.bio:
Ed H. Chi is VP of Research at Google DeepMind, leading machine learning research teams working on large language models (from LaMDA leading to launching Bard/Gemini), and universal assistant agents. With 39 patents and ~200 research articles, he is also known for research on user behavior in web and social media.  As the Research Platform Lead, he helped launched Bard/Gemini, a conversational chatbot experiment.  His research also delivered significant improvements for YouTube, News, Ads, Google Play Store at Google with >1000 product landings and ~$10.4B in annual revenue since 2013. 

Prior to Google, he was Area Manager and Principal Scientist at Xerox Palo Alto Research Center's Augmented Social Cognition Group in researching how social computing systems help groups of people to remember, think and reason. Ed earned his 3 degrees (B.S., M.S., and Ph.D.) in 6.5 years from University of Minnesota. Inducted as an ACM Fellow and into the CHI Academy, he also received a 20-year Test of Time award for research in information visualization. He has been featured and quoted in the press, including the Economist, Time Magazine, LA Times, and the Associated Press.  An avid golfer, swimmer, photographer and snowboarder in his spare time, he also has a blackbelt in Taekwondo.

speakers.name: Srini Namineni
speakers.bio:
As SVP and Chief Automation Officer, Srini Namineni leads the Cisco AI and Automation Center, developing Cisco’s next-generation AI platforms, intelligent assistants, agentic capabilities, and process optimization tools. He drives the company’s Enterprise AI strategy, embedding automation across all functions to accelerate innovation, enhance agility, and proactively manage risk. Srini is focused on turning AI potential into measurable business outcomes, enabling Cisco to deploy AI responsibly, securely, and at scale to deliver exceptional enterprise impact.
During his 30+ years in enterprise technology, Srini has held multiple executive leadership positions and developed a strong track record for delivering growth strategies and scaling operational excellence. In his most recent role as SVP of Growth Operations, Srini was accountable for accelerating Cisco’s revenue growth through simplified commercial experiences and critical cross-Cisco growth initiatives. Under his leadership, the team developed multiple AI-driven tools and programs that streamlined operations and improved the experience for customers, partners, and sellers. Prior to Cisco, Srini held leadership roles at Salesforce and IBM and co-founded a web startup.
Srini is a compassionate, visionary leader and an engineer at heart, known for his ability to harmonize deep technical innovation with operational precision. His passion for developing and empowering high-performing teams drives his commitment to shaping the leaders of tomorrow and fostering a culture of curiosity and innovation.
Srini holds a Master of Business Administration from The Wharton School, a Master of Science from Iowa State University, and a Bachelor of Technology from Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University. In recognition of his professional achievements, he was honored with the Distinguished Alumni Award by the Department of Computer Science at Iowa State University in October 2024.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: From Data to Deployment: Engineering Sovereign Language Models for a Changing World
sessions.abstract:
Get a glimpse into the day-to-day life of a sovereign LLM model maker. Learn what drives them—specifically, the motivations behind creating sovereign models and the roles these models play in today’s AI ecosystem, particularly in light of emerging sovereignty requirements across Europe and around the world.

We'll also explore what it takes to build state-of-the-art models—from data preparation and quality assurance to large-scale distributed training, compression optimization, and deployment (using tools such as NeMo, Model Optimizer, Dynamo, TRT-LLM)—as well as how to develop a successful developer ecosystem around your models and products to ensure long-term sustainability.

speakers.name: Adam Henryk Grzywaczewski
speakers.bio: Adam Grzywaczewski leads the Europe, Middle East, and Africa AI-focused Segment SA organization. He is a senior deep learning and applied research scientist at NVIDIA, where he focuses on helping customers scale and deploy deep learning workloads. Over the past decade, Adam has specialized in large-scale training, with expertise spanning deep learning systems and software design, as well as algorithms that enable large-batch data, model, and pipeline parallel training.

speakers.name: Paweł Kiszczak
speakers.bio: Paweł is a data scientist and AI engineer dedicated to bridging the gap between open-source research and large-scale industrial deployment. As a contributor to the SpeakLeash and Bielik.ai ecosystems, Paweł plays a vital role in building sovereign European AI. His current work focuses on agentic AI and multi-agent orchestration, alongside advanced LLM optimization techniques, including structured pruning and distillation, to bring high-performance models to production environments.

speakers.name: Stefan Mesken
speakers.bio: A lifelong science and technology enthusiast, Stefan studied mathematics and computer science at the University of Münster, where he also worked as a researcher before taking on a full-time role as a data scientist to start his career in tech. He transitioned into the AI space in 2018, and joined DeepL’s research team in 2020. As chief scientist, he shapes the strategy and prioritization of DeepL’s research agenda and coordinates large-scale research initiatives while aligning research goals with engineering and product teams. Stefan is passionate about building world-class AI tools for businesses, backed by cutting-edge research.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Bringing Sovereign AI From Ambition to a Strategic Foundation
sessions.abstract: Join Anne Bouverot, special envoy on AI, government of France, and Calista Redmond, vice president of global AI initiatives, NVIDIA, for an engaging conversation on how nations can chart their own AI paths while staying connected to the global innovation ecosystem. Learn how governments and industry are building sovereign AI foundations, advancing policy and partnerships, and scaling innovation for broad economic and social benefit.

speakers.name: Calista Redmond
speakers.bio:
Calista Redmond is vice president of global AI Initiatives at NVIDIA, working with regions, ecosystem partners, and product groups to develop and drive sovereign AI and other global programs that compel adoption of the NVIDIA platform for national and regional AI initiatives.

Previously, Calista was CEO of RISC-V International to grow value and adoption of the open RISC-V Instruction Set Architecture across the industry and around the world. Before that, she was vice president of IBM Z Ecosystem, leading strategic relationships across software vendors, system integraters, business partners, developer communities, and broader engagement across the industry. Her focus areas included execution of commercialization strategies, technical and business support for partners, and matchmaker to opportunities across the IBM Z and LinuxOne community. 

Calista's experience includes building and leading strategic business models within IBM Systems through open-source initiatives including Open Mainframe Project, OpenPOWER, and OpenDaylight, numerous acquisition and divestiture missions, and several strategic alliances. Prior to joining IBM, she was an entrepreneur in four successful startups in the IT industry. She holds degrees from the University of Michigan and Northwestern University.

speakers.name: Anne Bouverot
speakers.bio:
Anne Bouverot is France's special envoy for AI, co-chair of the AI and Digital Council, and chair of the board of the École normale supérieure.

After earning a Ph.D. in computer science, she spent most of her career in the technology sector, and now advises companies and scale-ups. She co-chaired the “AI: our ambition for France” report in March 2024. As France’s special envoy, she led preparations for the summit for action on AI held in Paris in February 2025.

Anne spent the first 20 years of her career at Orange, then became CEO of the GSMA, the global association of telecom operators, and then president of the digital security company Morpho, within the Safran Group.

sessions.title: Reinvent Semiconductor Design With Microsoft Discovery and Synopsys Agent Engineers (Presented by Microsoft)
sessions.abstract: Semiconductor teams face exploding design complexity, shrinking windows, and increasing verification challenges. This session shows how the Microsoft Discovery AI for Science platform, combined with Synopsys Agent Engineers, introduces an agentic approach to electronic design automation (EDA) that automates routine steps, accelerates expert decision‑making, and keeps data secure on Azure. Discovery’s Science Copilot orchestrates multi‑agent plans leveraging Azure AI resources and a graph‑grounded knowledge layer, while Synopsys agents drive proven flows levering domain expertise—spanning spec‑to‑RTL, simulation, timing, and sign‑off—directly on Azure HPC infrastructure. See how novel methodology integrates with existing Synopsys tools, and how NVIDIA‑accelerated Azure AI infrastructure results in faster time‑to‑PPA, fewer iterations, and more predictable schedules.

speakers.name: Prashant Varshney
speakers.bio: Prashant Varshney is head of product, physics and silicon, for Microsoft Discovery & Quantum. A product exec and entrepreneur with 25 years in AI, scientific computing, and cloud/HPC, he leads Discovery’s agentic-AI platform and infrastructure to speed research and engineering. His work positions Azure for semiconductor and government missions. Previously he led products at Siemens EDA and founded startups with patented chip and power tech.

sessions.title: Operationalize Agentic AI at Hyperscale: Microsoft Foundry in Azure AI Factories (Presented by Microsoft)
sessions.abstract:
As enterprises move from AI experimentation to always‑on, agentic systems, the challenge shifts from accessing models to operating intelligent agents reliably at scale. In this session, Microsoft will demonstrate what goes into developing purpose-built AI factories using NVIDIA technology to run production‑grade agentic AI workloads.

We begin with the design principles behind Microsoft’s AI factory approach—how NVIDIA GPU architectures are translated into deployable, repeatable platforms within Azure, for long‑running, multi‑step agent workflows across thousands of GPUs, and heterogeneous networks. See how agent behavior at scale reshapes system design, where performance, reliability, and operations must work together to keep agents responsive and continuously available.
 
Then we'll dive deeper into Microsoft Foundry as the control plane for agentic AI. Foundry enables teams to discover, deploy, and operate frontier and open models, including NVIDIA Nemotron reasoning models and NIM microservices, and turn them into AI agents. By unifying model catalogs, agent services, governance, and infrastructure, Microsoft Foundry and NVIDIA help enterprises move from single‑prompt interactions to coordinated, multi‑agent systems in production.
 
We conclude with an inside look at how Microsoft operates its global NVIDIA fleet through Foundry to deliver predictable performance, security, and cost efficiency, transforming AI factories into production platforms that power enterprise‑grade agentic AI at scale.

speakers.name: Nitin Nagarkatte
speakers.bio: Nitin is a product management leader at Microsoft Azure AI with over 20 years of experience spanning semiconductor and cloud. He is an expert at developing and launching AI Infrastructure offerings at hyperscale to enable and accelerate frontier AI workloads.

speakers.name: Anand Raman
speakers.bio:
Anand Raman is currently the General Manager for Cloud and AI Platforms at Microsoft. Anand is passionate about solving large scale problems responsibly and has been a long-time member of technical staff at CoreAI working on Microsoft Foundry, the GenAI platform for building Agents.

A Microsoft veteran for 21 years, Anand has led the product management and the development teams at Azure AI and Data Services, Visual Studio and Windows Server User Experience teams at Microsoft.

speakers.name: Vipul Modi
speakers.bio: Vipul Modi is currently a VP and Distinguished Engineer at Microsoft and a seasoned industry leader with more than 25 years of experience in building large scale distributed systems and services.   He leads the engineering org at CoreAI that is at the center of Microsoft's AI revolution providing a managed AI infrastructure for large-scale inference of latest LLMs, ML training, and fine-tuning workloads. Vipul and his team manage one of the world's largest GPU fleet hosting latest and greatest models for Azure Open AI service, and other Microsoft 1P services including all of the CoPilots.

sessions.title: UK AI Minister: AI Growth Zones and the UK’s AI Opportunities Action Plan
sessions.abstract: Join this fireside chat with Kanishka Narayan, MP and U.K. AI Minister, and Bea Longworth, Director of Government Relations, EMEA to examine how the U.K. Government is reshaping the country's global competitiveness in AI through massive infrastructure investment and innovative policy frameworks. You will leave with a clear understanding of the U.K. government's roadmap for AI leadership and how countries can unlock new economic value for enterprises, governments, scientists, and citizens around the world.

speakers.name: Kanishka Narayan
speakers.bio: Kanishka Narayan was appointed Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State in the UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology on 7 September 2025. He is responsible for AI Opportunities, AI Security Institute, Intellectual Property, Semiconductors, Tech for Growth and Online Safety. He was elected as the MP for Vale of Glamorgan in July 2024.

speakers.name: Bea Longworth
speakers.bio: Bea leads NVIDIA’s government relations in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. Her team focuses on policy, regulation, and standards relating to technologies such as artificial intelligence, high performance computing, and the metaverse. Bea joined NVIDIA in 2006 and worked across various PR, analyst relations, and corporate communications roles before helping to found the company’s government relations practice in Europe.

sessions.title: Category Theory in Commodities: Applying Sheaf-Based Graph Neural Networks and Quantum Portfolio Optimization
sessions.abstract: We apply sheaf neural networks, a generalization of graph neural networks (GNNs), to model commodities and capture cross-curve, cross-sector, and macro linkages. These models are used to generate probabilistic forecasts of returns, volatility, and co-movements. The resulting signals can then be combined with quantum-based portfolio optimization techniques to explore risk–return trade-offs.

speakers.name: Yigal Jhirad
speakers.bio: Yigal D. Jhirad is a portfolio manager and director of quantitative and derivatives strategies at Cohen & Steers. Prior to joining in 2007, he headed the Quantitative & Derivatives effort at Morgan Stanley. He has presented research on LLMs, generative AI, decision trees, neural networks, long short-term memory, reinforcement learning, and generative adversarial networks. Yigal graduated magna cum laude from the Wharton School of the University of Pennsylvania with a B.S. in economics.

speakers.name: Emanuel Scoullos
speakers.bio: Emanuel Scoullos is a data scientist on the AI Factory team at NVIDIA, where he focuses on GPU applications. Previously, he worked at a startup in the anti-money laundering space, applying data science techniques to construct machine learning pipelines. He earned his Ph.D. and master's degrees in chemical engineering from Princeton University and an undergraduate degree in chemical engineering from Rutgers University.

speakers.name: Siddharth Samsi
speakers.bio: Siddharth Samsi is a senior solutions architect in the Financial Services and Technology team at NVIDIA who focuses on GPU applications within the financial services industry. Prior to NVIDIA, he led research in distributed AI, high performance computing, and energy-aware computing for AI at the MIT Lincoln Laboratory Supercomputing Center. He earned his Ph.D. and master's degree in electrical and computer engineering from The Ohio State University.

sessions.title: From Replacement to Enhancement: A Human-Centered Approach to Multi-Modal AI Design
sessions.abstract: As the landscape of artificial intelligence evolves, the need for AI systems that embrace the full range of human experience has never been more important. Drawing on research from the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered AI (HAI), we'll explore strategies for embedding human-centered design principles into AI technologies. By emphasizing the power of interdisciplinary collaboration, we'll illustrate how AI can enhance human capability and shift the narrative around AI from one of replacement to one of augmentation. See how multi-modal user interfaces can create richer, more holistic interactions with AI, and how academia and industry can co-create AI solutions that serve as partners in enhancing human agency while fostering positive social impact within diverse communities.

speakers.name: James Landay
speakers.bio:
James Landay is a professor of computer science and the Anand Rajaraman and Venky Harinarayan Professor in the School of Engineering at Stanford University. He specializes in human-computer interaction. James is the co-founder and co-director of the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI). 

Prior to joining Stanford, James was a professor of information science at Cornell Tech in New York City for one year, and a professor of computer science and engineering at the University of Washington for 10 years. From 2003-06, he also served as the director of Intel Labs Seattle, a leading research lab that explored various aspects of ubiquitous computing. 

James Landay was also the chief scientist and co-founder of NetRaker, which was acquired by KeyNote Systems in 2004. Before that, he was an associate professor of computer science at UC Berkeley. James received his B.S. in electrical engineering and computer science from UC Berkeley in 1990, and M.S. and Ph.D. in computer science from Carnegie Mellon University in 1993 and 1996, respectively. His Ph.D. dissertation was the first to demonstrate the use of sketching in user interface design tools. 

He is a member of the ACM SIGCHI Academy and an ACM Fellow. He is an ACM SIGCHI Lifetime Research Award winner. He served for six years on the NSF CISE Advisory Committee.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Powering Agentic AI in Automotive: From Enterprise and Manufacturing Operations to In-Vehicle Intelligence
sessions.abstract: Automotive AI is rapidly evolving from isolated pilots into end-to-end, production-scale deployments—spanning manufacturing operations, enterprise workflows, and in-vehicle intelligence. In this session, industry leaders from ServiceNow, Visteon, Lyft, and Tata Consultancy Services share how NVIDIA Nemotron models are being operationalized across the automotive value chain to deliver measurable business, operational, and engineering impact.

speakers.name: Sri Subramanian
speakers.bio: Sri Subramanian is responsible for NVIDIA’s global automotive Gen AI business, including go-to-market strategy, business development, and customer and partner relations. He's focused on Gen AI opportunities with automotive customers everywhere, from in-car to manufacturing to enterprise, and establishing AI foundries using NVIDIA assets such as NeMo and NIMs. Sri has 25 years of experience spanning the automotive, mobile, and internet-of-things industries, leveraging cloud and embedded technologies. Prior to NVIDIA, he held product and engineering leadership roles at Amazon, Qualcomm, NXP, and Motorola, among others. Sri holds a master's degree in electrical engineering from Georgia Tech and an M.B.A. from the University of Texas, Austin.

speakers.name: Manoj Chathankulangara Rajan
speakers.bio: With 22 years of experience and primary expertise in AI and high computing devices, Manoj CR heads the AI/Gen AI & Autonomous Driving Center of Excellence in TCS. He is primarily responsible for defining futuristic solutions in AI and Gen AI for next-generation software-defined vehicles, and leveraging them for vehicle development for global manufacturers and Tier 1 suppliers. He owns 22 patents in AI and computer vision, and has published numerous conference papers and journals.

speakers.name: Jordan Tauriainen
speakers.bio: Jordan Tauriainen has 15 years of experience working across automotive, manufacturing, and technology. He currently serves as senior director of global automotive go-to-market at ServiceNow, where he helps automakers and suppliers streamline their businesses, modernize operations with automated workflows, and apply AI at scale. Previously, he led global customer experience and connected mobility strategy for automotive at Amazon Web Services (AWS). Jordan holds cloud certifications from both AWS and Microsoft Azure, a B.B.A. in supply chain management from Western Michigan University, and an M.B.A. in leadership from the University of Michigan.

speakers.name: Tao Ye
speakers.bio:
Tao Ye serves as the director of Gen AI at Lyft, where she is responsible for leading initiatives in agentic AI applications, thereby driving innovations across the Rideshare experience, marketplace, and the broader Lyft ecosystem. Prior to Lyft, she led teams of scientists and engineers at Amazon Music focusing on LLM applications, music conversations, and conversational recommendations. She was a founding member of the Pandora science team and director of science, spending over nine years on personalized music recommendation systems, search and voice, A/B measurements, and user modeling. 

In the larger research community, Tao co-founded Women in Recsys, has served on the steering committee of Recsys since 2018, and served as industry co-chair for 2018 Recsys, 2022 SIGIR, 2022 CIKM, 2024 SIGIR, and 2025 UMAP.

speakers.name: Harsha Padmanabha
speakers.bio: Harsha Padmanabha is Global VP of Product & Engineering - AI & User Experience, leading innovation in Vision-Language Models and hybrid AI architectures for automotive applications. His career began with HMI and graphics development in casino gaming, later advancing through video reasoning technologies and 3D advertising solutions. Today, his team's work spans from advanced 3D graphics deployed in over 10 million vehicles to cutting-edge AI systems for in-cabin experiences and ADAS. He specializes in bridging edge and cloud computing, combining real-time vision processing with contextual understanding through knowledge graphs and VLMs. Passionate about transforming automotive human-machine interaction through next-generation AI technologies.

speakers.name: Felix Friedmann
speakers.bio: Felix Friedmann is a product and engineering lead for the NVIDIA DRIVE platform, covering NVIDIA Embedded AI Inference and NVIDIA DriveWorks. He unites the latest technological innovations, like embedded visual language models, with the reliability and safety required for an automotive software platform. Felix worked with the NVIDIA DRIVE platform since its earliest generation in his previous roles, when he brought early deep learning models into embedded applications at Audi, and designed perception and system architecture for AVs at VW’s AID, and later Argo AI.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Break Communication Barriers: Scale AI and HPC with NCCL, NIXL, and NVSHMEM
sessions.abstract:
Learn how to eliminate communication bottlenecks with NVIDIA Communication stack. Over the past year, each library has evolved to address specific bottlenecks: NCCL introduced Symmetric Memory support and Copy Engine collectives, enabling zero-SM communication. NVSHMEM enhanced GPU-initiated operations in the Python ecosystem. NIXL emerged as the inference-focused solution, providing async, noncontiguous transfers across memory tiers (GPU HBM, CPU DRAM, local/networked storage) with backends including GDS, UCX, and S3—essential for disaggregated inference architectures.

Explore developer optimizations: custom collectives in NCCL, KV cache and storage innovations in NIXL, and expert parallelism patterns. Learn how Spectrum-X plugins extend capabilities across multi-site deployments for scale-across connectivity.

speakers.name: Matthew Nicely
speakers.bio: Matthew Nicely is a group product manager on NVIDIA’s AI Platform Software team, leading product strategy for GPU kernels and communication libraries, including cuDNN, CUTLASS, FlashInfer, NCCL, NIXL, NVSHMEM, and OpenAI Triton contributions. His work focuses on enabling high-performance training and inference across NVIDIA’s AI ecosystem. Matt earned his Ph.D. in computer engineering from the University of Alabama in Huntsville, where he specialized in algorithm optimizations for GPUs.

sessions.title: Fast-Tracking the Launch of L4 Robotaxis Across the Globe
sessions.abstract: Uber and NVIDIA are joining forces to accelerate the next generation of Level 4 robotaxi and autonomous delivery fleets, built on NVIDIA’s end‑to‑end AI architecture. This fireside chat will unpack Uber’s long‑term vision for deeply integrating robotaxis into its global marketplace to deliver safer, more reliable, and more sustainable urban mobility.

speakers.name: Ali Kani
speakers.bio: Ali Kani is the vice president of automotive at NVIDIA, overseeing NVIDIA DRIVE’s end-to-end platform for autonomous vehicle development and production. The NVIDIA automotive group is engaged with hundreds of car and truck makers, robotaxi companies, tier 1 suppliers, high-definition mapping companies, sensor manufacturers, and startup companies—all building on NVIDIA DRIVE and its high-performance AI capabilities.

speakers.name: Sarfraz Maredia
speakers.bio: Sarfraz Maredia is global head of autonomous mobility and delivery at Uber, leading the teams responsible for all of Uber’s autonomous marketplace and fleet operations, commercial strategy, partnerships, and expansion efforts. Previously, he led the two largest businesses at Uber as vice president for mobility in the U.S. and Canada, and most recently as head of Americas for Uber Eats. He first joined Uber as a general manager in 2014, and has held a number of senior operating roles over the years.

sessions.title: Market Primitives for an Agentic Economy
sessions.abstract: AI agents are emerging as significant economic actors, yet today's markets are designed for human participants, burdened by cognitive load, irrational behavior and biases, and a high-friction legal paradigm. Illia introduces a formal model for agentic markets where autonomous AI agents directly express and match intents. He proposes a new set of agentic market primitives including programmatic escrow, agentic dispute resolution, and intent‑based matching and settlement. This paradigm shift necessitates revising classical economic problems for agentic systems.

speakers.name: Illia Polosukhin
speakers.bio: Illia Polosukhin is co-founder of NEAR, on the mission to build user-owned AI. Before NEAR, Illia worked on AI research at Google and co-authored the landmark paper, "Attention Is All You Need."

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Transforming how Vehicles are Designed, Bought, and Serviced With Agentic AI (Presented by AWS)
sessions.abstract:
AWS and Cox Automotive, powered by NVIDIA, demonstrate how AI agents are transforming vehicle operations from experimental tools into foundational business infrastructure. Cox Automotive built an agentic workflow in just four days using proprietary data and multi-agent orchestration to automate vehicle inspections, ensure accurate billing, and deliver consistent recommendations. By deploying real-time, collaborative AI agents, they transformed a manual, technician-driven process into a production-ready solution—reducing turnaround time from two days to under 30 minutes while boosting satisfaction for both customers and technicians.

This session showcases how AWS and NVIDIA enable rapid deployment of intelligent automation that delivers measurable business outcomes: faster cycle times, improved accuracy, and enhanced customer experience. AWS is built for industry.

speakers.name: Ozgur Tohumcu
speakers.bio:
Ozgur Tohumcu is the general manager, automotive and manufacturing for Amazon Web Services (AWS), leading the development and execution of AWS’ business, technology, partnering, and go-to-market strategy for the global automotive and manufacturing organization. 

He is passionate about enabling the world’s largest automotive original equipment manufacturers (OEMs), suppliers, auto tech companies, and manufacturers to accelerate their digital transformation journeys, focusing on customer impact, value creation, and innovation. 

Prior to joining AWS in 2021, Ozgur was the CEO at Oxbotica, a venture capital-backed autonomous driving software company based in the U.K. Previously, he spent more than a decade at Ericsson, most recently as the senior vice president of digital, IT, and cloud for Europe. Ozgur held a number of other executive leadership roles in sales, software design and development, and running customer operations. He began his career in professional services focusing on software design and program management of large-scale digital transformation programs for private- and public-sector clients. 

Ozgur received an M.S. in technology management from the University of Pennsylvania, an M.B.A. from New York State University at Albany, and a B.S. in electrical engineering from Bogazici University Istanbul, Turkey. Outside of work, Ozgur volunteers with the Turkey Mozaik Foundation, which focuses on the advancement of education, health, children’s rights, gender equality, culture and arts, and environmental sustainability in Turkey.

speakers.name: Chris Dillon
speakers.bio: Chris Dillon is VP, Engineering & Operations Platforms at Cox Automotive, leading the teams that span core infrastructure through the engineering platform that powers the company's software development organizations. He is driving AI transformation across Cox's enterprise toolchain, with a focus on agentic development tooling, spec-driven development, and LLM infrastructure to accelerate developer productivity and business outcomes. His team is at the forefront of rethinking how enterprise software is built — integrating AI across the development lifecycle to reshape how thousands of engineers deliver software at scale.

sessions.title: How AI Systems Evolve Into Long-Horizon Agents
sessions.abstract: Learn how an AI system can evolve from simple search into long-horizon agents that run for hours or days to complete complex, real-world tasks. This session walks through four stages of that evolution—AI search, asynchronous workflows, agents, and long-horizon agents—and examines the architectural patterns and technical trade-offs required at each step. We will cover practical approaches we learned building Genspark, including model routing, tool execution frameworks, validation of agent trajectories, and reliability strategies needed to operate agent systems at scale.

speakers.name: Kay Zhu
speakers.bio: Kay Zhu is a co-founder and the chief technology officer at Genspark, the all-in-one AI workspace set on helping over 1 billion knowledge workers automate their busywork. A pioneering architect of AI-powered search, Kay launched the world's first deep neural network ranking model in production search at Baidu in 2013—two years before Google's RankBrain—and previously led core AI innovations at Google. His rare combination of deep expertise in machine learning systems, search architecture, and cutting-edge AI research has been instrumental in Genspark's meteoric rise, as it achieved a $1.25 billion valuation and raised $435 million in funding from top-tier investors including Emergence Capital, LG Technology Ventures, and SBI Investment, making it one of the fastest-growing AI companies in history.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Architects of the Accelerated Age: How CUDA Builders Changed the World (and How You’re Next)
sessions.abstract:
The world was changed by creators who wrote the first kernels and turned raw potential into reality. Now, they are sharing the blueprints. Join us for an unfiltered look at CUDA’s impact with prolific creators who have redefined the boundaries of their fields. Hear the "aha" moments and war stories behind the software that shifted society. 

Since 2007, CUDA has served as the foundation for an accelerated computing revolution, enabling breakthroughs from genomic decoding to generative AI. This scale wasn't built on "killer apps" alone, but through decades of continuous evolution: maturing compilers, expanding libraries, and simplifying the path from idea to deployment. From CUDA-X to accessibility leaps like tile, the platform has evolved alongside its builders. Explore the journey from the first line of code to global scale and leave ready to build your own world-changing legacy.

speakers.name: Stephen Jones
speakers.bio: Stephen is one of the architects of CUDA, working on defining the language, the platform, and the hardware that it runs on to span the needs of parallel programming, from high performance computing to artificial intelligence. Previously, he led the Simulation & Analytics group at SpaceX, working on large-scale simulation of rocket engines. Stephen has been a part of CUDA since 2008.

speakers.name: Joe Stam
speakers.bio: A 20-year GPU veteran, Joe began developing with CUDA version 0.8. His career began in the auto industry, pioneering computer vision systems with neural networks in the late 1990s, for which he was awarded dozens of patents. He joined NVIDIA in 2007, holding various roles and forming the company's first computer vision team. In 2012, he joined Jump Trading and leads their trailblazing efforts with GPU supercomputing in the finance industry.

speakers.name: Kate Clark
speakers.bio: Kate Clark works at the interface of applications, algorithms, and parallel computation. Before joining NVIDIA, Kate was a researcher in radio astronomy signal-processing algorithms at Harvard University and a postdoc at Boston University in Massachusetts, with a focus on multi-grid solver algorithm. She received her Ph.D. from the University of Edinburgh, Scotland, where her doctoral research focused on Monte Carlo algorithms for Lattice QCD.

speakers.name: Paulius Micikevicius
speakers.bio: Paulius Micikevicius is in Meta Superintelligence Labs, where he works on performance and numerics aspects of AI. Prior to that he was at NVIDIA for over 16 years in total, where he worked on various aspects of general compute acceleration on GPUs (porting and optimizing various workloads on GPU systems, contributing to GPU architecture improvements for those workloads, coordinating reduced precision and sparsity efforts for deep learning). In between NVIDIA stints Paulius was a perception engineer at Zoox, where he worked on sensor calibration and object detection. Prior to all that he was an assistant professor of Computer Science at Armstrong Atlantic State University. He holds a PhD in Computer Science from University of Central Florida. Paulius has been developing with CUDA since its beta release in 2006, but his initial work on GPU general compute started a few years prior to that using Cg shader language. Applications domains include machine learning, scientific computing (weather/climate simulation, nuclear transport simulation), seismic computing, autonomous driving, and computer vision.

speakers.name: Wen-Mei Hwu
speakers.bio: Wen-mei Hwu joined NVIDIA in February 2020 as senior distinguished research scientist after spending 32 years at the University of Illinois, where he was a professor, endowed chair, and chief scientist of the Parallel Computing Institute. In 2010, Wen-mei co-authored the popular textbook "Programming Massively Parallel Processors: a Hands-on Approach," thereby setting the foundation for programming and compiler techniques for modern GPU computing.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Accelerated Computing Libraries

sessions.title: All Roads, All Rides: Technical Foundations for a Universal Autonomy Platform
sessions.abstract: Self-driving vehicles represent the most immediate opportunity for AI to transform the physical world. This talk presents the technical architecture of Nuro’s autonomy platform, designed to generalize across geographies, use cases, and vehicles. Discover how Nuro’s end-to-end AI foundation model learns to drive safely in complex conditions. Learn how the Nuro Driver is integrated into passenger vehicles and how it is validated. See how this approach comes together through an applied example: Nuro’s global robotaxi program with Uber and Lucid.

speakers.name: Dave Ferguson
speakers.bio: Dave Ferguson is the co-founder and co-CEO of Nuro, a physical AI company bringing scalable self-driving to robotaxis, commercial fleets, and personally owned vehicles. Before starting Nuro in 2016, Dave was a principal engineer at Google's self-driving program (now Waymo), where he led the computer vision, machine learning, behavior prediction, and scene understanding teams. He also led the planning group for Carnegie Mellon University's winning team in the 2007 DARPA Urban Grand Challenge, the competition that helped launch the modern autonomous vehicle industry. He earned his M.S. and Ph.D. in robotics from Carnegie Mellon University.

sessions.title: GPU ♥ LPU: Everything You Wanted to Know
sessions.abstract: With language processing units (LPUs) and GPUs in the same data center, developers can optimize for both ultra-low latency and high throughput. LPUs excel at token-by-token and interactive workloads, while GPUs excel at massive parallelism and larger batches. This fireside chat helps developers understand the complementary strengths of LPUs and GPUs.

speakers.name: Jonathan Ross
speakers.bio: Jonathan Ross is Chief Software Architect at Nvidia. Prior he was the founder and CEO of Groq, where he created the LPU, and prior to that created Google's TPU.

speakers.name: David Senra
speakers.bio: David Senra is the host of Founders podcast. For the past 10 years David has intensely studied the life and work of hundreds of history’s greatest entrepreneurs. Every week he reads a biography of one of history’s greatest entrepreneurs and shares what he’s learned on his podcast.
