David has lectured on entrepreneurship at Harvard Business School, Columbia Business School, and Notre Dame. Founders is one of the top business podcasts in the world and hundreds of thousands of founders, investors, and executives listen every week.

sessions.title: Orchestrate Next-Generation AI Workloads With Open-Source Slurm
sessions.abstract: Scaling next-generation discovery requires more than increased computational power; it also depends on flexible, reliable workload orchestration at large scale. This session examines how Slurm can be used to manage traditional HPC jobs, Kubernetes-based AI workloads with complex topology requirements, and converged environments in a single ecosystem. We will outline the current architecture, recent enhancements, and ongoing community-driven work that target higher efficiency, portability, and interoperability for supercomputing workloads over the coming decade.

speakers.name: Tim Wickberg
speakers.bio: Tim Wickberg is responsible for the technical direction and development of the open-source Slurm Workload Manager, as well as the Slinky Project that supports converged Slurm and Kubernetes environments. Prior to NVIDIA's acquisition, he served as the chief technology officer of SchedMD, and has over 15 years of HPC industry experience alongside an M.S. In computer science from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute.

speakers.name: Danny Auble
speakers.bio: Danny Auble is responsible for the direction and development of the open-source Slurm Workload Manager, as well as the Slinky Project which supports converged Slurm and Kubernetes environments. Prior to NVIDIA's acquisition he served as the Chief Executive Officer of SchedMD, and has over 25 years of HPC industry experience alongside an B.S. In Computer Science from Brigham Young University.

sessions.title: Future of Enterprise AI: Infrastructure to Intelligence (Presented by Tata Consultancy Services)
sessions.abstract:
The future of enterprise AI is shaped by how intelligence is designed, deployed, and scaled across the enterprise. Next-gen computing platforms enable advanced models, including generative and agentic AI, to operate reliably in complex environments, allowing organizations to move beyond isolated automation toward AI-driven systems that reason, learn, and act in real time. 

This session examines how enterprises are designing AI-native architectures that combine high-performance compute, modern data platforms, and advanced models to support production-grade AI at scale. It explores how governance, trust, and resilience are embedded into AI systems from Day 1. With examples from industries such as banking, financial services, telecommunications, and media, the discussion highlights how accelerated AI is transforming enterprises from software-driven execution to intelligence-driven outcomes.

speakers.name: Amit Kapur
speakers.bio:
Amit Kapur heads the AI and Service transformation unit. In his previous role, Amit oversaw strategy and operations for TCS’s second-largest region, servicing large global and leading U.K. organizations. Since joining TCS in 1999, Amit has worked with some of the top global 500 firms in several consulting, advisory, and management roles, helping them drive efficiencies and improve their business results.

Amit, based in London, previously led the TCS Benelux business for seven years, during which TCS Benelux was able to demonstrate a full services play, and added several marquee brands to its customer portfolio in the region. Amit graduated from Mumbai University, India in 1999, with his master's in management studies. He lives in London with his family.

speakers.name: Sujatha Gopal
speakers.bio: Sujatha Gopal is the chief technology officer of the Communication, Media & Information Service business unit of Tata Consultancy Services. She's a seasoned technologist driving domain and tech strategy, enterprise architecture transformation, strategic road-mapping for tech-enabled business transformation, platform strategies, and new technology incubation. Sujatha is an industry advisor to several chief experience officers, and a speaker in multiple industry forums.

speakers.name: Babu Unnikrishnan
speakers.bio:
Babu Unnikrishnan (Unni) is the CTO for Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI) Americas at TCS. In this role, he is responsible for developing technology strategy, designing and delivering strategic digital programs, and driving co-innovation with TCS customers.

Unni also formulates differentiated industry solutions and cross-industry initiatives, leveraging emerging technologies and new ways of working. With 27 years of experience in leadership roles, Unni has led a number of successful digital initiatives, driving immense business value for customers.

His areas of interest include future digital architectures, applied AI, open data, multi cloud, core systems modernisation, and modern software platforms. An ardent proponent of continual learning, he believes in leveraging technology as a pragmatic differentiator in translating strategy to execution.

sessions.title: Build Open Models That Read, See, and Listen
sessions.abstract: Open models are rapidly evolving beyond text-only understanding to reason across documents, images, and video. In this session, we trace the technical evolution of the NVIDIA Nemotron family from early text-focused models to multi-modal models. We explore architectural decisions and trade-offs behind each phase. We also share how open backbones, combined with open datasets and post-training recipes, enable developers to build multi-modal models.

speakers.name: Padmavathy Subramanian
speakers.bio: Padmavathy Subramanian is a director of generative AI products at NVIDIA, building multi-modal Nemotron products. She previously worked in NVIDIA DRIVE AV and IX SW products and intelligent video analytics platform in NVIDIA autonomous machines products.

speakers.name: Andrew Tao
speakers.bio: Andrew Tao is vice president of applied research at NVIDIA, where he leads work across computer vision and generative, multi-modal AI, including the Nemotron model family. His research focuses on translating cutting-edge advances into practical systems, spanning vision-language models, high-resolution image synthesis, video-to-video translation, and semantic segmentation. Andrew is a co-author of several highly cited papers, including "High-Resolution Image Synthesis and Semantic Manipulation with Conditional GANs" and "Image Inpainting for Irregular Holes Using Partial Convolutions." He received his master’s degree in electrical engineering from Stanford University in 1992, with an emphasis on computer architecture.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: Optimizing Speech-To-Text Pipelines for Real-World Voice Agents
sessions.abstract: Every voice AI agent starts with the same problem: turning raw audio into something useful. This session shares what we've learned at Decagon building a production speech-to-text pipeline for customer service voice agents. We'll walk through the core components, including voice activity detection, turn detection, and transcription, and the failure modes we hit when deploying on real phone calls. Along the way, we'll cover the statistical and learned approaches we developed to handle these challenges, and how we think about evaluating ASR in a way that actually reflects agent performance.

speakers.name: Max Lu
speakers.bio: Max Lu brings an extensive background in AI, machine learning, and data-science engineering experience to the GTC program. As head of research at Decagon, he oversees efforts to build the leading conversational AI agents for concierge customer experiences. He is also a co-founder of CodeComplete AI, an AI-powered coding assistant designed for enterprise. Previously, he led ranking and recommendations teams at Facebook and Quora. He earned his computer science degree at Harvard University.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Text Generation

sessions.title: The New Compute Paradigm: Adapting Infrastructure for the Inference Era
sessions.abstract: As inference becomes a larger fraction of total global compute needs, “total cost of ownership” (TCO) and “cost per token” have displaced raw pre-training performance as the dominant metrics for production-grade LLMs. This session will deconstruct the key hardware and software constraints that affect inference tokenomics. Join us to learn directly from the architect behind the world’s most complex compute buildout.

speakers.name: Sachin Katti
speakers.bio: Sachin Katti is head of industrial compute at OpenAI, responsible for the strategy and execution of the compute infrastructure that powers frontier model training and global-scale inference. He previously served as chief technology and AI officer at Intel, where he led the company’s AI strategy and technology direction. Sachin has also been a professor of electrical engineering and computer science (EECS) at Stanford University since 2010, where his research has focused on next-generation networks and systems. He received his Ph.D. in EECS from MIT in 2009.

sessions.title: Scaling Deep Tabular: 360K Inferences per Second per GPU
sessions.abstract:
Criteo operates a real-time bidding and recommendation platform that processes over 500 billion requests per day under a strict 20ms latency budget, powered by dozens of production models. 

This session presents our transition from CPU-based, “classical” machine learning to GPU-accelerated Deep Tabular architectures designed as modular, drop-in replacements, enabling deep learning transition without a full platform rewrite.  We will share key architectural choices and dive into inference optimizations required to meet extreme throughput and latency constraints, including client-side batching, Tensor fusion, and resolving bottlenecks in Triton dynamic batching and serving layers. Learn how this transition delivered a 5% business uplift and established the technical foundation for transformer-based, sequence-driven commerce modeling.

speakers.name: Nicolas Chrysanthos
speakers.bio: Nicolas Chrysanthos is an engineering director at Criteo in Paris, leading teams building large-scale machine learning systems for performance advertising. After a Ph.D. in ML applied to the aerospace industry, he founded a startup building recommendation systems for music before joining Criteo. He has over a decade of experience applying advanced ML, privacy-preserving optimization, and high-performance engineering to real-world ad-tech problems. His work focuses on bridging research and production, from bidding algorithms to experimentation platforms at web scale.

speakers.name: Julien Cuquemelle
speakers.bio: Julien Cuquemelle is a senior site reliability engineer at Criteo in Paris, operating at the intersection of large‑scale infrastructure and data‑intensive machine learning. Drawing on a background in robotics, AI, and high performance computing, he brings deep experience in algorithmic profiling, multicore and GPU architectures, and distributed data systems such as Hadoop and Spark. At Criteo, he serves as a bridge between hardware efficiency and ML, helping engineering teams turn model requirements into scalable, cost‑effective training and inference pipelines. He focuses on building reliable, low‑latency platforms that keep mission‑critical systems performant at scale while enabling ML engineers to fully harness modern hardware.

sessions.title: Agentic AI 101
sessions.abstract:
Agentic AI is evolving rapidly, shifting AI systems from passive responders to active systems that can plan, reason, and take action. In this session, we break down what agentic AI is, how the space has changed in the last year, and how both frontier models and open models are enabling a new class of AI applications.

You will learn the core components of agentic systems, where different model types fit, and practical ways to get started building agents today. This session is designed to give developers and technical leaders a clear mental model for agentic AI and a concrete path from experimentation to real applications.

speakers.name: Erik Pounds
speakers.bio: Erik Pounds is a senior director of product marketing at NVIDIA, where he’s focused on generative AI and agentic AI software. Prior to NVIDIA, Erik led marketing at SwiftStack, and he has held numerous leadership roles in product management throughout his career at companies including EMC, Brocade, Drobo, and BitTorrent.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Transform Data Into Intelligence: How AI and Open Models are Shaping the Future of Financial Services
sessions.abstract: AI is transforming the world’s financial institutions from systems of record into systems of intelligence. In this session, we’ll explore why banks, asset managers, and payments firms are building AI “factories” that transform transactional exhaust into high‑value signals, powering everything from profitable trading and precision risk management to hyper‑personalized customer experiences and proactive fraud detection. We’ll discuss emerging patterns for domain‑adapted models, AI agents embedded in core banking and payments workflows, and the governance required to scale safely in a highly regulated environment. You'll leave with a practical roadmap for partnering with NVIDIA’s platforms and ecosystem to build AI‑native financial services that learn continuously and compete on intelligence, not just balance sheet size.

speakers.name: Yogesh Agrawal
speakers.bio:
Yogesh Agrawal leads worldwide business for NVIDIA's GPUs and AI solutions in enterprise data centers. He also leads NVIDIA’s business in financial services and telecommunication industries.

Before joining NVIDIA, Yogesh held vice president/general manager roles in the storage divisions of HPE, EMC, and Symantec. In these roles, he led global teams across product management and engineering. Before all that, Yogesh led alliances and business development with global partners at Veritas. 

Yogesh also co-founded Lanworth, a natural resource intelligence firm, and spent six years in engineering positions at Silicon Graphics. Yogesh holds an M.B.A. from Kellogg School of Management and a master’s in computer science from Iowa State University.

sessions.title: NVIDIA Vera: Reimagining CPUs for Agents, Reasoning, and What’s Next
sessions.abstract:
As AI workflows expand across training, inference, analytics, and reasoning-driven applications, CPUs play a growing role in AI factory performance—from orchestration and application execution to compute- and memory-intensive processing.

This session introduces NVIDIA Vera, a next-generation CPU architecture built for these demands. We'll cover the workload trends shaping CPU design and key Vera technologies, including the custom Olympus core, high-bandwidth LPDDR5X memory subsystem, and NVIDIA Scalable Coherency Fabric. Learn how Vera's architecture is designed to maximize CPU performance and efficiency across the AI factory.

speakers.name: Ian Finder
speakers.bio:
Ian Finder is a group product manager at NVIDIA, where he is responsible for NVIDIA’s data center CPU product lines, including the Grace and Vera chips—which are available as both standalone compute products and with GPU acceleration in platforms like Vera Rubin and Grace Blackwell.

Previously, Ian worked for Microsoft on Azure, where he led product, technical program management, and platform architecture for GPU and accelerated virtual machines—incubating some of the industry’s earliest accelerated AI programs, and delivering the first cloud infrastructure as a service offerings to rank among the world’s top 10 supercomputers. 

Ian holds a bachelor’s degree in computer engineering from the University of Washington Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering.

sessions.title: Accelerating Scientific Discovery Through Global Innovation
sessions.abstract: Join Undersecretary Dario Gil for a forward-looking update on the Genesis Mission—a bold initiative redefining how science, technology, and global collaboration drive scientific discovery. This session will showcase early successes of NVIDIA’s long-standing collaborations with the U.S. Department of Energy and ongoing work with leading research centers such as the Argonne, Los Alamos, and Oak Ridge national laboratories. Discover how these collaborations are advancing breakthroughs across energy, quantum technology, and materials science—and learn how developers, researchers, and business leaders alike can help shape the next frontier of scientific innovation.

speakers.name: Dario Gil
speakers.bio:
Darío Gil is under secretary for science at the U.S. Department of Energy. His office is the nation's largest federal sponsor of basic research in the physical sciences, supporting all 17 national laboratories of the United States, and responsible for programs including advanced computing, fusion, nuclear and high energy particle physics, basic energy sciences, and biological and environmental research. He is the department’s principal adviser on science and technology. 

Prior to his current position, Darío was IBM senior vice president and director of research, where he was responsible for one of the world’s largest and most influential corporate research labs. 

Darío was elected to the National Academy of Engineering “for his contributions to artificial intelligence and quantum computing,” and is a globally recognized leader of the quantum industry. Under his leadership, IBM was the first company in the world to build programmable quantum computers and make them universally available through the cloud. He is an inventor and an institutional innovator, the force behind the creation of the International Science Reserve, the AI Alliance, the MIT-IBM Watson AI Lab, and the COVID-19 High Performance Computing Consortium. 

Darío has served on the President’s Council of Science and Technology Advisors and on the National Science Board, where he was first member from industry to be elected chair in 30 years. He has served on numerous boards including the Center for Strategic and International Studies, the New York Academy of Sciences, the Semiconductor Industry Association, the New York Hall of Science, and Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute. He's the recipient of two honorary doctorates and received his Ph.D. in electrical engineering and computer science from MIT.

speakers.name: Ian Buck
speakers.bio: Ian is the vice president and general manager of NVIDIA's Accelerated Computing Data Center business and the inventor of CUDA, which is the established standard for GPU computing worldwide. CUDA powers the deep learning AI revolution used by Google, Facebook, Amazon, Alibaba, IBM, and others.

sessions.title: From Benchmark Silos to an Interoperable AI Evaluation Ecosystem
sessions.abstract: As agent benchmarks multiply, the AI research community is paying a growing integration tax, with each new benchmark requiring custom infrastructure and tooling. This session introduces Common Unified Benchmark Environments (CUBE), a universal benchmarking protocol built on MCP and Gym that defines shared APIs for tasks, benchmarks, packages, and registries. We explore CUBE’s design and show how it integrates with existing evaluation workflows, including early reference integrations validated in collaboration with NVIDIA using NeMo Gym and NeMo Evaluator. Learn how to adopt CUBE, integrate it into your own tooling, and contribute to a more interoperable and scalable benchmarking ecosystem.

speakers.name: Alexandre Lacoste
speakers.bio: Alexandre is a senior staff research scientist leading the Long Horizon Agent team at ServiceNow AI Research. Since completing his Ph.D. in theoretical machine learning, he's published various influential works around foundation models, sequential decision-making, and causality, as well as various works in collaboration with Yoshua Bengio. Prior to ServiceNow, Alexandre was a research scientist at Element AI, and he worked for three years at Google. He's also a founding member of Climate Change AI.

sessions.title: Advancing Autonomous Vehicles With World Models
sessions.abstract: Simulation remains a cornerstone for accelerating robotics and autonomous vehicle (AV) development, enabling policies to be evaluated ahead of real-world deployment and drastically reducing development time. Achieving meaningful results requires simulators that capture both the environment and the world’s response to AI agents with high fidelity, across diverse and challenging scenarios. NVIDIA Cosmos has been specialized for autonomous vehicles, incorporating novel architecture changes and optimizations to deliver real-time simulation for the first time. In this talk, we explore the breakthrough that powers closed-loop evaluation of AV policies, empowering developers to iterate faster, test more rigorously, and bring safer, more robust autonomous systems to the real world.

speakers.name: Sanja Fidler
speakers.bio: Sanja Fidler is an associate professor at the Department of Computer Science, University of Toronto, Ontario, which she joined in 2014. Sanja is also the vice president of AI research at NVIDIA. Previously, she was a research assistant professor at TTI-Chicago, a philanthropically endowed academic institute located on the campus of the University of Chicago, Illinois.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation

sessions.title: Open, Trusted, and Observable: Deploying AI Agents at Enterprise Scale
sessions.abstract: As AI agents move from prototype to production, enterprises face a critical challenge: how do you deploy agentic systems at scale while maintaining trust, visibility, and control? This session explores how organizations are building production-grade agents using LangSmith—the commercial platform for agent observability, evaluation, and deployment from the LangChain team—combined with the NVIDIA AI-Q blueprint. Learn how an open, trusted approach, paired with end-to-end tracing, enables teams to debug complex agent behaviors, drive optimizations across the full stack from model selection to infrastructure, and confidently deploy agents in safe, governed production environments.

speakers.name: Harrison Chase
speakers.bio: Harrison Chase is the CEO and co-founder of LangChain, a company formed around the popular open-source Python/Typescript packages. Prior to starting LangChain, he led the ML team at Robust Intelligence (an MLOps company focused on testing and validation of machine learning models), led the entity linking team at Kensho (a FinTech startup), and studied statistics and computer science at Harvard University.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Building the Future of Music and AI, With Sir Lucian Grainge From Universal Music Group
sessions.abstract: Join leaders from NVIDIA and Universal Music Group for a conversation exploring how technology innovation accelerated by artificial intelligence is reshaping the future of music and creative industries. The history of music transformation through technology over the course of one of the music industry's most influential careers will set the stage, with details about Sir Lucian’s experience engaging with artists on tech innovation and a focus on his vision for an AI partnership where technology and creativity can work together at scale. Moderated by Richard Kerris, general manager of Media2 at NVIDIA, former CTO of Lucasfilm, and previous global lead for Apple’s entertainment industry developer ecosystem, the discussion will offer candid insights and real-world use cases on how collaboration between tech and culture leaders is defining the next era of entertainment.

speakers.name: Richard Kerris
speakers.bio: Richard is the vice president and general manager of media & entertainment at NVIDIA, where he's responsible for industry strategy, business development, product management, developer relations, and marketing. At Alias/Wavefront, Apple, Lucasfilm, and other brands, Richard drove product development and marketing. At Alias/Wavefront, he was director of Maya technologies and led the teams that brought the groundbreaking 3D software to manufacturers and new platforms. At Apple, he was senior director of developer relations and managed the ProApps teams for Final Cut Pro, Logic, and Aperture. At Lucasfilm, he was CTO. He's a member of the Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers.

speakers.name: Sir Lucian Grainge
speakers.bio:
Sir Lucian has transformed Universal Music Group (UMG) into the most successful company in the history of the music industry by pioneering new approaches to signing and developing the most successful recording artists and songwriters and championing innovative business models with a wide range of media and technology partners. For more than two decades, UMG has been the No. 1 music company in the world.
 
Over the span of four decades at UMG, Sir Lucian has signed and worked with many worldwide stars, including ABBA, Andrea Bocelli, Sabrina Carpenter, Eminem, Elton John, Lady Gaga, Kendrick Lamar, The Rolling Stones, Taylor Swift, U2, and Amy Winehouse, among many others. In 2011, he led UMG’s successful acquisition of the recorded music assets of the legendary British music company EMI, revitalizing its iconic Capitol Records and, in the process, further strengthening UMG’s position as the global leader in music. 
 
Billboard magazine has listed Sir Lucian a record eight times at No. 1 on the publication’s annual Power 100 list of the most influential music executives, and Variety magazine has ranked him among the top 10 most powerful executives in the global entertainment industry. A native of London, Sir Lucian was bestowed with a knighthood in 2016 by Her Majesty Queen Elizabeth II in the Queen’s 90th Birthday Honours list for accomplishments in the music industry and leadership through its challenging times, contributions to British business and inward investment, as well as his development of innovative business models, technology, and media partnerships that have expanded UMG’s global presence. In 2020, Sir Lucian received a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame.
 
Sir Lucian lives in Los Angeles with his wife and has three children. He serves on the board of Northeastern University in Boston, Massachusetts.

sessions.topic: Content Creation / Rendering - Audio / Music Production & Post-Production

sessions.title: Expression About Expression: When AI Looks in the Mirror
sessions.abstract: Despite fears of replacement, we may be entering a new wave of human creativity. AI systems increasingly generate content that comments on themselves or reveals emergent behavior. What happens when human judgment and machine intelligence blend together? This panel looks at emerging forms of expression that arise from human–AI collaboration, including outputs that feel unfamiliar or self‑reflective.

speakers.name: Shari Reich
speakers.bio:
Shari Reich leads Developer Relations for Audio & Music within NVIDIA’s Media2 organization, where she accelerates collaboration between developers, researchers, and creators across the AI audio ecosystem.

Before NVIDIA, Shari spent more than a decade in the music industry across Artists & Repertoire, creative development, and music supervision. Her background spans the Sony and Warner ecosystems and later Concord, grounding her technical advocacy in real production needs and artist-first principles. She also managed artists and producers and led music supervision for TV and film at companies such as Nickelodeon, bridging creative and operational realities from studio to screen.

speakers.name: Grimes
speakers.bio:
Grimes is a multidisciplinary artist who came to prominence with her album visions, which she produced herself in GarageBand. This was followed by the critically acclaimed album "Art Angels" and the critically disliked (but much better-performing album) "Miss Anthropocene," which were also both fully self-produced. Some of her other artistic pursuits include scoring the Emmy-winning opening credits of the Netflix series "Hilda" and voicing a pop-star cyborg in the video game "Cyberpunk 2077." As a live act, she started out with many years of van touring, but has worked her way up to a festival headliner. 

Grimes is also a visual artist, often directing her music videos. She’s had one of the most successful NFT drops of all time and is known for her work in the digital art space. Her work with the AI music generation app Endel won the Apple Watch app of the year in 2020. A provocateur and philosopher, Grimes has been both criticized and lauded for her theories on AI, futurism, and luxury communism, among other topics.

speakers.name: Matt Zien
speakers.bio:
Matt Zien is a filmmaker and executive producer working at the intersection of cinema and technology. With 15 years in premium nonfiction, he was the founding creative executive and senior vice president of development at The Intellectual Property Corp., developing/producing Emmy-winning television through the company's $350 million acquisition by Sony Pictures Television. 

As the founder of KNGMKR Labs, Matt has since established himself as a pioneer in AI-native filmmaking, with work receiving tens of millions of views and winning international awards across Europe, Asia, and the United States. He is the co-creator of "The Tutorial with Grimes," a series about using AI in the creative process (currently in production), and his most recent feature doc, "Slauson Rec," premiered at the Cannes Film Festival in 2025. Matt has been a keynote speaker at Microsoft MLADS and Artist and the Machine on AI and creativity, and is currently in production on multiple AI-assisted series with major networks.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Video Generation

sessions.title: Operationalizing AI at Scale: NVIDIA’s End‑to‑End Journey to an Enterprise AI Factory
sessions.abstract: Learn how NVIDIA’s IT organization engineered an on‑premises AI factory to deliver agentic AI with enterprise‑grade security, reliability, and governance. We’ll walk through the end-to-end technical stack behind NVIDIA’s internal AI platform, from our on-prem enterprise AI factory infrastructure to containerized agentic applications. This includes scalable inference, secure enterprise data access, agent development and evaluation, standardized agent interaction, memory, runtime execution, and data flywheels for continuous improvement. You’ll see how NVIDIA has deployed AI agents for the chip design, IT, supply chain, and developer and employee productivity domains.

speakers.name: Nic Borensztein
speakers.bio: Nic Borensztein is a generative AI-focused software engineer, leader, and serial entrepreneur based in the San Francisco Bay Area. He is the lead solution architect for Astra, NVIDIA's internal enterprise AI factory. Previously, he was the founder/CTO of CrowdAI, a no-code computer-vision platform.

speakers.name: Rama Akkiraju
speakers.bio: Rama Akkiraju, vice president of enterprise AI and automation at NVIDIA, is a former IBM Fellow, master inventor, and CTO of IBM Watson AI Ops. She co-authored more than 100 papers, holds over 50 patents, and received multiple awards, including Forbes's "Top 20 Women in AI Research" (2017), the University of California Berkeley's Athena Award (2020), and AI Industry Leader of the Year 2022, awarded by the Women Leaders in Data & AI organization.

speakers.name: Ashwin Jha
speakers.bio:
Senior engineering leader focused on building platforms that improve how enterprises work. Leads global teams across enterprise applications, AI-enabled workflows, developer productivity, cloud platforms, and data infrastructure.
Drives automation, reliability, and operational clarity at scale. Has led enterprise AI rollouts, modern data platforms, and cost governance programs with measurable impact. Focused on turning internal systems into durable, high-leverage foundations that help teams move faster with less friction.

sessions.title: AI Agents at Work for Retail: How Leading Brands are Getting Real Results in Store Operations
sessions.abstract:
The retail AI hype cycle is over—now it’s about results. Leading retailers are moving beyond experimental chatbots to deploy AI agents that complete work end to end. From resolving maintenance tickets and managing in-store promotions to routing human resources cases and handling customer issues without human hand-offs, these agents drive bottom-line savings and top-line growth.

In this session, we’ll explore the inflection point retail faces with AI—why those who move now will lead, and what sets successful adopters apart. See how ServiceNow customers deploy AI agents, powered by NVIDIA’s AI infrastructure, to boost efficiency and deliver results. We’ll share what’s working and how AI agents drive workflow automation that solves challenges in store operations, promotions, and service—turning talk into tangible results.

speakers.name: Kylie Harris
speakers.bio: Kylie Harris is head of retail innovation and solutions, go-to-market at ServiceNow, where she drives AI-powered transformation across the retail and hospitality sectors globally. A rare blend of a deep retail practitioner and holistic enterprise technology strategist, Kylie brings authentic industry credibility from leading digital transformation inside retail operations before joining ServiceNow. As a trusted adviser and retail thought leader, she specializes in translating emerging AI capabilities into tangible business outcomes—developing comprehensive frameworks and industry-specific use cases that help retailers harness intelligent automation for competitive advantage. Kylie combines strategic vision with hands-on delivery experience to accelerate AI adoption and innovation in retail.

speakers.name: Lorraine Bacon
speakers.bio: Lorraine Bacon is the Americas head of retail and hospitality solution consulting at ServiceNow, where she partners with global brands to unlock business value through AI-powered digital transformation. Specializing in generative and agentic AI, Lorraine translates complex technology into strategic outcomes—streamlining operations, elevating customer experiences, and reimagining enterprise workflows. She's a trusted adviser to leading retailers navigating the future of work and customer engagement.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Digital Twins at Scale: The New Retail Growth Infrastructure
sessions.abstract: Retail brands are under pressure to produce high-quality content at unprecedented speed and scale without increasing costs, and this session demonstrates how AI-powered digital twin technology built with NVIDIA Omniverse addresses that challenge. By transforming physical products into hyper-realistic, physics-accurate virtual assets, retailers can instantly deploy them across marketing, advertising, and e-commerce. Using robotic capture systems pre-trained with NVIDIA Isaac Sim and AI lighting agents trained with NVIDIA Kaolin, products are converted into production-grade digital twins with optimized illumination and material realism. Real-world case studies illustrate how workflows built with Omniverse enable mass content creation, synthetic data generation, and real-time rendering—replacing traditional photoshoots with scalable, AI-driven pipelines that deliver measurable improvements in speed, efficiency, and performance.

speakers.name: Morgan Mao
speakers.bio:
Morgan Mao is the global chief executive officer of SKAI Intelligence and a veteran entrepreneur dedicated to scaling the frontiers of AI and digital commerce. As a co-founder of 1updigital and a strategic investor in pioneers like FancyTech and D1M, Morgan has a proven track record of identifying "what’s next."

Currently, he leads SKAI Intelligence in pioneering NVIDIA Omniverse-powered 3D AI solutions. By bridging the gap between physical retail and high-fidelity digital twins, his team is replacing traditional photo production with automated, data-driven content pipelines that empower brands to scale visual commerce at the speed of AI.

sessions.title: Science at the Speed of Light: The Genesis Mission Across DoE Labs
sessions.abstract: Panelists will discuss the key areas of the Genesis Mission and how the private-public partnership is working, including such key areas as energy, quantum, and open models.

speakers.name: Chris Ritter
speakers.bio:
Chris Ritter is the division director of scientific computing and AI and director of the Digital Innovation Center of Excellence at Idaho National Laboratory (INL). His team of around 100 computational and data scientists are changing the world’s future with AI/ML, digital twinning, digital engineering, multi-physics, HPC, and digital thread technologies across a portfolio of nuclear energy, non-proliferation, semiconductor, and defense applications.

Chris founded INL's nationally leading digital engineering team, which grew from zero to over 57 of the nation’s top researchers and developers in digital science. His team led the first nuclear reactor digital twin, built the Deep Lynx open digital thread platform, developed the first autonomous non-nuclear microreactor, and is actively building multiple petabyte-scale digital thread platforms. Additionally, he was co-president of Newcomers in Leadership and chief digital officer of SMART USA. Prior to INL, Chris co-founded and led development of the No. 1 cloud-native model-based systems engineering tool, Innoslate, used in 107 counties across 2,000 companies around the world.

speakers.name: Rick Stevens
speakers.bio: Rick Stevens is the associate laboratory director for computing, environment, and life sciences at Argonne National Laboratory, and a professor of computer science at the University of Chicago. Rick is interested in developing innovative tools and techniques that enable computational scientists to solve important large-scale problems effectively on advanced scientific computers. His research focuses on three principal areas: advanced collaboration and visualization environments, HPC architectures including grids, and computational problems in the life sciences. Rick teaches courses on computer architecture, collaboration technology, virtual reality, parallel computing, and computational science.

speakers.name: Brian Spears
speakers.bio: Brian Spears is a principal investigator at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory. Brian leads large-scale research in deep learning for inertial confinement fusion and physical sciences. His team develops novel deep learning architectures for challenging physics problems, and trains these models as enormous scale using the world's largest supercomputers.

speakers.name: Gina Tourasi
speakers.bio:
Georgia Tourassi is the associate laboratory director of the Computing and Computational Sciences Directorate at the Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL). Concurrently, she holds appointments as an adjunct professor of radiology at Duke University and as a joint UT-ORNL professor of the Bredesen Center Data Science Program at the University of Tennessee at Knoxville. Under her leadership, the Oak Ridge Leadership Computing Facility delivered Frontier in 2022, the world’s first exascale computing system dedicated to open science.

Her scholarly work includes 13 U.S. patents and innovation disclosures, two R&D 100 awards, and more than 260 peer-reviewed journal articles, conference proceedings articles, editorials, and book chapters.  She is an elected Fellow of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers, the American Institute of Medical and Biological Engineering, the American Association of Medical Physicists, the International Society for Optics and Photonics, and the American Association for the Advancement of Science.

Gina's research interests include high performance computing and artificial intelligence in biomedicine. For her leadership in the Joint Design of Advanced Computing Solutions for Cancer initiative, she received the DoE Secretary’s Appreciation Award in 2016. The U.S. Department of Energy (DoE) has also recognized Gina's contributions to HPC leadership with four DoE Secretary's Honor Awards for her contributions to the COVID-19 Insights Partnership Team (2021), the COVID-19 HPC Resource Team (2021), Driving U.S. Competitiveness and Innovation in HPC/AI team (2022), and the Frontier project (2024). Locally, she has been honored by the YWCA Knoxville for STEM mentorship of underrepresented groups and the UT-Battelle Distinguished Researcher Award and the ORNL Director’s Award for Outstanding Individual Accomplishment in Science and Technology in 2017.

speakers.name: John Josephakis
speakers.bio: John Josephakis is global vice president of sales and business development for HPC/Supercomputing at NVIDIA. He has more than 20 years’ experience in high performance computing, with a career that started in the labs at the U.S. Department of Energy. Prior to NVIDIA, he ran sales and operations for Cray. He holds a bachelor’s degree in economics and mechanical engineering from the University of Texas and an M.B.A. from Saint Edwards University.

sessions.title: Building Safer Physical AI: Certification, Cybersecurity, and Agentic Control
sessions.abstract:
Safety, cybersecurity, and AI safety are interconnected mission-critical properties of physical AI. As autonomous systems move into the real world, they require a foundation of trust spanning from sensors and hardware platforms to operating systems, reasoning models, and agentic functions. 

Join us to explore the NVIDIA Halos safety framework and learn how to architect certifiable systems for Level 4 autonomous vehicles and robotics. We’ll examine the design of robust hardware and software safety foundations—integrating hardware platform and operating system—to ensure rigorous safety and systematic cybersecurity by design across the entire stack.  You'll learn how to leverage NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion for automotive, and NVIDIA IGX for robotics, to implement advanced inside-out and outside-in functional safety use cases for scalable autonomy.

speakers.name: Riccardo Mariani
speakers.bio: Riccardo Mariani is the vice president of industry safety at NVIDIA and leads the NVIDIA Halos initiative. He's responsible for developing cohesive safety strategies and cross-segment safety processes, architecture, and products that can be leveraged across NVIDIA’s artificial intelligence-based hardware and software platforms.

speakers.name: Akshay Sharan
speakers.bio: Akshay Sharan works at the intersection of high-reliability AI systems and Security. At NVIDIA, Akshay has spent the past decade building ‘chip-to-cloud’ security architectures for cyber-physical AI systems. Leveraging his experience in both traditional embedded security and adversarial Al/ML security, Akshay helps ensure the world’s most complex, safety-relevant systems are trustworthy and robust against adversaries.

sessions.title: Continuous Inference Benchmarking of the AI Frontier
sessions.abstract:
As large language model architectures evolve, traditional peak performance metrics are becoming less predictive of real-world efficiency. Effective inference benchmarking now requires a multidimensional approach that balances raw throughput against energy consumption, cost-per-token, and the critical "interactivity" thresholds required for user-facing applications.

This session introduces InferenceX, SemiAnalysis’ open-source benchmark suite designed to navigate this complexity by sweeping the latest software optimizations across leading AI accelerators. It moves beyond static datasheets to examine nightly, reproducible results that reveal how frontier models perform under variable production workloads. Learn how the InferenceX benchmarks are designed, how to run them, and how to interpret the results to make informed decisions.

speakers.name: Cameron Quilici
speakers.bio: Cam Quilici is a member of the technical staff at SemiAnalysis, focused on performance engineering. He co-leads inference benchmarking for the InferenceX project, bringing hands-on experience from his time on MLPerf committee. Previously, Cam was an ML engineer at DeterminedAI, and later HPE, where he designed software to run machine learning workloads at scale on large supercomputers. Cam holds a bachelor's degree in computer science and mathematics Texas A&M University.

speakers.name: Dylan Patel
speakers.bio: Dylan is the founder, CEO, and chief analyst of SemiAnalysis—the preeminent authority on all things AI and semiconductors. Through Dylan’s unwavering commitment to excellence, he has built the firm from the ground up as the recognized authority on the semiconductor supply chain to the cloud ecosystem, machine learning models, and all things in between. Since 2020, SemiAnalysis has transformed its business from a solo venture into a cohesive and focused team to provide breaking news and in-depth analysis for the most strategic, complex, and escalating challenges in the semiconductor industry.

speakers.name: Kimbo Chen
speakers.bio:
Kimbo Chen is an analyst and member of Technical Staff at SemiAnalysis, focusing on machine learning, LLMs, and the evolving GPU hardware stack. With a background as a technical staff engineer in AI systems, he brings hands-on insight into model training, deployment, and the infrastructure powering frontier ML applications.

Kimbo holds a Bachelor’s in computer science from National Tsing Hua University and a Master’s in computer science from the University of California, Riverside.

sessions.title: GTC Live 2026 Keynote Pregame
sessions.abstract: Catch the excitement of the keynote pregame.  Be there for the live conversations between the hosts and industry leader panelists as they examine accelerated computing beyond AI, NVIDIA’s role as a full-stack infrastructure company, and the five-layer foundation behind one of the largest infrastructures build-outs in history — and the ecosystem required to deliver it worldwide.

speakers.name: Sarah Guo
speakers.bio: Sarah Guo is Founder, Conviction. Sarah Guo is a prominent Silicon Valley venture capitalist, founder of the AI-focused firm Conviction, and former general partner at Greylock. Known as one of the youngest partners in Greylock's history, she focuses on early-stage investments in AI, infrastructure, and software. She also co-hosts the No Priors AI podcast.

speakers.name: Alfred Lin
speakers.bio:
Alfred Lin is a Partner at Sequoia Capital, working with founders from idea to IPO and beyond. He partners with companies across various industries, including consumer marketplaces, fintech, robotics, and healthcare. He sits on the boards of Airbnb, DoorDash, Citadel Securities, Clay, Cobot, Formation Bio, Found, Kalshi, Nominal, Zipline, and more.
 
Before joining Sequoia in 2010, Alfred served as Chairman & COO of Zappos.com (AMZN), VP of Finance & Business Development of Tellme Networks (MSFT), and VP of Finance of LinkExchange (MSFT). Alfred also co-founded Venture Frogs, LLC, and invested in companies such as Ask Jeeves (IACI), MongoMusic (MSFT), and OpenTable (PCLN).

Alfred has a B.A. in Applied Mathematics from Harvard and an M.S. in Statistics from Stanford. He also writes a weekly column about company-building frameworks and principles that have helped founders stay on the Outlier’s Path.

sessions.title: Co-Engineering Grace CPU Platforms for Hyperscale
sessions.abstract: Meta operates one of the world’s largest and most diverse computing fleets, spanning AI training and inference, web services, ranking, caching, analytics, and video processing. In this technical session, leaders from Meta and NVIDIA share how they co-engineer NVIDIA Grace CPU–based platforms end‑to‑end to meet the performance, efficiency, and scale demands of hyperscale fleets. You’ll see how joint work across silicon, platform design, and infrastructure integration improves performance per watt, rack density, and overall fleet efficiency, and how these platforms enable the next generation of hyperscale infrastructure for AI and large‑scale services.

speakers.name: Abhishek Dhanotia
speakers.bio: Abhishek Dhanotia is a technical leader in the Infrastructure organization at Meta, where he leads hardware and software co-design for Meta’s next-generation compute platforms. His work bridges advanced hardware and scalable software, accelerating innovation in Meta’s compute systems.

speakers.name: Shobhit Kanaujia
speakers.bio: Shobhit leads the AI and Systems Co-Design organization at Meta responsible for research and development across AI, compute, and storage, with a focus on performance, efficiency, and scalability.

speakers.name: Ian Finder
speakers.bio:
Ian Finder is a group product manager at NVIDIA, where he is responsible for NVIDIA’s data center CPU product lines, including the Grace and Vera chips—which are available as both standalone compute products and with GPU acceleration in platforms like Vera Rubin and Grace Blackwell.

Previously, Ian worked for Microsoft on Azure, where he led product, technical program management, and platform architecture for GPU and accelerated virtual machines—incubating some of the industry’s earliest accelerated AI programs, and delivering the first cloud infrastructure as a service offerings to rank among the world’s top 10 supercomputers. 

Ian holds a bachelor’s degree in computer engineering from the University of Washington Paul G. Allen School of Computer Science & Engineering.

sessions.title: Open Models: Where We Are and Where We’re Headed
sessions.abstract:
Doors open at 11:30 a.m. Session begins at 12:30 p.m.


One of the most important shifts in AI over the past year has been the rapid advancement of open frontier models. Open innovation is accelerating progress across every company and every industry, making it clear that AI will proliferate everywhere.


NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang hosts industry leaders for a candid discussion on the state of the art in open frontier models and what comes next.

speakers.name: Harrison Chase
speakers.bio: Harrison Chase is the CEO and co-founder of LangChain, a company formed around the popular open-source Python/Typescript packages. Prior to starting LangChain, he led the ML team at Robust Intelligence (an MLOps company focused on testing and validation of machine learning models), led the entity linking team at Kensho (a FinTech startup), and studied statistics and computer science at Harvard University.

speakers.name: Jensen Huang
speakers.bio: Jensen Huang founded NVIDIA in 1993 and has served as president, CEO, and member of the board of directors since its inception. Since its founding, NVIDIA has pioneered accelerated computing. The company's invention of the GPU in 1999 sparked the growth of the PC gaming market, redefined computer graphics, and ignited the era of modern AI. NVIDIA is now driving the platform shift of accelerated computing and generative AI, transforming the world's largest industries and profoundly impacting society.

speakers.name: Michael Truell
speakers.bio: Michael Truell is chief executive officer and co-founder of Cursor, the AI coding platform trusted by more than half of the Fortune 500. Since launching in 2023, Cursor has become one of the fastest-growing software companies in history, crossing $1 billion in annualized revenue and serving millions of developers at organizations including NVIDIA, Salesforce, and Deloitte.

speakers.name: Misha Laskin
speakers.bio:
Misha Laskin is the co-founder and CEO of Reflection, a frontier open model lab that has raised $2.13 billion to advance open intelligence and help shape the future of global AI. He co-founded the company with Ioannis Antonoglou, co-creator of AlphaGo and AlphaZero, and together they have assembled a team that contributed to major advances in modern AI, including OpenAI’s GPT and O models, PaLM, Gemini, AlphaGo, AlphaCode, and AlphaProof.

Prior to founding Reflection, Misha made foundational contributions in reinforcement learning at UC Berkeley and Google DeepMind, including work on the Gemini models. He trained in theoretical physics at Yale and the University of Chicago before transitioning into AI research.

speakers.name: Aravind Srinivas
speakers.bio: Aravind Srinivas is the CEO and Co-founder of Perplexity. Perplexity is the AI-powered answer engine that provides more than 10 billion secure and accurate answers per year to people, companies, finance professionals, shoppers, publishers, students, and decision-makers of all kinds. Prior to co-founding Perplexity, Aravind served as a research scientist at OpenAI, Google, and DeepMind. His investors and advisors include some of the world’s leading research scientists, top VC’s, and founders of some of the world‘s most transformational companies. Aravind holds a PhD in computer science from UC Berkeley and a Bachelor’s and Master’s in Electrical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology, Madras. You can find him on Twitter/X and LinkedIn.

speakers.name: Mira Murati
speakers.bio: Mira Murati is the founder and CEO of Thinking Machines Lab, an AI company focused on building accessible, customizable systems that collaborate closely with human experts. Prior to starting Thinking Machines, she served as CTO at OpenAI, where she oversaw research, product, and safety teams and led development of high-profile systems including ChatGPT, DALL·E, and GPT4. Earlier in her career, she held senior engineering and product roles at Tesla, contributing to Model X and early Autopilot efforts, and at Leap Motion, and she is widely recognized as one of the most influential technical leaders in modern AI.

speakers.name: Arthur Mensch
speakers.bio: Arthur Mensch is a French entrepreneur and scientist. In 2023, Arthur, along with Guillaume Lample and Timothée Lacroix, founded Mistral AI with the mission of making generative AI ubiquitous and pioneering a new approach to AI—one that is more open, portable, independent, and accessible to all.

speakers.name: Daniel Nadler
speakers.bio:
Daniel is the founder of OpenEvidence, a Sequoia-backed AI company valued at $3.5 billion used by over a quarter of doctors in the United States. In 2025, Daniel was named to the TIME100 list of the 100 Most Influential People in global health.

While still a Ph.D. student at Harvard, Daniel founded Kensho Technologies, an AI company that became the most valuable AI company of the 2010s when it was acquired by S&P Global for $700 million in 2018. 

Outside of technology, Daniel is an award-winning poet and visual artist; his debut collection of poetry, "Lacunae," was published in 2016 and named by NPR as one of the Best Books of the Year.

speakers.name: Hanna Hajishirzi
speakers.bio: Hanna Hajishirzi is the Torode Family Associate Professor in the Allen School at the University of Washington, where she leads the H2Lab, and a senior director of natural language processing at AI2. She received her PhD in computer science from University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, and spent a year as postdoctoral associate at Disney Research and Carnegie Mellon University.

speakers.name: Anjney Midha
speakers.bio:
Anjney Midha is a general partner at Andreessen Horowitz, where he invests in AI, infrastructure, and open-source technology. He serves on the boards of Black Forest Labs, Luma AI, Mistral AI, and Sesame AI.

A former founder and CEO, Anjney joined the firm in 2023. Immediately prior, Anjney was vice president of platform ecosystem at Discord, where he oversaw all developer products for the company’s 200 million users. In this role, Anjney set up Discord’s first dedicated developer platform organization, launched its partnership with Midjourney, and helped it scale to one of the world’s fastest-growing generative AI businesses.

speakers.name: Robin Rombach
speakers.bio: Robin is co-founder and chief executive officer of Black Forest Labs, a frontier AI research lab dedicated to advancing generative models for images and videos. Before founding Black Forest Labs, he was a research director at Stability AI and pursued his PhD at LMU Munich. Robin's academic journey began with a master's degree in physics at the University of Heidelberg (2013-20). His research has centered around making generative models more efficient, contributing to the development of widely adopted models such as VQGAN, Latent Diffusion, Stable Diffusion, and Flux. He is a proponent of open-source models.

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Computing on the Road: Scaling the Waymo Driver for the Real World
sessions.abstract: Autonomous ride-hailing is increasingly a daily reality for more people with Waymo. As the most mature manifestation of AI in the physical world, Waymo’s path has been defined by 200 million miles and over 20 million trips completed without a human specialist present. Drawing on this unparalleled scale of experience, we will explore Waymo’s approach to developing its own custom hardware, the vital role AI compute plays in enabling it, and how this integrated foundation makes our demonstrably safe AI architecture possible.

speakers.name: Daniel Rosenband
speakers.bio:
Daniel leads the Compute Team at Waymo.  His team delivers the high-performance, safe, and efficient compute platform that serves as the brain of the Waymo Driver.  This work encompasses the full stack, including compute system architecture and design, custom silicon development, performance software, and compiler development.  He is passionate about delivering the most capable compute for the Waymo Driver. 

Before joining Google's self-driving car project in 2013, Daniel co-founded Sandburst, a networking chips company now part of Broadcom.  He also served as an architect at Metaram designing innovative memory and storage technologies. Daniel holds a PhD, Master’s, and Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, all from MIT.

sessions.title: 从 M2 到 M2.5：以更小的 (Mini) 参数释放最大的 (Max) 普惠智能
sessions.abstract: 本次分享将系统介绍 MiniMax M2 系列如何在仅 10B 激活参数规模下，实现 SOTA 级别的 coding 与 agentic co-work 性能。我们将深入解析在训练资源受限条件下的模型架构取舍、agentic 数据与环境的大规模扩展方法，以及如何基于 NVIDIA 软硬件全栈能力实现高效训练、强化学习与高吞吐推理落地。

speakers.name: 鹏宇 赵
speakers.bio: 赵鹏宇，MiniMax 通用模型负责人，带领团队构建 M1-M2 系列模型。本科和研究员毕业于北京大学。

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Empowering Human Creativity: A Discussion With Paramount Skydance’s Dane Glasgow
sessions.abstract: Whether it was the transition from black-and-white to color or from linear to streaming, the entertainment industry is well-versed in innovation, transformation, and disruption. As content produced by AI models becomes more widespread, media companies face a unique set of issues as the industry works to protect its intellectual property and maintain the trust of its creatives and audiences. In a fireside chat with Paramount’s Chief Product Officer Dane Glasgow, we will hear how he and his company leverage new and emerging technologies in support of their employees and the creative community, while taking definitive measures to protect its IP and storytelling.

speakers.name: Richard Kerris
speakers.bio: Richard is the vice president and general manager of media & entertainment at NVIDIA, where he's responsible for industry strategy, business development, product management, developer relations, and marketing. At Alias/Wavefront, Apple, Lucasfilm, and other brands, Richard drove product development and marketing. At Alias/Wavefront, he was director of Maya technologies and led the teams that brought the groundbreaking 3D software to manufacturers and new platforms. At Apple, he was senior director of developer relations and managed the ProApps teams for Final Cut Pro, Logic, and Aperture. At Lucasfilm, he was CTO. He's a member of the Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers.

speakers.name: Dane Glasgow
speakers.bio:
Dane Glasgow is the chief product officer for Paramount, a Skydance corporation. He leads the company's long-term product vision and strategy, in addition to driving innovation across digital platforms, immersive storytelling, advertising, and AI-powered capabilities.
 
Dane is a seasoned senior technology executive and serial entrepreneur. Prior to Paramount, Dane was the vice president of product management for Facebook, where he led the product teams delivering Feed, Stories, Reels, Groups, Marketplace, Gaming, Ads, and more to over 3 billion monthly active users around the world. He joined Meta in 2021, and previously led Entertainment for Facebook. Prior to Meta, Dane held senior leadership positions in product, design, engineering, and general management at Google, eBay, and Microsoft. He has also launched successful businesses in entertainment, finance, and communications.
 
Dane attended Cornell University, where he majored in computer science before leaving at the beginning of his senior year to focus his energies on launching a startup.

sessions.topic: Content Creation / Rendering - Media Streaming / Data Streaming

sessions.title: Scale Marketing Content Automation With 3D Digital Twin and Generative AI
sessions.abstract:
As brands scale personalized campaigns across channels and regions, traditional production models break down. This session explores how teams automate content creation with 3D digital twins and generative AI—transforming physical products into reusable systems that power high-volume pipelines.

Learn how automated workflows generate packshots, composite imagery, and product configurations at scale, replacing manual photoshoots with cloud-native, production-ready content pipelines.

speakers.name: Guido Quaroni
speakers.bio:
Guido Quaroni is senior director of product and engineering for Adobe’s 3D & Immersive division. A 20-year Pixar veteran, he served as supervising technical director on films including "Monsters University" and "Toy Story 3," and later as vice president of software R&D, overseeing both Academy Award–winning technologies—the Presto Animation System and OpenUSD.

Guido joined Adobe in January 2021 to lead engineering for the Substance 3D product line, championing the expansion of 3D technologies across Adobe products and surfaces while driving innovation and interoperability. In December 2025, he expanded his role to oversee both product and engineering for the 3D & Immersive division.

speakers.name: Varun Parmar
speakers.bio:
Varun Parmar is the general manager of GenStudio and Firefly Enterprise at Adobe. These industry-defining, generative AI-first offerings enable marketing teams to quickly plan, create, manage, activate,and measure on-brand content and automate repetitive tasks while accelerating production of high-quality asset variations for different audiences, channels and markets, respectively. 

Previously, Varun was chief operating officer at Miro, a digital collaboration platform, where he led a global team across product management, engineering, design, customer engagement and partnerships. Earlier, he was chief product officer at Box, a leader in enterprise content management. Varun co-founded a document collaboration startup that was acquired by Box and held senior product remits at Dell EMC. Earlier in his career, Varun spent six years at Adobe, during which he focused on corporate strategy and product management for Adobe Acrobat
and Document Cloud. Varun graduated from MIT with a joint master’s degree in business and engineering.

sessions.topic: Content Creation / Rendering - Image / Photo Editing

sessions.title: Safety in the OpenClaw Era
sessions.abstract:
AI has crossed a threshold. Autonomous agents like OpenClaw, no longer just assist but hold credentials, persist context across sessions, spawn subagents, and execute continuously against production systems. That's a fundamentally different threat model than anything enterprise security was built to handle. The attack surface is real. Every prompt injection is a potential credential leak. Every third-party skill is an unreviewed binary. Every major enterprise platform is becoming an agent platform, and the governance infrastructure to match that reality doesn't exist at scale yet.
This panel brings together security and AI leaders to ask the hard questions: what does a real security model for autonomous agents look like? 
The agents are ready. The infrastructure to trust them is what we're here to discuss.

speakers.name: Elia Zaitsev
speakers.bio:
Elia Zaitsev leads CrowdStrike technology vision and strategy, notably driving the company’s data science, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and malware research functions. Elia was promoted to CrowdStrike’s global chief technology officer in 2023. He joined the Office of the CTO in 2020, after having led the Americas Sales Engineering organization. 

Elia joined CrowdStrike in 2013 as the company’s first sales engineer. Throughout his tenure, he has worked closely as a trusted adviser to CrowdStrike's largest customers while collaborating with the product and R&D teams to ensure continued customer success and safety through innovation and evolution of the Falcon platform.

speakers.name: Ali Golshan
speakers.bio: Ali Golshan is senior director of AI software at NVIDIA, driving partner-led go-to-market strategy for agentic AI products. Prior to joining NVIDIA, he was co-founder and CEO of Gretel (synthetic data for AI), co-founder and CTO of StackRox (Kubernetes security, acquired by Red Hat), and co-founder and CTO of Cyphort (security analytics, acquired by Juniper). He began his career in the U.S. intelligence community, designing secure data systems.

speakers.name: Amit Zavery
speakers.bio: As president, chief product officer, and chief operating officer, Amit Zavery leads ServiceNow’s AI platform, products, engineering, cloud infrastructure, and user experience, with a steadfast focus on innovation and customer value. He also runs enterprise-wide operations, digital technology, and security to ensure business rigor — from idea to execution. Amit has a proven track record of designing and delivering innovative and market-leading enterprise products, scaling businesses, and building organizations by recruiting and mentoring world-class talent.

speakers.name: Francis deSouza
speakers.bio:
Francis deSouza is the Chief Operating Officer and President of Security Products at Google Cloud. He leads strategic operations to scale Google Cloud for the next wave of growth through differentiated technologies, strategies, policies, processes and systems. Francis also leads Google Cloud’s world-class security portfolio, driving innovation across products and solutions, threat intelligence, consulting and governance. 

He joined Google in January 2025 after three decades as an engineer, technology executive, security expert, entrepreneur, board member and investor. Prior to Google, Francis was the CEO of Illumina, where he led the company's growth and innovation in DNA sequencing. He also served as President at Symantec, overseeing their security and data management portfolio. Francis also co-founded three companies: SynthLabs (an AI alignment company), IMlogic (an IT security company acquired by Symantec), and Flash Communications (an instant messaging company acquired by Microsoft).

sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Code / Software Generation

sessions.title: Build-a-Claw Event
sessions.abstract:
Stop by NVIDIA’s build-a-claw event in the GTC Park running all week to customize and deploy a proactive, always-on AI assistant.


With support from NVIDIA experts, GTC attendees can quickly have an AI agent at their fingertips. Whether technical or just curious, participants will name an agent, define its personality and grant it access to the tools it needs. Think of it as the personal assistant you’ve always wanted, reachable via your preferred messaged app.


To run the custom agent, bring your own NVIDIA DGX Spark. Or buy one at the Gear Store (SJCC street level) or MicroCenter pop-up (GTC Park).


To run the custom agent, use cloud compute provided onsite, or harness local accelerated computing by bringing your NVIDIA DGX Spark or GeForce RTX laptop, with no personal data on the device. NVIDIA hardware — including DGX Spark and Jetson modules — will also be available to buy on site at Gear Store (SJCC street level) and Micro Center (pop-up at GTC Park).


Hours of operation:

Monday: 8:00 am - 5:00 pm PT (except during keynote)

Tuesday: 8:00 am - 5:00 pm PT

Wednesday: 8:00 am - 5:00 pm PT

Thursday: 8:00 am - 5:00 pm PT


Location: GTC Park
sessions.topic: Agentic AI / Generative AI - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

sessions.title: Vibe Hack: Rapid Prototyping, From Idea to Reality
sessions.abstract:
Experience the power of "Vibe Coding"—where momentum drives creation. In this accessible three-hour session, we are removing complex roadmaps to focus on rapid ideation and execution. Whether you are a business leader, a student, or a seasoned developer, you will use NVIDIA cutting-edge AI models to turn concepts into working applications in real time. Collaborate with others, meet community members, and learn from others by building together. All teams will compete for the main prizes, with a special bounty category dedicated to best use of NVIDIA Nemotron models.

All entries are eligible to be featured in a Developer Showcase.

sessions.title: Live Agent Gauntlet
sessions.abstract:
Put your AI agent to the test in a competitive, benchmark-driven challenge. In this esports-style hackathon, teams are given the challenge in advance and have dedicated time to build and optimize their agents before the event.

On the day of the competition, teams will load their agents live and evaluate performance head-to-head on standardized benchmarks. Results will be tracked in real time on a public leaderboard, showcasing the strongest approaches and optimizations.

sessions.title: Watch Party: cuTile Programming
sessions.abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。

会议主持人：

Ken He, Developer Community Manager, NVIDIA

Express your algorithm; cuTile and Tile IR will figure out the threads, data movement, and Tensor cores. In this talk, we'll present cuTile, NVIDIA's new block-based programming abstraction, and Tile IR, the compiler stack that it is built with. We'll explore examples in C++ and Python from a variety of domains, including inference of a mixture-of-experts large language model and a sparse conjugate gradient solver. CUDA tile programming divides inputs into local arrays that are processed concurrently by groups of threads. Users write sequential array-centric code, and the framework handles parallelization, synchronization, and data movement behind the scenes. Architecture-specific details are abstracted away—you express your algorithm once in a high-level way, and the system lowers it to the right implementation for any NVIDIA GPU architecture.

  • Learn the fundamental concepts of the CUDA tile programming model.
  • Understand the differences between tile and SIMT programming, and when each paradigm should be used.
  • Gain insight into the performance of cuTile and Tile IR and how to tune it.
  • See how tile programming provides portability across NVIDIA GPU architectures.

在会议开始时间前 15 分钟，登录状态下，下方会出现一个【Join Watch Party Now】的链接。点击该链接，可以在您的电脑上或从您的网络浏览器中启动 Microsoft Teams 参加会议。

speakers.name: Bryce Lelbach
speakers.bio: Bryce Adelstein Lelbach has spent over a decade developing programming languages, compilers, and libraries. He leads programming language efforts and drives the technical roadmap for NVIDIA's HPC compilers and libraries. He's passionate about C++ and is one of the leaders of the C++ community, having served as chair of the C++ Library Evolution group. Outside of work, Bryce is passionate about airplanes and watches.

speakers.name: Yossi Bar
speakers.bio:
Yossi is the CEO and Founder of LEM Surgical and a seasoned leader in the area of surgical robotics (ex-R&D Director at Mazor Robotics; former COO/VP R&D at Radiaction medical).
He comes from a mechanical-engineering/Med-Tech R&D background and pursued further Philosophy and Management studies at Tel-Aviv and Reichman Universities.

speakers.name: Filippo Filicori
speakers.bio: Dr. Filicori is a robotic surgeon and System Chief of Surgical Innovation at Northwell Health, New York’s largest provider. He leads the Intra-operative Performance Analytics Laboratory in collaboration with Columbia Engineering, applying machine learning to outcomes research, computer vision, performance analytics, and simulation in robotic surgery. He is an Associate Professor of Surgery at Northwell School of Medicine. Dr. Filicori graduated cum laude from the University of Bologna, completed his Surgery Residency at Weill Cornell Medicine, and a Minimally Invasive Surgery Fellowship at Providence Portland Medical Center. He chairs the AI committee at SAGES.

speakers.name: Axel Krieger
speakers.bio: Axel Krieger is an Associate Professor and Carol Croft Linde Faculty Scholar at Johns Hopkins where he leads a team of students and engineers researching smart and autonomous robotic systems for surgery and interventions. He's a recipient of the NSF CAREER award and an inventor of over 30 patents and patent applications. Axel was an assistant professor in mechanical engineering at the University of Maryland and assistant research professor at Children’s National. He has several years of experience in private industry at Sentinelle Medical and Hologic. Axel completed his undergraduate and master’s degrees at the University of Karlsruhe in Germany and his doctorate at Johns Hopkins University.

speakers.name: David Noonan
speakers.bio: David Noonan, PhD, chief technology officer at Moon Surgical, which is building the Maestro System. Billed as the first AI-powered collaborative robot for soft-tissue laparoscopic surgery, Maestro earned a CE mark in 2023 and FDA clearance in March 2025. Before joining Moon in 2020, Noonan spent five years in engineering roles at Auris Health and over three years at Philips Research North America working on R&D for minimally invasive interventions. He has a  B.Eng in Mechatronics Engineering from Dublin City University, an M.Sc in Mechanical Engineering Research from King's College London, and a Ph.D. from Imperial College London. He is a named inventor on over 50 granted US patents.

sessions.topic: Developer Tools & Techniques - Programming Languages / Compilers

sessions.title: Watch Party: Physical AI for the Real World: A Vision From NVIDIA Robotics Research
sessions.abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。

会议主持人：

Sharron Liu, Solution Architect, NVIDIA

Billy Wang, Solution Architect, NVIDIA

One of the hardest problems in physical AI is transforming robots from task-specific machines to general-purpose, intelligent collaborators. NVIDIA Research is pioneering a unified AI- and simulation-driven approach to solve this challenge, working from theoretical advances all the way to high-performance deployment. Join this session to learn about research breakthroughs in sim-to-real, real-world learning, model architecture, and model training, as well as key milestones on the path to generalist embodied intelligence for real-world applications. Gain perspectives about where robotics is headed, what technical hurdles remain, and how the next wave of research innovation could unlock entirely new markets.

  • The future of robotics is a collaborative ecosystem where general-purpose humanoids and specialized traditional robots will coexist, driven by a shift from modular to end-to-end AI systems.
  • Scaling these intelligent robots depends on closing the ""sim-to-real"" gap until simulated training data becomes virtually indistinguishable from real-world data.
  • This advancement in physical AI will move robots from controlled settings into our daily lives, opening entirely new markets and applications.

在会议开始时间前 15 分钟，登录状态下，下方会出现一个【Join Watch Party Now】的链接。点击该链接，可以在您的电脑上或从您的网络浏览器中启动 Microsoft Teams 参加会议。

speakers.name: Ming-Yu Liu
speakers.bio: Ming-Yu Liu is a vice president of research at NVIDIA and a fellow of IEEE. He leads the Deep Imagination Research group at NVIDIA, which focuses on deep generative models and their applications in content creation. NVIDIA Edify, NVIDIA Canvas [GauGAN], and NVIDIA Maxine [LivePortrait] are three products research from his group enabled.

sessions.topic: Simulation / Modeling / Design - Synthetic Data Generation

sessions.title: Watch Party: 擴展規模與跨域：千兆級 AI 系統的網路創新
sessions.abstract:
本場 GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 團隊所主持，將由台灣的技術專家 Sungta Tsai 以中文講解並導讀國外精選演講，會中可以即時與講師互動、發問，歡迎您一起加入討論。(請注意：由於時區差異，本場講座的原講者可能不會參加。)

隨著 AI 工廠擴張至數十萬、甚至即將達數百萬互聯 GPU，網路已成為大規模 AI 的關鍵架構。要實現卓越的效能，不僅需要能夠在單一資料中心內垂直與水平擴展，還需要能以可預測的延遲、無損吞吐量與兼顧端到端韌性在多個資料中心間擴展。在本場次中，NVIDIA 將全面展示用於建構千兆級 AI 基礎架構的網路創新。瞭解網路技術的進步，包括超高基數交換機、採用共同封裝光學的新一代 NVIDIA Quantum-X InfiniBand 和 Spectrum-X 乙太網路平台以及優化的 SuperNIC 網路加速器，如何提供訓練海量基礎模型、部署即時推論以及在全球範圍內互連聯邦式 AI 工廠所需的效能

在演講開始前 15 分鐘，下方將出現「Join Watch Party Now」連結。請點擊該連結，並在電腦應用程式或網頁瀏覽器開啟 Microsoft Teams 以線上加入。

speakers.name: Gilad Shainer
speakers.bio: Gilad Shainer focuses on high performance computing and artificial intelligence. He holds multiple patents in the field of high-speed networking. Gilad Shainer holds an M.S. and a B.S. in electrical engineering from the Technion Institute of Technology in Israel.

sessions.title: Watch Party: Building the Future of Manufacturing
sessions.abstract:
A GTC Session Watch Party is a replay of an original GTC talk hosted by our NVIDIA Team. This is an interactive session and we encourage you to join the discussion with any comments or questions. Please note that the original speakers of the talk listed below may not be in attendance.

Hosted by:

Pankaeo Sihawong, Sales lead in Thailand, NVIDIA

 The manufacturing industry is entering a new era, driven by AI, robotics, and simulation. Join distinguished leaders from the semiconductor and electronics, industrial and process manufacturing, and robotics industries as they share their pioneering use cases and how these technologies enable them to innovate, reimagine production systems, and transform the design and operation of advanced manufacturing and warehouse facilities./p>

 A “Join Watch Party Now” link will appear below, 15 minutes before the session start time. Click on the link to launch Microsoft Teams on your computer or from your web browser.

speakers.name: Seungyong Doh
speakers.bio: Seungyong Doh is chief information officer in charge of digital transformation of SK hynix, which he joined in December 2020. Prior to that, he worked for Samsung SDS as a vice president in charge of smart factory business. He has experience in building smart factories in various manufacturing fields such as semiconductors, smartphones, batteries, etc. Before that, he worked as a software engineer on the Manufacturing Execution System in the semiconductor division of Samsung Electronics from September 2003 through 2011.

speakers.name: Zhaopeng Chen
speakers.bio: Zhaopeng Chen is the founder and chief executive officer of Agile Robots. Under his leadership, Agile Robots has experienced rapid global growth by combining artificial intelligence and robotics to advance physical AI for smart manufacturing.
