Headquartered in Munich, Agile Robots employs more than 2,500 people from 60 countries and has installed over 20,000 robotic solutions worldwide. Zhaopeng studied engineering in China and earned his PhD at the German Aerospace Center (DLR).

speakers.name: Rainer Brehm
speakers.bio:
Rainer Brehm is the Chief Operations Officer (COO) for the automation business and Chief Technology Officer (CTO) at Siemens Digital Industries. In these roles, he leads the development of visionary technologies—including Industrial AI and software defined automation—and drives the strategic evolution of Siemens’ automation portfolio. His work focuses on making manufacturing more adaptive, sustainable, and autonomous, guided by his overarching ambition to automate automation.
Before taking on his current responsibilities, Rainer served as CEO of Siemens Factory Automation and previously held several leadership positions within the company, including Vice President of Automation Products and Systems for Digital Industries. His international experience includes serving in Brazil as Division Head for Process Industries & Drives and Power & Gas.
Born and educated in Germany, Rainer holds a degree in electrical engineering.

speakers.name: Marc Segura
speakers.bio: Marc Segura is President of ABB Robotics, appointed in January 2022. He drives the development and deployment of Autonomous Versatile Robotics (AVR™), accelerating Physical AI to transform the future of industry and manufacturing. Since joining ABB in 1998, he has held multiple leadership roles and oversaw ABB Robotics’ expansion into logistics, healthcare, retail, and food & beverage. Under his leadership, ABB Robotics has become the only company with a fully integrated, AI‑powered portfolio spanning robots, cobots, and autonomous mobile robots. ABB Robotics employs approximately 7,000 people worldwide, with manufacturing sites in the United States, China, and Sweden.

speakers.name: Rev Lebaredian
speakers.bio: Rev Lebaredian is vice president of Omniverse and simulation technology at NVIDIA. Lebaredian has over 30 years of experience in the computer graphics industry, working in roles at Warner Brothers Digital and Disney Dream Quest Images before launching his own venture, Steamboat Software. He joined NVIDIA in 2002 and led the development of several real-time, multi-GPU scalable rendering, physics simulation, and AI platforms and technologies. Today, he leads the product, engineering, and research teams of NVIDIA Omniverse, a software platform and operating system enabling developers to build physical AI and robotic system simulation applications.

Alan Zhang, AI Enterprise BD, NVIDIA

Join distinguished leaders from the semiconductor and electronics, industrial and process manufacturing, and robotics industries as they share their pioneering use cases and how these technologies enable them to innovate, reimagine production systems, and transform the design and operation of advanced manufacturing and warehouse facilities.

  • Discover how manufacturing leaders are applying AI, robotics, and simulation to accelerate innovation and efficiency.
  • Learn how these technologies are reshaping the design, planning, and operation of next-generation manufacturing facilities.
  • Explore pioneering real-world use cases that demonstrate the power of intelligent automation and industrial digitalization in driving innovation and resilience.

speakers.name: Roslyn Stuart
speakers.bio:
Roslyn Stuart serves as the vice president of synergy creation at Hitachi Digital, where she leads and accelerates the establishment of advanced digital solutions across the green energy and mobility sectors. In this role, Roslyn works closely with Hitachi Energy and Hitachi Rail, uniting the technological strengths of Hitachi Digital Services and GlobalLogic to co-create next-generation innovations that strengthen Hitachi’s global digital ecosystem. This ecosystems includes building external partnerships working very closely with NVIDIA, and has launched HMAX accelerated by NVIDIA.

Prior to joining Hitachi Digital, Roslyn successfully ran Hitachi Rail in Australia. Recognized for deep cross-industry expertise, Roslyn was invited by Hitachi Ltd. to help shape the One-Hitachi business strategy, fostering collaboration between Hitachi Energy, Hitachi Rail, and the broader Digital & Services organizations—including Hitachi Digital Services, Hitachi Vantara, and GlobalLogic—to accelerate digital transformation for sustainable, profitable growth.

With over 20 years of global experience in systems engineering and digital transformation, Roslyn has delivered industry-defining programs—including leading Rio Tinto’s AutoHaul, the world’s first fully autonomous heavy-haul rail system, which was recognized in the 2020 Guinness Book of Records as the world’s heaviest robot.

Earlier in Roslyn’s career, she developed advanced through-water communication systems for naval customers across Australia, the U.K., and the United States. Roslyn is recognized for authentic leadership and a commitment to uniting global teams to drive innovation and societal impact.

sessions.title: Watch Party: 利用數位孿生和即時模擬加速物理 AI 時代
sessions.abstract:
本場 GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 團隊所主持，將由台灣的技術專家 Kuan-Ting Yeh 以中文講解並導讀國外精選演講，會中可以即時與講師互動、發問，歡迎您一起加入討論。(請注意：由於時區差異，本場講座的原講者可能不會參加。)

本場次將介紹由 OpenUSD 和 NVIDIA 加速運算平台、函式庫和生態系統支援的數位孿生和即時模擬技術，如何加速物理 AI (physical AI) 時代各產業的突破。瞭解它們對產品設計、製造、機器人、自動駕駛汽車、智慧城市、氣候科學和太空探索等各個領域的影響。

sessions.title: Watch Party: From Concept to Production: Humanoid Robotics at Scale
sessions.abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。

会议主持人：

Lex Yang, Solution Architect, NVIDIA

Jeff Li, Solution Architect, NVIDIA

Humanoid robots are evolving from prototypes to production-ready systems, powered by breakthroughs in reasoning AI, large-scale simulation, and real-time edge computing. But to get there, the industry must bridge the gap between digital intelligence and physical reality. Hear from luminaries at the world’s leading robotics companies on the future of physical AI, and what it will take to unlock truly capable humanoids.

  • How leading teams are training and deploying humanoid robots today, from data collection and simulation to real-world rollout
  • What technical stack is emerging for physical AI—covering foundation models, world models, and control systems—and how it generalizes across different robot platforms
  • Where the most promising real-world use cases, business opportunities, and open research problems lie for humanoids over the next three to five years

在会议开始时间前 15 分钟，登录状态下，下方会出现一个【Join Watch Party Now】的链接。点击该链接，可以在您的电脑上或从您的网络浏览器中启动 Microsoft Teams 参加会议。

speakers.name: Amit Goel
speakers.bio: Amit Goel is director of product management for autonomous machines at NVIDIA, where he leads the product development of NVIDIA Jetson and developer relations for the NVIDIA Isaac robotics platform. Amit holds an M.S. in electrical engineering from Arizona State University and an M.B.A. from the University of California at Berkeley.

speakers.name: Ashok Elluswamy
speakers.bio: Ashok Elluswamy is vice president of AI at Tesla. Ashok leads AI development for the Tesla self-driving software and Optimus, the humanoid robot. He was the first engineer hired onto Tesla’s AI team in 2014 and has been a key driver of the company’s autonomy strategy, including the shift to vision-only, end-to-end neural network–based driving systems.

speakers.name: Arnaud Robert
speakers.bio: Arnaud Robert is an international C-level executive with extensive experience in building and scaling businesses, launching global products, bringing innovation to market, and driving deep transformations—across six industries. Currently president for robotics at Hexagon, he was previously chief digital officer and Executive Committee member at Sanofi, CDO at Viking Cruises, and also worked at Disney, Nike, and Microsoft. Arnaud has a Ph.D. in computer science. He's received multiple industry awards and recognitions and is a regular keynote speaker.

speakers.name: Pras Velagapudi
speakers.bio: Pras joined Agility in 2023, with decades of experience in robotics planning and control, creating and deploying robots into home, industrial, and outdoor environments. He previously was a vice president of engineering and chief architect at Berkshire Grey, applying fleets of robots to logistics applications. In a landscape constantly disrupted by new technology, Pras stays focused on continually exploring what’s new.

speakers.name: Deepak Pathak
speakers.bio: Deepak Pathak is CEO and co-founder of Skild AI and a professor at Carnegie Mellon University. He's known for pioneering contributions to AI including self-supervised learning, artificial curiosity, and adaptive robot learning. At Skild AI, he's developing an AI foundation model for robotics. Deepak has received several prestigious honors including MIT Technology Review's 35under35, Okawa, IIT Kanpur Young Alumnus Award, and multiple best paper awards.

speakers.name: Chelsea Finn
speakers.bio: Chelsea Finn is an Assistant Professor in Computer Science and Electrical Engineering at Stanford University and a co-founder of Physical Intelligence (Pi). Her research interests lie in the capability of robots and other agents to develop broadly intelligent behavior through learning and interaction. To this end, her work has pioneered end-to-end deep learning methods for vision-based robotic manipulation, meta-learning algorithms for few-shot learning, and approaches for scaling robot learning to broad datasets. Her research has been recognized by awards such as the Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists and Engineers, the Sloan Fellowship, and the ACM doctoral dissertation award, and has been covered by various media outlets including the New York Times, Wired, and Bloomberg.

sessions.title: Watch Party: 開源世界模型如何驅動物理 AI 下一個突破
sessions.abstract:
本場 GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 團隊所主持，將由台灣的技術專家 Cliff Chiu 以中文講解並導讀國外精選演講，會中可以即時與講師互動、發問，歡迎您一起加入討論。(請注意：由於時區差異，本場講座的原講者可能不會參加。)

產生逼真的數據和強大的推理能力，使機器能夠理解現實世界的複雜性，仍然是物理 AI (physical AI) 領域最大的瓶頸之一。
NVIDIA Cosmos 世界基礎模型是 AI 模型與代理的核心，它們能夠以前所未有的方式感知、理解和適應現實世界。開發者可利用 Cosmos 開源模型與工具，加速訓練、客製化工作流程，並在機器人、自動駕駛車輛及視覺 AI 代理上更快部署。本場次亦將探討 Cosmos 最新更新如何推進物理感知學習、解鎖強大模擬到現實轉移能力，並賦予開發者建構新一代智慧適應 AI 系統。

在演講開始前 15 分鐘，下方將出現「Join Watch Party Now」連結。請點擊該連結，並在電腦應用程式或網頁瀏覽器開啟 Microsoft Teams 以線上加入。

sessions.abstract:
A GTC Session Watch Party is a replay of an original GTC talk hosted by our NVIDIA Team. This is an interactive session and we encourage you to join the discussion with any comments or questions. Please note that the original speakers of the talk listed below may not be in attendance.

Hosted by:

Quan Tran Minh, Developer Relations, NVIDIA

 Generating the photorealistic data and robust reasoning capabilities needed for machines to understand real-world complexity remains one of the biggest bottlenecks in physical AI. NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models are at the core of AI models and agents that can perceive, understand, and adapt real-world like never before. Learn how developers can use Cosmos open models and tools to accelerate training, customize pipelines, and deploy faster across robotics, autonomous vehicles, and vision AI agents. This session will also explore how the latest updates for Cosmos advance physics-aware learning, unlock powerful simulation-to-real capabilities, and empower developers to build the next generation of intelligent, adaptive AI systems. . /p>

sessions.abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。

会议主持人：

Tara Tao, Senior Developer Relationship Manager, NVIDIA

Generating the photorealistic data and robust reasoning capabilities needed for machines to understand real-world complexity remains one of the biggest bottlenecks in physical AI. NVIDIA Cosmos world foundation models are at the core of AI models and agents that can perceive, understand, and adapt real-world like never before. Learn how developers can use Cosmos open models and tools to accelerate training, customize pipelines, and deploy faster across robotics, autonomous vehicles, and vision AI agents. This session will also explore how the latest updates for Cosmos advance physics-aware learning, unlock powerful simulation-to-real capabilities, and empower developers to build the next generation of intelligent, adaptive AI systems.

  • Learn how video foundation models are evolving as Ming-Yu, VP of generative AI research, provides an in-depth look at cutting-edge visual multi-modal research.
  • Learn how robotics, autonomous vehicle, and humanoid downstream model training can benefit from world foundation models.
  • Explore open tools for video processing and tokenization to optimize physical AI workflows.

sessions.title: Watch Party: Evaluation-Driven Development: Best Practices for Building Reliable Agents
sessions.abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。

会议主持人：

Yipeng Li, Developer Community Manager, NVIDIA

Building agents is easy; reliably improving them is hard. As workflows shift to complex long-horizon tasks, traditional testing fails. How do you debug and improve non-deterministic behavior? This session presents a framework for evaluation-driven development. We move beyond “vibe eval” to disciplined engineering covering ablations, unit tests, trajectory evaluation, and LLM-as-judge patterns. Using a case study of achieving No. 1 on a Deep Research leaderboard, we demonstrate how to build pipelines that catch hallucinations and regressions while preventing overfitting. You'll leave with a workflow for implementing evaluation methods that take agents from prototype to scalable deployment using a suite of tooling enabled by NVIDIA NeMo and open NVIDIA Nemotron models for advanced reasoning.

  • Building agents is easy; reliably improving them is hard—especially for long-horizon, non-deterministic tasks where traditional testing fails. This session introduces a disciplined framework for evaluation-driven development, moving beyond “vibe eval” to structured methods like ablations, unit tests, trajectory evaluation, and LLM-as-judge patterns.
  • Using a case study of achieving No. 1 on a Deep Research leaderboard, we demonstrate pipelines that catch hallucinations and regressions while preventing overfitting. Learn how to implement evaluation workflows that scale agents from prototype to production.
  • The workflow leverages NVIDIA NeMo Evaluator for reproducible benchmarking, NeMo Guardrails for safety, NeMo Agent Toolkit for orchestration, and NVIDIA Nemotron models for advanced reasoning. We’ll show how these tools integrate to enable robust, scalable agent development.

speakers.name: Bryan Catanzaro
speakers.bio: Bryan is vice president of applied deep learning research at NVIDIA, where his team solves problems in fields ranging from video games to chip design. Bryan joined NVIDIA as a research scientist in 2011, when he drove the creation of CUDNN, now used by most AI researchers to train neural networks. He also worked at Baidu, where he built systems for training deep learning-based speech recognition on extremely large datasets, in both English and Chinese. Bryan earned his Ph.D. from the UC Berkeley, where he built the Copperhead language and compiler. He earned both his M.S. and B.S. from Brigham Young University, where he worked on computer arithmetic for field-programmable gate arrays.

sessions.title: Watch Party: Physical AI for Healthcare Robotics—Part 1: Simulation-First Design and Accelerated Development
sessions.abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。

会议主持人：

Wei Yan, Solution Architect, NVIDIA

Holly Ye, Account Manager, NVIDIA

Join Part 1 of a two-part curated session on how simulation-first design is changing the way healthcare robots are built. You’ll see how teams are using NVIDIA Physical AI—spanning world foundation models, physics-based simulation, synthetic data, and real-time sensor-to-action deployment—to move faster with less risk. This session focuses on how simulation and digital twins are being used in practice to speed up development, test decisions earlier, and optimize systems long before they reach the operating room

  • How Simulation-First Design Accelerates Healthcare Robotic Development: Learn how teams use digital twins, physics-based environments, and generative simulation to rapidly design, test, and refine robotic systems before they touch the real world.
  • How World Foundation Models and Synthetic Data Expand Capability and Coverage: See how specialized foundation models and SDG pipelines create rich scenario diversity, improve training efficiency, and strengthen early-stage algorithm performance.
  • How Developers are Building Next-Generation Devices With NVIDIA Physical AI: Hear from industry leaders using NVIDIA’s simulation and modeling technologies to optimize planning, validate concepts, and shorten product development cycles.

新一代視覺 AI 代理以 NVIDIA Blueprint 視訊搜尋和摘要 (video search and summarization，VSS) 為起點，它支援創建互動式分析代理，這些代理能夠處理海量影片、評估物理過程並提供決策洞察，從而簡化操作。該 Blueprint 將強大的視覺語言模型 (vision language model，VLM)、以大型語言模型為基礎的進階搜尋功能以及 Cosmos Reason VLM 整合到一套完整的軟體中。我們簡化了在邊緣端、本地端或雲端進行客製化和部署的過程。搶先了解 VSS 的新功能，加速您的 AI 代理開發。

The next generation of visual AI agents starts with NVIDIA Blueprint for video search and summarization (VSS), which enables the creation of interactive analytical agents that reason through massive amounts of video, assess physical processes, and provide decision-making insights to streamline operations. The blueprint combines powerful vision language models (VLMs), advanced search capabilities with large language models, and Cosmos Reason VLM into a full suite of software for building, We've made it easier to customize and deploy at the edge, on-premises, or in the cloud. Be the first to hear about the new features of VSS to accelerate your AI agent development.

会议主持人：

Ken He, Developer Community Manager, NVIDIA

The CUDA platform is the foundation of the GPU computing ecosystem. Every application and framework that uses the GPU does so through CUDA's libraries, compilers, runtimes and language—which means CUDA is growing as fast as its ecosystem is evolving. Presented by one of the architects of CUDA, at this engineering-focused talk you will learn about all that's new and what's coming next for both CUDA and GPU computing as a whole.

  • What's new in CUDA
  • CUDA's roadmap: what's coming in 2026
  • A deep dive into the technical details of CUDA's latest features
  • A look into the future of GPU computing
  • Pointers to many other CUDA talks at GTC 2026

sessions.title: Watch Party: 加速工業工程：AI 超級運算時代的產品設計與製造
sessions.abstract:
本場 GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 團隊所主持，將由台灣的技術專家 Jay Chen 以中文講解並導讀國外精選演講，會中可以即時與講師互動、發問，歡迎您一起加入討論。(請注意：由於時區差異，本場講座的原講者可能不會參加。)

加速運算和人工智慧超級運算正在重新定義工業工程，其應用範圍涵蓋電腦輔助工程 (CAE)、半導體設計和製造。這些領域共同推動世界構思、模擬和建構從先進機械到下一代晶片等各種產品的方式，而這些工作越來越多地在實際生產開始之前先在數位世界中完成。在本次演講中，NVIDIA 將展示 CUDA 加速平台、人工智慧物理、DGX 和雲端規模人工智慧基礎設施以及 NVIDIA Omniverse 如何重塑電腦輔助工程和半導體設計與製造的工作流程。將展示客戶如何利用 GPU 加速求解器、AI 物理模型和代理 AI 來縮短週轉時間、拓展設計探索範圍並提高良率和可靠性，從而揭示一個涵蓋經典、人工智慧和新興量子加速的統一加速運算藍圖。

在演講開始前 15 分鐘，下方將出現「Join Watch Party Now」連結。請點擊該連結，並在電腦應用程式或網頁瀏覽器開啟 Microsoft Teams 以線上加入。

speakers.name: Timothy Costa
speakers.bio:
Dr. Timothy Costa is the General Manager for Industrial and Computational Engineering at NVIDIA, leading strategy and innovation across these sectors. He holds a Ph.D. in Mathematics focused on numerical modeling of fluids and semiconductors.
At NVIDIA, Tim has directed key initiatives in HPC, Quantum, and CUDA-X, including building the NVIDIA Quantum platform and launching the HPC SDK. He now drives the use of accelerated computing and AI to tackle complex challenges in industrial and semiconductor applications.

sessions.title: Watch Party: Accelerating Industrial Engineering: From Product Design to Manufacturing in the AI Supercomputing Era
sessions.abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。

会议主持人：

Lex Yang, Solution Architect, NVIDIA

Jeff Li, Solution Architect, NVIDIA

Accelerated computing and AI supercomputing are redefining industrial engineering, spanning computational engineering (CAE) to semiconductor design and manufacturing. Together, these fields drive how the world conceives, simulates, and builds everything from advanced machinery to next-generation chips increasingly in the digital world before physical production begins. In this address, NVIDIA’s Tim Costa, general manager for Industrial and Computational Engineering, will show how CUDA-accelerated platforms, AI physics, DGX and cloud-scale AI infrastructure, and NVIDIA Omniverse are reshaping workflows across computer-aided engineering and semiconductor design and manufacturing. He will demonstrate how customers use GPU-accelerated solvers, AI physics models, and agentic AI to cut turnaround, broaden design exploration, and boost yield and reliability, revealing a unified accelerated-computing blueprint spanning classical, AI, and emerging quantum acceleration.

  • Accelerated computing and AI supercomputing are turning industrial engineering workflows into end‑to‑end digital, simulation‑first pipelines, where products are designed, validated, and optimized before they reach the factory.​​
  • NVIDIA’s CUDA platforms, DGX and cloud-scale infrastructure, and Omniverse enable GPU-accelerated solvers, physics-ML models, and agentic AI to dramatically cut turnaround time, expand design-space exploration, and improve yield and reliability across industrial engineering.​​

在会议开始时间前 15 分钟，登录状态下，下方会出现一个【Join Watch Party Now】的链接。点击该链接，可以在您的电脑上或从您的网络浏览器中启动 Microsoft Teams 参加会议。

sessions.title: Watch Party: Artist to Audience: The Developer Platform for Real-Time AI in Media
sessions.abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。

会议主持人：

Maggie Jiang, Regional Industry Sales Lead, NVIDIA

In this session, we’ll show how real-time AI is becoming the new fabric of modern media—connecting artist workflows to studio pipelines to distribution at global scale. You’ll learn the reference architecture behind an AI-native media stack: accelerated compute, low-latency networking, and developer-ready SDKs and APIs that let teams build, integrate, and ship AI into real production systems. We’ll walk through practical examples across creation, live production, enhancement, understanding, and delivery—focusing on what it takes to move from demos to deployed workflows, with measurable gains in speed, quality, and cost.

  • Learn how AI-powered tools and generative agents are transforming media production workflows, enabling faster, more creative, and cost-effective content creation across multiple formats.
  • Discover how AI-driven recommendation systems and dynamic content optimization are enhancing audience engagement and discoverability across platforms.
  • Gain insights into how AI enables more targeted ads, microtransactions, and tailored subscription services, driving more efficient and sustainable revenue streams.

在会议开始时间前 15 分钟，登录状态下，下方会出现一个【Join Watch Party Now】的链接。点击该链接，可以在您的电脑上或从您的网络浏览器中启动 Microsoft Teams 参加会议。

speakers.name: Richard Kerris
speakers.bio: Richard is the vice president and general manager of media & entertainment at NVIDIA, where he's responsible for industry strategy, business development, product management, developer relations, and marketing. At Alias/Wavefront, Apple, Lucasfilm, and other brands, Richard drove product development and marketing. At Alias/Wavefront, he was director of Maya technologies and led the teams that brought the groundbreaking 3D software to manufacturers and new platforms. At Apple, he was senior director of developer relations and managed the ProApps teams for Final Cut Pro, Logic, and Aperture. At Lucasfilm, he was CTO. He's a member of the Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers.

sessions.title: Watch Party: Transforming Retail Commerce and Supply Chain Operations With AI
sessions.abstract:
GTC Watch Party 是由 NVIDIA 本地专家主持，以中文讲解，带领参与者同步观看某一精选演讲并解读和实时答疑的会议形式。这是一个互动式的会议，我们鼓励您在聊天区参与讨论，提出见解或问题。但请注意，原始演讲者可能不会出席本会议。

会议主持人：

Maggie Jiang, Regional Industry Sales Lead, NVIDIA

Ransiki Zhang, Solution Architect, NVIDIA

Retail and consumer packaged goods (CPG) are uniquely positioned to use AI to drive revenue growth and margin expansion. Leading retailers Walmart, Shopify, Amazon, and Lowe’s are partnering with NVIDIA to deploy AI at enterprise scale. NVIDIA’s vice president and general manager of retail will share how retailers can build an end-to-end, CEO-sponsored AI strategy and infrastructure that avoids siloed initiatives and delivers measurable return on investment. We'll cover how agentic AI and physical AI are transforming supply chains and store operations through simulation, robotics, and computer vision, while Agentic Commerce, AI Blueprints, and data science libraries unlock incremental ecommerce and retail media revenue. Learn best practices for deploying AI at scale with interoperability across any cloud and ownership of models trained on proprietary retail data.

  • How to build an end-to-end AI strategy and infrastructure to ensure AI initiatives drive measurable ROI
  • Understand the importance of IP ownership of models trained with your proprietary data, and how to reduce inference cost.
  • How to deploy AI at scale across the entire enterprise
  • How to be in the driver seat with Agentic Commerce

在会议开始时间前 15 分钟，登录状态下，下方会出现一个【Join Watch Party Now】的链接。点击该链接，可以在您的电脑上或从您的网络浏览器中启动 Microsoft Teams 参加会议。

speakers.name: Azita Martin
speakers.bio: Azita leads NVIDIA's AI initiatives for the retail and consumer package goods (CPG) industries, advising retail and CPG executives on their AI strategy and best practices of leveraging AI, physical AI, and agentic AI to become a highly agile and AI-first company. Azita’s team develops NVIDIA's retail/CPG solution stack, leveraging NVIDIA’s Accelerated computing platform, and builds the ecosystem of software partners for intelligent supply chain, intelligent stores, and ecommerce.
